#!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Local audit script for project state verification.\n3: \n4: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n5: \"\"\"\n6: import contextlib\n7: import json\n8: import subprocess\n9: import sys\n10: from pathlib import Path\n11: \n12: \n13: # WP-2026-122: Deferred path resolution via runtime.project_root\n14: try:\n15:     from runtime.project_root import get_agent_dir, resolve_project_root\n16: except ImportError:\n17:     # Fallback if runtime.project_root not available\n18:     get_agent_dir = None\n19:     resolve_project_root = None\n20: \n21: PROJECT_ROOT = (\n22:     resolve_project_root()\n23:     if resolve_project_root is not None\n24:     else Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n25: )\n26: AGENT_DIR = (\n27:     get_agent_dir()\n28:     if get_agent_dir is not None\n29:     else PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n30: )\n31: COLLAB_DIR = AGENT_DIR / \"collaboration\"\n32: RUNTIME_DIR = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\"\n33: AUDIT_DIR = RUNTIME_DIR / \"audit\"\n34: \n35: \n36: def run_cmd(cmd, cwd=PROJECT_ROOT):\n37:     try:\n38:         return subprocess.check_output(  # noqa: S603\n39:             cmd, cwd=cwd, text=True, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT\n40:         ).strip()\n41:     except Exception as e:\n42:         return f\"[Error: {e}]\"\n43: \n44: \n45: def get_versions():\n46:     versions = {}\n47:     project_md = PROJECT_ROOT / \"PROJECT.md\"\n48:     if project_md.exists():\n49:         for line in project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n50:             if line.startswith(\"- Version:\"):\n51:                 raw = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n52:                 versions[\"project_md\"] = raw.strip(\"`\").strip(\"'\").strip('\"').strip()\n53:                 break\n54: \n55:     version_manifest = AGENT_DIR / \".version_manifest.json\"\n56:     if version_manifest.exists():\n57:         with contextlib.suppress(Exception):\n58:             manifest = json.loads(version_manifest.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n59:             versions[\"manifest\"] = manifest.get(\"agent_core_version\", \"unknown\")\n60: \n61:     pyproject = PROJECT_ROOT / \"pyproject.toml\"\n62:     if pyproject.exists():\n63:         for line in pyproject.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n64:             if line.startswith(\"version =\"):\n65:                 versions[\"pyproject\"] = line.split(\"=\")[1].strip().strip('\"').strip(\"'\")\n66:                 break\n67: \n68:     return versions\n69: \n70: \n71: def check_version_drift(versions):\n72:     cleaned = {}\n73:     for k, v in versions.items():\n74:         if v and v != \"unknown\":\n75:             cleaned[k] = runtime.project_root\n28: try:\n29:     from runtime.project_root import get_agent_dir, resolve_project_root\n30: except ImportError:\n31:     # Fallback if runtime.project_root not available\n32:     get_agent_dir = None\n33:     resolve_project_root = None\n34: \n35: def _project_root() -> Path:\n36:     if resolve_project_root is not None:\n37:         return resolve_project_root()\n38:     return Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n39: \n40: \n41: class _LazyPath:\n42:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n43:         self._resolver = resolver\n44: \n45:     def resolve(self) -> return self.resolve() / other\n53: \n54:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n55:         return str(self.resolve())\n56: \n57:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n58:         return str(self.resolve())\n59: \n60: \n61: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n62: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(\n63:     lambda: get_agent_dir() if get_agent_dir is not None else _project_root() / \".agent\"\n64: )\n65: RUNTIME_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: AGENT_DIR.resolve() / \"runtime\" / \"pytest-safe\")\n66: LOCK_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: RUNTIME_DIR.resolve() / \"pytest.lock\")\n67: LAST_RUN_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: RUNTIME_DIR.resolve() / \"last-run.log\")\n68: LAST_RUN_JSON = _LazyPath(lambda: RUNTIME_DIR.resolve() / \"last-run.json\")\n69: \n70: DEFAULT_PYTEST_ARGS = [\n71:     \"tests/test_event_bus.py\",\n72:     \"tests/test_supervisor.py\",\n73:     \"tests/test_agent_controller.py\",\n74:     \"tests/test_redact.py\",\n75:     \"tests/test_event_bus_hygiene.py\",\n76:     \"tests/test_check_skill_collisions.py\",\n77:     \"tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py\",\n78:     \"tests/unit/test_work_plan_schema.py\",\n79:     \"tests/unit/test_run_gates_dispatch.py\",\n80:     \"tests/unit/test_check_deliverables_exist.py\",\n81:     \"tests/unit/test_review_strategy_selection.py\",\n82:     \"tests/unit/test_pip_audit_policy.py\",\n83:     \"tests/unit/test_check_ruff_hook_scope.py\",\n84:     \"tests/unit/test_install_agent_system.py\",\n85:     \"tests/unit/test_review_budget_retry.py\",\n86:     \"tests/unit/test_bus_utils.py\",\n87:     \"tests/unit/test_archive_execution_log.py\",\n88:     \"tests/unit/test_archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n89:     \"tests/unit/test_no_history_truncation.py\",\n90:     \"tests/unit/test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py\",\n91:     \"-q\",\n92:     \"-p\",\n93:     \"no:cacheprovider\",\n94: ]\n95: \n96: LEVEL_CHOICES = {\"unit\", \"integration\", \"all\"}\n97: \n98: \n99: def iso_now() -> str:\n100:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\n101: \n102: \n103: def ensure_runtime_dir() -> None:\n104:     RUNTIME_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n105: \n106: \n107: def is_pid_running(pid: int) -> bool:\n108:     if pid <= 0:\n109:         return False\n110:     try:\n111:         os.kill(pid, 0)\n112:     except OSError:\n113:         return False\n114:     return True\n115: \n116: \n117: def read_json(path: Path) -> dict:\n118:     try:\n119:         return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n120:     except Exception:\n121:         return {}\n122: \n123: \n124: def write_json(path: Path, payload: dict) -> None:\n125:     path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n126: \n127: \n128: def acquire_lock(force_unlock: bool = False) -> dict:\n129:     ensure_runtime_dir()\n130: \n131:     if LOCK_FILE.exists():\n132:         stale = True\n133:         lock_data = read_json(LOCK_FILE)\n134:         lock_pid = int(lock_data.get(\"pid\", 0) or 0)\n135:         if is_pid_running(lock_pid):\n136:             stale = False\n137:         if not stale and not force_unlock:\n138:             raise RuntimeError(\n139:                 f\"Ya hay un pytest activo (pid={lock_pid}). \"\n140:                 f\"Si estas seguro de que es stale, usa --force-unlock.\"\n141:             )\n142:         LOCK_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n143: \n144:     payload = {\n145:         \"pid\": os.getpid(),\n146:         \"started_at\": iso_now(),\n147:         \"cwd\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n148:     }\n149:     fd = os.open(str(LOCK_FILE), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)\n150:     try:\n151:         with os.fdopen(fd, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as handle:\n152:             json.dump(payload, handle, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n153:     except Exception:\n154:         LOCK_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n155:         raise\n156:     return payload\n157: \n158: \n159: def release_lock() -> None:\n160:     LOCK_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n161: \n162: \n163: def iter_project_temp_dirs() -> Iterable[Path]:\n164:     for entry in PROJECT_ROOT.iterdir():\n165:         if not entry.is_dir():\n166:             continue\n167:         if entry.name in {\".pytest_tmp\", \"_pytest_tmp\"} or entry.name.startswith(\n168:             \"_pytest_tmp_\"\n169:         ):\n170:             yield entry\n171: \n172: \n173: def remove_tree(path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n174:     try:\n175:         shutil.rmtree(path)\n176:         return True, \"\"\n177:     except FileNotFoundError:\n178:         return True, \"\"\n179:     except Exception as exc:\n180:         return False, str(exc)\n181: \n182: \n183: def cleanup_known_temp_dirs() -> dict:\n184:     removed: list[str] = []\n185:     failed: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n186: \n187:     for path in iter_project_temp_dirs():\n188:         ok, error = remove_tree(path)\n189:         if ok:\n190:             removed.append(path.name)\n191:         else:\n192:             failed.append({\"path\": str(path), \"error\": error})\n193: \n194:     return {\"removed\": removed, \"failed\": failed}\n195: \n196: \n197: def path_is_accessible(path: Path) -> bool:\n198:     try:\n199:         with os.scandir(path) as iterator:\n200:             for _ in iterator:\n201:                 break\n202:         return True\n203:     except (FileNotFoundError, NotADirectoryError, PermissionError):\n204:         return False\n205: \n206: \n207: def get_lock_status() -> dict:\n208:     if not LOCK_FILE.exists():\n209:         return {\"present\": False}\n210: \n211:     lock_data = read_json(LOCK_FILE)\n212:     lock_pid = int(lock_data.get(\"pid\", 0) or 0)\n213:     return {\n214:         \"present\": True,\n215:         \"pid\": lock_pid,\n216:         \"active\": is_pid_running(lock_pid),\n217:         \"data\": lock_data,\n218:     }\n219: \n220: \n\n(Showing lines 1-220 of 446. {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779391384086,"sessionID":"ses_1b402d937ffeqtTPG4uh0VppFe","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_ORzWHlEUL53p2td32575E4dv","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\agent_system\\scripts\\project_paths.py","offset":1,"limit":220},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\agent_system\\scripts\\project_paths.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Project paths resolution helper.\n4: \n5: Centralized logic for resolving canonical project root and agent directory.\n6: Detects path drift and ensures consistent path handling across scripts.\n7: \"\"\"\n8: \n9: from pathlib import Path\n10: \n11: \n12: class ProjectPathsResolver:\n13:     \"\"\"Resolve canonical project paths and detect drift.\"\"\"\n14: \n15:     CANONICAL_AGENT_MARKERS = (\n16:         \"agent_controller.py\",\n17:         \"project_manifest.toml\",\n18:         \".version_manifest.json\",\n19:     )\n20: \n21:     def __init__(self, start_dir: str | Path):\n22:         self.start_path = Path(start_dir).resolve()\n23:         if not self.start_path.exists():\n24:             raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Start directory not found: {start_dir}\")\n25: \n26:     def resolve_paths(self) -> dict[str, str | bool]:\n27:         \"\"\"\n28:         Resolve canonical project root and agent directory.\n29: \n30:         Returns dict with:\n31:         - project_root: str | None\n32:         - agent_dir: str | None\n33:         - drift_detected: bool\n34:         - drift_type: str | None ('multiple_agent_dirs', 'agent_not_at_root', 'none')\n35:         - message: str\n36: \n37:         Drift is detected if:\n38:         - Multiple .agent/ directories found in the tree\n39:         - .agent/ not at project root (though we still resolve it)\n40:         \"\"\"\n41:         # Find project root by searching upwards for .agent\n42:         project_root = self._find_project_root(self.start_path)\n43:         if not project_root:\n44:             return {\n45:                 \"project_root\": None,\n46:                 \"agent_dir\": None,\n47:                 \"drift_detected\": False,\n48:                 \"drift_type\": None,\n49:                 \"message\": \"No .agent directory found\",\n50:             }\n51: \n52:         agent_dir = project_root / \".agent\"\n53: \n54:         # Check for drift: multiple operational .agent roots in the tree.\n55:         # Sandbox fixtures are ignored because they are intentionally duplicated\n56:         # for tests and must not block the canonical runtime root.\n57:         all_agent_dirs = [\n58:             d\n59:             for d in project_root.rglob(\".agent\")\n60:             if d.is_dir() and \"backups\" not in d.parts\n61:         ]\n62: \n63:         drift_detected = False\n64:         drift_type = \"none\"\n65: \n66:         if len(all_agent_dirs) > 1:\n67:             drift_detected = True\n68:             drift_type = \"multiple_agent_dirs\"\n69:             project_root = None\n70:             agent_dir = None\n71: \n72:         message = (\n73:             \"Paths resolved successfully\"\n74:             if not drift_detected\n75:             else f\"Multiple .agent directories found: {[str(d) for d in all_agent_dirs]}\"\n76:         )\n77: \n78:         return {\n79:             \"project_root\": str(project_root) if project_root else None,\n80:             \"agent_dir\": str(agent_dir) if agent_dir else None,\n81:             \"drift_detected\": drift_detected,\n82:             \"drift_type\": drift_type,\n83:             \"message\": message,\n84:         }\n85: \n86:     def _find_project_root(self, start_path: Path) -> Path | None:\n87:         \"\"\"Find the nearest project root with a canonical .agent directory.\n88: \n89:         Resolution order:\n90:         1. Prefer the closest ancestor of ``start_path`` that already contains a\n91:            canonical ``.agent`` directory. This keeps local test fixtures and\n92:            nested projects self-contained.\n93: \n94:         If no ancestor matches, return ``None``. Callers that need the canonical\n95:         runtime root should provide it explicitly instead of inferring it from a\n96:         parent workspace. This keeps the resolver predictable for tests.\n97:         \"\"\"\n98:         current = start_path\n99:         sandbox_boundaries = {\"factory\", \"pytest\", \"tempfile\"}\n100: \n101:         # First pass: nearest ancestor containing a canonical .agent directory.\n102:         while current != current.parent:\n103:             agent_dir = current / \".agent\"\n104:             if (\n105:                 agent_dir.exists()\n106:                 and agent_dir.is_dir()\n107:                 and self._has_agent_markers(agent_dir)\n108:             ):\n109:                 return current\n110:             if current.name in sandbox_boundaries:\n111:                 break\n112:             current = current.parent\n113: \n114:         return None\n115: \n116:     def _has_agent_markers(self, agent_dir: Path) -> bool:\n117:         \"\"\"Check whether the agent directory exposes canonical runtime markers.\n118: \n119:         Accepts .agent/ if it contains at least one canonical marker.\n120:         Prevents empty or truly partial fixtures, but allows legacy structures.\n121:         \"\"\"\n122:         return any(\n123:             (agent_dir / marker).exists() for marker in self.CANONICAL_AGENT_MARKERS\n124:         )\n125: \n126:     def get_project_root(self) -> Path | None:\n127:         \"\"\"Get canonical project root path.\"\"\"\n128:         result = self.resolve_paths()\n129:         if result[\"project_root\"]:\n130:             return Path(result[\"project_root\"])\n131:         return None\n132: \n133:     def get_agent_dir(self) -> Path | None:\n134:         \"\"\"Get canonical agent directory path.\"\"\"\n135:         result = self.resolve_paths()\n136:         if result[\"agent_dir\"]:\n137:             return Path(result[\"agent_dir\"])\n138:         return None\n139: \n140:     def has_drift(self) -> bool:\n141:         \"\"\"Check if path drift is detected.\"\"\"\n142:         return self.resolve_paths()[\"drift_detected\"]\n143: \n144:     def get_drift_info(self) -> dict:\n145:         \"\"\"Get drift information.\"\"\"\n146:         result = self.resolve_paths()\n147:         return {\n148:             \"drift_detected\": result[\"drift_detected\"],\n149:             \"drift_type\": result[\"drift_type\"],\n150:             \"message\": result[\"message\"],\n151:         }\n\n(End of file - total 151 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nProject paths resolution helper.\n\nCentralized logic for resolving canonical project root and agent directory.\nDetects path drift and ensures consistent path handling across scripts.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\nclass ProjectPathsResolver:\n    \"\"\"Resolve canonical project if it exists\n41:             env_copy = os.environ.copy()\n42:             env_copy.pop(\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\", None)\n43:             with patch.dict(os.environ, env_copy, clear=True):\n44: importlib.reload(pr)\n50:                 root = pr.resolve_project_root()\n51: \n52:                 # Should be parent of runtime/ directory (which is project root)\n53:                 # runtime/project_root.py -> runtime/ -> project root\n54:                 expected = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent\n55:                 assert root == expected\n56: \n57:     def {"type":"text","timestamp":1779391432196,"sessionID":"ses_1b402d937ffeqtTPG4uh0VppFe","part":{"id":"prt_e4bfe811f001DOavtCMVdbDV5i","messageID":"msg_e4bfe193b001oPP9buMzAJtYSp","sessionID":"ses_1b402d937ffeqtTPG4uh0VppFe","type":"text","text":"## SUMMARY\nWP-2026-122 is not complete: the resolver exists, but the CLI wiring still cannot reliably switch the runtime root.\n\n## BLOCKERS\n- `.agent/agent_controller.py:47-121, 2429-2435` resolves `PROJECT_ROOT/AGENT_DIR/COLLAB_DIR` at import time, then sets `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT` inside `main()` too late; the `lru_cache` is already frozen to the repo root, so `--project-root` cannot actually redirect this process.\n- `scripts/manager_review_bridge.py:21-37, 253-347` still has no `--project-root` argument and uses a fixed import-time `PROJECT_ROOT`, so this entry point cannot operate against an external destination as required.\n\n## SUGGESTIONS\n- none\n\nDECISION: CHANGES","time":{"start":1779391430943,"end":1779391432195},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"msg_0f746f93166037f0016a0f5b95ef80819181d2cbe9d86070e9","phase":"final_answer"}}}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779391966930,"sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_wygKSHkwKbaLZSqAK1eH5E1l","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\runtime\\project_root.py","offset":1,"limit":260},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\runtime\\project_root.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: \"\"\"\n2: Project Root Resolution Module - Central contract for WP-2026-122.\n3: \n4: This module provides a single source of truth for resolving the project root\n5: path, supporting both the default motor repository and external destination\n6: workspaces via environment variable injection.\n7: \n8: Precedence (effective):\n9:     1. AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable (set by entry points after parsing --project-root)\n10:     2. Derived from Path(__file__) (defaults to motor repository)\n11: \n12: Usage:\n13:     from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root, get_agent_dir, get_collab_dir\n14: \n15:     root = resolve_project_root()  # Path to project root\n16:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()    # Path to .agent/ directory\n17:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()  # Path to .agent/collaboration/ directory\n18: \n19: Design:\n20:     - Import-safe: no side effects at import time\n21:     - Cacheable: results are memoized for performance\n22:     - Single mechanism: resolve_project_root() is the only resolution path\n23:     - Backward compatible: defaults to motor repo when no external root is injected\n24: \"\"\"\n25: \n26: from __future__ import annotations\n27: \n28: import os\n29: from functools import lru_cache\n30: from pathlib import Path\n31: \n32: \n33: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n34: def resolve_project_root() -> Path:\n35:     \"\"\"\n36:     Resolve the project root path with proper precedence.\n37: \n38:     Before: Requires no state; reads os.environ[\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\"] if set.\n39:     During: Checks environment variable first, then falls back to derivation from __file__.\n40:     After: Returns absolute Path to project root, cached for subsequent calls.\n41: \n42:     Precedence:\n43:         1. AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable (if set and non-empty)\n44:         2. Derived from this module's location (runtime/project_root.py -> parent)\n45: \n46:     Returns:\n47:         Absolute Path to the project root directory.\n48: \n49:     Note:\n50:         Entry points that accept --project-root should export the value to\n51:         AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable immediately after parsing,\n52:         before importing any modules that depend on the project root.\n53:         This ensures a single channel of propagation (env var) rather than\n54:         parallel mechanisms.\n55:     \"\"\"\n56:     # Check environment variable first (set by entry points after CLI parsing)\n57:     env_root = os.environ.get(\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\", \"\").strip()\n58:     if env_root:\n59:         return Path(env_root).resolve()\n60: \n61:     # Fallback: derive from this module's location\n62:     # runtime/project_root.py -> runtime/ -> project root\n63:     return Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n64: \n65: \n66: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n67: def get_agent_dir() -> Path:\n68:     \"\"\"\n69:     Get the .agent/ directory path.\n70: \n71:     Before: Requires resolve_project_root() to be available.\n72:     During: Calls resolve_project_root() and appends \".agent\".\n73:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/ directory, cached.\n74: \n75:     Returns:\n76:         Absolute Path to the .agent/ directory.\n77:     \"\"\"\n78:     return resolve_project_root() / \".agent\"\n79: \n80: \n81: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n82: def get_collab_dir() -> Path:\n83:     \"\"\"\n84:     Get the .agent/collaboration/ directory path.\n85: \n86:     Before: Requires get_agent_dir() to be available.\n87:     During: Calls get_agent_dir() and appends \"collaboration\".\n88:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/collaboration/ directory, cached.\n89: \n90:     Returns:\n91:         Absolute Path to the .agent/collaboration/ directory.\n92:     \"\"\"\n93:     return get_agent_dir() / \"collaboration\"\n94: \n95: \n96: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n97: def get_runtime_dir() -> Path:\n98:     \"\"\"\n99:     Get the .agent/runtime/ directory path.\n100: \n101:     Before: Requires get_agent_dir() to be available.\n102:     During: Calls get_agent_dir() and appends \"runtime\".\n103:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/runtime/ directory, cached.\n104: \n105:     Returns:\n106:         Absolute Path to the .agent/runtime/ directory.\n107:     \"\"\"\n108:     return get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\"\n109: \n110: \n111: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n112: def get_context_dir() -> Path:\n113:     \"\"\"\n114:     Get the .agent/context/ directory path.\n115: \n116:     Before: Requires get_agent_dir() to be available.\n117:     During: Calls get_agent_dir() and appends \"context\".\n118:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/context/ directory, cached.\n119: \n120:     Returns:\n121:         Absolute Path to .agent/context/ directory.\n122:     \"\"\"\n123:     return get_agent_dir() / \"context\"\n124: \n125: \n126: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n127: def get_scripts_dir() -> Path:\n128:     \"\"\"\n129:     Get the scripts/ directory path.\n130: \n131:     Before: Requires resolve_project_root() to be available.\n132:     During: Calls resolve_project_root() and appends \"scripts\".\n133:     After: Returns absolute Path to scripts/ directory, cached.\n134: \n135:     Returns:\n136:         Absolute Path to scripts/ directory.\n137:     \"\"\"\n138:     return resolve_project_root() / \"scripts\"\n139: \n140: \n141: def clear_cache() -> None:\n142:     \"\"\"\n143:     Clear all cached path resolutions.\n144: \n145:     Before: Requires no state.\n146:     During: Clears lru_cache for all cached functions.\n147:     After: Next call to any resolver will recompute the value.\n148: \n149:     Use this only in testing scenarios where you need to simulate\n150:     environment changes between calls.\n151:     \"\"\"\n152:     resolve_project_root.cache_clear()\n153:     get_agent_dir.cache_clear()\n154:     get_collab_dir.cache_clear()\n155:     get_runtime_dir.cache_clear()\n156:     get_context_dir.cache_clear()\n157:     get_scripts_dir.cache_clear()\n158: \n159: \n160: # ============================================================================\n161: # BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY: Lazy constants for gradual migration\n162: # ============================================================================\n163: # These are provided for modules that haven't been migrated to deferred access\n164: # yet. They are computed at first access, not at import time.\n165: \n166: \n167: class _LazyPaths:\n168:     \"\"\"Lazy path resolver for backward compatibility during migration.\"\"\"\n169: \n170:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n171:         self._project_root: Path | None = None\n172:         self._agent_dir: Path | None = None\n173:         self._collab_dir: Path | None = None\n174:         self._runtime_dir: Path | None = None\n175:         self._context_dir: Path | None = None\n176:         self._scripts_dir: Path | None = None\n177: \n178:     @property\n179:     def project_root(self) -> Path:\n180:         \"\"\"Lazy project_root for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n181:         if self._project_root is None:\n182:             self._project_root = resolve_project_root()\n183:         return self._project_root\n184: \n185:     @property\n186:     def agent_dir(self) -> Path:\n187:         \"\"\"Lazy agent_dir for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n188:         if self._agent_dir is None:\n189:             self._agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n190:         return self._agent_dir\n191: \n192:     @property\n193:     def collab_dir(self) -> Path:\n194:         \"\"\"Lazy collab_dir for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n195:         if self._collab_dir is None:\n196:             self._collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n197:         return self._collab_dir\n198: \n199:     @property\n200:     def runtime_dir(self) -> Path:\n201:         \"\"\"Lazy runtime_dir for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n202:         if self._runtime_dir is None:\n203:             self._runtime_dir = get_runtime_dir()\n204:         return self._runtime_dir\n205: \n206:     @property\n207:     def context_dir(self) -> Path:\n208:         \"\"\"Lazy context_dir for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n209:         if self._context_dir is None:\n210:             self._context_dir = get_context_dir()\n211:         return self._context_dir\n212: \n213:     @property\n214:     def scripts_dir(self) -> Path:\n215:         \"\"\"Lazy scripts_dir for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n216:         if self._scripts_dir is None:\n217:             self._scripts_dir = get_scripts_dir()\n218:         return self._scripts_dir\n219: \n220: \n221: # Singleton instance for backward compatibility\n222: lazy_paths = _LazyPaths()\n\n(End of file - total 222 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"\nProject Root Resolution Module - Central contract for WP-2026-122.\n\nThis module provides a single source AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable (set by entry points after parsing --project-root)\n    2. Derived from Path(__file__) (defaults to motor repository)\n\nUsage:\n    from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root, get_agent_dir, get_collab_dir\n\n    root = resolve_project_root()  # Path to project root\n    agent_dir = get_agent_dir()    # Path to .agent/ directory\n    collab_dir = get_collab_dir()  # Path to .agent/collaboration/ directory\n\nDesign:\n    - Import-safe: no side effects at import time","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"runtime\\project_root.py","time":{"start":1779391966920,"end":1779391966928}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_04a3d30e9e1527e3016a0f5dacbf648191b4ae95cd115e7951"}},"id":"prt_e4c06aa34001EU1Tc1uAA6tEw9","sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","messageID":"msg_e4c069c54001snYZUhY8eSuQ2b"}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779391974751,"sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_jkpxUhHTjzaQtjaBaXqzi1i9","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py","offset":1,"limit":220},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED), str(_AGENT_DIR)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # ============================================================================\n100: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n101: # ============================================================================\n102: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n103: \n104: \n105: # Deferred path resolution - no longer fixed at import time\n106: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n107: PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n108: AGENT_DIR = get_agent_dir()\n109: COLLAB_DIR = get_collab_dir()\n110: CONTEXT_DIR = get_context_dir()\n111: \n112: # State files\n113: WORK_PLAN = COLLAB_DIR / \"work_plan.md\"\n114: EXEC_LOG = COLLAB_DIR / \"execution_log.md\"\n115: REVIEW_QUEUE = COLLAB_DIR / \"review_queue.md\"\n116: NOTIFICATIONS = COLLAB_DIR / \"notifications.md\"\n117: TURN_FILE = COLLAB_DIR / \"TURN.md\"\n118: STATE_FILE = COLLAB_DIR / \"STATE.md\"\n119: PROJECT_MAP = CONTEXT_DIR / \"project_map.md\"\n120: ARCHIVE_DIR = COLLAB_DIR / \"archive\"\n121: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = AGENT_DIR / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n122: \n123: # Archive configuration\n124: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n125: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n126: \n127: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n128: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n129: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n130: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n131: \n132: \n133: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n134:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n135: \n136:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n137:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n138:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n139:     \"\"\"\n140:     try:\n141:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n142:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n143:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n144:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n145:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n146: \n147: \n148: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n149: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n150: \n151: # Estados validos para validacion\n152: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n153:     \"DRAFT\",\n154:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n155:     \"APPROVED\",\n156:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n157:     \"COMPLETED\",\n158:     \"N/A\",\n159: }\n160: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n161:     \"PENDING\",\n162:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n163:     \"BLOCKED\",\n164:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n165:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n166:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n167:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n168:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n169:     \"COMPLETED\",\n170:     \"N/A\",\n171: }\n172: \n173: # Ensure directories exist\n174: CONTEXT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n175: ARCHIVE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n176: \n177: # Event bus\n178: event_bus = EventBus(AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"events\") if BUS_AVAILABLE else None\n179: \n180: # Circuit breaker state\n181: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n182: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\"\n183: \n184: # ============================================================================\n185: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n186: # ============================================================================\n187: \n188: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n189:     \"work_plan.md\",\n190:     \"execution_log.md\",\n191:     \"STATE.md\",\n192:     \"TURN.md\",\n193:     \"notifications.md\",\n194:     \".session_state.json\",\n195: }\n196: EXCLUDE_FILES = {str((COLLAB_DIR / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n197: EXCLUDE_FILES.add(str((CONTEXT_DIR / \"project_map.md\").resolve()))\n198: # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n199: EXCLUDE_FILES.add(str((AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve()))\n200: # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n201: EXCLUDE_FILES.add(str((AGENT_DIR / \"config\").resolve()))\n202: \n203: \n204: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n205:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n206:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n207:     in_section = False\n208:     files = set()\n209: \n210:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n211:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n212:             return False\n213:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n214:             return True\n215:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n216:             return True\n217:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n218:         return \".\" in basename\n219: \n220:     for line in lines:\n\n(Showing lines 1-220 of 2511. Use offset=221 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n\"\"\"\nAgent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n==========================================\nOrquestador con:\n- Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n- Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n- Completion Verification antes de review\n- Quality Gates extendidos\n- Session Recovery\n- Project Map y estado del workflow\n\nUso:\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agent_controller.py","time":{"start":1779391974741,"end":1779391974749}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_0ad1c5a94e37cfd1016a0f5db55a6481918943b4f0af0f5e99"}},"id":"prt_e4c06cc0a001CGVZZb3p1T9IXE","sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","messageID":"msg_e4c06b0a6001NGxNd5zLiwIEzS"}}

try:\n17:     from runtime.project_root import get_collab_dir, resolve_project_root\n18: except ImportError:\n19:     # Fallback if runtime.project_root not available\n20:     get_collab_dir = None\n21:     resolve_project_root = None\n22: \n23: COLLAB_DIR = (\n24:     get_collab_dir()\n25:     if get_collab_dir is not None\n26:     else Path(__file__).parent / \"collaboration\"\n27: )\n28: PROJECT_ROOT = (\n29:     resolve_project_root()\n30:     if resolve_project_root is not None\n31:     else Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n32: )\n33: \n34: # Deferred path resolution for collaboration files\n35: WORK_PLAN = COLLAB_DIR / \"work_plan.md\"\n36: EXEC_LOG = COLLAB_DIR / \"execution_log.md\"\n37: REVIEW_QUEUE = COLLAB_DIR / \"review_queue.md\"\n38: \n39: \n40: def _read_text(path: Path) -> str:\n41:     try:\n42:         if not path.exists():\n43:             return \"\"\n44:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n45:     except Exception:\n46:         return \"\"\n47: \n48: \n49: def get_log_status(log_path: Path) -> str:\n50:     content = _read_text(log_path)\n51:     if not content:\n52:         return \"\"\n53:     for line in content.splitlines():\n54:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n55:             return line.split(\"**Estado:**\", 1)[1].strip().upper()\n56:     return \"\"\n57: \n58: \n59: def is_relaxed_completion_status(status: str) -> bool:\n60:     return status.upper() in {\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"COMPLETED\"}\n61: \n62: \n63: def check_tasks_completed(plan_path: Path, log_status: str) -> bool:\n64:     if is_relaxed_completion_status(log_status):\n65:         return True\n66:     content = _read_text(plan_path)\n67:     if not content:\n68:         return False\n69:     return \"- [ ]\" not in content\n70: \n71: \n72: def check_execution_summary(log_path: Path, log_status: str | None = None) -> bool:\n73:     if log_status and is_relaxed_completion_status(log_status):\n74:         return True\n75:     content = _read_text(log_path)\n76:     if not content:\n77:         return False\n78:     upper = content.upper()\n79:     if \"IN_PROGRESS\" in upper:\n80:         return False\n81:     return \"**ESTADO:**\" in upper or \"RESUMEN\" in upper\n82: \n83: \n84: def check_no_escalations(queue_path: Path) -> bool:\n85:     content = _read_text(queue_path)\n86:     if not content:\n87:         return True\n88:     upper = content.upper()\n89:     return \"PENDING\" not in upper and \"BLOCKED\" not in upper\n90: \n91: \n92: def resolve_test_command(project_root: Path) -> list[str]:\n93:     tests_dir = project_root / \"tests\"\n94:     if not tests_dir.exists():\n95:         return []\n96:     safe_runner = project_root / \"scripts\" / \"run_pytest_safe.py\"\n97:     if safe_runner.exists():\n98:         return [sys.executable, str(safe_runner), \"--level\", \"unit\"]\n99:     return [sys.executable, \"-m\", \"pytest\", \"tests\", \"-q\"]\n100: \n101: \n102: def run_tests(project_root: Path) -> bool:\n103:     cmd = resolve_test_command(project_root)\n104:     if not cmd:\n105:         return True\n106:     try:\n107:         result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=project_root)\n108:         return result.returncode == 0\n109:     except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError):\n110:         return True\n111: \n112: \n113: def check_quality_gates(project_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n114:     return {\n115:         \"tests\": run_tests(project_root),\n116:         \"tasks_completed\": check_tasks_completed(WORK_PLAN, get_log_status(EXEC_LOG)),\n117:         \"no_escalations\": check_no_escalations(REVIEW_QUEUE),\n118:         \"execution_summary\": check_execution_summary(\n119:             EXEC_LOG, get_log_status(EXEC_LOG)\n120:         ),\n121:     }\n\n(End of file - total 121 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603\n\"\"\"\nCompletion common utilities.\n\nWP-2026-122: {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779391984138,"sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_HVCL3uF5PwZbJUCuNQCqY8XE","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\session_tracker.py","offset":1,"limit":220},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\session_tracker.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: \"\"\"Session Tracker Lite para proyectos pequenos (<30 archivos).\n2: \n3: Version simplificada de Session Recovery optimizada para uso local\n4: con proyectos pequenos. No usa hashes MD5 complejos.\n5: \n6: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n7: \n8: Uso:\n9:     from .session_tracker import save_session, detect_stale_session, recover_session\n10: \n11:     # Al finalizar\n12:     save_session()\n13: \n14:     # Al iniciar (detectar sesion antigua)\n15:     if detect_stale_session():\n16:         recover_session()\n17: \"\"\"\n18: \n19: from __future__ import annotations\n20: \n21: import json\n22: from contextlib import suppress\n23: from datetime import datetime, timedelta\n24: from pathlib import Path\n25: \n26: \n27: # WP-2026-122: Deferred path resolution via runtime.project_root\n28: try:\n29:     from runtime.project_root import get_collab_dir\n30: except ImportError:\n31:     # Fallback if runtime.project_root not available\n32:     get_collab_dir = None\n33: \n34: COLLAB_DIR = (\n35:     get_collab_dir()\n36:     if get_collab_dir is not None\n37:     else Path(__file__).parent / \"collaboration\"\n38: )\n39: SESSION_FILE = COLLAB_DIR / \".session_state.json\"\n40: \n41: STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 2\n42: MAX_FILES_TO_LIST = 10\n43: \n44: \n45: def save_session() -> None:\n46:     \"\"\"Guarda estado simple de la sesion actual.\"\"\"\n47:     with suppress(OSError, TypeError, ValueError):\n48:         COLLAB_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n49: \n50:         session = {\n51:             \"last_activity\": datetime.now().isoformat(),\n52:             \"active_plan\": _get_current_plan_id(),\n53:             \"files_count\": _count_project_files(),\n54:             \"version\": \"1.0-lite\",\n55:         }\n56: \n57:         SESSION_FILE.write_text(\n58:             json.dumps(session, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n59:         )\n60: \n61: \n62: def detect_stale_session() -> bool:\n63:     \"\"\"Detecta si han pasado mas de 2 horas desde la ultima actividad.\"\"\"\n64:     if not SESSION_FILE.exists():\n65:         return False\n66: \n67:     try:\n68:         session = json.loads(SESSION_FILE.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n69:         last_activity = datetime.fromisoformat(session[\"last_activity\"])\n70:         return datetime.now() - last_activity > timedelta(hours=STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS)\n71:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError):\n72:         return False\n73: \n74: \n75: def recover_session() -> dict | None:\n76:     \"\"\"Recupera informacion de la sesion anterior.\"\"\"\n77:     if not SESSION_FILE.exists():\n78:         return None\n79: \n80:     with suppress(Exception):\n81:         session = json.loads(SESSION_FILE.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n82:         last = datetime.fromisoformat(session[\"last_activity\"])\n83:         elapsed = datetime.now() - last\n84:         hours_ago = elapsed.total_seconds() / 3600\n85: \n86:         print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n87:         print(\"[INFO] SESION ANTERIOR DETECTADA\")\n88:         print(\"=\" * 60)\n89:         print(f\"[INFO] Ultima actividad: hace {hours_ago:.1f} horas\")\n90:         print(f\"[INFO] Plan activo: {session.get('active_plan', 'N/A')}\")\n91:         print(f\"[INFO] Archivos en proyecto: {session.get('files_count', 'N/A')}\")\n92: \n93:         modified = _get_recently_modified_files(hours=int(hours_ago) + 1)\n94:         if modified:\n95:             print(\"\\n[INFO] Archivos modificados:\")\n96:             for filepath in modified[:MAX_FILES_TO_LIST]:\n97:                 print(f\"   - {filepath}\")\n98:             if len(modified) > MAX_FILES_TO_LIST:\n99:                 print(f\"   ... y {len(modified) - MAX_FILES_TO_LIST} mas\")\n100: \n101:         print(\"=\" * 60 + \"\\n\")\n102:         return session\n103:     return None\n104: \n105: \n106: def show_recovery_hint() -> None:\n107:     \"\"\"Muestra hint suave si hay sesion antigua.\"\"\"\n108:     if detect_stale_session():\n109:         print(\"[INFO] Hay una sesion anterior (>2h). Usa --recover para ver detalles.\")\n110: \n111: \n112: def _get_current_plan_id() -> str:\n113:     \"\"\"Obtiene el ID del plan actual desde work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n114:     work_plan = COLLAB_DIR / \"work_plan.md\"\n115:     if not work_plan.exists():\n116:         return \"N/A\"\n117: \n118:     with suppress(Exception):\n119:         content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n120:         for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n121:             if \"**ID:**\" in line:\n122:                 return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n123: \n124:     return \"N/A\"\n125: \n126: \n127: def _count_project_files() -> int:\n128:     \"\"\"Cuenta archivos relevantes en el proyecto.\"\"\"\n129:     project_root = COLLAB_DIR.parent.parent\n130:     ignore_dirs = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\"}\n131:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n132: \n133:     count = 0\n134:     with suppress(Exception):\n135:         for filepath in project_root.rglob(\"*\"):\n136:             if any(part in ignore_dirs for part in filepath.parts):\n137:                 continue\n138:             if filepath.is_file() and filepath.suffix in extensions:\n139:                 count += 1\n140:                 if count > 50:\n141:                     break\n142: \n143:     return count\n144: \n145: \n146: def _get_recently_modified_files(hours: int = 2) -> list[str]:\n147:     \"\"\"Obtiene archivos modificados en las ultimas N horas.\"\"\"\n148:     project_root = COLLAB_DIR.parent.parent\n149:     cutoff = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=hours)\n150: \n151:     ignore_dirs = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\"}\n152:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n153: \n154:     modified = []\n155:     with suppress(Exception):\n156:         for filepath in project_root.rglob(\"*\"):\n157:             if any(part in ignore_dirs for part in filepath.parts):\n158:                 continue\n159: \n160:             if filepath.is_file() and filepath.suffix in extensions:\n161:                 try:\n162:                     mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(filepath.stat().st_mtime)\n163:                     if mtime > cutoff:\n164:                         rel_path = filepath.relative_to(project_root)\n165:                         modified.append(str(rel_path))\n166:                 except (OSError, ValueError):\n167:                     continue\n168: \n169:     return sorted(modified)\n170: \n171: \n172: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n173:     import sys\n174: \n175:     if len(sys.argv) > 1:\n176:         command = sys.argv[1]\n177: \n178:         if command == \"save\":\n179:             save_session()\n180:             print(\"[OK] Sesion guardada\")\n181:         elif command == \"check\":\n182:             if detect_stale_session():\n183:                 print(\"[WARN] Sesion antigua detectada (>2h)\")\n184:             else:\n185:                 print(\"[INFO] Sesion reciente o no hay sesion\")\n186:         elif command == \"recover\":\n187:             result = recover_session()\n188:             if not result:\n189:                 print(\"[INFO] No hay sesion previa para recuperar\")\n190:         else:\n191:             print(f\"Comando desconocido: {command}\")\n192:             print(\"Usa: save | check | recover\")\n193:     else:\n194:         recover_session()\n\n(End of file - total 194 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"Session Tracker Lite para proyectos pequenos (<30 archivos).\n\nVersion simplificada de Session Recovery optimizada para uso local\ncon proyectos pequenos. Any]:\n50:     \"\"\"Verifica criterios de completitud simplificados.\"\"\"\n51:     checks = {\n52:         \"tasks_completed\": _check_all_tasks_done(),\n53:         \"tests_passing\": _check_findings_exist(),\n57:     }\n58: \n59:     passed = sum(1 for value in checks.values() if value)\n60:     total = len(checks)\n61:     percentage = int((passed / total) * 100) if total > 0 else 0\n62:     can_complete = passed >= 4\n63: \n64:     missing = []\n65:     check_names = {\n66:         \"tasks_completed\": \"Tareas pendientes en work_plan.md\",\n67:         \"tests_passing\": \"findings_exist\": \"No existe findings.md (opcional pero recomendado)\",\n71:     }\n72: \n73:     for check_name, passed_check in checks.items():\n74:         if not passed_check:\n75:             missing.append(check_names.get(check_name, check_name))\n76: \n77:     return {\n78:         \"can_complete\": can_complete,\n79:         \"percentage\": percentage,\n80:         \"checks\": checks,\n81:         \"missing\": missing,\n82:         \"passed\": passed,\n83:         \"total\": total,\n84:     }\n85: \n86: \n87: def show_completion_report(result: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n88:     \"\"\"Muestra reporte de completitud de forma legible.\"\"\"\n89:     emoji = \"âœ…\" if result[\"can_complete\"] else \"âš ï¸\"\n90:     status = \"LISTO PARA COMPLETAR\" if print(f\"Progreso: {result['passed']}/{result['total']} ({result['percentage']}%)\")\n96: \n97:     if result[\"missing\"]:\n98:         print(\"\\nItems faltantes:\")\n99:         for item in result[\"missing\"]:\n100:             print(f\"  - {item}\")\n101:     else:\n102:         print(\"\\nâœ… Todos los criterios cumplidos\")\n103: \n104:     print(\"=\" * 60 + \"\\n\")\n105: \n106: \n107: def safe_print(text: str, end: str = \"\\n\") -> None:\n108:     try:\n109:         print(text, end=end)\n110:     except UnicodeEncodeError:\n111:         ascii_text = text.encode(\"ascii\", errors=\"replace\").decode(\"ascii\")\n112:         print(ascii_text, end=end)\n113: \n114: \n115: def _check_all_tasks_done() -> bool:\n116:     \"\"\"Verifica que todas las tareas esten marcadas {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779392075905,"sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_sJXEZR2dhPVcCLcAGU62kJ4N","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agents_config.py","offset":360,"limit":80},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agents_config.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n360:         apply=_migrate_1_0_to_1_1,\n361:     ),\n362:     # Future migrations appended chronologically here.\n363: ]\n364: \n365: \n366: def migrate_agents_config(\n367:     path: Path | None = None,\n368:     *,\n369:     dry_run: bool = False,\n370: ) -> MigrationReport:\n371:     \"\"\"\n372:     Apply pending migrations to agents.json idempotently.\n373: \n374:     Before: agents.json existe con schema_version y opcionalmente _migrations.\n375:     During:\n376:       1. Load JSON (raise si falta o malformed).\n377:       2. Read current schema_version + _migrations (default []).\n378:       3. For each Migration en MIGRATIONS en orden:\n379:          - Si migration.id ya en _migrations: skip (idempotent).\n380:          - Else: backup, apply handler, update _migrations, persist.\n381:       4. Legacy backfill: si _migrations falta pero schema_version es current,\n382:          poblar _migrations retroactivamente sin re-ejecutar handlers.\n383:     After: Retorna MigrationReport con applied, skipped, backups.\n384: \n385:     Args:\n386:         path: Path to agents.json. Default: CONFIG_PATH.\n387:         dry_run: If True, report what would happen without writing.\n388: \n389:     Returns:\n390:         MigrationReport with applied, skipped, and backups lists.\n391: \n392:     Raises:\n393:         FileNotFoundError: If agents.json does not exist.\n394:         json.JSONDecodeError: If agents.json is malformed.\n395:     \"\"\"\n396:     if path is None:\n397:         path = CONFIG_PATH\n398: \n399:     config = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n400:     current_migrations = list(config.get(\"_migrations\", []))\n401:     applied: list[str] = []\n402:     skipped: list[str] = []\n403:     backups: list[Path] = []\n404: \n405:     # Legacy backfill: schema_version already current but _migrations missing\n406:     # Only backfill if schema_version equals the latest known migration to_version\n407:     latest_version = MIGRATIONS[-1].to_version if MIGRATIONS else \"1.0\"\n408:     if \"_migrations\" not in config and config.get(\"schema_version\") == latest_version:\n409:         # Retroactively claim all migrations up to current version\n410:         retroactive = [m.id for m in MIGRATIONS]\n411:         current_migrations.extend(retroactive)\n412:         config[\"_migrations\"] = current_migrations\n413:         if not dry_run:\n414:             path.write_text(\n415:                 json.dumps(config, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n416:             )\n417:         return MigrationReport(applied=[], skipped=retroactive, backups=[])\n418: \n419:     for migration in MIGRATIONS:\n420:         if migration.id in current_migrations:\n421:             skipped.append(migration.id)\n422:             continue\n423:         if not dry_run:\n424:             ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime(\"%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ\")\n425:             backup_path = path.with_suffix(f\".json.bak.{ts}\")\n426:             shutil.copy2(path, backup_path)\n427:             backups.append(backup_path)\n428:             config = migration.apply(config)\n429:             current_migrations.append(migration.id)\n430:             config[\"_migrations\"] = current_migrations\n431:             path.write_text(\n432:                 json.dumps(config, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n433:             )\n434:         applied.append(migration.id)\n435: \n436:     return MigrationReport(applied=applied, skipped=skipped, backups=backups)\n437: \n438: \n439: # CLI interface for PowerShell consumption\n\n(Showing lines 360-439 of 538. Use offset=440 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"        apply=_migrate_1_0_to_1_1,\n    ),\n    # Future migrations appended chronologically here.\n]\n\n\ndef migrate_agents_config(\n    path: Path | None = None,\n    *,\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n) -> MigrationReport:\n    \"\"\"\n    Apply pending migrations to agents.json idempotently.\n\n    Before: agents.json existe con schema_version y opcionalmente _migrations.\n    During:\n      1. Load JSON (raise si falta o malformed).\n      2. Read current schema_version + _migrations (default []).\n      3. For each Migration en MIGRATIONS en orden:\n         - Si migration.id ya en _migrations: skip (idempotent).","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agents_config.py","time":{"start":1779392075894,"end":1779392075904}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_09f79af96522735d016a0f5e1abb9c8191b857b827999ec5ff"}},"id":"prt_e4c0857d3001S6Xv9tT2WxOMP5","sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","messageID":"msg_e4c084c08001xj9nBKyJo72hYH"}}

{"type":"step_start","timestamp":1779392082536,"sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","part":{"id":"prt_e4c087266001jNtr0ydco08sey","messageID":"msg_e4c086d7b001rRzmNVnLZTqEsw","sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","snapshot":"b91877c7eb46d8672d952afb6ddebee8ddec9804","type":"step-start"}}
{"type":"text","timestamp":1779392114381,"sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","part":{"id":"prt_e4c08dd6d0012YUg4kXsOfe8u6","messageID":"msg_e4c086d7b001rRzmNVnLZTqEsw","sessionID":"ses_1b3f9d52bffe6kOt4xf9EH9LCa","type":"text","text":"## SUMMARY\nWP-2026-122 is not approved because `--project-root` still does not reliably reach the runtime; several modules freeze the project root at import time, so an external workspace can be ignored.\n\n## BLOCKERS\n- `.agent/agent_controller.py:105-121,2429-2435` computes `PROJECT_ROOT/AGENT_DIR/COLLAB_DIR/...` before `--project-root` is parsed/exported, so the flag cannot affect the already-imported module. Move root parsing/export before any root-dependent imports or remove module-level root constants.\n- `.agent/completion_common.py:23-32`, `.agent/completion_checker.py:32-46`, `.agent/session_tracker.py:34-40`, `.agent/hooks/stop_hook.py:24-39`, `.agent/agents_config.py:29-30,396-397` still resolve workspace paths at import time. Refactor these call sites to resolve via `resolve_project_root()`/`get_*_dir()` inside functions or factories so late env injection is honored.\n- Add/adjust tests that exercise a fresh import/process with `--project-root` and confirm the default repo-root fallback still works.\n\n## SUGGESTIONS\n- none\n\nDECISION: CHANGES","time":{"start":1779392109933,"end":1779392114380},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"msg_0632bce03048358b016a0f5e3ce4a08191ac9d51e9e0eac964","phase":"final_answer"}}}}

#### Summary
{"type":"step_start","timestamp":1779392636845,"sessionID":"ses_1b3ef21faffemLSuTp0StaX3i5","part":{"id":"prt_e4c10e7ac001OAK5mZN2wHHC19","messageID":"msg_e4c10df130014wUWJusSnAFOeG","sessionID":"ses_1b3ef21faffemLSuTp0StaX3i5","snapshot":"d2754117ebc6836c27957ed615a05b061036a5d7","type":"step-start"}}
{"type":"text","timestamp":1779392682536,"sessionID":"ses_1b3ef21faffemLSuTp0StaX3i5","part":{"id":"prt_e4c1192e7001xKhwp7nSsquvlr","messageID":"msg_e4c10df130014wUWJusSnAFOeG","sessionID":"ses_1b3ef21faffemLSuTp0StaX3i5","type":"text","text":"## SUMMARY\nNo apruebo: el diff no demuestra el desacople completo de `project_root` y todavía conserva rutas de resolución legacy en paralelo.\n\n## BLOCKERS\n- `.agent/agents_config.py:549-579` mantiene un `ImportError` fallback a `Path(__file__)` y, por tanto, dos mecanismos de resolución simultáneos; eso contradice el contrato de “un único mecanismo” y puede ocultar fallos de importación.\n- `.agent/agent_controller.py:517-523` solo evidencia el parse/export de `--project-root` en el controller; no queda verificado en el diff que los demás entry points/hook paths del plan (`ticket_supervisor.py`, `manager_review_bridge.py`, `stop_hook.py`, launcher PS1) estén realmente adaptados o propaguen `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT`.\n\n## SUGGESTIONS\n- none\n\nDECISION: CHANGES","time":{"start":1779392680679,"end":1779392682534},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"msg_0d5a74d769ac5cc6016a0f6077a03481918458319eb188f9c6","phase":"final_answer"}}}}

Commands:
  opencode completion          generate shell completion script
  opencode acp                 start ACP (Agent Client Protocol) server
  opencode mcp                 manage MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers
  opencode [project]           start opencode tui                                          [default]
  opencode attach <url>        attach to a running opencode server
  opencode run [message..]     run opencode with a message
  opencode debug               debugging and troubleshooting tools
  opencode providers           manage AI providers and credentials                   [aliases: auth]
  opencode agent               manage agents
  opencode upgrade [target]    upgrade opencode to the latest or a specific version
  opencode uninstall           uninstall opencode and remove all related files
  opencode serve               starts a headless opencode server
  opencode web                 start opencode server and open web interface
  opencode models [provider]   list all available models
  opencode stats               show token usage and cost statistics
  opencode export [sessionID]  export session data as JSON
  opencode import <file>       import session data from JSON file or URL
  opencode github              manage GitHub agent
  opencode pr <number>         fetch and checkout a GitHub PR branch, then run opencode
  opencode session             manage sessions
  opencode plugin <module>     install plugin and update config                      [aliases: plug]
  opencode db                  database tools

Positionals:
  project  path to start opencode in                                                        [string]

Options:
  -h, --help         show help                                                             [boolean]
  -v, --version      show version number                                                   [boolean]
      --print-logs   print logs to stderr                                                  [boolean]
      --log-level    log level                  [string] [choices: "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR"]
      --pure         run without external plugins                                          [boolean]
      --port         port to listen on                                         [number] [default: 0]
      --hostname     hostname to listen on                           [string] [default: "127.0.0.1"]
      --mdns         enable mDNS service discovery (defaults hostname to 0.0.0.0)
                                                                          [boolean] [default: false]
      --mdns-domain  custom domain name for mDNS service (default: opencode.local)
                                                                [string] [default: "opencode.local"]
      --cors         additional domains to allow for CORS                      [array] [default: []]
  -m, --model        model to use in the format of provider/model                           [string]
  -c, --continue     continue the last session                                             [boolean]
  -s, --session      session id to continue                                                 [string]
      --fork         fork the session when continuing (use with --continue or --session)   [boolean]
      --prompt       prompt to use                                                          [string]
      --agent        agent to use                                                           [string]

Commands:
  opencode completion          generate shell completion script
  opencode acp                 start ACP (Agent Client Protocol) server
  opencode mcp                 manage MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers
  opencode [project]           start opencode tui                                          [default]
  opencode attach <url>        attach to a running opencode server
  opencode run [message..]     run opencode with a message
  opencode debug               debugging and troubleshooting tools
  opencode providers           manage AI providers and credentials                   [aliases: auth]
  opencode agent               manage agents
  opencode upgrade [target]    upgrade opencode to the latest or a specific version
  opencode uninstall           uninstall opencode and remove all related files
  opencode serve               starts a headless opencode server
  opencode web                 start opencode server and open web interface
  opencode models [provider]   list all available models
  opencode stats               show token usage and cost statistics
  opencode export [sessionID]  export session data as JSON
  opencode import <file>       import session data from JSON file or URL
  opencode github              manage GitHub agent
  opencode pr <number>         fetch and checkout a GitHub PR branch, then run opencode
  opencode session             manage sessions
  opencode plugin <module>     install plugin and update config                      [aliases: plug]
  opencode db                  database tools

Positionals:
  project  path to start opencode in                                                        [string]

Options:
  -h, --help         show help                                                             [boolean]
  -v, --version      show version number                                                   [boolean]
      --print-logs   print logs to stderr                                                  [boolean]
      --log-level    log level                  [string] [choices: "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR"]
      --pure         run without external plugins                                          [boolean]
      --port         port to listen on                                         [number] [default: 0]
      --hostname     hostname to listen on                           [string] [default: "127.0.0.1"]
      --mdns         enable mDNS service discovery (defaults hostname to 0.0.0.0)
                                                                          [boolean] [default: false]
      --mdns-domain  custom domain name for mDNS service (default: opencode.local)
                                                                [string] [default: "opencode.local"]
      --cors         additional domains to allow for CORS                      [array] [default: []]
  -m, --model        model to use in the format of provider/model                           [string]
  -c, --continue     continue the last session                                             [boolean]
  -s, --session      session id to continue                                                 [string]
      --fork         fork the session when continuing (use with --continue or --session)   [boolean]
      --prompt       prompt to use                                                          [string]
      --agent        agent to use                                                           [string]

Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: --- work_plan.md ---\n4: # Work Plan - WP-2026-131\n5: \n6: ## Metadata\n7: - **ID:** WP-2026-131\n8: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n9: - **deliverable_type:** code\n10: - **Titulo:** Memory index cap for persistent observations\n11: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n12: \n13: ## Objetivo\n14: Convertir `MEMORY.md` en un indice humano corto y estable, dejando `observations.jsonl` como la fuente historica para busqueda profunda y trazabilidad.\n15: \n16: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n17: - `MEMORY.md` debe mantenerse acotado y util como indice, no como volcado historico.\n18: - La historia completa y la busqueda profunda pertenecen a `observations.jsonl`.\n19: - `scripts/memory_consolidate.py` es la unica pieza que materializa y recorta el indice.\n20: - `AGENTS.md` debe documentar la regla de memoria acotada de forma breve y operativa.\n21: - `scripts/memory_consolidate.py` debe declarar `MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP = 80` y truncar el indice con un marcador visible cuando se supere el limite.\n22: \n23: ## Files Likely Touched\n24: - `scripts/memory_consolidate.py`\n25: - `tests/unit/test_memory_consolidate.py`\n26: - `.agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md`\n27: - `AGENTS.md`\n28: - `.agent/collaboration/PLAN_WP-2026-131.md`\n29: - `.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-131.md`\n30: - `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`\n31: - `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`\n32: - `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`\n33: \n34: ## Fases\n35: 1. Definir el cap de lineas en `MEMORY.md` y la politica de recorte en `scripts/memory_consolidate.py`.\n36: 2. Ajustar la regeneracion del indice para priorizar resumen util sobre historial largo y truncar con marcador visible cuando sea necesario.\n37: 3. Añadir un test explicito que fuerce un output mayor a 80 lineas y verifique que el indice final queda acotado.\n38: 4. Actualizar `AGENTS.md` en la seccion `Memoria por proyecto` con la regla de memoria acotada y la ubicacion de la historia completa.\n39: 5. Validar que el indice no supera 80 lineas y que la historia sigue disponible en `observations.jsonl`.\n40: \n41: ## Calidad\n42: - `python scripts/memory_consolidate.py --dry-run`\n43: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_memory_consolidate.py -q`\n44: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n45: \n46: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n47: - `MEMORY.md` no supera 80 lineas.\n48: - `MEMORY.md` sigue siendo un indice humano util.\n49: - La trazabilidad historica queda en `observations.jsonl`.\n50: - `AGENTS.md` documenta la regla sin ambiguedades.\n51: - Los tests de memoria siguen pasando y cubren la regeneracion del indice.\n52: \n53: ## Nota\n54: Este ticket es intencionalmente pequeno y autocontenido. Si durante la implementacion aparece la necesidad de tocar el bus o el state machine, se considera deriva de alcance y debe escalarse aparte.\n55: \n56: \n57: --- STATE.md ---\n58: # State - WP-2026-131\n59: \n60: Plan Activo: WP-2026-131\n61: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n62: Rol activo: BUILDER\n63: \n64: Resumen:\n65: WP-2026-131 limita `MEMORY.md` para que siga siendo un indice humano corto y estable.\n66: \n67: Notas:\n68: - `observations.jsonl` sigue siendo la fuente historica para trazabilidad profunda.\n69: - El alcance es minimo: memoria, documentacion operativa y validacion del indice.\n70: - Si aparece deriva hacia bus o state machine, el ticket debe escalarse aparte.\n71: \n72: \n73: --- TURN.md ---\n74: # TURNO ACTUAL\n75: \n76: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 11:39:01\n77: \n78: ---\n79: \n80: ## Agente Activo\n81: \n82: | Campo | Valor |\n83: |-------|-------|\n84: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n85: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-131 |\n86: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n87: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n88: \n89: ---\n90: \n91: ## Instruccion\n92: \n93: > Builder completo WP-2026-131. Revisa el trabajo.\n94: \n95: ---\n96: \n97: ## Archivos a Leer\n98: \n99: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n100: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n101: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n102: \n103: ---\n104: \n105: ## Estado del Sistema\n106: \n107: | Archivo | Estado |\n108: |---------|--------|\n109: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n110: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n111: \n112: ---\n113: \n114: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n115: \n116: \n117: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n118: [execution_log section for WP-2026-131 not found]\n119: \n120: --- PLAN_WP-2026-131.md ---\n121: # PLAN_WP-2026-131 - Memory index cap for persistent observations\n122: \n123: ## Objetivo\n124: Limitar el crecimiento de `MEMORY.md` para que siga siendo un indice humano corto y estable, dejando `observations.jsonl` como la fuente historica de referencia.\n125: \n126: ## Alcance\n127: - `scripts/memory_consolidate.py`\n128: - `tests/unit/test_memory_consolidate.py`\n129: - `.agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md`\n130: - `AGENTS.md`\n131: - `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`\n132: - `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`\n133: \n134: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n135: - `MEMORY.md` debe tener un tope explicito de 80 lineas.\n136: - `observations.jsonl` conserva la historia completa y la busqueda profunda.\n137: - `memory_consolidate.py` es la unica pieza que regenera el indice.\n138: - `AGENTS.md` debe explicitar la regla para evitar drift documental.\n139: - `memory_consolidate.py` debe exponer `MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP = 80` y truncar el indice con un marcador visible si excede el limite.\n140: \n141: ## Fases\n142: 1. Introducir el cap de lineas para `MEMORY.md` en la regeneracion.\n143: 2. Ajustar el formato del indice para conservar solo senales de alto valor y truncar de forma honesta con marcador visible.\n144: 3. Añadir un test de cap que genere un indice demasiado largo y valide que el output final no supera 80 lineas.\n145: 4. Documentar la politica de memoria acotada en la seccion `Memoria por proyecto` de `AGENTS.md`.\n146: 5. Validar con tests y con `--validate` que no hay drift.\n147: \n148: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n149: - `MEMORY.md` queda por debajo de 80 lineas.\n150: - La historia completa sigue en `observations.jsonl`.\n151: - El indice sigue siendo util para una sesion nueva.\n152: - Las gates del ticket pasan sin tocar el bus ni el state machine.\n153: \n154: \n155: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-131.md ---\n156: # AUDIT_WP-2026-131 - Memory index cap for persistent observations\n157: \n158: ## Verificacion inicial\n159: La memoria persistente actual funciona, pero `MEMORY.md` sigue siendo un indice potencialmente abierto a crecimiento sin limite explicito. La historia detallada ya vive en `observations.jsonl`, asi que el cambio es de disciplina documental, no de arquitectura.\n160: \n161: ## Hallazgos\n162: - `scripts/memory_consolidate.py` ya regenera `MEMORY.md` desde `observations.jsonl`.\n163: - `AGENTS.md` ya documenta que `MEMORY.md` es un indice humano, pero no fija un cap cuantitativo.\n164: - `tests/unit/test_memory_consolidate.py` ya cubre la regeneracion basica del indice, por lo que el cambio puede validarse con cobertura existente y un ajuste puntual si hace falta.\n165: - `observations.jsonl` sigue siendo la fuente historica apropiada para busqueda profunda.\n166: - El mecanismo mas simple y auditable es `MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP = 80` con truncado al final y marcador visible cuando el indice exceda el limite.\n167: \n168: ## Riesgo observado\n169: Si se intenta ampliar el alcance hacia memoria semantica o busqueda historica automatica, el ticket deja de ser minimo y pasa a tocar mas capas de las necesarias.\n170: \n171: ## Verificacion esperada\n172: - `MEMORY.md` queda limitado a 80 lineas.\n173: - El indice conserva utilidades de alto valor y no historiales largos.\n174: - `AGENTS.md` explica la regla sin ambiguedad en la seccion `Memoria por proyecto`.\n175: -
