{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779706423838,"sessionID":"ses_1a152399dffeQZcBHcyd54oaj3","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_5e4679ffd1d64d898cd6e392","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\session-close-observations\\references\\schema.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\session-close-observations\\references\\schema.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Schema de Observaciones - Session Close\n2: \n3: ## Schema Base\n4: \n5: ```json\n6: {\n7:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n8:   \"signal\": \"Descripcion clara y concisa del hecho observado\",\n9:   \"category\": \"convention\",\n10:   \"source_ticket\": \"WP-2026-132\",\n11:   \"topic\": \"memory-management\",\n12:   \"source\": \"session-close\"\n13: }\n14: ```\n15: \n16: ## Campos\n17: \n18: ### timestamp (requerido)\n19: - **Tipo**: ISO 8601 string con timezone UTC\n20: - **Formato**: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.ffffffZ`\n21: - **Ejemplo**: `2026-05-25T12:00:00.000000Z`\n22: - **Nota**: Usar `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()`\n23: \n24: ### signal (requerido)\n25: - **Tipo**: string\n26: - **Longitud minima**: 30 caracteres\n27: - **Contenido**: Descripcion objetiva y verificable\n28: - **Evitar**: Opiniones, traces de herramientas, contexto efimero\n29: \n30: ### category (requerido)\n31: - **Tipo**: enum\n32: - **Valores validos**: `convention`, `decision`, `fact`, `pattern`\n33: - **Invalido**: cualquier otro valor\n34: \n35: ### source_ticket (requerido)\n36: - **Tipo**: string\n37: - **Formato**: `WP-YYYY-NNN` o referencia equivalente\n38: - **Ejemplo**: `WP-2026-132`, `MAN-2026-045`\n39: \n40: ### topic (requerido)\n41: - **Tipo**: string\n42: - **Formato**: kebab-case recomendado\n43: - **Ejemplos**: `memory-management`, `session-bootstrap`, `quality-gates`\n44: \n45: ### source (requerido)\n46: - **Tipo**: string\n47: - **Valores tipicos**: `session-close`, `builder`, `manager`, `supervisor`\n48: - **Proposito**: Trazabilidad del origen\n49: \n50: ## Ejemplos Validos\n51: \n52: ```json\n53: {\n54:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00.000000Z\",\n55:   \"signal\": \"Session-close observations skill creada con schema de 6 campos\",\n56:   \"category\": \"fact\",\n57:   \"source_ticket\": \"WP-2026-132\",\n58:   \"topic\": \"skill-creation\",\n59:   \"source\": \"session-close\"\n60: }\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ```json\n64: {\n65:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:05:00.000000Z\",\n66:   \"signal\": \"Filtros de curacion: hecho, persistencia entre sesiones, utilidad repetida\",\n67:   \"category\": \"convention\",\n68:   \"source_ticket\": \"WP-2026-132\",\n69:   \"topic\": \"memory-filters\",\n70:   \"source\": \"session-close\"\n71: }\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ## Ejemplos Invalidos\n75: \n76: ```json\n77: {\n78:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n79:   \"signal\": \"Tool edit called\",\n80:   \"category\": \"fact\"\n81: }\n82: ```\n83: **Invalido**: signal es trace de herramienta, < 30 chars\n84: \n85: ```json\n86: {\n87:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n88:   \"signal\": \"Deberiamos mejorar la memoria\",\n89:   \"category\": \"opinion\"\n90: }\n91: ```\n92: **Invalido**: categoria no valida, signal es opinion\n93: \n94: ```json\n95: {\n96:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n97:   \"signal\": \"Falta el campo category\"\n98: }\n99: ```\n100: **Invalido**: campo requerido ausente\n\n(End of file - total 100 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Schema de Observaciones - Session Close\n\n## Schema Base\n\n```json\n{\n  \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n  \"signal\": \"Descripcion clara y concisa del hecho observado\",\n  \"category\": \"convention\",\n  \"source_ticket\": \"WP-2026-132\",\n  \"topic\": \"memory-management\",\n  \"source\": \"session-close\"\n}\n```\n\n## Campos\n\n### timestamp (requerido)\n- **Tipo**: ISO 8601 string con timezone UTC\n- **Formato**: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.ffffffZ`","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"skills\\session-close-observations\\references\\schema.md","time":{"start":1779706423646,"end":1779706423835}},"id":"prt_e5eaeabec001ZXffrvl92dJ7rE","sessionID":"ses_1a152399dffeQZcBHcyd54oaj3","messageID":"msg_e5eae4244001549uqb2sFWks2i"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779706423861,"sessionID":"ses_1a152399dffeQZcBHcyd54oaj3","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_f2680b72fbb74ed2884c6d61","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\session-close-observations\\references\\filter-rules.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\session-close-observations\\references\\filter-rules.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Reglas de Filtrado - Session Close Observations\n2: \n3: ## Tres Reglas de Curacion\n4: \n5: Toda observacion candidata debe pasar estas tres reglas:\n6: \n7: ### 1. Es un hecho\n8: \n9: La observacion debe ser:\n10: - **Objetiva**: Verificable por terceros\n11: - **Concreta**: Describe algo que ocurrio o se decidio\n12: - **Atemporal**: No depende del estado emocional del autor\n13: \n14: **Ejemplos que PASAN**:\n15: - \"Session-close observations skill creada con schema de 6 campos\"\n16: - \"Filtros de curacion: hecho, persistencia, utilidad repetida\"\n17: - \"WP-2026-132 conecta skill en project-finalize paso 9c-9d\"\n18: \n19: **Ejemplos que FALLAN**:\n20: - \"Me parece que la memoria deberia ser mas eficiente\" (opinion)\n21: - \"Tool edit called\" (trace de herramienta)\n22: - \"Sesion productiva\" (subjetivo)\n23: \n24: ### 2. Sobrevive a otra sesion\n25: \n26: La observacion debe ser util dentro de 1+ semanas:\n27: \n28: **Preguntas de validacion**:\n29: - ¿Esta observacion ayudara en el proximo WP?\n30: - ¿Documenta algo que de otra forma se olvidaria?\n31: - ¿Es referencia para decisiones futuras?\n32: \n33: **Ejemplos que PASAN**:\n34: - Decisiones arquitectonicas\n35: - Convenciones de codigo establecidas\n36: - Patrones de implementacion reutilizables\n37: - Bugs fijos con causa raiz documentada\n38: \n39: **Ejemplos que FALLAN**:\n40: - \"Reunion a las 3pm hoy\" (temporal)\n41: - \"Archivo X modificado\" (sin contexto)\n42: - \"Error temporal en build\" (si ya se resolvio sin aprendizaje)\n43: \n44: ### 3. Evita trabajo repetido\n45: \n46: La observacion no debe duplicar conocimiento existente:\n47: \n48: **Check de duplicados**:\n49: - Comparar signal contra observaciones previas\n50: - Detectar equivalencia semantica, no solo exacta\n51: - Ventana de 24h para duplicados temporales\n52: \n53: **Ejemplos que PASAN**:\n54: - Nueva convencion no documentada antes\n55: - Decision que cambia un patron anterior\n56: - Hecho tecnico sin registro previo\n57: \n58: **Ejemplos que FALLAN**:\n59: - Duplicado exacto de observacion existente\n60: - Mismo hecho con redaccion ligeramente distinta\n61: - Re-iteracion de decision ya consolidada\n62: \n63: ## Filtros Automaticos\n64: \n65: ### Por longitud\n66: - **Regla**: signal < 30 caracteres → descartar\n67: - **Razon**: Entradas muy cortas suelen ser ruido\n68: \n69: ### Por patron\n70: - **Regla**: signal empieza con \"Tool \" y termina con \" called\" → descartar\n71: - **Razon**: Son traces de herramientas, no observaciones\n72: \n73: ### Por categoria\n74: - **Regla**: category no esta en [`convention`, `decision`, `fact`, `pattern`] → descartar\n75: - **Razon**: Categoria invalida indica schema incorrecto\n76: \n77: ### Por campos requeridos\n78: - **Regla**: Falta timestamp, signal, category, source_ticket, topic o source → descartar\n79: - **Razon**: Schema incompleto rompe trazabilidad\n80: \n81: ## Criterios de Promocion\n82: \n83: Una observacion es promovida a `observations.jsonl` cuando:\n84: \n85: 1. ✅ Schema valido (6 campos, tipos correctos)\n86: 2. ✅ Pasa los 3 filtros de curacion\n87: 3. ✅ No es duplicado de observacion existente\n88: 4. ✅ Signal >= 30 caracteres\n89: 5. ✅ Categoria valida\n90: \n91: ## Implementacion\n92: \n93: Ver `scripts/session_close_observations.py` para la implementacion de filtros.\n\n(End of file - total 93 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Reglas de Filtrado - Session Close Observations\n\n## Tres Reglas de Curacion\n\nToda observacion candidata debe pasar estas tres reglas:\n\n### 1. Es un hecho\n\nLa observacion debe ser:\n- **Objetiva**: Verificable por terceros\n- **Concreta**: Describe algo que ocurrio o se decidio\n- **Atemporal**: No depende del estado emocional del autor\n\n**Ejemplos que PASAN**:\n- \"Session-close observations skill creada con schema de 6 campos\"\n- \"Filtros de curacion: hecho, persistencia, utilidad repetida\"\n- \"WP-2026-132 conecta skill en project-finalize paso 9c-9d\"\n\n**Ejemplos que FALLAN**:\n- \"Me parece que la memoria deberia ser mas eficiente\" (opinion)","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"skills\\session-close-observations\\references\\filter-rules.md","time":{"start":1779706423660,"end":1779706423855}},"id":"prt_e5eaeaf94001sDzrBC454z3Okk","sessionID":"ses_1a152399dffeQZcBHcyd54oaj3","messageID":"msg_e5eae4244001549uqb2sFWks2i"}}

# Catalogo de Micro-Skills\n2: \n3: > **Sistema Multi-Agente v5** - Skills portables para Manager y Builder\n4: \n5: ## Resumen\n6: \n7: | # | Skill | Descripcion | Usuario | Tags |\n8: |---|-------|-------------|---------|------|\n9: | 1 | `man-review-implementation` | Revisar trabajo del Builder | Manager | `manager`, `review`, `quality` |\n10: | 2 | `man-create-work-plan` | Crear planes de trabajo | Manager | `manager`, `planning`, `architecture` |\n11: | 3 | `man-resolve-escalation` | Resolver bloqueos del Builder | Manager | `manager`, `escalation`, `decision` |\n12: | 4 | `bui-implement-from-plan` | Ejecutar un plan aprobado | Builder | `builder`, `implementation`, `coding` |\n13: | 5 | `bui-run-quality-gates` | Validar codigo con ruff y pytest | Builder | `builder`, `testing`, `quality` |\n14: | 6 | `bui-self-audit` | Auto-auditoria obligatoria antes de review | Builder | `builder`, `audit`, `quality` |\n15: | 7 | `graphify` | Construir un grafo persistente del codebase | Builder | `graphify`, `tokens`, `knowledge-graph` |\n16: | 8 | `project-finalize` | Cierre profesional: auditoria, limpieza y docs | Ambos | `closeout`, `cleanup`, `documentation` |\n17: | 9 | `version-changelog` | Gestion semantica de versiones y changelog | Ambos | `versioning`, `semver`, `changelog` |\n18: | 10 | `secure-existing-project` | Aplicar seguridad a proyecto existente | Ambos | `security`, `architecture`, `migration` |\n19: | 11 | `scaffold-python-project` | Crear estructura de proyecto Python nuevo | Ambos | `python`, `project`, `scaffold` |\n20: | 12 | `setup-agent-system` | Instalar el sistema multi-agente | Usuario | `setup`, `multi-agent`, `configuration` |\n21: | 13 | `create-agent-skill` | Crear nuevas micro-skills | Ambos | `meta`, `skill-creation`, `template` |\n22: | 14 | `code-audit` | AuditorÃ­a sistemÃ¡tica: dead code, deuda tÃ©cnica, abandono | Builder | `audit`, `quality`, `python`, `builder` |\n23: | 15 | `session-close-observations` | Generar observaciones curadas al cerrar sesion | Ambos | `core`, `system`, `memory` |\n24: \n25: ## Uso por Rol\n26: \n27: ### Manager\n28: - `man-review-implementation` - Revisar codigo\n29: - `man-create-work-plan` - Planificar\n30: - `man-resolve-escalation` - Resolver bloqueos\n31: \n32: ### Builder\n33: - `bui-implement-from-plan` - Implementar\n34: - `bui-run-quality-gates` - Validar calidad\n35: - `bui-self-audit` - Auto-auditar antes de review\n36: - `code-audit` - Auditar dead code, deuda tÃ©cnica, archivos inactivos\n37: - `graphify` - Explorar corpus grandes con menos tokens\n38: \n39: ### Compartidas\n40: - `project-finalize` - Cierre profesional\n41: - `version-changelog` - Versionado y changelog\n42: - `secure-existing-project` - Seguridad\n43: - `scaffold-python-project` - Crear proyectos\n44: - `create-agent-skill` - Crear skills\n45: - `session-close-observations` - Generar observaciones curadas al cerrar sesion\n46: \n47: ### Usuario / Setup\n48: - `setup-agent-system` - Instalacion inicial\n49: \n50: ## Estructura\n51: \n52: Cada skill contiene:\n53: ```\n54: skill-name/\n55: |-- SKILL.md              # Instrucciones principales\n56: `-- references/           # Documentacion de apoyo\n57:     |-- ref1.md\n58:     `-- ref2.md\n59: ```\n60: \n61: ## Validacion\n62: \n63: ```bash\n64: python skills/validate_all.py\n65: ```\n66: \n67: Verifica:\n68: - Frontmatter YAML valido\n69: - Campos requeridos presentes\n70: - Estructura correcta\n71: \n72: ## Convenciones\n73: \n74: **Nombres:**\n75: - `man-[accion]` - Skills del Manager\n76: - `bui-[accion]` - Skills del Builder\n77: - `[accion]` - Skills compartidas\n78: \n79: **Limites:**\n80: - SKILL.md: maximo 250 lineas\n81: - References: maximo 80 lineas cada una\n82: \n83: ## Referencias\n84: \n85: - [Sistema Multi-Agente](../EMPEZAR-AQUI.md)\n86: - [Flujo del Manager](../.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md)\n87: - [Flujo del Builder](../.agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md)\n\n(End of file - total 87 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Catalogo invocar solo en cierres de sesión largos o cuando `observations.jsonl` haya crecido significativamente\n190: - **Origen externo**: Oportunidad #4 de `garrytan/gbrain` (dream cycle pattern) - ver `CREDITS.md`\n191: - **Pre-requisito**: Haber ejecutado Paso 9d (`session_close_observations.py`) para generar observaciones de cierre\n192: \n193: ### Paso 9f: Builder verifica housekeeping del histórico (sugerencia)\n194: \n195: - Si `execution_log.md` supera ~10 entradas WP completadas, considerar ejecutar:\n196:   - `python scripts/archive_execution_log.py --dry-run` para previsualizar\n197:   - `python scripts/archive_execution_log.py --apply` para archivar tickets cerrados\n198: - Este paso es **solo una sugerencia**; el archivado requiere decisión humana\n199: - El guard `check_no_history_truncation` protege contra truncados peligrosos sin archive\n200: - **Origen externo**: integración interna basada en necesidad detectada tras WP-2026-097\n201: \n202: ### Paso 10: Manager cierra\n203: \n204: El Manager relee archivos cambiados, valida que el cierre atómico se ejecutó correctamente, comprueba\n205: coherencia entre docs, version y estado sincronizado, y marca el plan `COMPLETED`.\n206: \n207: Si el proyecto termina o cambia de manos, dejar explicito:\n208: - estado de mantenimiento\n209: - backlog pendiente\n210: - decision de handoff o archivado\n211: \n212: **Regla de cierre repetible:** El cierre solo se considera completo tras la ejecución del protocolo atómico por Builder y la validación por Manager. Esto asegura releases repetibles y reduce drift post-review.\n213: \n214: ## Output Format\n215: \n216: 1. `work_plan.md` actualizado y completado\n217: 2. `execution_log.md` con hallazgos, acciones y evidencia\n218: 3. `closeout_report.md` opcional como resumen permanente del cierre\n219: 4. Documentacion y metadatos sincronizados\n220: 5. `notifications.md` con handoffs o aprobaciones si aplica\n221: \n222: ## References\n223: \n224: - `references/closeout-modes.md` - Modos de cierre y cuando usarlos\n225: - `references/closeout-plan-template.md` - Esqueleto del plan de cierre\n226: - `references/closeout-checklist.md` - Checklist minimo, recomendado y avanzado\n227: - `../version-changelog/SKILL.md` - Versionado y changelog\n228: - `../bui-self-audit/SKILL.md` - Auditoria obligatoria del Builder\n229: - `../bui-run-quality-gates/SKILL.md` - Quality gates finales\n230: - `../man-review-implementation/SKILL.md` - Revision final del Manager\n231: - `../session-close-observations/SKILL.md` - Generar observaciones de cierre\n232: - `../memory-consolidate/SKILL.md` - Consolidacion de memoria\n233: \n234: ## Constraints\n235: \n236: - **NO** usar `work_plan_cierre.md` como plan activo sin actualizar antes el tooling\n237: - **NO** borrar archivos o mover estructura por intuicion\n238: - **NO** cerrar sin documentar que queda fuera de alcance y que va a backlog\n239: - **SIEMPRE** dejar explicito el estado de mantenimiento al cerrar\n240: - Revisión WP-2026-132\n\nHe revisado exhaustivamente la implementación contra el work_plan.md y los criterios de aceptación.\n\n### Verificación de Criterios de Aceptación\n\n| Criterio | Estado | Evidencia |\n|----------|--------|-----------|\n| Skill `session-close-observations` existe con frontmatter válido | ✅ | `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md` con YAML (name, version, description, author, tags) |\n| Helper script y tests validan schema y filtros | ✅ | `scripts/session_close_observations.py` + 16 tests en `test_session_close_observations.py` |\n| `skills/README.md` indexa la nueva skill | ✅ | Fila 15 en tabla + sección \"Compartidas\" línea 45 |\n| `project-finalize` referencia skill entre pasos 9c y 9d | ✅ | Nuevo Paso 9d insertado, consolidación movida a 9e |\n| Validación del sistema pasa sin errores | ✅ | `skills/validate_all.py` y `agent_controller --validate` reportados como exitosos |\n\n### Verificación de Decisiones Arquitectónicas\n\n- ✅ Skill compartida sin prefijo de rol\n- ✅ Schema de 6 campos: `timestamp`, `signal`, `category`, `source_ticket`, `topic`, `source`\n- ✅ Categorías válidas: `convention`, `decision`, `fact`, `pattern`\n- ✅ Sin campo `stable: bool`\n- ✅ Tres reglas de filtrado documentadas: es un hecho, sobrevive a otra sesión, evita trabajo repetido\n- ✅ Consolidación de memoria permanece en `memory_consolidate.py`\n\n### Verificación de Tests (cobertura de criterios del audit)\n\n- ✅ Categoría inválida rechazada (`test_validate_schema_invalid_category`)\n- ✅ Campo requerido ausente (`test_validate_schema_missing_field`)\n- ✅ Opinion/trace efímera filtrada (`test_is_noise_tool_called`, `test_is_noise_short_entry`)\n- ✅ Duplicado determinista (`test_is_duplicate_exact_match`, `test_is_duplicate_outside_window`)\n\n### Quality Gates Reportados\n\n- ✅ `python skills/validate_all.py` → VALID\n- ✅ `pytest` → 16 passed in 0.07s\n- ✅ `ruff check` → All checks passed\n- ✅ `ruff format` → 1 file reformatted\n- ✅ `agent_controller --validate` → Sin errores\n\n### Files Likely Touched\n\nTodos los archivos modificados/creados coinciden con la whitelist del work_plan.md. El mensaje \"Scope override\" en execution_log.md es informativo (directorio verificado), no un error.\n\n---\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Quality gates passed. Implementation matches scope and quality gates.\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779706464042,"end":1779706475097}}}

Este ticket ancla el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añade provenance del ultimo commit y mantiene el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n15: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n16: - Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n17: - `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.\n18: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` del prompt declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory y no sustituye el juicio del Manager.\n19: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n20: - No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n21: \n22: ## Files Likely Touched\n23: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n24: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n25: \n26: ## Fases\n27: 1. Cambiar el origen del diff a `origin/main...HEAD` en `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()`, con fallback a `git diff HEAD` si el remote base no es alcanzable.\n28: 2. Implementar `_git_provenance()` y añadir la seccion correspondiente al prompt del Manager.\n29: 3. Actualizar el bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` para dejar claro que la revision es advisory.\n30: 4. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n31: \n32: ## Calidad\n33: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n34: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n35: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n36: \n37: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n38: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n39: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n40: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n41: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n42: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n43: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n44: \n45: ## Nota\n46: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: --- work_plan.md ---\n4: # Work Plan - WP-2026-134\n5: \n6: ## Metadata\n7: - **ID:** WP-2026-134\n8: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n9: - **deliverable_type:** code\n10: - **Titulo:** Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n11: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n12: \n13: ## Objetivo\n14: El Manager no debe revisar el working tree vacio tras el commit del Builder. Este ticket ancla el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añade provenance del ultimo commit y mantiene el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n15: \n16: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n17: - `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n18: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n19: - Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n20: - `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.\n21: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` del prompt declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory y no sustituye el juicio del Manager.\n22: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n23: - No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n24: \n25: ## Files Likely Touched\n26: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n27: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n28: \n29: ## Fases\n30: 1. Cambiar el origen del diff a `origin/main...HEAD` en `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()`, con fallback a `git diff HEAD` si el remote base no es alcanzable.\n31: 2. Implementar `_git_provenance()` y añadir la seccion correspondiente al prompt del Manager.\n32: 3. Actualizar el bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` para dejar claro que la revision es advisory.\n33: 4. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n34: \n35: ## Calidad\n36: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n37: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n38: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n39: \n40: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n41: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n42: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n43: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n44: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n45: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n46: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n47: \n48: ## Nota\n49: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. El schema estructurado y el panel multi-reviewer quedan para un ticket posterior si hace falta.\n50: \n51: \n52: --- STATE.md ---\n53: # State - WP-2026-134\n54: \n55: Plan Activo: WP-2026-134\n56: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n57: Rol activo: BUILDER\n58: \n59: Resumen:\n60: WP-2026-134 ancla el review del Manager al diff real del ticket y añade provenance para evitar reviews vacios tras el commit del Builder.\n61: \n62: Notas:\n63: - `origin/main` es la base preferida, con fallback seguro si no esta disponible.\n64: - El contrato de decision sigue siendo `APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT`.\n65: - Si aparece deriva hacia schema estructurado o multi-reviewer, el ticket debe escalarse aparte.\n66: \n67: \n68: --- TURN.md ---\n69: # TURNO ACTUAL\n70: \n71: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 13:48:04\n72: \n73: ---\n74: \n75: ## Agente Activo\n76: \n77: | Campo | Valor |\n78: |-------|-------|\n79: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n80: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-134 |\n81: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n82: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n83: \n84: ---\n85: \n86: ## Instruccion\n87: \n88: > Builder completo WP-2026-134. Anclar el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añadir provenance del ultimo commit y mantener el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n120: \n121: ## Alcance\n122: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n123: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n124: \n125: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n126: - `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n127: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe, el bridge degrada a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning visible en el prompt.\n128: - Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n129: - `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff.\n130: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory.\n131: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n132: - No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n133: \n134: ## Fases\n135: 1. Cambiar el origen del diff a `origin/main...HEAD` en `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()`, con fallback a `git diff HEAD` si el remote base no es alcanzable.\n136: 2. Implementar `_git_provenance()` y añadir la seccion correspondiente al prompt del Manager.\n137: 3. Actualizar el bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` para dejar claro que la revision es advisory.\n138: 4. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n139: \n140: ## Files Likely Touched\n141: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n142: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n143: \n144: ## Calidad\n145: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n146: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n147: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n148: \n149: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n150: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n151: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n152: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n153: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n154: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n155: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n156: \n157: ### Casos de test esperados\n158: - `test_build_review_prompt_uses_branch_base_diff`\n159: - `test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote`\n160: - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_provenance_section`\n161: - Los tests existentes de composicion del prompt siguen pasando:\n162:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_generated_artifacts_block`\n163:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_allowed_skills_for_role`\n164: \n165: ## Nota\n166: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. El schema estructurado y el panel multi-reviewer quedan para un ticket posterior si hace falta.\n167: \n168: \n169: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-134.md ---\n170: # AUDIT_WP-2026-134 - Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n171: \n172: ## Verificacion inicial\n173: El review bridge ya empaqueta contexto canonical, pero hoy su diff sale de `git diff HEAD`, que puede quedar vacio una vez el Builder commitea. El gap real es de anclaje de diff y trazabilidad, no de state machine.\n174: \n175: ## Hallazgos\n176: - `bus/review_bridge.py` construye el diff con `git diff HEAD` y el diff-stat con el mismo anclaje al working tree.\n177: - El prompt del Manager ya contiene un bloque de instrucciones, pero no declara de forma explicita que la review es advisory.\n178: - No existe una seccion compacta de provenance del commit en el prompt actual.\n179: - El ticket necesita cubrir el caso en que `origin/main` no sea alcanzable o no exista en el entorno, degradando a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n180: - Los tests del bridge tendran que validar el nuevo origen del diff, el fallback y la presencia de provenance sin cambiar `DECISION`.\n181: \n182: ## Riesgo observado\n183: Si el ticket intenta meter schema JSON estructurado o panel multi-reviewer, el alcance se amplifica y se aleja del objetivo principal: revisar el diff real del ticket con trazabilidad.\n184: \n185: ## Verificacion esperada\n186: - El Manager recibe un diff anclado a `origin/main...HEAD` cuando el remote base esta disponible.\n187: - Si `origin/main` no es accesible, el prompt muestra un warning y degrada a `git diff HEAD`.\n188: - El prompt incorpora provenance del ultimo commit del ticket.\n189: - La instruccion advisory queda explicita.\n190: - La salida canonica del review sigue siendo `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT`.\n191: \n192: ## Casos de test esperados\n193: - `test_build_review_prompt_uses_branch_base_diff`\n194: - `test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote`\n195: - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_provenance_section`\n196: - Mantener verdes:\n197:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_generated_artifacts_block`\n198:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_allowed_skills_for_role`\n199: \n200: \n201: --- git provenance ---\n202: [WARNING: origin/main not reachable] bd4034c6ccad23c5af2dfd60bcc0a459f48f6c15 2026-05-25 13:16:53 +0200 fdl\n203: \n204: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n205: Available skills for this role:\n206:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n207:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n208:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n209: \n210: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n211: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n212: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n213: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n214: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n215: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n216: - .session_state.json\n217: \n218: \n219: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n220: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n221: \n222: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n223: \n224: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n225: DECISION: APPROVE\n226: \n227: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n228: \n229: ## SUMMARY\n230: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n231: \n232: ## BLOCKERS\n233: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n234: \n235: ## SUGGESTIONS\n236: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n237: \n238: DECISION: CHANGES\n239: \n240: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n241: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 241 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-134. transition_ticket(self, ticket_id: str, new_state: str, reason: str, source_event_id: str | None = None) -> None:\n20:         self.transitions.append((ticket_id, new_state, reason))\n21: \n22: \n23: def _make_bridge(tmp_path: Path) -> tuple[ReviewBridge, EventBus, Path]:\n24:     runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n25:     event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n26:     bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n27:     legacy_manager_exe = tmp_path / \"manager_legacy.exe\"\n28:     legacy_manager_exe.write_text(\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n29:     return bridge, event_bus, legacy_manager_exe\n30: \n31: \n32: def test_manager_review_cycle_approves(monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n33:     bridge, event_bus, legacy_manager_exe = _make_bridge(tmp_path)\n34:     supervisor = DummySupervisor()\n35: \n36:     def fake_run(cmd, **kwargs):\n37:         return __import__(\"subprocess\").CompletedProcess(\n38:             cmd,\n39:             0,\n40:             stdout=\"APPROVE: no findings. Ready for closeout.\",\n41:             stderr=\"\",\n42:         )\n43: \n44:     monkeypatch.setattr(\"bus.review_bridge.subprocess.run\", fake_run)\n45:     monkeypatch.setattr(bridge.state_ingest, \"_latest_state\", lambda _: \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n46:     # Force the legacy Manager backend for this compatibility test\n47:     monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"legacy_manager\")\n48: \n49:     result = bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n50:         ticket_id=\"WP-2026-025\",\n51:         supervisor=supervisor,\n52:         manager_executable=legacy_manager_exe,\n53:         timeout_seconds=5,\n54:     )\n55: \n56:     assert result.decision.value == \"approve\"\n57:     assert supervisor.transitions[-1][1] == \"READY_TO_CLOSE\"\n58:     events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=\"WP-2026-025\")\n59:     assert any(event.event_type == \"MANAGER_REVIEWING\" for event in events)\n60:     assert any(event.event_type == \"REVIEW_DECISION\" for event in events)\n61: \n62: \n63: def test_manager_review_cycle_requests_changes(monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n64:     \"\"\"Test review cycle handles CHANGES decision (WP-2026-106: single CHANGES then approve).\n65: \n66:     Note: With WP-2026-106, 5 consecutive CHANGES lead to HUMAN_GATE.\n67:     This test returns CHANGES once, then APPROVE to verify CHANGES path\n68:     without triggering escalation.\n69:     \"\"\"\n70:     bridge, event_bus, legacy_manager_exe = _make_bridge(tmp_path)\n71:     supervisor = DummySupervisor()\n72:     calls: list[list[str]] = []\n73: \n74:     call_count = {\"n\": 0}\n75: \n76:     def fake_run(cmd, **kwargs):\n77:         calls.append(cmd)\n78:         if any(\"agent_controller.py\" in str(part) for part in cmd):\n79:             return __import__(\"subprocess\").CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, stdout=\"[OK] requeue\", stderr=\"\")\n80:         call_count[\"n\"] += 1\n81:         # Return CHANGES first time, then APPROVE\n82:         if call_count[\"n\"] == 1:\n83:             # Include structured sections to pass validation\n84:             return __import__(\"subprocess\").CompletedProcess(\n85:                 cmd,\n86:                 0,\n87:                 stdout=\"## SUMMARY\\nNeeds fixes.\\n## BLOCKERS\\n- Parsing\\n## SUGGESTIONS\\n- Fix parsing\\nDECISION: CHANGES\",\n88:                 stderr=\"\",\n89:             )\n90:         return __import__(\"subprocess\").CompletedProcess(\n91:             cmd,\n92:             0,\n93:             stdout=\"DECISION: APPROVE\",\n94:             stderr=\"\",\n95:         )\n96: \n97:     monkeypatch.setattr(\"bus.review_bridge.subprocess.run\", fake_run)\n98:     monkeypatch.setattr(bridge.state_ingest, \"_latest_state\", lambda _: \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n99:     # Force the legacy Manager backend\n100:     monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"legacy_manager\")\n101: \n102:     result = bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n103:         ticket_id=\"WP-2026-025\",\n104:         supervisor=supervisor,\n105:         manager_executable=legacy_manager_exe,\n106:         timeout_seconds=5,\n107:     )\n108: \n109:     # First call returns CHANGES, second returns APPROVE\n110:     # Since we got APPROVE on retry, final decision is APPROVE\n111:     assert result.decision.value == \"approve\"\n112:     # Should have called twice (CHANGES then APPROVE)\n113:     assert call_count[\"n\"] == 2\n114:     # Should transition to READY_TO_CLOSE\n115:     assert any(t[1] == \"READY_TO_CLOSE\" for t in supervisor.transitions)\n116:     # The --request-changes call happens when CHANGES is the FINAL decision\n117:     # Since we got APPROVE, no requeue call expected\n118:     events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=\"WP-2026-025\")\n119:     assert any(event.event_type == \"MANAGER_REVIEWING\" for event in ticket_id=\"WP-2026-072\",\n528:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n529:             timeout_seconds=5,\n530:         )\n531: \n532:         assert result.decision == approved\")\n535: \n536:     def test_run_manager_review_cycle_dispatches_legacy_manager(self, monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n537:         \"\"\"Test review cycle dispatches to the legacy Manager reason):\n557:                 captured[\"transition\"] = (ticket_id, new_state, reason)\n558: \n559:         fake_executable = tmp_path / \"fake.exe\"\n560: captured[\"ticket_id\"] == \"WP-2026-072\"\n571:         assert captured[\"transition\"] == (\"WP-2026-072\", \"READY_TO_CLOSE\", \"Manager approved\")\n572: \n573:     def test_opencode_review_cmd_length_is_safe(self, monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n574:         \"\"\"Test the constructed command line respects the Windows CMD limit (~8k chars) by using a prompt file.\"\"\"\n575:         import subprocess\n576:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n577: \n578:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n579:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n580:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n581: \n582:         # Create 100 dummy files to simulate a large canonical_files list\n583:         dummy_files = []\n584:         for i in range(100):\n585:             p = tmp_path / f\"dummy_file_{i}.py\"\n586:             p.write_text(\"\")\n587:             dummy_files.append(p)\n588: \n589:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_canonical_files\", lambda: dummy_files)\n590:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_active_ticket_id\", lambda: \"WP-2026-077\")\n591:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge.state_ingest, \"_latest_state\", lambda _: \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n592:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n593: \n594:         captured_cmd = []\n595: \n596:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n597:             captured_cmd.extend(cmd_args)\n598:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(args=cmd_args, returncode=0, stdout=\"DECISION: APPROVE\", stderr=\"\")\n599: \n600:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n601: \n602:         class DummySupervisor:\n603:             def transition_ticket(self, *args, **kwargs):\n604:                 pass\n605: \n606:         bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n607:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-077\",\n608:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n609:             timeout_seconds=5\n610:         )\n611: \n612:         import shlex\n613:         cmd_string = shlex.join(captured_cmd)\n614: \n615:         # Verify the length is well under the 6000 character safety margin\n616:         assert len(cmd_string) < 6000\n617:         # Verify that no -f flags are passed\n618:         assert \"-f\" not in captured_cmd\n619: \n620: \n621: class TestReviewPacketTransport:\n622:     \"\"\"WP-2026-126: el contexto del review va por un review packet en disco,\n623:     no por --file (flag array que consume el mensaje) ni por argv largo.\"\"\"\n624: \n625:     @staticmethod\n626:     def _bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, os_name):\n627:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n628: \n629:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n630:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n631:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_model\", lambda: None)\n632:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_supports_json_format\", False)\n633:         monkeypatch.setattr(\"os.name\", os_name)\n634:         return bridge\n635: \n636:     def test_writes_review_packet(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n637:         \"\"\"El contexto completo se escribe a .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md.\"\"\"\n638:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n639:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n640:             subprocess,\n641:             \"run\",\n642:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n643:         )\n644: \n645:         prompt = \"CONTEXTO DE REVIEW\\n\" + (\"x\" * 8000)\n646:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n647:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n648:         )\n649: \n650:         packet = (\n651:             tmp_path\n652:             / \".agent\"\n653:             / \"runtime\"\n654:             / \"review_packets\"\n655:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n656:         )\n657:         assert packet.exists()\n658:         assert packet.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == prompt\n659: \n660:     def test_review_packet_is_separate_per_attempt(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n661:         \"\"\"Cada intento escribe su propio packet y preserva la provenance.\"\"\"\n662:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n663: \n664:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n665:             subprocess,\n666:             \"run\",\n667:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n668:         )\n669: \n670:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n671:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 1\", attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n672:         )\n673:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n674:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 2\", attempt=2, timeout_seconds=5\n675:         )\n676: \n677:         packet_1 = (\n678:             tmp_path\n679:             / \".agent\"\n680:             / \"runtime\"\n681:             / \"review_packets\"\n682:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n683:         )\n684:         packet_2 = (\n685:             tmp_path\n686:             / \".agent\"\n687:             / \"runtime\"\n688:             / \"review_packets\"\n689:             / \"WP-T_attempt-2.md\"\n690:         )\n691:         assert packet_1.exists()\n692:         assert packet_2.exists()\n693:         assert packet_1.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 1\"\n694:         assert packet_2.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 2\"\n695: \n696:     def test_positional_message_short_and_no_file_flag(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n697:         \"\"\"El prompt posicional es corto y referencia el packet; sin --file\n698:         y sin el prompt completo en la linea de comandos.\"\"\"\n699:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n700: \n701:         captured = {}\n702: \n703:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n704:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n705:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n706: \n707:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n708: \n709:         prompt = \"PROMPT-LARGO-UNICO \" + (\"x\" * 9000)\n710:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n711:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, timeout_seconds=5\n712:         )\n713: \n714:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n715:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n716:         assert prompt not in cmd\n717:         message = cmd[-1]\n718:         assert \"WP-T\" in message\n719:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in message\n720:         assert len(message) < 400\n721: \n722:     def test_windows_invocation_is_string_without_file_flag(\n723:         self, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n724:     ):\n725:         \"\"\"En Windows shell=True: subprocess.run recibe un string (list2cmdline),\n726:         sin --file; el prompt completo nunca toca la CLI.\"\"\"\n727:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"nt\")\n728: \n729:         captured = {}\n730: \n731:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n732:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n733:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n734: \n735:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n736: \n737:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n738:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"x\" * 9000, timeout_seconds=5\n739:         )\n740: \n741:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n742:         assert isinstance(cmd, str)\n743:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n744:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in cmd\n745: \n746: \n747: # ingest.get_ticket_context()\n868:         assert ctx is None\n869: \n870: \n871: class TestBridgeHandshakeWithoutSnapshots:\n872:     \"\"\"Tests for bridge handshake without depending on old \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n880:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n881: \n882:         # Create minimal work_plan.md\n883:         (collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\").write_text(\n884:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n### Metadata\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-102\\n- **deliverable_type:** code\\n\",\n885:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n886:         )\n887: \n888:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n889:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n890:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n891: \n892:         # Emit current state directly to bus (no snapshot files)\n893:         event_bus.emit(\n894:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n895:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n896:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n897:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n898:         )\n899: \n900:         # Mock the review execution to capture that it was called\n901:         captured = {}\n902: \n903:         def fake_opencode_review(*, ticket_id, prompt, attempt=1, manager_executable=None, timeout_seconds):\n904:             captured[\"prompt_includes_ticket\"] = \"WP-2026-102\" in prompt\n905:             return \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\", 0\n906: \n907:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n908:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_run_opencode_review\", fake_opencode_review)\n909: \n910:         class DummySupervisor:\n911:             def transition_ticket(self, ticket_id, new_state, reason):\n912:                 captured[\"transition\"] = (ticket_id, new_state, reason)\n913: \n914:         result = bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n915:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n916:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n917:             timeout_seconds=5,\n918:         )\n919: \n920:         assert result.decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n921:         assert captured.get(\"prompt_includes_ticket\") is True\n922:         assert captured.get(\"transition\") is not None\n923: \n924: \n925: class TestSupervisorNonTerminalStates:\n926:     \"\"\"Tests for supervisor NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant (WP-2026-102).\"\"\"\n927: \n928:     def test_non_terminal_states_constant_exists(self):\n929:         \"\"\"Test NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant is defined.\"\"\"\n930:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n931: \n932:         assert NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not None\n933:         assert isinstance(NON_TERMINAL_STATES, frozenset)\n934: \n935:     def test_is_state_terminal_method(self, tmp_path):\n936:         \"\"\"Test _is_state_terminal correctly identifies terminal vs non-terminal states.\"\"\"\n937:         from bus.state_machine import TicketState\n938:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n939: \n940:         # Non-terminal states should return False\n941:         for state in NON_TERMINAL_STATES:\n942:             assert state not in set(), \"Sanity check: NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not empty\"\n943: \n944:         # COMPLETED and UNKNOWN are terminal\n945:         assert TicketState.COMPLETED not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n946:         assert TicketState.UNKNOWN not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n947: \n948:     def test_supervisor_bootstrap_preserves_active_ticket_in_non_terminal_state(self, tmp_path):\n949:         \"\"\"Test bootstrap does NOT clear active_ticket when bus is in non-terminal state.\"\"\"\n950:         from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor, SupervisorState\n951: \n952:         collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n953:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n954:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n955:         runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n956: \n957:         # Create execution_log showing COMPLETED (but bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW)\n958:         (collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\").write_text(\n959:             \"# Execution Log\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n**Estado:** COMPLETED\\n\",\n960:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n961:         )\n962: \n963:         supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n964:             project_root=tmp_path,\n965:             collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n966:             runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n967:             auto_sync=False,\n968:         )\n969: \n970:         # Set up state with active ticket\n971:         supervisor.save_state(\n972:             SupervisorState(active_ticket=\"WP-2026-102\", completed_tickets=[])\n973:         )\n974: \n975:         # Bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal) - this takes precedence over execution_log\n976:         supervisor.event_bus.emit(\n977:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n978:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n979:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n980:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n981:         )\n982: \n983:         supervisor.bootstrap()\n984: \n985:         # active_ticket should be preserved because bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal)\n986:         # The bootstrap logic only clears active_ticket when:\n987:         # 1. execution_log_status == \"COMPLETED\" AND\n988:         # 2. current_state (from bus) is terminal\n989:         # Since current_state is READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal), active_ticket is preserved\n990:         state = supervisor.load_state()\n991:         assert state.active_ticket == \"WP-2026-102\"\n992: \n993: \n994: # =============================================================================\n995: # Tests WP-2026-106: Structured Manager Reviews and Human Gate Escalation\n996: # bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n1005: \n1006:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n1007:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n1008:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n1009: \n1010:         stdout = \"\"\"\n1011: ## SUMMARY\n1012: Implementation has \"\"\"\n1024:         is_valid, missing = bridge._validate_changes_structure(stdout)\n1025:         assert is_valid is True\n1026:         assert missing == []\n1027: \n1028:     def test_validate_changes_structure_missing_sections(self, tmp_path):\n1029:         \"\"\"Test validation detects missing required sections.\"\"\"\n1030:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n1035: \n1036:         stdout = \"\"\"\n1037: ## SUMMARY\n1038: Implementation has issues.\n1039: \n1040: DECISION: CHANGES\n1041: \"\"\"\n1042:         is_valid, missing = bridge._validate_changes_structure(stdout)\n1043:         assert is_valid is False\n1044:         assert \"BLOCKERS\" in missing\n1045:         assert
