stdout=\"a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2 2026-05-25 10:00:00 +0200 Test Author\\n\",\n328:                 stderr=\"\",\n329:             )\n330:         return Since TURN.md doesn't exist, role defaults to BUILDER\n400:     assert \"BUILDER\" in prompt\n401: \n402: \n403: class TestOpencodeReviewRoute:\n404:     \"\"\"Tests for OpenCode backend review route.\"\"\"\n405: \n406:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_approve(self, tmp_path):\n407:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: APPROVE.\"\"\"\n408:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, ReviewDecision, EventBus\n409: \n410:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n411:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n412:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n413: \n414:         stdout = \"Review complete. All criteria met.\\nDECISION: APPROVE\"\n415:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n416:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n417:         assert method == \"text_regex\"\n418: \n419:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_changes(self, tmp_path):\n420:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: CHANGES.\"\"\"\n421:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, ReviewDecision, EventBus\n422: \n423:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n424:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n425:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n426: \n427:         stdout = \"Found issues:\\n- Missing tests\\n\\nDECISION: CHANGES\"\n428:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n429:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.CHANGES\n430:         assert method == \"text_regex\"\n431: \n432:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_no_decision_fallback_inspect(self, tmp_path):\n433:         \"\"\"Test parser returns INSPECT+fallback_inspect when no decision found.\"\"\"\n434:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, ReviewDecision, EventBus\n435: \n436:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n437:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n438:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n439: \n440:         stdout = \"Review complete but output lacks decision format.\"\n441:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n442:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n443:         assert method == \"fallback_inspect\"\n444: \n445:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_explicit_inspect(self, tmp_path):\n446:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: INSPECT explicitly.\"\"\"\n447:         from bus.review_bridge import bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n452: \n453:         stdout = \"Cannot verify acceptance criteria remotely.\\nDECISION: INSPECT\"\n454:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n455:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n456:         assert method == \"explicit_inspect\"\n457: \n458:     def test_opencode_review_cmd_length_is_safe(self, monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n558:         \"\"\"Test the constructed command line respects the Windows CMD limit (~8k chars) by using a prompt file.\"\"\"\n559:         import subprocess\n560:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n561: \n562:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n563:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n564:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n565: \n566:         # Create 100 dummy files to simulate a large canonical_files list\n567:         dummy_files = []\n568:         for i in range(100):\n569:             p = tmp_path / f\"dummy_file_{i}.py\"\n570:             p.write_text(\"\")\n571:             dummy_files.append(p)\n572: \n573:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_canonical_files\", lambda: dummy_files)\n574:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_active_ticket_id\", lambda: \"WP-2026-077\")\n575:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge.state_ingest, \"_latest_state\", lambda _: \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n576:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n577: \n578:         captured_cmd = []\n579: \n580:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n581:             captured_cmd.extend(cmd_args)\n582:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(args=cmd_args, returncode=0, stdout=\"DECISION: APPROVE\", stderr=\"\")\n583: \n584:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n585: \n586:         class DummySupervisor:\n587:             def transition_ticket(self, *args, **kwargs):\n588:                 pass\n589: \n590:         bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n591:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-077\",\n592:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n593:             timeout_seconds=5\n594:         )\n595: \n596:         import shlex\n597:         cmd_string = shlex.join(captured_cmd)\n598: \n599:         # Verify the length is well under the 6000 character safety margin\n600:         assert len(cmd_string) < 6000\n601:         # Verify that no -f flags are passed\n602:         assert \"-f\" not in captured_cmd\n603: \n604: \n605: class TestReviewPacketTransport:\n606:     \"\"\"WP-2026-126: el contexto del review va por un review packet en disco,\n607:     no por --file (flag array que consume el mensaje) ni por argv largo.\"\"\"\n608: \n609:     @staticmethod\n610:     def _bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, os_name):\n611:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n612: \n613:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n614:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n615:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_model\", lambda: None)\n616:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_supports_json_format\", False)\n617:         monkeypatch.setattr(\"os.name\", os_name)\n618:         return bridge\n619: \n620:     def test_writes_review_packet(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n621:         \"\"\"El contexto completo se escribe a .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md.\"\"\"\n622:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n623:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n624:             subprocess,\n625:             \"run\",\n626:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n627:         )\n628: \n629:         prompt = \"CONTEXTO DE REVIEW\\n\" + (\"x\" * 8000)\n630:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n631:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n632:         )\n633: \n634:         packet = (\n635:             tmp_path\n636:             / \".agent\"\n637:             / \"runtime\"\n638:             / \"review_packets\"\n639:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n640:         )\n641:         assert packet.exists()\n642:         assert packet.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == prompt\n643: \n644:     def test_review_packet_is_separate_per_attempt(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n645:         \"\"\"Cada intento escribe su propio packet y preserva la provenance.\"\"\"\n646:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n647: \n648:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n649:             subprocess,\n650:             \"run\",\n651:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n652:         )\n653: \n654:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n655:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 1\", attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n656:         )\n657:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n658:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 2\", attempt=2, timeout_seconds=5\n659:         )\n660: \n661:         packet_1 = (\n662:             tmp_path\n663:             / \".agent\"\n664:             / \"runtime\"\n665:             / \"review_packets\"\n666:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n667:         )\n668:         packet_2 = (\n669:             tmp_path\n670:             / \".agent\"\n671:             / \"runtime\"\n672:             / \"review_packets\"\n673:             / \"WP-T_attempt-2.md\"\n674:         )\n675:         assert packet_1.exists()\n676:         assert packet_2.exists()\n677:         assert packet_1.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 1\"\n678:         assert packet_2.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 2\"\n679: \n680:     def test_positional_message_short_and_no_file_flag(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n681:         \"\"\"El prompt posicional es corto y referencia el packet; sin --file\n682:         y sin el prompt completo en la linea de comandos.\"\"\"\n683:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n684: \n685:         captured = {}\n686: \n687:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n688:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n689:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n690: \n691:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n692: \n693:         prompt = \"PROMPT-LARGO-UNICO \" + (\"x\" * 9000)\n694:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n695:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, timeout_seconds=5\n696:         )\n697: \n698:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n699:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n700:         assert prompt not in cmd\n701:         message = cmd[-1]\n702:         assert \"WP-T\" in message\n703:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in message\n704:         assert len(message) < 400\n705: \n706:     def test_windows_invocation_is_string_without_file_flag(\n707:         self, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n708:     ):\n709:         \"\"\"En Windows shell=True: subprocess.run recibe un string (list2cmdline),\n710:         sin --file; el prompt completo nunca toca la CLI.\"\"\"\n711:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"nt\")\n712: \n713:         captured = {}\n714: \n715:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n716:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n717:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n718: \n719:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n720: \n721:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n722:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"x\" * 9000, timeout_seconds=5\n723:         )\n724: \n725:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n726:         assert isinstance(cmd, str)\n727:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n728:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in cmd\n729: \n730: \n731: # event_bus.emit(\n805:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n806:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n807:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n808:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n864:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n865: \n866:         # Create minimal work_plan.md\n867:         (collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\").write_text(\n868:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n### Metadata\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-102\\n- **deliverable_type:** code\\n\",\n869:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n870:         )\n871: \n872:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n873:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n874:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n875: \n876:         # Emit current state directly to bus (no snapshot files)\n877:         event_bus.emit(\n878:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n879:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n880:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n881:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n882:         )\n883: \n884:         # Mock the review execution to capture that it was called\n885:         captured = {}\n886: \n887:         def fake_opencode_review(*, ticket_id, prompt, attempt=1, manager_executable=None, timeout_seconds):\n888:             captured[\"prompt_includes_ticket\"] = \"WP-2026-102\" in prompt\n889:             return \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\", 0\n890: \n891:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n892:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_run_opencode_review\", fake_opencode_review)\n893: \n894:         class DummySupervisor:\n895:             def transition_ticket(self, ticket_id, new_state, reason):\n896:                 captured[\"transition\"] = (ticket_id, new_state, reason)\n897: \n898:         result = bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n899:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n900:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n901:             timeout_seconds=5,\n902:         )\n903: \n904:         assert result.decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n905:         assert captured.get(\"prompt_includes_ticket\") is True\n906:         assert captured.get(\"transition\") is not None\n907: \n908: \n909: class TestSupervisorNonTerminalStates:\n910:     \"\"\"Tests for supervisor NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant (WP-2026-102).\"\"\"\n911: \n912:     def test_non_terminal_states_constant_exists(self):\n913:         \"\"\"Test NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant is defined.\"\"\"\n914:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n915: \n916:         assert NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not None\n917:         assert isinstance(NON_TERMINAL_STATES, frozenset)\n918: \n919:     def test_is_state_terminal_method(self, tmp_path):\n920:         \"\"\"Test _is_state_terminal correctly identifies terminal vs non-terminal states.\"\"\"\n921:         from bus.state_machine import TicketState\n922:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n923: \n924:         # Non-terminal states should return False\n925:         for state in NON_TERMINAL_STATES:\n926:             assert state not in set(), \"Sanity check: NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not empty\"\n927: \n928:         # COMPLETED and UNKNOWN are terminal\n929:         assert TicketState.COMPLETED not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n930:         assert TicketState.UNKNOWN not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n931: \n932:     def test_supervisor_bootstrap_preserves_active_ticket_in_non_terminal_state(self, tmp_path):\n933:         \"\"\"Test bootstrap does NOT clear active_ticket when bus is in non-terminal state.\"\"\"\n934:         from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor, SupervisorState\n935: \n936:         collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n937:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n938:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n939:         runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n940: \n941:         # Create execution_log showing COMPLETED (but bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW)\n942:         (collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\").write_text(\n943:             \"# Execution Log\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n**Estado:** COMPLETED\\n\",\n944:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n945:         )\n946: \n947:         supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n948:             project_root=tmp_path,\n949:             collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n950:             runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n951:             auto_sync=False,\n952:         )\n953: \n954:         # Set up state with active ticket\n955:         supervisor.save_state(\n956:             SupervisorState(active_ticket=\"WP-2026-102\", completed_tickets=[])\n957:         )\n958: \n959:         # Bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal) - this takes precedence over execution_log\n960:         supervisor.event_bus.emit(\n961:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n962:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n963:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n964:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n965:         )\n966: \n967:         supervisor.bootstrap()\n968: \n969:         # active_ticket should be preserved because bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal)\n970:         # The bootstrap logic only clears active_ticket when:\n971:         # 1. execution_log_status == \"COMPLETED\" AND\n972:         # 2. current_state (from bus) is terminal\n973:         # Since current_state is READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal), active_ticket is preserved\n974:         state = supervisor.load_state()\n975:         assert state.active_ticket == \"WP-2026-102\"\n976: \n977: \n978: # ticket_id=\"WP-2026-106\",\n1137:             attempt=1,\n1138:             stdout=\"First attempt\",\n1139:             stderr=\"\",\n1140:             decision=ReviewDecision.CHANGES,\n1141: path\n1163:         assert path1 == path2\n1164:         # Content is the second write (idempotent overwrite)\n1165:         content = path1.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1166:         assert \"Second attempt\" in content\n1167:         assert \"## Review Packet\" in content\n1168:         assert \"WP-2026-106_attempt-1.md\" in content\n1169: \n1170:     def Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: --- work_plan.md ---\n4: # Work Plan - WP-2026-134\n5: \n6: ## Metadata\n7: - **ID:** WP-2026-134\n8: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n9: - **deliverable_type:** code\n10: - **Titulo:** Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n11: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n12: \n13: ## Objetivo\n14: El Manager no debe revisar el working tree vacio tras el commit del Builder. Este ticket ancla el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añade provenance del ultimo commit y mantiene el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n15: \n16: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n17: - `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n18: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n19: - Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n20: - `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.\n21: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` del prompt declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory y no sustituye el juicio del Manager.\n22: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n23: - No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n24: \n25: ## Files Likely Touched\n26: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n27: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n28: \n29: ## Fases\n30: 1. Cambiar el origen del diff a `origin/main...HEAD` en `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()`, con fallback a `git diff HEAD` si el remote base no es alcanzable.\n31: 2. Implementar `_git_provenance()` y añadir la seccion correspondiente al prompt del Manager.\n32: 3. Actualizar el bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` para dejar claro que la revision es advisory.\n33: 4. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n34: \n35: ## Calidad\n36: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n37: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n38: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n39: \n40: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n41: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n42: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n43: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n44: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n45: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n46: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n47: \n48: ## Nota\n49: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. El schema estructurado y el panel multi-reviewer quedan para un ticket posterior si hace falta.\n50: \n51: \n52: --- STATE.md ---\n53: # State - WP-2026-134\n54: \n55: Plan Activo: WP-2026-134\n56: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n57: Rol activo: BUILDER\n58: \n59: Resumen:\n60: WP-2026-134 ancla el review del Manager al diff real del ticket y añade provenance para evitar reviews vacios tras el commit del Builder.\n61: \n62: Notas:\n63: - `origin/main` es la base preferida, con fallback seguro si no esta disponible.\n64: - El contrato de decision sigue siendo `APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT`.\n65: - Si aparece deriva hacia schema estructurado o multi-reviewer, el ticket debe escalarse aparte.\n66: \n67: \n68: --- TURN.md ---\n69: # TURNO ACTUAL\n70: \n71: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 13:48:04\n72: \n73: ---\n74: \n75: ## Agente Activo\n76: \n77: | Campo | Valor |\n78: |-------|-------|\n79: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n80: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-134 |\n81: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n82: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n83: \n84: ---\n85: \n86: ## Instruccion\n87: \n88: > Builder completo WP-2026-134. Anclar el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añadir provenance del ultimo commit y mantener el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n120: \n121: ## Alcance\n122: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n123: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n124: \n125: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n126: - `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n127: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe, el bridge degrada a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning visible en el prompt.\n128: - Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n129: - `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff.\n130: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory.\n131: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n132: - No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n133: \n134: ## Fases\n135: 1. Cambiar el origen del diff a `origin/main...HEAD` en `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()`, con fallback a `git diff HEAD` si el remote base no es alcanzable.\n136: 2. Implementar `_git_provenance()` y añadir la seccion correspondiente al prompt del Manager.\n137: 3. Actualizar el bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` para dejar claro que la revision es advisory.\n138: 4. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n139: \n140: ## Files Likely Touched\n141: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n142: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n143: \n144: ## Calidad\n145: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n146: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n147: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n148: \n149: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n150: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n151: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n152: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n153: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n154: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n155: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n156: \n157: ### Casos de test esperados\n158: - `test_build_review_prompt_uses_branch_base_diff`\n159: - `test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote`\n160: - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_provenance_section`\n161: - Los tests existentes de composicion del prompt siguen pasando:\n162:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_generated_artifacts_block`\n163:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_allowed_skills_for_role`\n164: \n165: ## Nota\n166: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. El schema estructurado y el panel multi-reviewer quedan para un ticket posterior si hace falta.\n167: \n168: \n169: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-134.md ---\n170: # AUDIT_WP-2026-134 - Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n171: \n172: ## Verificacion inicial\n173: El review bridge ya empaqueta contexto canonical, pero hoy su diff sale de `git diff HEAD`, que puede quedar vacio una vez el Builder commitea. El gap real es de anclaje de diff y trazabilidad, no de state machine.\n174: \n175: ## Hallazgos\n176: - `bus/review_bridge.py` construye el diff con `git diff HEAD` y el diff-stat con el mismo anclaje al working tree.\n177: - El prompt del Manager ya contiene un bloque de instrucciones, pero no declara de forma explicita que la review es advisory.\n178: - No existe una seccion compacta de provenance del commit en el prompt actual.\n179: - El ticket necesita cubrir el caso en que `origin/main` no sea alcanzable o no exista en el entorno, degradando a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n180: - Los tests del bridge tendran que validar el nuevo origen del diff, el fallback y la presencia de provenance sin cambiar `DECISION`.\n181: \n182: ## Riesgo observado\n183: Si el ticket intenta meter schema JSON estructurado o panel multi-reviewer, el alcance se amplifica y se aleja del objetivo principal: revisar el diff real del ticket con trazabilidad.\n184: \n185: ## Verificacion esperada\n186: - El Manager recibe un diff anclado a `origin/main...HEAD` cuando el remote base esta disponible.\n187: - Si `origin/main` no es accesible, el prompt muestra un warning y degrada a `git El prompt la inserta antes del diff.\n\n4. ✅ **Instrucción advisory explícita**: El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` ahora comienza con: *\"This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\"*\n\n5. ✅ **Contrato de decisión intacto**: El prompt conserva exactamente `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sin cambios estructurales.\n\n6. ✅ **Tests actualizados**: Se añadieron 3 tests nuevos (`test_build_review_prompt_uses_branch_base_diff`, `test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote`, `test_build_review_prompt_includes_provenance_section`) y los existentes pasan.\n\n### Quality Gates (según execution_log.md)\n- `ruff check .` → All checks passed!\n- `ruff format --check .` → 81 files already formatted\n- `pytest` → 4 passed (tests específicos del ticket)\n- `agent_controller --validate` → sin errores\n\n### Whitelist Check\nFiles touched: `bus/review_bridge.py`, `tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py` → Coincide exactamente con `Files Likely Touched` del work_plan.md.\n\n---\n\nReview complete. WP-2026-135\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-135\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** code\n7: - **Titulo:** Selective context recovery lite for pre-compact hook\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: Hacer que el hook de pre-compactacion recupere contexto util de forma ligera, proyectando una seccion `Memoria relevante` basada en `observations.jsonl` y en el `work_plan` activo, sin fallar si la memoria falta o esta corrupta.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `pre_compact_hook.py` debe leer `observations.jsonl` de forma segura y devolver una proyeccion util, aunque la memoria no exista o este corrupta.\n15: - La recuperacion de contexto usa dos senales simples: recencia y coincidencia de keywords extraidas del `work_plan` activo.\n16: - La salida relevante se limita a un maximo de 5 observaciones para mantener la compaction ligera.\n17: - El contrato actual del hook se preserva: `continue=true` y JSON estable de entrada/salida.\n18: - La proyeccion compacta se entrega en el campo `additionalContext` del JSON de salida, porque ese es el canal que consume Claude Code antes de compactar.\n19: - La resolucion de rutas debe derivarse desde `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` para obtener `AGENT_DIR` y no depender de `Path.cwd()`.\n20: - No se introducen embeddings, sqlite, LLM ni dependencias pesadas.\n21: \n22: ## Files Likely Touched\n23: - `.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py`\n24: - `tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py`\n25: \n26: ## Fases\n27: 1. Implementar carga segura de observaciones y extraccion de keywords desde el `work_plan` activo.\n28: 2. Derivar `AGENT_DIR` de `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` y localizar `observations.jsonl` y `work_plan.md` sin depender del cwd.\n29: 3. Rankear observaciones por recencia y coincidencia de palabras clave, con cap de 5.\n30: 4. Formatear la seccion `Memoria relevante` dentro de `additionalContext` para la fase de compactacion.\n31: 5. Añadir tests para memoria vacia, archivo ausente, JSONL corrupto, matching por keywords, ranking por recencia y presencia de `additionalContext`.\n32: 6. Validar que el hook sigue devolviendo JSON estable sin romper el contrato `continue`.\n33: \n34: ## Calidad\n35: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n36: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py -q`\n37: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n38: \n39: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n40: - El hook no falla si `observations.jsonl` no existe o esta corrupto.\n41: - La proyeccion incluye una seccion `Memoria relevante` compacta dentro de `additionalContext` cuando hay observaciones utiles.\n42: - La salida relevante no supera 5 observaciones.\n43: - El ranking combina recencia y coincidencia de palabras clave del `work_plan`.\n44: - Los tests cubren memoria vacia, archivo ausente, JSONL corrupto, ranking basico y `additionalContext`.\n45: \n46: ## Nota\n47: Este ticket es pequeno y autocontenido. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: --- work_plan.md ---\n4: # Work Plan - WP-2026-135\n5: \n6: ## Metadata\n7: - **ID:** WP-2026-135\n8: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n9: - **deliverable_type:** code\n10: - **Titulo:** Selective context recovery lite for pre-compact hook\n11: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n12: \n13: ## Objetivo\n14: Hacer que el hook de pre-compactacion recupere contexto util de forma ligera, proyectando una seccion `Memoria relevante` basada en `observations.jsonl` y en el `work_plan` activo, sin fallar si la memoria falta o esta corrupta.\n15: \n16: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n17: - `pre_compact_hook.py` debe leer `observations.jsonl` de forma segura y devolver una proyeccion util, aunque la memoria no exista o este corrupta.\n18: - La recuperacion de contexto usa dos senales simples: recencia y coincidencia de keywords extraidas del `work_plan` activo.\n19: - La salida relevante se limita a un maximo de 5 observaciones para mantener la compaction ligera.\n20: - El contrato actual del hook se preserva: `continue=true` y JSON estable de entrada/salida.\n21: - La proyeccion compacta se entrega en el campo `additionalContext` del JSON de salida, porque ese es el canal que consume Claude Code antes de compactar.\n22: - La resolucion de rutas debe derivarse desde `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` para obtener `AGENT_DIR` y no depender de `Path.cwd()`.\n23: - No se introducen embeddings, sqlite, LLM ni dependencias pesadas.\n24: \n25: ## Files Likely Touched\n26: - `.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py`\n27: - `tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py`\n28: \n29: ## Fases\n30: 1. Implementar carga segura de observaciones y extraccion de keywords desde el `work_plan` activo.\n31: 2. Derivar `AGENT_DIR` de `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` y localizar `observations.jsonl` y `work_plan.md` sin depender del cwd.\n32: 3. Rankear observaciones por recencia y coincidencia de palabras clave, con cap de 5.\n33: 4. Formatear la seccion `Memoria relevante` dentro de `additionalContext` para la fase de compactacion.\n34: 5. Añadir tests para memoria vacia, archivo ausente, JSONL corrupto, matching por keywords, ranking por recencia y presencia de `additionalContext`.\n35: 6. Validar que el hook sigue devolviendo JSON estable sin romper el contrato `continue`.\n36: \n37: ## Calidad\n38: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n39: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py -q`\n40: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n41: \n42: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n43: - El hook no falla si `observations.jsonl` no existe o esta corrupto.\n44: - La proyeccion incluye una seccion `Memoria relevante` compacta dentro de `additionalContext` cuando hay observaciones utiles.\n45: - La salida relevante no supera 5 observaciones.\n46: - El ranking combina recencia y coincidencia de palabras clave del `work_plan`.\n47: - Los tests cubren memoria vacia, archivo ausente, JSONL corrupto, ranking basico y `additionalContext`.\n48: \n49: ## Nota\n50: Este ticket es pequeno y autocontenido. {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779714177896,"sessionID":"ses_1a0c5ad52ffeEbHTwqDzzuZPTo","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_d2722f64fbc345ff927bf742","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\hooks\\pre_compact_hook.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\hooks\\pre_compact_hook.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Pre-compact hook for preparation before compaction.\n3: \n4: This hook recovers relevant context from observations.jsonl and the active\n5: work_plan.md, projecting a compact \"Memoria relevante\" section into\n6: additionalContext for Claude Code to consume before compacting.\n7: \n8: The hook remains lightweight: no embeddings, no LLM, no heavy dependencies.\n9: It uses two simple signals: recency and keyword matching from work_plan.\n10: Output is capped at 5 observations maximum.\n11: \"\"\"\n12: \n13: from __future__ import annotations\n14: \n15: import json\n16: import re\n17: import sys\n18: from pathlib import Path\n19: from typing import Any\n20: \n21: \n22: # Derive AGENT_DIR from __file__ to avoid depending on cwd\n23: AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n24: MEMORY_DIR = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"memory\"\n25: OBSERVATIONS_FILE = MEMORY_DIR / \"observations.jsonl\"\n26: WORK_PLAN_FILE = AGENT_DIR / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n27: \n28: # Maximum observations to include in the projection\n29: MAX_OBSERVATIONS = 5\n30: \n31: \n32: def load_observations_safe() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n33:     \"\"\"Load observations from observations.jsonl safely.\n34: \n35:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n36:         - observations.jsonl may exist or not in MEMORY_DIR.\n37:         - File may be empty, valid JSONL, or corrupted.\n38: \n39:     During (Process and Resources):\n40:         - Opens OBSERVATIONS_FILE if it exists.\n41:         - Parses each line as JSON, skipping invalid lines.\n42:         - Catches all I/O and JSON exceptions gracefully.\n43: \n44:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n45:         - Returns a list of valid observation dicts (may be empty).\n46:         - Never raises; returns [] on any error.\n47:     \"\"\"\n48:     observations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n49:     if not OBSERVATIONS_FILE.exists():\n50:         return observations\n51: \n52:     try:\n53:         with open(OBSERVATIONS_FILE, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n54:             for line in f:\n55:                 line = line.strip()\n56:                 if not line:\n57:                     continue\n58:                 try:\n59:                     obs = json.loads(line)\n60:                     if isinstance(obs, dict):\n61:                         observations.append(obs)\n62:                 except json.JSONDecodeError:\n63:                     # Skip corrupted lines silently\n64:                     pass\n65:     except OSError:\n66:         # File read error - return empty list\n67:         return []\n68: \n69:     return observations\n70: \n71: \n72: def extract_keywords_from_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n73:     \"\"\"Extract keywords from the active work_plan.md.\n74: \n75:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n76:         - work_plan.md may exist or not in COLLABORATION_DIR.\n77:         - File contains markdown text with objectives, metadata, etc.\n78: \n79:     During (Process and Resources):\n80:         - Reads WORK_PLAN_FILE if it exists.\n81:         - Extracts significant words from Objetivo, Titulo, and metadata sections.\n82:         - Filters out common stop words and markdown artifacts.\n83: \n84:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n85:         - Returns a list of lowercase keyword strings (may be empty).\n86:         - Never raises; returns [] on any error.\n87:     \"\"\"\n88:     if not WORK_PLAN_FILE.exists():\n89:         return []\n90: \n91:     try:\n92:         content = WORK_PLAN_FILE.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n93:     except OSError:\n94:         return []\n95: \n96:     # Extract relevant sections: Objetivo, Titulo, metadata\n97: \n98:     # Simple word extraction: alphanumeric words, lowercase\n99:     words = re.findall(r\"[a-zA-Z\\u00C0-\\u00FF0-9]+\", content.lower())\n100: \n101:     # Stop words to filter out (Spanish + common English)\n102:     stop_words = {\n103:         \"el\",\n104:         \"la\",\n105:         \"los\",\n106:         \"las\",\n107:         \"un\",\n108:         \"una\",\n109:         \"unos\",\n110:         \"unas\",\n111:         \"de\",\n112:         \"del\",\n113:         \"al\",\n114:         \"en\",\n115:         \"con\",\n116:         \"sin\",\n117:         \"para\",\n118:         \"por\",\n119:         \"que\",\n120:         \"se\",\n121:         \"no\",\n122:         \"si\",\n123:         \"es\",\n124:         \"son\",\n125:         \"ser\",\n126:         \"esta\",\n127:         \"este\",\n128:         \"the\",\n129:         \"a\",\n130:         \"an\",\n131:         \"and\",\n132:         \"or\",\n133:         \"in\",\n134:         \"on\",\n135:         \"at\",\n136:         \"to\",\n137:         \"for\",\n138:         \"is\",\n139:         \"are\",\n140:         \"was\",\n141:         \"were\",\n142:         \"be\",\n143:         \"been\",\n144:         \"being\",\n145:         \"wp\",\n146:         \"md\",\n147:         \"agent\",\n148:         \"hook\",\n149:         \"file\",\n150:         \"test\",\n151:         \"tests\",\n152:     }\n153: \n154:     # Filter significant words: not in stop_words and length >= 3\n155:     filtered_words = [\n156:         word for word in words if word not in stop_words and len(word) >= 3\n157:     ]\n158: \n159:     # Return unique keywords, preserving order\n160:     seen = set()\n161:     unique_keywords = []\n162:     for kw in filtered_words:\n163:         if kw not in seen:\n164:             seen.add(kw)\n165:             unique_keywords.append(kw)\n166: \n167:     return unique_keywords\n168: \n169: \n170: def score_observation(obs: dict[str, Any], keywords: list[str]) -> int:\n171:     \"\"\"Score an observation based on recency and keyword matching.\n172: \n173:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n174:         - obs is a dict with optional 'timestamp', 'topic', 'signal', 'source'.\n175:         - keywords is a list of lowercase keyword strings.\n176: \n177:     During (Process and Resources):\n178:         - Computes recency score based on timestamp (newer = higher).\n179:         - Computes keyword match score based on topic and signal content.\n180:         - Combines both scores with recency weighted higher.\n181: \n182:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n183:         - Returns an integer score (higher = more relevant).\n184:         - Returns 0 if obs is empty or invalid.\n185:     \"\"\"\n186:     if not obs:\n187:         return 0\n188: \n189:     score = 0\n190: \n191:     # Recency score: parse timestamp, newer observations get higher scores\n192:     timestamp = obs.get(\"timestamp\", \"\")\n193:     if timestamp:\n194:         try:\n195:             # Extract date part for simple recency scoring\n196:             # Format: ISO 8601 like \"2026-05-24T21:45:59.064645Z\"\n197:             date_part = timestamp[:10] if len(timestamp) >= 10 else \"\"\n198:             if date_part:\n199:                 # Simple recency: more recent dates get higher scores\n200:                 # Use year, month, day as numeric components\n201:                 parts = date_part.split(\"-\")\n202:                 if len(parts) == 3:\n203:                     year, month, day = int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]), int(parts[2])\n204:                     # Base score from date (higher for more recent)\n205:                     score += year * 10000 + month * 100 + day\n206:         except (ValueError, IndexError):\n207:             pass\n208: \n209:     # Keyword matching score\n210:     topic = obs.get(\"topic\", \"\").lower()\n211:     signal = obs.get(\"signal\", \"\").lower()\n212:     source = obs.get(\"source\", \"\").lower()\n213: \n214:     text_to_match = f\"{topic} {signal} {source}\"\n215: \n216:     for kw in keywords:\n217:         if kw in text_to_match:\n218:             score += 50  # Bonus for each keyword match\n219: \n220:     return score\n221: \n222: \n223: def rank_observations(\n224:     observations: list[dict[str, Any]], keywords: list[str]\n225: ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n226:     \"\"\"Rank observations by score (recency + keyword matching).\n227: \n228:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n229:         - observations is a list of observation dicts.\n230:         - keywords is a list of lowercase keyword strings.\n231: \n232:     During (Process and Resources):\n233:         - Scores each observation using score_observation().\n234:         - Sorts by score descending (highest first).\n235:         - Caps the result at MAX_OBSERVATIONS.\n236: \n237:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n238:         - Returns a list of top observations (max MAX_OBSERVATIONS).\n239:         - Returns [] if observations is empty.\n240:     \"\"\"\n241:     if not observations:\n242:         return []\n243: \n244:     # Score and sort\n245:     scored_obs = [(score_observation(obs, keywords), obs) for obs in observations]\n246:     scored_obs.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)\n247: \n248:     # Return top observations, capped at MAX_OBSERVATIONS\n249:     top_obs = [obs for score, obs in scored_obs[:MAX_OBSERVATIONS]]\n250:     return top_obs\n251: \n252: \n253: def format_memory_section(observations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n254:     \"\"\"Format observations as a compact \"Memoria relevante\" section.\n255: \n256:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n257:         - observations is a list of ranked observation dicts.\n258: \n259:     During (Process and Resources):\n260:         - Formats each observation as a bullet point.\n261:         - Includes timestamp, topic, and signal summary.\n262: \n263:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n264:         - Returns a formatted markdown string.\n265:         - Returns empty string if observations is empty.\n266:     \"\"\"\n267:     if not observations:\n268:         return \"\"\n269: \n270:     lines = [\"**Memoria relevante**:\", \"\"]\n271:     for obs in observations:\n272:         timestamp = obs.get(\"timestamp\", \"\")[:10]  # Just date part\n273:         topic = obs.get(\"topic\", \"sin-topic\")\n274:         signal = obs.get(\"signal\", \"\")[:100]  # Truncate long signals\n275:         source = obs.get(\"source\", \"\")\n276: \n277:         if signal:\n278:             lines.append(f\"- [{timestamp}] **{topic}**: {signal} (fuente: {source})\")\n279:         else:\n280:             lines.append(f\"- [{timestamp}] **{topic}** (fuente: {source})\")\n281: \n282:     return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n283: \n284: \n285: def build_additional_context(observations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n286:     \"\"\"Build the additionalContext string for the hook output.\n287: \n288:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n289:         - observations is a list of ranked observation dicts.\n290: \n291:     During (Process and Resources):\n292:         - Calls format_memory_section() to get formatted text.\n293: \n294:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n295:         - Returns the formatted context string (may be empty).\n296:     \"\"\"\n297:     return format_memory_section(observations)\n298: \n299: \n300: def main() -> None:\n301:     \"\"\"Main entry point for pre-compact hook.\n302: \n303:     Before (Pre-conditions):\n304:         - stdin contains valid JSON input (or empty/invalid).\n305:         - observations.jsonl may exist, be empty, or corrupted.\n306:         - work_plan.md may exist or not.\n307: \n308:     During (Process and Resources):\n309:         - Reads and parses stdin JSON safely.\n310:         - Loads observations from MEMORY_DIR safely.\n311:         - Extracts keywords from WORK_PLAN_FILE.\n312:         - Ranks observations by recency and keyword matching.\n313:         - Builds additionalContext with \"Memoria relevante\" section.\n314:         - Outputs JSON result with continue=true.\n315: \n316:     After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n317:         - Prints valid JSON to stdout with \"continue\" and \"additionalContext\".\n318:         - Exits with code 0 (continue) or 1 (error).\n319:         - Never crashes on missing/corrupted files.\n320:     \"\"\"\n321:     try:\n322:         input_data = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())\n323:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, Exception):\n324:         input_data = {}\n325: \n326:     # Load observations safely (never fails)\n327:     observations = load_observations_safe()\n328: \n329:     # Extract keywords from work_plan\n330:     keywords = extract_keywords_from_work_plan()\n331: \n332:     # Rank observations by recency and keyword matching\n333:     ranked_obs = rank_observations(observations, keywords)\n334: \n335:     # Build additionalContext with \"Memoria relevante\" section\n336:     additional_context = build_additional_context(ranked_obs)\n337: \n338:     # Always continue - this hook is for context projection only\n339:     continue_flag = True\n340: \n341:     # Output result\n342:     result: dict[str, Any] = {\n343:         \"continue\": continue_flag,\n344:         \"input\": input_data,\n345:     }\n346: \n347:     # Only include additionalContext if there's relevant memory\n348:     if additional_context:\n349:         result[\"additionalContext\"] = additional_context\n350: \n351:     print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))\n352:     sys.exit(0 if continue_flag else 1)\n353: \n354: \n355: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n356:     main()\n\n(End of file - total 356 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Pre-compact hook for preparation before compaction.\n\nThis hook recovers relevant context from observations.jsonl and the active\nwork_plan.md, projecting a compact \"Memoria relevante\" section into\nadditionalContext for Claude Code to consume before compacting.\n\nThe hook remains lightweight: no embeddings, no LLM, no heavy dependencies.\nIt uses two simple signals: recency and keyword matching from work_plan.\nOutput is capped at 5 observations maximum.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\hooks\\pre_compact_hook.py","time":{"start":1779714177867,"end":1779714177893}},"id":"prt_e5f3b341e001t7JpKMPb6kxhwD","sessionID":"ses_1a0c5ad52ffeEbHTwqDzzuZPTo","messageID":"msg_e5f3abefe00175VsKBxzzRNo5G"}}

#!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Tests para pre_compact_hook.py.\n4: \n5: Cubre:\n6: - Memoria vacia\n7: - Archivo ausente\n8: - JSONL corrupto\n9: - Matching por keywords\n10: - Ranking por recencia\n11: - Presencia de additionalContext\n12: \"\"\"\n13: \n14: import json\n15: import pathlib\n16: import pytest\n17: import sys\n18: from io import StringIO\n19: from unittest.mock import patch\n20: \n21: # Importar el hook\n22: HOOK_PATH = pathlib.Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / \".agent\" / \"hooks\"\n23: sys.path.insert(0, str(HOOK_PATH))\n24: import pre_compact_hook as hook\n25: \n26: \n27: class TestLoadObservationsSafe:\n28:     \"\"\"Tests para load_observations_safe().\"\"\"\n29: \n30:     def test_file_not_exists(self, tmp_path):\n31:         \"\"\"Test cuando observations.jsonl no existe.\"\"\"\n32:         with patch.object(hook, \"OBSERVATIONS_FILE\", tmp_path / \"nonexistent.jsonl\"):\n33:             obs = hook.load_observations_safe()\n34:             assert obs == []\n35: \n36:     def test_empty_file(self, tmp_path):\n37:         \"\"\"Test cuando el archivo existe pero esta vacio.\"\"\"\n38:         obs_file = tmp_path / \"observations.jsonl\"\n39:         obs_file.write_text(\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n40:         with patch.object(hook, \"OBSERVATIONS_FILE\", obs_file):\n41:             obs = hook.load_observations_safe()\n42:             assert obs == []\n43: \n44:     def test_valid_jsonl(self, tmp_path):\n45:         \"\"\"Test con archivo JSONL valido.\"\"\"\n46:         obs_file = tmp_path / \"observations.jsonl\"\n47:         obs_file.write_text(\n48:             '{\"timestamp\": \"2026-05-24T21:45:59Z\", \"topic\": \"test\", \"signal\": \"signal1\", \"source\": \"builder\"}\\n'\n49:             '{\"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T10:00:00Z\", \"topic\": \"arch\", \"signal\": \"signal2\", \"source\": \"manager\"}\\n',\n50:             encoding=\"utf-8\"\n51:         )\n52:         with patch.object(hook, \"OBSERVATIONS_FILE\", obs_file):\n53:             obs = hook.load_observations_safe()\n54:             assert len(obs) == 2\n55:             assert obs[0][\"topic\"] == \"test\"\n56:             assert obs[1][\"topic\"] == \"arch\"\n57: \n58:     def test_corrupted_jsonl(self, tmp_path):\n59:         \"\"\"Test con lineas JSONL corruptas (se saltan).\"\"\"\n60:         obs_file = tmp_path / \"observations.jsonl\"\n61:         obs_file.write_text(\n62:             '{\"timestamp\": \"2026-05-24T21:45:59Z\", \"topic\": \"test\", \"signal\": \"signal1\", \"source\": \"builder\"}\\n'\n63:             'not valid json\\n'\n64:             '{\"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T10:00:00Z\", \"topic\": \"arch\", \"signal\": \"signal2\", \"source\": \"manager\"}\\n'\n65:             'another bad line\\n',\n66:             encoding=\"utf-8\"\n67:         )\n68:         with patch.object(hook, \"OBSERVATIONS_FILE\", obs_file):\n69:             obs = hook.load_observations_safe()\n70:             assert len(obs) == 2  # Solo las lineas validas\n71: \n72:     def test_io_error(self, tmp_path):\n73:         \"\"\"Test cuando hay error de I/O al leer.\"\"\"\n74:         # Simular error de lectura\n75:         with patch.object(hook, \"OBSERVATIONS_FILE\", tmp_path / \"nonexistent.jsonl\"):\n76:             with patch.object(pathlib.Path, \"exists\", return_value=True):\n77:                 with patch.object(pathlib.Path, \"open\", side_effect=IOError(\"Permission denied\")):\n78:                     obs = hook.load_observations_safe()\n79:                     assert obs == []\n80: \n81: \n82: class TestExtractKeywordsFromWorkPlan:\n83:     \"\"\"Tests para extract_keywords_from_work_plan().\"\"\"\n84: \n85:     def test_file_not_exists(self, tmp_path):\n86:         \"\"\"Test cuando work_plan.md no existe.\"\"\"\n87:         with patch.object(hook, \"WORK_PLAN_FILE\", tmp_path /  \"## Objetivo\\n\"\n97:             \"Hacer que el hook de pre-compactacion recupere contexto util.\\n\"\n98:             \"## Files Likely Touched\\n\"\n99:             \"- keywords or \"pre\" in keywords or \"compactacion\" in keywords\n107:             assert \"contexto\" in keywords\n108:             assert \"hook\" not in keywords  # es stop word\n109: {"type":"text","timestamp":1779714226545,"sessionID":"ses_1a0c5ad52ffeEbHTwqDzzuZPTo","part":{"id":"prt_e5f3bcdd3001OAyPgljzc1hpjR","messageID":"msg_e5f3b3d13001nugliyCQnxoXUB","sessionID":"ses_1a0c5ad52ffeEbHTwqDzzuZPTo","type":"text","text":"## Revisión WP-2026-135\n\nHe revisado la implementación del hook de pre-compactación con recuperación selectiva de contexto.\n\n### Verificación de Criterios de Aceptación\n\n1. **Hook no falla si `observations.jsonl` no existe o está corrupto** ✅\n   - `load_observations_safe()` verifica existencia del archivo antes de leer\n   - Captura `OSError` y `json.JSONDecodeError` silenciosamente, retornando `[]`\n   - Tests: `test_file_not_exists`, `test_corrupted_jsonl`, `test_io_error`\n\n2. **Proyección incluye `Memoria relevante` dentro de `additionalContext`** ✅\n   - `build_additional_context()` → `format_memory_section()` genera la sección\n   - `main()` incluye `additionalContext` solo cuando hay contenido relevante\n   - Test: `test_main_with_memory` verifica presencia del campo\n\n3. **Salida limitada a máximo 5 observaciones** ✅\n   - Constante `MAX_OBSERVATIONS = 5` definida\n   - `rank_observations()` aplica slicing `[:MAX_OBSERVATIONS]`\n   - Test: `test_cap_at_max` con 10 observaciones verifica el cap\n\n4. **Ranking combina recencia y coincidencia de keywords** ✅\n   - `score_observation()` suma: fecha (recencia) + 50 puntos por keyword match\n   - `rank_observations()` ordena por score descendente\n   - Test: `test_ranking_by_recency_and_keywords` verifica que keywords priorizan sobre recencia\n\n5. **Tests cubren todos los casos requeridos** ✅\n   - 24 tests creados según execution_log.md\n   - Cobertura: memoria vacía, archivo ausente, JSONL corrupto, matching keywords, ranking, additionalContext\n\n### Files Likely Touched\n- ✅ `.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py` - implementado (356 líneas)\n- ✅ `tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py` - implementado (317 líneas)\n\n### Quality Gates (según execution_log.md)\n- ✅ `ruff check .`: All checks passed!\n- ✅ `pytest tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py -q`: 24 passed\n- ✅ `agent_controller --validate --json --force`: errors vacíos, warnings vacíos\n\n### Decisiones Arquitectónicas Verificadas\n- ✅ `AGENT_DIR` derivado de `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent`\n- ✅ Contrato preservado: `continue=true`, JSON estable de entrada/salida\n- ✅ Sin dependencias pesadas (stdlib only: `json`, `re`, `pathlib`, `sys`, `typing`)\n- ✅ `additionalContext` como canal de proyección para Claude Code\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Quality gates passed.\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779714215379,"end":1779714226536}}}

Actualizar SKILL.md: ejemplo con --candidates, nota de exclusion mutua.\n\n(End of file - total 40 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-136\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-136\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Semantic --candidates input for session_close_observations\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nPermitir que agentes externos, LLMs o usuarios aporten candidatos de observacion\nya construidos semanticamente a `session_close_observations.py` mediante un flag\n`--candidates <json_file>`, desacoplando la generacion de candidatos de su\nvalidacion y filtrado.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `--candidates` y `--ticket` viven en `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`.\n  Una unica fuente de entrada por ejecucion; impuesto por argparse sin logica manual.\n- `load_candidates_from_file(path)` maneja de forma estricta: FileNotFoundError,\n  ValueError (UTF-8 corrupto), ValueError (JSON roto), ValueError (top-level no-lista).","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779719907558,"end":1779719907608}},"id":"prt_e5f9295a2001xLvaC8tPHbR90E","sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","messageID":"msg_e5f92322a001Wc0XJ4YNBDXV3J"}}

Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n4: - Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n5: - Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n6: \n7: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n8: - Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n9: \n10: --- work_plan.md ---\n11: # Work Plan - WP-2026-136\n12: \n13: ## Metadata\n14: - **ID:** WP-2026-136\n15: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n16: - **deliverable_type:** code\n17: - **Titulo:** Semantic --candidates input for session_close_observations\n18: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n19: \n20: ## Objetivo\n21: Permitir que agentes externos, LLMs o usuarios aporten candidatos de observacion\n22: ya construidos semanticamente a `session_close_observations.py` mediante un flag\n23: `--candidates <json_file>`, desacoplando la generacion de candidatos de su\n24: validacion y filtrado.\n25: \n26: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n27: - `--candidates` y `--ticket` viven en `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`.\n28:   Una unica fuente de entrada por ejecucion; impuesto por argparse sin logica manual.\n29: - `load_candidates_from_file(path)` maneja de forma estricta: FileNotFoundError,\n30:   ValueError (UTF-8 corrupto), ValueError (JSON roto), ValueError (top-level no-lista).\n31:   Elementos no-dict en la lista se saltan con warning.\n32: - `load_existing_observations()` se endurece con `errors=\"replace\"` para tolerar\n33:   bytes UTF-8 invalidos en `observations.jsonl` (mismo patron que WP-2026-135).\n34: - `process_candidates()` y `append_observations()` no se tocan.\n35: - stdlib only; sin dependencias nuevas.\n36: \n37: ## Files Likely Touched\n38: - `scripts/session_close_observations.py`\n39: - `tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`\n40: - `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`\n41: \n42: ## Fases\n43: 1. Endurecer `load_existing_observations()`: `errors=\"replace\"` en el open().\n44: 2. Implementar `load_candidates_from_file(path)` con manejo predecible de los\n45:    cuatro casos de error (archivo ausente, UTF-8, JSON, top-level no-lista).\n46: 3. Refactorizar argparse: `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`.\n47: 4. Ramificar `main()` segun el flag activo.\n48: 5. 6 tests: exclusion mutua, JSON valido, archivo ausente, JSON corrupto,\n49:    top-level invalido, dispatch correcto sin llamar extract_candidates_from_ticket.\n50: 6. Actualizar SKILL.md: ejemplo con --candidates, nota de exclusion mutua.\n51: \n52: \n53: --- STATE.md ---\n54: # State - WP-2026-136\n55: \n56: Plan Activo: WP-2026-136\n57: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n58: Rol activo: BUILDER\n59: \n60: Resumen:\n61: WP-2026-136 anade el flag `--candidates <json_file>` a session_close_observations.py.\n62: Permite inyectar candidatos semanticamente ricos sin tocar el pipeline de validacion.\n63: `--ticket` y `--candidates` son mutuamente excluyentes.\n64: \n65: Notas:\n66: - El pipeline process_candidates/append_observations no se modifica.\n67: - stdlib only; sin dependencias nuevas.\n68: - Tests cubren todos los casos de error de carga y el happy path.\n69: \n70: \n71: --- TURN.md ---\n72: # TURNO ACTUAL\n73: \n74: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 16:37:55\n75: \n76: ---\n77: \n78: ## Agente Activo\n79: \n80: | Campo | Valor |\n81: |-------|-------|\n82: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n83: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-136 |\n84: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n85: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n86: \n87: ---\n88: \n89: ## Instruccion\n90: \n91: > Builder completo WP-2026-136. Revisa el trabajo.\n92: \n93: ---\n94: \n95: ## Archivos a Leer\n96: \n97: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n98: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n99: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n100: \n101: ---\n102: \n103: ## Estado del Sistema\n104: \n105: | Archivo | Estado |\n106: |---------|--------|\n107: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n108: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n109: \n110: ---\n111: \n112: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n113: \n114: \n115: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n116: [execution_log section for WP-2026-136 not found]\n117: \n118: --- PLAN_WP-2026-136.md ---\n119: # PLAN_WP-2026-136 - Semantic --candidates input for session_close_observations\n120: \n121: ## Objetivo\n122: Permitir que agentes externos, LLMs o usuarios aporten candidatos de observacion\n123: ya construidos semanticamente a `session_close_observations.py` mediante un flag\n124: `--candidates <json_file>`, desacoplando la generacion de candidatos de su\n125: validacion y filtrado.\n126: \n127: ## Alcance\n128: - `scripts/session_close_observations.py`\n129: - `tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`\n130: - `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`\n131: \n132: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n133: - `--candidates` y `--ticket` viven en un `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`.\n134:   El contrato queda claro: una unica fuente de entrada por ejecucion, impuesto por\n135:   argparse sin validacion manual en `main()`.\n136: - `load_candidates_from_file(path: str) -> list[dict]` es el unico punto de entrada\n137:   del modo candidates. Maneja de forma predecible y estricta:\n138:     - Archivo ausente           -> `FileNotFoundError` con mensaje claro.\n139:     - Bytes UTF-8 invalidos     -> `ValueError(\"UTF-8 decode error: ...\")`\n140:     - JSON invalido             -> `ValueError(\"JSON decode error: ...\")`\n141:     - Top-level no es lista     -> `ValueError(\"Expected list, got <type>\")`\n142:     - Elemento que no es dict   -> se salta con warning (no interrumpe el lote).\n143:   No añade validaciones de schema: eso lo hace `validate_schema()` como antes.\n144: - `load_existing_observations()` se endurece con `errors=\"replace\"` para tolerar\n145:   `UnicodeDecodeError` en `observations.jsonl`, igual que ya se hizo en\n146:   `pre_compact_hook.py` (WP-2026-135). Un archivo danado no debe romper el cierre.\n147: - `process_candidates()` y `append_observations()` no se tocan.\n148: - stdlib only; sin dependencias nuevas.\n149: \n150: ## Files Likely Touched\n151: - `scripts/session_close_observations.py`\n152: - `tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`\n153: - `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`\n154: \n155: ## Fases\n156: 1. Endurecer `load_existing_observations()`: anadir `errors=\"replace\"` al `open()`\n157:    para sobrevivir bytes UTF-8 invalidos en `observations.jsonl`.\n158: 2. Implementar `load_candidates_from_file(path: str) -> list[dict]`:\n159:    - `FileNotFoundError` si el archivo no existe.\n160:    - `ValueError(\"UTF-8 decode error: ...\")` si hay bytes invalidos.\n161:    - `ValueError(\"JSON decode error: ...\")` si el JSON esta roto.\n162:    - `ValueError(\"Expected list, got <type>\")` si el top-level no es lista.\n163:    - Saltar con warning los elementos que no sean dict.\n164: 3. Refactorizar `argparse` en `main()`: mover `--ticket` y `--candidates` a un\n165:    `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`. Eliminar cualquier validacion\n166:    manual equivalente.\n167: 4. Ramificar en `main()`: si `args.candidates`, llamar `load_candidates_from_file()`;\n168:    si `args.ticket`, llamar `extract_candidates_from_ticket()`.\n169: 5. Anadir tests en `test_session_close_observations.py`:\n170:    - `--ticket` y `--candidates` son mutuamente excluyentes (argparse error).\n171:    - `load_candidates_from_file()` con JSON valido -> devuelve lista de dicts.\n172:    - `load_candidates_from_file()` con archivo ausente -> `FileNotFoundError`.\n173:    - `load_candidates_from_file()` con JSON corrupto -> `ValueError`.\n174:    - `load_candidates_from_file()` con top-level invalido (dict, string) -> `ValueError`.\n175:    - `main()` con `--candidates` no llama a `extract_candidates_from_ticket()`\n176:      (verificar dispatch con mock o patch).\n177: 6. Actualizar `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`:\n178:    - Comando de ejemplo con `--candidates`.\n179:    - Workflow: nota de que la fuente puede ser `--ticket` o `--candidates`.\n180:    - Nota explicita de exclusion mutua.\n181: \n182: ## Calidad\n183: - `ruff check scripts/session_close_observations.py tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`\n184: - `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py -v`\n185: - `python scripts/session_close_observations.py --candidates <fichero.json> --dry-run --verbose`\n186: \n187: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n188: - `--candidates <file>` funciona en modo dry-run sin errores.\n189: - `--candidates + --ticket` a la vez produce error de argparse (exit != 0).\n190: - Ninguna de las dos flags -> error de argparse (exit != 0).\n191: - `load_candidates_from_file` lanza excepciones tipadas y predecibles para los\n192:   cuatro casos de error.\n193: - `load_existing_observations` tolera bytes UTF-8 invalidos sin romper el cierre.\n194: - Los 6 tests nuevos pasan. `ruff` limpio.\n195: \n196: \n197: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-136.md ---\n198: # AUDIT_WP-2026-136 - Semantic --candidates input for session_close_observations\n199: \n200: ## Verificacion inicial\n201: \n202: `session_close_observations.py` ya tiene un pipeline completo de validacion,\n203: filtrado y append (`validate_schema`, `is_duplicate`, `process_candidates`,\n204: `append_observations`). Solo acepta candidatos auto-generados via `--ticket`,\n205: que extrae dos observaciones fijas del `work_plan.md` activo. No hay forma de\n206: inyectar candidatos externos. `--ticket` esta marcado como `required=True` en\n207: argparse.\n208: \n209: ## Hallazgos\n210: \n211: - El modo `--ticket` genera candidatos demasiado genericos: siempre el mismo\n212:   par de senales (ticket-completion + architecture decision).\n213: - No existe un punto de entrada para candidatos semanticamente ricos.\n214: - `load_candidates_from_file` no existe.\n215: - El parser actual (`required=True` en `--ticket`) impide anadir `--candidates`\n216:   sin refactorizar el grupo de argumentos. La forma correcta es\n217:   `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`, no validacion manual.\n218: - `load_existing_observations()` abre `observations.jsonl` con\n219:   `encoding=\"utf-8\"` sin `errors=\"replace\"`, igual que el bug ya corregido en\n220:   `pre_compact_hook.py` (WP-2026-135). Un archivo con bytes invalidos lanza\n221:   `UnicodeDecodeError` y rompe el cierre de sesion.\n222: - `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md` solo documenta `--ticket`.\n223: \n224: ## Riesgos a acotar\n225: \n226: - No corromper el pipeline existente: `process_candidates` y `append_observations`\n227:   no deben tocarse.\n228: - `load_candidates_from_file` no debe re-validar schema: eso ya lo hace\n229:   `validate_schema`. Solo valida estructura de carga (lista de dicts).\n230: - Elementos no-dict en el JSON de entrada deben saltarse con warning, no abortar\n231:   el lote entero.\n232: - `source_ticket` ausente es un error de schema, no de carga.\n233: - El endurecimiento de `load_existing_observations` sigue el mismo patron que\n234:   `pre_compact_hook.py`: `errors=\"replace\"` en el `open()`, sin mas cambios.\n235: \n236: ## Verificacion esperada\n237: \n238: - `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)` impone la exclusion a nivel de\n239:   argparse, sin logica manual en `main()`.\n240: - `load_candidates_from_file` lanza excepciones tipadas para: archivo ausente,\n241:   UTF-8 invalido, JSON roto, top-level no-lista.\n242: - `load_existing_observations` tolera `observations.jsonl` con bytes invalidos.\n243: - `main()` despacha a la rama correcta segun el flag activo.\n244: - 6 tests nuevos cubren todos los casos relevantes.\n245: - `SKILL.md` refleja el nuevo modo y la exclusion mutua.\n246: - `ruff` y `pytest` pasan en verde.\n247: \n248: \n249: --- git provenance ---\n250: fc951a2ca065ade5c4a17cdd0960282ea832f041 2026-05-25 16:37:25 +0200 fdl\n251: \n252: --- git diff (Files Likely Touched) ---\n253: diff --git a/.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-134.md b/.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-134.md\n254: deleted file mode 100644\n255: index 41c4694..0000000\n256: --- a/.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-134.md\n257: +++ /dev/null\n258: @@ -1,29 +0,0 @@\n259: -# AUDIT_WP-2026-134 - Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n260: -\n261: -## Verificacion inicial\n262: -El review bridge ya empaqueta contexto canonical, pero hoy su diff sale de `git diff HEAD`, que puede quedar vacio una vez el Builder commitea. Maneja de forma predecible y estricta:\n425: +    - Archivo ausente           -> `FileNotFoundError` con mensaje claro.\n426: +    - Bytes UTF-8 invalidos     -> `ValueError(\"UTF-8 decode error: ...\")`\n427: +    - JSON invalido             -> `ValueError(\"JSON decode error: ...\")`\n428: +    - Top-level no es lista     -> `ValueError(\"Expected list, got <type>\")`\n429: +    - Elemento que no es dict   -> se salta con warning (no interrumpe el lote).\n430: +  No añade validaciones de schema: eso lo hace `validate_schema()` como antes.\n431: +- `load_existing_observations()` se endurece con `errors=\"replace\"` para tolerar\n432: +  `UnicodeDecodeError` en `observations.jsonl`, igual que ya se hizo en\n433: +  `pre_compact_hook.py` (WP-2026-135). Un archivo danado no debe romper el cierre.\n434: +- `process_candidates()` y `append_observations()` no se tocan.\n435: +- stdlib only; sin dependencias nuevas.\n436: +\n437: +## Files Likely Touched\n438: +- `scripts/session_close_observations.py`\n439: +- `tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`\n440: +- `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`\n441: +\n442: +## Fases\n443: +1. Endurecer `load_existing_observations()`: anadir `errors=\"replace\"` al `open()`\n444: +   para sobrevivir bytes UTF-8 invalidos en `observations.jsonl`.\n445: +2. Implementar `load_candidates_from_file(path: str) -> list[dict]`:\n446: +   - `FileNotFoundError` si el archivo no existe.\n447: +   - `ValueError(\"UTF-8 decode error: ...\")` si hay bytes invalidos.\n448: +   - `ValueError(\"JSON decode error: ...\")` si el JSON esta roto.\n449: +   - `ValueError(\"Expected list, got <type>\")` si el top-level no es lista.\n450: +   - Saltar con warning los elementos que no sean dict.\n451: +3. Refactorizar `argparse` en `main()`: mover `--ticket` y `--candidates` a un\n452: +   `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`. Eliminar cualquier validacion\n453: +   manual equivalente.\n454: +4. Ramificar en `main()`: si `args.candidates`, llamar `load_candidates_from_file()`;\n455: +   si `args.ticket`, llamar `extract_candidates_from_ticket()`.\n456: +5. Anadir tests en `test_session_close_observations.py`:\n457: +   - `--ticket` y `--candidates` son mutuamente excluyentes (argparse error).\n458: +   - `load_candidates_from_file()` con JSON valido -> devuelve lista de dicts.\n459: +   - `load_candidates_from_file()` con archivo ausente -> `FileNotFoundError`.\n460: +   - `load_candidates_from_file()` con JSON corrupto -> `ValueError`.\n461: +   - `load_candidates_from_file()` con top-level invalido (dict, string) -> `ValueError`.\n462: +   - `main()` con `--candidates` no llama a `extract_candidates_from_ticket()`\n463: +     (verificar dispatch con mock o patch).\n464: +6. Actualizar `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`:\n465: +   - Comando de ejemplo con `--candidates`.\n466: +   - Workflow: nota de que la fuente puede ser `--ticket` o `--candidates`.\n467: +   - Nota explicita de exclusion mutua.\n468: +\n469: +## Calidad\n470: +- `ruff check scripts/session_close_observations.py tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`\n471: +- `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py -v`\n472: +- `python scripts/session_close_observations.py --candidates <fichero.json> --dry-run --verbose`\n473: +\n474: +## Criterios de aceptacion\n475: +- `--candidates <file>` funciona en modo dry-run sin errores.\n476: +- `--candidates + --ticket` a la vez produce error de argparse (exit != 0).\n477: +- Ninguna de las dos flags -> error de argparse (exit != 0).\n478: +- `load_candidates_from_file` lanza excepciones tipadas y predecibles para los\n479: +  cuatro casos de error.\n480: +- `load_existing_observations` tolera bytes UTF-8 invalidos sin romper el cierre.\n481: +- Los 6 tests nuevos pasan. `ruff` limpio.\n482: diff --git a/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md b/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md\n483: index f16edd4..f1ba5e3 100644\n484: --- a/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md\n485: +++ b/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md\n486: @@ -1,13 +1,12 @@\n487: -# State - WP-2026-134\n488: +# State - WP-2026-135\n489:  \n490: -Plan Activo: WP-2026-134\n491: +Plan Activo: WP-2026-135\n492:  Estado actual: COMPLETED\n493: -Rol activo: MANAGER\n494: +Rol activo: -\n495:  \n496:  Resumen:\n497: -WP-2026-134 ancla el review del Manager al diff real del ticket y añade provenance para evitar reviews vacios tras el commit del Builder.\n498: -\n499: -Notas:\n500: -- `origin/main` es la base preferida, con fallback seguro si no esta disponible.\n501: -- El contrato de decision sigue siendo `APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT`.\n502: -- El ticket queda cerrado de forma canonica y listo para el siguiente ciclo.\n503: +WP-2026-135 completado. Decision Arquitectonica\n641: -- `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n642: -- Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n643: -- Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n644: -- `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.\n645: -- El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` del prompt declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory y no sustituye el juicio del Manager.\n646: -- El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n647: -- No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n648: +- `pre_compact_hook.py` debe leer `observations.jsonl` de forma segura y devolver una proyeccion util, aunque la memoria no exista o este corrupta.\n649: +- La recuperacion de contexto usa dos senales simples: recencia y coincidencia de keywords extraidas del `work_plan` activo.\n650: +- La salida relevante se limita a un maximo de 5 observaciones para mantener la compaction ligera.\n651: +- El contrato actual del hook se preserva: `continue=true` y JSON estable de entrada/salida.\n652: +- La proyeccion compacta se entrega en el campo `additionalContext` del JSON de salida, porque ese es el canal que consume Claude Code antes de compactar.\n653: +- La resolucion de rutas debe derivarse desde `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` para obtener `AGENT_DIR` y no depender de `Path.cwd()`.\n654: +- No se introducen embeddings, sqlite, LLM ni dependencias pesadas.\n655:  \n656:  ##
