b/.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py\n697: index 78dcf39..95a6989 100644\n698: --- a/.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py\n699: +++ b/.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py\n700: @@ -1,28 +1,339 @@\n701:  #!/usr/bin/env python3\n702: -\"\"\"Pre-compact hook for preparation before compaction.\"\"\"\n703: +\"\"\"Pre-compact hook for preparation before compaction.\n704: +\n705: +This hook recovers relevant context from observations.jsonl and the active\n706: +work_plan.md, projecting a compact \"Memoria relevante\" section into\n707: +additionalContext for Claude Code to consume before compacting.\n708: +\n709: +The hook remains lightweight: no embeddings, no LLM, no heavy dependencies.\n710: +It uses two simple signals: recency and keyword matching from work_plan.\n711: +Output is capped at 5 observations maximum.\n712: +\"\"\"\n713:  \n714:  from __future__ import annotations\n715:  \n716:  import json\n717: +import re\n718:  import sys\n719: +from pathlib import Path\n720: +from typing import Any\n721: +\n722: +\n723: +# Derive AGENT_DIR from __file__ to avoid depending on cwd\n724: +AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n725: +MEMORY_DIR = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"memory\"\n726: +OBSERVATIONS_FILE = MEMORY_DIR / \"observations.jsonl\"\n727: +WORK_PLAN_FILE = AGENT_DIR / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n728: +\n729: +# Maximum observations to include in the projection\n730: +MAX_OBSERVATIONS = 5\n731: +\n732: +\n733: +def load_observations_safe() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n734: +    \"\"\"Load observations from observations.jsonl safely.\n735: +\n736: +    Before (Pre-conditions):\n737: +        - observations.jsonl may exist or not in MEMORY_DIR.\n738: +        - File may be empty, valid JSONL, or corrupted.\n739: +\n740: +    During (Process and Resources):\n741: +        - Opens OBSERVATIONS_FILE if it exists.\n742: +        - Parses each line as JSON, skipping invalid lines.\n743: +        - Catches all I/O and JSON exceptions gracefully.\n744: +\n745: +    After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n746: +        - Returns a list of valid observation dicts (may be empty).\n747: +        - Never raises; returns [] on any error.\n748: +    \"\"\"\n749: +    observations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n750: +    if not OBSERVATIONS_FILE.exists():\n751: +        return observations\n752: +\n753: +    try:\n754: +        with open(OBSERVATIONS_FILE, encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\") as f:\n755: +            for line in f:\n756: +                line = line.strip()\n757: +                if not line:\n758: +                    continue\n759: +                try:\n760: +                    obs = json.loads(line)\n761: +                    if isinstance(obs, dict):\n762: +                        observations.append(obs)\n763: +                except json.JSONDecodeError:\n764: +                    # Skip corrupted lines silently\n765: +                    pass\n766: +    except OSError:\n767: +        # File read error - return empty list\n768: +        return []\n769: +\n770: +    return observations\n771: +\n772: +\n773: +def extract_keywords_from_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n774: +    \"\"\"Extract keywords from the active work_plan.md.\n775: +\n776: +    Before (Pre-conditions):\n777: +        - work_plan.md may exist or not in COLLABORATION_DIR.\n778: +        - File contains markdown text with objectives, metadata, etc.\n779: +\n780: +    During (Process and Resources):\n781: +        - Reads WORK_PLAN_FILE if it exists.\n782: +        - Extracts significant words from Objetivo, Titulo, and metadata sections.\n783: +        - Filters out common stop words and markdown artifacts.\n784: +\n785: +    After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n786: +        - Returns a list of lowercase keyword strings (may be empty).\n787: +        - Never raises; returns [] on any error.\n788: +    \"\"\"\n789: +    if not WORK_PLAN_FILE.exists():\n790: +        return []\n791: +\n792: +    try:\n793: +        content = WORK_PLAN_FILE.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n794: +    except OSError:\n795: +        return []\n796: +\n797: +    # Extract relevant sections: Objetivo, Titulo, metadata\n798: +\n799: +    # Simple word extraction: alphanumeric words, lowercase\n800: +    words = re.findall(r\"[a-zA-Z\\u00C0-\\u00FF0-9]+\", content.lower())\n801: +\n802: +    # Stop words to filter out (Spanish + common English)\n803: +    stop_words = {\n804: +        \"el\",\n805: +        \"la\",\n806: +        \"los\",\n807: +        \"las\",\n808: +        \"un\",\n809: +        \"una\",\n810: +        \"unos\",\n811: +        \"unas\",\n812: +        \"de\",\n813: +        \"del\",\n814: +        \"al\",\n815: +        \"en\",\n816: +        \"con\",\n817: +        \"sin\",\n818: +        \"para\",\n819: +        \"por\",\n820: +        \"que\",\n821: +        \"se\",\n822: +        \"no\",\n823: +        \"si\",\n824: +        \"es\",\n825: +        \"son\",\n826: +        \"ser\",\n827: +        \"esta\",\n828: +        \"este\",\n829: +        \"the\",\n830: +        \"a\",\n831: +        \"an\",\n832: +        \"and\",\n833: +        \"or\",\n834: +        \"in\",\n835: +        \"on\",\n836: +        \"at\",\n837: +        \"to\",\n838: +        \"for\",\n839: +        \"is\",\n840: +        \"are\",\n841: +        \"was\",\n842: +        \"were\",\n843: +        \"be\",\n844: +        \"been\",\n845: +        \"being\",\n846: +        \"wp\",\n847: +        \"md\",\n848: +        \"agent\",\n849: +        \"hook\",\n850: +        \"file\",\n851: +        \"test\",\n852: +        \"tests\",\n853: +    }\n854: +\n855: +    # Filter significant words: not in stop_words and length >= 3\n856: +    filtered_words = [\n857: +        word for word in words if word not in stop_words and len(word) >= 3\n858: +    ]\n859: +\n860: +    # Return unique keywords, preserving order\n861: +    seen = set()\n862: +    unique_keywords = []\n863: +    for kw in filtered_words:\n864: +        if kw not in seen:\n865: +            seen.add(kw)\n866: +            unique_keywords.append(kw)\n867: +\n868: +    return unique_keywords\n869: +\n870: +\n871: +def score_observation(obs: dict[str, Any], keywords: list[str]) -> int:\n872: +    \"\"\"Score an observation based on recency and keyword matching.\n873: +\n874: +    Before (Pre-conditions):\n875: +        - obs is a dict with optional 'timestamp', 'topic', 'signal', 'source'.\n876: +        - keywords is a list of lowercase keyword strings.\n877: +\n878: +    During (Process and Resources):\n879: +        - Computes recency score based on timestamp (newer = higher).\n880: +        - Computes keyword match score based on topic and signal content.\n881: +        - Combines both scores with recency weighted higher.\n882: +\n883: +    After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n884: +        - Returns an integer score (higher = more relevant).\n885: +        - Returns 0 if obs is empty or invalid.\n886: +    \"\"\"\n887: +    if not obs:\n888: +        return 0\n889: +\n890: +    score = 0\n891: +\n892: +    # Recency score: parse timestamp, newer observations get higher scores\n893: +    timestamp = obs.get(\"timestamp\", \"\")\n894: +    if timestamp:\n895: +        try:\n896: +            # Extract date part for simple recency scoring\n897: +            # Format: ISO 8601 like \"2026-05-24T21:45:59.064645Z\"\n898: +            date_part = timestamp[:10] if len(timestamp) >= 10 else \"\"\n899: +            if date_part:\n900: +                # Simple recency: more recent dates get higher scores\n901: +                # Use year, month, day as numeric components\n902: +                parts = date_part.split(\"-\")\n903: +                if len(parts) == 3:\n904: +                    year, month, day = int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]), int(parts[2])\n905: +                    # Base score from date (higher for more recent)\n906: +                    score += year * 10000 + month * 100 + day\n907: +        except (ValueError, IndexError):\n908: +            pass\n909: +\n910: +    # Keyword matching score\n911: +    topic = str(obs.get(\"topic\") or \"\").lower()\n912: +    signal = str(obs.get(\"signal\") or \"\").lower()\n913: +    source = str(obs.get(\"source\") or \"\").lower()\n914: +\n915: +    text_to_match = f\"{topic} {signal} {source}\"\n916: +\n917: +    for kw in keywords:\n918: +        if kw in text_to_match:\n919: +            score += 50  # Bonus for each keyword match\n920: +\n921: +    return score\n922: +\n923: +\n924: +def rank_observations(\n925: +    observations: list[dict[str, Any]], keywords: list[str]\n926: +) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n927: +    \"\"\"Rank observations by score (recency + keyword matching).\n928: +\n929: +    Before (Pre-conditions):\n930: +        - observations is a list of observation dicts.\n931: +        - keywords is a list of lowercase keyword strings.\n932: +\n933: +    During (Process and Resources):\n934: +        - Scores each observation using score_observation().\n935: +        - Sorts by score descending (highest first).\n936: +        - Caps the result at MAX_OBSERVATIONS.\n937: +\n938: +    After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n939: +        - Returns a list of top observations (max MAX_OBSERVATIONS).\n940: +        - Returns [] if observations is empty.\n941: +    \"\"\"\n942: +    if not observations:\n943: +        return []\n944: +\n945: +    # Score and sort\n946: +    scored_obs = [(score_observation(obs, keywords), obs) for obs in observations]\n947: +    scored_obs.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)\n948: +\n949: +    # Return top observations, capped at MAX_OBSERVATIONS\n950: +    top_obs = [obs for score, obs in scored_obs[:MAX_OBSERVATIONS]]\n951: +    return top_obs\n952: +\n953: +\n954: +def format_memory_section(observations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n955: +    \"\"\"Format observations as a compact \"Memoria relevante\" section.\n956: +\n957: +    Before (Pre-conditions):\n958: +        - observations is a list of ranked observation dicts.\n959: +\n960: +    During (Process and Resources):\n961: +        - Formats each observation as a bullet point.\n962: +        - Includes timestamp, topic, and signal summary.\n963: +\n964: +    After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n965: +        - Returns a formatted markdown string.\n966: +        - Returns empty string if observations is empty.\n967: +    \"\"\"\n968: +    if not observations:\n969: +        return \"\"\n970: +\n971: +    lines = [\"**Memoria relevante**:\", \"\"]\n972: +    for obs in observations:\n973: +        timestamp = str(obs.get(\"timestamp\") or \"\")[:10]  # Just date part\n974: +        topic = str(obs.get(\"topic\") or \"sin-topic\")\n975: +        signal = str(obs.get(\"signal\") or \"\")[:100]  # Truncate long signals\n976: +        source = str(obs.get(\"source\") or \"\")\n977: +\n978: +        if signal:\n979: +            lines.append(f\"- [{timestamp}] **{topic}**: {signal} (fuente: {source})\")\n980: +        else:\n981: +            lines.append(f\"- [{timestamp}] **{topic}** (fuente: {source})\")\n982: +\n983: +    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n984:  \n985:  \n986:  def main() -> None:\n987: -    \"\"\"Main entry point for pre-compact hook.\"\"\"\n988: +    \"\"\"Main entry point for pre-compact hook.\n989: +\n990: +    Before (Pre-conditions):\n991: +        - stdin contains valid JSON input (or empty/invalid).\n992: +        - observations.jsonl may exist, be empty, or corrupted.\n993: +        - work_plan.md may exist or not.\n994: +\n995: +    During (Process and Resources):\n996: +        - Reads and parses stdin JSON safely.\n997: +        - Loads observations from MEMORY_DIR safely.\n998: +        - Extracts keywords from WORK_PLAN_FILE.\n999: +        - Ranks observations by recency and keyword matching.\n1000: +        - Builds additionalContext with \"Memoria relevante\" section.\n1001: +        - Outputs JSON result with continue=true.\n1002: +\n1003: +    After (Post-conditions and Errors):\n1004: +        - Prints valid JSON to stdout with \"continue\" and \"additionalContext\".\n1005: +        - Exits with code 0 (continue) or 1 (error).\n1006: +        - Never crashes on missing/corrupted files.\n1007: +    \"\"\"\n1008:      try:\n1009:          input_data = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())\n1010: -    except Exception:\n1011: +    except (json.JSONDecodeError, Exception):\n1012:          input_data = {}\n1013:  \n1014: -    # Perform pre-compaction checks\n1015: +    # Load observations safely (never fails)\n1016: +    observations = load_observations_safe()\n1017: +\n1018: +    # Extract keywords from work_plan\n1019: +    keywords = extract_keywords_from_work_plan()\n1020: +\n1021: +    # Rank observations by recency and keyword matching\n1022: +    ranked_obs = rank_observations(observations, keywords)\n1023: +\n1024: +    # Build additionalContext with \"Memoria relevante\" section\n1025: +    additional_context = format_memory_section(ranked_obs)\n1026: +\n1027: +    # Always continue - this hook is for context projection only\n1028:      continue_flag = True\n1029:  \n1030:      # Output result\n1031: -    result = {\n1032: +    result: dict[str, Any] = {\n1033:          \"continue\": continue_flag,\n1034:          \"input\": input_data,\n1035:      }\n1036: -    print(json.dumps(result))\n1037: +\n1038: +    # Only include additionalContext if there's relevant memory\n1039: +    if additional_context:\n1040: +        result[\"additionalContext\"] = additional_context\n1041: +\n1042: +    print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))\n1043:      sys.exit(0 if continue_flag else 1)\n1044:  \n1045:  \n1046: diff --git a/AGENTS.md b/AGENTS.md\n1047: index b148dde..714a286 100644\n1048: --- a/AGENTS.md\n1049: +++ b/AGENTS.md\n1050: @@ -121,6 +121,14 @@ Para evitar la inflación artificial de cobertura sin validación lógica real,\n1051:  - **Test Inútil (descartable):** Aquel que solo hace aserciones pasivas como `assert obj is not None` o verifica constantes mocked estáticas sin desencadenar lógica real.\n1052:  - **Test Útil (aceptado):** Aquel que fuerza casos límite (boundary limits), valida transiciones reales de estado (en el Event Bus, archivos de configuración o memoria persistente) y verifica explícitamente el lanzamiento de excepciones esperadas (`pytest.raises`).\n1053:  \n1054: +**Anti-patrones de test que el Builder debe evitar y el Manager debe rechazar:**\n1055: +- **Mock drift:** el patch apunta a `X` pero el código llama a `Y` (distinta API). El test pasa sin probar nada real. Ejemplo: parchear `pathlib.Path.open` cuando el código usa el built-in `open()`.\n1056: +- **Floor assertion:** el umbral de una aserción numérica es satisfecho por el valor base sin la feature probada. Ejemplo: `assert score >= 150` cuando el score de recencia solo ya es `~20_000_000`.\n1057: +\n1058: +### 3. Anti-patrones de implementación\n1059: +\n1060: +- **Zero-logic wrapper:** una función cuyo cuerpo completo es una única llamada delegada 1:1 sin lógica propia debe ser inlineada o eliminada. Añade indirección cognitiva sin valor.\n1061: +\n1062:  ## Skills Formales de Proceso\n1063:  \n1064:  El repositorio define skills operativas formales para estructurar el trabajo del agente.\n1065: diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md\n1066: index 160be1b..d8f1bcf 100644\n1067: --- a/CHANGELOG.md\n1068: +++ b/CHANGELOG.md\n1069: @@ -1,3 +1,33 @@\n1070: +# 2026-05-25 - WP-2026-135 Selective context recovery lite for pre-compact hook\n1071: +\n1072: +### Added\n1073: +- `.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py`: hook funcional que carga `observations.jsonl`,\n1074: +  extrae keywords de `work_plan.md`, rankea por recencia + keyword matching (cap 5) y\n1075: +  proyecta `additionalContext` con sección `Memoria relevante` antes de compactar.\n1076: +- `tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py`: 25 tests (TestLoadObservationsSafe,\n1077: +  TestExtractKeywordsFromWorkPlan, TestScoreObservation, TestRankObservations,\n1078: +  TestFormatMemorySection, TestMainHook, TestRobustness).\n1079: +\n1080: +### Changed\n1081: +- `bus/review_bridge.py`: rubric de `code` y `mixed` ampliad con checklist de\n1082: +  anti-patrones como BLOCKERs: mock drift, floor assertion, zero-logic wrapper.\n1083: +- `AGENTS.md`: documentados los tres anti-patrones bajo secciones de testing (§2)\n1084: +  e implementación (§3) para guiar a Builder y Manager en cada revisión.\n1085: +\n1086: +### Fixed\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1086. Use offset=1087 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-136. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n\n--- work_plan.md ---\n# Work Plan - WP-2026-136\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-136\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Semantic --candidates input for session_close_observations\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-136_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779719907570,"end":1779719907645}},"id":"prt_e5f929d5c001QpHlIDnT8sLVx8","sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","messageID":"msg_e5f92322a001Wc0XJ4YNBDXV3J"}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779719936772,"sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_05d14bd0e75e489f9d47814a","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\session_close_observations.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\session_close_observations.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Session Close Observations: Generate curated observations at session end.\n3: \n4: This script generates candidate observations from a completed work cycle,\n5: filters them through curation rules, and appends valid ones to\n6: observations.jsonl for later consolidation by memory_consolidate.py.\n7: \n8: WP-2026-132: Session-close observations for self-improving memory.\n9: \"\"\"\n10: \n11: from __future__ import annotations\n12: \n13: import argparse\n14: import json\n15: import re\n16: import sys\n17: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n18: from pathlib import Path\n19: from typing import Any\n20: \n21: \n22: # WP-2026-122: Deferred path resolution via runtime.project_root\n23: try:\n24:     from runtime.project_root import get_agent_dir\n25: except ImportError:\n26:     # Fallback if runtime.project_root not available\n27:     get_agent_dir = None\n28: \n29: AGENT_DIR = (\n30:     get_agent_dir()\n31:     if get_agent_dir is not None\n32:     else Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \".agent\"\n33: )\n34: MEMORY_DIR = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"memory\"\n35: OBS_FILE = MEMORY_DIR / \"observations.jsonl\"\n36: REPORT_FILE = MEMORY_DIR / \"session_close_report.md\"\n37: \n38: # Valid categories per work_plan.md\n39: VALID_CATEGORIES = {\"convention\", \"decision\", \"fact\", \"pattern\"}\n40: \n41: # Minimum signal length\n42: MIN_SIGNAL_LEN = 30\n43: \n44: # Noise patterns\n45: NOISE_PREFIXES = (\"Tool \",)\n46: \n47: \n48: def parse_timestamp(ts_str: str) -> datetime:\n49:     \"\"\"Parse ISO timestamp string to datetime.\n50: \n51:     Before: Requires ISO format timestamp string.\n52:     During: Handles Z suffix and timezone conversion.\n53:     After: Returns timezone-aware datetime object.\n54:     \"\"\"\n55:     ts_str = ts_str.replace(\"Z\", \"+00:00\")\n56:     try:\n57:         return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str)\n58:     except ValueError:\n59:         return datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n60: \n61: \n62: def is_noise(signal: str) -> bool:\n63:     \"\"\"Check if signal should be dropped as noise.\n64: \n65:     Before: Requires signal string.\n66:     During: Checks length and noise prefix patterns.\n67:     After: Returns True if noise, False if valid signal.\n68:     \"\"\"\n69:     signal_stripped = signal.strip()\n70:     if len(signal_stripped) < MIN_SIGNAL_LEN:\n71:         return True\n72:     for prefix in NOISE_PREFIXES:\n73:         if signal_stripped.startswith(prefix) and signal_stripped.endswith(\"called\"):\n74:             return True\n75:     return False\n76: \n77: \n78: def validate_schema(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:\n79:     \"\"\"Validate observation schema.\n80: \n81:     Before: Requires observation dict.\n82:     During: Checks required fields and types.\n83:     After: Returns (is_valid, list_of_errors).\n84:     \"\"\"\n85:     errors = []\n86:     required_fields = [\n87:         \"timestamp\",\n88:         \"signal\",\n89:         \"category\",\n90:         \"source_ticket\",\n91:         \"topic\",\n92:         \"source\",\n93:     ]\n94: \n95:     # Check required fields\n96:     missing = [f for f in required_fields if f not in entry]\n97:     errors.extend(f\"Campo requerido ausente: {field}\" for field in missing)\n98: \n99:     if errors:\n100:         return False, errors\n101: \n102:     # Validate category\n103:     if entry[\"category\"] not in VALID_CATEGORIES:\n104:         errors.append(\n105:             f\"Categoria invalida: {entry['category']} (validas: {VALID_CATEGORIES})\"\n106:         )\n107: \n108:     # Validate signal length\n109:     if len(entry[\"signal\"].strip()) < MIN_SIGNAL_LEN:\n110:         errors.append(\n111:             f\"Signal muy corto: {len(entry['signal'])} chars (min {MIN_SIGNAL_LEN})\"\n112:         )\n113: \n114:     # Validate signal is not noise\n115:     if is_noise(entry[\"signal\"]):\n116:         errors.append(\"Signal es ruido (Tool X called pattern o muy corto)\")\n117: \n118:     # Validate timestamp format\n119:     try:\n120:         parse_timestamp(entry[\"timestamp\"])\n121:     except Exception:\n122:         errors.append(f\"Timestamp invalido: {entry['timestamp']}\")\n123: \n124:     return len(errors) == 0, errors\n125: \n126: \n127: def is_duplicate(entry: dict[str, Any], existing: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool:\n128:     \"\"\"Check if entry is duplicate of existing observation.\n129: \n130:     Before: Requires new entry and list of existing observations.\n131:     During: Compares signal+category+topic within 24h window.\n132:     After: Returns True if duplicate, False if unique.\n133:     \"\"\"\n134:     new_signal = entry.get(\"signal\", \"\")\n135:     new_category = entry.get(\"category\", \"\")\n136:     new_topic = entry.get(\"topic\", \"\")\n137:     new_ts = parse_timestamp(entry.get(\"timestamp\", \"\"))\n138: \n139:     for existing_entry in existing:\n140:         existing_signal = existing_entry.get(\"signal\", \"\")\n141:         existing_category = existing_entry.get(\"category\", \"\")\n142:         existing_topic = existing_entry.get(\"topic\", \"\")\n143:         existing_ts = parse_timestamp(existing_entry.get(\"timestamp\", \"\"))\n144: \n145:         # Exact match on signal+category+topic\n146:         if (\n147:             new_signal == existing_signal\n148:             and new_category == existing_category\n149:             and new_topic == existing_topic\n150:         ):\n151:             # Check if within 24h window\n152:             hours_diff = abs((new_ts - existing_ts).total_seconds()) / 3600\n153:             if hours_diff <= 24:\n154:                 return True\n155: \n156:     return False\n157: \n158: \n159: def load_existing_observations() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n160:     \"\"\"Load existing observations from file.\n161: \n162:     Before: Requires observations.jsonl to exist (or not).\n163:     During: Parses JSONL, skipping malformed lines. Tolerates invalid UTF-8 bytes.\n164:     After: Returns list of observation dicts.\n165:     \"\"\"\n166:     if not OBS_FILE.exists():\n167:         return []\n168: \n169:     entries = []\n170:     with open(OBS_FILE, encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\") as f:\n171:         for line_num, line in enumerate(f, 1):\n172:             line = line.strip()\n173:             if not line:\n174:                 continue\n175:             try:\n176:                 entry = json.loads(line)\n177:                 entries.append(entry)\n178:             except json.JSONDecodeError:\n179:                 print(f\"Warning: Skipping malformed JSON at line {line_num}\")\n180:     return entries\n181: \n182: \n183: def append_observations(entries: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:\n184:     \"\"\"Append observations to file.\n185: \n186:     Before: Requires list of validated entries.\n187:     During: Appends each entry as JSON line.\n188:     After: File updated with new entries.\n189:     \"\"\"\n190:     MEMORY_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n191:     with open(OBS_FILE, \"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n192:         for entry in entries:\n193:             f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + \"\\n\")\n194: \n195: \n196: def load_candidates_from_file(path: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n197:     \"\"\"Load candidate observations from JSON file.\n198: \n199:     Before: Requires path to JSON file containing list of candidate observations.\n200:     During: Reads file, decodes UTF-8 (tolerant), parses JSON, validates top-level is list.\n201:             Non-dict elements are skipped with warning.\n202:     After: Returns list of candidate dicts. Raises:\n203:            - FileNotFoundError if file does not exist\n204:            - ValueError(\"UTF-8 decode error: ...\") if invalid UTF-8 bytes\n205:            - ValueError(\"JSON decode error: ...\") if JSON is malformed\n206:            - ValueError(\"Expected list, got <type>\") if top-level is not a list\n207:     \"\"\"\n208:     file_path = Path(path)\n209:     if not file_path.exists():\n210:         raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Candidates file not found: {path}\")\n211: \n212:     try:\n213:         content = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n214:     except Exception as e:\n215:         raise ValueError(f\"UTF-8 decode error: {e}\") from e\n216: \n217:     try:\n218:         data = json.loads(content)\n219:     except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n220:         raise ValueError(f\"JSON decode error: {e}\") from e\n221: \n222:     if not isinstance(data, list):\n223:         data_type = type(data).__name__\n224:         raise ValueError(f\"Expected list, got {data_type}\")\n225: \n226:     candidates = []\n227:     for idx, item in enumerate(data):\n228:         if not isinstance(item, dict):\n229:             item_type = type(item).__name__\n230:             print(f\"Warning: Skipping element {idx} (expected dict, got {item_type})\")\n231:             continue\n232:         candidates.append(item)\n233: \n234:     return candidates\n235: \n236: \n237: def generate_report(\n238:     generated: int,\n239:     passed: int,\n240:     rejected: int,\n241:     appended: int,\n242:     rejected_reasons: list[str],\n243:     topics: list[str],\n244: ) -> str:\n245:     \"\"\"Generate session close report.\n246: \n247:     Before: Requires stats and rejection reasons.\n248:     During: Formats markdown report.\n249:     After: Returns report string.\n250:     \"\"\"\n251:     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC\")\n252: \n253:     report = f\"\"\"# Session Close Report\n254: \n255: **Generated:** {now}\n256: \n257: ## Summary\n258: \n259: | Metric | Count |\n260: |--------|-------|\n261: | Observaciones generadas | {generated} |\n262: | Pasaron filtros | {passed} |\n263: | Rechazadas | {rejected} |\n264: | Appendeadas (no duplicadas) | {appended} |\n265: \n266: ## Topics Cubiertos\n267: \n268: {chr(10).join(f\"- {t}\" for t in topics) if topics else \"- Ninguno\"}\n269: \n270: ## Razones de Rechazo\n271: \n272: {chr(10).join(f\"- {r}\" for r in rejected_reasons) if rejected_reasons else \"- Ninguna\"}\n273: \n274: ## Next Steps\n275: \n276: 1. Review `.agent/runtime/memory/observations.jsonl` for new entries\n277: 2. Run `python scripts/memory_consolidate.py --verbose` if session is long\n278: 3. Continue with project-finalize Paso 9d\n279: \"\"\"\n280:     return report\n281: \n282: \n283: def process_candidates(\n284:     candidates: list[dict[str, Any]], existing: list[dict[str, Any]]\n285: ) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[str]]:\n286:     \"\"\"Process candidate observations through filters.\n287: \n288:     Before: Requires list of candidate observations and existing observations.\n289:     During: Validates schema, applies curation filters, checks duplicates.\n290:     After: Returns (appended_entries, rejection_reasons).\n291:     \"\"\"\n292:     appended = []\n293:     rejected_reasons = []\n294: \n295:     for candidate in candidates:\n296:         # Validate schema\n297:         is_valid, errors = validate_schema(candidate)\n298:         if not is_valid:\n299:             rejected_reasons.extend(\n300:                 f\"[{candidate.get('source_ticket', 'unknown')}] {error}\"\n301:                 for error in errors\n302:             )\n303:             continue\n304: \n305:         # Check duplicate\n306:         if is_duplicate(candidate, existing):\n307:             rejected_reasons.append(\n308:                 f\"[{candidate.get('source_ticket', 'unknown')}] Duplicado de observacion existente\"\n309:             )\n310:             continue\n311: \n312:         appended.append(candidate)\n313: \n314:     return appended, rejected_reasons\n315: \n316: \n317: def extract_candidates_from_ticket(ticket_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n318:     \"\"\"Extract candidate observations from work plan ticket.\n319: \n320:     Before: Requires ticket ID (e.g., WP-2026-132).\n321:     During: Reads work_plan.md and execution_log.md for events.\n322:     After: Returns list of candidate observation dicts.\n323:     \"\"\"\n324:     candidates = []\n325:     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n326: \n327:     # Read work_plan.md\n328:     work_plan_path = AGENT_DIR / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n329:     if work_plan_path.exists():\n330:         content = work_plan_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n331: \n332:         # Extract ticket metadata\n333:         if f\"ID:** {ticket_id}\" in content or f\"ID: {ticket_id}\" in content:\n334:             # Extract deliverable_type if present\n335:             deliverable_match = re.search(r\"deliverable_type:\\s*(\\w+)\", content)\n336:             deliverable = deliverable_match.group(1) if deliverable_match else \"unknown\"\n337: \n338:             # Extract title\n339:             title_match = re.search(r\"Titulo:\\s*(.+)\", content)\n340:             title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else \"Unknown\"\n341: \n342:             # Generate observation for ticket completion\n343:             candidates.append(\n344:                 {\n345:                     \"timestamp\": now.isoformat(),\n346:                     \"signal\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} completado: {title} (deliverable_type={deliverable})\",\n347:                     \"category\": \"fact\",\n348:                     \"source_ticket\": ticket_id,\n349:                     \"topic\": \"ticket-completion\",\n350:                     \"source\": \"session-close\",\n351:                 }\n352:             )\n353: \n354:         # Extract decisions from Decision Arquitectonica section\n355:         if \"Decision Arquitectonica\" in content:\n356:             candidates.append(\n357:                 {\n358:                     \"timestamp\": now.isoformat(),\n359:                     \"signal\": f\"Decisiones arquitectonicas documentadas en {ticket_id}\",\n360:                     \"category\": \"decision\",\n361:                     \"source_ticket\": ticket_id,\n362:                     \"topic\": \"architecture\",\n363:                     \"source\": \"session-close\",\n364:                 }\n365:             )\n366: \n367:     return candidates\n368: \n369: \n370: def _write_report(\n371:     generated: int,\n372:     passed: int,\n373:     rejected: int,\n374:     appended: int,\n375:     rejected_reasons: list[str],\n376:     topics: list[str],\n377:     dry_run: bool,\n378: ) -> str:\n379:     \"\"\"Generate and write session close report.\n380: \n381:     Before: Requires stats, rejection reasons, topics, and dry-run flag.\n382:     During: Formats markdown report and writes to REPORT_FILE.\n383:     After: Report file updated, summary printed to stdout. Returns report string.\n384:     \"\"\"\n385:     report = generate_report(\n386:         generated=generated,\n387:         passed=passed,\n388:         rejected=rejected,\n389:         appended=appended,\n390:         rejected_reasons=rejected_reasons,\n391:         topics=topics,\n392:     )\n393: \n394:     mode = \"DRY-RUN\" if dry_run else \"APPLIED\"\n395:     REPORT_FILE.write_text(report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n396:     print(f\"[{mode}] Report written to {REPORT_FILE}\")\n397:     return report\n398: \n399: \n400: def _load_candidates(args: argparse.Namespace, verbose: bool) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n401:     \"\"\"Load candidates from ticket or file based on active flag.\n402: \n403:     Before: Requires parsed args with --ticket or --candidates, and verbose flag.\n404:     During: Dispatches to extract_candidates_from_ticket() or load_candidates_from_file().\n405:     After: Returns list of candidate observations. Prints verbose info if enabled.\n406:     \"\"\"\n407:     if args.ticket:\n408:         candidates = extract_candidates_from_ticket(args.ticket)\n409:         if verbose:\n410:             print(f\"Extracted {len(candidates)} candidates from ticket {args.ticket}\")\n411:     elif args.candidates:\n412:         candidates = load_candidates_from_file(args.candidates)\n413:         if verbose:\n414:             print(f\"Loaded {len(candidates)} candidates from {args.candidates}\")\n415:     else:\n416:         # Should never reach here due to mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)\n417:         print(\"Error: Either --ticket or --candidates is required\", file=sys.stderr)\n418:         return []\n419:     return candidates\n420: \n421: \n422: def main() -> int:\n423:     \"\"\"Main entry point.\n424: \n425:     Before: Requires command-line arguments (--ticket or --candidates, mutually exclusive).\n426:     During: Loads candidates from ticket or file, processes filters, appends valid observations.\n427:     After: Returns exit code (0 success, 1 error).\n428:     \"\"\"\n429:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n430:         description=\"Generate curated observations at session close\"\n431:     )\n432:     group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)\n433:     group.add_argument(\"--ticket\", type=str, help=\"Ticket ID (e.g., WP-2026-132)\")\n434:     group.add_argument(\n435:         \"--candidates\", type=str, help=\"Path to JSON file with candidate observations\"\n436:     )\n437:     parser.add_argument(\"--verbose\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Print detailed output\")\n438:     parser.add_argument(\n439:         \"--dry-run\",\n440:         action=\"store_true\",\n441:         help=\"Simulate without writing to observations.jsonl (default: write)\",\n442:     )\n443: \n444:     args = parser.parse_args()\n445: \n446:     try:\n447:         # Load existing observations\n448:         existing = load_existing_observations()\n449:         if args.verbose:\n450:             print(f\"Loaded {len(existing)} existing observations\")\n451: \n452:         # Generate/load candidates based on active flag\n453:         candidates = _load_candidates(args, args.verbose)\n454:         if not candidates:\n455:             return 1\n456: \n457:         # Process through filters\n458:         appended, rejected_reasons = process_candidates(candidates, existing)\n459: \n460:         # Append valid observations (skip on dry-run)\n461:         if appended and not args.dry_run:\n462:             append_observations(appended)\n463:             if args.verbose:\n464:                 print(f\"Appended {len(appended)} observations to {OBS_FILE}\")\n465:         elif appended and args.dry_run:\n466:             if args.verbose:\n467:                 print(\n468:                     f\"[DRY-RUN] Would append {len(appended)} observations to {OBS_FILE}\"\n469:                 )\n470: \n471:         # Write report\n472:         report = _write_report(\n473:             generated=len(candidates),\n474:             passed=len(appended),\n475:             rejected=len(candidates) - len(appended),\n476:             appended=len(appended),\n477:             rejected_reasons=rejected_reasons,\n478:             topics=list(set(c.get(\"topic\", \"unknown\") for c in candidates)),\n479:             dry_run=args.dry_run,\n480:         )\n481: \n482:         if args.verbose:\n483:             print(\"\\n\" + report)\n484: \n485:         return 0\n486: \n487:     except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as e:\n488:         print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n489:         return 1\n490:     except Exception as e:\n491:         print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n492:         return 1\n493: \n494: \n495: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n496:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 496 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Session Close Observations: Generate curated observations at session end.\n\nThis script generates candidate observations from a completed work cycle,\nfilters them through curation rules, and appends valid ones to\nobservations.jsonl for later consolidation by memory_consolidate.py.\n\nWP-2026-132: Session-close observations for self-improving memory.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport json\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"scripts\\session_close_observations.py","time":{"start":1779719936742,"end":1779719936770}},"id":"prt_e5f930fd6001n1Rp00gKYWKL4M","sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","messageID":"msg_e5f92ac2c001D0hPR1n8uB0Lt6"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779719936776,"sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_bc9a5f4a925949c09502ea76","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\session-close-observations\\SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\session-close-observations\\SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: session-close-observations\n3: version: 1.0.0\n4: description: Generar observaciones curadas al final de cada sesion para memoria auto-mejorable\n5: triggers: [/session-close, /close-observations, /generate-observations]\n6: author: agent\n7: tags: [core, system, memory]\n8: ---\n9: \n10: # session-close-observations\n11: \n12: Skill compartida para convertir lo aprendido al final de cada ciclo en observaciones curadas y reutilizables. El sistema mejora con las ejecuciones sin mezclar memoria viva con ruido operativo.\n13: \n14: ## Overview\n15: \n16: Esta skill es invocada por Builder, Manager o Supervisor al cerrar una sesion para generar observaciones candidatas que seran filtradas y potencialmente promovidas a `observations.jsonl`.\n17: \n18: ### Cuando activar\n19: \n20: - Al finalizar un WP (Work Plan) completado\n21: - Al cerrar una sesion de trabajo larga (>2 horas)\n22: - Cuando hay decisiones arquitectonicas o patrones descubiertos\n23: - Antes de ejecutar `memory-consolidate` (Paso 9d de `project-finalize`)\n24: \n25: ### Cuando NO activar\n26: \n27: - Durante implementacion activa de un WP\n28: - Si no hay eventos significativos que registrar\n29: - En sesiones cortas de mantenimiento rutinario\n30: \n31: ## Workflow\n32: \n33: ### Paso 1: Recopilar eventos del ciclo\n34: \n35: Identificar eventos significativos del ciclo actual:\n36: - Decisiones arquitectonicas tomadas\n37: - Convenciones establecidas o modificadas\n38: - Patrones de codigo descubiertos\n39: - Hechos tecnicos relevantes (bugs fijos, optimizaciones)\n40: - Cambios en la estructura del proyecto\n41: \n42: Fuentes tipicas:\n43: - `execution_log.md` - registro de implementacion\n44: - `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md` - plan completado\n45: - Commits recientes del repositorio\n46: - Cambios en archivos de configuracion\n47: \n48: ### Paso 2: Generar observaciones candidatas\n49: \n50: Para cada evento significativo, crear una observacion con el schema base:\n51: \n52: ```json\n53: {\n54:   \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n55:   \"signal\": \"Descripcion clara y concisa del hecho\",\n56:   \"category\": \"convention|decision|fact|pattern\",\n57:   \"source_ticket\": \"WP-2026-XXX\",\n58:   \"topic\": \"tema principal\",\n59:   \"source\": \"session-close\"\n60: }\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Paso 3: Aplicar filtros de curacion\n64: \n65: Cada observacion candidata debe pasar tres reglas:\n66: \n67: 1. **Es un hecho**: Debe ser verificable y objetivo, no una opinion\n68: 2. **Sobrevive a otra sesion**: Debe ser util dentro de 1+ semanas\n69: 3. **Evita trabajo repetido**: No debe duplicar observaciones existentes\n70: \n71: Filtros de exclusion automatica:\n72: - Opiniones subjetivas (\"me parece que...\", \"deberiamos...\")\n73: - Contexto efimero (traces de herramientas, logs temporales)\n74: - Duplicados exactos o semanticamente equivalentes\n75: - Entradas < 30 caracteres\n76: \n77: ### Paso 4: Escribir observaciones validadas\n78: \n79: Las observaciones que pasan los filtros se appendean a:\n80: - `.agent/runtime/memory/observations.jsonl`\n81: \n82: Formato: una linea JSON por observacion, encoding UTF-8.\n83: \n84: ### Paso 5: Reportar resultado\n85: \n86: Generar un resumen de cierre:\n87: - Total de observaciones generadas\n88: - Cuantas pasaron los filtros\n89: - Cuantas fueron descartadas y por que\n90: - Topics cubiertos en este ciclo\n91: \n92: ## Schema de Observacion\n93: \n94: | Campo | Tipo | Requerido | Descripcion |\n95: |-------|------|-----------|-------------|\n96: | `timestamp` | ISO 8601 | SI | Fecha/hora UTC de la observacion |\n97: | `signal` | string | SI | Descripcion clara del hecho (min 30 chars) |\n98: | `category` | enum | SI | `convention`, `decision`, `fact`, `pattern` |\n99: | `source_ticket` | string | SI | WP-ID o referencia al origen |\n100: | `topic` | string | SI | Tema principal para agrupacion |\n101: | `source` | string | SI | Origen: `session-close`, `builder`, `manager`, etc. |\n102: \n103: ### Categorias validas\n104: \n105: - **convention**: Convenciones de codigo, nombres, estructura\n106: - **decision**: Decisiones arquitectonicas o de diseño\n107: - **fact**: Hechos tecnicos verificables\n108: - **pattern**: Patrones de diseño o implementacion\n109: \n110: ## Output Format\n111: \n112: La skill no tiene output directo en stdout. Su efecto es:\n113: - Append de observaciones a `observations.jsonl`\n114: - Reporte opcional en `.agent/runtime/memory/session_close_report.md`\n115: \n116: ## References\n117: \n118: - `references/schema.md` - Detalle del schema de observaciones\n119: - `references/filter-rules.md` - Reglas de filtrado y curacion\n120: - `../memory-consolidate/SKILL.md` - Consolidacion posterior\n121: - `../project-finalize/SKILL.md` - Integracion en cierre de proyecto\n122: \n123: ## Constraints\n124: \n125: - **NO** reemplaza `memory-consolidate`: solo genera candidatas\n126: - **NO** escribe en `MEMORY.md` directamente: eso lo hace consolidate\n127: - **NO** usa LLM para sintesis: filtrado deterministico\n128: - **SIEMPRE** valida schema antes de appendear\n129: - **SIEMPRE** pasa filtros de curacion\n130: \n131: ## Integration\n132: \n133: Esta skill se invoca en `project-finalize` entre:\n134: - Paso 9c: `local_audit.py` (snapshot de auditoria)\n135: - Paso 9d: `memory_consolidate.py` (consolidacion de memoria)\n136: \n137: Comandos tipicos:\n138: ```bash\n139: # Modo ticket: extrae candidatos automaticos del work_plan.md activo\n140: python scripts/session_close_observations.py --ticket WP-2026-XXX\n141: \n142: # Modo candidates: inyecta candidatos semanticos desde archivo JSON externo\n143: python scripts/session_close_observations.py --candidates candidates.json\n144: \n145: # Dry-run para previsualizar sin escribir\n146: python scripts/session_close_observations.py --ticket WP-2026-XXX --dry-run --verbose\n147: ```\n148: \n149: **Nota de exclusion mutua:** `--ticket` y `--candidates` son mutuamente excluyentes.\n150: Argparse impone que exactamente una de las dos flags debe estar presente por ejecucion.\n151: No es posible usar ambas simultaneamente ni omitir las dos.\n152: \n153: **Workflow con --candidates:**\n154: 1. El agente/LLM construye un archivo JSON con candidatos semanticamente ricos\n155: 2. Cada candidato debe seguir el schema de observacion (timestamp, signal, category, etc.)\n156: 3. El script valida schema, aplica filtros de curacion y chequea duplicados\n157: 4. Las observaciones validas se appendean a `observations.jsonl`\n158: \n159: ## Related Skills\n160: \n161: - `memory-consolidate`: Dedupe + filter + archive observations\n162: - `project-finalize`: Orquesta cierre profesional\n163: - `local-audit`: Genera snapshot del estado del repo\n\n(End of file - total 163 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: session-close-observations\nversion: 1.0.0\ndescription: Generar observaciones curadas al final de cada sesion para memoria auto-mejorable\ntriggers: [/session-close, /close-observations, /generate-observations]\nauthor: agent\ntags: [core, system, memory]\n---\n\n# session-close-observations\n\nSkill compartida para convertir lo aprendido al final de cada ciclo en observaciones curadas y reutilizables. El sistema mejora con las ejecuciones sin mezclar memoria viva con ruido operativo.\n\n## Overview\n\nEsta skill es invocada por Builder, Manager o Supervisor al cerrar una sesion para generar observaciones candidatas que seran filtradas y potencialmente promovidas a `observations.jsonl`.\n\n### Cuando activar\n\n- Al finalizar un WP (Work Plan) completado","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"skills\\session-close-observations\\SKILL.md","time":{"start":1779719936757,"end":1779719936774}},"id":"prt_e5f931721001FUedc4Pyoy8cRp","sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","messageID":"msg_e5f92ac2c001D0hPR1n8uB0Lt6"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779719936782,"sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_7fa90adcb07e48778231397c","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\tests\\unit\\test_session_close_observations.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\tests\\unit\\test_session_close_observations.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Tests for session_close_observations.py.\"\"\"\n3: \n4: from __future__ import annotations\n5: \n6: import sys\n7: from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone\n8: from pathlib import Path\n9: \n10: import pytest\n11: from scripts.session_close_observations import (\n12:     VALID_CATEGORIES,\n13:     is_duplicate,\n14:     is_noise,\n15:     parse_timestamp,\n16:     process_candidates,\n17:     validate_schema,\n18: )\n19: \n20: \n21: def test_is_noise_tool_called() -> None:\n22:     \"\"\"Tool X called patterns should be dropped.\"\"\"\n23:     assert is_noise(\"Tool view_file called\") is True\n24:     assert is_noise(\"Tool bash called\") is True\n25:     assert is_noise(\"  Tool edit called  \") is True\n26: \n27: \n28: def test_is_noise_short_entry() -> None:\n29:     \"\"\"Entries < 30 chars should be dropped.\"\"\"\n30:     assert is_noise(\"Short signal\") is True\n31:     assert is_noise(\"x\" * 29) is True\n32:     assert is_noise(\"x\" * 30) is False\n33: \n34: \n35: def test_is_noise_valid_entry() -> None:\n36:     \"\"\"Valid entries should not be marked as noise.\"\"\"\n37:     assert is_noise(\"This is a valid observation signal with enough length\") is False\n38:     assert is_noise(\"WP-2026-132 completed successfully after implementation\") is False\n39: \n40: \n41: def test_validate_schema_missing_field() -> None:\n42:     \"\"\"Schema validation should fail with missing required field.\"\"\"\n43:     entry = {\n44:         \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-25T12:00:00Z\",\n45:         \"signal\": \"Test signal\",\n46:         # Missing: category, source_ticket, topic, source\n47:     }\n48:     is_valid, errors = validate_schema(entry)\n49: {"type":"text","timestamp":1779719989574,"sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","part":{"id":"prt_e5f93b7be001lsp8hUCJtVIpio","messageID":"msg_e5f931c3400107wcy0SyXCwk40","sessionID":"ses_1a06dce9fffeVGSDz7nqs7u4Mg","type":"text","text":"## Revisión WP-2026-136\n\nHe revisado la implementación comparando contra los criterios de aceptación del work_plan.md y verificando los archivos del whitelist.\n\n### Verificación de Criterios de Aceptación\n\n| Criterio | Estado |\n|----------|--------|\n| `--candidates <file>` funciona en dry-run sin errores | ✅ PASSED |\n| `--candidates + --ticket` produce error argparse (exit != 0) | ✅ PASSED |\n| Sin flags -> error argparse (exit != 0) | ✅ PASSED |\n| `load_candidates_from_file` lanza excepciones tipadas (4 casos) | ✅ Implementado |\n| `load_existing_observations` tolera UTF-8 invalido (`errors=\"replace\"`) | ✅ Línea 170 |\n| Tests nuevos pasan + ruff limpio | ✅ 23/23 tests, ruff PASSED |\n\n### Verificación de Files Likely Touched\n\n| Archivo | Estado |\n|---------|--------|\n| `scripts/session_close_observations.py` | ✅ Modificado |\n| `tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py` | ✅ Modificado |\n| `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md` | ✅ Actualizado |\n\n### Verificación de Implementación\n\n1. **`load_existing_observations()`**: Línea 170 con `errors=\"replace\"` para tolerar bytes UTF-8 invalidos.\n\n2. **`load_candidates_from_file(path)`**: Líneas 196-236, maneja los 4 casos de error especificados:\n   - `FileNotFoundError` si archivo no existe\n   - `ValueError(\"UTF-8 decode error: ...\")` para bytes invalidos\n   - `ValueError(\"JSON decode error: ...\")` para JSON roto\n   - `ValueError(\"Expected list, got <type>\")` para top-level no-lista\n   - Elementos no-dict se saltan con warning\n\n3. **Argparse**: Líneas 426-432 con `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`.\n\n4. **Dispatch en main()**: Función `_load_candidates()` (líneas 401-419) ramifica según flag activo.\n\n5. **Tests**: 7 tests nuevos (líneas 267-367) cubren todos los casos requeridos.\n\n6. **SKILL.md**: Documenta modo `--candidates` y nota de exclusion mutua (líneas 147-159).\n\n### Quality Gates (según execution_log.md)\n\n- ruff check: PASSED\n- ruff format: PASSED\n- pytest: 23/23 tests PASSED\n- pip-audit: No vulnerabilities\n\n### Anti-patrones Verificados\n\n- **Mock drift**: No detectado. Los tests parchean correctamente `scripts.session_close_observations`.\n- **Floor assertion**: No detectado. Las aserciones validan comportamientos específicos.\n- **Zero-logic wrapper**: No detectado. `_load_candidates()` tiene lógica propia de dispatch y logging verbose.\n\n---\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Quality gates passed. Implementation matches scope.\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779719976894,"end":1779719989572}}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779725068084,"sessionID":"ses_1a01ed930ffeq4YWsC7dZ4yqN4","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_ca00517cdc5a47899e02b49d","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Work Plan - WP-2026-137\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-137\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** code\n7: - **Titulo:** Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: Eliminar la repeticion de `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` cuando el supervisor se arranca varias veces sobre el mismo ticket, reforzando el arranque con lock atómico, verificacion de liveness e idempotencia de reconciliacion.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - El supervisor debe comportarse como una instancia unica por ticket activo.\n15: - El lock debe adquirirse de forma atomica (`O_CREAT | O_EXCL`) para evitar TOCTOU.\n16: - La reconciliacion debe ser idempotente para el mismo par `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket`.\n17: - El launcher no debe bloquear el arranque por presencia del lock; como mucho puede informar y dejar que Python decida por liveness.\n18: - Si el PID check no esta disponible, el fallback de mtime debe ser corto y conservador.\n19: - La semantica de `run_reactive()` debe distinguir claramente entre \"no hubo cambios\" y \"no se pudo adquirir lock\".\n20: - El fix se limita a supervisor, launcher y tests; no toca la memoria persistente ni el flujo de auditoria.\n21: \n22: ## Files Likely Touched\n23: - `bus/supervisor.py`\n24: - `scripts/launch_agent_terminals.ps1`\n25: - `scripts/ticket_supervisor.py`\n26: - `tests/test_supervisor.py`\n27: - `tests/test_launch_agent_terminals_script.py`\n28: \n29: ## Fases\n30: 1. Añadir el lock de arranque atomico del supervisor y definir la semantica de liberacion/liveness.\n31: 2. Hacer idempotente la emision de `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` para el mismo ticket recuperado.\n32: 3. Revisar el launcher para que no sea el mecanismo de seguridad y solo actue como capa informativa/operativa.\n33: 4. Cubrir el comportamiento con tests de arranque repetido, contencion de lock y dedupe.\n34: \n35: ## Calidad\n36: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_supervisor.py tests/test_launch_agent_terminals_script.py -q`\n37: - `ruff check bus scripts tests`\n38: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n39: \n40: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n41: - `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` no se emite mas de una vez para el mismo `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket`.\n42: - El lock se adquiere de forma atomica y no hay ventana TOCTOU.\n43: - El launcher no bloquea el sistema si el lock queda huérfano; Python resuelve la liveness.\n44: - El estado del ticket recuperado sigue siendo correcto tras arranques repetidos.\n45: - Los tests cubren el camino feliz y el path de contencion del lock.\n46: - La validacion canonica pasa sin errores.\n\n(End of file - total 46 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-137\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-137\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nEliminar la repeticion de `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` cuando el supervisor se arranca varias veces sobre el mismo ticket, reforzando el arranque con lock atómico, verificacion de liveness e idempotencia de reconciliacion.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- El supervisor debe comportarse como una instancia unica por ticket activo.\n- El lock debe adquirirse de forma atomica (`O_CREAT | O_EXCL`) para evitar TOCTOU.\n- La reconciliacion debe ser idempotente para el mismo par `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket`.\n- El launcher no debe bloquear el arranque por presencia del lock; como mucho puede informar y dejar que Python decida por liveness.\n- Si el PID check no esta disponible, el fallback de mtime debe ser corto y conservador.\n- La semantica de `run_reactive()` debe distinguir claramente entre \"no hubo cambios\" y \"no se pudo adquirir lock\".\n- El fix se limita a supervisor, launcher y tests; no toca la memoria persistente ni el flujo de auditoria.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779725068019,"end":1779725068082}},"id":"prt_e5fe1586c0013OhwYVI0n8Gcef","sessionID":"ses_1a01ed930ffeq4YWsC7dZ4yqN4","messageID":"msg_e5fe1278e001GaiMhkadHYCwk5"}}

Phase 3: revisar el launcher para evitar relanzar instancias duplicadas.\n17: - Phase 4: validar con tests de arranque repetido, lock contention y reconciliacion deduplicada.\n18: \n19: ## Registro de Implementacion\n20: \n21: ### Preparacion Canonica\n22: - `work_plan.md`: ticket aprobado para el nuevo ciclo.\n23: - `STATE.md`: estado inicial del nuevo ticket.\n24: - `TURN.md`: turno del Builder preparado.\n25: - `PLAN_WP-2026-137.md`: alcance y estrategia del ticket.\n26: - `AUDIT_WP-2026-137.md`: criterios de auditoria definidos.\n27: \n28: ### Calidad Esperada\n29: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_supervisor.py tests/test_launch_agent_terminals_script.py -q`\n30: - `ruff check bus scripts tests`\n31: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n32: \n33: ## Criterios de Aceptacion\n34: - [ ] `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` no se duplica para el mismo par de recuperacion.\n35: - [ ] El launcher respeta el lock y no levanta instancias duplicadas.\n36: - [ ] El estado del ticket recuperado queda correcto tras arranques repetidos.\n37: - [ ] Los tests cubren el camino feliz y el path de contencion.\n38: - [ ] La validacion canonica pasa sin errores.\n39: \n40: ## Evidencia de Implementacion\n41: \n42: ### Fase 1 completada - Lock atómico de supervisor\n43: - `bus/supervisor.py`: \n44:   - `_acquire_supervisor_lock()`: lock atómico con `O_CREAT | O_EXCL` para evitar TOCTOU\n45:   - `_is_supervisor_lock_stale()`: detección de locks huérfanos via PID + mtime fallback (15 min)\n46:   - `_release_supervisor_lock()`: liberación limpia con `contextlib.suppress`\n47:   - `_get_supervisor_lock_holder()`: inspección del lock holder\n48:   - `bootstrap()`: ahora adquiere lock y retorna bool (True=adquirido, False=rechazado)\n49:   - `run_reactive()`: adquiere lock via bootstrap, libera en finally\n50:   - `run_loop()`: adquiere lock via bootstrap, libera en finally\n51: - `scripts/ticket_supervisor.py`: maneja retorno de bootstrap, libera lock en --once mode\n52: \n53: ### Fase 2 completada - Idempotencia de SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED\n54: - `_has_reconciled_event()`: verifica si ya existe evento para el mismo par previous/recovered ticket\n55: - `bootstrap()`: usa `_has_reconciled_event()` antes de emitir SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED\n56: \n57: ### Fase 3 completada - Launcher no bloqueante\n58: - `scripts/launch_agent_terminals.ps1`: no verifica lock del supervisor (solo builder_lock.txt)\n59: - El supervisor maneja su propio lock internamente en Python\n60: \n61: ### Fase 4 completada - Tests añadidos\n62: - `test_supervisor_lock_acquire_atomic`: verifica adquisición atómica del lock\n63: - `test_supervisor_lock_contention`: segunda instancia no puede adquirir lock cuando primera lo tiene\n64: - `test_supervisor_lock_stale_by_mtime`: lock viejo (>15 min) se rompe y re-adquiere\n65: - `test_bootstrap_rejects_duplicate_instance`: bootstrap retorna False si otra instancia tiene el lock\n66: - `test_bootstrap_reconciled_deduplication`: SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED no se emite twice para mismo par\n67: - `test_bootstrap_reconciled_different_pair_emits`: SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED se emite para pares diferentes\n68: - `test_run_reactive_releases_lock_on_exit`: lock se libera al salir de run_reactive\n69: - `test_run_loop_releases_lock_on_exception`: lock se libera incluso con excepción\n70: \n71: ### Quality Gates\n72: - `ruff check bus scripts tests`: Passed\n73: - `ruff format bus scripts tests`: Passed  \n74: - `pytest tests/test_supervisor.py`: 57 passed\n75: - `pytest tests/test_launch_agent_terminals_script.py`: 6 passed\n76: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`: No errors\n77: \n78: ### Criterios de Aceptacion\n79: - [x] `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` no se duplica para el mismo par de recuperacion.\n80: - {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779725068132,"sessionID":"ses_1a01ed930ffeq4YWsC7dZ4yqN4","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_276e54a708484c35bd4486a5","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-137_attempt-2.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-137_attempt-2.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: Review code ticket WP-2026-137. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n4: - Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n5: - Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n6: \n7: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n8: - Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n9: \n10: --- work_plan.md ---\n11: # Work Plan - WP-2026-137\n12: \n13: ## Metadata\n14: - **ID:** WP-2026-137\n15: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n16: - **deliverable_type:** code\n17: - **Titulo:** Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n18: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n19: \n20: ## Objetivo\n21: Eliminar la repeticion de `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` cuando el supervisor se arranca varias veces sobre el mismo ticket, reforzando el arranque con lock atómico, verificacion de liveness e idempotencia de reconciliacion.\n22: \n23: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n24: - El supervisor debe comportarse como una instancia unica por ticket activo.\n25: - El lock debe adquirirse de forma atomica (`O_CREAT | O_EXCL`) para evitar TOCTOU.\n26: - La reconciliacion debe ser idempotente para el mismo par `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket`.\n27: - El launcher no debe bloquear el arranque por presencia del lock; como mucho puede informar y dejar que Python decida por liveness.\n28: - Si el PID check no esta disponible, el fallback de mtime debe ser corto y conservador.\n29: - La semantica de `run_reactive()` debe distinguir claramente entre \"no hubo cambios\" y \"no se pudo adquirir lock\".\n30: - El fix se limita a supervisor, launcher y tests; no toca la memoria persistente ni el flujo de auditoria.\n31: \n32: ## Files Likely Touched\n33: - `bus/supervisor.py`\n34: - `scripts/launch_agent_terminals.ps1`\n35: - `scripts/ticket_supervisor.py`\n36: - `tests/test_supervisor.py`\n37: - `tests/test_launch_agent_terminals_script.py`\n38: \n39: ## Fases\n40: 1. Añadir el lock de arranque atomico del supervisor y definir la semantica de liberacion/liveness.\n41: 2. Hacer idempotente la emision de `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` para el mismo ticket recuperado.\n42: 3. Revisar el launcher para que no sea el mecanismo de seguridad y solo actue como capa informativa/operativa.\n43: 4. Cubrir el comportamiento con tests de arranque repetido, contencion de lock y dedupe.\n44: \n45: ## Calidad\n46: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_supervisor.py tests/test_launch_agent_terminals_script.py -q`\n47: - `ruff check bus scripts tests`\n48: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n49: \n50: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n51: - `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` no se emite mas de una vez para el mismo `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket`.\n52: - El lock se adquiere de forma atomica y no hay ventana TOCTOU.\n53: - El launcher no bloquea el sistema si el lock queda huérfano; Python resuelve la liveness.\n54: - El estado del ticket recuperado sigue siendo correcto tras arranques repetidos.\n55: - Los tests cubren el camino feliz y el path de contencion del lock.\n56: - La validacion canonica pasa sin errores.\n57: \n58: \n59: --- STATE.md ---\n60: # State - WP-2026-137\n61: \n62: Plan Activo: WP-2026-137\n63: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n64: Rol activo: BUILDER\n65: \n66: Resumen:\n67: WP-2026-137 protege el arranque del supervisor con lock de instancia e idempotencia de reconciliacion.\n68: \n69: Notas:\n70: - El bus es la fuente canonica del ciclo y debe permanecer monotono.\n71: - `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` no debe repetirse para el mismo ticket recuperado.\n72: - Si aparece deriva hacia memoria, rubrica o pipeline de observaciones, el ticket debe escalarse aparte.\n73: \n74: \n75: \n76: --- TURN.md ---\n77: # TURNO ACTUAL\n78: \n79: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 17:58:56\n80: \n81: ---\n82: \n83: ## Agente Activo\n84: \n85: | Campo | Valor |\n86: |-------|-------|\n87: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n88: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-137 |\n89: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n90: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n91: \n92: ---\n93: \n94: ## Instruccion\n95: \n96: > Builder completo WP-2026-137. Revisa el trabajo.\n97: \n98: ---\n99: \n100: ## Archivos a Leer\n101: \n102: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n103: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n104: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n105: \n106: ---\n107: \n108: ## Estado del Sistema\n109: \n110: | Archivo | Estado |\n111: |---------|--------|\n112: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n113: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n114: \n115: ---\n116: \n117: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n118: \n119: \n120: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n121: [execution_log section for WP-2026-137 not found]\n122: \n123: --- PLAN_WP-2026-137.md ---\n124: # PLAN_WP-2026-137 - Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n125: \n126: ## Goal\n127: \n128: Stop repeated `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` emissions when the supervisor or launcher is started multiple times on the same active ticket, without making the launcher the source of truth for safety.\n129: \n130: ## Strategy\n131: \n132: 1. Add an atomic supervisor instance lock so only one bootstrap path can own the active ticket at a time.\n133: 2. Make reconciliation idempotent for the same `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket` pair.\n134: 3. Keep the launcher non-blocking: it may warn or short-circuit cosmetically, but Python owns the real liveness decision.\n135: 4. Use a short fallback TTL when PID-based liveness is unavailable.\n136: 5. Add regression tests for repeated bootstrap, stale lock handling, duplicate reconciliation prevention, and launcher non-blocking behavior.\n137: \n138: ## Scope Notes\n139: \n140: - This ticket is intentionally narrow.\n141: - It does not change the observation pipeline, review memory, or the review rubric.\n142: - Any broader launcher lifecycle rewrite should be split into a separate ticket.\n143: - Semantics for \"no changes\" vs \"lock rejected\" must be documented in code, not inferred from a bare boolean.\n144: \n145: ## Acceptance Shape\n146: \n147: - A repeated supervisor start for the same ticket does not emit duplicate `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` events.\n148: - The lock acquisition is atomic and has no TOCTOU window.\n149: - The launcher does not become the safety gate; Python handles liveness and lock rejection.\n150: - The fallback TTL for stale-lock detection is short enough to avoid hour-long dead time.\n151: - The recovered ticket remains on the correct active state.\n152: - The tests prove the issue is fixed and the lock path is safe.\n153: \n154: \n155: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-137.md ---\n156: # AUDIT_WP-2026-137 - Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n157: \n158: ## Audit Focus\n159: \n160: Verify that the supervisor startup path is single-instance, that lock acquisition is atomic, and that reconciliation is idempotent for the same recovered ticket.\n161: \n162: ## What Must Be True\n163: \n164: - Only one live supervisor instance can own the active ticket lock.\n165: - Lock acquisition uses an atomic create path, not check-then-write.\n166: - `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` is not re-emitted for the same `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket` pair.\n167: - The launcher does not enforce safety by blocking on a file presence check.\n168: - The launcher may be informational, but Python owns the real liveness and lock decision.\n169: - If PID liveness is unavailable, the stale-lock fallback is short and does not freeze the system for an hour.\n170: - Repeated bootstrap does not corrupt the ticket state.\n171: \n172: ## Failure Modes\n173: \n174: 1. A second supervisor instance starts while the first one is still active.\n175: 2. The same reconciliation payload is emitted more than once.\n176: 3. Lock acquisition has a TOCTOU window.\n177: 4. The launcher becomes the safety gate and blocks on stale locks forever.\n178: 5. A stale lock prevents a legitimate startup for too long.\n179: 6. Tests only cover the happy path and miss the duplicate-start regression.\n180: \n181: ## Review Checklist\n182: \n183: - Confirm the lock path is canonical and cleaned up safely.\n184: - Confirm the lock is acquired atomically.\n185: - Confirm reconciliation dedupe keys the same logical recovery event.\n186: - Confirm launcher behavior is non-destructive and non-blocking when the lock already exists.\n187: - Confirm tests cover repeated startup, contended startup, stale locks, and launcher informational behavior.\n188: \n189: \n190: --- git provenance ---\n191: b18884d86d265fe777e8f8edb4cbf3a7cec4a355 2026-05-25 17:56:25 +0200 fdl\n192: \n193: --- git diff (Files Likely Touched) ---\n194: diff --git a/.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-137.md b/.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-137.md\n195: new file mode 100644\n196: index 0000000..47eb3cd\n197: --- /dev/null\n198: +++ b/.agent/collaboration/AUDIT_WP-2026-137.md\n199: @@ -0,0 +1,32 @@\n200: +# AUDIT_WP-2026-137 - Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n201: +\n202: +## Audit Focus\n203: +\n204: +Verify that the supervisor startup path is single-instance, that lock acquisition is atomic, and that reconciliation is idempotent for the same recovered ticket.\n205: +\n206: +## What Must Be True\n207: +\n208: +- Only one live supervisor instance can own the active ticket lock.\n209: +- Lock acquisition uses an atomic create path, not check-then-write.\n210: +- `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` is not re-emitted for the same `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket` pair.\n211: +- The launcher does not enforce safety by blocking on a file presence check.\n212: +- The launcher may be informational, but Python owns the real liveness and lock decision.\n213: +- If PID liveness is unavailable, the stale-lock fallback is short and does not freeze the system for an hour.\n214: +- Repeated bootstrap does not corrupt the ticket state.\n215: +\n216: +## Failure Modes\n217: +\n218: +1. A second supervisor instance starts while the first one is still active.\n219: +2. The same reconciliation payload is emitted more than once.\n220: +3. Lock acquisition has a TOCTOU window.\n221: +4. The launcher becomes the safety gate and blocks on stale locks forever.\n222: +5. A stale lock prevents a legitimate startup for too long.\n223: +6. Tests only cover the happy path and miss the duplicate-start regression.\n224: +\n225: +## Review Checklist\n226: +\n227: +- Confirm the lock path is canonical and cleaned up safely.\n228: +- Confirm the lock is acquired atomically.\n229: +- Confirm reconciliation dedupe keys the same logical recovery event.\n230: +- Confirm launcher behavior is non-destructive and non-blocking when the lock already exists.\n231: +- Confirm tests cover repeated startup, contended startup, stale locks, and launcher informational behavior.\n232: diff --git a/.agent/collaboration/PLAN_WP-2026-137.md b/.agent/collaboration/PLAN_WP-2026-137.md\n233: new file mode 100644\n234: index 0000000..aeba149\n235: --- /dev/null\n236: +++ b/.agent/collaboration/PLAN_WP-2026-137.md\n237: @@ -0,0 +1,29 @@\n238: +# PLAN_WP-2026-137 - Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n239: +\n240: +## Goal\n241: +\n242: +Stop repeated `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` emissions when the supervisor or launcher is started multiple times on the same active ticket, without making the launcher the source of truth for safety.\n243: +\n244: +## Strategy\n245: +\n246: +1. Add an atomic supervisor instance lock so only one bootstrap path can own the active ticket at a time.\n247: +2. Make reconciliation idempotent for the same `previous_ticket` / `recovered_ticket` pair.\n248: +3. Keep the launcher non-blocking: it may warn or short-circuit cosmetically, but Python owns the real liveness decision.\n249: +4. Use a short fallback TTL when PID-based liveness is unavailable.\n250: +5. Add regression tests for repeated bootstrap, stale lock handling, duplicate reconciliation prevention, and launcher non-blocking behavior.\n251: +\n252: +## Scope Notes\n253: +\n254: +- This ticket is intentionally narrow.\n255: +- It does not change the observation pipeline, review memory, or the review rubric.\n256: +- Any broader launcher lifecycle rewrite should be split into a separate ticket.\n257: +- Semantics for \"no changes\" vs \"lock rejected\" must be documented in code, not inferred from a bare boolean.\n258: +\n259: +## Acceptance Shape\n260: +\n261: +- A repeated supervisor start for the same ticket does not emit duplicate `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` events.\n262: +- The lock acquisition is atomic and has no TOCTOU window.\n263: +- The launcher does not become the safety gate; Python handles liveness and lock rejection.\n264: +- The fallback TTL for stale-lock detection is short enough to avoid hour-long dead time.\n265: +- The recovered ticket remains on the correct active state.\n266: +- The tests prove the issue is fixed and the lock path is safe.\n267: diff --git a/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md b/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md\n268: index 6197530..cc6ae20 100644\n269: --- a/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md\n270: +++ b/.agent/collaboration/STATE.md\n271: @@ -1,13 +1,14 @@\n272: -# State - WP-2026-136\n273: +# State - WP-2026-137\n274:  \n275: -Plan Activo: WP-2026-136\n276: -Estado actual: COMPLETED\n277: -Rol activo: -\n278: +Plan Activo: WP-2026-137\n279: +Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n280: +Rol activo: BUILDER\n281:  \n282:  Resumen:\n283: -WP-2026-136 completado. Flag --candidates implementado con exclusion mutua real\n284: -(add_mutually_exclusive_group), load_candidates_from_file estricta (ValueError para\n285: -UTF-8 invalido), lista vacia -> exit 0. load_existing_observations endurecida.\n286: -25 tests verdes. SKILL.md actualizado.\n287: +WP-2026-137 protege el arranque del supervisor con lock de instancia e idempotencia de reconciliacion.\n288: +\n289: +Notas:\n290: +- El bus es la fuente canonica del ciclo y debe permanecer monotono.\n291: +- `SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED` no debe repetirse para el mismo ticket recuperado.\n292: +- Si aparece deriva hacia memoria, rubrica o pipeline de observaciones, el ticket debe escalarse aparte.\n293:  \n294: -Post-review human fix: strict UTF-8 decode y exit 0 para lista vacia.\n295: diff --git a/.agent/collaboration/TURN.md b/.agent/collaboration/TURN.md\n296: index a4efb28..ba3ec1a 100644\n297: --- a/.agent/collaboration/TURN.md\n298: +++ b/.agent/collaboration/TURN.md\n299: @@ -1,6 +1,6 @@\n300:  # TURNO ACTUAL\n301:  \n302: -**Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 16:37:55\n303: +**Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-25 17:17:30\n304:  \n305:  ---\n306:  \n307: @@ -8,24 +8,27 @@\n308:  \n309:  | Campo | Valor |\n310:  |-------|-------|\n311: -| **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n312: -| **Plan ID** | WP-2026-136 |\n313: +| **ROL** | **BUILDER** |\n314: +| **Plan ID** | WP-2026-137 |\n315:  | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n316: -| **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n317: +| **Accion** | IMPLEMENT |\n318:  \n319:  ---\n320:  \n321:  ## Instruccion\n322:  \n323: -> Builder completo WP-2026-136. distinguir claramente entre \"no hubo cambios\" y \"no se pudo adquirir lock\".\n463: +- El fix se limita a supervisor, launcher y tests; no toca la memoria persistente ni el flujo de auditoria.\n464:
