Add a cached loader in `ReviewBridge.__init__()` for the canonical AP inventory.\n148: 2. Refactor `_rubric_for_type()` so the AP inventory section comes from the cached IDs/names, while the detailed detection guidance stays in code and references AP IDs.\n149: 3. Keep the rubric base, dynamic learnings, and review decision contract unchanged.\n150: 4. Add tests for single-load behavior, rubric composition, and safe fallback when the inventory file is unavailable.\n151: \n152: ## Scope Notes\n153: \n154: - The canonical inventory remains in `skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md` as the canonical ID/name index.\n155: - `code-rules.md` and `review-checklist.md` stay as derived views with the detailed detection guidance.\n156: - `review_bridge.py` reads the inventory via `Path(__file__).parents[1] / \"skills\" / \"_shared\" / \"anti-patterns.md\"`, not `project_root`.\n157: - This ticket does not change the observation pipeline or the closeout pipeline.\n158: - The cache should avoid file I/O on every review prompt build.\n159: - If the inventory file is missing or unreadable, the bridge emits `warnings.warn` and omits the AP inventory section instead of raising.\n160: - Any future AP-08 extends the shared inventory first, then the derived views.\n161: \n162: ## Acceptance Shape\n163: \n164: - `ReviewBridge` reads the canonical AP inventory from file once per instance.\n165: - `_rubric_for_type()` uses the cached inventory section and keeps the detailed AP checks in code.\n166: - The Manager rubric still includes AP-01..AP-07.\n167: - The prompt remains functional if the inventory file is missing or unreadable.\n168: - Tests cover loading, caching, composition, and the safe fallback path.\n169: \n170: \n171: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-139.md ---\n172: # AUDIT_WP-2026-139 - Cached canonical anti-pattern inventory for review_bridge\n173: \n174: ## Audit Focus\n175: \n176: Verify that `review_bridge.py` loads the canonical anti-pattern inventory from `skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md` once, caches the ID/name index, and uses that cache to compose the Manager rubric while keeping the detailed detection guidance in code.\n177: \n178: ## What Must Be True\n179: \n180: - AP-01..AP-07 come from the shared canonical inventory as IDs + names.\n181: - The bridge does not re-read the inventory file for every prompt build.\n182: - The rubric composition still preserves the base review instructions, the AP detail blocks, and the dynamic learnings block.\n183: - The review prompt remains valid if the shared inventory file is unavailable.\n184: - The change does not alter the `APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` contract.\n185: - The derived views remain derived, not authoritative.\n186: - The bridge resolves the inventory path relative to `bus/review_bridge.py`, not via `project_root`.\n187: - Missing inventory files emit a warning and omit the AP inventory section.\n188: \n189: ## Failure Modes\n190: \n191: 1. The bridge still embeds the AP list inline and the shared file becomes decorative.\n192: 2. The inventory file is read on every review prompt build.\n193: 3. A missing inventory file crashes review transport.\n194: 4. The cached inventory is stale and drifts from `skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md`.\n195: 5. The rubric loses the dynamic learnings block or the review decision contract.\n196: \n197: ## Review Checklist\n198: \n199: - Confirm the AP inventory is loaded once and cached on bridge initialization.\n200: - Confirm `_rubric_for_type()` consumes the cached inventory section.\n201: - Confirm the Manager prompt still includes AP-01..AP-07.\n202: - Confirm the fallback path keeps review transport alive.\n203: - Confirm tests cover caching, composition, and missing-file safety.\n204: \n205: \n206: --- git provenance ---\n207: [no new commits since origin/main]\n208: \n209: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n210: Available skills for this role:\n211:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n212:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n213:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n214: \n215: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n216: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n217: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n218: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n219: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n220: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n221: - .session_state.json\n222: \n223: \n224: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n225: This review is advisory.  CHANGES = \"changes\"\n31:     INSPECT = \"inspect\"\n32:     UNKNOWN = \"unknown\"\n33:     TRANSPORT_FAILED = \"transport_failed\"\n34: \n35: \n36: @dataclass(slots=True)\n37: class True\n44:     transport_error: str = \"\"\n45:     parse_method: str = \"\"\n46: \n47: \n48: @dataclass(slots=True)\n49: class TicketContext:\n50:     \"\"\"Canonical ticket state ingested from the STATE_CHANGED event.\n55:     After: Returns immutable snapshot of ticket state for review transport.\n56:     \"\"\"\n57: \n58:     ticket_id: str\n59:     state: str\n60:     deliverable_type: str\n61: \n62: \n63: class TicketStateIngest:\n64:     \"\"\"Canonical ticket state ingestion from the bus.\n65: \n66:     This class is responsible for reading the current ticket state from the\n67:     canonical sources (event_bus, work_plan.md) without any transport logic.\n68: \n69:     Before: Requires event_bus and project_root.\n70:     During: Queries event_bus for STATE_CHANGED events, parses work_plan.md\n71:             for deliverable_type and active ticket_id.\n72:     After: Returns TicketContext snapshots for review transport.\n73:     \"\"\"\n74: \n75:     def __init__(self, event_bus: EventBus, project_root: Path):\n76:         self.event_bus = event_bus\n77:         self.project_root = Path(project_root)\n78: \n79:     def _read_canonical(self, name: str) -> str:\n80:         \"\"\"Read a canonical collaboration file.\"\"\"\n81:         path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n82:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else \"\"\n83: \n84:     def _read_canonical_optional(self, name: str) -> str | None:\n85:         \"\"\"Read an optional canonical collaboration file.\"\"\"\n86:         path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n87:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else None\n88: \n89:     def _get_active_ticket_id(self) -> str | None:\n90:         \"\"\"Read active ticket ID from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n91:         work_plan = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n92:         if not work_plan.exists():\n93:             return None\n94:         content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n95:         match = re.search(r\"\\*\\*ID:\\*\\*\\s*(WP-\\d{4}-\\d+)\", content)\n96:         return match.group(1) if match else None\n97: \n98:     def _read_deliverable_type(self) -> str:\n99:         \"\"\"Read the deliverable_type from work_plan.md. Defaults to 'code'.\"\"\"\n100:         work_plan = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n101:         if not work_plan.exists():\n102:             return \"code\"\n103:         try:\n104:             content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n105:             match = re.search(\n106:                 r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n107:                 content,\n108:                 re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n109:             )\n110:             if not match:\n111:                 return \"code\"\n112:             value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n113:             if \"+\" in value:\n114:                 return \"mixed\"\n115:             valid_types = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n116:             return value if value in valid_types else \"code\"\n117:         except Exception:\n118:             return \"code\"\n119: \n120:     def _latest_state(self, ticket_id: str) -> str:\n121:         \"\"\"Get the latest state from the event bus for a ticket.\"\"\"\n122:         latest = self.event_bus.latest_event(\n123:             ticket_id=ticket_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n124:         )\n125:         if latest:\n126:             return str((latest.payload or {}).get(\"to_state\", \"\")).upper()\n127:         return \"IN_PROGRESS\"\n128: \n129:     def get_ticket_context(self, ticket_id: str | None = None) -> TicketContext | None:\n130:         \"\"\"Get a canonical TicketContext for review transport.\n131: \n132:         Before: Requires ticket_id or falls back to work_plan.md active ticket.\n133:         During: Queries event_bus for state, work_plan.md for deliverable_type.\n134:         After: Returns TicketContext or None if ticket cannot be resolved.\n135:         \"\"\"\n136:         resolved_id = ticket_id or self._get_active_ticket_id()\n137:         if not resolved_id:\n138:             return None\n139:         return TicketContext(\n140:             ticket_id=resolved_id,\n141:             state=self._latest_state(resolved_id),\n142:             deliverable_type=self._read_deliverable_type(),\n143:         )\n144: \n145: \n146: class ReviewBridge:\n147:     \"\"\"Review transport bridge between bus and Manager backend.\n148: \n149:     This class handles the transport of review prompts and parsing of decisions.\n150:     It delegates state ingestion to TicketStateIngest for ReviewResult with decision and emits events to bus.\n155:     \"\"\"\n156: \n157:     def __init__(\n158:         self,\n159:         event_bus: EventBus,\n160:         project_root: Path,\n161:         skill_resolver: SkillResolver | None = None,\n162:     ):\n163:         self.event_bus = event_bus\n164:         self.project_root = Path(project_root)\n165:         self.state_ingest = TicketStateIngest(event_bus, project_root)\n166:         self.skill_resolver = skill_resolver or create_resolver(\n167:             project_root, validate=False\n168:         )\n169:         self._supports_json_format = self._detect_json_format_support()\n170:         self._canonical_anti_patterns = self._load_canonical_anti_patterns()\n171: \n172:     def _get_current_role(self) -> str:\n173:         \"\"\"Get the current active role from TURN.md.\n174: \n175:         Returns:\n176:             Role name (e.g., \"BUILDER\", \"MANAGER\") or \"BUILDER\" as fallback.\n177: \"BUILDER\"\n184: \n185:     @staticmethod\n186:     def _looks_like_opencode_help(stdout: str, stderr: str) -> bool:\n187:         \"\"\"Detect an OpenCode help banner instead of model output.\n188: model read, not as an actual help banner. Only inspect stderr\n192:         in that case, since OpenCode writes its CLI help to stderr.\n193:         \"\"\"\n194:         ndjson_signatures = ('\"type\":\"step_finish\"', '\"type\":\"step_start\"')\n195:         stdout_has_ndjson = any(sig in stdout for sig in ndjson_signatures)\n196:         candidate = (\n197:             stderr.lower() if stdout_has_ndjson else f\"{stdout}\\n{stderr}\".lower()\n198:         )\n199:         markers = (\n200:             \"opencode run [message..]\",\n201:             \"run opencode with a message\",\n202:             \"show help\",\n203:         )\n204:         return any(marker in candidate for marker in markers)\n205: \n206:     def _classify_transport_result(\n207:         self, stdout: str, stderr: str, exit_code: int\n208:     ) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n209:         \"\"\"Classify whether the OpenCode invocation reached the model.\"\"\"\n210:         if \"TimeoutExpired\" in stderr:\n211:             return True, \"timeout_retryable\"\n212:         if exit_code != 0:\n213:             return False, f\"exit_code={exit_code}\"\n214:         if self._looks_like_opencode_help(stdout, stderr):\n215:             return False, \"help_output_detected\"\n216:         return True, \"\"\n217: \n218:     def _detect_json_format_support(self) -> bool:\n219:         try:\n220:             executable = \"opencode\"\n221:             if os.name == \"nt\":\n222:                 executable = \"opencode.cmd\"\n223:             exe_full = shutil.which(executable) or executable\n224:             detect_args = [exe_full, \"run\", \"--help\"]\n225:             use_detect_shell = os.name == \"nt\"\n226:             result = subprocess.run(\n227:                 subprocess.list2cmdline(detect_args)\n228:                 if use_detect_shell\n229:                 else detect_args,\n230:                 capture_output=True,\n231:                 text=True,\n232:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n233:                 timeout=5,\n234:                 shell=use_detect_shell,\n235:             )\n236:             return \"--format\" in (result.stdout + result.stderr)\n237:         except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n238:             return False\n239: \n240:     def _review_env(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n241:         \"\"\"Return the inherited process environment for review execution.\n242: \n243:         Before: Redirected HOME, USERPROFILE, and CODEX_HOME to .codex,\n244:                 isolating the review in an artificial home directory.\n245:         During: Copies os.environ without modification, preserving the\n246:                 natural process inheritance for OpenCode and other backends.\n247:         After: Returns the unmodified environment dict for subprocess execution.\n248:         \"\"\"\n249:         # WP-2026-129: Preserve inherited environment; do not redirect home vars\n250:         return os.environ.copy()\n251: \n252:     def _get_manager_backend(self) -> str:\n253:         \"\"\"Get the backend assigned to MANAGER role from agents.json.\n254: \n255:         Before: Fell back to a legacy backend when agents.json was unreadable.\n256:         During: Reads agents.json and returns the MANAGER backend assignment.\n257:         After: Returns \"opencode\" as the default fallback for the Manager.\n258:         \"\"\"\n259:         try:\n260:             from agents_config import get_backend_for_role, load_agents_config\n261: \n262:             config = load_agents_config(self.project_root)\n263:             return get_backend_for_role(\"MANAGER\", config)\n264:         except Exception:\n265:             # WP-2026-129: Fallback to opencode for the Manager\n266:             return \"opencode\"\n267: \n268:     def _get_manager_model(self) -> str | None:\n269:         \"\"\"Get the model override for MANAGER from role_models.\"\"\"\n270:         try:\n271:             from agents_config import get_model_for_role, load_agents_config\n272: \n273:             config = load_agents_config(self.project_root)\n274:             return get_model_for_role(\"MANAGER\", config)\n275:         except Exception:\n276:             return None\n277: \n278:     def _get_canonical_files(self) -> list[Path]:\n279:         \"\"\"Get list of canonical collaboration files to attach to OpenCode review.\"\"\"\n280:         collaboration_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n281:         files = [\n282:             collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\",\n283:             collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\",\n284:             collaboration_dir / \"TURN.md\",\n285:             collaboration_dir / \"STATE.md\",\n286:         ]\n287:         ticket_id = self.state_ingest._get_active_ticket_id()\n288:         if ticket_id:\n289:             plan_file = collaboration_dir / f\"PLAN_{ticket_id}.md\"\n290:             audit_file = collaboration_dir / f\"AUDIT_{ticket_id}.md\"\n291:             if plan_file.exists():\n292:                 files.append(plan_file)\n293:             if audit_file.exists():\n294:                 files.append(audit_file)\n295:         return [f for f in files if f.exists()]\n296: \n297:     def _get_active_ticket_id(self) -> str | None:\n298:         \"\"\"Read active ticket ID from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n299:         work_plan = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n300:         if not work_plan.exists():\n301:             return None\n302:         content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n303:         # Look for **- ID:** WP-XXXX-XXX pattern\n304:         match = re.search(r\"\\*\\*ID:\\*\\*\\s*(WP-\\d{4}-\\d+)\", content)\n305:         if match:\n306:             return match.group(1)\n307:         return None\n308: \n309:     def _read_deliverable_type(self) -> str:\n310:         \"\"\"Read the deliverable_type from work_plan.md. Defaults to 'code'.\"\"\"\n311:         work_plan = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n312:         if not work_plan.exists():\n313:             return \"code\"\n314:         try:\n315:             content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n316:             match = re.search(\n317:                 r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n318:                 content,\n319:                 re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n320:             )\n321:             if not match:\n322:                 return \"code\"\n323:             value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n324:             if \"+\" in value:\n325:                 return \"mixed\"\n326:             valid_types = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n327:             if value not in valid_types:\n328:                 return \"code\"\n329:             return value\n330:         except Exception:\n331:             return \"code\"\n332: \n333:     def _read_canonical(self, name: str) -> str:\n334:         path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n335:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else \"\"\n336: \n337:     def _read_canonical_optional(self, name: str) -> str | None:\n338:         path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n339:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else None\n340: \n341:     def _extract_ticket_section(self, ticket_id: str) -> str:\n342:         content = self.state_ingest._read_canonical(\"execution_log.md\")\n343:         pattern = rf\"### {re.escape(ticket_id)}.*?(?=\\n### WP-|\\Z)\"\n344:         match = re.search(pattern, content, re.DOTALL)\n345:         return (\n346:             match.group(0)\n347:             if match\n348:             else f\"[execution_log section for {ticket_id} not found]\"\n349:         )\n350: \n351:     def _git_diff_stat(self) -> str:\n352:         try:\n353:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n354:             result = subprocess.run(\n355:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"--stat\", \"origin/main...HEAD\"],\n356:                 capture_output=True,\n357:                 text=True,\n358:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n359:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n360:                 timeout=10,\n361:             )\n362:             if result.returncode == 0:\n363:                 return result.stdout or \"[git diff --stat empty]\"\n364:             result = subprocess.run(\n365:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"--stat\", \"HEAD\"],\n366:                 capture_output=True,\n367:                 text=True,\n368:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n369:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n370:                 timeout=10,\n371:             )\n372:             return \"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable, using HEAD fallback]\\n\" + (\n373:                 result.stdout or \"[git diff --stat empty]\"\n374:             )\n375:         except Exception as e:\n376:             return f\"[Error fetching git diff --stat: {e}]\"\n377: \n378:     def _build_diff_for_files_likely_touched(\n379:         self, ticket_id: str, budget_bytes: int\n380:     ) -> str:\n381:         try:\n382:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n383:             result = subprocess.run(\n384:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"origin/main...HEAD\"],\n385:                 capture_output=True,\n386:                 text=True,\n387:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n388:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n389:                 timeout=15,\n390:             )\n391:             warning_prefix = \"\"\n392:             if result.returncode != 0:\n393:                 result = subprocess.run(\n394:                     [git_bin, \"diff\", \"HEAD\"],\n395:                     capture_output=True,\n396:                     text=True,\n397:                     encoding=\"utf-8\",\n398:                     cwd=self.project_root,\n399:                     timeout=15,\n400:                 )\n401:                 warning_prefix = \"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable, using git diff HEAD fallback]\\n\"\n402:             diff = warning_prefix + (result.stdout or \"\")\n403:             diff_bytes = diff.encode(\"utf-8\")\n404:             if len(diff_bytes) <= budget_bytes:\n405:                 return diff\n406:             truncated = diff_bytes[:budget_bytes].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n407:             return truncated + \"\\n\\n[diff truncado por budget]\"\n408:         except Exception as e:\n409:             return f\"[Error fetching git diff: {e}]\"\n410: \n411:     def _git_provenance(self) -> str:\n412:         try:\n413:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n414:             result = subprocess.run(\n415:                 [\n416:                     git_bin,\n417:                     \"log\",\n418:                     \"origin/main..HEAD\",\n419:                     \"--format=%H %ai %an\",\n420:                     \"--no-merges\",\n421:                     \"-1\",\n422:                 ],\n423:                 capture_output=True,\n424:                 text=True,\n425:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n426:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n427:                 timeout=10,\n428:             )\n429:             if result.returncode == 0:\n430:                 return result.stdout.strip() or \"[no new commits since origin/main]\"\n431:             # Remote genuinely unreachable — fall back to latest HEAD commit\n432:             result = subprocess.run(\n433:                 [git_bin, \"log\", \"HEAD\", \"--format=%H %ai %an\", \"--no-merges\", \"-1\"],\n434:                 capture_output=True,\n435:                 text=True,\n436:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n437:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n438:                 timeout=10,\n439:             )\n440:             line = result.stdout.strip() or \"[no commits found]\"\n441:             return f\"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable] {line}\"\n442:         except Exception as e:\n443:             return f\"[Error fetching git provenance: {e}]\"\n444: \n445:     def _observations_path(self) -> Path:\n446:         return (\n447:             self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"memory\" / \"observations.jsonl\"\n448:         )\n449: \n450:     def _canonical_anti_patterns_path(self) -> Path:\n451:         return (\n452:             Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n453:             / \"skills\"\n454:             / \"_shared\"\n455:             / \"anti-patterns.md\"\n456:         )\n457: \n458:     @staticmethod\n459:     def _parse_canonical_anti_patterns(content: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n460:         inventory: list[tuple[str, str]] = []\n461:         pattern = re.compile(r\"^##\\s+(AP-\\d{2})\\s*-\\s*(.+?)\\s*$\")\n462:         for raw_line in content.splitlines():\n463:             match = pattern.match(raw_line.strip())\n464:             if not match:\n465:                 continue\n466:             inventory.append((match.group(1), match.group(2).strip()))\n467:         return inventory\n468: \n469:     def _load_canonical_anti_patterns(self) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n470:         path = self._canonical_anti_patterns_path()\n471:         try:\n472:             content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n473:         except OSError as exc:\n474:             warnings.warn(\n475:                 f\"Canonical anti-pattern inventory unavailable at {path}: {exc}\",\n476:                 RuntimeWarning,\n477:                 stacklevel=2,\n478:             )\n479:             return []\n480: \n481:         inventory = self._parse_canonical_anti_patterns(content)\n482:         if not inventory:\n483:             warnings.warn(\n484:                 f\"Canonical anti-pattern inventory at {path} is empty or invalid.\",\n485:                 RuntimeWarning,\n486:                 stacklevel=2,\n487:             )\n488:         return inventory\n489: \n490:     def _render_canonical_anti_pattern_inventory(self) -> str:\n491:         if not self._canonical_anti_patterns:\n492:             return \"\"\n493:         lines = [\n494:             \"Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\"\n495:         ]\n496:         for ap_id, ap_name in self._canonical_anti_patterns:\n497:             lines.append(f\"- {ap_id} {ap_name}\")\n498:         return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n499: \n500:     @staticmethod\n501:     def _parse_observation_timestamp(raw_timestamp: object) -> datetime:\n502:         if isinstance(raw_timestamp, str):\n503:             stamp = raw_timestamp.strip().replace(\"Z\", \"+00:00\")\n504:             try:\n505:                 parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(stamp)\n506:                 return (\n507:                     parsed\n508:                     if parsed.tzinfo is not None\n509:                     else parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n510:                 )\n511:             except ValueError:\n512:                 pass\n513:         return datetime.min.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n514: \n515:     @staticmethod\n516:     def _truncate_observation_signal(signal: object) -> str:\n517:         text = str(signal or \"\").strip()\n518:         if len(text) <= MAX_OBSERVATION_SIGNAL_CHARS:\n519:             return text\n520:         return text[: MAX_OBSERVATION_SIGNAL_CHARS - 3].rstrip() + \"...\"\n521: \n522:     @staticmethod\n523:     def _observation_matches_dtype(record: dict, dtype: str) -> bool:\n524:         \"\"\"Return True if the observation applies to the given deliverable_type.\"\"\"\n525:         applies_to = record.get(\"applies_to\")\n526:         if applies_to is None or applies_to == \"all\":\n527:             return True\n528:         targets = applies_to if isinstance(applies_to, list) else [applies_to]\n529:         return dtype in targets or \"all\" in targets\n530: \n531:     def _load_manager_review_observations(\n532:         self, dtype: str = \"all\"\n533:     ) -> list[tuple[datetime, str, str]]:\n534:         \"\"\"Load manager-review-rubric observations filtered by deliverable_type scope.\n535: \n536:         Absent applies_to = all types (legacy compat). \"all\" = all types.\n537:         List or string value = include only when dtype matches.\n538:         \"\"\"\n539:         path = self._observations_path()\n540:         if not path.exists():\n541:             return []\n542:         try:\n543:             lines = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\").splitlines()\n544:         except Exception:\n545:             return []\n546: \n547:         observations: list[tuple[datetime, str, str]] = []\n548:         for raw_line in lines:\n549:             line = raw_line.strip()\n550:             if not line:\n551:                 continue\n552:             try:\n553:                 record = json.loads(line)\n554:             except json.JSONDecodeError:\n555:                 continue\n556:             if not isinstance(record, dict):\n557:                 continue\n558:             if record.get(\"topic\") != \"manager-review-rubric\":\n559:                 continue\n560:             if not self._observation_matches_dtype(record, dtype):\n561:                 continue\n562:             signal = self._truncate_observation_signal(record.get(\"signal\", \"\"))\n563:             if not signal:\n564:                 continue\n565:             timestamp = self._parse_observation_timestamp(record.get(\"timestamp\"))\n566:             source_ticket = str(record.get(\"source_ticket\", \"\")).strip() or \"unknown\"\n567:             observations.append((timestamp, signal, source_ticket))\n568: \n569:         observations.sort(key=lambda item: item[0], reverse=True)\n570:         return observations[:MAX_RUBRIC_OBSERVATIONS]\n571: \n572:     def _render_manager_review_learnings(self, dtype: str = \"all\") -> str:\n573:         observations = self._load_manager_review_observations(dtype=dtype)\n574:         if not observations:\n575:             return \"\"\n576: \n577:         lines = [\"Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\"]\n578:         for timestamp, signal, source_ticket in observations:\n579:             date = timestamp.astimezone(timezone.utc).date().isoformat()\n580:             lines.append(f\"- [{date}] {signal} ({source_ticket})\")\n581:         return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n582: \n583:     def _rubric_for_type(self, dtype: str, ticket_id: str) -> str:\n584:         scaffolding_precheck = (\n585:             \"AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are \"\n586:             \"structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, \"\n587:             \"config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — \"\n588:             \"the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. \"\n589:             \"Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error \"\n590:             \"(SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\"\n591:         )\n592:         canonical_anti_patterns = self._render_canonical_anti_pattern_inventory()\n593:         canonical_anti_patterns_block = (\n594:             f\"{canonical_anti_patterns}\\n\\n\" if canonical_anti_patterns else \"\"\n595:         )\n596:         if dtype == \"code\":\n597:             return (\n598:                 f\"Review code ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n599:                 f\"Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. \"\n600:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\\n\\n\"\n601:                 f\"{scaffolding_precheck}\\n\\n\"\n602:                 f\"{canonical_anti_patterns_block}\"\n603:                 f\"Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\\n\"\n604:                 f\"- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls \"\n605:                 f\"(e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\\n\"\n606:                 f\"- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists \"\n607:                 f\"without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if \"\n608:                 f\"the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\\n\\n\"\n609:                 f\"Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\\n\"\n610:                 f\"- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate \"\n611:                 f\"call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\\n\"\n612:                 f\"- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces \"\n613:                 f\"exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file \"\n614:                 f\"creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or \"\n615:                 f\"called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify \"\n616:                 f\"that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\\n\"\n617:                 f\"- AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes \"\n618:                 f\"a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every \"\n619:                 f\"caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, \"\n620:                 f\"`if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently \"\n621:                 f\"breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. \"\n622:                 f\"Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\"\n623:             )\n624:         elif dtype == \"mixed\":\n625:             return (\n626:                 f\"Review mixed ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n627:                 f\"Verify code correctness, tests, and style guides, and additionally verify \"\n628:                 f\"that all declared non-code deliverables exist, are well-structured, and are fully complete. \"\n629:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\\n\\n\"\n630:                 f\"{scaffolding_precheck}\\n\\n\"\n631:                 f\"{canonical_anti_patterns_block}\"\n632:                 f\"Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\\n\"\n633:                 f\"- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls.\\n\"\n634:                 f\"- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that \"\n635:                 f\"exists without the tested feature.\\n\\n\"\n636:                 f\"Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\\n\"\n637:                 f\"- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate \"\n638:                 f\"call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\\n\"\n639:                 f\"- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces \"\n640:                 f\"exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file \"\n641:                 f\"creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or \"\n642:                 f\"called twice on the same instance, verify that an explicit reentrancy guard exists. \"\n643:                 f\"Without it: BLOCKER.\\n\"\n644:                 f\"- AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes \"\n645:                 f\"a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify all callers use \"\n646:                 f\"`is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`). \"\n647:                 f\"Any caller still using generic truthiness after the change: BLOCKER.\"\n648:             )\n649:         else:  # documentation, research, analysis\n650:             return (\n651:                 f\"Review non-code {dtype} ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n652:                 f\"Since this is a non-code deliverable, focus strictly on the clarity, depth, correctness, \"\n653:                 f\"structure, and completeness of the requested document deliverables. \"\n654:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n655:             )\n656: \n657:     def _build_review_prompt(self, ticket_id: str, dtype: str) -> str:\n658:         # P1-2: work_plan, STATE, TURN (intocables)\n659:         sections = [\n660:             (name, self.state_ingest._read_canonical(name))\n661:             for name in (\"work_plan.md\", \"STATE.md\", \"TURN.md\")\n662:         ]\n663: \n664:         # P3: execution_log section\n665:         sections.append(\n666:             (\n667:                 \"execution_log.md (ticket section)\",\n668:                 self._extract_ticket_section(ticket_id),\n669:             )\n670:         )\n671: \n672:         # P4: PLAN + AUDIT opcionales\n673:         for name in (f\"PLAN_{ticket_id}.md\", f\"AUDIT_{ticket_id}.md\"):\n674:             content = self.state_ingest._read_canonical_optional(name)\n675:             if content is not None:\n676:                 sections.append((name, content))\n677: \n678:         # Calculate used bytes\n679:         used = sum(len(c.encode(\"utf-8\")) for _, c in sections)\n680: \n681:         hard_cap_bytes = 80 * 1024\n682:         canonical_budget = 60 * 1024\n683: \n684:         # P4.5: git provenance (compact, inserted before diff)\n685:         provenance = self._git_provenance()\n686:         sections.append((\"git provenance\", provenance))\n687:         used += len(provenance.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n688: \n689:         # P5: diff con budget restante\n690:         if used < canonical_budget:\n691:             remaining = hard_cap_bytes - used\n692:             diff = self._build_diff_for_files_likely_touched(ticket_id, remaining)\n693:             if diff:\n694:                 sections.append((\"git diff (Files Likely Touched)\", diff))\n695:         else:\n696:             sections.append(\n697:                 (\n698:                     \"git diff --stat\",\n699:                     self._git_diff_stat()\n700:                     + \"\\n[diff omitido por budget. Read files directly if needed.]\",\n701:                 )\n702:             )\n703: \n704:         # Compose\n705:         parts = [self._rubric_for_type(dtype, ticket_id)]\n706: \n707:         # Cross-cutting: validator evidence gate (all types)\n708:         parts.append(\n709:             \"\\n--- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\\n\"\n710:             \"AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a \"\n711:             \"quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), \"\n712:             \"execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and \"\n713:             \"(3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', \"\n714:             \"'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\"\n715:         )\n716: \n717:         # Dynamic learnings — all types, scoped by dtype\n718:         learnings = self._render_manager_review_learnings(dtype=dtype)\n719:         if learnings:\n720:             parts.append(f\"\\n--- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\\n{learnings}\")\n721:         for name, content in sections:\n722:             parts.append(f\"\\n--- {name} ---\\n{content}\")\n723: \n724:         # WP-2026-127: Add skill filtering by role\n725:         current_role = self._get_current_role()\n726:         allowed_skills = self.skill_resolver.filter_skills_for_prompt(\n727:             current_role, include_metadata=False\n728:         )\n729:         parts.append(\n730:             f\"\\n--- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE {current_role} ---\\n{allowed_skills}\"\n731:         )\n732: \n733:         parts.append(\n734:             \"\\n--- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\\n\"\n735:             \"Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\\n\"\n736:             \"Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\\n\"\n737:             \"- PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\\n\"\n738:             \"- review_queue.md, notifications.md\\n\"\n739:             \"- archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\\n\"\n740:             \"- .session_state.json\\n\"\n741:         )\n742:         parts.append(\n743:             \"\\n--- INSTRUCTIONS ---\\n\"\n744:             \"This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\\n\\n\"\n745:             \"Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion \"\n746:             \"are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed \"\n747:             \"by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\\n\\n\"\n748:             \"If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\\n\"\n749:             \"DECISION: APPROVE\\n\\n\"\n750:             \"If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure \"\n751:             \"(headings included) before the final line:\\n\\n\"\n752:             \"## SUMMARY\\n\"\n753:             \"<one line: why the ticket is not approved>\\n\\n\"\n754:             \"## BLOCKERS\\n\"\n755:             \"- <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\\n\\n\"\n756:             \"## SUGGESTIONS\\n\"\n757:             \"- <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\\n\\n\"\n758:             \"DECISION: CHANGES\\n\\n\"\n759:             \"If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\\n\"\n760:             \"DECISION: INSPECT\\n\"\n761:         )\n762:         return \"\\n\".join(parts)\n763: \n764:     def _run_legacy_manager_review(\n765:         self,\n766:         *,\n767:         ticket_id: str,\n768:         manager_executable: Path,\n769:         timeout_seconds: int,\n770:     ) -> tuple[str, str, int]:\n771:         \"\"\"Legacy Manager review route. Preserved for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n772:         exe_str = str(manager_executable)\n773:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_str.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n774:             cmd_candidate = Path(exe_str).with_suffix(\".cmd\")\n775:             bat_candidate = Path(exe_str).with_suffix(\".bat\")\n776:             if cmd_candidate.exists():\n777:                 exe_str = str(cmd_candidate)\n778:             elif bat_candidate.exists():\n779:                 exe_str = str(bat_candidate)\n780: \n781:         command = [exe_str, \"review\", ticket_id]\n782:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_str.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n783:             command = [\n784:                 \"powershell.exe\",\n785:                 \"-NoProfile\",\n786:                 \"-ExecutionPolicy\",\n787:                 \"Bypass\",\n788:                 \"-File\",\n789:                 *command,\n790:             ]\n791: \n792:         result = subprocess.run(\n793:             command,\n794:             capture_output=True,\n795:             text=True,\n796:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n797:             cwd=self.project_root,\n798:             env=self._review_env(),\n799:             timeout=timeout_seconds,\n800:         )\n801:         return result.stdout or \"\", result.stderr or \"\", result.returncode\n802: \n803:     def _run_opencode_review(  # noqa: C901\n804:         self,\n805:         *,\n806:         ticket_id: str,\n807:         prompt: str,\n808:         attempt: int = 1,\n809:         manager_executable: Path | None = None,\n810:         timeout_seconds: int,\n811:     ) -> tuple[str, str, int]:\n812:         \"\"\"OpenCode review route using manager agent spec with context prompt.\n813: \n814:         Transporte: el contexto completo se escribe a un review packet en\n815:         .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md y el prompt\n816:         posicional es corto, indicando al Manager que lo lea. Evita el flag\n817:         --file (array que consume el mensaje) y el limite de longitud de la\n818:         CLI.\n819:         \"\"\"\n820:         model = self._get_manager_model()\n821: \n822:         executable = str(manager_executable) if manager_executable else \"opencode\"\n823:         if os.name == \"nt\" and executable == \"opencode\":\n824:             executable = \"opencode.cmd\"\n825: \n826:         exe_full = shutil.which(executable) or executable\n827:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_full.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n828:             cmd_candidate = Path(exe_full).with_suffix(\".cmd\")\n829:             bat_candidate = Path(exe_full).with_suffix(\".bat\")\n830:             if cmd_candidate.exists():\n831:                 exe_full = str(cmd_candidate)\n832:             elif bat_candidate.exists():\n833:                 exe_full = str(bat_candidate)\n834: \n835:         # Review packet: el contexto completo del review se escribe a un\n836:         # archivo dentro del repo; el Manager lo lee con sus herramientas.\n837:         # El prompt posicional es corto (sin metacaracteres de cmd.exe).\n838:         packet_path = self._get_review_packet_path(ticket_id, attempt)\n839:         packet_path.write_text(prompt, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n840:         packet_rel = packet_path.relative_to(self.project_root).as_posix()\n841: \n842:         review_message = (\n843:             f\"Revisa {ticket_id} leyendo el archivo {packet_rel} con tus \"\n844:             \"herramientas de lectura. Termina con exactamente \"\n845:             \"DECISION: APPROVE o DECISION: CHANGES.\"\n846:         )\n847: \n848:         if not review_message.isascii():\n849:             raise ValueError(\n850:                 \"Review message must be ASCII for Windows command transport.\"\n851:             )\n852: \n853:         cmd_args = [exe_full, \"run\", \"--agent\", \"manager\"]\n854:         if model:\n855:             cmd_args.extend([\"--model\", model])\n856:         if self._supports_json_format:\n857:             cmd_args.extend([\"--format\", \"json\"])\n858:         cmd_args.append(review_message)\n859: \n860:         use_shell = False\n861:         if os.name == \"nt\" and (\n862:             exe_full.lower().endswith(\".cmd\")\n863:             or exe_full.lower().endswith(\".bat\")\n864:             or \"opencode\" in exe_full.lower()\n865:         ):\n866:             use_shell = True\n867: \n868:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_full.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n869:             cmd_args = [\n870:                 \"powershell.exe\",\n871:                 \"-NoProfile\",\n872:                 \"-ExecutionPolicy\",\n873:                 \"Bypass\",\n874:                 \"-File\",\n875:                 *cmd_args,\n876:             ]\n877: \n878:         try:\n879:             # En Windows, shell=True con una lista hace que cmd.exe trate\n880:             # args[1:] como argumentos del propio shell: opencode arrancaria\n881:             # sin ninguno. list2cmdline produce el string correcto.\n882:             run_args = subprocess.list2cmdline(cmd_args) if use_shell else cmd_args\n883:             result = subprocess.run(\n884:                 run_args,\n885:                 capture_output=True,\n886:                 text=True,\n887:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n888:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n889:                 timeout=timeout_seconds,\n890:                 shell=use_shell,\n891:             )\n892:         except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError, OSError) as exc:\n893:             err_msg = f\"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}\"\n894:             if isinstance(exc, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):\n895:                 err_msg = f\"TimeoutExpired: {exc}\"\n896:             return \"\", err_msg, 1\n897: \n898:         return result.stdout or \"\", result.stderr or \"\", result.returncode\n899: \n900:     def _load_review_config(self) -> dict:\n901:         \"\"\"Load review configuration from agents.json with WP-2026-106 defaults.\n902: \n903:         Before: max_attempts was 2 (legacy default).\n904:         During: Reads agents.json manager_review section, applies WP-2026-106 threshold of 5.\n905:         After: Returns config dict with max_attempts=5 for HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n906:         \"\"\"\n907:         config_path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n908:         defaults = {\n909:             \"timeout_seconds\": 180,\n910:             \"max_attempts\": 5,  # WP-2026-106: elevated from 3 to 5 for HUMAN_GATE\n911:             \"retry_backoff_multiplier\": 2.0,\n912:         }\n913:         if not config_path.exists():\n914:             return defaults\n915:         try:\n916:             data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n917:             cfg = data.get(\"manager_review\", {})\n918:             return {\n919:                 \"timeout_seconds\": int(\n920:                     cfg.get(\"timeout_seconds\", defaults[\"timeout_seconds\"])\n921:                 ),\n922:                 \"max_attempts\": int(cfg.get(\"max_attempts\", defaults[\"max_attempts\"])),\n923:                 \"retry_backoff_multiplier\": float(\n924:                     cfg.get(\n925:                         \"retry_backoff_multiplier\", defaults[\"retry_backoff_multiplier\"]\n926:                     )\n927:                 ),\n928:             }\n929:         except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, TypeError):\n930:             return defaults\n931: \n932:     def _get_review_log_path(self, ticket_id: str) -> Path:\n933:         \"\"\"Get the review log directory for a ticket.\n934: \n935:         Before: Requires ticket_id string.\n936:         During: Creates directory structure under .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/.\n937:         After: Returns Path object for the ticket's review log directory.\n938:         \"\"\"\n939:         reviews_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"reviews\"\n940:         ticket_dir = reviews_dir / ticket_id\n941:         ticket_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n942:         return ticket_dir\n943: \n944:     def _get_review_packet_path(self, ticket_id: str, attempt: int) -> Path:\n945:         \"\"\"Get the canonical review packet path for a ticket attempt.\"\"\"\n946:         packets_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"review_packets\"\n947:         packets_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n948:         return packets_dir / f\"{ticket_id}_attempt-{attempt}.md\"\n949: \n950:     def _persist_review_attempt(\n951:         self,\n952:         ticket_id: str,\n953:         attempt: int,\n954:         stdout: str,\n955:         stderr: str,\n956:         decision: ReviewDecision,\n957:         review_packet_path: Path | None = None,\n958:         parse_method: str = \"\",\n959:         transport_ok: bool = True,\n960:         transport_error: str = \"\",\n961:     ) -> Path:\n962:         \"\"\"Persist a review attempt to attempt-N.md idempotently.\n963: \n964:         Before: Requires ticket_id, attempt number, stdout, stderr, and decision.\n965:         During: Writes review content to .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/attempt-N.md,\n966:                 overwriting any existing file with the same attempt number.\n967:         After: Returns the Path to the persisted review file.\n968:         \"\"\"\n969:         ticket_dir = self._get_review_log_path(ticket_id)\n970:         attempt_file = ticket_dir / f\"attempt-{attempt}.md\"\n971: \n972:         # Build structured review content\n973:         content_parts = [\n974:             f\"# Review Attempt {attempt}\",\n975:             \"\",\n976:             f\"## Ticket: {ticket_id}\",\n977:             f\"## Decision: {decision.value.upper()}\",\n978:             f\"## Parse Method: {parse_method or 'unknown'}\",\n979:             f\"## Transport OK: {transport_ok}\",\n980:             \"\",\n981:             \"## Review Packet\",\n982:             \"\",\n983:             str(review_packet_path.relative_to(self.project_root).as_posix())\n984:             if review_packet_path is not None\n985:             else \"[not recorded]\",\n986:             \"\",\n987:             \"## Transport Error\",\n988:             \"\",\n989:             transport_error or \"[none]\",\n990:             \"\",\n991:             \"## STDOUT\",\n992:             \"\",\n993:             stdout or \"[empty]\",\n994:             \"\",\n995:             \"## STDERR\",\n996:             \"\",\n997:             stderr or \"[empty]\",\n998:             \"\",\n999:         ]\n1000: \n1001:         # Parse and add structured sections for CHANGES decisions\n1002:         if decision == ReviewDecision.CHANGES:\n1003:             structured = self._parse_changes_structure(stdout)\n1004:             content_parts.extend(\n1005:                 [\n1006:                     \"\",\n1007:                     \"## SUMMARY\",\n1008:                     \"\",\n1009:                     structured.get(\"summary\", \"[no summary provided]\"),\n1010:                     \"\",\n1011:                     \"## BLOCKERS\",\n1012:                     \"\",\n1013:                     structured.get(\"blockers\", \"[no blockers provided]\"),\n1014:                     \"\",\n1015:                     \"## SUGGESTIONS\",\n1016:                     \"\",\n1017:                     structured.get(\"suggestions\", \"[no suggestions provided]\"),\n1018:                     \"\",\n1019:                 ]\n1020:             )\n1021: \n1022:         attempt_file.write_text(\"\\n\".join(content_parts), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1023:         return attempt_file\n1024: \n1025:     def _parse_changes_structure(self, stdout: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n1026:         \"\"\"Parse structured sections from CHANGES review output.\n1027: \n1028:         Before: Requires stdout string from Manager review.\n1029:         During: Extracts SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS sections using regex patterns.\n1030:         After: Returns dict with 'summary', 'blockers', 'suggestions' keys (defaults if not found).\n1031:         \"\"\"\n1032:         result = {\"summary\": \"\", \"blockers\": \"\", \"suggestions\": \"\"}\n1033: \n1034:         # Pattern for ## SUMMARY section\n1035:         summary_match = re.search(\n1036:             r\"##\\s*SUMMARY\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:BLOCKERS|SUGGESTIONS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n1037:             stdout,\n1038:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n1039:         )\n1040:         if summary_match:\n1041:             result[\"summary\"] = summary_match.group(1).strip()\n1042: \n1043:         # Pattern for ## BLOCKERS section\n1044:         blockers_match = re.search(\n1045:             r\"##\\s*BLOCKERS\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:SUMMARY|SUGGESTIONS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n1046:             stdout,\n1047:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n1048:         )\n1049:         if blockers_match:\n1050:             result[\"blockers\"] = blockers_match.group(1).strip()\n1051: \n1052:         # Pattern for ## SUGGESTIONS section\n1053:         suggestions_match = re.search(\n1054:             r\"##\\s*SUGGESTIONS\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:SUMMARY|BLOCKERS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n1055:             stdout,\n1056:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n1057:         )\n1058:         if suggestions_match:\n1059:             result[\"suggestions\"] = suggestions_match.group(1).strip()\n1060: \n1061:         return result\n1062: \n1063:     def _validate_changes_structure(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:\n1064:         \"\"\"Validate that CHANGES response has required structure.\n1065: \n1066:         Before: Requires stdout string from Manager review.\n1067:         During: Checks for presence of SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS, and DECISION: CHANGES.\n1068:         After: Returns (is_valid, missing_sections) tuple.\n1069:         \"\"\"\n1070:         missing = []\n1071:         stdout_upper = stdout.upper()\n1072: \n1073:         # Require the exact heading form, not a loose word in prose, so a review\n1074:         # that merely mentions \"summary\" cannot pass structure validation.\n1075:         if \"## SUMMARY\" not in stdout_upper:\n1076:             missing.append(\"SUMMARY\")\n1077:         if \"## BLOCKERS\" not in stdout_upper:\n1078:             missing.append(\"BLOCKERS\")\n1079:         if \"## SUGGESTIONS\" not in stdout_upper:\n1080:             missing.append(\"SUGGESTIONS\")\n1081:         if \"DECISION: CHANGES\" not in stdout_upper:\n1082:             missing.append(\"DECISION: CHANGES\")\n1083: \n1084:         return len(missing) == 0, missing\n1085: \n1086:     def _generate_human_review_report(\n1087:         self,\n1088:         ticket_id: str,\n1089:         review_attempts: list[dict],\n1090:         last_decision: ReviewDecision,\n1091:     ) -> Path:\n1092:         \"\"\"Generate human_review_report.md from template at 5th rejection.\n1093: \n1094:         Before: Requires ticket_id, list of review attempt payloads, and last decision.\n1095:         During: Reads template from .agent/templates/human_review_report.md,\n1096:                 fills placeholders with consolidated review data,\n1097:                 writes to .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/human_review_report.md.\n1098:         After: Returns Path to generated report file.\n1099:         \"\"\"\n1100:         template_path = (\n1101:             self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"templates\" / \"human_review_report.md\"\n1102:         )\n1103: \n1104:         # Load template\n1105:         if template_path.exists():\n1106:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1107:         else:\n1108:             # Fallback inline template\n1109:             template = \"\"\"# Human Review Report\n1110: \n1111: ## Ticket\n1112: - **Ticket ID:** {{ticket_id}}\n1113: - **Generated at:** {{generated_at}}\n1114: - **Review attempts:** {{review_attempt_count}}\n1115: \n1116: ## Summary\n1117: {{summary}}\n1118: \n1119: ## Decision Context\n1120: - **Last decision:** {{last_decision}}\n1121: - **Escalation reason:** {{escalation_reason}}\n1122: \n1123: ## Consolidated Blockers\n1124: {{blockers}}\n1125: \n1126: ## Notes\n1127: {{notes}}\n1128: \"\"\"\n1129: \n1130:         # Consolidate blockers from all attempts\n1131:         all_blockers = []\n1132:         for attempt in review_attempts:\n1133:             payload = attempt.get(\"payload\", {})\n1134:             blockers = payload.get(\"blockers\", \"\")\n1135:             if blockers:\n1136:                 all_blockers.append(\n1137:                     f\"### Attempt {attempt.get('attempt', '?')}\\n{blockers}\"\n1138:                 )\n1139: \n1140:         # Build summary\n1141:         summary_lines = [\n1142:             f\"Ticket {ticket_id} reached HUMAN_GATE after {len(review_attempts)} consecutive CHANGES decisions.\",\n1143:             \"\",\n1144:             \"## Review History\",\n1145:             \"\",\n1146:         ]\n1147:         for attempt in review_attempts:\n1148:             payload = attempt.get(\"payload\", {})\n1149:             summary_lines.append(\n1150:                 f\"- Attempt {attempt.get('attempt', '?')}: \"\n1151:                 f\"exit_code={payload.get('exit_code', 'N/A')}, \"\n1152:                 f\"duration={payload.get('duration_seconds', 'N/A')}s\"\n1153:             )\n1154: \n1155:         # Fill template\n1156:         report_content = (\n1157:             template.replace(\"{{ticket_id}}\", ticket_id)\n1158:             .replace(\"{{generated_at}}\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())\n1159:             .replace(\"{{review_attempt_count}}\", str(len(review_attempts)))\n1160:             .replace(\"{{summary}}\", \"\\n\".join(summary_lines))\n1161:             .replace(\"{{last_decision}}\", last_decision.value.upper())\n1162:             .replace(\n1163:                 \"{{escalation_reason}}\",\n1164:                 f\"Reached {len(review_attempts)} consecutive CHANGES decisions (threshold: 5)\",\n1165:             )\n1166:             .replace(\n1167:                 \"{{blockers}}\",\n1168:                 \"\\n\\n\".join(all_blockers)\n1169:                 if all_blockers\n1170:                 else \"[No structured blockers found]\",\n1171:             )\n1172:             .replace(\n1173:                 \"{{notes}}\",\n1174:                 \"This report was auto-generated when the review budget was exhausted. Human review required.\",\n1175:             )\n1176:         )\n1177: \n1178:         # Write report\n1179:         ticket_dir = self._get_review_log_path(ticket_id)\n1180:         report_path = ticket_dir / \"human_review_report.md\"\n1181:         report_path.write_text(report_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1182: \n1183:         return report_path\n1184: \n1185:     def _emit_review_attempt(\n1186:         self,\n1187:         ticket_id: str,\n1188:         attempt: int,\n1189:         timeout_s: int,\n1190:         exit_code: int,\n1191:         duration: float,\n1192:         stdout: str,\n1193:         decision: ReviewDecision | None = None,\n1194:         review_log_path: Path | None = None,\n1195:         parse_method: str = \"\",\n1196:         transport_ok: bool = True,\n1197:         transport_error: str = \"\",\n1198:     ):\n1199:         \"\"\"Emit a lightweight MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT event.\n1200: \n1201:         Before: Requires attempt metadata and optional decision/review_log_path.\n1202:         During: Emits event with only review_log_path + a short stdout_tail.\n1203:         After: Event is recorded in bus; full review content (stdout, stderr,\n1204:             parsed blockers) is persisted separately in attempt-N.md, not the bus.\n1205: \n1206:         WP-2026-118: Fail-safe added - if emit() fails, log audibly and continue\n1207:         without crashing the review cycle.\n1208:         \"\"\"\n1209:         try:\n1210:             # WP-2026-106 B2: lightweight payload only. Full stdout/stderr and\n1211:             # parsed blockers live in attempt-N.md; the bus keeps a pointer\n1212:             # (review_log_path) plus a short stdout_tail for quick forensics.\n1213:             payload = {\n1214:                 \"attempt\": attempt,\n1215:                 \"timeout_seconds\": timeout_s,\n1216:                 \"exit_code\": exit_code,\n1217:                 \"duration_seconds\": round(duration, 2),\n1218:                 \"stdout_tail\": (stdout or \"\")[-500:],\n1219:                 \"transport_ok\": transport_ok,\n1220:             }\n1221:             if decision:\n1222:                 payload[\"decision\"] = decision.value\n1223:             if review_log_path:\n1224:                 payload[\"review_log_path\"] = str(review_log_path)\n1225:             if parse_method:\n1226:                 payload[\"parse_method\"] = parse_method\n1227:             if transport_error:\n1228:                 payload[\"transport_error\"] = transport_error\n1229: \n1230:             self.event_bus.emit(\n1231:                 \"MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT\",\n1232:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1233:                 actor=\"MANAGER\",\n1234:                 payload=payload,\n1235:             )\n1236:         except Exception as exc:\n1237:             # WP-2026-118: Fail-safe - log the error audibly but don't crash\n1238:             print(\n1239:                 f\"[manager-review-bridge] FAIL-SAFE: event_bus.emit() failed for \"\n1240:                 f\"ticket {ticket_id}, attempt {attempt}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}. \"\n1241:                 f\"Review cycle continues; check attempt-{attempt}.md for full content.\",\n1242:                 file=sys.stderr,\n1243:             )\n1244: \n1245:     def _count_prior_changes_from_bus(self, ticket_id: str) -> int:\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1245. def fake_run(cmd, **kwargs):\n115:         return __import__(\"subprocess\").CompletedProcess(\n116:             cmd,\n117:             0,\n118:             stdout=\"APPROVE: no findings. route.\"\"\"\n616: \n617:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_approve(self, tmp_path):\n618:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: APPROVE.\"\"\"\n619:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, ReviewDecision, EventBus\n620: \n621:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n622:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n623: All criteria met.\\nDECISION: APPROVE\"\n626:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n627:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n628:         assert method == \"text_regex\"\n629: \n630:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_changes(self, tmp_path):\n631:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: CHANGES.\"\"\"\n632:         from bus.review_bridge import bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n637: \n638:         stdout = \"Found issues:\\n- Missing tests\\n\\nDECISION: CHANGES\"\n639:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n640:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.CHANGES\n641:         assert method == \"text_regex\"\n642: \n643:     def EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n649:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n650: \n651:         stdout = \"Review complete but output lacks decision format.\"\n652:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n653:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n654:         assert method == \"fallback_inspect\"\n655: bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n663: \n664:         stdout = \"Cannot verify acceptance criteria remotely.\\nDECISION: INSPECT\"\n665:         decision, method = captured[\"ticket_id\"] == \"WP-2026-072\"\n766:         assert captured[\"transition\"] == (\"WP-2026-072\", \"READY_TO_CLOSE\", \"Manager approved\")\n767: \n768:     def test_opencode_review_cmd_length_is_safe(self, monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n769:         \"\"\"Test the constructed command line respects the Windows CMD limit (~8k chars) by using a prompt file.\"\"\"\n770:         import subprocess\n771:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n772: \n773:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n774:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n775:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n776: \n777:         # Create 100 dummy files to simulate a large canonical_files list\n778:         dummy_files = []\n779:         for i in range(100):\n780:             p = tmp_path / f\"dummy_file_{i}.py\"\n781:             p.write_text(\"\")\n782:             dummy_files.append(p)\n783: \n784:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_canonical_files\", lambda: dummy_files)\n785:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_active_ticket_id\", lambda: \"WP-2026-077\")\n786:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge.state_ingest, \"_latest_state\", lambda _: \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n787:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n788: \n789:         captured_cmd = []\n790: \n791:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n792:             captured_cmd.extend(cmd_args)\n793:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(args=cmd_args, returncode=0, stdout=\"DECISION: APPROVE\", stderr=\"\")\n794: \n795:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n796: \n797:         class DummySupervisor:\n798:             def transition_ticket(self, *args, **kwargs):\n799:                 pass\n800: \n801:         bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n802:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-077\",\n803:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n804:             timeout_seconds=5\n805:         )\n806: \n807:         import shlex\n808:         cmd_string = shlex.join(captured_cmd)\n809: \n810:         # Verify the length is well under the 6000 character safety margin\n811:         assert len(cmd_string) < 6000\n812:         # Verify that no -f flags are passed\n813:         assert \"-f\" not in captured_cmd\n814: \n815: \n816: class TestReviewPacketTransport:\n817:     \"\"\"WP-2026-126: el contexto del review va por un review packet en disco,\n818:     no por --file (flag array que consume el mensaje) ni por argv largo.\"\"\"\n819: \n820:     @staticmethod\n821:     def _bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, os_name):\n822:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n823: \n824:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n825:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n826:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_model\", lambda: None)\n827:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_supports_json_format\", False)\n828:         monkeypatch.setattr(\"os.name\", os_name)\n829:         return bridge\n830: \n831:     def test_writes_review_packet(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n832:         \"\"\"El contexto completo se escribe a .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md.\"\"\"\n833:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n834:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n835:             subprocess,\n836:             \"run\",\n837:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n838:         )\n839: \n840:         prompt = \"CONTEXTO DE REVIEW\\n\" + (\"x\" * 8000)\n841:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n842:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n843:         )\n844: \n845:         packet = (\n846:             tmp_path\n847:             / \".agent\"\n848:             / \"runtime\"\n849:             / \"review_packets\"\n850:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n851:         )\n852:         assert packet.exists()\n853:         assert packet.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == prompt\n854: \n855:     def test_review_packet_is_separate_per_attempt(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n856:         \"\"\"Cada intento escribe su propio packet y preserva la provenance.\"\"\"\n857:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n858: \n859:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n860:             subprocess,\n861:             \"run\",\n862:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n863:         )\n864: \n865:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n866:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 1\", attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n867:         )\n868:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n869:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 2\", attempt=2, timeout_seconds=5\n870:         )\n871: \n872:         packet_1 = (\n873:             tmp_path\n874:             / \".agent\"\n875:             / \"runtime\"\n876:             / \"review_packets\"\n877:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n878:         )\n879:         packet_2 = (\n880:             tmp_path\n881:             / \".agent\"\n882:             / \"runtime\"\n883:             / \"review_packets\"\n884:             / \"WP-T_attempt-2.md\"\n885:         )\n886:         assert packet_1.exists()\n887:         assert packet_2.exists()\n888:         assert packet_1.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 1\"\n889:         assert packet_2.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 2\"\n890: \n891:     def test_positional_message_short_and_no_file_flag(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n892:         \"\"\"El prompt posicional es corto y referencia el packet; sin --file\n893:         y sin el prompt completo en la linea de comandos.\"\"\"\n894:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n895: \n896:         captured = {}\n897: \n898:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n899:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n900:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n901: \n902:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n903: \n904:         prompt = \"PROMPT-LARGO-UNICO \" + (\"x\" * 9000)\n905:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n906:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, timeout_seconds=5\n907:         )\n908: \n909:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n910:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n911:         assert prompt not in cmd\n912:         message = cmd[-1]\n913:         assert \"WP-T\" in message\n914:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in message\n915:         assert len(message) < 400\n916: \n917:     def test_windows_invocation_is_string_without_file_flag(\n918: test_state_ingest_latest_state_from_bus(self, tmp_path):\n965:         \"\"\"Test state ingest reads latest state from event bus.\"\"\"\n966:         from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n967:          ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n977:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n978:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n979:         )\n980: \n981:         state = ingest._latest_state(\"WP-2026-102\")\n982:         assert state == \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n983: \n984:     def test_state_ingest_latest_state_default(self, tmp_path):\n985:    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n990:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n991:         ingest = TicketStateIngest(event_bus=event_bus, Mock drift\n6: - El patch/mocking apunta a un símbolo distinto del que el código realmente llama.\n7: - Efecto: el test puede pasar sin estar verificando el camino real.\n8: \n9: ## AP-02 - Floor assertion\n10: - El umbral numérico ya queda satisfecho por el baseline sin la feature.\n11: - Efecto: la aserción no prueba nada relevante.\n12: \n13: ## AP-03 - Zero-logic wrapper\n14: - La función solo delega 1:1 sin aportar lógica propia.\n15: - Efecto: añade indirección sin valor.\n16: \n17: ## AP-04 - Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n18: - Un método que adquiere un recurso exclusivo puede ser reentrado desde varios call sites sin guarda de instancia.\n19: - Efecto: segundo intento falla con el propio proceso vivo y aborta el flujo.\n20: \n21: ## AP-05 - Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n22: - Un método cambia de retorno implícito `None` a `bool`, pero los callers siguen usando truthiness genérica.\n23: - Efecto: los mocks y paths legacy producen falsos positivos o falsos negativos.\n24: \n25: ## AP-06 - Validator evidence missing\n26: - El plan declara un validador, pero el execution log no conserva el comando exacto y la salida limpia.\n27: - Efecto: no hay trazabilidad verificable del gate.\n28: \n29: ## AP-07 - Scaffolding misclassified as code\n30: - Un ticket que solo crea estructura, placeholders o `.gitkeep` se clasifica como `code`.\n31: - Efecto: se activan gates que no aportan señal y generan ruido.\n\n(End of file - total 31 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Inventario Canónico de Anti-Patrones\n\nEste archivo es la fuente compartida de referencia para Builder y Manager.\n\n## AP-01 - Zero-logic wrapper\n- La función solo delega 1:1 sin aportar lógica propia.\n- Efecto: añade indirección sin valor.\n\n## AP-04 - Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- Un método que adquiere un recurso exclusivo puede ser reentrado desde varios call sites sin guarda de instancia.\n- Efecto: segundo intento falla con el propio proceso vivo y aborta el flujo.\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"skills\\_shared\\anti-patterns.md","time":{"start":1779742077308,"end":1779742077364}},"id":"prt_e60e4ee09001ZW7M0COqoHio7z","sessionID":"ses_19f1bf221ffeALAB2JPaqFWZ9b","messageID":"msg_e60e48842001mpVEje3KN5H4uF"}}

Analyzing WP-2026-139 implementation against acceptance criteria:\n\n## Verification Summary\n\n**Acceptance Criteria Check:**\n\n1. ✅ **`bus/review_bridge.py` carga AP canónicos desde archivo una sola vez y los reutiliza con caché**\n   - `ReviewBridge.__init__()` (línea 167) llama a `_load_canonical_anti_patterns()` una sola vez\n   - Resultado cacheado en `self._canonical_anti_patterns`\n   - `_canonical_anti_patterns_path()` resuelve ruta relativa desde `bus/review_bridge.py` hacia `skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md`\n\n2. ✅ **El rubric del Manager conserva `AP-01..AP-07` sin la triple copia inline**\n   - `_render_canonical_anti_pattern_inventory()` (líneas 490-498) compone el bloque desde caché\n   - `_rubric_for_type()` (líneas 596-654) usa el inventario cacheado via `self._render_canonical_anti_pattern_inventory()`\n\n3. ✅ **Fallback seguro mantiene el prompt funcional si el archivo canónico no se puede leer**\n   - `_load_canonical_anti_patterns()` (líneas 467-488) usa `warnings.warn()` en OSError\n   - Retorna lista vacía; `_render_canonical_anti_pattern_inventory()` omite sección si está vacía\n\n4. ✅ **Los tests cubren carga, composición y fallback seguro**\n   - `test_build_review_prompt_loads_canonical_anti_patterns_once_per_instance`: verifica carga única\n   - `test_build_review_prompt_warns_and_omits_inventory_when_shared_file_missing`: verifica fallback con warning\n   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_manager_learnings_for_code_and_preserves_static_rubric`: verifica composición con AP-01..AP-07\n\n**Quality Gates (según execution_log.md):**\n- `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`: 52 passed ✅\n- `ruff check bus/review_bridge.py tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py`: All checks passed ✅\n- `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`: Sin errores ✅\n\n**Files Likely Touched whitelist:**\n- `bus/review_bridge.py` ✅\n- `tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py` ✅\n\nTodos los criterios de aceptación están cumplidos. La implementación carga el inventario canónico una sola vez en `__init__`, lo cachea, compone el rubric desde caché, y degrada de forma segura si el archivo no está disponible.\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779742091536,"end":1779742101994}}}

Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\n--- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\nAP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n\n--- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\nLecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n- [2026-05-25] Validator evidence gate: when a work_plan explicitly declares a validator as a quality gate (skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), the Manager must find explicit outpu... (unknown)\n\n--- work_plan.md ---\n# Work Plan - WP-2026-141\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-141\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** documentation\n- **Titulo:** Google eng-practices review standards alignment\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-141_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779787048945,"end":1779787049048}},"id":"prt_e639314a7001aJubBqxbZbwyJC","sessionID":"ses_19c6d3403ffe6n01Xw2NehGJ7T","messageID":"msg_e6392ccb50014vtTH0INKBhBmQ"}}
