{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779792397172,"sessionID":"ses_19c1ba212ffeoYC9Ynwb0DVMFW","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_9098aac5843345d3a58c6a00","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_AGENT_DIR), str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # ============================================================================\n100: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n101: # ============================================================================\n102: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n103: \n104: \n105: def _project_root() -> Path:\n106:     return resolve_project_root()\n107: \n108: \n109: class _LazyPath:\n110:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n111:         self._resolver = resolver\n112: \n113:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n114:         return self._resolver()\n115: \n116:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n117:         return self.resolve() / other\n118: \n119:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n120:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n121: \n122:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n123:         return str(self.resolve())\n124: \n125:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n126:         return str(self.resolve())\n127: \n128:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n129:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n130: \n131: \n132: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n133: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n134: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_agent_dir)\n135: COLLAB_DIR = _LazyPath(get_collab_dir)\n136: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_context_dir)\n137: \n138: # State files\n139: WORK_PLAN = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\")\n140: EXEC_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"execution_log.md\")\n141: REVIEW_QUEUE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"review_queue.md\")\n142: NOTIFICATIONS = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"notifications.md\")\n143: TURN_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"TURN.md\")\n144: STATE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"STATE.md\")\n145: PROJECT_MAP = _LazyPath(lambda: get_context_dir() / \"project_map.md\")\n146: ARCHIVE_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"archive\")\n147: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\")\n148: \n149: # Archive configuration\n150: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n151: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n152: \n153: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n154: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n155: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n156: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n157: \n158: \n159: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n160:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n161: \n162:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n163:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n164:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n165:     \"\"\"\n166:     try:\n167:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n168:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n169:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n170:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n171:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n172: \n173: \n174: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n175: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n176: \n177: # Estados validos para validacion\n178: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n179:     \"DRAFT\",\n180:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n181:     \"APPROVED\",\n182:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n183:     \"COMPLETED\",\n184:     \"N/A\",\n185: }\n186: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n187:     \"PENDING\",\n188:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n189:     \"BLOCKED\",\n190:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n191:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n192:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n193:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n194:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n195:     \"COMPLETED\",\n196:     \"N/A\",\n197: }\n198: \n199: \n200: def _ensure_runtime_dirs() -> None:\n201:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n202:     archive_dir = get_collab_dir() / \"archive\"\n203:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n204:     archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n205: \n206: \n207: def _get_event_bus() -> EventBus | None:\n208:     global event_bus\n209:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE:\n210:         return None\n211:     if event_bus is None:\n212:         event_bus = EventBus(get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n213:     return event_bus\n214: \n215: \n216: # Event bus is initialized lazily after the project root is known.\n217: event_bus = None\n218: \n219: # Circuit breaker state\n220: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = _LazyPath(\n221:     lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n222: )\n223: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\")\n224: \n225: # ============================================================================\n226: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n227: # ============================================================================\n228: \n229: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n230:     \"work_plan.md\",\n231:     \"execution_log.md\",\n232:     \"STATE.md\",\n233:     \"TURN.md\",\n234:     \"notifications.md\",\n235:     \".session_state.json\",\n236: }\n237: \n238: \n239: def _exclude_files() -> set[str]:\n240:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n241:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n242:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n243:     exclude_files = {str((collab_dir / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n244:     exclude_files.add(str((context_dir / \"project_map.md\").resolve()))\n245:     # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n246:     exclude_files.add(\n247:         str((agent_dir / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve())\n248:     )\n249:     # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n250:     exclude_files.add(str((agent_dir / \"config\").resolve()))\n251:     return exclude_files\n252: \n253: \n254: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n255:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n256:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n257:     in_section = False\n258:     files = set()\n259: \n260:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n261:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n262:             return False\n263:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n264:             return True\n265:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n266:             return True\n267:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n268:         return \".\" in basename\n269: \n270:     for line in lines:\n271:         line = line.strip()\n272:         if \"## Files Likely Touched\" in line:\n273:             in_section = True\n274:             continue\n275:         if in_section and line.startswith(\"## \"):\n276:             break  # next section\n277:         if in_section and line and not line.startswith(\"---\"):\n278:             # normalize: remove backticks, quotes, bullets, trim\n279:             normalized = (\n280:                 line.lstrip(\"*- \")\n281:                 .replace(\"`\", \"\")\n282:                 .replace('\"', \"\")\n283:                 .replace(\"'\", \"\")\n284:                 .strip()\n285:             )\n286:             if normalized and _looks_like_path_token(normalized):\n287:                 # resolve relative to project root\n288:                 path = (PROJECT_ROOT / normalized).resolve()\n289:                 files.add(str(path))\n290:     return files\n291: \n292: \n293: def get_changed_files() -> set[str] | None:\n294:     \"\"\"Get all changed files: staged, unstaged, untracked. None if not git repo.\"\"\"\n295:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n296:         return None\n297:     try:\n298:         # Use -z for null-byte separated output to handle paths with spaces and renames safely\n299:         result = subprocess.run(\n300:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\", \"-z\"],\n301:             capture_output=True,\n302:             text=True,\n303:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n304:         )\n305:         changed = set()\n306:         # Split by null byte, each entry is: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status\n307:         # Renames have two consecutive entries: \"R old_path\" followed by \"new_path\"\n308:         entries = result.stdout.split(\"\\0\")\n309:         i = 0\n310:         while i < len(entries):\n311:             entry = entries[i]\n312:             if not entry:\n313:                 i += 1\n314:                 continue\n315:             # Format: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status (e.g., \"M \", \"??\", \"R \")\n316:             # When Y=' ' (space), format is \"M path\" (path at index 2)\n317:             # When Y!=' ', format is \"XY path\" with space separator (path at index 3)\n318:             if len(entry) >= 3:\n319:                 status = entry[:2]\n320:                 # Determine path start: if entry[2] is space, path starts at 3; otherwise at 2\n321:                 path = entry[3:] if entry[2] == \" \" else entry[2:]\n322:                 # Handle renames: status starts with 'R', next entry is the new path\n323:                 if status[0] == \"R\" and i + 1 < len(entries):\n324:                     new_path = entries[i + 1]\n325:                     if new_path:\n326:                         changed.add(new_path)\n327:                     i += 2\n328:                     continue\n329:                 else:\n330:                     changed.add(path)\n331:             i += 1\n332:         # resolve to absolute paths\n333:         resolved = set()\n334:         for f in changed:\n335:             path = (PROJECT_ROOT / f).resolve()\n336:             resolved.add(str(path))\n337:         return resolved\n338:     except FileNotFoundError:\n339:         return None\n340: \n341: \n342: def check_scope_gate(\n343:     work_plan_content: str, changed_files: set[str] | None, exclude_files: set[str]\n344: ) -> dict:\n345:     \"\"\"Check if changed files are within scope.\"\"\"\n346:     if changed_files is None:\n347:         return {\n348:             \"valid\": True,\n349:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n350:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n351:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n352:             \"warnings\": [\"Repository is not git-managed\"],\n353:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n354:         }\n355: \n356:     whitelist = parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n357:     if not whitelist:\n358:         return {\n359:             \"valid\": True,\n360:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n361:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n362:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n363:             \"warnings\": [\"No Files Likely Touched section in work_plan.md\"],\n364:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n365:         }\n366: \n367:     relevant_changed = changed_files - exclude_files\n368:     relevant_whitelist = whitelist - exclude_files\n369:     covered_files = relevant_changed & relevant_whitelist\n370:     missing_from_diff = relevant_whitelist - relevant_changed\n371:     out_of_scope = relevant_changed - relevant_whitelist\n372: \n373:     warnings = []\n374:     blocked_reason = None\n375:     valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0\n376: \n377:     if relevant_whitelist and not covered_files:\n378:         valid = False\n379:         blocked_reason = (\n380:             \"None of the declared Files Likely Touched entries appeared in the diff\"\n381:         )\n382:     elif covered_files and missing_from_diff:\n383:         warnings.append(\n384:             \"Partial scope coverage: \"\n385:             f\"{len(covered_files)} of {len(relevant_whitelist)} declared files touched\"\n386:         )\n387: \n388:     return {\n389:         \"valid\": valid,\n390:         \"out_of_scope\": out_of_scope,\n391:         \"missing_from_diff\": missing_from_diff,\n392:         \"covered_files\": covered_files,\n393:         \"warnings\": warnings,\n394:         \"blocked_reason\": blocked_reason,\n395:     }\n396: \n397: \n398: def _load_mark_ready_context() -> tuple[str, str, str]:\n399:     \"\"\"Load the plan and log content for --mark-ready.\"\"\"\n400:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n401:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n402:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n403:     return plan_content, log_content, plan_id\n404: \n405: \n406: def _record_scope_override(scope_override: str, problem_files: set[str]) -> None:\n407:     \"\"\"Record a scope override in the execution log.\"\"\"\n408:     note = (\n409:         f\"Scope override: {scope_override}. \"\n410:         f\"Affected files: {', '.join(sorted(problem_files))}\"\n411:     )\n412:     update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", note)\n413: \n414: \n415: def _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result: dict, scope_override: str | None) -> bool:\n416:     \"\"\"Apply scope gate decision for mark-ready.\"\"\"\n417:     if gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n418:         for warning in gate_result[\"warnings\"]:\n419:             print(f\"[WARN] {warning}\")\n420:         update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Marked ready by Builder\")\n421:         return True\n422: \n423:     if not scope_override:\n424:         print(\"[ERROR] Scope violation detected:\")\n425:         if gate_result.get(\"blocked_reason\"):\n426:             print(f\"  {gate_result['blocked_reason']}\")\n427:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]):\n428:             print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n429:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())):\n430:             print(f\"  - missing: {file_path}\")\n431:         print('Use --scope-override \"reason\" to proceed.')\n432:         return False\n433: \n434:     print(f\"[INFO] Scope override applied: {scope_override}\")\n435:     problem_files = set(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]) | set(\n436:         gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())\n437:     )\n438:     _record_scope_override(scope_override, problem_files)\n439:     return True\n440: \n441: \n442: def _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id: str, plan_content: str) -> None:\n443:     \"\"\"Update TURN.md and STATE.md after mark-ready.\n444: \n445:     WP-2026-124: Uses bus-derived state as authority, not execution_log.md.\n446:     \"\"\"\n447:     # WP-2026-124: Get bus-derived state\n448:     log_status_before = get_status(read_file(EXEC_LOG), \"**Estado:**\")\n449:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n450:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n451: \n452:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n453:         bus_state = (\n454:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n455:             if events\n456:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n457:         )\n458:         # Use bus state if available, fallback to log\n459:         from_state = (\n460:             bus_state.value if bus_state != TicketState.UNKNOWN else log_status_before\n461:         )\n462:     else:\n463:         from_state = log_status_before\n464: \n465:     action = {\n466:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n467:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n468:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n469:         \"instruction\": f\"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n470:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n471:         \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n472:         \"log_status\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n473:         \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n474:         \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n475:     }\n476:     update_turn_file(action)\n477: \n478:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n479:     if not state_content:\n480:         return\n481: \n482:     lines = state_content.split(\"\\n\")\n483:     for index, line in enumerate(lines):\n484:         if \"**Estado actual:**\" in line:\n485:             lines[index] = \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n486:             write_file(STATE_FILE, \"\\n\".join(lines))\n487:             break\n488: \n489:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus idempotently\n490:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n491:         # Check if already emitted for this ticket and state\n492:         latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n493:             ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n494:         )\n495:         if (\n496:             not latest_state_event\n497:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n498:         ):\n499:             event_bus.emit(\n500:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n501:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n502:                 actor=\"BUILDER\",\n503:                 payload={\n504:                     \"from_state\": from_state,\n505:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n506:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n507:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n508:                 },\n509:             )\n510:         if (\n511:             not latest_state_event\n512:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n513:         ):\n514:             bus_from_state = (\n515:                 latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n516:                 if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n517:                 else log_status_before\n518:             )\n519:             event_bus.emit(\n520:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n521:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n522:                 actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n523:                 payload={\n524:                     \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n525:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n526:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n527:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n528:                 },\n529:             )\n530: \n531: \n532: # ============================================================================\n533: # CIRCUIT BREAKER AND CHECKOUT UTILITIES\n534: # ============================================================================\n535: \n536: \n537: def _read_circuit_breaker() -> dict:\n538:     \"\"\"Read circuit breaker state from JSON file.\"\"\"\n539:     if not CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.exists():\n540:         return {\n541:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n542:             \"failures\": 0,\n543:             \"last_failure\": None,\n544:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n545:         }\n546:     try:\n547:         return json.loads(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n548:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n549:         return {\n550:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n551:             \"failures\": 0,\n552:             \"last_failure\": None,\n553:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n554:         }\n555: \n556: \n557: def _write_circuit_breaker(state: dict) -> None:\n558:     \"\"\"Write circuit breaker state to JSON file.\"\"\"\n559:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n560:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.write_text(\n561:         json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n562:     )\n563: \n564: \n565: def _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> dict:\n566:     \"\"\"\n567:     Check circuit breaker state for a ticket.\n568:     Returns dict with 'open', 'reason', 'failures', 'no_progress_count'.\n569:     \"\"\"\n570:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n571:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\":\n572:         return {\n573:             \"open\": True,\n574:             \"reason\": breaker.get(\"reason\", \"Circuit breaker triggered\"),\n575:             \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n576:             \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n577:         }\n578:     return {\n579:         \"open\": False,\n580:         \"reason\": None,\n581:         \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n582:         \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n583:     }\n584: \n585: \n586: def _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n587:     reason: str, plan_id: str, is_no_progress: bool = False\n588: ) -> None:\n589:     \"\"\"Trigger circuit breaker to OPEN state.\"\"\"\n590:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n591:     breaker[\"state\"] = \"OPEN\"\n592:     breaker[\"reason\"] = reason\n593:     breaker[\"last_triggered\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n594:     breaker[\"ticket_id\"] = plan_id\n595:     if is_no_progress:\n596:         breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n597:     else:\n598:         breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n599:     _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n600: \n601: \n602: def _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n603:     \"\"\"Reset circuit breaker to CLOSED state after successful completion.\"\"\"\n604:     _write_circuit_breaker(\n605:         {\n606:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n607:             \"failures\": 0,\n608:             \"last_failure\": None,\n609:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n610:             \"last_reset\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n611:             \"last_ticket\": plan_id,\n612:         }\n613:     )\n614: \n615: \n616: def _record_error_for_breaker(plan_id: str, error_msg: str) -> None:\n617:     \"\"\"Record an error for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n618:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n619:     breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n620:     breaker[\"last_error\"] = error_msg\n621:     breaker[\"last_error_time\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n622: \n623:     # Trigger if 3+ consecutive errors\n624:     if breaker[\"failures\"] >= 3:\n625:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(f\"Repeated errors: {error_msg[:100]}\", plan_id)\n626:     else:\n627:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n628: \n629: \n630: def _record_no_progress_for_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n631:     \"\"\"Record no-progress event for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n632:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n633:     breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n634:     breaker[\"last_no_progress\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n635: \n636:     # Trigger if 3+ no-progress events\n637:     if breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] >= 3:\n638:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n639:             \"No progress detected in multiple iterations\", plan_id, is_no_progress=True\n640:         )\n641:     else:\n642:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n643: \n644: \n645: def _read_builder_lock() -> dict | None:\n646:     \"\"\"Read builder lock file if it exists.\"\"\"\n647:     if not BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.exists():\n648:         return None\n649:     try:\n650:         return json.loads(BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n651:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n652:         return None\n653: \n654: \n655: def _acquire_builder_lock(\n656:     plan_id: str, pid: int, role: str = \"BUILDER\", backend: str = \"unknown\"\n657: ) -> bool:\n658:     \"\"\"\n659:     Attempt to acquire builder lock atomically.\n660:     Returns True if lock was acquired, False if another session holds it.\n661:     \"\"\"\n662:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n663:     if existing is not None:\n664:         # Check if lock is stale (different ticket or dead process)\n665:         if existing.get(\"ticket_id\") != plan_id:\n666:             # Stale lock from different ticket - override\n667:             lock_data = {\n668:                 \"pid\": pid,\n669:                 \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n670:                 \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n671:                 \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n672:                 \"role\": role,\n673:                 \"backend\": backend,\n674:                 \"round\": 1,\n675:             }\n676:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n677:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n678:                 json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n679:             )\n680:             return True\n681:         # Same ticket - check if process is alive (simplified: just allow re-acquisition)\n682:         lock_data = {\n683:             \"pid\": pid,\n684:             \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n685:             \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n686:             \"started_at\": existing.get(\n687:                 \"started_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n688:             ),\n689:             \"role\": role,\n690:             \"backend\": backend,\n691:             \"round\": existing.get(\"round\", 1) + 1,\n692:         }\n693:         BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n694:             json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n695:         )\n696:         return True\n697: \n698:     # No existing lock - acquire fresh\n699:     lock_data = {\n700:         \"pid\": pid,\n701:         \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n702:         \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n703:         \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n704:         \"role\": role,\n705:         \"backend\": backend,\n706:         \"round\": 1,\n707:     }\n708:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n709:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n710:         json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n711:     )\n712:     return True\n713: \n714: \n715: def _release_builder_lock(plan_id: str) -> None:\n716:     \"\"\"Release builder lock after completion.\"\"\"\n717:     import contextlib\n718: \n719:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n720:     if existing and existing.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id:\n721:         with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n722:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.unlink()\n723: \n724: \n725: def _emit_builder_exit(plan_id: str, exit_reason: str, completion_summary: str) -> None:\n726:     \"\"\"Emit BUILDER_EXIT event to the bus - required for ticket closure.\"\"\"\n727:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n728:         event_bus.emit(\n729:             event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\",\n730:             ticket_id=plan_id,\n731:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n732:             payload={\n733:                 \"exit_reason\": exit_reason,\n734:                 \"completion_summary\": completion_summary,\n735:                 \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n736:             },\n737:         )\n738: \n739: \n740: def _auto_archive_closed_artifacts() -> None:\n741:     \"\"\"Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts during mark-ready.\n742: \n743:     Before: Requires COLLAB_DIR to exist with potential closed PLAN/AUDIT files.\n744:     During: Imports and calls archive_collaboration_artifacts.py as a library function.\n745:     After: Closed PLAN/AUDIT files are moved to _archive/plan_audit/ (idempotent, silent on no-op).\n746:     \"\"\"\n747:     try:\n748:         # Import the archive script as a module\n749:         import importlib.util\n750: \n751:         archive_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n752:             \"archive_collaboration_artifacts\",\n753:             PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n754:         )\n755:         if archive_spec and archive_spec.loader:\n756:             archive_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(archive_spec)\n757:             archive_spec.loader.exec_module(archive_mod)\n758: \n759:             # Call the archive function (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n760:             archive_mod.archive_collaboration_artifacts(\n761:                 collaboration_dir=COLLAB_DIR,\n762:                 dry_run=False,\n763:             )\n764:     except Exception as exc:\n765:         # Silent fail with debug logging - archiving is best-effort, not critical path\n766:         print(f\"[WARN] Auto-archive failed: {exc}\")\n767: \n768: \n769: # ============================================================================\n770: # UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n771: # ============================================================================\n772: \n773: \n774: def read_file(path: Path) -> str:\n775:     \"\"\"Lee un archivo si existe, retorna string vacio si no.\"\"\"\n776:     if not path.exists():\n777:         return \"\"\n778:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n779:         return f.read()\n780: \n781: \n782: def write_file(path: Path, content: str) -> None:\n783:     \"\"\"Escribe contenido a un archivo, creando directorios si es necesario.\"\"\"\n784:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n785:     with open(path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n786:         f.write(content)\n787: \n788: \n789: def get_status(content: str, marker: str) -> str:\n790:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado de un archivo buscando un marcador.\"\"\"\n791:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n792:         if marker in line:\n793:             return line.split(marker)[1].strip()\n794:     return \"UNKNOWN\"\n795: \n796: \n797: def get_plan_id(content: str) -> str:\n798:     \"\"\"Extrae el ID del plan de trabajo.\"\"\"\n799:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n800:         if \"**ID:**\" in line or \"**Plan ID:**\" in line:\n801:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n802:     return \"N/A\"\n803: \n804: \n805: def get_plan_type(content: str) -> str:\n806:     \"\"\"Extrae el tipo de plan. Valores: IMPLEMENTATION (default) | FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n807:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n808:         if \"**Tipo:**\" in line:\n809:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip().upper()\n810:     return \"IMPLEMENTATION\"\n811: \n812: \n813: def check_git_status() -> bool | None:\n814:     \"\"\"Verifica si el repositorio esta limpio.\"\"\"\n815:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n816:         return None\n817:     try:\n818:         result = subprocess.run(\n819:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\"],\n820:             capture_output=True,\n821:             text=True,\n822:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n823:         )\n824:         return not result.stdout.strip()\n825:     except FileNotFoundError:\n826:         return None\n827: \n828: \n829: def extract_status_emoji(status_str: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n830:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado limpio y el emoji.\"\"\"\n831:     emojis = {\"🟢\", \"🟡\", \"🔴\", \"🟣\", \"✅\", \"⏳\", \"❌\", \"⚠️\"}\n832:     status_clean = status_str.strip()\n833:     found_emoji = \"\"\n834:     for emoji in emojis:\n835:         if emoji in status_clean:\n836:             found_emoji = emoji\n837:             status_clean = status_clean.replace(emoji, \"\").strip()\n838:             break\n839:     return status_clean, found_emoji\n840: \n841: \n842: _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n843: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n844:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n845:     re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n846: )\n847: \n848: \n849: def _check_deliverable_type(content: str) -> list[str]:\n850:     \"\"\"Check deliverable_type field in work_plan.md content.\n851: \n852:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n853:     During: Searches for deliverable_type field using regex, validates against allowed values.\n854:     After: Returns list of warning strings (empty if valid). Never returns errors (V1 informational).\n855:     \"\"\"\n856:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n857:     if not match:\n858:         return [\n859:             \"work_plan.md missing deliverable_type field. \"\n860:             \"Add one of: code, documentation, research, analysis, mixed.\"\n861:         ]\n862:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n863:     if \"+\" in value:\n864:         return [\n865:             f\"work_plan.md deliverable_type='{value}' uses compound syntax. \"\n866:             \"Use 'mixed' instead of 'code+documentation' etc.\"\n867:         ]\n868:     if value not in _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES:\n869:         return [\n870:             f\"work_plan.md has unknown deliverable_type '{value}'. \"\n871:             f\"Expected one of: {sorted(_VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES)}\"\n872:         ]\n873:     return []\n874: \n875: \n876: def _validate_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n877:     \"\"\"Validate work_plan.md file.\"\"\"\n878:     errors = []\n879:     content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n880:     if not content:\n881:         return errors\n882: \n883:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n884:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n885:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_PLAN_STATES:\n886:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n887:     if \"**ID:**\" not in content:\n888:         errors.append(\"Falta campo **ID:**\")\n889:     return errors\n890: \n891: \n892: def _validate_execution_log() -> list[str]:\n893:     \"\"\"Validate execution_log.md file.\"\"\"\n894:     errors = []\n895:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n896:     if not content:\n897:         return errors\n898: \n899:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n900:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n901:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_LOG_STATES:\n902:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n903:     return errors\n904: \n905: \n906: def _validate_turn_file() -> list[str]:\n907:     \"\"\"Validate TURN.md file.\"\"\"\n908:     errors = []\n909:     content = read_file(TURN_FILE)\n910:     if content and \"## Agente Activo\" not in content:\n911:         errors.append(\"Falta secciÃƒÂ³n '## Agente Activo'\")\n912:     return errors\n913: \n914: \n915: def _validate_notifications() -> list[str]:\n916:     \"\"\"Validate notifications.md file.\"\"\"\n917:     errors = []\n918:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n919:     if content and \"</thinking>\" in content:\n920:         errors.append(\"Contiene etiquetas </thinking> (corrupto)\")\n921:     return errors\n922: \n923: \n924: def _validate_cross_file_consistency() -> list[str]:\n925:     \"\"\"Validate consistency across files.\"\"\"\n926:     errors = []\n927:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n928:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n929: \n930:     if not plan_content or not log_content:\n931:         return errors\n932: \n933:     plan_status_raw = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n934:     log_status_raw = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n935:     plan_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(plan_status_raw)\n936:     log_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(log_status_raw)\n937: \n938:     # Plan APPROVED pero log COMPLETED\n939:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_clean and \"COMPLETED\" in log_clean:\n940:         errors.append(\n941:             \"DRIFT: plan=APPROVED pero log=COMPLETED -- \"\n942:             \"el log pertenece a un ciclo anterior. Limpia execution_log.md.\"\n943:         )\n944: \n945:     # Plan COMPLETED pero log IN_PROGRESS\n946:     if \"COMPLETED\" in plan_clean and \"IN_PROGRESS\" in log_clean:\n947:         errors.append(\n948:             \"DRIFT: plan=COMPLETED pero log=IN_PROGRESS -- \"\n949:             \"el Builder no cerro su bitacora correctamente.\"\n950:         )\n951: \n952:     # Plan IN_PLANNING con log READY_FOR_REVIEW\n953:     if \"IN_PLANNING\" in plan_clean and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_clean:\n954:         errors.append(\n955:             \"DRIFT: plan=IN_PLANNING pero log=READY_FOR_REVIEW -- \"\n956:             \"estado imposible. El plan debe estar APPROVED antes de que el Builder entregue.\"\n957:         )\n958: \n959:     # Plan N/A pero log activo\n960:     if (\"N/A\" in plan_clean or not plan_clean) and log_clean not in (\n961:         \"N/A\",\n962:         \"COMPLETED\",\n963:         \"PENDING\",\n964:         \"\",\n965:     ):\n966:         errors.append(\n967:             f\"DRIFT: no hay plan activo pero log={log_clean} -- \"\n968:             \"limpia execution_log.md o crea un nuevo work_plan.md.\"\n969:         )\n970: \n971:     return errors\n972: \n973: \n974: def validate_state_files() -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n975:     \"\"\"Valida el formato y consistencia cruzada de los archivos de estado.\"\"\"\n976:     return {\n977:         \"work_plan.md\": _validate_work_plan(),\n978:         \"execution_log.md\": _validate_execution_log(),\n979:         \"notifications.md\": _validate_notifications(),\n980:         \"TURN.md\": _validate_turn_file(),\n981:         \"consistency\": _validate_cross_file_consistency(),\n982:     }\n983: \n984: \n985: def fix_corrupted_notifications() -> bool:\n986:     \"\"\"Intenta reparar notifications.md si esta corrupto.\"\"\"\n987:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n988:     if not content:\n989:         return False\n990:     original = content\n991:     content = re.sub(r\"</thinking>\\s*\", \"\", content)\n992:     content = re.sub(r\"\\n{4,}\", \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\", content)\n993:     if content != original:\n994:         write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, content)\n995:         return True\n996:     return False\n997: \n998: \n999: def archive_old_notifications() -> str | None:\n1000:     \"\"\"Archiva notificaciones antiguas si el archivo es muy grande.\"\"\"\n1001:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1002:     if not content:\n1003:         return None\n1004: \n1005:     file_size_kb = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\")) / 1024\n1006:     entry_count = len([e for e in content.split(\"---\") if e.strip()])\n1007: \n1008:     if (\n1009:         file_size_kb < MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB\n1010:         and entry_count <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES\n1011:     ):\n1012:         return None\n1013: \n1014:     parts = content.split(\"---\")\n1015:     entries = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]\n1016: \n1017:     if len(entries) <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:\n1018:         return None\n1019: \n1020:     entries_to_archive = entries[:-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES]\n1021:     entries_to_keep = entries[-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:]\n1022: \n1023:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n1024:     archive_file = ARCHIVE_DIR / f\"notifications_{timestamp}.md\"\n1025: \n1026:     archive_content = \"# Notificaciones Archivadas\\n\\n\"\n1027:     archive_content += f\"**Fecha:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\\n\"\n1028:     archive_content += \"\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_archive)\n1029: \n1030:     write_file(archive_file, archive_content)\n1031: \n1032:     new_content = \"# Registro de Notificaciones\\n\\n---\\n\\n\"\n1033:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_keep)\n1034:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\"\n1035:     write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, new_content)\n1036: \n1037:     return str(archive_file)\n1038: \n1039: \n1040: def run_finalization_checks() -> dict:\n1041:     \"\"\"Checks adicionales para planes de tipo FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n1042:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"summary\": []}\n1043:     checks = {\n1044:         \"README.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"README.md\",\n1045:         \"CHANGELOG.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n1046:         \"closeout_report.md\": COLLAB_DIR / \"closeout_report.md\",\n1047:     }\n1048:     for name, path in checks.items():\n1049:         if path.exists():\n1050:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[OK] {name}: Presente\")\n1051:         else:\n1052:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[WARN] {name}: No encontrado\")\n1053:     return results\n1054: \n1055: \n1056: def run_quality_gates(plan_type: str = \"IMPLEMENTATION\") -> dict:\n1057:     \"\"\"Ejecuta validaciones automaticas.\"\"\"\n1058:     print(\"\\n[QUALITY GATES] Ejecutando Quality Gates...\")\n1059:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"errors\": [], \"summary\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1060: \n1061:     state_errors = validate_state_files()\n1062:     total_state_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in state_errors.values())\n1063:     if total_state_errors > 0:\n1064:         results[\"warnings\"].append(\n1065:             f\"Archivos de estado: {total_state_errors} problemas\"\n1066:         )\n1067:     else:\n1068:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Estado: Archivos validos\")\n1069: \n1070:     src_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"src\"\n1071:     tests_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"tests\"\n1072:     dirs_to_check = [str(d) for d in [src_dir, tests_dir] if d.exists()]\n1073:     if not dirs_to_check:\n1074:         dirs_to_check = [\".\"]\n1075: \n1076:     try:\n1077:         ruff = subprocess.run(\n1078:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"ruff\", \"check\", *dirs_to_check],\n1079:             capture_output=True,\n1080:             text=True,\n1081:             timeout=60,\n1082:         )\n1083:         if ruff.returncode != 0:\n1084:             results[\"passed\"] = False\n1085:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Ruff: Errores de linting\")\n1086:         else:\n1087:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Ruff: Limpio\")\n1088:     except FileNotFoundError:\n1089:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Ruff: No instalado\")\n1090: \n1091:     if tests_dir.exists():\n1092:         try:\n1093:             pytest_result = subprocess.run(\n1094:                 [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pytest\", \"-q\"],\n1095:                 capture_output=True,\n1096:                 timeout=120,\n1097:                 cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n1098:             )\n1099:             if pytest_result.returncode != 0:\n1100:                 results[\"passed\"] = False\n1101:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Pytest: Tests fallando\")\n1102:             else:\n1103:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Pytest: Tests OK\")\n1104:         except FileNotFoundError:\n1105:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Pytest: No instalado\")\n1106: \n1107:     if plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1108:         fin_results = run_finalization_checks()\n1109:         results[\"summary\"].extend(fin_results[\"summary\"])\n1110: \n1111:     status = \"[PASSED]\" if results[\"passed\"] else \"[FAILED]\"\n1112:     print(f\"   {status}\")\n1113:     return results\n1114: \n1115: \n1116: def update_log_status(new_status: str, note: str) -> bool:\n1117:     \"\"\"Actualiza el estado en execution_log.md.\"\"\"\n1118:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1119:     if not content:\n1120:         return False\n1121:     lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n1122:     for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n1123:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n1124:             lines[i] = f\"**Estado:** {new_status}\"\n1125:             new_content = \"\\n\".join(lines) + f\"\\n\\n{note}\"\n1126:             write_file(EXEC_LOG, new_content)\n1127:             return True\n1128:     return False\n1129: \n1130: \n1131: def create_findings_file(plan_id: str = \"N/A\") -> Path:\n1132:     \"\"\"Crea findings.md desde template.\"\"\"\n1133:     findings_path = COLLAB_DIR / \"findings.md\"\n1134: \n1135:     if findings_path.exists():\n1136:         return findings_path\n1137: \n1138:     template_path = AGENT_DIR / \"templates\" / \"findings_template.md\"\n1139: \n1140:     try:\n1141:         if template_path.exists():\n1142:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1143:         else:\n1144:             template = \"\"\"# Hallazgos de Investigacion\n1145: \n1146: **Plan ID:** {{PLAN_ID}}\n1147: **Creado:** {{DATE}}\n1148: \n1149: ---\n1150: \n1151: ## Hallazgos\n1152: \n1153: <!-- Documenta aqui tus hallazgos durante la investigacion -->\n1154: \"\"\"\n1155: \n1156:         content = template.replace(\"{{PLAN_ID}}\", plan_id)\n1157:         content = content.replace(\n1158:             \"{{DATE}}\", datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1159:         )\n1160: \n1161:         write_file(findings_path, content)\n1162:         print(\"  [OK] Creado findings.md\")\n1163: \n1164:     except Exception as e:\n1165:         print(f\"  [WARN] Error creando findings.md: {e}\")\n1166: \n1167:     return findings_path\n1168: \n1169: \n1170: def generate_project_map() -> str:\n1171:     \"\"\"Genera un mapa actualizado del proyecto.\"\"\"\n1172:     output = [\n1173:         \"# Mapa del Proyecto\",\n1174:         f\"**Actualizado:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\",\n1175:         \"\",\n1176:         \"## Estructura de Archivos\",\n1177:         \"```\",\n1178:     ]\n1179: \n1180:     ignore = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\", \".ruff_cache\"}\n1181:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".txt\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n1182: \n1183:     files_found = []\n1184:     for path in sorted(PROJECT_ROOT.rglob(\"*\")):\n1185:         if any(part in ignore for part in path.parts):\n1186:             continue\n1187:         if path.is_file() and path.suffix in extensions:\n1188:             files_found.append(str(path.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)))\n1189: \n1190:     output.extend(files_found[:50])\n1191:     if len(files_found) > 50:\n1192:         output.append(f\"... (+{len(files_found) - 50} archivos mas)\")\n1193: \n1194:     output.append(\"```\")\n1195:     content = \"\\n\".join(output)\n1196:     write_file(PROJECT_MAP, content)\n1197:     return content\n1198: \n1199: \n1200: def _run_pre_action_hooks(\n1201:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, action_type: str\n1202: ) -> None:\n1203:     \"\"\"Run pre-action hooks if available.\"\"\"\n1204:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1205:         hook_registry.execute(\n1206:             \"pre_action\",\n1207:             {\n1208:                 \"action_type\": action_type,\n1209:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1210:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1211:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1212:             },\n1213:         )\n1214: \n1215: \n1216: def _check_quality_gates(\n1217:     plan_id: str, plan_type: str, plan_status: str, skip_gates: bool\n1218: ) -> dict | None:\n1219:     \"\"\"Check quality gates and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1220:     gate_result = run_quality_gates(plan_type=plan_type)\n1221:     if not gate_result[\"passed\"]:\n1222:         update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"AUTO-REJECTED: Quality Gates fallaron\")\n1223:         return {\n1224:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1225:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1226:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1227:             \"instruction\": \"RECHAZADO. Quality Gates fallaron. Corrige errores.\",\n1228:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1229:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1230:             \"log_status\": \"AUTO-REJECTED\",\n1231:             \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1232:         }\n1233:     return None\n1234: \n1235: \n1236: def _check_completion_verification(\n1237:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_type: str, strict_mode: bool\n1238: ) -> dict | None:\n1239:     \"\"\"Check completion verification in strict mode and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1240:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1241:         stop_result = stop_hook(\n1242:             {\n1243:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1244:                 \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1245:                 \"mode\": \"strict\" if strict_mode else \"normal\",\n1246:             }\n1247:         )\n1248:         if strict_mode and not stop_result.get(\"can_complete\", True):\n1249:             return {\n1250:                 \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1251:                 \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1252:                 \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1253:                 \"instruction\": (\n1254:                     f\"Plan {plan_id} no supera Completion Verification. \"\n1255:                     \"Corrige advertencias antes de review.\"\n1256:                 ),\n1257:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1258:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1259:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1260:                 \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1261:             }\n1262:     return None\n1263: \n1264: \n1265: # Transition table for base routing\n1266: TRANSITION_TABLE = {\n1267:     (\"no_plan\", \"any\"): (\n1268:         \"MANAGER\",\n1269:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1270:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1271:     ),\n1272:     (\"completed\", \"any\"): (\n1273:         \"MANAGER\",\n1274:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1275:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1276:     ),\n1277:     (\"in_planning\", \"any\"): (\n1278:         \"MANAGER\",\n1279:         \"FINALIZE_PLAN\",\n1280:         \"Plan {plan_id} en borrador. Finaliza y cambia a APPROVED\",\n1281:     ),\n1282:     (\"approved\", \"ready_for_review\"): (\n1283:         \"MANAGER\",\n1284:         \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n1285:         \"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n1286:     ),\n1287:     (\"approved\", \"any\"): (\n1288:         \"BUILDER\",\n1289:         \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1290:         \"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Implementa segun work_plan.md\",\n1291:     ),\n1292:     (\"in_review\", \"any\"): (\n1293:         \"MANAGER\",\n1294:         \"REVIEW_CHANGES\",\n1295:         \"Plan {plan_id} en revision. Verifica cambios.\",\n1296:     ),\n1297:     (\"any\", \"blocked\"): (\n1298:         \"MANAGER\",\n1299:         \"RESOLVE_BLOCK\",\n1300:         \"Builder BLOQUEADO en {plan_id}. Resuelve en review_queue.md\",\n1301:     ),\n1302: }\n1303: \n1304: \n1305: def _lookup_transition(\n1306:     plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_id: str\n1307: ) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:\n1308:     \"\"\"Lookup transition from table.\"\"\"\n1309:     # Normalize status for lookup\n1310:     plan_key = (\n1311:         \"no_plan\"\n1312:         if not plan_status or plan_status in (\"COMPLETED\", \"N/A\")\n1313:         else plan_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\")\n1314:     )\n1315:     log_key = log_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\") if log_status else \"any\"\n1316: \n1317:     # Check specific matches first\n1318:     for (p_key, l_key), (role, action, template) in TRANSITION_TABLE.items():\n1319:         if (p_key == plan_key or p_key == \"any\") and (\n1320:             l_key == log_key or l_key == \"any\"\n1321:         ):\n1322:             instruction = template.format(plan_id=plan_id)\n1323:             return role, action, instruction\n1324: \n1325:     return None\n1326: \n1327: \n1328: def determine_next_action(skip_gates: bool = False, strict_mode: bool = False) -> dict:\n1329:     \"\"\"Analiza el estado y determina la siguiente accion.\"\"\"\n1330:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1331:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1332: \n1333:     plan_status = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1334:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1335:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1336:     plan_type = get_plan_type(plan_content)\n1337: \n1338:     # Determine pre-action type for hooks\n1339:     pre_action_type = \"CHECK_STATUS\"\n1340:     if not plan_content.strip() or \"COMPLETED\" in plan_status or \"N/A\" in plan_status:\n1341:         pre_action_type = \"CREATE_PLAN\"\n1342:     elif \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1343:         pre_action_type = \"REVIEW_WORK\"\n1344:     elif \"APPROVED\" in plan_status:\n1345:         pre_action_type = \"IMPLEMENT\"\n1346: \n1347:     _run_pre_action_hooks(plan_id, plan_status, log_status, pre_action_type)\n1348: \n1349:     # Check quality gates for review state\n1350:     if (\n1351:         \"APPROVED\" in plan_status\n1352:         and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status\n1353:         and not skip_gates\n1354:     ):\n1355:         gate_action = _check_quality_gates(plan_id, plan_type, plan_status, skip_gates)\n1356:         if gate_action:\n1357:             return {\"plan_type\": plan_type, **gate_action}\n1358: \n1359:     # Check completion verification in strict mode\n1360:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1361:         verification_action = _check_completion_verification(\n1362:             plan_id, plan_status, log_status, plan_type, strict_mode\n1363:         )\n1364:         if verification_action:\n1365:             return {\"plan_type\": plan_type, **verification_action}\n1366: \n1367:     # Handle finalization instruction variation\n1368:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1369:         instruction = f\"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Ejecuta cierre segun work_plan.md\"\n1370:         return {\n1371:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1372:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1373:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1374:             \"instruction\": instruction,\n1375:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1376:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1377:             \"log_status\": log_status,\n1378:             \"action_type\": \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1379:             \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1380:         }\n1381: \n1382:     # Lookup base transition\n1383:     transition = _lookup_transition(plan_status, log_status, plan_id)\n1384:     if transition:\n1385:         role, action_type, instruction = transition\n1386:         context_file = \".manager_rules\" if role == \"MANAGER\" else \".builder_rules\"\n1387:         workflow_file = (\n1388:             \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\"\n1389:             if role == \"MANAGER\"\n1390:             else \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\"\n1391:         )\n1392:         return {\n1393:             \"role\": role,\n1394:             \"context_file\": context_file,\n1395:             \"workflow_file\": workflow_file,\n1396:             \"instruction\": instruction,\n1397:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1398:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1399:             \"log_status\": log_status,\n1400:             \"action_type\": action_type,\n1401:             \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1402:         }\n1403: \n1404:     # Fallback for unknown states\n1405:     return {\n1406:         \"role\": \"UNKNOWN\",\n1407:         \"context_file\": \"N/A\",\n1408:         \"workflow_file\": \"N/A\",\n1409:         \"instruction\": \"Estado indeterminado. Revisa archivos manualmente.\",\n1410:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1411:         \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1412:         \"log_status\": log_status,\n1413:         \"action_type\": \"MANUAL_INTERVENTION\",\n1414:         \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1415:     }\n1416: \n1417: \n1418: def should_overwrite_turn(turn_path: Path, force_reset: bool = False) -> bool:\n1419:     \"\"\"Devuelve True solo si TURN.md debe ser regenerado.\"\"\"\n1420:     if force_reset or not turn_path.exists():\n1421:         return True\n1422:     try:\n1423:         content = turn_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1424:         return bool(\"UNKNOWN\" in content or \"MANUAL_INTERVENTION\" in content)\n1425:     except Exception:\n1426:         return True\n1427: \n1428: \n1429: def update_turn_file(action: dict) -> None:\n1430:     \"\"\"Actualiza TURN.md con informacion del turno actual.\"\"\"\n1431:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1432: \n1433:     content = f\"\"\"# TURNO ACTUAL\n1434: \n1435: **Ultima actualizacion:** {timestamp}\n1436: \n1437: ---\n1438: \n1439: ## Agente Activo\n1440: \n1441: | Campo | Valor |\n1442: |-------|-------|\n1443: | **ROL** | **{action[\"role\"]}** |\n1444: | **Plan ID** | {action[\"plan_id\"]} |\n1445: | **Tipo** | {action.get(\"plan_type\", \"IMPLEMENTATION\")} |\n1446: | **Accion** | {action[\"action_type\"]} |\n1447: \n1448: ---\n1449: \n1450: ## Instruccion\n1451: \n1452: > {action[\"instruction\"]}\n1453: \n1454: ---\n1455: \n1456: ## Archivos a Leer\n1457: \n1458: 1. `{action[\"context_file\"]}` (Contexto del rol)\n1459: 2. `{action[\"workflow_file\"]}` (Flujo de trabajo)\n1460: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n1461: \n1462: ---\n1463: \n1464: ## Estado del Sistema\n1465: \n1466: | Archivo | Estado |\n1467: |---------|--------|\n1468: | work_plan.md | {action[\"plan_status\"]} |\n1469: | execution_log.md | {action[\"log_status\"]} |\n1470: \n1471: ---\n1472: \n1473: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n1474: \"\"\"\n1475:     write_file(TURN_FILE, content)\n1476: \n1477: \n1478: def print_human_readable(action: dict) -> None:\n1479:     \"\"\"Muestra el estado de forma legible.\"\"\"\n1480:     role = action[\"role\"]\n1481:     role_emoji = {\"MANAGER\": \"MANAGER\", \"BUILDER\": \"BUILDER\", \"UNKNOWN\": \"UNKNOWN\"}.get(\n1482: \"\"\n1487: \n1488:     print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n1489:     print(\"  SISTEMA MULTI-AGENTE v5 - Panel de Control\")\n1490:     print(\"=\" * 70)\n1491:     print(f\"\\n  TURNO ACTUAL: {role_emoji} {role}{type_label}\")\n1492:     print(f\"  Plan: {action['plan_id']}\")\n1493:     print(\"\\n  ESTADOS:\")\n1494:     print(f\"     - Plan:     {action['plan_status']}\")\n1495:     print(f\"     - if result[\"can_complete\"] else 1\n1539:     print(\"[WARN] Completion checker no disponible.\")\n1540:     return 1\n1541: \n1542: \n1543: def _handle_mark_ready(  # noqa: C901 - linear guard chain (HUMAN_GATE, already-ready, breaker)\n1544:     scope_override: str | None, json_output: bool, force_mode: bool\n1545: ) -> int:\n1546:     \"\"\"Handle --mark-ready flag.\"\"\"\n1547:     plan_content, log_content, plan_id = _load_mark_ready_context()\n1548:     if not plan_id or plan_id == \"N/A\":\n1549:         print(\"[ERROR] No active plan found.\")\n1550:         return 1\n1551: \n1552:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1553: \n1554:     # WP-2026-106 hotfix: a ticket escalated to HUMAN_GATE can only leave that\n1555:     # state by explicit human intervention. The Builder must not be able to\n1556:     # re-declare READY_FOR_REVIEW and bypass the Manager review cycle.\n1557:     if \"HUMAN_GATE\" in log_status:\n1558:         msg = (\n1559:             f\"Ticket {plan_id} is in HUMAN_GATE. mark-ready is blocked: \"\n1560:             \"only a human can move it out of HUMAN_GATE.\"\n1561:         )\n1562:         if json_output:\n1563:             print(\n1564:                 json.dumps(\n1565:                     {\"status\": \"blocked\", \"reason\": msg, \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2\n1566:                 )\n1567:             )\n1568:         else:\n1569:             print(f\"[ERROR] {msg}\")\n1570:         return 1\n1571: \n1572:     if \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1573:         if json_output:\n1574:             print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"already_ready\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2))\n1575:         else:\n1576:             print(f\"[INFO] Ticket {plan_id} already marked as ready.\")\n1577:         # Ensure STATE_CHANGED and BUILDER_EXIT are emitted idempotently\n1578:         # BUILDER_EXIT MUST come before STATE_CHANGED to maintain order invariant\n1579:         if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n1580:             # Check if BUILDER_EXIT already exists\n1581:             builder_exit = event_bus.latest_event(\n1582:                 ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\"\n1583:             )\n1584:             if not builder_exit:\n1585:                 _emit_builder_exit(\n1586:                     plan_id=plan_id,\n1587:                     exit_reason=\"Implementation completed and ready for review\",\n1588:                     completion_summary=f\"Ticket {plan_id} implementation completed. All quality gates passed. Scope validated against Files Likely Touched.\",\n1589:                 )\n1590: \n1591:             # Check if STATE_CHANGED already exists\n1592:             latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n1593:                 ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n1594:             )\n1595:             if (\n1596:                 not latest_state_event\n1597:                 or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n1598:             ):\n1599:                 bus_from_state = (\n1600:                     latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n1601:                     if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n1602:                     else log_status\n1603:                 )\n1604:                 event_bus.emit(\n1605:                     event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n1606:                     ticket_id=plan_id,\n1607:                     actor=\"BUILDER\",\n1608:                     payload={\n1609:                         \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n1610:                         \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n1611:                         \"reason\": \"Ticket already in ready state\",\n1612:                         \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n1613:                     },\n1614:                 )\n1615:         return 0\n1616: \n1617:     # Exit gate: Check circuit breaker before allowing close\n1618:     breaker_status = _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id)\n1619:     if breaker_status[\"open\"]:\n1620:         print(f\"[ERROR] Circuit breaker is OPEN: {breaker_status['reason']}\")\n1621:         print(\n1622:             f\"  Failures: {breaker_status['failures']}, No-progress count: {breaker_status['no_progress_count']}\"\n1623:         )\n1624:         print(\"  Resolve the underlying issue before marking ready.\")\n1625:         return 1\n1626: \n1627:     gate_result = check_scope_gate(plan_content, get_changed_files(), _exclude_files())\n1628:     if not _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result, scope_override):\n1629:         return 1\n1630: \n1631:     # Exit gate: Emit BUILDER_EXIT event - REQUIRED for ticket closure\n1632:     # MUST occur BEFORE STATE_CHANGED to maintain order invariant\n1633:     _emit_builder_exit(\n1634:         plan_id=plan_id,\n1635:         exit_reason=\"Implementation completed and ready for review\",\n1636:         completion_summary=f\"Ticket {plan_id} implementation completed. Scope validated against Files Likely Touched.\",\n1637:     )\n1638: \n1639:     # Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n1640:     _auto_archive_closed_artifacts()\n1641: \n1642:     _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id, plan_content)\n1643: \n1644:     # Reset circuit breaker on successful completion\n1645:     _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id)\n1646: \n1647:     # Release builder lock\n1648:     _release_builder_lock(plan_id)\n1649: \n1650:     if json_output:\n1651:         print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"marked_ready\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2))\n1652:     else:\n1653:         print(f\"[OK] Ticket {plan_id} marked as ready for review.\")\n1654: \n1655:     return 0\n1656: \n1657: \n1658: def _handle_bootstrap_ticket(json_output: bool) -> int:\n1659:     \"\"\"Handle --bootstrap-ticket flag.\n1660: \n1661:     Emit STATE_CHANGED -> IN_PROGRESS for the active ticket if no such event\n1662:     exists yet in the bus. Called by launch_agent_terminals.ps1 before opening\n1663:     agent windows, ensuring the bridge and other consumers can derive initial\n1664:     state from events.jsonl instead of falling back to UNKNOWN.\n1665:     Idempotent: if a STATE_CHANGED event already exists for this ticket,\n1666:     returns early with status 'already_bootstrapped'.\n1667:     \"\"\"\n1668:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1669:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1670: \n1671:     if not plan_id or plan_id == \"N/A\":\n1672:         if json_output:\n1673:             print(\n1674:                 json.dumps(\n1675:                     {\"error\": \"No active plan found\", \"status\": \"no_plan\"}, indent=2\n1676:                 )\n1677:             )\n1678:         else:\n1679:             print(\"[ERROR] No active plan found in work_plan.md\")\n1680:         return 1\n1681: \n1682:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1683:         if json_output:\n1684:             print(\n1685:                 json.dumps(\n1686:                     {\n1687:                         \"status\": \"skipped\",\n1688:                         \"reason\": \"bus_not_available\",\n1689:                         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1690:                     },\n1691:                     indent=2,\n1692:                 )\n1693:             )\n1694:         else:\n1695:             print(f\"[WARN] Event bus not available. Skipping bootstrap for {plan_id}.\")\n1696:         return 0\n1697: \n1698:     latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n1699:         ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n1700:     )\n1701:     if latest_state_event:\n1702:         if json_output:\n1703:             print(\n1704:                 json.dumps(\n1705:                     {\"status\": \"already_bootstrapped\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2\n1706:                 )\n1707:             )\n\n(Showing lines 1508-1707 of 2958. Aquí está mi análisis:\n\n## Revisión WP-2026-142: Symmetric mark-ready scope gate\n\n### Verificación de Criterios de Aceptación\n\n1. **`check_scope_gate` bloquea cobertura cero** ✅ - Implementado en líneas 375-378 de `agent_controller.py`. El test `test_check_scope_gate_zero_overlap_blocks` lo verifica.\n\n2. **`check_scope_gate` sigue bloqueando archivos fuera de scope** ✅ - Implementado en línea 371 (`valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0`). El test `test_check_scope_gate_partial_overlap_warns` lo verifica.\n\n4. **`--mark-ready` respeta el nuevo gate sin romper emisión del bus** ✅ - `_handle_mark_ready` (línea 1628) llama a `_scope_gate_allows_close` antes de proceder. Los tests en `test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py` verifican la emisión idempotente de STATE_CHANGED.\n\n5. **Validación canónica pasa sin errores** ✅ - execution_log.md muestra evidencia:\n   - `test_scope_gate.py` → 16 passed\n   - `test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py` → 5 passed\n   - `--validate --json --force` → sin errores ni warnings\n\n### Verificación de Anti-patrones (AP-01 a AP-08)\n\n- **AP-01 Mock drift**: No detectado. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The Builder runs the existing suite, sees it pass, and declares quality gates satisfied — but the new functions introduced in the diff have no direct tests. Th... (unknown)\n30: - [2026-05-25] deliverable_type classification for scaffolding tickets: when Files Likely Touched contains only structural non-Python files (.gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic, the co... (unknown)\n31: - [2026-05-25] Validator evidence gate: when a work_plan explicitly declares a validator as a quality gate (skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), the Manager must find explicit outpu... (unknown)\n32: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard. When a method acquires an exclusive resource (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire, lock-file creation) AND can be reached f... (unknown)\n33: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts. When a method changes from returning implicit None to returning explicit bool, all callers must be updated from generic t... (unknown)\n34: \n35: --- work_plan.md ---\n36: # Work Plan - WP-2026-142\n37: \n38: ## Metadata\n39: - **ID:** WP-2026-142\n40: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n41: - **deliverable_type:** code\n42: - **Titulo:** Symmetric mark-ready scope gate\n43: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n44: \n45: ## Objetivo\n46: Endurecer `--mark-ready` para que el Builder no pueda superar el scope gate declarando archivos tocados que no aparecen en el diff real. El gate conservara el bloqueo actual por archivos fuera de scope, añadira bloqueo por cobertura cero y dejara cobertura parcial como warning.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - `check_scope_gate` comparara en ambas direcciones `Files Likely Touched` contra los archivos realmente cambiados.\n50: - El bloqueo actual por `changed_files - whitelist` se conserva sin cambios.\n51: - Si `whitelist ∩ changed_files = ∅`, el cierre se bloquea por fabricacion total.\n52: - Si `whitelist - changed_files ≠ ∅`, se registrara advertencia de cobertura parcial sin bloquear.\n53: - `changed_files is None` y whitelist vacia mantienen el comportamiento actual de paso.\n54: \n55: ## Files Likely Touched\n56: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n57: - `tests/unit/test_scope_gate.py`\n58: - `tests/unit/test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py`\n59: \n60: ## Fases\n61: 1. Extender `check_scope_gate` con cobertura simetrica y resultados diferenciados por severidad.\n62: 2. Añadir pruebas unitarias para cobertura cero, cobertura parcial y preservacion de los casos base.\n63: 3. Verificar el flujo end-to-end de `--mark-ready` y el evento de bus asociado.\n64: 4. Validar con los gates locales del repositorio.\n65: \n66: ## Calidad\n67: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_scope_gate.py -q`\n68: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py -q`\n69: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n70: \n71: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n72: - `check_scope_gate` bloquea cobertura cero y sigue bloqueando archivos fuera de scope.\n73: - La cobertura parcial genera warning, pero no bloquea.\n74: - `--mark-ready` respeta el nuevo resultado del gate sin romper la emision del bus.\n75: - La validacion canonica pasa sin errores.\n76: - La validacion canonica pasa sin errores.\n77: \n78: \n79: --- STATE.md ---\n80: # State - WP-2026-142\n81: \n82: Plan Activo: WP-2026-142\n83: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n84: Rol activo: BUILDER\n85: \n86: Resumen:\n87: WP-2026-142 endurece `--mark-ready` para evitar fabricacion de scope: bloqueo por cobertura cero, warning por cobertura parcial y preservacion del bloqueo por cambios fuera de la whitelist.\n88: \n89: Notas:\n90: - El alcance es code.\n91: - `events.jsonl` y el circuit breaker quedan fuera de alcance.\n92: - El cambio debe respetar los casos base no-git y whitelist vacia.\n93: \n94: \n95: --- TURN.md ---\n96: # TURNO ACTUAL\n97: \n98: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-26 12:46:08\n99: \n100: ---\n101: \n102: ## Agente Activo\n103: \n104: | Campo | Valor |\n105: |-------|-------|\n106: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n107: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-142 |\n108: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n109: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n110: \n111: ---\n112: \n113: ## Instruccion\n114: \n115: > Builder completo WP-2026-142. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-142_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779792748626,"end":1779792748726}},"id":"prt_e63ea1883001z49WtwBuea9nU0","sessionID":"ses_19c163b8effeQqB9l32886p8x7","messageID":"msg_e63e9c527001TZW1Xkrr7W64yF"}}
