if result[\"can_complete\"] else 1\n1539:     print(\"[WARN] Completion checker no disponible.\")\n1540:     return 1\n1541: \n1542: \n1543: def _handle_mark_ready(  # noqa: C901 - linear guard chain (HUMAN_GATE, already-ready, breaker)\n1544:     scope_override: str | None, json_output: bool, force_mode: bool\n1545: ) -> int:\n1546:     \"\"\"Handle --mark-ready flag.\n1547: \n1548:     WP-2026-143: Bus-backed idempotency. Consults bus-derived state as authority.\n1549:     - READY_FOR_REVIEW, READY_TO_CLOSE, COMPLETED: clean no-op (no duplicate events).\n1550:     - HUMAN_GATE: blocked (requires human intervention).\n1551:     - Fallback to markdown-based logic if bus is unavailable.\n1552:     \"\"\"\n1553:     from bus.state_machine import TicketState\n1554: \n1555:     plan_content, log_content, plan_id = _load_mark_ready_context()\n1556:     if not plan_id or plan_id == \"N/A\":\n1557:         print(\"[ERROR] No active plan found.\")\n1558:         return 1\n1559: \n1560:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1561: \n1562:     # WP-2026-143: Get bus-derived state as authority\n1563:     bus_state = None\n1564:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n1565:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine\n1566: \n1567:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n1568:         if events:\n1569:             bus_state = StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n1570: \n1571:     # WP-2026-106 hotfix: a ticket escalated to HUMAN_GATE can only leave that\n1572:     # state by explicit human intervention. The Builder must not be able to\n1573:     # re-declare READY_FOR_REVIEW and bypass the Manager review cycle.\n1574:     if bus_state == TicketState.HUMAN_GATE or (\n1575:         bus_state is None and \"HUMAN_GATE\" in log_status\n1576:     ):\n1577:         msg = (\n1578:             f\"Ticket {plan_id} is in HUMAN_GATE. mark-ready is blocked: \"\n1579:             \"only a human can move it out of HUMAN_GATE.\"\n1580:         )\n1581:         if json_output:\n1582:             print(\n1583:                 json.dumps(\n1584:                     {\"status\": \"blocked\", \"reason\": msg, \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2\n1585:                 )\n1586:             )\n1587:         else:\n1588:             print(f\"[ERROR] {msg}\")\n1589:         return 1\n1590: \n1591:     # WP-2026-143: Bus-backed idempotency guard\n1592:     # If bus state is READY_FOR_REVIEW, READY_TO_CLOSE, or COMPLETED, exit cleanly\n1593:     # without emitting duplicate BUILDER_EXIT or STATE_CHANGED events.\n1594:     if bus_state is not None and bus_state in (\n1595:         TicketState.READY_FOR_REVIEW,\n1596:         TicketState.READY_TO_CLOSE,\n1597:         TicketState.COMPLETED,\n1598:     ):\n1599:             if json_output:\n1600:                 print(\n1601:                     json.dumps(\n1602:                         {\n1603:                             \"status\": \"already_ready\",\n1604:                             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1605:                             \"bus_state\": bus_state.value,\n1606:                         },\n1607:                         indent=2,\n1608:                     )\n1609:                 )\n1610:             else:\n1611:                 print(\n1612:                     f\"[INFO] Ticket {plan_id} bus state is {bus_state.value}. No action needed.\"\n1613:                 )\n1614:             return 0\n1615: \n1616:     # Fallback: markdown-based idempotency (bus unavailable or no events)\n1617:     if bus_state is None and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1618:         if json_output:\n1619:             print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"already_ready\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2))\n1620:         else:\n1621:             print(f\"[INFO] Ticket {plan_id} already marked as ready.\")\n1622:         # Ensure STATE_CHANGED and BUILDER_EXIT are emitted idempotently\n1623:         # BUILDER_EXIT MUST come before STATE_CHANGED to maintain order invariant\n1624:         if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n1625:             # Check if BUILDER_EXIT already exists\n1626:             builder_exit = event_bus.latest_event(\n1627:                 ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\"\n1628:             )\n1629:             if not builder_exit:\n1630:                 _emit_builder_exit(\n1631:                     plan_id=plan_id,\n1632:                     exit_reason=\"Implementation completed and ready for review\",\n1633:                     completion_summary=f\"Ticket {plan_id} implementation completed. All quality gates passed. Scope validated against Files Likely Touched.\",\n1634:                 )\n1635: \n1636:             # Check if STATE_CHANGED already exists\n1637:             latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n1638:                 ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n1639:             )\n1640:             if (\n1641:                 not latest_state_event\n1642:                 or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n1643:             ):\n1644:                 bus_from_state = (\n1645:                     latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n1646:                     if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n1647:                     else log_status\n1648:                 )\n1649:                 event_bus.emit(\n1650:                     event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n1651:                     ticket_id=plan_id,\n1652:                     actor=\"BUILDER\",\n1653:                     payload={\n1654:                         \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n1655:                         \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n1656:                         \"reason\": \"Ticket already in ready state\",\n1657:                         \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n1658:                     },\n1659:                 )\n1660:         return 0\n1661: \n1662:     # Exit gate: Check circuit breaker before allowing close\n1663:     breaker_status = _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id)\n1664:     if breaker_status[\"open\"]:\n1665:         print(f\"[ERROR] Circuit breaker is OPEN: {breaker_status['reason']}\")\n1666:         print(\n1667:             f\"  Failures: {breaker_status['failures']}, No-progress count: {breaker_status['no_progress_count']}\"\n1668:         )\n1669:         print(\"  Resolve the underlying issue before marking ready.\")\n1670:         return 1\n1671: \n1672:     gate_result = check_scope_gate(plan_content, get_changed_files(), _exclude_files())\n1673:     if not _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result, scope_override):\n1674:         return 1\n1675: \n1676:     # Exit gate: Emit BUILDER_EXIT event - REQUIRED for ticket closure\n1677:     # MUST occur BEFORE STATE_CHANGED to maintain order invariant\n1678:     _emit_builder_exit(\n1679:         plan_id=plan_id,\n1680:         exit_reason=\"Implementation completed and ready for review\",\n1681:         completion_summary=f\"Ticket {plan_id} implementation completed. All quality gates passed. Scope validated against Files Likely Touched.\",\n1682:     )\n1683: \n1684:     # Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n1685:     _auto_archive_closed_artifacts()\n1686: \n1687:     _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id, plan_content)\n1688: \n1689:     # Reset circuit breaker on successful completion\n1690:     _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id)\n1691: \n1692:     # Release builder lock\n1693:     _release_builder_lock(plan_id)\n1694: \n1695:     if json_output:\n1696:         print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"marked_ready\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2))\n1697:     else:\n1698:         print(f\"[OK] Ticket {plan_id} marked as ready for review.\")\n1699: \n1700:     return 0\n1701: \n1702: \n1703: def _handle_bootstrap_ticket(json_output: bool) -> int:\n1704:     \"\"\"Handle --bootstrap-ticket flag.\n1705: \n1706:     Emit STATE_CHANGED -> IN_PROGRESS for the active ticket if no such event\n1707:     exists yet in the bus. Called by launch_agent_terminals.ps1 before opening\n1708:     agent windows, ensuring the bridge and other consumers can derive initial\n1709:     state from events.jsonl instead of falling back to UNKNOWN.\n1710:     Idempotent: if a STATE_CHANGED event already exists for this ticket,\n1711:     returns early with status 'already_bootstrapped'.\n1712:     \"\"\"\n1713:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1714:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1715: \n1716:     if not plan_id or plan_id == \"N/A\":\n1717:         if json_output:\n1718:             print(\n1719:                 json.dumps(\n1720:                     {\"error\": \"No active plan found\", \"status\": \"no_plan\"}, indent=2\n1721:                 )\n1722:             )\n1723:         else:\n1724:             print(\"[ERROR] No active plan found in work_plan.md\")\n1725:         return 1\n1726: \n1727:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1728:         if json_output:\n1729:             print(\n1730:                 json.dumps(\n1731:                     {\n1732:                         \"status\": \"skipped\",\n1733:                         \"reason\": \"bus_not_available\",\n1734:                         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1735:                     },\n1736:                     indent=2,\n1737:                 )\n1738:             )\n1739:         else:\n1740:             print(f\"[WARN] Event bus not available. Skipping bootstrap for {plan_id}.\")\n1741:         return 0\n1742: \n1743:     latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n1744:         ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n1745:     )\n1746:     if latest_state_event:\n1747:         if json_output:\n1748:             print(\n1749:                 json.dumps(\n1750:                     {\"status\": \"already_bootstrapped\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2\n1751:                 )\n1752:             )\n1753:         else:\n1754:             print(\n1755:                 f\"[INFO] Ticket {plan_id} already has STATE_CHANGED in bus. Skipping bootstrap.\"\n1756:             )\n1757:         return 0\n1758: \n1759:     event_bus.emit(\n1760:         event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n1761:         ticket_id=plan_id,\n1762:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n1763:         payload={\n1764:             \"from_state\": \"BOOTSTRAP\",\n1765:             \"to_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n1766:             \"reason\": \"Initial state bootstrap for launcher preflight\",\n1767:             \"source\": \"bootstrap\",\n1768:         },\n1769:     )\n1770: \n1771:     if json_output:\n1772:         print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"bootstrapped\", \"plan_id\": plan_id}, indent=2))\n1773:     else:\n1774:         print(f\"[OK] Bootstrapped STATE_CHANGED -> IN_PROGRESS for {plan_id}\")\n1775: \n1776:     return 0\n1777: \n1778: \n1779: def _check_bus_drift(plan_content: str, log_status: str) -> list[str]:\n1780:     \"\"\"Check for drift between Markdown state and bus events.\"\"\"\n1781:     warnings = []\n1782:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1783:         warnings.append(\"Event bus not available for drift detection\")\n1784:         return warnings\n1785: \n1786:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1787:     if not plan_id or plan_id == \"N/A\":\n1788:         warnings.append(\"No active ticket found for bus drift check\")\n1789:         return warnings\n1790: \n1791:     latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n1792:         ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n1793:     )\n1794:     if latest_state_event:\n1795:         bus_state = latest_state_event.payload.get(\n1796:             \"to_state\"\n1797:         ) or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"state\")\n1798:         if bus_state != log_status:\n1799:             warnings.append(\n1800:                 f\"Drift detected: Markdown state='{log_status}' vs Bus state='{bus_state}' for ticket {plan_id}\"\n1801:             )\n1802:     else:\n1803:         warnings.append(f\"No STATE_CHANGED event found in bus for ticket {plan_id}\")\n1804: \n1805:     return warnings\n1806: \n1807: \n1808: def _check_pre_closure_invariants(plan_id: str) -> list[str]:\n1809:     \"\"\"Check pre-closure invariants (IN_PROGRESS, APPROVED, PENDING).\"\"\"\n1810:     result = []\n1811:     # Check: no BUILDER_EXIT should exist before close\n1812:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1813:         return result\n1814:     builder_exit = event_bus.latest_event(ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\")\n1815:     if builder_exit:\n1816:         result.append(\n1817:             \"BUILDER_EXIT exists but ticket not in READY_FOR_REVIEW/COMPLETED\"\n1818:         )\n1819:     return result\n1820: \n1821: \n1822: def _check_post_closure_built_exit(\n1823:     plan_id: str, log_status: str\n1824: ) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:\n1825:     \"\"\"Check BUILDER_EXIT invariant. Returns (errors, warnings).\"\"\"\n1826:     errors, warnings = [], []\n1827:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1828:         return errors, warnings\n1829: \n1830:     builder_exit = event_bus.latest_event(ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\")\n1831:     if not builder_exit:\n1832:         errors.append(\n1833:             f\"INVARIANT: Missing BUILDER_EXIT event for ticket {plan_id} in state {log_status}\"\n1834:         )\n1835:     else:\n1836:         # ticket_id is at event level, exit_reason and completion_summary are in payload\n1837:         payload = builder_exit.payload\n1838:         if not builder_exit.ticket_id:\n1839:             errors.append(f\"INVARIANT: BUILDER_EXIT missing ticket_id for {plan_id}\")\n1840:         if not payload.get(\"exit_reason\"):\n1841:             errors.append(\n1842:                 f\"INVARIANT: BUILDER_EXIT missing required field exit_reason for {plan_id}\"\n1843:             )\n1844:         if not payload.get(\"completion_summary\"):\n1845:             warnings.append(f\"BUILDER_EXIT missing completion_summary for {plan_id}\")\n1846:     return errors, warnings\n1847: \n1848: \n1849: def _check_post_closure_breaker(log_status: str) -> list[str]:\n1850:     \"\"\"Check circuit breaker invariant. Returns errors.\"\"\"\n1851:     errors = []\n1852:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n1853:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\" and log_status == \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\":\n1854:         errors.append(\"INVARIANT: Circuit breaker OPEN but ticket in READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n1855:     return errors\n1856: \n1857: \n1858: def _check_post_closure_lock(plan_id: str, log_status: str) -> list[str]:\n1859:     \"\"\"Check builder lock invariant. Returns warnings.\"\"\"\n1860:     warnings = []\n1861:     lock = _read_builder_lock()\n1862:     if lock and lock.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id and log_status == \"COMPLETED\":\n1863:         warnings.append(f\"Builder lock still held for completed ticket {plan_id}\")\n1864:     return warnings\n1865: \n1866: \n1867: def _check_post_closure_state_changed(\n1868:     plan_id: str, log_status: str\n1869: ) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:\n1870:     \"\"\"Check STATE_CHANGED invariant. Returns (errors, warnings).\"\"\"\n1871:     errors, warnings = [], []\n1872:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1873:         return errors, warnings\n1874: \n1875:     state_event = event_bus.latest_event(ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\")\n1876:     if not state_event:\n1877:         errors.append(f\"INVARIANT: Missing STATE_CHANGED event for ticket {plan_id}\")\n1878:     else:\n1879:         bus_state = state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n1880:         if bus_state and bus_state != log_status:\n1881:             warnings.append(\n1882:                 f\"STATE_CHANGED to_state='{bus_state}' differs from log_status='{log_status}'\"\n1883:             )\n1884:     return errors, warnings\n1885: \n1886: \n1887: def _check_builder_exit_order(plan_id: str) -> list[str]:\n1888:     \"\"\"\n1889:     Check that BUILDER_EXIT comes before STATE_CHANGED READY_FOR_REVIEW in sequence.\n1890:     Returns warnings list (not errors, to avoid invalidating historical tickets).\n1891: \n1892:     This function checks the COMPLETE sequence of events, not just the latest.\n1893:     For each STATE_CHANGED -> READY_FOR_REVIEW, there must be a BUILDER_EXIT\n1894:     with a lower sequence number.\n1895:     \"\"\"\n1896:     warnings = []\n1897:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n1898:         return warnings\n1899: \n1900:     # Get all BUILDER_EXIT events for this ticket\n1901:     builder_exits = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\")\n1902:     if not builder_exits:\n1903:         # No BUILDER_EXIT yet - invariant doesn't apply\n1904:         return warnings\n1905: \n1906:     # Get all STATE_CHANGED events for this ticket with to_state=READY_FOR_REVIEW\n1907:     state_events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\")\n1908:     ready_for_review_events = [\n1909:         e for e in state_events if e.payload.get(\"to_state\") == \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n1910:     ]\n1911: \n1912:     if not ready_for_review_events:\n1913:         # No STATE_CHANGED READY_FOR_REVIEW yet - invariant doesn't apply\n1914:         return warnings\n1915: \n1916:     # Check order: for EACH STATE_CHANGED READY_FOR_REVIEW, there must be a\n1917:     # BUILDER_EXIT with a lower sequence number.\n1918:     # This detects inversions at any point in the sequence, not just the latest.\n1919:     for ready_event in ready_for_review_events:\n1920:         # Find if there's any BUILDER_EXIT before this STATE_CHANGED\n1921:         has_prior_exit = any(\n1922:             exit_event.sequence_number < ready_event.sequence_number\n1923:             for exit_event in builder_exits\n1924:         )\n1925: \n1926:         if not has_prior_exit:\n1927:             warnings.append(\n1928:                 f\"ORDER INVARIANT: STATE_CHANGED READY_FOR_REVIEW (seq={ready_event.sequence_number}) \"\n1929:                 f\"has no prior BUILDER_EXIT for ticket {plan_id}. \"\n1930:                 f\"BUILDER_EXIT should be emitted before STATE_CHANGED READY_FOR_REVIEW.\"\n1931:             )\n1932: \n1933:     return warnings\n1934: \n1935: \n1936: def _check_post_closure_invariants(plan_id: str, log_status: str) -> dict:\n1937:     \"\"\"Check post-closure invariants (READY_FOR_REVIEW, COMPLETED).\"\"\"\n1938:     result = {\"errors\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1939: \n1940:     # Invariant 1: BUILDER_EXIT\n1941:     exit_errors, exit_warnings = _check_post_closure_built_exit(plan_id, log_status)\n1942:     result[\"errors\"].extend(exit_errors)\n1943:     result[\"warnings\"].extend(exit_warnings)\n1944: \n1945:     # Invariant 2: Circuit breaker\n1946:     result[\"errors\"].extend(_check_post_closure_breaker(log_status))\n1947: \n1948:     # Invariant 3: Builder lock\n1949:     result[\"warnings\"].extend(_check_post_closure_lock(plan_id, log_status))\n1950: \n1951:     # Invariant 4: STATE_CHANGED\n1952:     state_errors, state_warnings = _check_post_closure_state_changed(\n1953:         plan_id, log_status\n1954:     )\n1955:     result[\"errors\"].extend(state_errors)\n1956:     result[\"warnings\"].extend(state_warnings)\n1957: \n1958:     # Invariant 5: Order check - BUILDER_EXIT must come before STATE_CHANGED READY_FOR_REVIEW\n1959:     order_warnings = _check_builder_exit_order(plan_id)\n1960:     result[\"warnings\"].extend(order_warnings)\n1961: \n1962:     return result\n1963: \n1964: \n1965: def _check_invariants(plan_content: str, log_content: str, log_status: str) -> dict:\n1966:     \"\"\"\n1967:     Check pre and post-closure invariants.\n1968:     Returns dict with 'errors' and 'warnings' lists.\n1969:     \"\"\"\n1970:     result = {\"errors\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1971:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1972: \n1973:     if not plan_id or plan_id == \"N/A\":\n1974:         result[\"warnings\"].append(\"No active plan for invariant check\")\n1975:         return result\n1976: \n1977:     # Pre-closure invariants\n1978:     if log_status in (\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"APPROVED\", \"PENDING\"):\n1979:         result[\"warnings\"].extend(_check_pre_closure_invariants(plan_id))\n1980: \n1981:     # Post-closure invariants\n1982:     if log_status in (\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"COMPLETED\"):\n1983:         result.update(_check_post_closure_invariants(plan_id, log_status))\n1984: \n1985:     return result\n1986: \n1987: \n1988: def _check_scope_for_validate(\n1989:     plan_content: str, log_status: str\n1990: ) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:\n1991:     \"\"\"Check scope violations for validate command. Returns (errors, warnings).\"\"\"\n1992:     errors, warnings = [], []\n1993:     if \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1994:         changed_files = get_changed_files()\n1995:         gate_result = check_scope_gate(plan_content, changed_files, _exclude_files())\n1996:         if not gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n1997:             # Scope violations in READY_FOR_REVIEW are warnings only, not errors\n1998:             warnings.extend(\n1999:                 [f\"Out of scope: {f}\" for f in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"])]\n2000:             )\n2001:         warnings.extend([f\"Warning: {w}\" for w in gate_result[\"warnings\"]])\n2002:     return errors, warnings\n2003: \n2004: \n2005: def _emit_manager_approve_cascade(event_bus, ticket_id: str) -> None:\n2006:     \"\"\"Emit the canonical closeout cascade for manager approve.\"\"\"\n2007:     # 1. REVIEW_DECISION with approve\n2008:     event_bus.emit(\n2009:         event_type=\"REVIEW_DECISION\",\n2010:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2011:         actor=\"MANAGER\",\n2012:         payload={\n2013:             \"decision\": \"approve\",\n2014:             \"note\": \"Canonical closeout approved\",\n2015:             \"source\": \"manager-approve\",\n2016:         },\n2017:     )\n2018: \n2019:     # 2. STATE_CHANGED -> READY_TO_CLOSE\n2020:     event_bus.emit(\n2021:         event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n2022:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2023:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2024:         payload={\n2025:             \"from_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n2026:             \"to_state\": \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n2027:             \"reason\": \"Manager approved canonical closeout\",\n2028:             \"source\": \"manager-approve\",\n2029:         },\n2030:     )\n2031: \n2032:     # 3. CLOSE_CONFIRMED\n2033:     event_bus.emit(\n2034:         event_type=\"CLOSE_CONFIRMED\",\n2035:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2036:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2037:         payload={\n2038:             \"source\": \"manager_closeout\",\n2039:             \"reason\": \"Canonical closeout approved by Manager\",\n2040:         },\n2041:     )\n2042: \n2043:     # 4. STATE_CHANGED -> COMPLETED\n2044:     event_bus.emit(\n2045:         event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n2046:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2047:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2048:         payload={\n2049:             \"from_state\": \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n2050:             \"to_state\": \"COMPLETED\",\n2051:             \"reason\": \"Canonical closeout completed\",\n2052:             \"source\": \"manager-approve\",\n2053:         },\n2054:     )\n2055: \n2056:     # 5. SUPERVISOR_CLOSED\n2057:     event_bus.emit(\n2058:         event_type=\"SUPERVISOR_CLOSED\",\n2059:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2060:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2061:         payload={\n2062:             \"source\": \"manager-approve\",\n2063:             \"reason\": \"Canonical closeout completed\",\n2064:         },\n2065:     )\n2066: \n2067: \n2068: def _sync_markdowns_to_completed(ticket_id: str) -> None:\n2069:     \"\"\"Sync markdown files to COMPLETED state.\"\"\"\n2070:     update_log_status(\n2071:         \"COMPLETED\", f\"Manager approved canonical closeout for {ticket_id}\"\n2072:     )\n2073: \n2074:     # Normalize the execution log projections as well.\n2075:     exec_log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n2076:     if exec_log_content:\n2077:         updated_exec_log = exec_log_content\n2078:         updated_exec_log = updated_exec_log.replace(\n2079:             f\"- Current state: {ticket_id} IN_PROGRESS\",\n2080:             f\"- Current state: {ticket_id} COMPLETED\",\n2081:             1,\n2082:         )\n2083:         updated_exec_log = updated_exec_log.replace(\n2084:             \"**Fase:** IMPLEMENTATION\", \"**Fase:** CLOSEOUT\", 1\n2085:         )\n2086:         updated_exec_log = updated_exec_log.replace(\n2087:             \"**Estado:** IN_PROGRESS\", \"**Estado:** COMPLETED\", 1\n2088:         )\n2089:         write_file(EXEC_LOG, updated_exec_log)\n2090: \n2091:     # Update work_plan.md to COMPLETED so the canonical ticket snapshot closes too.\n2092:     work_plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n2093:     if work_plan_content:\n2094:         updated_work_plan = work_plan_content\n2095:         updated_work_plan = updated_work_plan.replace(\n2096:             \"- **Estado:** APPROVED\", \"- **Estado:** COMPLETED\", 1\n2097:         )\n2098:         updated_work_plan = updated_work_plan.replace(\n2099:             \"- **Estado del ticket:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n2100:             \"- **Estado del ticket:** COMPLETED\",\n2101:             1,\n2102:         )\n2103:         updated_work_plan = updated_work_plan.replace(\n2104:             \"- **Accion:** REVIEW_WORK\", \"- **Accion:** CLOSEOUT\", 1\n2105:         )\n2106:         write_file(WORK_PLAN, updated_work_plan)\n2107: \n2108:     # Update TURN.md for next cycle\n2109:     action = {\n2110:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n2111:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n2112:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n2113:         \"instruction\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} closed. Create new work_plan.md for next cycle.\",\n2114:         \"plan_id\": \"N/A\",\n2115:         \"plan_status\": \"COMPLETED\",\n2116:         \"log_status\": \"COMPLETED\",\n2117:         \"action_type\": \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n2118:     }\n2119:     update_turn_file(action)\n2120: \n2121:     # Update STATE.md\n2122:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n2123:     if state_content:\n2124:         updated_state = state_content\n2125:         updated_state = updated_state.replace(\n2126:             \"- **Quality Gates:** PENDING (WP-2026-069 no ejecutado)\",\n2127:             \"- **Quality Gates:** GREEN (WP-2026-069 completed)\",\n2128:             1,\n2129:         )\n2130:         updated_state = updated_state.replace(\n2131:             \"- **Ultimo task en execution_log.md:** WP-2026-069 IN_PROGRESS\",\n2132:             \"- **Ultimo task en execution_log.md:** WP-2026-069 COMPLETED\",\n2133:             1,\n2134:         )\n2135:         updated_state = updated_state.replace(\n2136:             \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n2137:             \"- **Estado actual:** COMPLETED\",\n2138:             1,\n2139:         )\n2140:         write_file(STATE_FILE, updated_state)\n2141: \n2142: \n2143: def _handle_manager_approve(  # noqa: C901 - flag handler intentionally branches across validation and closeout\n2144:     ticket_id: str, json_output: bool, force_mode: bool\n2145: ) -> int:\n2146:     \"\"\"\n2147:     Handle --manager-approve flag.\n2148:     Emits canonical closeout cascade:\n2149:     REVIEW_DECISION (approve) -> STATE_CHANGED READY_TO_CLOSE -> CLOSE_CONFIRMED ->\n2150:     STATE_CHANGED COMPLETED -> SUPERVISOR_CLOSED\n2151: \n2152:     Validation:\n2153:     - ticket_id must be provided\n2154:     - ticket_id must match the active ticket in work_plan.md\n2155:     - Ticket must be in READY_FOR_REVIEW state\n2156:     - Idempotent: returns already_completed if ticket already has SUPERVISOR_CLOSED event in bus\n2157:     \"\"\"\n2158:     if not ticket_id or ticket_id == \"N/A\":\n2159:         if json_output:\n2160:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": \"No ticket_id provided\"}, indent=2))\n2161:         else:\n2162:             print(\"[ERROR] No ticket_id provided. Use --ticket WP-XXXX\")\n2163:         return 1\n2164: \n2165:     # Load current state\n2166:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n2167:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n2168:     current_plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n2169:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n2170: \n2171:     # Validate ticket_id matches active ticket\n2172:     if not current_plan_id or current_plan_id == \"N/A\":\n2173:         if json_output:\n2174:             print(\n2175:                 json.dumps(\n2176:                     {\"error\": \"No active ticket found in work_plan.md\"}, indent=2\n2177:                 )\n2178:             )\n2179:         else:\n2180:             print(\"[ERROR] No active ticket found in work_plan.md\")\n2181:         return 1\n2182: \n2183:     if ticket_id != current_plan_id:\n2184:         if json_output:\n2185:             print(\n2186:                 json.dumps(\n2187:                     {\n2188:                         \"error\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\",\n2189:                         \"provided_ticket\": ticket_id,\n2190:                         \"active_ticket\": current_plan_id,\n2191:                     },\n2192:                     indent=2,\n2193:                 )\n2194:             )\n2195:         else:\n2196:             print(\n2197:                 f\"[ERROR] Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\"\n2198:             )\n2199:         return 1\n2200: \n2201:     # Check idempotency per-ticket using bus events (not just global markdown state)\n2202:     # If there's already a SUPERVISOR_CLOSED event for this ticket, it's already completed\n2203:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n2204:         supervisor_closed_events = event_bus.read_events(\n2205:             ticket_id=ticket_id, event_type=\"SUPERVISOR_CLOSED\"\n2206:         )\n2207:         if supervisor_closed_events:\n2208:             # Ticket already closed in bus - idempotent return\n2209:             if json_output:\n2210:                 print(\n2211:                     json.dumps(\n2212:                         {\"status\": \"already_completed\", \"ticket_id\": ticket_id},\n2213:                         indent=2,\n2214:                     )\n2215:                 )\n2216:             else:\n2217:                 print(\n2218:                     f\"[INFO] Ticket {ticket_id} is already COMPLETED (SUPERVISOR_CLOSED event exists).\"\n2219:                 )\n2220:             return 0\n2221: \n2222:     # Fallback: also check markdown state for tickets without bus events\n2223:     if \"COMPLETED\" in log_status:\n2224:         if json_output:\n2225:             print(\n2226:                 json.dumps(\n2227:                     {\"status\": \"already_completed\", \"ticket_id\": ticket_id}, indent=2\n2228:                 )\n2229:             )\n2230:         else:\n2231:             print(f\"[INFO] Ticket {ticket_id} is already COMPLETED.\")\n2232:         return 0\n2233: \n2234:     # Check if ticket is in READY_FOR_REVIEW\n2235:     if \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" not in log_status:\n2236:         if json_output:\n2237:             print(\n2238:                 json.dumps(\n2239:                     {\n2240:                         \"error\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} is not in READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n2241:                         \"current_state\": log_status,\n2242:                     },\n2243:                     indent=2,\n2244:                 )\n2245:             )\n2246:         else:\n2247:             print(\n2248:                 f\"[ERROR] Ticket {ticket_id} is not in READY_FOR_REVIEW (current state: {log_status})\"\n2249:             )\n2250:         return 1\n2251: \n2252:     # Emit canonical closeout cascade\n2253:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n2254:         _emit_manager_approve_cascade(event_bus, ticket_id)\n2255: \n2256:     # Sync markdowns to COMPLETED\n2257:     _sync_markdowns_to_completed(ticket_id)\n2258: \n2259:     # Reset circuit breaker on successful completion\n2260:     _reset_circuit_breaker(ticket_id)\n2261: \n2262:     # Release builder lock\n2263:     _release_builder_lock(ticket_id)\n2264: \n2265:     if json_output:\n2266:         print(json.dumps({\"status\": \"closed\", \"ticket_id\": ticket_id}, indent=2))\n2267:     else:\n2268:         print(f\"[OK] Ticket {ticket_id} closed canonically.\")\n2269: \n2270:     return 0\n2271: \n2272: \n2273: def _materialize_state_transition(\n2274:     ticket_id: str,\n2275:     to_state: str,\n2276:     reason: str,\n2277:     actor: str = \"SUPERVISOR\",\n2278:     source: str = \"canonical\",\n2279: ) -> None:\n2280:     \"\"\"Materialize a state transition canonically.\n2281: \n2282:     This is the shared route for all state transitions (approve, changes, inspect).\n2283:     It ensures that STATE.md, TURN.md, execution_log.md and the bus are synchronized.\n2284: \n2285:     Before: Requires ticket_id, to_state, reason, and optional actor/source.\n2286:     During: Emits STATE_CHANGED to bus, updates execution_log.md, TURN.md, STATE.md.\n2287:     After: All projections reflect the new state derived from the bus event.\n2288:     \"\"\"\n2289:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n2290:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n2291:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n2292: \n2293:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus first (bus is the authority)\n2294:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n2295:         event_bus.emit(\n2296:             event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n2297:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2298:             actor=actor,\n2299:             payload={\n2300:                 \"from_state\": log_status,\n2301:                 \"to_state\": to_state,\n2302:                 \"reason\": reason,\n2303:                 \"source\": source,\n2304:             },\n2305:         )\n2306: \n2307:     # Sync execution_log.md\n2308:     update_log_status(to_state, reason)\n2309: \n2310:     # Sync TURN.md based on target state\n2311:     if to_state == \"HUMAN_GATE\":\n2312:         action = {\n2313:             \"role\": \"SUPERVISOR\",\n2314:             \"context_file\": \".supervisor_rules\",\n2315:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/supervisor_workflow.md\",\n2316:             \"instruction\": f\"Escalated to HUMAN_GATE: {reason}\",\n2317:             \"plan_id\": ticket_id,\n2318:             \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n2319:             \"log_status\": to_state,\n2320:             \"action_type\": \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n2321:             \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n2322:         }\n2323:     elif to_state == \"IN_PROGRESS\":\n2324:         action = {\n2325:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n2326:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n2327:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n2328:             \"instruction\": f\"Requeued to Builder: {reason}\",\n2329:             \"plan_id\": ticket_id,\n2330:             \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n2331:             \"log_status\": to_state,\n2332:             \"action_type\": \"IMPLEMENT\",\n2333:             \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n2334:         }\n2335:     elif to_state == \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\":\n2336:         action = {\n2337:             \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n2338:             \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n2339:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n2340:             \"instruction\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} listo para review: {reason}\",\n2341:             \"plan_id\": ticket_id,\n2342:             \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n2343:             \"log_status\": to_state,\n2344:             \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n2345:             \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n2346:         }\n2347:     elif to_state == \"READY_TO_CLOSE\":\n2348:         action = {\n2349:             \"role\": \"SUPERVISOR\",\n2350:             \"context_file\": \".supervisor_rules\",\n2351:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/supervisor_workflow.md\",\n2352:             \"instruction\": f\"Ticket approved, pending close: {reason}\",\n2353:             \"plan_id\": ticket_id,\n2354:             \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n2355:             \"log_status\": to_state,\n2356:             \"action_type\": \"CLOSEOUT\",\n2357:             \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n2358:         }\n2359:     elif to_state == \"COMPLETED\":\n2360:         action = {\n2361:             \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n2362:             \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n2363:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n2364:             \"instruction\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} closed. Create new work_plan.md for next cycle.\",\n2365:             \"plan_id\": \"N/A\",\n2366:             \"plan_status\": \"COMPLETED\",\n2367:             \"log_status\": \"COMPLETED\",\n2368:             \"action_type\": \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n2369:         }\n2370:     else:\n2371:         # Fallback for unknown states\n2372:         action = {\n2373:             \"role\": \"UNKNOWN\",\n2374:             \"context_file\": \"N/A\",\n2375:             \"workflow_file\": \"N/A\",\n2376:             \"instruction\": f\"State transition to {to_state}: {reason}\",\n2377:             \"plan_id\": ticket_id,\n2378:             \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n2379:             \"log_status\": to_state,\n2380:             \"action_type\": \"STATE_TRANSITION\",\n2381:             \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n2382:         }\n2383: \n2384:     update_turn_file(action)\n2385: \n2386:     # Sync STATE.md\n2387:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n2388:     if state_content:\n2389:         updated_state = state_content\n2390:         # Replace the current state line\n2391:         import re\n2392: \n2393:         updated_state = re.sub(\n2394:             r\"^- \\*\\*Estado actual:\\*\\* .+\",\n2395:             f\"- **Estado actual:** {to_state}\",\n2396:             updated_state,\n2397:             flags=re.MULTILINE,\n2398:         )\n2399:         write_file(STATE_FILE, updated_state)\n2400: \n2401: \n2402: def _handle_escalate_human_gate(ticket_id: str, json_output: bool) -> int:\n2403:     \"\"\"Handle --escalate-human-gate flag for inspect decisions.\n2404: \n2405:     This emits STATE_CHANGED -> HUMAN_GATE (actor SUPERVISOR) and synchronizes\n2406:     all projections (STATE.md, TURN.md, execution_log.md) via the canonical\n2407:     materialization route. Does NOT touch the rejection counter.\n2408: \n2409:     Before: Requires ticket_id string.\n2410:     During: Calls _materialize_state_transition with to_state=HUMAN_GATE.\n2411:     After: All projections reflect HUMAN_GATE state, bus has STATE_CHANGED event.\n2412:     \"\"\"\n2413:     if not ticket_id or ticket_id == \"N/A\":\n2414:         if json_output:\n2415:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": \"No ticket_id provided\"}, indent=2))\n2416:         else:\n2417:             print(\"[ERROR] No ticket_id provided. Use --ticket WP-XXXX\")\n2418:         return 1\n2419: \n2420:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n2421:     current_plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n2422: \n2423:     if not current_plan_id or current_plan_id == \"N/A\":\n2424:         if json_output:\n2425:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": \"No active ticket found\"}, indent=2))\n2426:         else:\n2427:             print(\"[ERROR] No active ticket found in work_plan.md\")\n2428:         return 1\n2429: \n2430:     if ticket_id != current_plan_id:\n2431:         if json_output:\n2432:             print(\n2433:                 json.dumps(\n2434:                     {\n2435:                         \"error\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\"\n2436:                     },\n2437:                     indent=2,\n2438:                 )\n2439:             )\n2440:         else:\n2441:             print(\n2442:                 f\"[ERROR] Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\"\n2443:             )\n2444:         return 1\n2445: \n2446:     # Materialize the transition canonically\n2447:     _materialize_state_transition(\n2448:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2449:         to_state=\"HUMAN_GATE\",\n2450:         reason=\"Manager inspect: human review required\",\n2451:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2452:         source=\"escalate-human-gate\",\n2453:     )\n2454: \n2455:     if json_output:\n2456:         print(\n2457:             json.dumps(\n2458:                 {\"status\": \"escalated_to_human_gate\", \"ticket_id\": ticket_id}, indent=2\n2459:             )\n2460:         )\n2461:     else:\n2462:         print(f\"[OK] Ticket {ticket_id} escalated to HUMAN_GATE (inspect).\")\n2463: \n2464:     return 0\n2465: \n2466: \n2467: def _handle_resume_human_gate(ticket_id: str, json_output: bool) -> int:\n2468:     \"\"\"Handle --resume-human-gate flag.\n2469: \n2470:     Resolucion humana de un ticket en HUMAN_GATE: emite STATE_CHANGED ->\n2471:     READY_FOR_REVIEW (actor SUPERVISOR) y sincroniza las proyecciones via la\n2472:     ruta canonica de materializacion. Es la salida que faltaba al contrato de\n2473:     --escalate-human-gate. Solo valida si el estado derivado del bus es\n2474:     HUMAN_GATE.\n2475: \n2476:     Before: Requires ticket_id; bus-derived state must be HUMAN_GATE.\n2477:     During: Verifies bus state, calls _materialize_state_transition.\n2478:     After: Ticket returns to READY_FOR_REVIEW for a fresh review cycle.\n2479:     \"\"\"\n2480:     if not ticket_id or ticket_id == \"N/A\":\n2481:         if json_output:\n2482:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": \"No ticket_id provided\"}, indent=2))\n2483:         else:\n2484:             print(\"[ERROR] No ticket_id provided. Use --ticket WP-XXXX\")\n2485:         return 1\n2486: \n2487:     current_plan_id = get_plan_id(read_file(WORK_PLAN))\n2488:     if ticket_id != current_plan_id:\n2489:         msg = f\"Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\"\n2490:         if json_output:\n2491:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": msg}, indent=2))\n2492:         else:\n2493:             print(f\"[ERROR] {msg}\")\n2494:         return 1\n2495: \n2496:     # El bus es la autoridad: solo se resume desde HUMAN_GATE.\n2497:     from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n2498: \n2499:     bus_state = None\n2500:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n2501:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=ticket_id)\n2502:         if events:\n2503:             bus_state = StateMachine.derive_state_from_events(\n2504:                 [e.to_dict() for e in events]\n2505:             )\n2506:     if bus_state != TicketState.HUMAN_GATE:\n2507:         msg = (\n2508:             f\"Ticket {ticket_id} bus state is {bus_state}, not HUMAN_GATE. \"\n2509:             \"--resume-human-gate solo aplica a tickets en HUMAN_GATE.\"\n2510:         )\n2511:         if json_output:\n2512:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": msg}, indent=2))\n2513:         else:\n2514:             print(f\"[ERROR] {msg}\")\n2515:         return 1\n2516: \n2517:     _materialize_state_transition(\n2518:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2519:         to_state=\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n2520:         reason=\"Human resolution: resumed from HUMAN_GATE for a fresh review\",\n2521:         actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2522:         source=\"resume-human-gate\",\n2523:     )\n2524: \n2525:     if json_output:\n2526:         print(\n2527:             json.dumps(\n2528:                 {\"status\": \"resumed_to_review\", \"ticket_id\": ticket_id}, indent=2\n2529:             )\n2530:         )\n2531:     else:\n2532:         print(f\"[OK] Ticket {ticket_id} resumed from HUMAN_GATE to READY_FOR_REVIEW.\")\n2533: \n2534:     return 0\n2535: \n2536: \n2537: def _assert_bus_projection_consistency(ticket_id: str) -> list[str]:\n2538:     \"\"\"Assert that bus-derived state matches projection state.\n2539: \n2540:     WP-2026-124: Post-cycle verification that bus and projections are aligned.\n2541:     Does NOT auto-heal - returns warnings for manual intervention.\n2542: \n2543:     Before: Requires ticket_id string.\n2544:     During: Compares bus-derived state with STATE.md and execution_log.md states.\n2545:     After: Returns list of drift warnings (empty if consistent).\n2546:     \"\"\"\n2547:     warnings = []\n2548:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE or not event_bus:\n2549:         warnings.append(\"Bus not available for consistency check\")\n2550:         return warnings\n2551: \n2552:     # Get bus-derived state\n2553:     events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=ticket_id)\n2554:     if not events:\n2555:         warnings.append(f\"No events in bus for ticket {ticket_id}\")\n2556:         return warnings\n2557: \n2558:     from bus.state_machine import StateMachine\n2559: \n2560:     bus_state = StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n2561: \n2562:     # Get projection states\n2563:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n2564:     exec_log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n2565: \n2566:     state_md_state = (\n2567:         get_status(state_content, \"**Estado actual:**\") if state_content else \"UNKNOWN\"\n2568:     )\n2569:     exec_log_state = (\n2570:         get_status(exec_log_content, \"**Estado:**\") if exec_log_content else \"UNKNOWN\"\n2571:     )\n2572: \n2573:     # Compare\n2574:     if bus_state.value != state_md_state:\n2575:         warnings.append(\n2576:             f\"DRIFT: bus_state={bus_state.value} != STATE.md={state_md_state}\"\n2577:         )\n2578:     if bus_state.value != exec_log_state:\n2579:         warnings.append(\n2580:             f\"DRIFT: bus_state={bus_state.value} != execution_log.md={exec_log_state}\"\n2581:         )\n2582: \n2583:     return warnings\n2584: \n2585: \n2586: def _handle_request_changes(  # noqa: C901\n2587:     ticket_id: str, json_output: bool, force_mode: bool\n2588: ) -> int:\n2589:     \"\"\"\n2590:     Handle --request-changes flag.\n2591:     Transitions ticket to IN_PROGRESS (requeue builder) if N < threshold, or\n2592:     HUMAN_GATE if N >= threshold. The threshold is the single source of truth\n2593:     in agents.json (manager_review.max_attempts); see get_human_gate_threshold().\n2594: \n2595:     WP-2026-124: Now uses bus-derived state via _materialize_state_transition.\n2596:     \"\"\"\n2597:     if not ticket_id or ticket_id == \"N/A\":\n2598:         if json_output:\n2599:             print(json.dumps({\"error\": \"No ticket_id provided\"}, indent=2))\n2600:         else:\n2601:             print(\"[ERROR] No ticket_id provided. Use --ticket WP-XXXX\")\n2602:         return 1\n2603: \n2604:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n2605:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n2606:     current_plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n2607:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n2608: \n2609:     if not current_plan_id or current_plan_id == \"N/A\":\n2610:         if json_output:\n2611:             print(\n2612:                 json.dumps(\n2613:                     {\"error\": \"No active ticket found in work_plan.md\"}, indent=2\n2614:                 )\n2615:             )\n2616:         else:\n2617:             print(\"[ERROR] No active ticket found in work_plan.md\")\n2618:         return 1\n2619: \n2620:     if ticket_id != current_plan_id:\n2621:         if json_output:\n2622:             print(\n2623:                 json.dumps(\n2624:                     {\n2625:                         \"error\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\"\n2626:                     },\n2627:                     indent=2,\n2628:                 )\n2629:             )\n2630:         else:\n2631:             print(\n2632:                 f\"[ERROR] Ticket {ticket_id} does not match active ticket {current_plan_id}\"\n2633:             )\n2634:         return 1\n2635: \n2636:     # WP-2026-124: Check bus-derived state first\n2637:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n2638:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n2639: \n2640:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=ticket_id)\n2641:         bus_state = (\n2642:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n2643:             if events\n2644:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n2645:         )\n2646: \n2647:         # Fallback to execution_log.md if bus state is UNKNOWN\n2648:         if bus_state == TicketState.UNKNOWN:\n2649:             if \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" not in log_status:\n2650:                 if json_output:\n2651:                     print(\n2652:                         json.dumps(\n2653:                             {\"error\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} is not in READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n2654:                             indent=2,\n2655:                         )\n2656:                     )\n2657:                 else:\n2658:                     print(\n2659:                         f\"[ERROR] Ticket {ticket_id} is not in READY_FOR_REVIEW (current state: {log_status})\"\n2660:                     )\n2661:                 return 1\n2662:         elif bus_state != TicketState.READY_FOR_REVIEW:\n2663:             if json_output:\n2664:                 print(\n2665:                     json.dumps(\n2666:                         {\n2667:                             \"error\": f\"Ticket {ticket_id} bus state is {bus_state.value}, not READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n2668:                         },\n2669:                         indent=2,\n2670:                     )\n2671:                 )\n2672:             else:\n2673:                 print(\n2674:                     f\"[ERROR] Ticket {ticket_id} bus state is {bus_state.value}, not READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n2675:                 )\n2676:             return 1\n2677: \n2678:     rejection_count = 0\n2679:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n2680:         # Single source of truth for the consecutive count: bus.utils.\n2681:         # A history of changes->approve->changes counts as 1, not 2.\n2682:         rejection_count = count_trailing_changes(\n2683:             event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=ticket_id, event_type=\"REVIEW_DECISION\")\n2684:         )\n2685: \n2686:         # If the latest event is not already a REVIEW_DECISION->changes (e.g.\n2687:         # --request-changes invoked manually without the bridge), emit one so\n2688:         # the bus stays the single source of truth. The bridge path already\n2689:         # emits it, so this is a no-op there (no duplicate).\n2690:         latest = event_bus.latest_event(ticket_id=ticket_id)\n2691:         if (\n2692:             not latest\n2693:             or latest.event_type != \"REVIEW_DECISION\"\n2694:             or str((latest.payload or {}).get(\"decision\", \"\")).lower() != \"changes\"\n2695:         ):\n2696:             event_bus.emit(\n2697:                 event_type=\"REVIEW_DECISION\",\n2698:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2699:                 actor=\"MANAGER\",\n2700:                 payload={\n2701:                     \"decision\": \"changes\",\n2702:                     \"source\": \"agent_controller\",\n2703:                 },\n2704:             )\n2705:             # Recompute from the bus now that this changes is recorded.\n2706:             rejection_count = count_trailing_changes(\n2707:                 event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=ticket_id, event_type=\"REVIEW_DECISION\")\n2708:             )\n2709: \n2710:         threshold = get_human_gate_threshold()\n2711:         to_state = \"HUMAN_GATE\" if rejection_count >= threshold else \"IN_PROGRESS\"\n2712:         reason = (\n2713:             f\"Manager requested changes ({rejection_count} rejections)\"\n2714:             if rejection_count < threshold\n2715:             else f\"Escalated to HUMAN_GATE after {rejection_count} rejections\"\n2716:         )\n2717: \n2718:         # WP-2026-124: Use canonical materialization route\n2719:         _materialize_state_transition(\n2720:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n2721:             to_state=to_state,\n2722:             reason=reason,\n2723:             actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n2724:             source=\"request-changes\",\n2725:         )\n2726:     else:\n2727:         to_state = \"IN_PROGRESS\"\n2728:         # Fallback: legacy route without bus\n2729:         update_log_status(\n2730:             to_state,\n2731:             f\"Manager requested changes ({rejection_count} rejections). Requeuing Builder.\",\n2732:         )\n2733:         action = {\n2734:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n2735:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n2736:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n2737:             \"instruction\": f\"Manager requested changes on {ticket_id}. Re-implement fixes.\",\n2738:             \"plan_id\": ticket_id,\n2739:             \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n2740:             \"log_status\": to_state,\n2741:             \"action_type\": \"IMPLEMENT\",\n2742:             \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n2743:         }\n2744:         update_turn_file(action)\n2745:         state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n2746:         if state_content:\n2747:             updated_state = state_content.replace(\n2748:                 \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n2749:                 \"- **Estado actual:** IN_PROGRESS\",\n2750:                 1,\n2751:             )\n2752:             write_file(STATE_FILE, updated_state)\n2753: \n2754:     if to_state == \"HUMAN_GATE\":\n2755:         if json_output:\n2756:             print(\n2757:                 json.dumps(\n2758:                     {\n2759:                         \"status\": \"escalated_to_human_gate\",\n2760:                         \"ticket_id\": ticket_id,\n2761:                         \"rejections\": rejection_count,\n2762:                     },\n2763:                     indent=2,\n2764:                 )\n2765:             )\n2766:         else:\n2767:             print(\n2768:                 f\"[OK] Ticket {ticket_id} escalated to HUMAN_GATE after {rejection_count} rejections.\"\n2769:             )\n2770:     else:\n2771:         if json_output:\n2772:             print(\n2773:                 json.dumps(\n2774:                     {\n2775:                         \"status\": \"changes_requested\",\n2776:                         \"ticket_id\": ticket_id,\n2777:                         \"rejections\": rejection_count,\n2778:                     },\n2779:                     indent=2,\n2780:                 )\n2781:             )\n2782:         else:\n2783:             print(\n2784:                 f\"[OK] Ticket {ticket_id} transitioned to IN_PROGRESS ({rejection_count} rejections).\"\n2785:             )\n2786: \n2787:     return 0\n2788: \n2789: \n2790: def _collect_deliverable_type_warnings(plan_content: str) -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n2791:     \"\"\"Helper for _handle_validate: returns {file: [warnings]} or {} if clean.\"\"\"\n2792:     if not plan_content:\n2793:         return {}\n2794:     deliverable_warnings = _check_deliverable_type(plan_content)\n2795:     if not deliverable_warnings:\n2796:         return {}\n2797:     return {\"work_plan.md\": deliverable_warnings}\n2798: \n2799: \n2800: def _handle_validate(json_output: bool) -> int:  # noqa: C901\n2801:     \"\"\"Handle --validate flag.\n2802: \n2803:     Aggregates many independent validators (state files, scope, bus drift,\n2804:     deliverable_type). Each branch is simple; the function is intentionally\n2805:     a thin coordinator. Splitting it further would harm readability.\n2806:     \"\"\"\n2807:     errors = validate_state_files()\n2808:     warnings = {}\n2809: \n2810:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n2811:     for file_key, warns in _collect_deliverable_type_warnings(plan_content).items():\n2812:         warnings.setdefault(file_key, []).extend(warns)\n2813: \n2814:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n2815:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n2816: \n2817:     # Check scope violations\n2818:     scope_errors, scope_warnings = _check_scope_for_validate(plan_content, log_status)\n2819:     if scope_errors:\n2820:         errors.setdefault(\"scope\", []).extend(scope_errors)\n2821:     if scope_warnings:\n2822:         warnings.setdefault(\"scope\", []).extend(scope_warnings)\n2823: \n2824:     # Check bus drift\n2825:     drift_warnings = _check_bus_drift(plan_content, log_status)\n2826:     if drift_warnings:\n2827:         warnings.setdefault(\"bus_drift\", []).extend(drift_warnings)\n2828: \n2829:     # Check invariants (pre and post-closure)\n2830:     invariant_result = _check_invariants(plan_content, log_content, log_status)\n2831:     if invariant_result[\"errors\"]:\n2832:         errors.setdefault(\"invariants\", []).extend(invariant_result[\"errors\"])\n2833:     if invariant_result[\"warnings\"]:\n2834:         warnings.setdefault(\"invariants\", []).extend(invariant_result[\"warnings\"])\n2835: \n2836:     total_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in errors.values())\n2837:     total_warnings = sum(len(warns) for warns in warnings.values())\n2838: \n2839:     if json_output:\n2840:         output = {\"errors\": errors, \"warnings\": warnings}\n2841:         print(json.dumps(output, indent=2))\n2842:     else:\n2843:         if total_errors == 0 and total_warnings == 0:\n2844:             print(\"[OK] Todos los archivos de estado son validos.\")\n2845:         else:\n2846:             if total_errors > 0:\n2847:                 print(f\"[ERROR] {total_errors} problema(s) encontrados.\")\n2848:             if total_warnings > 0:\n2849:                 print(f\"[WARN] {total_warnings} advertencia(s) encontradas.\")\n2850:     # Only fail on actual errors, not on warnings\n2851:     return 0 if total_errors == 0 else 1\n2852: \n2853: \n2854: def _handle_archive() -> int:\n2855:     \"\"\"Handle --archive flag.\"\"\"\n2856:     archive_path = archive_old_notifications()\n2857:     if archive_path:\n2858:         print(f\"[OK] Archivado: {archive_path}\")\n2859:     else:\n2860:         print(\"[INFO] No es necesario archivar.\")\n2861:     return 0\n2862: \n2863: \n2864: def _handle_main_action(\n2865:     skip_gates: bool, strict_mode: bool, json_output: bool, reset_turn_mode: bool\n2866: ) -> int:\n2867:     \"\"\"Handle main action determination and output.\"\"\"\n2868:     if SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE:\n2869:         show_recovery_hint()\n2870: \n2871:     print(\"\\n  Generando mapa del proyecto...\")\n2872:     generate_project_map()\n2873:     print(\"  [OK] Mapa actualizado\")\n2874: \n2875:     notif_errors = validate_state_files().get(\"notifications.md\", [])\n2876:     if notif_errors:\n2877:         fix_corrupted_notifications()\n2878: \n2879:     archive_old_notifications()\n2880: \n2881:     action = determine_next_action(skip_gates=skip_gates, strict_mode=strict_mode)\n2882: \n2883:     if should_overwrite_turn(TURN_FILE, force_reset=reset_turn_mode):\n2884:         update_turn_file(action)\n2885: \n2886:     if json_output:\n2887:         print(json.dumps(action, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))\n2888:     else:\n2889:         print_human_readable(action)\n2890: \n2891:     if SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE:\n2892:         save_session()\n2893: \n2894:     return 0\n2895: \n2896: \n2897: # Flag dispatch table\n2898: FLAG_HANDLERS = {\n2899:     \"--recover\": _handle_recover,\n2900:     \"--check-completion\": _handle_check_completion,\n2901:     \"--validate\": _handle_validate,\n2902:     \"--archive\": _handle_archive,\n2903:     \"--manager-approve\": _handle_manager_approve,\n2904:     \"--request-changes\": _handle_request_changes,\n2905: }\n2906: \n2907: \n2908: def main():  # noqa: C901 - CLI dispatch intentionally centralizes flag handling\n2909:     \"\"\"Funcion principal del controller.\"\"\"\n2910:     # WP-2026-122: Parse --project-root FIRST and export to environment\n2911:     # This must happen before any imports that depend on project_root\n2912:     if \"--project-root\" in sys.argv:\n2913:         idx = sys.argv.index(\"--project-root\")\n2914:         if idx + 1 < len(sys.argv) and not sys.argv[idx + 1].startswith(\"--\"):\n2915:             project_root_value = sys.argv[idx + 1]\n2916:             os.environ[\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\"] = str(Path(project_root_value).resolve())\n2917: \n2918:     _ensure_runtime_dirs()\n2919:     _get_event_bus()\n2920: \n2921:     skip_gates = \"--skip-gates\" in sys.argv\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. {"type":"text","timestamp":1779794954802,"sessionID":"ses_19bf55e10ffe8UsNjBT7LAJ2je","part":{"id":"prt_e640ba875001kBe3wLVtByhpGa","messageID":"msg_e640b28e2001WfvWcZT7je1syh","sessionID":"ses_19bf55e10ffe8UsNjBT7LAJ2je","type":"text","text":"## Review complete\n\nHe revisado la implementación del WP-2026-143 \"Bus-backed mark-ready idempotency\".\n\n### Verificación de Criterios de Aceptación\n\n1. **`--mark-ready` no emite eventos duplicados** ✓ - Implementado en líneas 1592-1614 de `agent_controller.py`. Cuando el bus state es `READY_FOR_REVIEW`, `READY_TO_CLOSE` o `COMPLETED`, la función retorna 0 sin llamar a `event_bus.emit()`.\n\n2. **El guard lee el estado del bus como autoridad** ✓ - Líneas 1562-1569: consulta `StateMachine.derive_state_from_events()` para obtener el estado derivado del bus, no de markdown.\n\n3. **Fallback conservado cuando bus no disponible** ✓ - Líneas 1616-1660: lógica markdown-based cuando `bus_state is None`.\n\n4. **Validación canónica pasa sin errores** ✓ - execution_log.md reporta:\n   - `test_mark_ready_idempotency.py`: 8 passed\n   - `test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py`: 5 passed\n   - ruff: limpio\n   - `--validate`: sin errores\n\n### Verificación de Anti-patrones\n\n- **AP-01 Mock drift**: No detectado. Los tests parchean `agent_controller.event_bus` correctamente.\n- **AP-02 Floor assertion**: No aplica (no hay aserciones numéricas con umbrales triviales).\n- **AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper**: No detectado. `_handle_mark_ready` tiene lógica propia sustancial.\n- **AP-06 Validator evidence missing**: No detectado. execution_log.md contiene comandos exactos + resultados numéricos.\n\n### Coverage de Tests\n\nLos 8 tests en `test_mark_ready_idempotency.py` cubren:\n- no-op para READY_FOR_REVIEW, READY_TO_CLOSE, COMPLETED\n- bloqueado para HUMAN_GATE\n- fallback markdown cuando bus unavailable\n- emite eventos cuando IN_PROGRESS\n- JSON output incluye bus_state\n- procede cuando no hay eventos\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779794946165,"end":1779794954798}}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779809099286,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_89c79d636bc74ab6ae9d6d37","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-144_attempt-2.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-144_attempt-2.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: Review mixed ticket WP-2026-144. Verify code correctness, tests, and style guides, and additionally verify that all declared non-code deliverables exist, are well-structured, and are fully complete. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls.\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature.\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance, verify that an explicit reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify all callers use `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`). Any caller still using generic truthiness after the change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. (unknown)\n34: \n35: --- work_plan.md ---\n36: # Work Plan - WP-2026-144\n37: \n38: ## Metadata\n39: - **ID:** WP-2026-144\n40: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n41: - **deliverable_type:** mixed\n42: - **Titulo:** Destination ticket prefix onboarding\n43: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n44: \n45: ## Objetivo\n46: Make destination projects self-describing for ticket IDs. The installer should accept `--prefix XXX` and persist it into the destination metadata, validation should warn when a host-project lacks `Ticket prefix`, and the onboarding docs should describe the local `XXX-YYYY-NNN` namespace. The motor repo keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - `scripts/install_agent_system.py --install --prefix XXX` writes the namespace into the destination `PROJECT.md` and into `motor_destination_link.json`.\n50: - `--sync` preserves the destination prefix and keeps the link file auditable.\n51: - `--validate` warns when `active_profile == host-project` and `PROJECT.md` lacks `Ticket prefix:`.\n52: - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` uses destination-prefixed examples for plan/audit and ticket IDs.\n53: - Cross-cutting docs (`AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `QUICKSTART.md`, `RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`, `CLOSURE_MODEL.md`, `PROJECT.md`) stay aligned with the same contract.\n54: - No change to the motor namespace: `WP-YYYY-NNN` remains for this repo.\n55: \n56: ## Files Likely Touched\n57: - `scripts/install_agent_system.py`\n58: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n59: - `tests/unit/test_install_agent_system.py`\n60: - `tests/unit/test_validate_host_prefix.py`\n61: - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md`\n62: - `AGENTS.md`\n63: - `PROJECT.md`\n64: - `README.md`\n65: - `QUICKSTART.md`\n66: - `RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`\n67: - `CLOSURE_MODEL.md`\n68: \n69: ## Fases\n70: 1. Add prefix plumbing to the installer and link metadata.\n71: 2. Add validation warnings when a destination has no declared prefix.\n72: 3. Align bootstrap and public docs with the destination namespace convention.\n73: 4. Verify the local tests and canonical validation path.\n74: \n75: Note: `motor_destination_link.json` is generated by the installer and lives in the destination workspace; it is not edited directly in the motor repo.\n76: \n77: ## Calidad\n78: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_install_agent_system.py -q`\n79: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_validate_host_prefix.py -q`\n80: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n81: \n82: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n83: - `--install --prefix XXX` writes the prefix into the destination metadata and link file.\n84: - `--validate` warns when a host-project is missing `Ticket prefix: XXX` in `PROJECT.md`.\n85: - The bootstrap docs use `XXX-YYYY-NNN` for destination examples and keep `WP-YYYY-NNN` for the motor.\n86: - The canonical validation path passes without new errors.\n87: \n88: \n89: --- STATE.md ---\n90: # State - WP-2026-144\n91: \n92: Plan Activo: WP-2026-144\n93: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n94: Rol activo: BUILDER\n95: \n96: Resumen:\n97: WP-2026-144 en progreso: el Builder implementara el namespace local de tickets para proyectos destino y la trazabilidad asociada.\n98: \n99: Notas:\n100: - El alcance es mixed.\n101: - El motor conserva `WP-YYYY-NNN`.\n102: - Los destinos usaran `XXX-YYYY-NNN` con `Ticket prefix: XXX` declarado en `PROJECT.md`.\n103: \n104: \n105: --- TURN.md ---\n106: # TURNO ACTUAL\n107: \n108: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-26 17:21:27\n109: \n110: ---\n111: \n112: ## Agente Activo\n113: \n114: | Campo | Valor |\n115: |-------|-------|\n116: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n117: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-144 |\n118: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n119: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n120: \n121: ---\n122: \n123: ## Instruccion\n124: \n125: > Builder completo WP-2026-144. Revisa el trabajo.\n126: \n127: ---\n128: \n129: ## Archivos a Leer\n130: \n131: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n132: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n133: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n134: \n135: ---\n136: \n137: ## Estado del Sistema\n138: \n139: | Archivo | Estado |\n140: |---------|--------|\n141: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n142: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n143: \n144: ---\n145: \n146: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n147: \n148: \n149: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n150: [execution_log section for WP-2026-144 not found]\n151: \n152: --- git provenance ---\n153: [no new commits since origin/main]\n154: \n155: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n156: Available skills for this role:\n157:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n158:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n159:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n160: \n161: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n162: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n163: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n164: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n165: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n166: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n167: - .session_state.json\n168: \n169: \n170: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n171: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n172: \n173: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n174: \n175: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n176: DECISION: APPROVE\n177: \n178: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n179: \n180: ## SUMMARY\n181: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n182: \n183: ## BLOCKERS\n184: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n185: \n186: ## SUGGESTIONS\n187: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n188: \n189: DECISION: CHANGES\n190: \n191: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n192: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 192 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review mixed ticket WP-2026-144. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'.
