#!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Install or sync the Agent System from orquestador_de_agentes/ motor central.\n4: \n5: ARCHITECTURE: Central motor + destination workspace.\n6: - The motor (operational code) lives once in the source repo (orquestador_de_agentes).\n7: - The destination workspace keeps only .agent/ state, memory, events, and config.\n8: - The installer prepares (bootstraps) the destination to consume the external motor.\n9: - The motor is NOT copied to the destination.\n10: \n11: Usage:\n12:     python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --install\n13:     python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --sync\n14:         → Sync + auto-remove residues (strict, default)\n15: \n16:     python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --sync --dry-run\n17:         → Preview changes without modifying\n18: \n19:     python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --sync --prune\n20:         → Sync + interactively choose which residues to remove\n21: \"\"\"\n22: \n23: from __future__ import annotations\n24: \n25: import argparse\n26: import json\n27: import shutil\n28: import subprocess\n29: import sys\n30: from collections.abc import Iterable\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: # Paths\n36: REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent\n37: TEMPLATE_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / \"orquestador_de_agentes\"\n38: TEMPLATE_AGENT = TEMPLATE_ROOT / \".agent\"\n39: PROJECT_AGENT = REPO_ROOT / \".agent\"\n40: \n41: # Directories to preserve in the project instance.\n42: LOCAL_DIRS = {\"collaboration\", \"runtime\"}\n43: \n44: # Generated / transient directories that should not be part of canonical sync.\n45: IGNORED_NAMES = {\"__pycache__\", \".ruff_cache\", \".tmp\"}\n46: \n47: VERSION_MANIFEST_NAME = \".version_manifest.json\"\n48: HOOKS_CONFIG_REL = Path(\"config\") / \"hooks_config.json\"\n49: \n50: # Host setup hook candidates (detected post-copy in destination)\n51: HOST_SETUP_CANDIDATES = (\"host-setup.sh\", \"host-setup.ps1\")\n52: \n53: # Manifest file for portable workspace allowlist\n54: MANIFEST_WORKSPACE = \"MANIFEST.workspace\"\n55: MANIFEST_WORKSPACE_VERSION = \"1.0\"\n56: \n57: \n58: def read_json(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n59:     if not path.exists():\n60:         return None\n61:     try:\n62:         return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n63:     except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\n64:         print(f\"[WARN] Invalid JSON in {path}: {exc}\")\n65:         return None\n66: \n67: \n68: def write_json(path: Path, payload: dict, dry_run: bool = False) -> None:\n69:     if dry_run:\n70:         return\n71:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n72:     path.write_text(\n73:         json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + \"\\n\",\n74:         encoding=\"utf-8\",\n75:     )\n76: \n77: \n78: def read_manifest_allowlist(template_root: Path) -> set[str]:\n79:     \"\"\"\n80:     Read MANIFEST.workspace and return set of allowed paths.\n81: \n82:     Before: Manifest file may or may not exist at template_root/MANIFEST.workspace.\n83:     During: Parses file, skipping comments and empty lines, stripping whitespace.\n84:     After: Returns set of path strings, or empty set if file missing.\n85: \n86:     Args:\n87:         template_root: Root path of the template repository.\n88: \n89:     Returns:\n90:         Set of allowed path strings from the manifest.\n91:     \"\"\"\n92:     manifest_path = template_root / MANIFEST_WORKSPACE\n93:     if not manifest_path.exists():\n94:         return set()\n95: \n96:     allowed: set[str] = set()\n97:     for line in manifest_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n98:         line = line.strip()\n99:         # Skip comments and empty lines\n100:         if not line or line.startswith(\"#\"):\n101:             continue\n102:         allowed.add(line)\n103: \n104:     return allowed\n105: \n106: \n107: def get_manifest_version(agent_dir: Path) -> str | None:\n108:     manifest = read_json(agent_dir / VERSION_MANIFEST_NAME)\n109:     if not manifest:\n110:         return None\n111:     version = manifest.get(\"version\")\n112:     if isinstance(version, str) and version.strip():\n113:         return version.strip()\n114:     agent_core_version = manifest.get(\"agent_core_version\")\n115:     if isinstance(agent_core_version, str) and agent_core_version.strip():\n116:         return agent_core_version.strip()\n117:     return None\n118: \n119: \n120: def is_preserved(rel_path: Path) -> bool:\n121:     return bool(rel_path.parts) and rel_path.parts[0] in LOCAL_DIRS\n122: \n123: \n124: def is_ignored(rel_path: Path) -> bool:\n125:     return any(part in IGNORED_NAMES for part in rel_path.parts)\n126: \n127: \n128: def is_in_allowlist(rel_path: Path, allowlist: set[str]) -> bool:\n129:     \"\"\"\n130:     Check if a relative path is in the allowlist.\n131: \n132:     Supports both exact matches and directory prefixes (e.g., 'skills/' matches 'skills/foo/bar.py').\n133: \n134:     Args:\n135:         rel_path: Relative path to check.\n136:         allowlist: Set of allowed paths from MANIFEST.workspace.\n137: \n138:     Returns:\n139:         True if path is allowed, False otherwise.\n140:     \"\"\"\n141:     path_str = \".agent/\" + rel_path.as_posix()\n142: \n143:     # Exact match\n144:     if path_str in allowlist:\n145:         return True\n146: \n147:     # Directory prefix match (e.g., 'skills/' matches 'skills/test.py')\n148:     for allowed in allowlist:\n149:         if allowed.endswith(\"/\") and path_str.startswith(allowed):\n150:             return True\n151: \n152:     return False\n153: \n154: \n155: def iter_canonical_entries(root: Path, include_ignored: bool = False) -> list[Path]:\n156:     entries: list[Path] = []\n157:     if not root.exists():\n158:         return entries\n159: \n160:     for item in root.rglob(\"*\"):\n161:         rel = item.relative_to(root)\n162:         if is_preserved(rel):\n163:             continue\n164:         if not include_ignored and is_ignored(rel):\n165:             continue\n166:         entries.append(rel)\n167: \n168:     return entries\n169: \n170: \n171: def compact_paths(paths: Iterable[Path]) -> list[Path]:\n172:     ordered = sorted(paths, key=lambda p: (len(p.parts), str(p)))\n173:     compacted: list[Path] = []\n174:     for rel in ordered:\n175:         if any(\n176:             rel == existing or rel.is_relative_to(existing) for existing in compacted\n177:         ):\n178:             continue\n179:         compacted.append(rel)\n180:     return compacted\n181: \n182: \n183: def detect_destination_residues(source: Path, dest: Path) -> list[Path]:\n184:     source_entries = set(iter_canonical_entries(source, include_ignored=False))\n185:     dest_entries = set(iter_canonical_entries(dest, include_ignored=True))\n186:     residues = dest_entries - source_entries\n187:     return compact_paths(residues)\n188: \n189: \n190: def ensure_parent_dirs(path: Path, dry_run: bool) -> None:\n191:     if path.exists():\n192:         if path.is_file() or path.is_symlink():\n193:             if dry_run:\n194:                 return\n195:             path.unlink()\n196:         else:\n197:             return\n198: \n199:     if dry_run:\n200:         return\n201:     path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n202: \n203: \n204: def copy_tree(\n205:     source: Path, dest: Path, dry_run: bool = False, allowlist: set[str] | None = None\n206: ) -> list[Path]:\n207:     \"\"\"\n208:     Copy tree from source to dest, respecting allowlist if provided.\n209: \n210:     Before: source directory exists; allowlist may be None (copy all non-ignored).\n211:     During: Iterates source items, skips preserved/ignored paths, checks allowlist.\n212:     After: Returns list of copied relative paths; raises if non-allowlisted path found.\n213: \n214:     Args:\n215:         source: Source directory root.\n216:         dest: Destination directory root.\n217:         dry_run: If True, simulate without modifying filesystem.\n218:         allowlist: Set of allowed paths from MANIFEST.workspace, or None for legacy behavior.\n219: \n220:     Returns:\n221:         List of relative paths that were copied.\n222: \n223:     Raises:\n224:         RuntimeError: If a path outside the allowlist would be copied.\n225:     \"\"\"\n226:     copied: list[Path] = []\n227:     if not source.exists():\n228:         return copied\n229: \n230:     for item in source.iterdir():\n231:         rel = item.relative_to(source)\n232:         if is_preserved(rel) or is_ignored(rel):\n233:             continue\n234: \n235:         # Check allowlist if provided\n236:         if allowlist is not None and not is_in_allowlist(rel, allowlist):\n237:             raise RuntimeError(\n238:                 f\"Contract violation: '{rel.as_posix()}' is outside the allowlist. \"\n239:                 \"The destination workspace must not include unauthorized motor/legacy code.\"\n240:             )\n241: \n242:         dst_item = dest / item.name\n243: \n244:         if item.is_dir():\n245:             if dry_run:\n246:                 copied.append(rel)\n247:                 continue\n248: \n249:             if dst_item.exists() and dst_item.is_file():\n250:                 dst_item.unlink()\n251:             shutil.copytree(\n252:                 item,\n253:                 dst_item,\n254:                 dirs_exist_ok=True,\n255:                 ignore=shutil.ignore_patterns(*IGNORED_NAMES),\n256:             )\n257:             copied.append(rel)\n258:             continue\n259: \n260:         if dry_run:\n261:             copied.append(rel)\n262:             continue\n263: \n264:         if dst_item.exists() and dst_item.is_dir():\n265:             shutil.rmtree(dst_item)\n266: \n267:         ensure_parent_dirs(dst_item.parent, dry_run=False)\n268:         shutil.copy2(item, dst_item)\n269:         copied.append(rel)\n270: \n271:     return copied\n272: \n273: \n274: def _select_residues_interactive(residues: list[Path]) -> list[Path]:\n275:     \"\"\"Interactive selection of residues to prune.\"\"\"\n276:     print(\"\\n[PRUNE] Residues detected:\")\n277:     for idx, rel in enumerate(residues, start=1):\n278:         print(f\"  {idx}. {rel.as_posix()}\")\n279:     try:\n280:         answer = (\n281:             input(\"\\nClean which residues? [all/comma-list/none]: \").strip().lower()\n282:         )\n283:     except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):\n284:         print(\"[PRUNE] Cancelled.\")\n285:         return []\n286: \n287:     if not answer or answer in {\"none\", \"no\", \"n\"}:\n288:         return []\n289:     if answer == \"all\":\n290:         return residues\n291: \n292:     indices: list[int] = []\n293:     for chunk in answer.split(\",\"):\n294:         chunk = chunk.strip()\n295:         if not chunk:\n296:             continue\n297:         if not chunk.isdigit():\n298:             print(f\"[WARN] Ignoring invalid residue index: {chunk}\")\n299:             continue\n300:         idx = int(chunk)\n301:         if 1 <= idx <= len(residues):\n302:             indices.append(idx)\n303:     return [residues[i - 1] for i in sorted(set(indices))]\n304: \n305: \n306: def _prune_selected_residues(\n307:     dest: Path, selected: list[Path], dry_run: bool\n308: ) -> list[Path]:\n309:     \"\"\"Prune the selected residues.\"\"\"\n310:     removed: list[Path] = []\n311:     for rel in selected:\n312:         target = dest / rel\n313:         if dry_run:\n314:             print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Would prune: {rel.as_posix()}\")\n315:             removed.append(rel)\n316:             continue\n317: \n318:         if target.is_dir() and not target.is_symlink():\n319:             shutil.rmtree(target)\n320:         elif target.exists() or target.is_symlink():\n321:             target.unlink()\n322:         removed.append(rel)\n323:         print(f\"[PRUNED] {rel.as_posix()}\")\n324: \n325:     return removed\n326: \n327: \n328: def prune_residues(\n329:     dest: Path, residues: list[Path], dry_run: bool, interactive: bool\n330: ) -> list[Path]:\n331:     if not residues:\n332:         return []\n333: \n334:     selected = residues\n335:     if interactive and not dry_run:\n336:         selected = _select_residues_interactive(residues)\n337:         if not selected:\n338:             return []\n339: \n340:     return _prune_selected_residues(dest, selected, dry_run)\n341: \n342: \n343: def ensure_hooks_config_integrity(agent_dir: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:\n344:     \"\"\"\n345:     Validate hooks_config.json structural and semantic integrity.\n346: \n347:     Checks:\n348:     - File exists and is valid JSON\n349:     - Root is a dict\n350:     - Has required fields: \"version\" (string), \"enabled\" (bool)\n351:     - Does NOT modify any fields (validation only)\n352: \n353:     Returns False if validation fails (no drift allowed).\n354:     \"\"\"\n355:     hooks_config = agent_dir / HOOKS_CONFIG_REL\n356:     payload = read_json(hooks_config)\n357: \n358:     # Check 1: Valid JSON and dict\n359:     if not payload or not isinstance(payload, dict):\n360:         print(\"[ERROR] hooks_config.json is not a valid dict\")\n361:         return False\n362: \n363:     # Check 2: Required fields exist with correct types\n364:     required_fields = {\n365:         \"version\": str,\n366:         \"enabled\": bool,\n367:     }\n368: \n369:     for field_name, expected_type in required_fields.items():\n370:         if field_name not in payload:\n371:             print(f\"[ERROR] hooks_config.json missing required field: {field_name}\")\n372:             return False\n373: \n374:         value = payload[field_name]\n375:         if not isinstance(value, expected_type):\n376:             print(\n377:                 f\"[ERROR] hooks_config.json field '{field_name}' \"\n378:                 f\"has type {type(value).__name__}, expected {expected_type.__name__}\"\n379:             )\n380:             return False\n381: \n382:     # All checks passed\n383:     return True\n384: \n385: \n386: def flip_profile_in_destination(project_agent: Path, dry_run: bool = False) -> None:\n387:     \"\"\"\n388:     Ensure the active_profile in agents.json is set to 'host-project' at destination.\n389: \n390:     Before: The template has 'active_profile': 'engine-dev'.\n391:     During: If CWD is the destination project, we update it to 'host-project' to flip profile.\n392:     After: Destination project has the host-project profile.\n393:     \"\"\"\n394:     config_file = project_agent / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n395:     if not config_file.exists():\n396:         return\n397:     try:\n398:         content = config_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n399:         payload = json.loads(content)\n400:         if payload.get(\"active_profile\") == \"engine-dev\":\n401:             if dry_run:\n402:                 print(\n403:                     \"[DRY-RUN] Would flip active_profile in agents.json to 'host-project'\"\n404:                 )\n405:                 return\n406:             payload[\"active_profile\"] = \"host-project\"\n407:             config_file.write_text(\n408:                 json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + \"\\n\",\n409:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n410:             )\n411:             print(\"[INFO] Flipped destination active_profile to 'host-project'\")\n412:     except Exception as e:\n413:         print(f\"[WARN] Failed to flip active_profile in {config_file}: {e}\")\n414: \n415: \n416: def write_motor_destination_link(\n417:     project_agent: Path,\n418:     motor_root: Path,\n419:     destination_root: Path,\n420:     motor_version: str | None,\n421:     destination_id: str | None = None,\n422:     ticket_prefix: str | None = None,\n423:     dry_run: bool = False,\n424: ) -> None:\n425:     \"\"\"\n426:     Write the motor-destination link file in the destination workspace.\n427: \n428:     Before: Destination .agent/config/ directory exists; link file may or may not exist.\n429:     During: Creates/overwrites motor_destination_link.json with schema fields.\n430:     After: Link file exists with motor_root, destination_root, motor_version, ticket_prefix, etc.\n431: \n432:     Schema:\n433:     - motor_root: absolute or derived path to the external motor\n434:     - destination_root: absolute path to the destination workspace\n435:     - motor_version: technical version from .agent/.version_manifest.json\n436:     - destination_id: stable identifier for the destination (optional, derived from path)\n437:     - ticket_prefix: local ticket namespace prefix (e.g., 'XXX' for 'XXX-YYYY-NNN')\n438:     - created_at: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of install/sync\n439:     - manifest_version: MANIFEST.workspace contract version applied\n440: \n441:     Args:\n442:         project_agent: Path to destination .agent/ directory.\n443:         motor_root: Path to the motor central repository.\n444:         destination_root: Path to the destination project root.\n445:         motor_version: Version string from motor's .version_manifest.json.\n446:         destination_id: Optional stable identifier; defaults to destination_root name.\n447:         ticket_prefix: Optional ticket prefix for destination namespace (e.g., 'XXX').\n448:         dry_run: If True, simulate without writing.\n449:     \"\"\"\n450:     config_dir = project_agent / \"config\"\n451:     link_file = config_dir / \"motor_destination_link.json\"\n452: \n453:     if destination_id is None:\n454:         destination_id = destination_root.name\n455: \n456:     payload = {\n457:         \"motor_root\": str(motor_root.resolve()),\n458:         \"destination_root\": str(destination_root.resolve()),\n459:         \"motor_version\": motor_version or \"unknown\",\n460:         \"destination_id\": destination_id,\n461:         \"ticket_prefix\": ticket_prefix,\n462:         \"created_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n463:         \"manifest_version\": MANIFEST_WORKSPACE_VERSION,\n464:     }\n465: \n466:     if dry_run:\n467:         print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Would write motor-destination link: {link_file}\")\n468:         print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Link payload: {json.dumps(payload, indent=2)}\")\n469:         return\n470: \n471:     config_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n472:     link_file.write_text(\n473:         json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + \"\\n\",\n474:         encoding=\"utf-8\",\n475:     )\n476:     print(f\"[INFO] Wrote motor-destination link: {link_file}\")\n477: \n478: \n479: def _detect_host_setup(destination: Path) -> Path | None:\n480:     \"\"\"\n481:     Return the first existing host-setup.{sh,ps1} in destination/.agent/, or None.\n482: \n483:     Before: destination/.agent/ directory exists (post-copy).\n484:     During: Scans for host-setup.sh first, then host-setup.ps1.\n485:     After: Returns Path to hook if found, None otherwise.\n486:     \"\"\"\n487:     agent_dir = destination / \".agent\"\n488:     for name in HOST_SETUP_CANDIDATES:\n489:         candidate = agent_dir / name\n490:         if candidate.exists() and candidate.is_file():\n491:             return candidate\n492:     return None\n493: \n494: \n495: def _maybe_invoke_host_setup(\n496:     destination: Path,\n497:     *,\n498:     auto_yes: bool = False,\n499:     dry_run: bool = False,\n500:     input_fn=input,\n501: ) -> int:\n502:     \"\"\"\n503:     Detect and optionally invoke .agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1} in destination.\n504: \n505:     Before: destination/.agent/ exists post-copy; hook may or may not be present.\n506:     During:\n507:       - Detects hook via _detect_host_setup().\n508:       - If dry_run: prints \"Would invoke\" message, returns 0.\n509:       - If found: prints first 20 lines, prompts for confirmation (unless auto_yes).\n510:       - If confirmed: executes hook with cwd=destination.\n511:     After:\n512:       - Returns 0 if no hook, user declined, or success.\n513:       - Returns non-zero exit code if hook failed (propagates, does not mask).\n514: \n515:     Args:\n516:         destination: Project root directory.\n517:         auto_yes: Skip interactive confirmation (for CI).\n518:         dry_run: Print what would happen without executing.\n519:         input_fn: Injected input function for testing (default: built-in input).\n520: \n521:     Returns:\n522:         Exit code: 0 = no hook/skipped/success, non-zero = hook failed.\n523:     \"\"\"\n524:     hook = _detect_host_setup(destination)\n525:     if hook is None:\n526:         return 0\n527:     rel = hook.relative_to(destination)\n528:     if dry_run:\n529:         print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Would invoke host setup hook: {rel}\")\n530:         return 0\n531:     print(f\"[host-setup] Detected: {rel}\")\n532:     print(\"[host-setup] First 20 lines:\")\n533:     for line in hook.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()[:20]:\n534:         print(f\"  {line}\")\n535:     if not auto_yes:\n536:         answer = input_fn(\"[host-setup] Execute this script? (y/N) \").strip().lower()\n537:         if answer != \"y\":\n538:             print(\"[host-setup] Skipped by user.\")\n539:             return 0\n540:     if hook.suffix == \".sh\":\n541:         cmd = [\"bash\", str(hook)]\n542:     else:\n543:         cmd = [\"powershell\", \"-ExecutionPolicy\", \"Bypass\", \"-File\", str(hook)]\n544:     rc = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=destination).returncode  # noqa: S603\n545:     if rc != 0:\n546:         print(f\"[host-setup] FAILED with exit code {rc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n547:     return rc\n548: \n549: \n550: def _write_prefix_to_project_md(destination_root: Path, prefix: str, dry_run: bool) -> None:\n551:     \"\"\"\n552:     Write or update 'Ticket prefix: XXX' in destination PROJECT.md.\n553: \n554:     Before: destination_root/PROJECT.md may or may not exist; prefix is a non-empty string.\n555:     During: Reads PROJECT.md, adds or updates 'Ticket prefix:' line.\n556:     After: PROJECT.md contains 'Ticket prefix: {prefix}' near the top.\n557: \n558:     Args:\n559:         destination_root: Project root directory.\n560:         prefix: Ticket prefix string (e.g., 'XXX').\n561:         dry_run: If True, simulate without writing.\n562:     \"\"\"\n563:     project_md = destination_root / \"PROJECT.md\"\n564:     if not project_md.exists():\n565:         # Create minimal PROJECT.md with prefix\n566:         content = f\"# Project: {destination_root.name}\\nTicket prefix: {prefix}\\n\"\n567:         if dry_run:\n568:             print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Would create {project_md} with ticket prefix\")\n569:             return\n570:         project_md.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n571:         print(f\"[INFO] Created {project_md} with ticket prefix\")\n572:         return\n573: \n574:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n575:     lines = content.splitlines()\n576: \n577:     # Check if 'Ticket prefix:' already exists\n578:     prefix_line_found = False\n579:     new_lines = []\n580:     for line in lines:\n581:         if line.strip().startswith(\"Ticket prefix:\"):\n582:             prefix_line_found = True\n583:             new_lines.append(f\"Ticket prefix: {prefix}\")\n584:         else:\n585:             new_lines.append(line)\n586: \n587:     if not prefix_line_found:\n588:         # Insert after first line (header)\n589:         new_lines.insert(1, f\"Ticket prefix: {prefix}\")\n590: \n591:     new_content = \"\\n\".join(new_lines) + \"\\n\"\n592:     if dry_run:\n593:         print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Would update {project_md} with ticket prefix\")\n594:         return\n595:     project_md.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n596:     print(f\"[INFO] Updated {project_md} with ticket prefix\")\n597: \n598: \n599: def install_agent_system(\n600:     template_agent: Path,\n601:     project_agent: Path,\n602:     ticket_prefix: str | None = None,\n603:     dry_run: bool = False,\n604:     auto_yes: bool = False,\n605: ) -> int:\n606:     print(\"[INSTALL] Agent System from orquestador_de_agentes/\")\n607: \n608:     if project_agent.exists():\n609:         print(f\"[WARN] {project_agent} already exists. Use --sync to update.\")\n610:         return 1\n611: \n612:     if dry_run:\n613:         print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Would create {project_agent}\")\n614: \n615:     if not dry_run:\n616:         project_agent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n617: \n618:     # Read allowlist from MANIFEST.workspace\n619:     template_root = template_agent.parent\n620:     allowlist = read_manifest_allowlist(template_root)\n621:     if allowlist:\n622:         print(f\"[INSTALL] Using MANIFEST.workspace: {len(allowlist)} allowed paths\")\n623:     else:\n624:         print(\"[WARN] MANIFEST.workspace not found, using legacy copy-all behavior\")\n625: \n626:     copied = copy_tree(\n627:         template_agent, project_agent, dry_run=dry_run, allowlist=allowlist\n628:     )\n629:     flip_profile_in_destination(project_agent, dry_run=dry_run)\n630: \n631:     # Write motor-destination link file\n632:     motor_version = get_manifest_version(template_agent)\n633:     destination_root = project_agent.parent\n634:     motor_root = template_agent.parent\n635:     write_motor_destination_link(\n636:         project_agent=project_agent,\n637:         motor_root=motor_root,\n638:         destination_root=destination_root,\n639:         motor_version=motor_version,\n640:         ticket_prefix=ticket_prefix,\n641:         dry_run=dry_run,\n642:     )\n643: \n644:     # Write ticket prefix to PROJECT.md if provided\n645:     if ticket_prefix:\n646:         _write_prefix_to_project_md(destination_root, ticket_prefix, dry_run=dry_run)\n647: \n648:     integrity_ok = ensure_hooks_config_integrity(project_agent, dry_run=dry_run)\n649: \n650:     if dry_run:\n651:         print(\n652:             f\"\\n[DRY-RUN] Install plan: {len(copied)} top-level entries would be copied.\"\n653:         )\n654:         return 0\n655: \n656:     # Validate hooks config integrity (critical for \"no drift\" policy)\n657:     if not integrity_ok:\n658:         print(\"[ERROR] Hooks config integrity check failed. Install aborted.\")\n659:         return 1\n660: \n661:     # Invoke host setup hook if present\n662:     rc = _maybe_invoke_host_setup(\n663:         project_agent.parent, auto_yes=auto_yes, dry_run=dry_run\n664:     )\n665:     if rc != 0:\n666:         print(\"[install] Host setup failed; aborting install.\", file=sys.stderr)\n667:         return rc\n668: \n669:     print(f\"\\n[SUCCESS] Agent System installed at {PROJECT_AGENT}\")\n670:     return 0\n671: \n672: \n673: def sync_agent_system(  # noqa: C901\n674:     template_agent: Path,\n675:     project_agent: Path,\n676:     ticket_prefix: str | None = None,\n677:     dry_run: bool = False,\n678:     strict_sync: bool = False,\n679:     prune: bool = False,\n680:     auto_yes: bool = False,\n681: ) -> int:\n682:     print(\"[SYNC] Agent System from orquestador_de_agentes/\")\n683: \n684:     if not project_agent.exists():\n685:         print(\"[ERROR] Project .agent/ not found. Run --install first.\")\n686:         return 1\n687: \n688:     manifest_version = get_manifest_version(template_agent)\n689:     if manifest_version:\n690:         print(f\"[INFO] Template version: {manifest_version}\")\n691:     else:\n692:         print(\"[WARN] Template version manifest not found.\")\n693: \n694:     current_manifest = read_json(project_agent / VERSION_MANIFEST_NAME)\n695:     if current_manifest:\n696:         current_version = current_manifest.get(\"version\") or current_manifest.get(\n697:             \"agent_core_version\", \"unknown\"\n698:         )\n699:         print(f\"[INFO] Current project version: {current_version}\")\n700: \n701:     # Read allowlist from MANIFEST.workspace\n702:     template_root = template_agent.parent\n703:     allowlist = read_manifest_allowlist(template_root)\n704:     if allowlist:\n705:         print(f\"[SYNC] Using MANIFEST.workspace: {len(allowlist)} allowed paths\")\n706:     else:\n707:         print(\"[WARN] MANIFEST.workspace not found, using legacy copy-all behavior\")\n708: \n709:     residues = detect_destination_residues(template_agent, project_agent)\n710:     if residues:\n711:         print(f\"[WARN] Destination residues detected: {len(residues)}\")\n712:         for rel in residues:\n713:             print(f\"  - {rel.as_posix()}\")\n714:     else:\n715:         print(\"[OK] No destination residues detected.\")\n716: \n717:     copied = copy_tree(\n718:         template_agent, project_agent, dry_run=dry_run, allowlist=allowlist\n719:     )\n720:     flip_profile_in_destination(project_agent, dry_run=dry_run)\n721: \n722:     # Write motor-destination link file (idempotent update)\n723:     motor_version = get_manifest_version(template_agent)\n724:     destination_root = project_agent.parent\n725:     motor_root = template_agent.parent\n726:     write_motor_destination_link(\n727:         project_agent=project_agent,\n728:         motor_root=motor_root,\n729:         destination_root=destination_root,\n730:         motor_version=motor_version,\n731:         ticket_prefix=ticket_prefix,\n732:         dry_run=dry_run,\n733:     )\n734: \n735:     # Update PROJECT.md with ticket prefix if provided\n736:     if ticket_prefix:\n737:         _write_prefix_to_project_md(destination_root, ticket_prefix, dry_run=dry_run)\n738: \n739:     integrity_ok = ensure_hooks_config_integrity(project_agent, dry_run=dry_run)\n740: \n741:     # Policy: \"no drift\" — remove residues by default (strict mode)\n742:     # --prune overrides to ask interactively\n743:     # These flags are now mutually exclusive; default to strict if neither specified\n744:     pruned: list[Path] = []\n745:     if prune:\n746:         # Interactive mode: ask user which residues to remove\n747:         pruned = prune_residues(\n748:             project_agent, residues, dry_run=dry_run, interactive=True\n749:         )\n750:     else:\n751:         # Strict mode (default): remove all residues automatically\n752:         pruned = prune_residues(\n753:             project_agent, residues, dry_run=dry_run, interactive=False\n754:         )\n755: \n756:     if dry_run:\n757:         print(\n758:             f\"\\n[DRY-RUN] Sync plan: {len(copied)} top-level entries would be copied/updated.\"\n759:         )\n760:         print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Residues to prune: {len(residues)}\")\n761:         if pruned:\n762:             mode = \"interactive\" if prune else \"automatic (strict)\"\n763:             print(f\"[DRY-RUN] Residues selected for cleanup ({mode}): {len(pruned)}\")\n764:         return 0\n765: \n766:     # Validate hooks config integrity (critical for \"no drift\" policy)\n767:     if not integrity_ok:\n768:         print(\"[ERROR] Hooks config integrity check failed. Sync aborted.\")\n769:         return 1\n770: \n771:     print(\"[OK] Hooks config integrity verified.\")\n772: \n773:     # Invoke host setup hook if present\n774:     rc = _maybe_invoke_host_setup(\n775:         project_agent.parent, auto_yes=auto_yes, dry_run=dry_run\n776:     )\n777:     if rc != 0:\n778:         print(\"[sync] Host setup failed; aborting sync.\", file=sys.stderr)\n779:         return rc\n780: \n781:     print(\n782:         f\"\\n[SUCCESS] Agent System synced. \"\n783:         f\"Local dirs preserved: {', '.join(sorted(LOCAL_DIRS))}\"\n784:     )\n785:     return 0\n786: \n787: \n788: def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:\n789:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n790:         description=\"Install or sync the Agent System from orquestador_de_agentes/.agent\"\n791:     )\n792:     group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)\n793:     group.add_argument(\"--install\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Initial setup\")\n794:     group.add_argument(\"--sync\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Sync updates\")\n795:     parser.add_argument(\n796:         \"--source\",\n797:         type=Path,\n798:         help=\"Path to orquestador_de_agentes template (auto-detected if omitted)\",\n799:     )\n800:     parser.add_argument(\n801:         \"--dest\",\n802:         type=Path,\n803:         default=Path.cwd(),\n804:         help=\"Project root destination (default: current directory)\",\n805:     )\n806:     parser.add_argument(\n807:         \"--prefix\",\n808:         type=str,\n809:         default=None,\n810:         help=\"Ticket prefix for destination namespace (e.g., 'XXX' for 'XXX-YYYY-NNN')\",\n811:     )\n812:     parser.add_argument(\n813:         \"--dry-run\",\n814:         action=\"store_true\",\n815:         help=\"Preview changes without copying or deleting\",\n816:     )\n817:     # Prune options: mutually exclusive\n818:     prune_group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group()\n819:     prune_group.add_argument(\n820:         \"--prune\",\n821:         action=\"store_true\",\n822:         help=\"Interactively choose which destination residues to delete\",\n823:     )\n824:     prune_group.add_argument(\n825:         \"--strict-sync\",\n826:         action=\"store_true\",\n827:         help=\"Explicit strict sync (deprecated: this is now the default)\",\n828:     )\n829:     parser.add_argument(\n830:         \"--force\",\n831:         action=\"store_true\",\n832:         help=\"Keep for backward compatibility (no longer blocks sync)\",\n833:     )\n834:     parser.add_argument(\n835:         \"--yes\",\n836:         action=\"store_true\",\n837:         help=\"Skip interactive confirmation for host-setup hook (CI mode)\",\n838:     )\n839:     return parser\n840: \n841: \n842: def detect_template_root(user_source: Path | None) -> Path:\n843:     if user_source:\n844:         candidate = user_source.resolve()\n845:         if (candidate / \".agent\" / \"agent_controller.py\").exists():\n846:             return candidate\n847:         raise FileNotFoundError(\n848:             f\"Provided source does not contain a valid .agent tree: {candidate}\"\n849:         )\n850: \n851:     candidate = TEMPLATE_ROOT.resolve()\n852:     if (candidate / \".agent\" / \"agent_controller.py\").exists():\n853:         return candidate\n854: \n855:     raise FileNotFoundError(\n856:         \"Could not detect orquestador_de_agentes template. Provide --source manually.\"\n857:     )\n858: \n859: \n860: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:\n861:     parser = build_parser()\n862:     args = parser.parse_args(argv)\n863: \n864:     dest = args.dest.resolve()\n865:     if not (dest / \".agent\").exists() and not args.install:\n866:         print(f\"[ERROR] No .agent/ found at destination: {dest}\")\n867:         print(\"Run --install first or pass the correct --dest.\")\n868:         return 1\n869: \n870:     try:\n871:         template_root = detect_template_root(args.source)\n872:     except FileNotFoundError as exc:\n873:         print(f\"[ERROR] {exc}\")\n874:         return 1\n875: \n876:     template_agent = template_root / \".agent\"\n877:     project_agent = dest / \".agent\"\n878: \n879:     print(f\"[INFO] Template detected: {template_root}\")\n880:     print(f\"[INFO] Destination:      {dest}\")\n881:     print(f\"[INFO] Mode:            {'DRY-RUN' if args.dry_run else 'LIVE'}\")\n882:     if args.prefix:\n883:         print(f\"[INFO] Ticket prefix:    {args.prefix}\")\n884: \n885:     # Keep behavior explicit: install never touches an existing destination.\n886:     if args.install:\n887:         if project_agent.exists():\n888:             print(f\"[WARN] {project_agent} already exists. Use --sync for updates.\")\n889:             return 1\n890:         return install_agent_system(\n891:             template_agent=template_agent,\n892:             project_agent=project_agent,\n893:             ticket_prefix=args.prefix,\n894:             dry_run=args.dry_run,\n895:             auto_yes=args.yes,\n896:         )\n897: \n898:     # Sync updates from template to project root.\n899:     # The template root is canonical, but the current implementation is local-only\n900:     # and the repository layout is fixed, so we sync using the resolved roots.\n901:     return sync_agent_system(\n902:         template_agent=template_agent,\n903:         project_agent=project_agent,\n904:         ticket_prefix=args.prefix,\n905:         dry_run=args.dry_run,\n906:         strict_sync=args.strict_sync,\n907:         prune=args.prune,\n908:         auto_yes=args.yes,\n909:     )\n910: \n911: \n912: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n913:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 913 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nInstall or sync the Agent System from orquestador_de_agentes/ motor central.\n\nARCHITECTURE: Central motor + destination workspace.\n- The motor (operational code) lives once in the source repo (orquestador_de_agentes).\n- The destination workspace keeps only .agent/ state, memory, events, and config.\n- The installer prepares (bootstraps) the destination to consume the external motor.\n- The motor is NOT copied to the destination.\n\nUsage:\n    python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --install\n    python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --sync\n        → Sync + auto-remove residues (strict, default)\n\n    python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --sync --dry-run\n        → Preview changes without modifying\n\n    python orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --sync --prune\n        → Sync + interactively choose which residues to remove","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"scripts\\install_agent_system.py","time":{"start":1779809127616,"end":1779809127689}},"id":"prt_e64e3fe3c001IVLFcFl3HxvpxA","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e39fbe001A0InTqz0XRuyzV"}}

\"XXX\", dry_run=False)\n10: \n11:     project_md = tmp_path / \"PROJECT.md\"\n12:     assert project_md.exists()\n13:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n14:     assert \"Ticket prefix: XXX\" in content\n15: \n16: \n17: def test_write_prefix_to_project_md_updates_existing(tmp_path):\n18:     \"\"\"When PROJECT.md exists without prefix, add it after the header.\"\"\"\n19: dry_run=False)\n23: \n24:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n25:     lines = content.splitlines()\n26:     assert \"Ticket prefix: DEST\" in content\n27:     assert lines[0] == replace it with the new one.\"\"\"\n33:     project_md = tmp_path / \"PROJECT.md\"\n34:     project_md.write_text(\n35:         \"# Project: test\\nTicket prefix: OLD\\nContent\\n\", prefix: NEW\" in content\n42:     assert \"Ticket prefix: OLD\" not in content\n43: \n44: \n45: def test_write_prefix_to_project_md_dry_run(tmp_path, capsys):\n46:     \"\"\"When dry_run=True, no file is modified but message is printed.\"\"\"\n47:     project_md = tmp_path / \"PROJECT.md\"\n48:     project_md.write_text(\"# Project: test\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n49: \n50:     _write_prefix_to_project_md(tmp_path, \"XXX\", dry_run=True)\n51: \n52:     # File should not be modified\n53:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n54:     assert \"Ticket prefix:\" not in content\n55: \n56:     out = capsys.readouterr().out\n57:     assert \"Would update\" in out\n58: \n59: \n60: def test_write_prefix_to_project_md_dry_run_create(tmp_path, capsys):\n61:
