ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_AGENT_DIR), str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # ============================================================================\n100: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n101: # ============================================================================\n102: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n103: \n104: \n105: def _project_root() -> Path:\n106:     return resolve_project_root()\n107: \n108: \n109: class _LazyPath:\n110:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n111:         self._resolver = resolver\n112: \n113:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n114:         return self._resolver()\n115: \n116:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n117:         return self.resolve() / other\n118: \n119:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n120:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n121: \n122:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n123:         return str(self.resolve())\n124: \n125:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n126:         return str(self.resolve())\n127: \n128:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n129:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n130: \n131: \n132: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n133: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n134: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_agent_dir)\n135: COLLAB_DIR = _LazyPath(get_collab_dir)\n136: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_context_dir)\n137: \n138: # State files\n139: WORK_PLAN = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\")\n140: EXEC_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"execution_log.md\")\n141: REVIEW_QUEUE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"review_queue.md\")\n142: NOTIFICATIONS = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"notifications.md\")\n143: TURN_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"TURN.md\")\n144: STATE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"STATE.md\")\n145: PROJECT_MAP = _LazyPath(lambda: get_context_dir() / \"project_map.md\")\n146: ARCHIVE_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"archive\")\n147: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\")\n148: \n149: # Archive configuration\n150: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n151: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n152: \n153: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n154: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n155: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n156: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n157: \n158: \n159: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n160:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n161: \n162:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n163:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n164:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n165:     \"\"\"\n166:     try:\n167:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n168:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n169:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n170:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n171:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n172: \n173: \n174: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n175: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n176: \n177: # Estados validos para validacion\n178: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n179:     \"DRAFT\",\n180:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n181:     \"APPROVED\",\n182:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n183:     \"COMPLETED\",\n184:     \"N/A\",\n185: }\n186: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n187:     \"PENDING\",\n188:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n189:     \"BLOCKED\",\n190:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n191:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n192:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n193:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n194:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n195:     \"COMPLETED\",\n196:     \"N/A\",\n197: }\n198: \n199: \n200: def _ensure_runtime_dirs() -> None:\n201:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n202:     archive_dir = get_collab_dir() / \"archive\"\n203:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n204:     archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n205: \n206: \n207: def _get_event_bus() -> EventBus | None:\n208:     global event_bus\n209:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE:\n210:         return None\n211:     if event_bus is None:\n212:         event_bus = EventBus(get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n213:     return event_bus\n214: \n215: \n216: # Event bus is initialized lazily after the project root is known.\n217: event_bus = None\n218: \n219: # Circuit breaker state\n220: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = _LazyPath(\n221:     lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n222: )\n223: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\")\n224: \n225: # ============================================================================\n226: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n227: # ============================================================================\n228: \n229: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n230:     \"work_plan.md\",\n231:     \"execution_log.md\",\n232:     \"STATE.md\",\n233:     \"TURN.md\",\n234:     \"notifications.md\",\n235:     \".session_state.json\",\n236: }\n237: \n238: \n239: def _exclude_files() -> set[str]:\n240:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n241:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n242:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n243:     exclude_files = {str((collab_dir / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n244:     exclude_files.add(str((context_dir / \"project_map.md\").resolve()))\n245:     # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n246:     exclude_files.add(\n247:         str((agent_dir / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve())\n248:     )\n249:     # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n250:     exclude_files.add(str((agent_dir / \"config\").resolve()))\n251:     return exclude_files\n252: \n253: \n254: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n255:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n256:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n257:     in_section = False\n258:     files = set()\n259: \n260:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n261:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n262:             return False\n263:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n264:             return True\n265:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n266:             return True\n267:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n268:         return \".\" in basename\n269: \n270:     for line in lines:\n271:         line = line.strip()\n272:         if \"## Files Likely Touched\" in line:\n273:             in_section = True\n274:             continue\n275:         if in_section and line.startswith(\"## \"):\n276:             break  # next section\n277:         if in_section and line and not line.startswith(\"---\"):\n278:             # normalize: remove backticks, quotes, bullets, trim\n279:             normalized = (\n280:                 line.lstrip(\"*- \")\n281:                 .replace(\"`\", \"\")\n282:                 .replace('\"', \"\")\n283:                 .replace(\"'\", \"\")\n284:                 .strip()\n285:             )\n286:             if normalized and _looks_like_path_token(normalized):\n287:                 # resolve relative to project root\n288:                 path = (PROJECT_ROOT / normalized).resolve()\n289:                 files.add(str(path))\n290:     return files\n291: \n292: \n293: def get_changed_files() -> set[str] | None:\n294:     \"\"\"Get all changed files: staged, unstaged, untracked. None if not git repo.\"\"\"\n295:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n296:         return None\n297:     try:\n298:         # Use -z for null-byte separated output to handle paths with spaces and renames safely\n299:         result = subprocess.run(\n300:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\", \"-z\"],\n301:             capture_output=True,\n302:             text=True,\n303:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n304:         )\n305:         changed = set()\n306:         # Split by null byte, each entry is: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status\n307:         # Renames have two consecutive entries: \"R old_path\" followed by \"new_path\"\n308:         entries = result.stdout.split(\"\\0\")\n309:         i = 0\n310:         while i < len(entries):\n311:             entry = entries[i]\n312:             if not entry:\n313:                 i += 1\n314:                 continue\n315:             # Format: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status (e.g., \"M \", \"??\", \"R \")\n316:             # When Y=' ' (space), format is \"M path\" (path at index 2)\n317:             # When Y!=' ', format is \"XY path\" with space separator (path at index 3)\n318:             if len(entry) >= 3:\n319:                 status = entry[:2]\n320:                 # Determine path start: if entry[2] is space, path starts at 3; otherwise at 2\n321:                 path = entry[3:] if entry[2] == \" \" else entry[2:]\n322:                 # Handle renames: status starts with 'R', next entry is the new path\n323:                 if status[0] == \"R\" and i + 1 < len(entries):\n324:                     new_path = entries[i + 1]\n325:                     if new_path:\n326:                         changed.add(new_path)\n327:                     i += 2\n328:                     continue\n329:                 else:\n330:                     changed.add(path)\n331:             i += 1\n332:         # resolve to absolute paths\n333:         resolved = set()\n334:         for f in changed:\n335:             path = (PROJECT_ROOT / f).resolve()\n336:             resolved.add(str(path))\n337:         return resolved\n338:     except FileNotFoundError:\n339:         return None\n340: \n341: \n342: def check_scope_gate(\n343:     work_plan_content: str, changed_files: set[str] | None, exclude_files: set[str]\n344: ) -> dict:\n345:     \"\"\"Check if changed files are within scope.\"\"\"\n346:     if changed_files is None:\n347:         return {\n348:             \"valid\": True,\n349:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n350:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n351:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n352:             \"warnings\": [\"Repository is not git-managed\"],\n353:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n354:         }\n355: \n356:     whitelist = parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n357:     if not whitelist:\n358:         return {\n359:             \"valid\": True,\n360:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n361:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n362:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n363:             \"warnings\": [\"No Files Likely Touched section in work_plan.md\"],\n364:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n365:         }\n366: \n367:     relevant_changed = changed_files - exclude_files\n368:     relevant_whitelist = whitelist - exclude_files\n369:     covered_files = relevant_changed & relevant_whitelist\n370:     missing_from_diff = relevant_whitelist - relevant_changed\n371:     out_of_scope = relevant_changed - relevant_whitelist\n372: \n373:     warnings = []\n374:     blocked_reason = None\n375:     valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0\n376: \n377:     if relevant_whitelist and not covered_files:\n378:         valid = False\n379:         blocked_reason = (\n380:             \"None of the declared Files Likely Touched entries appeared in the diff\"\n381:         )\n382:     elif covered_files and missing_from_diff:\n383:         warnings.append(\n384:             \"Partial scope coverage: \"\n385:             f\"{len(covered_files)} of {len(relevant_whitelist)} declared files touched\"\n386:         )\n387: \n388:     return {\n389:         \"valid\": valid,\n390:         \"out_of_scope\": out_of_scope,\n391:         \"missing_from_diff\": missing_from_diff,\n392:         \"covered_files\": covered_files,\n393:         \"warnings\": warnings,\n394:         \"blocked_reason\": blocked_reason,\n395:     }\n396: \n397: \n398: def _load_mark_ready_context() -> tuple[str, str, str]:\n399:     \"\"\"Load the plan and log content for --mark-ready.\"\"\"\n400:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n401:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n402:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n403:     return plan_content, log_content, plan_id\n404: \n405: \n406: def _record_scope_override(scope_override: str, problem_files: set[str]) -> None:\n407:     \"\"\"Record a scope override in the execution log.\"\"\"\n408:     note = (\n409:         f\"Scope override: {scope_override}. \"\n410:         f\"Affected files: {', '.join(sorted(problem_files))}\"\n411:     )\n412:     update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", note)\n413: \n414: \n415: def _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result: dict, scope_override: str | None) -> bool:\n416:     \"\"\"Apply scope gate decision for mark-ready.\"\"\"\n417:     if gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n418:         for warning in gate_result[\"warnings\"]:\n419:             print(f\"[WARN] {warning}\")\n420:         update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Marked ready by Builder\")\n421:         return True\n422: \n423:     if not scope_override:\n424:         print(\"[ERROR] Scope violation detected:\")\n425:         if gate_result.get(\"blocked_reason\"):\n426:             print(f\"  {gate_result['blocked_reason']}\")\n427:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]):\n428:             print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n429:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())):\n430:             print(f\"  - missing: {file_path}\")\n431:         print('Use --scope-override \"reason\" to proceed.')\n432:         return False\n433: \n434:     print(f\"[INFO] Scope override applied: {scope_override}\")\n435:     problem_files = set(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]) | set(\n436:         gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())\n437:     )\n438:     _record_scope_override(scope_override, problem_files)\n439:     return True\n440: \n441: \n442: def _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id: str, plan_content: str) -> None:\n443:     \"\"\"Update TURN.md and STATE.md after mark-ready.\n444: \n445:     WP-2026-124: Uses bus-derived state as authority, not execution_log.md.\n446:     \"\"\"\n447:     # WP-2026-124: Get bus-derived state\n448:     log_status_before = get_status(read_file(EXEC_LOG), \"**Estado:**\")\n449:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n450:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n451: \n452:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n453:         bus_state = (\n454:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n455:             if events\n456:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n457:         )\n458:         # Use bus state if available, fallback to log\n459:         from_state = (\n460:             bus_state.value if bus_state != TicketState.UNKNOWN else log_status_before\n461:         )\n462:     else:\n463:         from_state = log_status_before\n464: \n465:     action = {\n466:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n467:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n468:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n469:         \"instruction\": f\"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n470:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n471:         \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n472:         \"log_status\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n473:         \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n474:         \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n475:     }\n476:     update_turn_file(action)\n477: \n478:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n479:     if not state_content:\n480:         return\n481: \n482:     lines = state_content.split(\"\\n\")\n483:     for index, line in enumerate(lines):\n484:         if \"**Estado actual:**\" in line:\n485:             lines[index] = \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n486:             write_file(STATE_FILE, \"\\n\".join(lines))\n487:             break\n488: \n489:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus idempotently\n490:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n491:         # Check if already emitted for this ticket and state\n492:         latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n493:             ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n494:         )\n495:         if (\n496:             not latest_state_event\n497:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n498:         ):\n499:             event_bus.emit(\n500:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n501:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n502:                 actor=\"BUILDER\",\n503:                 payload={\n504:                     \"from_state\": from_state,\n505:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n506:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n507:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n508:                 },\n509:             )\n510:         if (\n511:             not latest_state_event\n512:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n513:         ):\n514:             bus_from_state = (\n515:                 latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n516:                 if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n517:                 else log_status_before\n518:             )\n519:             event_bus.emit(\n520:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n521:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n522:                 actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n523:                 payload={\n524:                     \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n525:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n526:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n527:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n528:                 },\n529:             )\n530: \n531: \n532: # ============================================================================\n533: # CIRCUIT BREAKER AND CHECKOUT UTILITIES\n534: # ============================================================================\n535: \n536: \n537: def _read_circuit_breaker() -> dict:\n538:     \"\"\"Read circuit breaker state from JSON file.\"\"\"\n539:     if not CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.exists():\n540:         return {\n541:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n542:             \"failures\": 0,\n543:             \"last_failure\": None,\n544:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n545:         }\n546:     try:\n547:         return json.loads(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n548:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n549:         return {\n550:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n551:             \"failures\": 0,\n552:             \"last_failure\": None,\n553:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n554:         }\n555: \n556: \n557: def _write_circuit_breaker(state: dict) -> None:\n558:     \"\"\"Write circuit breaker state to JSON file.\"\"\"\n559:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n560:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.write_text(\n561:         json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n562:     )\n563: \n564: \n565: def _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> dict:\n566:     \"\"\"\n567:     Check circuit breaker state for a ticket.\n568:     Returns dict with 'open', 'reason', 'failures', 'no_progress_count'.\n569:     \"\"\"\n570:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n571:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\":\n572:         return {\n573:             \"open\": True,\n574:             \"reason\": breaker.get(\"reason\", \"Circuit breaker triggered\"),\n575:             \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n576:             \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n577:         }\n578:     return {\n579:         \"open\": False,\n580:         \"reason\": None,\n581:         \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n582:         \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n583:     }\n584: \n585: \n586: def _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n587:     reason: str, plan_id: str, is_no_progress: bool = False\n588: ) -> None:\n589:     \"\"\"Trigger circuit breaker to OPEN state.\"\"\"\n590:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n591:     breaker[\"state\"] = \"OPEN\"\n592:     breaker[\"reason\"] = reason\n593:     breaker[\"last_triggered\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n594:     breaker[\"ticket_id\"] = plan_id\n595:     if is_no_progress:\n596:         breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n597:     else:\n598:         breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n599:     _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n600: \n601: \n602: def _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n603:     \"\"\"Reset circuit breaker to CLOSED state after successful completion.\"\"\"\n604:     _write_circuit_breaker(\n605:         {\n606:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n607:             \"failures\": 0,\n608:             \"last_failure\": None,\n609:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n610:             \"last_reset\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n611:             \"last_ticket\": plan_id,\n612:         }\n613:     )\n614: \n615: \n616: def _record_error_for_breaker(plan_id: str, error_msg: str) -> None:\n617:     \"\"\"Record an error for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n618:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n619:     breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n620:     breaker[\"last_error\"] = error_msg\n621:     breaker[\"last_error_time\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n622: \n623:     # Trigger if 3+ consecutive errors\n624:     if breaker[\"failures\"] >= 3:\n625:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(f\"Repeated errors: {error_msg[:100]}\", plan_id)\n626:     else:\n627:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n628: \n629: \n630: def _record_no_progress_for_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n631:     \"\"\"Record no-progress event for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n632:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n633:     breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n634:     breaker[\"last_no_progress\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n635: \n636:     # Trigger if 3+ no-progress events\n637:     if breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] >= 3:\n638:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n639:             \"No progress detected in multiple iterations\", plan_id, is_no_progress=True\n640:         )\n641:     else:\n642:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n643: \n644: \n645: def _read_builder_lock() -> dict | None:\n646:     \"\"\"Read builder lock file if it exists.\"\"\"\n647:     if not BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.exists():\n648:         return None\n649:     try:\n650:         return json.loads(BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n651:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n652:         return None\n653: \n654: \n655: def _acquire_builder_lock(\n656:     plan_id: str, pid: int, role: str = \"BUILDER\", backend: str = \"unknown\"\n657: ) -> bool:\n658:     \"\"\"\n659:     Attempt to acquire builder lock atomically.\n660:     Returns True if lock was acquired, False if another session holds it.\n661:     \"\"\"\n662:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n663:     if existing is not None:\n664:         # Check if lock is stale (different ticket or dead process)\n665:         if existing.get(\"ticket_id\") != plan_id:\n666:             # Stale lock from different ticket - override\n667:             lock_data = {\n668:                 \"pid\": pid,\n669:                 \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n670:                 \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n671:                 \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n672:                 \"role\": role,\n673:                 \"backend\": backend,\n674:                 \"round\": 1,\n675:             }\n676:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n677:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n678:                 json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n679:             )\n680:             return True\n681:         # Same ticket - check if process is alive (simplified: just allow re-acquisition)\n682:         lock_data = {\n683:             \"pid\": pid,\n684:             \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n685:             \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n686:             \"started_at\": existing.get(\n687:                 \"started_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n688:             ),\n689:             \"role\": role,\n690:             \"backend\": backend,\n691:             \"round\": existing.get(\"round\", 1) + 1,\n692:         }\n693:         BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n694:             json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n695:         )\n696:         return True\n697: \n698:     # No existing lock - acquire fresh\n699:     lock_data = {\n700:         \"pid\": pid,\n701:         \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n702:         \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n703:         \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n704:         \"role\": role,\n705:         \"backend\": backend,\n706:         \"round\": 1,\n707:     }\n708:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n709:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n710:         json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n711:     )\n712:     return True\n713: \n714: \n715: def _release_builder_lock(plan_id: str) -> None:\n716:     \"\"\"Release builder lock after completion.\"\"\"\n717:     import contextlib\n718: \n719:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n720:     if existing and existing.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id:\n721:         with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n722:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.unlink()\n723: \n724: \n725: def _emit_builder_exit(plan_id: str, exit_reason: str, completion_summary: str) -> None:\n726:     \"\"\"Emit BUILDER_EXIT event to the bus - required for ticket closure.\"\"\"\n727:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n728:         event_bus.emit(\n729:             event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\",\n730:             ticket_id=plan_id,\n731:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n732:             payload={\n733:                 \"exit_reason\": exit_reason,\n734:                 \"completion_summary\": completion_summary,\n735:                 \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n736:             },\n737:         )\n738: \n739: \n740: def _auto_archive_closed_artifacts() -> None:\n741:     \"\"\"Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts during mark-ready.\n742: \n743:     Before: Requires COLLAB_DIR to exist with potential closed PLAN/AUDIT files.\n744:     During: Imports and calls archive_collaboration_artifacts.py as a library function.\n745:     After: Closed PLAN/AUDIT files are moved to _archive/plan_audit/ (idempotent, silent on no-op).\n746:     \"\"\"\n747:     try:\n748:         # Import the archive script as a module\n749:         import importlib.util\n750: \n751:         archive_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n752:             \"archive_collaboration_artifacts\",\n753:             PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n754:         )\n755:         if archive_spec and archive_spec.loader:\n756:             archive_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(archive_spec)\n757:             archive_spec.loader.exec_module(archive_mod)\n758: \n759:             # Call the archive function (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n760:             archive_mod.archive_collaboration_artifacts(\n761:                 collaboration_dir=COLLAB_DIR,\n762:                 dry_run=False,\n763:             )\n764:     except Exception as exc:\n765:         # Silent fail with debug logging - archiving is best-effort, not critical path\n766:         print(f\"[WARN] Auto-archive failed: {exc}\")\n767: \n768: \n769: # ============================================================================\n770: # UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n771: # ============================================================================\n772: \n773: \n774: def read_file(path: Path) -> str:\n775:     \"\"\"Lee un archivo si existe, retorna string vacio si no.\"\"\"\n776:     if not path.exists():\n777:         return \"\"\n778:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n779:         return f.read()\n780: \n781: \n782: def write_file(path: Path, content: str) -> None:\n783:     \"\"\"Escribe contenido a un archivo, creando directorios si es necesario.\"\"\"\n784:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n785:     with open(path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n786:         f.write(content)\n787: \n788: \n789: def get_status(content: str, marker: str) -> str:\n790:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado de un archivo buscando un marcador.\"\"\"\n791:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n792:         if marker in line:\n793:             return line.split(marker)[1].strip()\n794:     return \"UNKNOWN\"\n795: \n796: \n797: def get_plan_id(content: str) -> str:\n798:     \"\"\"Extrae el ID del plan de trabajo.\"\"\"\n799:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n800:         if \"**ID:**\" in line or \"**Plan ID:**\" in line:\n801:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n802:     return \"N/A\"\n803: \n804: \n805: def get_plan_type(content: str) -> str:\n806:     \"\"\"Extrae el tipo de plan. Valores: IMPLEMENTATION (default) | FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n807:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n808:         if \"**Tipo:**\" in line:\n809:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip().upper()\n810:     return \"IMPLEMENTATION\"\n811: \n812: \n813: def check_git_status() -> bool | None:\n814:     \"\"\"Verifica si el repositorio esta limpio.\"\"\"\n815:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n816:         return None\n817:     try:\n818:         result = subprocess.run(\n819:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\"],\n820:             capture_output=True,\n821:             text=True,\n822:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n823:         )\n824:         return not result.stdout.strip()\n825:     except FileNotFoundError:\n826:         return None\n827: \n828: \n829: def extract_status_emoji(status_str: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n830:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado limpio y el emoji.\"\"\"\n831:     emojis = {\"🟢\", \"🟡\", \"🔴\", \"🟣\", \"✅\", \"⏳\", \"❌\", \"⚠️\"}\n832:     status_clean = status_str.strip()\n833:     found_emoji = \"\"\n834:     for emoji in emojis:\n835:         if emoji in status_clean:\n836:             found_emoji = emoji\n837:             status_clean = status_clean.replace(emoji, \"\").strip()\n838:             break\n839:     return status_clean, found_emoji\n840: \n841: \n842: _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n843: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n844:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n845:     re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n846: )\n847: \n848: \n849: def _check_deliverable_type(content: str) -> list[str]:\n850:     \"\"\"Check deliverable_type field in work_plan.md content.\n851: \n852:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n853:     During: Searches for deliverable_type field using regex, validates against allowed values.\n854:     After: Returns list of warning strings (empty if valid). Never returns errors (V1 informational).\n855:     \"\"\"\n856:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n857:     if not match:\n858:         return [\n859:             \"work_plan.md missing deliverable_type field. \"\n860:             \"Add one of: code, documentation, research, analysis, mixed.\"\n861:         ]\n862:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n863:     if \"+\" in value:\n864:         return [\n865:             f\"work_plan.md deliverable_type='{value}' uses compound syntax. \"\n866:             \"Use 'mixed' instead of 'code+documentation' etc.\"\n867:         ]\n868:     if value not in _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES:\n869:         return [\n870:             f\"work_plan.md has unknown deliverable_type '{value}'. \"\n871:             f\"Expected one of: {sorted(_VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES)}\"\n872:         ]\n873:     return []\n874: \n875: \n876: def _validate_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n877:     \"\"\"Validate work_plan.md file.\"\"\"\n878:     errors = []\n879:     content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n880:     if not content:\n881:         return errors\n882: \n883:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n884:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n885:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_PLAN_STATES:\n886:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n887:     if \"**ID:**\" not in content:\n888:         errors.append(\"Falta campo **ID:**\")\n889:     return errors\n890: \n891: \n892: def _validate_execution_log() -> list[str]:\n893:     \"\"\"Validate execution_log.md file.\"\"\"\n894:     errors = []\n895:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n896:     if not content:\n897:         return errors\n898: \n899:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n900:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n901:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_LOG_STATES:\n902:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n903:     return errors\n904: \n905: \n906: def _validate_turn_file() -> list[str]:\n907:     \"\"\"Validate TURN.md file.\"\"\"\n908:     errors = []\n909:     content = read_file(TURN_FILE)\n910:     if content and \"## Agente Activo\" not in content:\n911:         errors.append(\"Falta secciÃƒÂ³n '## Agente Activo'\")\n912:     return errors\n913: \n914: \n915: def _validate_notifications() -> list[str]:\n916:     \"\"\"Validate notifications.md file.\"\"\"\n917:     errors = []\n918:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n919:     if content and \"</thinking>\" in content:\n920:         errors.append(\"Contiene etiquetas </thinking> (corrupto)\")\n921:     return errors\n922: \n923: \n924: def _validate_cross_file_consistency() -> list[str]:\n925:     \"\"\"Validate consistency across files.\"\"\"\n926:     errors = []\n927:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n928:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n929: \n930:     if not plan_content or not log_content:\n931:         return errors\n932: \n933:     plan_status_raw = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n934:     log_status_raw = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n935:     plan_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(plan_status_raw)\n936:     log_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(log_status_raw)\n937: \n938:     # Plan APPROVED pero log COMPLETED\n939:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_clean and \"COMPLETED\" in log_clean:\n940:         errors.append(\n941:             \"DRIFT: plan=APPROVED pero log=COMPLETED -- \"\n942:             \"el log pertenece a un ciclo anterior. Limpia execution_log.md.\"\n943:         )\n944: \n945:     # Plan COMPLETED pero log IN_PROGRESS\n946:     if \"COMPLETED\" in plan_clean and \"IN_PROGRESS\" in log_clean:\n947:         errors.append(\n948:             \"DRIFT: plan=COMPLETED pero log=IN_PROGRESS -- \"\n949:             \"el Builder no cerro su bitacora correctamente.\"\n950:         )\n951: \n952:     # Plan IN_PLANNING con log READY_FOR_REVIEW\n953:     if \"IN_PLANNING\" in plan_clean and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_clean:\n954:         errors.append(\n955:             \"DRIFT: plan=IN_PLANNING pero log=READY_FOR_REVIEW -- \"\n956:             \"estado imposible. El plan debe estar APPROVED antes de que el Builder entregue.\"\n957:         )\n958: \n959:     # Plan N/A pero log activo\n960:     if (\"N/A\" in plan_clean or not plan_clean) and log_clean not in (\n961:         \"N/A\",\n962:         \"COMPLETED\",\n963:         \"PENDING\",\n964:         \"\",\n965:     ):\n966:         errors.append(\n967:             f\"DRIFT: no hay plan activo pero log={log_clean} -- \"\n968:             \"limpia execution_log.md o crea un nuevo work_plan.md.\"\n969:         )\n970: \n971:     return errors\n972: \n973: \n974: def _validate_host_project_prefix() -> list[str]:\n975:     \"\"\"Validate that host-project destinations have 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md.\n976: \n977:     Before: Requires PROJECT.md to exist at project root; agents.json to be readable.\n978:     During: Checks if active_profile is 'host-project' and if PROJECT.md has 'Ticket prefix:'.\n979:     After: Returns list of warnings (empty if validation passes).\n980: \n981:     Returns:\n982:         List of warning strings (empty if no issues).\n983:     \"\"\"\n984:     warnings = []\n985:     agents_config = AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH\n986:     if not agents_config.exists():\n987:         return warnings\n988: \n989:     try:\n990:         config_data = json.loads(agents_config.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n991:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n992:         return warnings\n993: \n994:     active_profile = config_data.get(\"active_profile\", \"\")\n995:     if active_profile != \"host-project\":\n996:         return warnings\n997: \n998:     # Only check for host-project profile\n999:     project_md = PROJECT_ROOT / \"PROJECT.md\"\n1000:     if not project_md.exists():\n1001:         warnings.append(\n1002:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks PROJECT.md. \"\n1003:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace.\"\n1004:         )\n1005:         return warnings\n1006: \n1007:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1008:     if \"Ticket prefix:\" not in content:\n1009:         warnings.append(\n1010:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md. \"\n1011:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace (XXX-YYYY-NNN).\"\n1012:         )\n1013: \n1014:     return warnings\n1015: \n1016: \n1017: def validate_state_files() -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n1018:     \"\"\"Valida el formato y consistencia cruzada de los archivos de estado.\"\"\"\n1019:     return {\n1020:         \"work_plan.md\": _validate_work_plan(),\n1021:         \"execution_log.md\": _validate_execution_log(),\n1022:         \"notifications.md\": _validate_notifications(),\n1023:         \"TURN.md\": _validate_turn_file(),\n1024:         \"consistency\": _validate_cross_file_consistency(),\n1025:         \"host_project_prefix\": _validate_host_project_prefix(),\n1026:     }\n1027: \n1028: \n1029: def fix_corrupted_notifications() -> bool:\n1030:     \"\"\"Intenta reparar notifications.md si esta corrupto.\"\"\"\n1031:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1032:     if not content:\n1033:         return False\n1034:     original = content\n1035:     content = re.sub(r\"</thinking>\\s*\", \"\", content)\n1036:     content = re.sub(r\"\\n{4,}\", \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\", content)\n1037:     if content != original:\n1038:         write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, content)\n1039:         return True\n1040:     return False\n1041: \n1042: \n1043: def archive_old_notifications() -> str | None:\n1044:     \"\"\"Archiva notificaciones antiguas si el archivo es muy grande.\"\"\"\n1045:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1046:     if not content:\n1047:         return None\n1048: \n1049:     file_size_kb = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\")) / 1024\n1050:     entry_count = len([e for e in content.split(\"---\") if e.strip()])\n1051: \n1052:     if (\n1053:         file_size_kb < MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB\n1054:         and entry_count <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES\n1055:     ):\n1056:         return None\n1057: \n1058:     parts = content.split(\"---\")\n1059:     entries = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]\n1060: \n1061:     if len(entries) <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:\n1062:         return None\n1063: \n1064:     entries_to_archive = entries[:-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES]\n1065:     entries_to_keep = entries[-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:]\n1066: \n1067:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n1068:     archive_file = ARCHIVE_DIR / f\"notifications_{timestamp}.md\"\n1069: \n1070:     archive_content = \"# Notificaciones Archivadas\\n\\n\"\n1071:     archive_content += f\"**Fecha:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\\n\"\n1072:     archive_content += \"\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_archive)\n1073: \n1074:     write_file(archive_file, archive_content)\n1075: \n1076:     new_content = \"# Registro de Notificaciones\\n\\n---\\n\\n\"\n1077:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_keep)\n1078:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\"\n1079:     write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, new_content)\n1080: \n1081:     return str(archive_file)\n1082: \n1083: \n1084: def run_finalization_checks() -> dict:\n1085:     \"\"\"Checks adicionales para planes de tipo FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n1086:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"summary\": []}\n1087:     checks = {\n1088:         \"README.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"README.md\",\n1089:         \"CHANGELOG.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n1090:         \"closeout_report.md\": COLLAB_DIR / \"closeout_report.md\",\n1091:     }\n1092:     for name, path in checks.items():\n1093:         if path.exists():\n1094:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[OK] {name}: Presente\")\n1095:         else:\n1096:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[WARN] {name}: No encontrado\")\n1097:     return results\n1098: \n1099: \n1100: def run_quality_gates(plan_type: str = \"IMPLEMENTATION\") -> dict:\n1101:     \"\"\"Ejecuta validaciones automaticas.\"\"\"\n1102:     print(\"\\n[QUALITY GATES] Ejecutando Quality Gates...\")\n1103:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"errors\": [], \"summary\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1104: \n1105:     state_errors = validate_state_files()\n1106:     total_state_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in state_errors.values())\n1107:     if total_state_errors > 0:\n1108:         results[\"warnings\"].append(\n1109:             f\"Archivos de estado: {total_state_errors} problemas\"\n1110:         )\n1111:     else:\n1112:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Estado: Archivos validos\")\n1113: \n1114:     src_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"src\"\n1115:     tests_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"tests\"\n1116:     dirs_to_check = [str(d) for d in [src_dir, tests_dir] if d.exists()]\n1117:     if not dirs_to_check:\n1118:         dirs_to_check = [\".\"]\n1119: \n1120:     try:\n1121:         ruff = subprocess.run(\n1122:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"ruff\", \"check\", *dirs_to_check],\n1123:             capture_output=True,\n1124:             text=True,\n1125:             timeout=60,\n1126:         )\n1127:         if ruff.returncode != 0:\n1128:             results[\"passed\"] = False\n1129:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Ruff: Errores de linting\")\n1130:         else:\n1131:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Ruff: Limpio\")\n1132:     except FileNotFoundError:\n1133:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Ruff: No instalado\")\n1134: \n1135:     if tests_dir.exists():\n1136:         try:\n1137:             pytest_result = subprocess.run(\n1138:                 [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pytest\", \"-q\"],\n1139:                 capture_output=True,\n1140:                 timeout=120,\n1141:                 cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n1142:             )\n1143:             if pytest_result.returncode != 0:\n1144:                 results[\"passed\"] = False\n1145:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Pytest: Tests fallando\")\n1146:             else:\n1147:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Pytest: Tests OK\")\n1148:         except FileNotFoundError:\n1149:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Pytest: No instalado\")\n1150: \n1151:     if plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1152:         fin_results = run_finalization_checks()\n1153:         results[\"summary\"].extend(fin_results[\"summary\"])\n1154: \n1155:     status = \"[PASSED]\" if results[\"passed\"] else \"[FAILED]\"\n1156:     print(f\"   {status}\")\n1157:     return results\n1158: \n1159: \n1160: def update_log_status(new_status: str, note: str) -> bool:\n1161:     \"\"\"Actualiza el estado en execution_log.md.\"\"\"\n1162:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1163:     if not content:\n1164:         return False\n1165:     lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n1166:     for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n1167:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n1168:             lines[i] = f\"**Estado:** {new_status}\"\n1169:             new_content = \"\\n\".join(lines) + f\"\\n\\n{note}\"\n1170:             write_file(EXEC_LOG, new_content)\n1171:             return True\n1172:     return False\n1173: \n1174: \n1175: def create_findings_file(plan_id: str = \"N/A\") -> Path:\n1176:     \"\"\"Crea findings.md desde template.\"\"\"\n1177:     findings_path = COLLAB_DIR / \"findings.md\"\n1178: \n1179:     if findings_path.exists():\n1180:         return findings_path\n1181: \n1182:     template_path = AGENT_DIR / \"templates\" / \"findings_template.md\"\n1183: \n1184:     try:\n1185:         if template_path.exists():\n1186:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1187:         else:\n1188:             template = \"\"\"# Hallazgos de Investigacion\n1189: \n1190: **Plan ID:** {{PLAN_ID}}\n1191: **Creado:** {{DATE}}\n1192: \n1193: ---\n1194: \n1195: ## Hallazgos\n1196: \n1197: <!-- Documenta aqui tus hallazgos durante la investigacion -->\n1198: \"\"\"\n1199: \n1200:         content = template.replace(\"{{PLAN_ID}}\", plan_id)\n1201:         content = content.replace(\n1202:             \"{{DATE}}\", datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1203:         )\n1204: \n1205:         write_file(findings_path, content)\n1206:         print(\"  [OK] Creado findings.md\")\n1207: \n1208:     except Exception as e:\n1209:         print(f\"  [WARN] Error creando findings.md: {e}\")\n1210: \n1211:     return findings_path\n1212: \n1213: \n1214: def generate_project_map() -> str:\n1215:     \"\"\"Genera un mapa actualizado del proyecto.\"\"\"\n1216:     output = [\n1217:         \"# Mapa del Proyecto\",\n1218:         f\"**Actualizado:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\",\n1219:         \"\",\n1220:         \"## Estructura de Archivos\",\n1221:         \"```\",\n1222:     ]\n1223: \n1224:     ignore = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\", \".ruff_cache\"}\n1225:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".txt\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n1226: \n1227:     files_found = []\n1228:     for path in sorted(PROJECT_ROOT.rglob(\"*\")):\n1229:         if any(part in ignore for part in path.parts):\n1230:             continue\n1231:         if path.is_file() and path.suffix in extensions:\n1232:             files_found.append(str(path.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)))\n1233: \n1234:     output.extend(files_found[:50])\n1235:     if len(files_found) > 50:\n1236:         output.append(f\"... (+{len(files_found) - 50} archivos mas)\")\n1237: \n1238:     output.append(\"```\")\n1239:     content = \"\\n\".join(output)\n1240:     write_file(PROJECT_MAP, content)\n1241:     return content\n1242: \n1243: \n1244: def _run_pre_action_hooks(\n1245:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, action_type: str\n1246: ) -> None:\n1247:     \"\"\"Run pre-action hooks if available.\"\"\"\n1248:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1249:         hook_registry.execute(\n1250:             \"pre_action\",\n1251:             {\n1252:                 \"action_type\": action_type,\n1253:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1254:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1255:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1256:             },\n1257:         )\n1258: \n1259: \n1260: def _check_quality_gates(\n1261:     plan_id: str, plan_type: str, plan_status: str, skip_gates: bool\n1262: ) -> dict | None:\n1263:     \"\"\"Check quality gates and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1264:     gate_result = run_quality_gates(plan_type=plan_type)\n1265:     if not gate_result[\"passed\"]:\n1266:         update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"AUTO-REJECTED: Quality Gates fallaron\")\n1267:         return {\n1268:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1269:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1270:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1271:             \"instruction\": \"RECHAZADO. Quality Gates fallaron. Corrige errores.\",\n1272:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1273:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1274:             \"log_status\": \"AUTO-REJECTED\",\n1275:             \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1276:         }\n1277:     return None\n1278: \n1279: \n1280: def _check_completion_verification(\n1281:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_type: str, strict_mode: bool\n1282: ) -> dict | None:\n1283:     \"\"\"Check completion verification in strict mode and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1284:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1285:         stop_result = stop_hook(\n1286:             {\n1287:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1288:                 \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1289:                 \"mode\": \"strict\" if strict_mode else \"normal\",\n1290:             }\n1291:         )\n1292:         if strict_mode and not stop_result.get(\"can_complete\", True):\n1293:             return {\n1294:                 \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1295:                 \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1296:                 \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1297:                 \"instruction\": (\n1298:                     f\"Plan {plan_id} no supera Completion Verification. \"\n1299:                     \"Corrige advertencias antes de review.\"\n1300:                 ),\n1301:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1302:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1303:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1304:                 \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1305:             }\n1306:     return None\n1307: \n1308: \n1309: # Transition table for base routing\n1310: TRANSITION_TABLE = {\n1311:     (\"no_plan\", \"any\"): (\n1312:         \"MANAGER\",\n1313:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1314:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1315:     ),\n1316:     (\"completed\", \"any\"): (\n1317:         \"MANAGER\",\n1318:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1319:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1320:     ),\n1321:     (\"in_planning\", \"any\"): (\n1322:         \"MANAGER\",\n1323:         \"FINALIZE_PLAN\",\n1324:         \"Plan {plan_id} en borrador. Finaliza y cambia a APPROVED\",\n1325:     ),\n1326:     (\"approved\", \"ready_for_review\"): (\n1327:         \"MANAGER\",\n1328:         \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n1329:         \"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n1330:     ),\n1331:     (\"approved\", \"any\"): (\n1332:         \"BUILDER\",\n1333:         \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1334:         \"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Implementa segun work_plan.md\",\n1335:     ),\n1336:     (\"in_review\", \"any\"): (\n1337:         \"MANAGER\",\n1338:         \"REVIEW_CHANGES\",\n1339:         \"Plan {plan_id} en revision. Verifica cambios.\",\n1340:     ),\n1341:     (\"any\", \"blocked\"): (\n1342:         \"MANAGER\",\n1343:         \"RESOLVE_BLOCK\",\n1344:         \"Builder BLOQUEADO en {plan_id}. Resuelve en review_queue.md\",\n1345:     ),\n1346: }\n1347: \n1348: \n1349: def _lookup_transition(\n1350:     plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_id: str\n1351: ) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:\n1352:     \"\"\"Lookup transition from table.\"\"\"\n1353:     # Normalize status for lookup\n1354:     plan_key = (\n1355:         \"no_plan\"\n1356:         if not plan_status or plan_status in (\"COMPLETED\", \"N/A\")\n1357:         else plan_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\")\n1358:     )\n1359:     log_key = log_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\") if log_status else \"any\"\n1360: \n1361:     # Check specific matches first\n1362:     for (p_key, l_key), (role, action, template) in TRANSITION_TABLE.items():\n1363:         if (p_key == plan_key or p_key == \"any\") and (\n1364:             l_key == log_key or l_key == \"any\"\n1365:         ):\n1366:             instruction = template.format(plan_id=plan_id)\n1367:             return role, action, instruction\n1368: \n1369:     return None\n1370: \n1371: \n1372: def determine_next_action(skip_gates: bool = False, strict_mode: bool = False) -> dict:\n1373:     \"\"\"Analiza el estado y determina la siguiente accion.\"\"\"\n1374:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1375:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1376: \n1377:     plan_status = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1378:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1379:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1380:     plan_type = get_plan_type(plan_content)\n1381: \n1382:     # Determine pre-action type for hooks\n1383:     pre_action_type = \"CHECK_STATUS\"\n1384:     if not plan_content.strip() or \"COMPLETED\" in plan_status or \"N/A\" in plan_status:\n1385:         pre_action_type = \"CREATE_PLAN\"\n1386:     elif \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1387:         pre_action_type = \"REVIEW_WORK\"\n1388:     elif \"APPROVED\" in plan_status:\n1389:         pre_action_type = \"IMPLEMENT\"\n1390: \n1391:     _run_pre_action_hooks(plan_id, plan_status, log_status, pre_action_type)\n1392: \n1393:     # Check quality gates for review state\n1394:     if (\n1395:         \"APPROVED\" in plan_status\n1396:         and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status\n1397:         and not skip_gates\n1398:     ):\n1399:         gate_action = _check_quality_gates(plan_id, plan_type, plan_status, skip_gates)\n1400:         if gate_action:\n1401:             return {\"plan_type\": plan_type, **gate_action}\n1402: \n1403:     # Check completion verification in strict mode\n1404:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1405:         verification_action = _check_completion_verification(\n1406:             plan_id, plan_status, log_status, plan_type, strict_mode\n1407:         )\n1408:         if verification_action:\n1409:             return {\"plan_type\": plan_type, **verification_action}\n1410: \n1411:     # Handle finalization instruction variation\n1412:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1413:         instruction = f\"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Ejecuta cierre segun work_plan.md\"\n1414:         return {\n1415:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1416:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1417:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1418:             \"instruction\": instruction,\n1419:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1420:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1421:             \"log_status\": log_status,\n1422:             \"action_type\": \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1423:             \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1424:         }\n1425: \n1426:     # Lookup base transition\n1427:     transition = _lookup_transition(plan_status, log_status, plan_id)\n1428:     if transition:\n1429:         role, action_type, instruction = transition\n1430:         context_file = \".manager_rules\" if role == \"MANAGER\" else \".builder_rules\"\n1431:         workflow_file = (\n1432:             \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\"\n1433:             if role == \"MANAGER\"\n1434:             else \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\"\n1435:         )\n1436:         return {\n1437:             \"role\": role,\n1438:             \"context_file\": context_file,\n1439:             \"workflow_file\": workflow_file,\n1440:             \"instruction\": instruction,\n1441:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1442:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1443:             \"log_status\": log_status,\n1444:             \"action_type\": action_type,\n1445:             \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1446:         }\n1447: \n1448:     # Fallback for unknown states\n1449:     return {\n1450:         \"role\": \"UNKNOWN\",\n1451:         \"context_file\": \"N/A\",\n1452:         \"workflow_file\": \"N/A\",\n1453:         \"instruction\": \"Estado indeterminado. Revisa archivos manualmente.\",\n1454:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1455:         \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1456:         \"log_status\": log_status,\n1457:         \"action_type\": \"MANUAL_INTERVENTION\",\n1458:         \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1459:     }\n1460: \n1461: \n1462: def should_overwrite_turn(turn_path: Path, force_reset: bool = False) -> bool:\n1463:     \"\"\"Devuelve True solo si TURN.md debe ser regenerado.\"\"\"\n1464:     if force_reset or not turn_path.exists():\n1465:         return True\n1466:     try:\n1467:         content = turn_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1468:         return bool(\"UNKNOWN\" in content or \"MANUAL_INTERVENTION\" in content)\n1469:     except Exception:\n1470:         return True\n1471: \n1472: \n1473: def update_turn_file(action: dict) -> None:\n1474:     \"\"\"Actualiza TURN.md con informacion del turno actual.\"\"\"\n1475:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1476: \n1477:     content = f\"\"\"# TURNO ACTUAL\n1478: \n1479: **Ultima actualizacion:** {timestamp}\n1480: \n1481: ---\n1482: \n1483: ## Agente Activo\n1484: \n1485: | Campo | Valor |\n1486: |-------|-------|\n1487: | **ROL** | **{action[\"role\"]}** |\n1488: | **Plan ID** | {action[\"plan_id\"]} |\n1489: | **Tipo** | {action.get(\"plan_type\", \"IMPLEMENTATION\")} |\n1490: | **Accion** | {action[\"action_type\"]} |\n1491: \n1492: ---\n1493: \n1494: ## Instruccion\n1495: \n1496: > {action[\"instruction\"]}\n1497: \n1498: ---\n1499: \n1500: ## Archivos a Leer\n1501: \n1502: 1. `{action[\"context_file\"]}` (Contexto del rol)\n1503: 2. `{action[\"workflow_file\"]}` (Flujo de trabajo)\n1504: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n1505: \n1506: ---\n1507: \n1508: ## Estado del Sistema\n1509: \n1510: | Archivo | Estado |\n1511: |---------|--------|\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1511. Use offset=1512 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n\"\"\"\nAgent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n==========================================\nOrquestador con:\n- Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n- Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n- Completion Verification antes de review\n- Quality Gates extendidos\n- Session Recovery\n- Project Map y estado del workflow\n\nUso:\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agent_controller.py","time":{"start":1779809127625,"end":1779809127701}},"id":"prt_e64e40174001ztXFh67WvFdKey","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e39fbe001A0InTqz0XRuyzV"}}
