motor keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`.\n10: \n11: ## Current readiness\n12: \n13: - The repository remains ready for terminal-driven execution.\n14: \n15: ## Source of truth\n16: \n17: > See `[AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)` for the canonical runtime paths and operational contract.\n18: \n19: - Destination projects declare `Ticket prefix: XXX` in their local `PROJECT.md` (or via `--install --prefix XXX`) and use `XXX-YYYY-NNN`; this motor keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`.\n20: \n21: - `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`\n22: - `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`\n23: - `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`\n24: - `.agent/runtime/memory/`\n25: - `.agent/council/`\n26: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n27: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`\n\n(End of file - total 27 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Project: orquestador_de_agentes\n**Version:** v9.14.0\n**State:** WP-2026-144 COMPLETED (destination ticket prefix onboarding)\n\n## Current Cycle\n\n- Active ticket: WP-2026-144 COMPLETED (2026-05-26).\n- Mode: completed - Builder implemented destination namespace support.\n- Outcome: local destination repos now use `XXX-YYYY-NNN` with `Ticket prefix: XXX` written by installer; motor keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`.\n\n## Current readiness\n\n- The repository remains ready for terminal-driven execution.\n\n## Source of truth\n\n> See `[AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)` for the canonical runtime paths and operational contract.\n\n- Destination projects declare `Ticket prefix: XXX` in their local `PROJECT.md` (or via `--install --prefix XXX`) and use `XXX-YYYY-NNN`; this motor keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`.\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"PROJECT.md","time":{"start":1779809157536,"end":1779809157562}},"id":"prt_e64e47e26001j8EtHkCTbCFt79","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e40ef3001haYeynGMcYtmQJ"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779809157570,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_05df9f06f9b4477abea33631","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\README.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\README.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # orquestador_de_agentes\n2: \n3: Central motor for multi-agent orchestration. Operational code lives once here; destination projects keep only their `.agent/` workspace (state, memory, events, config) and reference the motor externally.\n4: \n5: ## What this is\n6: \n7: A **domain-agnostic central motor** that automates work by making one agent generate plans, another implement them, and a third review the result. The motor lives once in this repo; destination projects prepare a workspace (`.agent/`) and reference the motor externally. No copying of operational code.\n8: \n9: ### Three-agent loop\n10: \n11: | Role | Responsibility | Default backend |\n12: |---|---|---|\n13: | **Supervisor** | Generates plans, derives tickets, coordinates the cycle | Claude Code |\n14: | **Builder** | Implements tickets, runs gates, emits `BUILDER_EXIT` | OpenCode → Qwen 3.5 (`opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus`) |\n15: | **Manager** | Reviews Builder output with a deliverable-type-aware rubric; emits `APPROVE` / `CHANGES` | OpenCode → OpenAI (`openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, routed natively since WP-072) |\n16: \n17: Backends are pluggable per role in `.agent/config/agents.json` (`role_assignments` + `role_models`).\n18: \n19: ### Current reference setup\n20: \n21: The author's working configuration on Windows:\n22: \n23: - **Supervisor**: Claude Code (chat-driven planning + terminal control)\n24: - **Manager**: OpenCode routed to OpenAI Codex / GPT (`openai/gpt-5.4-mini`)\n25: - **Builder**: OpenCode routed to Qwen 3.5 Plus via the [opencode.ai/go](https://opencode.ai/go) plan\n26: - **Approximate cost**: ~50 EUR/month (Anthropic + opencode-go bundle)\n27: \n28: This is a **reference** setup, not a constraint. Swap any role for another backend by editing `agents.json`. The bus, state machine and review bridge are backend-agnostic.\n29: \n30: ## Current state\n31: \n32: - **Version**: `v9.14.0`\n33: - **Status**: Stable. Review cycle aligned with Google eng-practices standards (WP-2026-141).\n34: - **Last work**: `WP-2026-141` — Google eng-practices review standards alignment.\n35: - **Tests**: 255 passing. Validation 0 errors. Ruff clean. pip-audit clean.\n36: \n37: ### What changed since v9.9.0\n38: \n39: | WP | Topic |\n40: |----|-------|\n41: | WP-2026-086 | Regex-based redaction module (`bus/redact.py`) for secrets/PII scrubbing on the event bus |\n42: | WP-2026-087 | Smoke test bus agnóstico + 5-gap analysis (proved bus is domain-neutral) |\n43: | WP-2026-088 | `deliverable_type` field in work_plan schema (V1 informational) |\n44: | WP-2026-089 | Pluggable quality-gates dispatch by deliverable_type (`scripts/run_gates_dispatch.py`) |\n45: | WP-2026-090 | **Host-first skill precedence + config profiles** (`active_profile: engine-dev/host-project`) |\n46: | WP-2026-091 | Pluggable manager review rubric by deliverable_type (code / mixed / docs / research / analysis) |\n47: | WP-2026-092 | Conditional pip-audit by dependency surface (only runs when manifest files in scope) |\n48: | WP-2026-093 | Pre-commit ruff scope guard (Python-only enforcement) |\n49: | WP-2026-094 | **Host setup hook** for post-install bootstrap (`.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}`) |\n50: | WP-2026-095 | **Manager review V2**: single-shot prompt with budget, retry, forensic events |\n51: | WP-2026-111 | **Central motor + destination workspace**: motor lives once, destination references externally |\n52: | WP-2026-113 | **Central motor release consolidation**: manifiestos, documentacion e instalador alineados |\n53: | WP-2026-123 | **Workspace minimo del destino**: enlace motor-destino con schema explicito |\n54: | WP-2026-135 | **Pre-compact hook**: recuperacion selectiva de contexto antes de compactacion |\n55: | WP-2026-136 | **session_close_observations `--candidates`**: canal de inyeccion semantica para el bucle de autoaprendizaje del Manager |\n56: | WP-2026-139 | **Anti-patterns index file**: inventario canonico AP-01..AP-08 con cache y separacion indice/instrucciones |\n57: | WP-2026-140 | **Bus import boundary firewall**: test AST+grep que protege el seam `bus/→scripts/` (`scripts.discover_skills` unica excepcion); 255 tests, ruff limpio |\n58: | WP-2026-141 | **Review standards (eng-practices)**: criterio de aprobacion Google, convencion `Nit`, trazabilidad en AGENTS.md y CREDITS.md |\n59: \n60: ## Central motor architecture (WP-2026-111)\n61: \n62: The motor lives **once** in this repo. A destination project:\n63: \n64: 1. **Prepares a workspace**: `.agent/` with state, memory, events, config.\n65: 2. **References the motor externally**: no copying of operational code.\n66: 3. **Can override skills**: put its own skills in `<destination>/.agent/skills/`. The motor catalog acts as fallback. Host-first precedence in `scripts/discover_skills.py`.\n67: 4. **Can bootstrap itself**: drop `.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}` in the destination. `scripts/install_agent_system.py` detects and invokes it with interactive confirmation post-copy.\n68: 5. **Chooses deliverable type per ticket**: `deliverable_type: code | documentation | research | analysis | mixed` in `work_plan.md` switches the gate dispatch and Manager review rubric.\n69: 6. **Flips profile automatically**: `agents.json.active_profile` goes from `engine-dev` (motor repo) to `host-project` (destination) during install/sync.\n70: 7. **Receives motor-destination link file**: `.agent/config/motor_destination_link.json` with schema (motor_root, destination_root, motor_version, destination_id, created_at, manifest_version) for portable traceability (WP-2026-123).\n71: 8. **Uses a local ticket namespace in the destination**: the destination `PROJECT.md` declares `Ticket prefix: XXX`, and tickets in that repo use `XXX-YYYY-NNN`. The motor repo keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`. The installer can write this prefix with `--install --prefix XXX` or `--sync --prefix XXX`.\n72: \n73: ## Version contract\n74: \n75: - `pyproject.toml` → portable package version.\n76: - `.agent/.version_manifest.json` → installed core/system version.\n77: - Canonical upgrade scripts: `scripts/detect_version.py`, `scripts/upgrade.py`, `scripts/rollback.py`.\n78: \n79: ## Main layers\n80: \n81: - `.agent/collaboration/` — canonical operational state (`TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, `work_plan.md`, `execution_log.md`)\n82: - `.agent/runtime/events/events.jsonl` — append-only event bus (authoritative)\n83: - `.agent/runtime/memory/` — persistent project memory (`observations.jsonl` + `MEMORY.md`)\n84: - `.agent/runtime/audit/` — local audit snapshots\n85: - `.agent/runtime/compare/` — repo-compare reports (gitignored)\n86: - `.agent/config/agents.json` — backends + roles + `manager_review` tuning\n87: - `.agent/hooks/` — safety and validation hooks\n88: - `bus/` — event bus, supervisor, state machine, review bridge, redaction\n89: - `skills/` — bundled default skills (catalog of 19); destination can override via `.agent/skills/`\n90: - `scripts/` — install, upgrade, gates dispatch, skill discovery, audits, maintenance\n91: - `MANIFEST.distribute` — frontera del motor central (codigo operativo)\n92: - `MANIFEST.workspace` — contrato del workspace destino (estado, memoria, eventos, config)\n93: \n94: ## Typical flow — engine development\n95: \n96: 1. Edit `work_plan.md` with the next WP.\n97: 2. `python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket --force` emits initial bus event.\n98: 3. Builder implements, then `python .agent/agent_controller.py --mark-ready --json --force` emits `BUILDER_EXIT` + `STATE_CHANGED → READY_FOR_REVIEW`.\n99: 4. Manager review bridge dispatches; on `DECISION: APPROVE` the canonical close cascade fires.\n100: 5. If the bridge times out / inspects: `python .agent/agent_controller.py --manager-approve --ticket WP-XXXX --force` closes manually.\n101: \n102: ## Typical flow — installing in a new project\n103: \n104: ```bash\n105: # From destination project root:\n106: python /path/to/orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --install\n107: ```\n108: \n109: The installer:\n110: 1. Prepares the destination workspace (`.agent/` structure with state, memory, config).\n111: 2. Flips `active_profile` to `host-project` in `agents.json`.\n112: 3. Detects `.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}` if present and (with `y/N` confirmation, or `--yes` for CI) executes it.\n113: 4. Reports the source (host vs motor) of each discovered skill.\n114: 5. Does NOT copy motor code (scripts/, skills/, bus/, agent_system/, core .agent/).\n115: 6. Writes `.agent/config/motor_destination_link.json` with schema explicit for portable traceability (WP-2026-123).\n116: \n117: Before the first ticket in the destination, set the namespace in that repo's `PROJECT.md`:\n118: \n119: ```text\n120: Ticket prefix: XXX\n121: ```\n122: \n123: Or let the installer write it for you:\n124: \n125: ```bash\n126: python /path/to/orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --install --prefix XXX\n127: ```\n128: \n129: Use that prefix for all local ticket IDs and work-plan documents in the destination. Do not reuse the motor's `WP-YYYY-NNN` namespace there.\n130: \n131: ## Common commands\n132: \n133: ```powershell\n134: # Validation & gates\n135: python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force\n136: python scripts/run_gates_dispatch.py        # dispatches by deliverable_type\n137: python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py\n138: ruff check .\n139: uv run pip-audit .\n140: \n141: # Skill discovery\n142: python scripts/discover_skills.py --json\n143: python scripts/check_skill_collisions.py\n144: \n145: # Memory & audit\n146: python scripts/memory_consolidate.py --apply\n147: python scripts/local_audit.py\n148: \n149: # Config migration (idempotent)\n150: python .agent/agents_config.py --migrate [--dry-run]\n151: \n152: # Compare with another GitHub repo\n153: # via repo-compare skill (uses MCP GitHub tools)\n154: ```\n155: \n156: ## Documentation map\n157: \n158: - `AGENTS.md` — transversal rules + dual-mode architecture notes\n159: - `CLAUDE.md` — Claude Code entry guide\n160: - `PROJECT.md` — current project state and contract\n161: - `QUICKSTART.md` — minimal setup checklist and daily commands\n162: - `INTERACTION_MODES.md` — chat-driven vs terminal-driven flow\n163: - `REPOSITORY_STRUCTURE.md` — internal layout and public boundary\n164: - `CHANGELOG.md` — change history (WP-by-WP)\n165: - `CREDITS.md` — external attributions (every WP with `Origen externo` has a row)\n166: - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` — canonical onboarding prompt for fresh agent sessions\n167: \n168: ## License\n169: \n170: MIT (core). See `LICENSE`.\n\n(End of file - total 170 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# orquestador_de_agentes\n\nCentral motor for multi-agent orchestration. Operational code lives once here; destination projects keep only their `.agent/` workspace (state, memory, events, config) and reference the motor externally.\n\n## What this is\n\nA **domain-agnostic central motor** that automates work by making one agent generate plans, another implement them, and a third review the result. The motor lives once in this repo; destination projects prepare a workspace (`.agent/`) and reference the motor externally. No copying of operational code.\n\n### Three-agent loop\n\n| Role | Responsibility | Default backend |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Supervisor** | Generates plans, derives tickets, coordinates the cycle | Claude Code |\n| **Builder** | Implements tickets, runs gates, emits `BUILDER_EXIT` | OpenCode → Qwen 3.5 (`opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus`) |\n| **Manager** | Reviews Builder output with a deliverable-type-aware rubric; emits `APPROVE` / `CHANGES` | OpenCode → OpenAI (`openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, routed natively since WP-072) |\n\nBackends are pluggable per role in `.agent/config/agents.json` (`role_assignments` + `role_models`).\n\n### Current reference setup\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"README.md","time":{"start":1779809157527,"end":1779809157567}},"id":"prt_e64e47ac700160495hYqanxxtH","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e40ef3001haYeynGMcYtmQJ"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779809157589,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_97f292ce64dd4961b1c45f39","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\prompts\\session_bootstrap.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\prompts\\session_bootstrap.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Session Bootstrap Prompt\n2: \n3: Pega este bloque tal cual al iniciar una nueva conversacion con un agente nuevo (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode o cualquier backend) que vaya a operar sobre `orquestador_de_agentes`. Esta optimizado para orientar al agente sin gastar la ventana de contexto inicial cargando documentacion completa: apunta a archivos canonicos en lugar de embeber contenido.\n4: \n5: ---\n6: \n7: ## Prompt (copia y pega)\n8: \n9: ```\n10: Eres el agente principal del sistema multi-agente del repositorio orquestador_de_agentes.\n11: \n12: ## Lectura obligatoria antes de actuar\n13: \n14: Lee en este orden, sin omitir ninguno:\n15: \n16: 0. `.agent/runtime/audit/AUDIT.md` si existe (snapshot estructurado del cierre anterior, ~40 lineas con version, plan activo, git posture, skills, WPs recientes, health). Si esta presente y fresco (mtime < 24h), puede sustituir los puntos 2-4 siguientes en sesiones rapidas. Si falta o esta stale, regenera con `python scripts/local_audit.py`.\n17: 1. `CLAUDE.md` y `AGENTS.md` (instrucciones transversales).\n18: 2. `QUICKSTART.md` (como arrancar el flujo terminal-driven).\n19: 3. `PROJECT.md` y `CHANGELOG.md` (estado del proyecto, decisiones).\n20: 4. `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, `work_plan.md`, `execution_log.md` (estado canonico).\n21: 5. `.agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md` y `.agent/runtime/memory/observations.jsonl` (memoria persistente del proyecto).\n22: \n23: **Lectura bajo demanda (no en cada arranque):** si necesitas ubicar un subsistema o entender el arbol de carpetas, lee `REPOSITORY_STRUCTURE.md`. No lo cargues por defecto: solo cuando la tarea lo requiera.\n24: \n25: ## Resumen breve del sistema\n26: \n27: - **Runtime activo:** `orquestador_de_agentes/` (repo portable).\n28: - **Roles:** Manager (OpenCode via `scripts/manager_review_bridge.py`, modelo configurable en `.agent/config/agents.json`) y Builder (OpenCode, modelo `opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus`).\n29: - **Bus canonico:** `.agent/runtime/events/events.jsonl` (append-only, autoridad absoluta).\n30: - **Proyecciones:** `TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, `work_plan.md`, `execution_log.md` se derivan del bus.\n31: - **Namespaces de tickets:** motor `WP-YYYY-NNN`; destino `XXX-YYYY-NNN` con `Ticket prefix: XXX` declarado en el `PROJECT.md` local del destino.\n32: - **Launcher:** `scripts/launch_agent_terminals.ps1` abre Supervisor + Bridge + Builder segun `TURN.md`. WP-2026-067 integro OpenCode con prompt compuesto desde ticket.\n33: - **Config de agentes:** `.agent/config/agents.json` mapea backend->ejecutable. Builder=opencode, Manager=opencode, Supervisor=default.\n34: - **Validate:** `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force` debe pasar antes de cualquier cierre. Verifica entre otras cosas que destinos `host-project` tengan `Ticket prefix:` declarado.\n35: - **Quality gates:** `ruff check .`, `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`, `pip-audit`.\n36: \n37: ## Ciclo canonico de un ticket\n38: \n39: > Flujo completo y arquitectura: ver [PROJECT.md sección \"Current architecture\"](../PROJECT.md#current-architecture).\n40: \n41: 1. Manager crea `work_plan.md` (DRAFT) y opcionalmente `PLAN_WP-XXXX.md` (estrategia tecnica) + `AUDIT_WP-XXXX.md` (criterios de auditoria). User aprueba editando work_plan a APPROVED.\n42:    - En un proyecto destino, el ID debe usar el namespace local definido en `PROJECT.md` (`XXX-YYYY-NNN`), no el del motor. El instalador puede escribir este prefijo con `--install --prefix XXX`.\n43: 2. Builder implementa y al final ejecuta `python .agent/agent_controller.py --mark-ready --json --force` que emite `BUILDER_EXIT` y luego `STATE_CHANGED -> READY_FOR_REVIEW` al bus (orden fijado por WP-074).\n44: 3. Bridge dispara OpenCode review automaticamente. Si aprueba -> cascada hasta COMPLETED.\n45: 4. Markdowns se sincronizan a COMPLETED. Commit + push.\n46: \n47: **Manager devuelve `inspect` / CHANGES fantasma:** la causa raiz se corrigio en WP-2026-120 (el parser JSON del bridge leia un schema inexistente). Ya NO es comportamiento esperado: si reaparece un `changes` con `attempt-N.md` de BLOCKERS vacios, es una regresion del parser en `bus/review_bridge.py` — investigarla, no normalizarla. Cierre manual canonico si hace falta: `python .agent/agent_controller.py --manager-approve --ticket WP-XXXX --force`.\n48: \n49: ## Reglas no negociables\n50: \n51: - **Verifica antes de actuar.** No confies en reportes de Builder o agentes externos: `git status`, `tail events.jsonl`, `--validate`. El patron de fabricacion esta documentado en [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).\n52: - **No mezcles chat y terminal** sin sincronizar TURN/STATE/execution_log.\n53: - **`.codex/` y `*.log` estan gitignorados** (rollouts con prompts sensibles). No los toques.\n54: - **OAuth race Codex:** Resuelto por WP-072 mediante el cambio al backend OpenCode. La dependencia de Codex como backend del Manager ha sido eliminada por defecto.\n55: - **Manager-approve CLI:** Se realiza mediante `python .agent/agent_controller.py --manager-approve --ticket WP-XXXX --force` (canonical closeout sin scripts ad-hoc).\n56: - **No abras WP nuevos sin instruccion explicita del usuario.**\n57: \n58: ## Comportamiento esperado\n59: \n60: - Responde **breve**, optimizando tokens. Sin emojis salvo que el usuario los use.\n61: - Antes de cambios destructivos (git push, edits a `.agent/`, ejecucion de cascade), confirma con el usuario.\n62: - Si el usuario pide algo que el codigo ya hace, **revisa el codigo primero** antes de proponer nada nuevo.\n63: - Si vas a tocar la rama Codex/Kilo del launcher: para. Eso es scope-creep y no entra sin un WP nuevo.\n64: \n65: Cuando termines la lectura, di \"Sistema internalizado\" y enumera en 5 lineas maximo: ultimo ticket cerrado, archivos clave que leiste, drift detectado (si hay), siguiente accion recomendada.\n66: ```\n67: \n68: ---\n69: \n70: ## Cuando usarlo\n71: \n72: - Primera interaccion con un agente nuevo en una sesion limpia.\n73: - Al recuperarse de una conversacion comprimida donde el agente perdio contexto.\n74: - Al cambiar de backend (de Claude Code a Codex, de Kilo a OpenCode, etc.) y necesitar que el nuevo backend asuma rapido.\n75: \n76: ## Cuando NO usarlo\n77: \n78: - A mitad de un ticket en curso (rompe el flujo establecido).\n79: - Si ya hay un `work_plan.md` activo IN_PROGRESS — el agente debe leer primero ese, no este bootstrap.\n80: - En llamadas one-shot a OpenCode/Codex desde el launcher — ahi sirve el prompt compuesto que ya genera `Get-OpenCodeBuilderPrompt`.\n81: \n82: ## Mantenimiento\n83: \n84: Actualiza este archivo cuando:\n85: - Cambia el modelo por defecto del Builder o Manager.\n86: - Aparece una nueva regla operativa relevante (memoria, ticket cerrado con leccion).\n87: - Se anade o quita un archivo canonico al flujo.\n88: \n89: No lo conviertas en sustituto de `PROJECT.md` o `CHANGELOG.md`: este bootstrap apunta, no documenta.\n\n(End of file - total 89 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Session Bootstrap Prompt\n\nPega este bloque tal cual al iniciar una nueva conversacion con un agente nuevo (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode o cualquier backend) que vaya a operar sobre `orquestador_de_agentes`. Esta optimizado para orientar al agente sin gastar la ventana de contexto inicial cargando documentacion completa: apunta a archivos canonicos en lugar de embeber contenido.\n\n---\n\n## Prompt (copia y pega)\n\n```\nEres el agente principal del sistema multi-agente del repositorio orquestador_de_agentes.\n\n## Lectura obligatoria antes de actuar\n\nLee en este orden, sin omitir ninguno:\n\n0. `.agent/runtime/audit/AUDIT.md` si existe (snapshot estructurado del cierre anterior, ~40 lineas con version, plan activo, git posture, skills, WPs recientes, health). Si esta presente y fresco (mtime < 24h), puede sustituir los puntos 2-4 siguientes en sesiones rapidas. Si falta o esta stale, regenera con `python scripts/local_audit.py`.\n1. `CLAUDE.md` y `AGENTS.md` (instrucciones transversales).\n2. `QUICKSTART.md` (como arrancar el flujo terminal-driven).\n3. `PROJECT.md` y `CHANGELOG.md` (estado del proyecto, decisiones).\n4. `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, `work_plan.md`, `execution_log.md` (estado canonico).","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"prompts\\session_bootstrap.md","time":{"start":1779809157518,"end":1779809157585}},"id":"prt_e64e4776e001NfjDM7yfoDvToe","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e40ef3001haYeynGMcYtmQJ"}}

{"type":"step_start","timestamp":1779809181236,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"id":"prt_e64e4de31001oVp5MoLx7yG6aN","messageID":"msg_e64e4839a001dAipoG3gMalxYO","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","snapshot":"3ccef2dda39dcba317123b365015235ea184560a","type":"step-start"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779809194041,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_3ff4061ebe944ba7ac577dfd","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Release Checklist — Publicación limpia de `orquestador_de_agentes`\n2: \n3: Checklist manual para publicar el motor en un git nuevo y limpio. **No es flujo\n4: operativo diario ni un WP** — se ejecuta una sola vez, cuando el motor esté\n5: consolidado y listo para su repo definitivo.\n6: \n7: ## Contexto\n8: \n9: El repo privado actual (`FDL32/orquestacion-agentes`) tiene el historial\n10: contaminado: `.codex/auth.json` con tokens OAuth reales de OpenAI quedó en\n11: commits antiguos (gitignorado después, pero presente en la historia, ~249\n12: commits). Estrategia acordada: NO reescribir ese historial — empezar un git\n13: nuevo desde cero. El árbol de trabajo en HEAD está limpio; solo el historial\n14: está contaminado, y un git nuevo no lo arrastra.\n15: \n16: ## Checklist\n17: \n18: ### 0. Definir namespace del destino\n19: - [ ] En el `PROJECT.md` del proyecto destino, declarar `Ticket prefix: XXX`.\n20: - [ ] Opcional: usar el instalador con `--install --prefix XXX` para escribirlo automaticamente.\n21: - [ ] Confirmar que el destino usara `XXX-YYYY-NNN` para sus tickets y no el namespace `WP-YYYY-NNN` del motor.\n22: \n23: ### 1. Git nuevo desde cero\n24: - [ ] Crear el repo nuevo con `git init` sobre el árbol de trabajo limpio.\n25: - [ ] **NUNCA** `git clone` del repo viejo ni copiar la carpeta `.git/` — eso\n26:       arrastraría el historial con los tokens. Primer commit = historia nueva.\n27: \n28: ### 2. Verificar `.gitignore` antes del primer commit\n29: - [ ] `.gitignore` excluye `.codex/`, `*.log`, `.venv/`, caches y runtime\n30:       (`.agent/runtime/...`, `__pycache__/`, etc.).\n31: - [ ] `git status` antes del primer commit no muestra ningún archivo sensible.\n32: \n33: ### 3. gitleaks sobre el árbol\n34: - [ ] Ejecutar `gitleaks` sobre el árbol de trabajo. Confirmar 0 hallazgos\n35:       antes de publicar.\n36: \n37: ### 4. Revocar sesiones OpenAI\n38: - [ ] En `chatgpt.com` → ajustes → cerrar todas las sesiones. Invalida el\n39:       `refresh_token` que quedó en el `auth.json` del repo viejo.\n40: - [ ] Hacerlo cuanto antes, sin esperar al resto del checklist.\n41: \n42: ### 5. Archivar el repo viejo\n43: - [ ] Cuando el git nuevo esté publicado y verificado, borrar o archivar\n44:       `FDL32/orquestacion-agentes` — no dejar vivo un repo con los secretos.\n45: \n46: ## Orden\n47: \n48: - Paso 4 (revocar sesiones OpenAI): hacer YA, no esperar.\n49: - Pasos 1-3 y 5: cuando el motor esté consolidado y listo para su repo\n50:   definitivo.\n\n(End of file - total 50 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Release Checklist — Publicación limpia de `orquestador_de_agentes`\n\nChecklist manual para publicar el motor en un git nuevo y limpio. **No es flujo\noperativo diario ni un WP** — se ejecuta una sola vez, cuando el motor esté\nconsolidado y listo para su repo definitivo.\n\n## Contexto\n\nEl repo privado actual (`FDL32/orquestacion-agentes`) tiene el historial\ncontaminado: `.codex/auth.json` con tokens OAuth reales de OpenAI quedó en\ncommits antiguos (gitignorado después, pero presente en la historia, ~249\ncommits). Estrategia acordada: NO reescribir ese historial — empezar un git\nnuevo desde cero. El árbol de trabajo en HEAD está limpio; solo el historial\nestá contaminado, y un git nuevo no lo arrastra.\n\n## Checklist\n\n### 0. Definir namespace del destino\n- [ ] En el `PROJECT.md` del proyecto destino, declarar `Ticket prefix: XXX`.\n- [ ] Opcional: usar el instalador con `--install --prefix XXX` para escribirlo automaticamente.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md","time":{"start":1779809194010,"end":1779809194038}},"id":"prt_e64e509490016TJZ4fxskrrF7k","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e4839a001dAipoG3gMalxYO"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779809194055,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_e35b77f936c8402e883daf23","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\QUICKSTART.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\QUICKSTART.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Quickstart\n2: \n3: Use this file to start the template in a fresh project, relaunch the terminal-driven workflow, or begin the next planning cycle after canonical closure.\n4: \n5: > **Onboarding de agente nuevo:** si arrancas una sesion limpia con un agente que no conoce el repo, pega primero el bootstrap canonico en `prompts/session_bootstrap.md`. Eso lo orienta sobre roles, archivos canonicos y reglas sin gastar contexto cargando docs completas.\n6: \n7: Namespace de tickets:\n8: - Motor: `WP-YYYY-NNN`\n9: - Destino: `XXX-YYYY-NNN` con `Ticket prefix: XXX` en el `PROJECT.md` local (el instalador lo escribe con `--install --prefix XXX`)\n10: \n11: ## 0. Reproducible launcher\n12: \n13: The recommended way to start the three canonical terminals is the repo-local launcher:\n14: \n15: ```powershell\n16: .\\scripts\\launch_agent_terminals.ps1\n17: ```\n18: \n19: The launcher performs a comprehensive startup hygiene check before opening any window:\n20: - Compares `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`, `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md` and `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md` to ensure alignment (strict by default, abort on drift)\n21: - Closes stale project sessions before preflight so old supervisor or bridge windows cannot reattach to the previous ticket\n22: - Clears a stale `manager_bridge_state.json` cursor when it points to an older ticket\n23: - Clears a stale `supervisor_state.json` cursor when it points to a different ticket than `work_plan.md`\n24: - Launches Builder only when `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md` says `BUILDER` or when manual override is specified via `-BuilderPrompt`\n25: - Prevents duplicate Builder sessions scoped to the current project using a lock file\n26: - Closes stale project sessions from previous tickets before opening the next cycle\n27: - Reads the active ticket from `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md` and applies the prompt automatically\n28: - Resolves the current PowerShell host, Codex CLI and OpenCode executable dynamically instead of pinning one extension build\n29: - Emits short reports per launched window for clarity\n30: - **OpenCode integration (historical: WP-2026-067)**: When the Builder backend is OpenCode, the launcher invokes `opencode run \"<msg>\" --agent builder --model <model> --dir <root> -f <canonicals>` with a composed prompt from the active ticket, model read from `.opencode/opencode.json`, and canonical files attached. No manual paste required.\n31: \n32: The launcher opens windows independently for each agent:\n33: - **Supervisor window**: Runs `python scripts\\ticket_supervisor.py --reactive`\n34: - **Review Bridge window**: Runs `python scripts\\manager_review_bridge.py --watch --backend-path <path>`\n35: - **Builder window**: Opens OpenCode with the active ticket prompt and the Builder contract in `.opencode/agents/builder.md` (only when appropriate)\n36: \n37: Use `-StrictLaunch:$false` to skip strict validation. The launcher still cleans stale or corrupt bridge state in both modes, but strict mode additionally aborts on alignment drift before opening windows.\n38: In flexible mode, the preflight output also reports whether `manager_bridge_state.json` and `supervisor_state.json` were repaired.\n39: \n40: The launcher is intelligent:\n41: - Always launches Supervisor and Review Bridge (if enabled).\n42: - Launches Builder when the active role in `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md` is `BUILDER` or when `-BuilderPrompt` is provided for manual override.\n43: - Prevents duplicate Builder sessions scoped to the current project using a lock file.\n44: - Closes stale project sessions from previous tickets before opening the next cycle.\n45: - Reconciles stale supervisor runtime state so the next ticket does not inherit a previous `active_ticket`.\n46: - Schedules cleanup for the manager prompt temp file after the Review Bridge starts.\n47: - Cleans up old bridge state to avoid carrying over tickets between sessions.\n48: - **OpenCode backend (historical: WP-2026-067)**: Composes the prompt from the active ticket, reads the model from config, and attaches canonical files via `-f`.\n49: \n50: Optional manual Builder auto-run (for testing or override):\n51: \n52: ```powershell\n53: .\\scripts\\launch_agent_terminals.ps1 -BuilderPrompt \"Actua como BUILDER para [NEXT_TICKET]. Lee .agent/collaboration/TURN.md, .agent/collaboration/work_plan.md, .agent/collaboration/execution_log.md, .agent/collaboration/STATE.md y PROJECT.md. Implementa solo [NEXT_TICKET] siguiendo .agent/collaboration/work_plan.md. No cambies el alcance. No reescribas el plan. Registra evidencia clara en .agent/collaboration/execution_log.md. Mantente en el runtime bus-first y evita editar .agent/collaboration/TURN.md, .agent/collaboration/STATE.md o .agent/collaboration/execution_log.md a mano. Ejecuta ruff y pytest-safe sobre lo tocado. Ejecuta python .agent\\agent_controller.py --mark-ready --json --force al final.\"\n54: ```\n55: \n56: **Note:** When the Builder backend is OpenCode (default for this repo), the launcher automatically composes the prompt and invokes `opencode run` with the model from `.opencode/opencode.json` and canonical files attached. The manual paste step from WP-2026-066 and earlier is no longer required.\n57: \n58: OpenCode model selection lives in the repo-local config in:\n59: \n60: ```text\n61: .opencode/opencode.json\n62: ```\n63: \n64: The `model` field in that file is the place to switch the AI used by OpenCode. The current target label is `Qwen3.5 Plus`, but the exact `provider/model` mapping remains [NO VERIFICADO].\n65: \n66: ## 0b. Role-to-backend mapping\n67: \n68: Builder and Manager are roles; the backend can vary.\n69: \n70: | Backend | Builder use | Manager use | Where to configure |\n71: |---------|-------------|-------------|--------------------|\n72: | OpenCode | Default Builder sessions for this repo | N/A for review flow | `.opencode/opencode.json` and `.opencode/agents/builder.md` |\n73: | Claude Code | Chat-driven builder sessions or terminal sessions | Review bridge / orchestration | Claude Code app/session settings |\n74: | Codex | File-driven builder or manager runs | Review bridge and terminal orchestration | `~/.codex/config.toml` |\n75: | Cline | VS Code agent manager for Builder or Manager tasks | Manual review or bridge-like workflows | `~/.cline/data/settings/cline_mcp_settings.json` |\n76: | Kilo | Alternate Builder or Manager backend when explicitly selected | Alternate Builder or Manager backend when explicitly selected | `~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc` |\n77: \n78: Rule:\n79: - If you switch backend during a ticket, update `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`, `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`, `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`, and `.agent/collaboration/notifications.md`.\n80: - The role stays the same even if the backend changes.\n81: \n82: ## 0c. Startup Templates\n83: \n84: `WP-2026-040` introduced startup templates by role/backend to eliminate manual prompt copying. `WP-2026-041` added automated review loop reusing templates with previous feedback. `WP-2026-042` achieved canonical closure with full archival and documentation synchronization. `WP-2026-043` hardened the closeout boundary so completion required explicit Manager approval before returning to `COMPLETED`, and the repository is now back in idle clean state. `WP-2026-066` aligns integration tests and public documentation with the recovered baseline.\n85: \n86: The current cycle is `WP-2026-075` completed (Event Bus Observability). DeepEval / LLM evals are kept separate from the deterministic supervisor flow. `WP-2026-053` remains closed as session closeout robustness.\n87: \n88: If you need a clean restart after an interrupted session, use the supervisor directly:\n89: \n90: ```powershell\n91: Set-Location <repo_root>\n92: \n93: python scripts\\ticket_supervisor.py --once\n94: ```\n95: \n96: Regla operativa:\n97: - Builder, Manager y Supervisor comparten la misma estructura de arranque.\n98: - El launcher inyecta `ticket_id`, `work_plan`, `close_command`, `role` y `backend`.\n99: - Si cambias backend durante un ticket, actualiza `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`, `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`, `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md` y `.agent/collaboration/notifications.md`.\n100: \n101: ## 1. Preflight\n102: \n103: ```powershell\n104: python .agent\\agent_controller.py --validate --json --force\n105: ```\n106: \n107: If validation is clean, the template is ready to run.\n108: \n109: ## 2. Terminal-driven startup\n110: \n111: For the current active ticket, the launcher follows `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`. If the repository is idle, begin the next planning cycle with:\n112: \n113: 1. **Manager creates new `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`** with the next approved ticket\n114:    - En un proyecto destino, el ID debe usar el namespace local definido en `PROJECT.md` (`XXX-YYYY-NNN`), no el del motor. El instalador escribe este prefijo con `--install --prefix XXX`.\n115: 2. **Validate clean state**: Run `python .agent\\agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n116: 3. **Launch terminals**: Use `.\\scripts\\launch_agent_terminals.ps1` for template-based startup\n117: \n118: ### Terminal 1: Builder (when active ticket is ready)\n119: \n120: ```powershell\n121: code .\n122: ```\n123: \n124: In VS Code, open OpenCode and launch the Agent Manager.\n125: The launcher will automatically apply the correct Builder prompt for the active ticket when the backend is OpenCode.\n126: \n127: **Automatic prompt composition (OpenCode backend):**\n128: When the Builder backend is OpenCode, the launcher composes the prompt from the active ticket and invokes:\n129: \n130: ```powershell\n131: opencode run \"<msg>\" --agent builder --model <model> --dir <root> -f <canonicals>\n132: ```\n133: \n134: Where:\n135: - `<msg>` is composed from `ticket_id` with closure instructions\n136: - `<model>` is read from `.opencode/opencode.json`\n137: - `<canonicals>` are `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`, `TURN.md`, `execution_log.md`, `STATE.md`, and optionally `PLAN_<ticket>.md` / `AUDIT_<ticket>.md`\n138: \n139: No manual paste is required.\n140: \n141: ### Terminal 2: Supervisor\n142: \n143: ```powershell\n144: python scripts\\ticket_supervisor.py --reactive\n145: ```\n146: \n147: Supervisor prompt, if you need to run it manually:\n148: \n149: ```text\n150: Actua como SUPERVISOR del flujo terminal-driven.\n151: Mantén la cola coherente.\n152: No permitas trabajo fuera del ticket activo.\n153: Si el ticket pasa a READY_FOR_REVIEW, deja que el review bridge dispare la revision.\n154: Si el ticket se cierra, prepara el siguiente turno sin inventar tickets.\n155: ```\n156: \n157: ### Terminal 3: Review Bridge\n158: \n159: ```powershell\n160: python scripts\\manager_review_bridge.py --watch --backend-path \"<path-to-codex.exe>\"\n161: ```\n162: \n163: Manager review prompt, if you need to run it manually:\n164: \n165: ```text\n166: Actua como MANAGER reviewer para el ticket activo definido en .agent/collaboration/work_plan.md.\n167: Revisa la implementacion contra .agent/collaboration/work_plan.md y .agent/collaboration/execution_log.md.\n168: Decide solo: APPROVE, CHANGES o INSPECT.\n169: Si apruebas, deja evidencia de aprobacion y permite el paso a READY_TO_CLOSE.\n170: Si pides cambios, enumera solo los ajustes necesarios.\n171: No amplíes el plan.\n172: No reescribas el ticket entero.\n173: ```\n174: \n175: ## 3. What each terminal does\n176: \n177: - **Builder**: Implements approved tickets following `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`, registers evidence in `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`, executes quality gates, and performs mandatory closure protocol.\n178: - **Supervisor**: Orchestrates the sequential queue via terminal-driven workflow, synchronizes `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`, `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`, and `.agent/collaboration/notifications.md`, advances the queue when appropriate.\n179: - **Review Bridge**: Launches Manager review automatically when tickets reach READY_FOR_REVIEW, handles APPROVE/CHANGES decisions with deterministic flow.\n180: \n181: ## 3b. Flujo operativo para Builder\n182: \n183: Builder follows this sequence for each ticket:\n184: \n185: 1. **Read Context:** Read `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`, `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`, `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`, `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`, `PROJECT.md`.\n186: 2. **Implement:** Execute only the active ticket scope using allowed tools and files. Treat `Files Likely Touched` as a hard whitelist. Do not widen scope without Manager approval.\n187: 3. **Register Evidence:** Document all changes with timestamps and commands in `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`.\n188: 4. **Quality Gates:** Run `ruff check`, `pytest-safe`, `pip-audit` on touched files.\n189: 5. **Closeout Post-Review:** Execute mandatory closure protocol. The hard scope gate will block `--mark-ready` if any changed files are outside the whitelist unless `--scope-override \"reason\"` is used.\n190: \n191: ## 3c. Cierre Post-Review Repetible\n192: \n193: After implementation and before reporting work done:\n194: \n195: ```powershell\n196: python .agent/agent_controller.py --mark-ready --json --force\n197: ```\n198: \n199: This command:\n200: - Marks the ticket as `READY_FOR_REVIEW` in `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`\n201: - Updates `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md` to Manager/REVIEW_WORK\n202: - Regenerates `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md` with current status\n203: - Validates no drift between canonical files\n204: - Emits event for Supervisor and Review Bridge detection\n205: - Leaves the runtime easier to reconcile on the next confirmed closeout because stale cursor files are treated as disposable runtime artifacts.\n206: \n207: **Do not consider work finished without executing this command.** It ensures the release is repeatable and reduces post-review drift.\n208: \n209: ## 4. Reconciliación si Supervisor/Bridge ya están en ejecución\n210: \n211: If you see stale state (e.g., Supervisor still watching an old ticket), you must reconcile:\n212: \n213: ```powershell\n214: # Terminal 1: Kill existing Supervisor/Bridge if they are running\n215: # (Ctrl+C in the terminal, or use Task Manager if needed)\n216: \n217: # Terminal 2: Reconcile state\n218: python scripts/ticket_supervisor.py --once\n219: \n220: # Then restart Supervisor in reactive mode\n221: python scripts/ticket_supervisor.py --reactive\n222: \n223: # And restart the Review Bridge\n224: python scripts/manager_review_bridge.py --watch --backend-path \"...\"\n225: ```\n226: \n227: **Why:** Supervisor caches runtime state. If you execute `--mark-ready` while Supervisor is still watching an old ticket, it may not detect the transition immediately. A restart ensures clean reconciliation. Do not delete `.agent/runtime/manager_bridge_state.json`; keep the cursor so the bridge does not replay an already-reviewed ticket.\n228: \n229: ## 5. Si empiezas desde chat en vez de terminal\n230: \n231: > Comparativa completa de modos (chat-driven vs terminal-driven, cuándo usar cada uno, transiciones): ver [INTERACTION_MODES.md](INTERACTION_MODES.md). Esta sección cubre solo el atajo operacional.\n232: \n233: Use the current ticket, then update:\n234: - `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`\n235: - `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`\n236: - `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`\n237: - `.agent/collaboration/notifications.md`\n238: - `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`\n239: \n240: ## 6. Comandos diarios\n241: \n242: > Comandos de instalación y sincronización: ver [AGENTS.md sección \"Comandos principales\"](AGENTS.md#comandos-principales). Esta sección cubre solo los comandos del ciclo diario (validate, tests, lint).\n243: \n244: ```powershell\n245: python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py\n246: ruff check .\n247: uv run pip-audit .\n248: python .agent/agent_controller.py --health\n249: ```\n250: \n251: Prefer `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py` for normal Windows runs. Use raw `pytest` only for targeted diagnostics when the environment is already safe.\n252: \n253: El comando `--health` proporciona un resumen operativo derivado de manifests y estado canónico.\n254: \n255: ## 7. Multi-Ticket Integration Smoke\n256: \n257: Para validar la seguridad multi-ticket y que tickets consecutivos no arrastran estado:\n258: \n259: ```powershell\n260: python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/integration/test_multi_ticket_integration_smoke.py -q\n261: ```\n262: \n263: Este smoke test de `WP-2026-039` ejecuta tres escenarios deterministas:\n264: - **Escenario A**: Recorrido feliz completo con APPROVE limpio.\n265: - **Escenario B**: CHANGES una vez, re-implementacion y APPROVE en segundo intento.\n266: - **Escenario C**: Cierre directo canónico sin review.\n267: \n268: Verifica alineacion canónica antes de cada lanzamiento y ausencia de residuos entre tickets.\n269: \n270: ## 8. Cierre de sesión\n271: \n272: Antes de terminar una sesión de trabajo, ejecuta estos pasos para que el motor quede listo para la siguiente sesión o para ser usado como base en un nuevo proyecto.\n273: \n274: ```powershell\n275: # 1. Archivar artefactos de planificación cerrados (PLAN/AUDIT de .agent/collaboration/)\n276: python scripts/archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n277: \n278: # 2. Mover PLAN/AUDIT que hayan quedado en la raíz (el archivador no los recoge)\n279: python -c \"\n280: import shutil; from pathlib import Path\n281: archive = Path('.agent/collaboration/_archive/plan_audit')\n282: archive.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n283: moved = []\n284: for p in list(Path('.').glob('PLAN_WP-*.md')) + list(Path('.').glob('AUDIT_WP-*.md')):\n285:     shutil.move(str(p), archive / p.name); moved.append(p.name)\n286: print('Moved:', moved if moved else 'nothing')\n287: \"\n288: \n289: # 3. Validación canónica\n290: python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force\n291: \n292: # 4. Verificar git limpio\n293: git status --short\n294: \n295: # 5. Sincronizar README/CHANGELOG si el último ticket no quedó reflejado\n296: # (ver sección 6 para comandos de calidad antes del commit)\n297: ```\n298: \n299: **Criterio de sesión cerrada:**\n300: - `git status` vacío\n301: - `--validate` sin errores\n302: - Sin `PLAN_WP-*.md` / `AUDIT_WP-*.md` en raíz\n303: - README `Current state` y CHANGELOG reflejan el último WP cerrado\n\n(End of file - total 303 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Quickstart\n\nUse this file to start the template in a fresh project, relaunch the terminal-driven workflow, or begin the next planning cycle after canonical closure.\n\n> **Onboarding de agente nuevo:** si arrancas una sesion limpia con un agente que no conoce el repo, pega primero el bootstrap canonico en `prompts/session_bootstrap.md`. Eso lo orienta sobre roles, archivos canonicos y reglas sin gastar contexto cargando docs completas.\n\nNamespace de tickets:\n- Motor: `WP-YYYY-NNN`\n- Destino: `XXX-YYYY-NNN` con `Ticket prefix: XXX` en el `PROJECT.md` local (el instalador lo escribe con `--install --prefix XXX`)\n\n## 0. Reproducible launcher\n\nThe recommended way to start the three canonical terminals is the repo-local launcher:\n\n```powershell\n.\\scripts\\launch_agent_terminals.ps1\n```\n\nThe launcher performs a comprehensive startup hygiene check before opening any window:\n- Compares `.agent/collaboration/work_plan.md`, `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md` and `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md` to ensure alignment (strict by default, abort on drift)","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"QUICKSTART.md","time":{"start":1779809194001,"end":1779809194053}},"id":"prt_e64e50640001zJMPqW63YytIXL","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e4839a001dAipoG3gMalxYO"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779809194062,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_ca1e435c76c24db4ac794ed5","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\CLOSURE_MODEL.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\CLOSURE_MODEL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Closure Model\n2: \n3: This document defines the two closure contracts used by `orquestador_de_agentes`.\n4: \n5: ## 1. Definitions\n6: \n7: ### System Closeout\n8: \n9: The process that neutralizes the repository as a reusable template for another project.\n10: \n11: Goal:\n12: - Remove ticket-specific wording from public docs.\n13: - Keep traceability in `.agent/`.\n14: - Leave the repository ready to be copied or published as a clean starter.\n15: - Keep the destination ticket namespace rule documented so the next project can declare its own `Ticket prefix: XXX` in `PROJECT.md`.\n16: \n17: ### Session Closeout\n18: \n19: The process that finalizes one operational ticket or runtime cycle inside the current repository.\n20: \n21: Goal:\n22: - Remove residual runtime artifacts.\n23: - Preserve canonical traceability in `.agent/collaboration/`.\n24: - Prepare the next runtime cycle without leaving stale cursors behind.\n25: \n26: ## 2. Responsibility Matrix\n27: \n28: | Artifact | Session Closeout | System Closeout |\n29: |----------|------------------|-----------------|\n30: | `work_plan.md` | Keep canonical ticket history | Keep only neutral template wording when exported |\n31: | `TURN.md` | Keep canonical turn history | Neutralize for template reuse |\n32: | `STATE.md` | Keep canonical state history | Neutralize for template reuse |\n33: | `execution_log.md` | Keep canonical execution history | Keep history in `.agent/`, not in public docs |\n34: | `SESSION_BRIEF.md` | Regenerate minimal runtime summary | Keep minimal and neutral |\n35: | `supervisor_state.json` | Remove if confirmed closeout | Remove or omit from exported template |\n36: | `manager_bridge_state.json` | Remove if confirmed closeout | Remove or omit from exported template |\n37: | `builder_lock.txt` | Remove if confirmed closeout | Remove or omit from exported template |\n38: | `README.md` / `PROJECT.md` / `QUICKSTART.md` | Usually unchanged except for active-cycle guidance | Must be neutral and reusable |\n39: | `.agent/` history | Preserve | Preserve |\n40: \n41: ## 3. Validation Expectations\n42: \n43: ### Session Closeout\n44: \n45: - `python .agent\\agent_controller.py --validate --json --force` passes without drift.\n46: - Note: the standalone `scripts/session_closeout.py` utility referenced in earlier revisions was removed in `WP-2026-061` cleanup. Closeout is currently a manual canonical handoff (edit `work_plan.md` and `execution_log.md` to `COMPLETED`, regenerate `TURN.md` with `--reset-turn`, then validate). An integrated replacement is pending design.\n47: \n48: ### System Closeout\n49: \n50: - Public docs contain no ticket-specific history.\n51: - Public docs do not require parent-workspace knowledge.\n52: - The repository root can be copied into another project and used as a neutral template.\n53: \n54: ## 4. Practical Workflow\n55: \n56: ### To close a session\n57: \n58: 1. Run the dry run.\n59: 2. Confirm the cleanup.\n60: 3. Regenerate the session brief.\n61: 4. Validate the canonical state.\n62: 5. **Reconcile the next-session startup chain.** Ask: did this session change\n63:    anything the startup system describes? Concretely — canonical paths or\n64:    commands, agent backends/models, the ticket cycle flow, or the root cause of\n65:    a bug that `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` still presents as \"expected\n66:    behavior\". If yes:\n67:    - Update `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` so it points at the new reality\n68:      (it points, it does not embed).\n69:    - Update or delete affected entries in `.agent/runtime/memory/` — memory\n70:      holds only stable knowledge; volatile state (active ticket, version,\n71:      baseline) must NOT live there.\n72:    - Regenerate `AUDIT.md` (`python scripts/local_audit.py`) so the volatile\n73:      snapshot is fresh.\n74:    If nothing startup-relevant changed, skip — do not rewrite the bootstrap as\n75:    a ritual. Goal: a chained startup — the next session begins with bootstrap,\n76:    memory and `AUDIT.md` all consistent with what actually happened.\n77: \n78: ### To close the system\n79: \n80: 1. Finalize the session closeout first.\n81: 2. Neutralize public docs and keep history in `.agent/`.\n82: 3. Re-run validation from the repository root.\n83: 4. Publish or copy the repository as a reusable template.\n84: 5. Ensure exported docs describe the destination namespace (`XXX-YYYY-NNN`) and where it is declared, without carrying over the motor's `WP-YYYY-NNN` history.\n85: \n86: ## 5. Examples\n87: \n88: ### Example: session closeout\n89: \n90: - A Builder ticket finishes.\n91: - The runtime cursor files are removed.\n92: - `SESSION_BRIEF.md` is regenerated.\n93: - The next ticket can start cleanly.\n94: \n95: ### Example: system closeout\n96: \n97: - The repository is ready to be copied into another project.\n98: - Public docs explain the template but do not mention historical tickets.\n99: - All traceability stays inside `.agent/`.\n\n(End of file - total 99 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Closure Model\n\nThis document defines the two closure contracts used by `orquestador_de_agentes`.\n\n## 1. Definitions\n\n### System Closeout\n\nThe process that neutralizes the repository as a reusable template for another project.\n\nGoal:\n- Remove ticket-specific wording from public docs.\n- Keep traceability in `.agent/`.\n- Leave the repository ready to be copied or published as a clean starter.\n- Keep the destination ticket namespace rule documented so the next project can declare its own `Ticket prefix: XXX` in `PROJECT.md`.\n\n### Session Closeout\n\nThe process that finalizes one operational ticket or runtime cycle inside the current repository.\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"CLOSURE_MODEL.md","time":{"start":1779809194017,"end":1779809194058}},"id":"prt_e64e50ca90015cTsYkl8AeYhJf","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","messageID":"msg_e64e4839a001dAipoG3gMalxYO"}}

{"type":"text","timestamp":1779809241938,"sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","part":{"id":"prt_e64e5963c001oKXCNCFHQPiVzb","messageID":"msg_e64e511bf001THwmzC421rjLka","sessionID":"ses_19b1c9bf2ffe22DetpXnTMZ74c","type":"text","text":"## Review de WP-2026-144: Destination ticket prefix onboarding\n\nHe revisado la implementación completa del ticket WP-2026-144. Aquí está mi evaluación:\n\n### Criterios de Aceptación Verificados\n\n1. ✅ **`--install --prefix XXX` escribe el prefijo en metadata destino y link file**\n   - `install_agent_system.py`: parámetro `--prefix` en CLI (línea 800)\n   - `_write_prefix_to_project_md()`: escribe/actualiza `Ticket prefix:` en PROJECT.md (líneas 550-597)\n   - `write_motor_destination_link()`: incluye `ticket_prefix` en el schema JSON (línea 454)\n   - Tests: 5 tests en `test_validate_host_prefix.py` + tests en `test_install_agent_system.py`\n\n2. ✅ **`--validate` advierte cuando host-project no tiene `Ticket prefix:`**\n   - `agent_controller.py`: `_validate_host_project_prefix()` (líneas 974-1015)\n   - `validate_state_files()`: incluye categoría `host_project_prefix` (línea 1053)\n   - Se activa cuando `active_profile == host-project`\n\n3. ✅ **Bootstrap docs usan `XXX-YYYY-NNN` para destino y mantienen `WP-YYYY-NNN` para motor**\n   - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` línea 25: \"Namespaces de tickets: motor `WP-YYYY-NNN`; destino `XXX-YYYY-NNN`\"\n   - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` línea 34: menciona `--install --prefix XXX`\n   - `README.md` líneas 71-72, 119-129: documenta el namespace y el instalador\n\n4. ✅ **Canonical validation path pasa sin errores nuevos**\n   - `execution_log.md`: `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force` → 0 errors\n\n### Verificación de Anti-patrones (Review Packet)\n\n- **AP-01 Mock drift**: No detectado\n- **AP-02 Floor assertion**: No aplica\n- **AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper**: Funciones nuevas tienen lógica propia\n- **AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition**: No aplica\n- **AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression**: No hay cambios en contratos de retorno\n- **AP-06 Validator evidence missing**: ✅ execution_log.md tiene evidencia clara de comandos y resultados\n- **AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code**: ✅ deliverable_type=\"mixed\" es correcto (toca código Python + documentación)\n- **AP-08 Test coverage drift**: ✅ Tests específicos para las nuevas funciones\n\n### Documentación Actualizada\n\n- ✅ `prompts/session_bootstrap.md`\n- ✅ `README.md`\n- ✅ `QUICKSTART.md` (línea 9, 119)\n- ✅ `RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md` (paso 0)\n- ✅ `CLOSURE_MODEL.md` (sección 5)\n- ✅ `PROJECT.md` (actualizado a COMPLETED)\n\n### Quality Gates (según execution_log.md)\n\n- Tests: 20 passed\n- Ruff: clean\n- Validación canónica: 0 errors\n\n---\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Quality gates passed. No blocking anti-patterns found.\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779809228348,"end":1779809241935}}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779820109002,"sessionID":"ses_19a74d4e1ffe1Llk7ekzEwQMUL","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_51a4f5ea9e044c81844c8d12","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Work Plan - WP-2026-145\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-145\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** research\n7: - **Titulo:** Deterministic STATE projection probe\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: Build a standalone read-only probe that reconstructs the active ticket state deterministically from `events.jsonl`, compares it against `STATE.md`, and reports any drift. The goal is to validate whether the bus can serve as single source of truth for state projection, before deciding whether to promote this into the runtime controller.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `scripts/state_projection_probe.py` will read the active ticket's events from the bus and materialize the projected state.\n15: - The probe will compare the derived state against `STATE.md` and report drift explicitly.\n16: - The probe will not mutate canonical files; it is a read-only experiment.\n17: - `TURN.md` stays under `agent_controller.py` ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n18: - The state machine in `bus/state_machine.py` remains the transition authority; the probe consumes it rather than reimplementing transitions.\n19: - Events that produce invalid transitions are logged as warnings, not exceptions; the probe continues and reports the anomaly.\n20: - This ticket is about validating the feasibility of bus-first state reconstruction, not integrating it into the runtime controller yet.\n21: \n22: ## Files Likely Touched\n23: - `scripts/state_projection_probe.py`\n24: - `tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py`\n25: \n26: ## Fases\n27: 1. Implement a small read-only probe that derives the active ticket state from `events.jsonl`.\n28: 2. Add unit tests for matching state, drift detection, and missing/empty bus cases.\n29: 3. Validate the probe against the current canonical ticket history.\n30: 4. Confirm the probe does not alter `TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, or `execution_log.md`.\n31: \n32: ## Calidad\n33: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py -q`\n34: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n35: \n36: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n37: - The probe reconstructs the same state as `STATE.md` for the current active ticket history.\n38: - Drift is reported clearly when the bus-derived state and markdown state differ.\n39: - The probe is read-only and does not mutate canonical collaboration files.\n40: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n41: - The probe emits a structured summary (`matched` / `drifted` / `bus_empty`) suitable for the Manager to decide whether to open a follow-up integration WP.\n\n(End of file - total 41 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-145\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-145\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** research\n- **Titulo:** Deterministic STATE projection probe\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nBuild a standalone read-only probe that reconstructs the active ticket state deterministically from `events.jsonl`, compares it against `STATE.md`, and reports any drift. The goal is to validate whether the bus can serve as single source of truth for state projection, before deciding whether to promote this into the runtime controller.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `scripts/state_projection_probe.py` will read the active ticket's events from the bus and materialize the projected state.\n- The probe will compare the derived state against `STATE.md` and report drift explicitly.\n- The probe will not mutate canonical files; it is a read-only experiment.\n- `TURN.md` stays under `agent_controller.py` ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n- The state machine in `bus/state_machine.py` remains the transition authority; the probe consumes it rather than reimplementing transitions.\n- Events that produce invalid transitions are logged as warnings, not exceptions; the probe continues and reports the anomaly.\n- This ticket is about validating the feasibility of bus-first state reconstruction, not integrating it into the runtime controller yet.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779820108939,"end":1779820109000}},"id":"prt_e658b8d55001gLgZLqFz7littH","sessionID":"ses_19a74d4e1ffe1Llk7ekzEwQMUL","messageID":"msg_e658b2c2a001I1PuCDOOEP2ZaG"}}

Implement a small read-only probe that derives the active ticket state from `events.jsonl`.\n38: 2. Add unit tests for matching state, drift detection, and missing/empty bus cases.\n39: 3. Validate the probe against the current canonical ticket history.\n40: 4. Confirm the probe does not alter `TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, or `execution_log.md`.\n41: \n42: ## Calidad\n43: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py -q`\n44: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n45: \n46: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n47: - The probe reconstructs the same state as `STATE.md` for the current active ticket history.\n48: - Drift is reported clearly when the bus-derived state and markdown state differ.\n49: - The probe is read-only and does not mutate canonical collaboration files.\n50: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n51: - The probe emits a structured summary (`matched` / `drifted` / `bus_empty`) suitable for the Manager to decide whether to open a follow-up integration WP.\n52: \n53: \n54: --- STATE.md ---\n55: # State - WP-2026-145\n56: \n57: Plan Activo: WP-2026-145\n58: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n59: Rol activo: BUILDER\n60: \n61: Resumen:\n62: WP-2026-145 en progreso: el Builder construira un probe de lectura para reconstruir el estado activo desde `events.jsonl` y compararlo con `STATE.md`.\n63: \n64: Notas:\n65: - El alcance es research.\n66: - `TURN.md` sigue bajo control del controller.\n67: - El probe no debe mutar archivos canonicos.\n68: \n69: \n70: --- TURN.md ---\n71: # TURNO ACTUAL\n72: \n73: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-26 20:27:58\n74: \n75: ---\n76: \n77: ## Agente Activo\n78: \n79: | Campo | Valor |\n80: |-------|-------|\n81: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n82: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-145 |\n83: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n84: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n85: \n86: ---\n87: \n88: ## Instruccion\n89: \n90: > Builder completo WP-2026-145. Revisa el trabajo.\n91: \n92: ---\n93: \n94: ## Archivos a Leer\n95: \n96: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n97: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n98: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n99: \n100: ---\n101: \n102: ## Estado del Sistema\n103: \n104: | Archivo | Estado |\n105: |---------|--------|\n106: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n107: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n108: \n109: ---\n110: \n111: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n112: \n113: \n114: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n115: [execution_log section for WP-2026-145 not found]\n116: \n117: --- git provenance ---\n118: [no new commits since origin/main]\n119: \n120: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n121: Available skills for this role:\n122:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n123:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n124:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n125: \n126: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n127: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n128: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n129: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n130: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n131: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n132: - .session_state.json\n133: \n134: \n135: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n136: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n137: \n138: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n139: \n140: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n141: DECISION: APPROVE\n142: \n143: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n144: \n145: ## SUMMARY\n146: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n147: \n148: ## BLOCKERS\n149: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n150: \n151: ## SUGGESTIONS\n152: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n153: \n154: DECISION: CHANGES\n155: \n156: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n157: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 157 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review non-code research ticket WP-2026-145. Since this is a non-code deliverable, focus strictly on the clarity, depth, correctness, structure, and completeness of the requested document deliverables. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\n--- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\nAP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n\n--- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\nLecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n- [2026-05-25] Validator evidence gate: when a work_plan explicitly declares a validator as a quality gate (skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), the Manager must find explicit outpu... (unknown)\n\n--- work_plan.md ---\n# Work Plan - WP-2026-145\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-145\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** research\n- **Titulo:** Deterministic STATE projection probe\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-145_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779820108930,"end":1779820109040}},"id":"prt_e658b8927001OgVCqBav7QHgsd","sessionID":"ses_19a74d4e1ffe1Llk7ekzEwQMUL","messageID":"msg_e658b2c2a001I1PuCDOOEP2ZaG"}}
