{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779820138572,"sessionID":"ses_19a74d4e1ffe1Llk7ekzEwQMUL","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_188b689e8f844f8f9b4a74e0","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\state_projection_probe.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\state_projection_probe.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Deterministic STATE projection probe.\n4: \n5: Read-only probe that reconstructs the active ticket state from events.jsonl,\n6: compares it against STATE.md, and reports any drift.\n7: \n8: This probe validates whether the bus can serve as single source of truth\n9: for state projection, without mutating canonical files.\n10: \n11: Usage:\n12:     python scripts/state_projection_probe.py [--ticket-id WP-YYYY-NNN] [--json]\n13: \n14: Exit codes:\n15:     0 - State matched or drift detected (informational)\n16:     1 - Error (file not found, parse error, etc.)\n17: \"\"\"\n18: \n19: from __future__ import annotations\n20: \n21: import argparse\n22: import json\n23: import sys\n24: from dataclasses import dataclass\n25: from enum import Enum\n26: from pathlib import Path\n27: \n28: \n29: # Add project root to path\n30: _PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n31: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n32:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n33: \n34: _AGENT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n35: if str(_AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n36:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_AGENT_DIR))\n37: \n38: from bus.state_machine import StateMachine  # noqa: E402\n39: \n40: \n41: class ProbeResult(str, Enum):\n42:     \"\"\"Result status of the state projection probe.\"\"\"\n43: \n44:     MATCHED = \"matched\"\n45:     DRIFTED = \"drifted\"\n46:     BUS_EMPTY = \"bus_empty\"\n47:     ERROR = \"error\"\n48: \n49: \n50: @dataclass(slots=True)\n51: class ProbeOutput:\n52:     \"\"\"Structured output from the state projection probe.\"\"\"\n53: \n54:     result: ProbeResult\n55:     ticket_id: str\n56:     bus_derived_state: str | None\n57:     markdown_state: str | None\n58:     drift_detected: bool\n59:     events_count: int\n60:     message: str\n61: \n62:     def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n63:         return {\n64:             \"result\": self.result.value,\n65:             \"ticket_id\": self.ticket_id,\n66:             \"bus_derived_state\": self.bus_derived_state,\n67:             \"markdown_state\": self.markdown_state,\n68:             \"drift_detected\": self.drift_detected,\n69:             \"events_count\": self.events_count,\n70:             \"message\": self.message,\n71:         }\n72: \n73: \n74: def _read_events_jsonl(events_path: Path) -> list[dict]:\n75:     \"\"\"\n76:     Read events from the JSONL bus file.\n77: \n78:     Before: File must exist at events_path.\n79:     During: Parses each line as JSON, skipping empty lines and malformed entries.\n80:     After: Returns list of event dicts; empty list if file missing.\n81:     \"\"\"\n82:     if not events_path.exists():\n83:         return []\n84: \n85:     events: list[dict] = []\n86:     for line in events_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n87:         if not line.strip():\n88:             continue\n89:         try:\n90:             events.append(json.loads(line))\n91:         except json.JSONDecodeError:\n92:             # Skip malformed lines silently (read-only probe)\n93:             continue\n94:     return events\n95: \n96: \n97: def _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path: Path) -> str | None:\n98:     \"\"\"\n99:     Extract active ticket ID from work_plan.md.\n100: \n101:     Before: File must exist at work_plan_path.\n102:     During: Parses markdown metadata section for **ID:** field.\n103:     After: Returns ticket ID string or None if not found.\n104:     \"\"\"\n105:     if not work_plan_path.exists():\n106:         return None\n107: \n108:     content = work_plan_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n109:     for line in content.splitlines():\n110:         # Match both \"**ID:** WP-2026-145\" and \"- **ID:** WP-2026-145\"\n111:         if \"**ID:**\" in line:\n112:             # Extract everything after the colon, strip markdown and whitespace\n113:             parts = line.split(\":\", 1)\n114:             if len(parts) == 2:\n115:                 ticket_id = parts[1].strip()\n116:                 # Remove markdown bold markers\n117:                 ticket_id = ticket_id.replace(\"**\", \"\").strip()\n118:                 return ticket_id\n119:     return None\n120: \n121: \n122: def _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path: Path, ticket_id: str) -> str | None:\n123:     \"\"\"\n124:     Parse current state from STATE.md.\n125: \n126:     Before: File must exist at state_md_path.\n127:     During: Searches for 'Estado actual:' line or plan header matching ticket_id.\n128:     After: Returns state string or None if not found.\n129:     \"\"\"\n130:     if not state_md_path.exists():\n131:         return None\n132: \n133:     content = state_md_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n134:     for line in content.splitlines():\n135:         line_stripped = line.strip()\n136:         # Look for \"Estado actual: STATE\"\n137:         if line_stripped.startswith(\"Estado actual:\"):\n138:             return line_stripped.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n139:     return None\n140: \n141: \n142: def _filter_events_for_ticket(all_events: list[dict], ticket_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n143:     \"\"\"\n144:     Filter events belonging to a specific ticket.\n145: \n146:     Before: all_events is a list of event dicts.\n147:     During: Filters by ticket_id field.\n148:     After: Returns filtered list (may be empty).\n149:     \"\"\"\n150:     return [e for e in all_events if e.get(\"ticket_id\") == ticket_id]\n151: \n152: \n153: def run_probe(\n154:     runtime_dir: Path | None = None,\n155:     collaboration_dir: Path | None = None,\n156:     ticket_id: str | None = None,\n157: ) -> ProbeOutput:\n158:     \"\"\"\n159:     Run the deterministic state projection probe.\n160: \n161:     Before:\n162:         - runtime_dir must point to .agent/runtime/memory/ or similar.\n163:         - collaboration_dir must point to .agent/collaboration/.\n164:         - If ticket_id not provided, reads from work_plan.md.\n165:         - events.jsonl may or may not exist.\n166:         - STATE.md may or may not exist.\n167: \n168:     During:\n169:         - Reads events from events.jsonl (read-only).\n170:         - Derives state using StateMachine.derive_state_from_events().\n171:         - Parses STATE.md for comparison.\n172:         - Compares bus-derived state vs markdown state.\n173: \n174:     After:\n175:         - Returns ProbeOutput with result status.\n176:         - Does NOT mutate any canonical files.\n177:         - May log warnings for invalid transitions (non-blocking).\n178: \n179:     Raises:\n180:         No exceptions are raised; errors are captured in ProbeOutput.\n181:     \"\"\"\n182:     # Resolve paths\n183:     if runtime_dir is None:\n184:         runtime_dir = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"memory\"\n185: \n186:     if collaboration_dir is None:\n187:         collaboration_dir = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n188: \n189:     events_path = runtime_dir / \"events.jsonl\"\n190:     state_md_path = collaboration_dir / \"STATE.md\"\n191:     work_plan_path = collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\"\n192: \n193:     # Determine ticket ID\n194:     if ticket_id is None:\n195:         ticket_id = _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path)\n196:         if not ticket_id:\n197:             return ProbeOutput(\n198:                 result=ProbeResult.ERROR,\n199:                 ticket_id=\"unknown\",\n200:                 bus_derived_state=None,\n201:                 markdown_state=None,\n202:                 drift_detected=False,\n203:                 events_count=0,\n204:                 message=\"Could not determine ticket ID from work_plan.md\",\n205:             )\n206: \n207:     # Read events from bus\n208:     if not events_path.exists():\n209:         return ProbeOutput(\n210:             result=ProbeResult.BUS_EMPTY,\n211:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n212:             bus_derived_state=None,\n213:             markdown_state=None,\n214:             drift_detected=False,\n215:             events_count=0,\n216:             message=f\"Bus file not found: {events_path}\",\n217:         )\n218: \n219:     all_events = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n220:     ticket_events = _filter_events_for_ticket(all_events, ticket_id)\n221: \n222:     if not ticket_events:\n223:         return ProbeOutput(\n224:             result=ProbeResult.BUS_EMPTY,\n225:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n226:             bus_derived_state=None,\n227:             markdown_state=None,\n228:             drift_detected=False,\n229:             events_count=len(all_events),\n230:             message=f\"No events found for ticket {ticket_id}\",\n231:         )\n232: \n233:     # Derive state from bus events using the canonical StateMachine\n234:     try:\n235:         derived_state = StateMachine.derive_state_from_events(ticket_events)\n236:         bus_derived_state = derived_state.value\n237:     except Exception as e:\n238:         return ProbeOutput(\n239:             result=ProbeResult.ERROR,\n240:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n241:             bus_derived_state=None,\n242:             markdown_state=None,\n243:             drift_detected=False,\n244:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n245:             message=f\"Error deriving state from events: {e}\",\n246:         )\n247: \n248:     # Parse markdown state\n249:     markdown_state = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, ticket_id)\n250: \n251:     # Compare states\n252:     if markdown_state is None:\n253:         return ProbeOutput(\n254:             result=ProbeResult.DRIFTED,\n255:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n256:             bus_derived_state=bus_derived_state,\n257:             markdown_state=None,\n258:             drift_detected=True,\n259:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n260:             message=\"STATE.md not found or state not parseable\",\n261:         )\n262: \n263:     drift_detected = bus_derived_state != markdown_state\n264: \n265:     if drift_detected:\n266:         return ProbeOutput(\n267:             result=ProbeResult.DRIFTED,\n268:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n269:             bus_derived_state=bus_derived_state,\n270:             markdown_state=markdown_state,\n271:             drift_detected=True,\n272:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n273:             message=f\"Drift detected: bus={bus_derived_state}, markdown={markdown_state}\",\n274:         )\n275:     else:\n276:         return ProbeOutput(\n277:             result=ProbeResult.MATCHED,\n278:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n279:             bus_derived_state=bus_derived_state,\n280:             markdown_state=markdown_state,\n281:             drift_detected=False,\n282:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n283:             message=f\"State matched: {bus_derived_state}\",\n284:         )\n285: \n286: \n287: def main() -> int:\n288:     \"\"\"\n289:     Main entry point for the state projection probe.\n290: \n291:     Before: Command-line arguments parsed.\n292:     During: Runs probe and formats output.\n293:     After: Prints result and returns exit code.\n294:     \"\"\"\n295:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n296:         description=\"Deterministic STATE projection probe (read-only)\"\n297:     )\n298:     parser.add_argument(\n299:         \"--ticket-id\",\n300:         type=str,\n301:         default=None,\n302:         help=\"Ticket ID to probe (default: read from work_plan.md)\",\n303:     )\n304:     parser.add_argument(\n305:         \"--json\",\n306:         action=\"store_true\",\n307:         help=\"Output in JSON format\",\n308:     )\n309:     parser.add_argument(\n310:         \"--runtime-dir\",\n311:         type=Path,\n312:         default=None,\n313:         help=\"Runtime directory containing events.jsonl\",\n314:     )\n315: \n316:     args = parser.parse_args()\n317: \n318:     output = run_probe(runtime_dir=args.runtime_dir, ticket_id=args.ticket_id)\n319: \n320:     if args.json:\n321:         print(json.dumps(output.to_dict(), indent=2))\n322:     else:\n323:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Ticket: {output.ticket_id}\")\n324:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Result: {output.result.value}\")\n325:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Bus-derived state: {output.bus_derived_state or 'N/A'}\")\n326:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Markdown state: {output.markdown_state or 'N/A'}\")\n327:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Events count: {output.events_count}\")\n328:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Drift detected: {output.drift_detected}\")\n329:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Message: {output.message}\")\n330: \n331:     # Exit code: 0 for informational results, 1 for errors\n332:     if output.result == ProbeResult.ERROR:\n333:         return 1\n334:     return 0\n335: \n336: \n337: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n338:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 338 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nDeterministic STATE projection probe.\n\nRead-only probe that reconstructs the Error (file not found, parse error, etc.)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import \"\"\"Tests for the deterministic state projection probe.\"\"\"\n2: \n3: import sys\n4: from pathlib import Path\n5: \n6: \n7: # Add project root to path\n8: _PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\n9: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n10:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n11: \n12: _AGENT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n13: if str(_AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n14:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_AGENT_DIR))\n15: \n16: from scripts.state_projection_probe import (  # noqa: E402\n17:     ProbeOutput,\n18:     ProbeResult,\n19:     _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan,\n20:     _filter_events_for_ticket,\n21:     _parse_markdown_state,\n22:     _read_events_jsonl,\n23:     run_probe,\n24: )\n25: \n26: \n27: class TestReadEventsJsonl:\n28:     \"\"\"Test reading events from JSONL bus file.\"\"\"\n29: \n30:     def test_read_empty_file(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n31:         \"\"\"Empty file returns empty list.\"\"\"\n32:         events_path = tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"\n33:         events_path.write_text(\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n34:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n35:         assert result == []\n36: \n37:     def test_read_valid_events(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n38:         \"\"\"Valid JSONL returns parsed events.\"\"\"\n39:         events_path = tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"\n40:         events_path.write_text(\n41:             '{\"event_type\": \"STATE_CHANGED\", \"ticket_id\": \"WP-2026-001\"}\\n'\n42:             '{\"event_type\": \"REVIEW_DECISION\", \"ticket_id\": \"WP-2026-001\"}\\n',\n43:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n44:         )\n45:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n46:         assert len(result) == 2\n47:         assert result[0][\"event_type\"] == \"STATE_CHANGED\"\n48:         assert result[1][\"event_type\"] == \"REVIEW_DECISION\"\n49: \n50:     def test_skip_malformed_lines(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n51:         \"\"\"Malformed JSON lines are skipped silently.\"\"\"\n52:         events_path = tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"\n53:         events_path.write_text(\n54:             '{\"event_type\": \"VALID\"}\\nnot valid json\\n{\"event_type\": \"ALSO_VALID\"}\\n',\n55:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n56:         )\n57:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n58:         assert len(result) == 2\n59:         assert result[0][\"event_type\"] == \"VALID\"\n60:         assert result[1][\"event_type\"] == \"ALSO_VALID\"\n61: \n62:     def test_file_not_exists(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n63:         \"\"\"Non-existent file returns empty list.\"\"\"\n64:         events_path = tmp_path / \"nonexistent.jsonl\"\n65:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n66: The implementation should reuse the approval-resolution contract already present in `bus/approval.py`, keep the current state machine semantics intact, and stay small enough to close in one cycle.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `agent_controller.py` will include timeout metadata when a ticket is escalated to `HUMAN_GATE`.\n15: - `bus/approval.py` already provides the persistent approval model; the ticket escalation must create an `ApprovalRequest` there instead of inventing a new timeout store.\n16: - `bus/supervisor.py` already calls `check_and_expire_all()` and will consume the persisted approval request on its normal loop.\n17: - `agents.json` should provide the timeout value alongside `manager_review.max_attempts`, with a documented fallback if the setting is absent.\n18: - The existing `APPROVAL_RESOLVED` -> `BLOCKED` path remains the canonical expiry contract.\n19: - `TURN.md` stays under controller ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n20: - No new terminal state is introduced; the timeout uses the approval resolution contract already understood by the bus.\n21: - The change is about bounded waiting and explicit expiry, not a broader runtime refactor.\n22: \n23: ## Files Likely Touched\n24: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n25: - `bus/approval.py`\n26: - `bus/supervisor.py`\n27: - `tests/unit/test_human_gate_timeout.py`\n28: \n29: ## Fases\n30: 1. Add timeout metadata to the `STATE_CHANGED -> HUMAN_GATE` handoff.\n31: 2. Create and persist an `ApprovalRequest` when the ticket is escalated to `HUMAN_GATE`.\n32: 3. Let the supervisor expire the stored request via its existing approval timeout loop.\n33: 4. Add unit tests for expired, non-expired, and restart-survivability cases.\n34: 5. Confirm the timeout flow does not change the existing `resume-human-gate` behavior.\n35: \n36: ## Calidad\n37: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_human_gate_timeout.py -q`\n38: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n39: \n40: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n41: - `HUMAN_GATE` carries explicit timeout metadata at handoff.\n42: - The escalation persists an `ApprovalRequest` so the gate survives restarts.\n43: - An expired `HUMAN_GATE` resolves automatically through the canonical approval-resolution path.\n44: - A fresh `HUMAN_GATE` does not expire early.\n45: - The timeout value is sourced from configuration with a documented fallback.\n46: - The existing `resume-human-gate` path remains valid after the timeout change.\n47: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n\n(End of file - total 47 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-146\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-146\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Human gate approval timeout wiring\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nWire `HUMAN_GATE` escalation into the existing `ApprovalStore` so timeout handling is persistent and survives restarts instead of being recreated ad hoc in the supervisor loop. The implementation should reuse the approval-resolution contract already present in `bus/approval.py`, keep the current state machine semantics intact, and stay small enough to close in one cycle.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `agent_controller.py` will include timeout metadata when a ticket is escalated to `HUMAN_GATE`.\n- `bus/approval.py` already provides the persistent approval model; the ticket escalation must create an `ApprovalRequest` there instead of inventing a new timeout store.\n- `bus/supervisor.py` already calls `check_and_expire_all()` and will consume the persisted approval request on its normal loop.\n- `agents.json` should provide the timeout value alongside `manager_review.max_attempts`, with a documented fallback if the setting is absent.\n- The existing `APPROVAL_RESOLVED` -> `BLOCKED` path remains the canonical expiry contract.\n- `TURN.md` stays under controller ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n- No new terminal state is introduced; the timeout uses the approval resolution contract already understood by the bus.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779824938176,"end":1779824938238}},"id":"prt_e65d53db3001zMCycQe7NwCEkE","sessionID":"ses_19a2af79effeISQ1K84oR5HC46","messageID":"msg_e65d50947001sRzADLVyrpuSz0"}}

Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The implementation should reuse the approval-resolution contract already present in `bus/approval.py`, keep the current state machine semantics intact, and stay small enough to close in one cycle.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - `agent_controller.py` will include timeout metadata when a ticket is escalated to `HUMAN_GATE`.\n50: - `bus/approval.py` already provides the persistent approval model; the ticket escalation must create an `ApprovalRequest` there instead of inventing a new timeout store.\n51: - `bus/supervisor.py` already calls `check_and_expire_all()` and will consume the persisted approval request on its normal loop.\n52: - `agents.json` should provide the timeout value alongside `manager_review.max_attempts`, with a documented fallback if the setting is absent.\n53: - The existing `APPROVAL_RESOLVED` -> `BLOCKED` path remains the canonical expiry contract.\n54: - `TURN.md` stays under controller ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n55: - No new terminal state is introduced; the timeout uses the approval resolution contract already understood by the bus.\n56: - The change is about bounded waiting and explicit expiry, not a broader runtime refactor.\n57: \n58: ## Files Likely Touched\n59: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n60: - `bus/approval.py`\n61: - `bus/supervisor.py`\n62: - `tests/unit/test_human_gate_timeout.py`\n63: \n64: ## Fases\n65: 1. Add timeout metadata to the `STATE_CHANGED -> HUMAN_GATE` handoff.\n66: 2. Create and persist an `ApprovalRequest` when the ticket is escalated to `HUMAN_GATE`.\n67: 3. Let the supervisor expire the stored request via its existing approval timeout loop.\n68: 4. Add unit tests for expired, non-expired, and restart-survivability cases.\n69: 5. Confirm the timeout flow does not change the existing `resume-human-gate` behavior.\n70: \n71: ## Calidad\n72: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_human_gate_timeout.py -q`\n73: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n74: \n75: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n76: - `HUMAN_GATE` carries explicit timeout metadata at handoff.\n77: - The escalation persists an `ApprovalRequest` so the gate survives restarts.\n78: - An expired `HUMAN_GATE` resolves automatically through the canonical approval-resolution path.\n79: - A fresh `HUMAN_GATE` does not expire early.\n80: - The timeout value is sourced from configuration with a documented fallback.\n81: - The existing `resume-human-gate` path remains valid after the timeout change.\n82: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n83: \n84: \n85: --- STATE.md ---\n86: # State - WP-2026-146\n87: \n88: Plan Activo: WP-2026-146\n89: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n90: Rol activo: BUILDER\n91: \n92: Resumen:\n93: WP-2026-146 en progreso: el Builder implementara un timeout bounded para `HUMAN_GATE` usando la ruta canonica de resolucion de approvals.\n94: \n95: Notas:\n96: - El alcance es code.\n97: - `TURN.md` sigue bajo control del controller.\n98: - El timeout no introduce un estado nuevo; reutiliza la resolucion canonica existente.\n99: \n100: \n101: --- TURN.md ---\n102: # TURNO ACTUAL\n103: \n104: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-26 21:45:26\n105: \n106: ---\n107: \n108: ## Agente Activo\n109: \n110: | Campo | Valor |\n111: |-------|-------|\n112: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n113: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-146 |\n114: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n115: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n116: \n117: ---\n118: \n119: ## Instruccion\n120: \n121: > Builder completo WP-2026-146. Revisa el trabajo.\n122: \n123: ---\n124: \n125: ## Archivos a Leer\n126: \n127: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n128: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n129: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n130: \n131: ---\n132: \n133: ## Estado del Sistema\n134: \n135: | Archivo | Estado |\n136: |---------|--------|\n137: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n138: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n139: \n140: ---\n141: \n142: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n143: \n144: \n145: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n146: [execution_log section for WP-2026-146 not found]\n147: \n148: --- git provenance ---\n149: [no new commits since origin/main]\n150: \n151: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n152: Available skills for this role:\n153:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n154:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n155:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n156: \n157: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n158: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n159: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n160: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n161: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n162: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n163: - .session_state.json\n164: \n165: \n166: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n167: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n168: \n169: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n170: \n171: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n172: DECISION: APPROVE\n173: \n174: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n175: \n176: ## SUMMARY\n177: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n178: \n179: ## BLOCKERS\n180: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n181: \n182: ## SUGGESTIONS\n183: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n184: \n185: DECISION: CHANGES\n186: \n187: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n188: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 188 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-146. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-146_attempt-2.md","time":{"start":1779824938166,"end":1779824938273}},"id":"prt_e65d53936001TgcrdoW3Neu75q","sessionID":"ses_19a2af79effeISQ1K84oR5HC46","messageID":"msg_e65d50947001sRzADLVyrpuSz0"}}
