ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_AGENT_DIR), str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # WP-2026-146: Import approval system for HUMAN_GATE timeout\n100: try:\n101:     from bus.approval import (\n102:         ApprovalPolicy,\n103:         ApprovalReason,\n104:         ApprovalStatus,\n105:         ApprovalStore,\n106:     )\n107: \n108:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = True\n109: except ImportError:\n110:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = False\n111:     ApprovalPolicy = None\n112:     ApprovalReason = None\n113:     ApprovalStatus = None\n114:     ApprovalStore = None\n115: \n116: # ============================================================================\n117: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n118: # ============================================================================\n119: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n120: \n121: \n122: def _project_root() -> Path:\n123:     return resolve_project_root()\n124: \n125: \n126: class _LazyPath:\n127:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n128:         self._resolver = resolver\n129: \n130:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n131:         return self._resolver()\n132: \n133:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n134:         return self.resolve() / other\n135: \n136:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n137:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n138: \n139:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n140:         return str(self.resolve())\n141: \n142:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n143:         return str(self.resolve())\n144: \n145:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n146:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n147: \n148: \n149: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n150: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n151: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_agent_dir)\n152: COLLAB_DIR = _LazyPath(get_collab_dir)\n153: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_context_dir)\n154: \n155: # State files\n156: WORK_PLAN = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\")\n157: EXEC_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"execution_log.md\")\n158: REVIEW_QUEUE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"review_queue.md\")\n159: NOTIFICATIONS = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"notifications.md\")\n160: TURN_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"TURN.md\")\n161: STATE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"STATE.md\")\n162: PROJECT_MAP = _LazyPath(lambda: get_context_dir() / \"project_map.md\")\n163: ARCHIVE_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"archive\")\n164: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\")\n165: \n166: # Archive configuration\n167: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n168: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n169: \n170: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n171: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n172: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n173: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n174: \n175: # WP-2026-146: HUMAN_GATE timeout configuration. Single source of truth is\n176: # manager_review.timeout_seconds in agents.json. Fallback used if missing.\n177: HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK = 300  # 5 minutes\n178: \n179: \n180: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n181:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n182: \n183:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n184:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n185:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n186:     \"\"\"\n187:     try:\n188:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n189:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n190:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n191:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n192:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n193: \n194: \n195: def get_human_gate_timeout() -> int:\n196:     \"\"\"Return the timeout in seconds for HUMAN_GATE approval requests.\n197: \n198:     Reads manager_review.timeout_seconds from agents.json so the timeout\n199:     is configurable. Falls back to HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK if the\n200:     config is absent or malformed.\n201: \n202:     Returns:\n203:         Timeout in seconds (positive integer).\n204:     \"\"\"\n205:     try:\n206:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n207:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"timeout_seconds\"))\n208:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n209:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n210:         return HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n211: \n212: \n213: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n214: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n215: \n216: # Estados validos para validacion\n217: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n218:     \"DRAFT\",\n219:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n220:     \"APPROVED\",\n221:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n222:     \"COMPLETED\",\n223:     \"N/A\",\n224: }\n225: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n226:     \"PENDING\",\n227:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n228:     \"BLOCKED\",\n229:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n230:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n231:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n232:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n233:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n234:     \"COMPLETED\",\n235:     \"N/A\",\n236: }\n237: \n238: \n239: def _ensure_runtime_dirs() -> None:\n240:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n241:     archive_dir = get_collab_dir() / \"archive\"\n242:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n243:     archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n244: \n245: \n246: def _get_event_bus() -> EventBus | None:\n247:     global event_bus\n248:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE:\n249:         return None\n250:     if event_bus is None:\n251:         event_bus = EventBus(get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n252:     return event_bus\n253: \n254: \n255: def _get_approval_store() -> ApprovalStore | None:\n256:     \"\"\"Get or create the approval store for HUMAN_GATE timeout.\n257: \n258:     Before: No approval store existed in agent_controller.\n259:     During: Creates ApprovalStore under runtime_dir with configurable timeout.\n260:     After: Returns configured ApprovalStore or None if approval system unavailable.\n261:     \"\"\"\n262:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n263:         return None\n264:     store_path = get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"approvals\" / \"store.json\"\n265:     timeout_seconds = get_human_gate_timeout()\n266:     policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n267:         policy_name=\"human_gate\",\n268:         timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n269:         auto_resolve=True,\n270:         auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n271:     )\n272:     return ApprovalStore(store_path=store_path, policy=policy)\n273: \n274: \n275: # Event bus is initialized lazily after the project root is known.\n276: event_bus = None\n277: \n278: # Circuit breaker state\n279: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = _LazyPath(\n280:     lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n281: )\n282: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\")\n283: \n284: # ============================================================================\n285: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n286: # ============================================================================\n287: \n288: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n289:     \"work_plan.md\",\n290:     \"execution_log.md\",\n291:     \"STATE.md\",\n292:     \"TURN.md\",\n293:     \"notifications.md\",\n294:     \".session_state.json\",\n295: }\n296: \n297: \n298: def _exclude_files() -> set[str]:\n299:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n300:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n301:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n302:     exclude_files = {str((collab_dir / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n303:     exclude_files.add(str((context_dir / \"project_map.md\").resolve()))\n304:     # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n305:     exclude_files.add(\n306:         str((agent_dir / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve())\n307:     )\n308:     # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n309:     exclude_files.add(str((agent_dir / \"config\").resolve()))\n310:     return exclude_files\n311: \n312: \n313: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n314:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n315:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n316:     in_section = False\n317:     files = set()\n318: \n319:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n320:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n321:             return False\n322:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n323:             return True\n324:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n325:             return True\n326:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n327:         return \".\" in basename\n328: \n329:     for line in lines:\n330:         line = line.strip()\n331:         if \"## Files Likely Touched\" in line:\n332:             in_section = True\n333:             continue\n334:         if in_section and line.startswith(\"## \"):\n335:             break  # next section\n336:         if in_section and line and not line.startswith(\"---\"):\n337:             # normalize: remove backticks, quotes, bullets, trim\n338:             normalized = (\n339:                 line.lstrip(\"*- \")\n340:                 .replace(\"`\", \"\")\n341:                 .replace('\"', \"\")\n342:                 .replace(\"'\", \"\")\n343:                 .strip()\n344:             )\n345:             if normalized and _looks_like_path_token(normalized):\n346:                 # resolve relative to project root\n347:                 path = (PROJECT_ROOT / normalized).resolve()\n348:                 files.add(str(path))\n349:     return files\n350: \n351: \n352: def get_changed_files() -> set[str] | None:\n353:     \"\"\"Get all changed files: staged, unstaged, untracked. None if not git repo.\"\"\"\n354:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n355:         return None\n356:     try:\n357:         # Use -z for null-byte separated output to handle paths with spaces and renames safely\n358:         result = subprocess.run(\n359:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\", \"-z\"],\n360:             capture_output=True,\n361:             text=True,\n362:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n363:         )\n364:         changed = set()\n365:         # Split by null byte, each entry is: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status\n366:         # Renames have two consecutive entries: \"R old_path\" followed by \"new_path\"\n367:         entries = result.stdout.split(\"\\0\")\n368:         i = 0\n369:         while i < len(entries):\n370:             entry = entries[i]\n371:             if not entry:\n372:                 i += 1\n373:                 continue\n374:             # Format: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status (e.g., \"M \", \"??\", \"R \")\n375:             # When Y=' ' (space), format is \"M path\" (path at index 2)\n376:             # When Y!=' ', format is \"XY path\" with space separator (path at index 3)\n377:             if len(entry) >= 3:\n378:                 status = entry[:2]\n379:                 # Determine path start: if entry[2] is space, path starts at 3; otherwise at 2\n380:                 path = entry[3:] if entry[2] == \" \" else entry[2:]\n381:                 # Handle renames: status starts with 'R', next entry is the new path\n382:                 if status[0] == \"R\" and i + 1 < len(entries):\n383:                     new_path = entries[i + 1]\n384:                     if new_path:\n385:                         changed.add(new_path)\n386:                     i += 2\n387:                     continue\n388:                 else:\n389:                     changed.add(path)\n390:             i += 1\n391:         # resolve to absolute paths\n392:         resolved = set()\n393:         for f in changed:\n394:             path = (PROJECT_ROOT / f).resolve()\n395:             resolved.add(str(path))\n396:         return resolved\n397:     except FileNotFoundError:\n398:         return None\n399: \n400: \n401: def check_scope_gate(\n402:     work_plan_content: str, changed_files: set[str] | None, exclude_files: set[str]\n403: ) -> dict:\n404:     \"\"\"Check if changed files are within scope.\"\"\"\n405:     if changed_files is None:\n406:         return {\n407:             \"valid\": True,\n408:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n409:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n410:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n411:             \"warnings\": [\"Repository is not git-managed\"],\n412:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n413:         }\n414: \n415:     whitelist = parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n416:     if not whitelist:\n417:         return {\n418:             \"valid\": True,\n419:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n420:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n421:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n422:             \"warnings\": [\"No Files Likely Touched section in work_plan.md\"],\n423:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n424:         }\n425: \n426:     relevant_changed = changed_files - exclude_files\n427:     relevant_whitelist = whitelist - exclude_files\n428:     covered_files = relevant_changed & relevant_whitelist\n429:     missing_from_diff = relevant_whitelist - relevant_changed\n430:     out_of_scope = relevant_changed - relevant_whitelist\n431: \n432:     warnings = []\n433:     blocked_reason = None\n434:     valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0\n435: \n436:     if relevant_whitelist and not covered_files:\n437:         valid = False\n438:         blocked_reason = (\n439:             \"None of the declared Files Likely Touched entries appeared in the diff\"\n440:         )\n441:     elif covered_files and missing_from_diff:\n442:         warnings.append(\n443:             \"Partial scope coverage: \"\n444:             f\"{len(covered_files)} of {len(relevant_whitelist)} declared files touched\"\n445:         )\n446: \n447:     return {\n448:         \"valid\": valid,\n449:         \"out_of_scope\": out_of_scope,\n450:         \"missing_from_diff\": missing_from_diff,\n451:         \"covered_files\": covered_files,\n452:         \"warnings\": warnings,\n453:         \"blocked_reason\": blocked_reason,\n454:     }\n455: \n456: \n457: def _load_mark_ready_context() -> tuple[str, str, str]:\n458:     \"\"\"Load the plan and log content for --mark-ready.\"\"\"\n459:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n460:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n461:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n462:     return plan_content, log_content, plan_id\n463: \n464: \n465: def _record_scope_override(scope_override: str, problem_files: set[str]) -> None:\n466:     \"\"\"Record a scope override in the execution log.\"\"\"\n467:     note = (\n468:         f\"Scope override: {scope_override}. \"\n469:         f\"Affected files: {', '.join(sorted(problem_files))}\"\n470:     )\n471:     update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", note)\n472: \n473: \n474: def _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result: dict, scope_override: str | None) -> bool:\n475:     \"\"\"Apply scope gate decision for mark-ready.\"\"\"\n476:     if gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n477:         for warning in gate_result[\"warnings\"]:\n478:             print(f\"[WARN] {warning}\")\n479:         update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Marked ready by Builder\")\n480:         return True\n481: \n482:     if not scope_override:\n483:         print(\"[ERROR] Scope violation detected:\")\n484:         if gate_result.get(\"blocked_reason\"):\n485:             print(f\"  {gate_result['blocked_reason']}\")\n486:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]):\n487:             print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n488:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())):\n489:             print(f\"  - missing: {file_path}\")\n490:         print('Use --scope-override \"reason\" to proceed.')\n491:         return False\n492: \n493:     print(f\"[INFO] Scope override applied: {scope_override}\")\n494:     problem_files = set(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]) | set(\n495:         gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())\n496:     )\n497:     _record_scope_override(scope_override, problem_files)\n498:     return True\n499: \n500: \n501: def _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id: str, plan_content: str) -> None:\n502:     \"\"\"Update TURN.md and STATE.md after mark-ready.\n503: \n504:     WP-2026-124: Uses bus-derived state as authority, not execution_log.md.\n505:     \"\"\"\n506:     # WP-2026-124: Get bus-derived state\n507:     log_status_before = get_status(read_file(EXEC_LOG), \"**Estado:**\")\n508:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n509:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n510: \n511:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n512:         bus_state = (\n513:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n514:             if events\n515:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n516:         )\n517:         # Use bus state if available, fallback to log\n518:         from_state = (\n519:             bus_state.value if bus_state != TicketState.UNKNOWN else log_status_before\n520:         )\n521:     else:\n522:         from_state = log_status_before\n523: \n524:     action = {\n525:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n526:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n527:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n528:         \"instruction\": f\"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n529:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n530:         \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n531:         \"log_status\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n532:         \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n533:         \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n534:     }\n535:     update_turn_file(action)\n536: \n537:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n538:     if not state_content:\n539:         return\n540: \n541:     lines = state_content.split(\"\\n\")\n542:     for index, line in enumerate(lines):\n543:         if \"**Estado actual:**\" in line:\n544:             lines[index] = \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n545:             write_file(STATE_FILE, \"\\n\".join(lines))\n546:             break\n547: \n548:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus idempotently\n549:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n550:         # Check if already emitted for this ticket and state\n551:         latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n552:             ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n553:         )\n554:         if (\n555:             not latest_state_event\n556:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n557:         ):\n558:             event_bus.emit(\n559:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n560:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n561:                 actor=\"BUILDER\",\n562:                 payload={\n563:                     \"from_state\": from_state,\n564:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n565:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n566:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n567:                 },\n568:             )\n569:         if (\n570:             not latest_state_event\n571:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n572:         ):\n573:             bus_from_state = (\n574:                 latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n575:                 if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n576:                 else log_status_before\n577:             )\n578:             event_bus.emit(\n579:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n580:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n581:                 actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n582:                 payload={\n583:                     \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n584:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n585:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n586:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n587:                 },\n588:             )\n589: \n590: \n591: # ============================================================================\n592: # CIRCUIT BREAKER AND CHECKOUT UTILITIES\n593: # ============================================================================\n594: \n595: \n596: def _read_circuit_breaker() -> dict:\n597:     \"\"\"Read circuit breaker state from JSON file.\"\"\"\n598:     if not CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.exists():\n599:         return {\n600:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n601:             \"failures\": 0,\n602:             \"last_failure\": None,\n603:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n604:         }\n605:     try:\n606:         return json.loads(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n607:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n608:         return {\n609:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n610:             \"failures\": 0,\n611:             \"last_failure\": None,\n612:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n613:         }\n614: \n615: \n616: def _write_circuit_breaker(state: dict) -> None:\n617:     \"\"\"Write circuit breaker state to JSON file.\"\"\"\n618:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n619:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.write_text(\n620:         json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n621:     )\n622: \n623: \n624: def _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> dict:\n625:     \"\"\"\n626:     Check circuit breaker state for a ticket.\n627:     Returns dict with 'open', 'reason', 'failures', 'no_progress_count'.\n628:     \"\"\"\n629:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n630:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\":\n631:         return {\n632:             \"open\": True,\n633:             \"reason\": breaker.get(\"reason\", \"Circuit breaker triggered\"),\n634:             \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n635:             \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n636:         }\n637:     return {\n638:         \"open\": False,\n639:         \"reason\": None,\n640:         \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n641:         \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n642:     }\n643: \n644: \n645: def _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n646:     reason: str, plan_id: str, is_no_progress: bool = False\n647: ) -> None:\n648:     \"\"\"Trigger circuit breaker to OPEN state.\"\"\"\n649:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n650:     breaker[\"state\"] = \"OPEN\"\n651:     breaker[\"reason\"] = reason\n652:     breaker[\"last_triggered\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n653:     breaker[\"ticket_id\"] = plan_id\n654:     if is_no_progress:\n655:         breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n656:     else:\n657:         breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n658:     _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n659: \n660: \n661: def _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n662:     \"\"\"Reset circuit breaker to CLOSED state after successful completion.\"\"\"\n663:     _write_circuit_breaker(\n664:         {\n665:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n666:             \"failures\": 0,\n667:             \"last_failure\": None,\n668:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n669:             \"last_reset\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n670:             \"last_ticket\": plan_id,\n671:         }\n672:     )\n673: \n674: \n675: def _record_error_for_breaker(plan_id: str, error_msg: str) -> None:\n676:     \"\"\"Record an error for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n677:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n678:     breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n679:     breaker[\"last_error\"] = error_msg\n680:     breaker[\"last_error_time\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n681: \n682:     # Trigger if 3+ consecutive errors\n683:     if breaker[\"failures\"] >= 3:\n684:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(f\"Repeated errors: {error_msg[:100]}\", plan_id)\n685:     else:\n686:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n687: \n688: \n689: def _record_no_progress_for_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n690:     \"\"\"Record no-progress event for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n691:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n692:     breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n693:     breaker[\"last_no_progress\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n694: \n695:     # Trigger if 3+ no-progress events\n696:     if breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] >= 3:\n697:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n698:             \"No progress detected in multiple iterations\", plan_id, is_no_progress=True\n699:         )\n700:     else:\n701:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n702: \n703: \n704: def _read_builder_lock() -> dict | None:\n705:     \"\"\"Read builder lock file if it exists.\"\"\"\n706:     if not BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.exists():\n707:         return None\n708:     try:\n709:         return json.loads(BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n710:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n711:         return None\n712: \n713: \n714: def _acquire_builder_lock(\n715:     plan_id: str, pid: int, role: str = \"BUILDER\", backend: str = \"unknown\"\n716: ) -> bool:\n717:     \"\"\"\n718:     Attempt to acquire builder lock atomically.\n719:     Returns True if lock was acquired, False if another session holds it.\n720:     \"\"\"\n721:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n722:     if existing is not None:\n723:         # Check if lock is stale (different ticket or dead process)\n724:         if existing.get(\"ticket_id\") != plan_id:\n725:             # Stale lock from different ticket - override\n726:             lock_data = {\n727:                 \"pid\": pid,\n728:                 \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n729:                 \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n730:                 \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n731:                 \"role\": role,\n732:                 \"backend\": backend,\n733:                 \"round\": 1,\n734:             }\n735:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n736:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n737:                 json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n738:             )\n739:             return True\n740:         # Same ticket - check if process is alive (simplified: just allow re-acquisition)\n741:         lock_data = {\n742:             \"pid\": pid,\n743:             \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n744:             \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n745:             \"started_at\": existing.get(\n746:                 \"started_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n747:             ),\n748:             \"role\": role,\n749:             \"backend\": backend,\n750:             \"round\": existing.get(\"round\", 1) + 1,\n751:         }\n752:         BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n753:             json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n754:         )\n755:         return True\n756: \n757:     # No existing lock - acquire fresh\n758:     lock_data = {\n759:         \"pid\": pid,\n760:         \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n761:         \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n762:         \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n763:         \"role\": role,\n764:         \"backend\": backend,\n765:         \"round\": 1,\n766:     }\n767:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n768:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n769:         json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n770:     )\n771:     return True\n772: \n773: \n774: def _release_builder_lock(plan_id: str) -> None:\n775:     \"\"\"Release builder lock after completion.\"\"\"\n776:     import contextlib\n777: \n778:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n779:     if existing and existing.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id:\n780:         with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n781:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.unlink()\n782: \n783: \n784: def _emit_builder_exit(plan_id: str, exit_reason: str, completion_summary: str) -> None:\n785:     \"\"\"Emit BUILDER_EXIT event to the bus - required for ticket closure.\"\"\"\n786:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n787:         event_bus.emit(\n788:             event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\",\n789:             ticket_id=plan_id,\n790:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n791:             payload={\n792:                 \"exit_reason\": exit_reason,\n793:                 \"completion_summary\": completion_summary,\n794:                 \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n795:             },\n796:         )\n797: \n798: \n799: def _create_human_gate_approval_request(\n800:     ticket_id: str, timeout_seconds: int | None = None\n801: ) -> bool:\n802:     \"\"\"Create and persist an ApprovalRequest when escalating to HUMAN_GATE.\n803: \n804:     Before: HUMAN_GATE escalation had no persistent timeout tracking.\n805:     During: Creates an ApprovalRequest with timeout metadata in the ApprovalStore.\n806:     After: The approval request persists across restarts and can be expired by\n807:            the supervisor's check_and_expire_all() loop.\n808: \n809:     Args:\n810:         ticket_id: The ticket ID being escalated to HUMAN_GATE.\n811:         timeout_seconds: Optional timeout override. Defaults to config value.\n812: \n813:     Returns:\n814:         True if the approval request was created successfully, False otherwise.\n815:     \"\"\"\n816:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n817:         print(\n818:             \"[WARN] Approval system unavailable; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n819:             file=sys.stderr,\n820:             flush=True,\n821:         )\n822:         return False\n823: \n824:     store = _get_approval_store()\n825:     if store is None:\n826:         print(\n827:             \"[WARN] Could not create ApprovalStore; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n828:             file=sys.stderr,\n829:             flush=True,\n830:         )\n831:         return False\n832: \n833:     # Generate a unique approval ID based on ticket ID and timestamp\n834:     import hashlib\n835: \n836:     timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n837:     approval_id = hashlib.sha256(\n838:         f\"{ticket_id}:{timestamp}:human_gate\".encode()\n839:     ).hexdigest()[:16]\n840: \n841:     # Create and persist the approval request\n842:     timeout = timeout_seconds if timeout_seconds is not None else get_human_gate_timeout()\n843:     metadata = {\n844:         \"escalation_type\": \"human_gate\",\n845:         \"timeout_seconds\": timeout,\n846:         \"created_by\": \"agent_controller\",\n847:         \"source\": \"escalate-human-gate\",\n848:     }\n849: \n850:     try:\n851:         # Use custom policy if timeout_seconds was provided\n852:         if timeout_seconds is not None:\n853:             custom_policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n854:                 policy_name=\"human_gate_custom\",\n855:                 timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n856:                 auto_resolve=True,\n857:                 auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n858:             )\n859:             store.create_request(\n860:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n861:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n862:                 metadata=metadata,\n863:                 policy=custom_policy,\n864:             )\n865:         else:\n866:             store.create_request(\n867:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n868:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n869:                 metadata=metadata,\n870:             )\n871:         print(\n872:             f\"[OK] Created HUMAN_GATE approval request {approval_id} for {ticket_id} \"\n873:             f\"(timeout={timeout}s).\",\n874:             flush=True,\n875:         )\n876:         return True\n877:     except Exception as exc:\n878:         print(\n879:             f\"[ERROR] Failed to create HUMAN_GATE approval request: {exc}\",\n880:             file=sys.stderr,\n881:             flush=True,\n882:         )\n883:         return False\n884: \n885: \n886: def _auto_archive_closed_artifacts() -> None:\n887:     \"\"\"Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts during mark-ready.\n888: \n889:     Before: Requires COLLAB_DIR to exist with potential closed PLAN/AUDIT files.\n890:     During: Imports and calls archive_collaboration_artifacts.py as a library function.\n891:     After: Closed PLAN/AUDIT files are moved to _archive/plan_audit/ (idempotent, silent on no-op).\n892:     \"\"\"\n893:     try:\n894:         # Import the archive script as a module\n895:         import importlib.util\n896: \n897:         archive_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n898:             \"archive_collaboration_artifacts\",\n899:             PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n900:         )\n901:         if archive_spec and archive_spec.loader:\n902:             archive_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(archive_spec)\n903:             archive_spec.loader.exec_module(archive_mod)\n904: \n905:             # Call the archive function (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n906:             archive_mod.archive_collaboration_artifacts(\n907:                 collaboration_dir=COLLAB_DIR,\n908:                 dry_run=False,\n909:             )\n910:     except Exception as exc:\n911:         # Silent fail with debug logging - archiving is best-effort, not critical path\n912:         print(f\"[WARN] Auto-archive failed: {exc}\")\n913: \n914: \n915: # ============================================================================\n916: # UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n917: # ============================================================================\n918: \n919: \n920: def read_file(path: Path) -> str:\n921:     \"\"\"Lee un archivo si existe, retorna string vacio si no.\"\"\"\n922:     if not path.exists():\n923:         return \"\"\n924:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n925:         return f.read()\n926: \n927: \n928: def write_file(path: Path, content: str) -> None:\n929:     \"\"\"Escribe contenido a un archivo, creando directorios si es necesario.\"\"\"\n930:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n931:     with open(path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n932:         f.write(content)\n933: \n934: \n935: def get_status(content: str, marker: str) -> str:\n936:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado de un archivo buscando un marcador.\"\"\"\n937:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n938:         if marker in line:\n939:             return line.split(marker)[1].strip()\n940:     return \"UNKNOWN\"\n941: \n942: \n943: def get_plan_id(content: str) -> str:\n944:     \"\"\"Extrae el ID del plan de trabajo.\"\"\"\n945:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n946:         if \"**ID:**\" in line or \"**Plan ID:**\" in line:\n947:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n948:     return \"N/A\"\n949: \n950: \n951: def get_plan_type(content: str) -> str:\n952:     \"\"\"Extrae el tipo de plan. Valores: IMPLEMENTATION (default) | FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n953:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n954:         if \"**Tipo:**\" in line:\n955:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip().upper()\n956:     return \"IMPLEMENTATION\"\n957: \n958: \n959: def check_git_status() -> bool | None:\n960:     \"\"\"Verifica si el repositorio esta limpio.\"\"\"\n961:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n962:         return None\n963:     try:\n964:         result = subprocess.run(\n965:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\"],\n966:             capture_output=True,\n967:             text=True,\n968:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n969:         )\n970:         return not result.stdout.strip()\n971:     except FileNotFoundError:\n972:         return None\n973: \n974: \n975: def extract_status_emoji(status_str: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n976:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado limpio y el emoji.\"\"\"\n977:     emojis = {\"🟢\", \"🟡\", \"🔴\", \"🟣\", \"✅\", \"⏳\", \"❌\", \"⚠️\"}\n978:     status_clean = status_str.strip()\n979:     found_emoji = \"\"\n980:     for emoji in emojis:\n981:         if emoji in status_clean:\n982:             found_emoji = emoji\n983:             status_clean = status_clean.replace(emoji, \"\").strip()\n984:             break\n985:     return status_clean, found_emoji\n986: \n987: \n988: _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n989: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n990:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n991:     re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n992: )\n993: \n994: \n995: def _check_deliverable_type(content: str) -> list[str]:\n996:     \"\"\"Check deliverable_type field in work_plan.md content.\n997: \n998:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n999:     During: Searches for deliverable_type field using regex, validates against allowed values.\n1000:     After: Returns list of warning strings (empty if valid). Never returns errors (V1 informational).\n1001:     \"\"\"\n1002:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n1003:     if not match:\n1004:         return [\n1005:             \"work_plan.md missing deliverable_type field. \"\n1006:             \"Add one of: code, documentation, research, analysis, mixed.\"\n1007:         ]\n1008:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n1009:     if \"+\" in value:\n1010:         return [\n1011:             f\"work_plan.md deliverable_type='{value}' uses compound syntax. \"\n1012:             \"Use 'mixed' instead of 'code+documentation' etc.\"\n1013:         ]\n1014:     if value not in _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES:\n1015:         return [\n1016:             f\"work_plan.md has unknown deliverable_type '{value}'. \"\n1017:             f\"Expected one of: {sorted(_VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES)}\"\n1018:         ]\n1019:     return []\n1020: \n1021: \n1022: def _validate_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n1023:     \"\"\"Validate work_plan.md file.\"\"\"\n1024:     errors = []\n1025:     content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1026:     if not content:\n1027:         return errors\n1028: \n1029:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1030:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1031:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_PLAN_STATES:\n1032:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1033:     if \"**ID:**\" not in content:\n1034:         errors.append(\"Falta campo **ID:**\")\n1035:     return errors\n1036: \n1037: \n1038: def _validate_execution_log() -> list[str]:\n1039:     \"\"\"Validate execution_log.md file.\"\"\"\n1040:     errors = []\n1041:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1042:     if not content:\n1043:         return errors\n1044: \n1045:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1046:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1047:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_LOG_STATES:\n1048:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1049:     return errors\n1050: \n1051: \n1052: def _validate_turn_file() -> list[str]:\n1053:     \"\"\"Validate TURN.md file.\"\"\"\n1054:     errors = []\n1055:     content = read_file(TURN_FILE)\n1056:     if content and \"## Agente Activo\" not in content:\n1057:         errors.append(\"Falta secciÃƒÂ³n '## Agente Activo'\")\n1058:     return errors\n1059: \n1060: \n1061: def _validate_notifications() -> list[str]:\n1062:     \"\"\"Validate notifications.md file.\"\"\"\n1063:     errors = []\n1064:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1065:     if content and \"</thinking>\" in content:\n1066:         errors.append(\"Contiene etiquetas </thinking> (corrupto)\")\n1067:     return errors\n1068: \n1069: \n1070: def _validate_cross_file_consistency() -> list[str]:\n1071:     \"\"\"Validate consistency across files.\"\"\"\n1072:     errors = []\n1073:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1074:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1075: \n1076:     if not plan_content or not log_content:\n1077:         return errors\n1078: \n1079:     plan_status_raw = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1080:     log_status_raw = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1081:     plan_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(plan_status_raw)\n1082:     log_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(log_status_raw)\n1083: \n1084:     # Plan APPROVED pero log COMPLETED\n1085:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_clean and \"COMPLETED\" in log_clean:\n1086:         errors.append(\n1087:             \"DRIFT: plan=APPROVED pero log=COMPLETED -- \"\n1088:             \"el log pertenece a un ciclo anterior. Limpia execution_log.md.\"\n1089:         )\n1090: \n1091:     # Plan COMPLETED pero log IN_PROGRESS\n1092:     if \"COMPLETED\" in plan_clean and \"IN_PROGRESS\" in log_clean:\n1093:         errors.append(\n1094:             \"DRIFT: plan=COMPLETED pero log=IN_PROGRESS -- \"\n1095:             \"el Builder no cerro su bitacora correctamente.\"\n1096:         )\n1097: \n1098:     # Plan IN_PLANNING con log READY_FOR_REVIEW\n1099:     if \"IN_PLANNING\" in plan_clean and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_clean:\n1100:         errors.append(\n1101:             \"DRIFT: plan=IN_PLANNING pero log=READY_FOR_REVIEW -- \"\n1102:             \"estado imposible. El plan debe estar APPROVED antes de que el Builder entregue.\"\n1103:         )\n1104: \n1105:     # Plan N/A pero log activo\n1106:     if (\"N/A\" in plan_clean or not plan_clean) and log_clean not in (\n1107:         \"N/A\",\n1108:         \"COMPLETED\",\n1109:         \"PENDING\",\n1110:         \"\",\n1111:     ):\n1112:         errors.append(\n1113:             f\"DRIFT: no hay plan activo pero log={log_clean} -- \"\n1114:             \"limpia execution_log.md o crea un nuevo work_plan.md.\"\n1115:         )\n1116: \n1117:     return errors\n1118: \n1119: \n1120: def _validate_host_project_prefix() -> list[str]:\n1121:     \"\"\"Validate that host-project destinations have 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md.\n1122: \n1123:     Before: Requires PROJECT.md to exist at project root; agents.json to be readable.\n1124:     During: Checks if active_profile is 'host-project' and if PROJECT.md has 'Ticket prefix:'.\n1125:     After: Returns list of warnings (empty if validation passes).\n1126: \n1127:     Returns:\n1128:         List of warning strings (empty if no issues).\n1129:     \"\"\"\n1130:     warnings = []\n1131:     agents_config = AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH\n1132:     if not agents_config.exists():\n1133:         return warnings\n1134: \n1135:     try:\n1136:         config_data = json.loads(agents_config.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n1137:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n1138:         return warnings\n1139: \n1140:     active_profile = config_data.get(\"active_profile\", \"\")\n1141:     if active_profile != \"host-project\":\n1142:         return warnings\n1143: \n1144:     # Only check for host-project profile\n1145:     project_md = PROJECT_ROOT / \"PROJECT.md\"\n1146:     if not project_md.exists():\n1147:         warnings.append(\n1148:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks PROJECT.md. \"\n1149:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace.\"\n1150:         )\n1151:         return warnings\n1152: \n1153:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1154:     if \"Ticket prefix:\" not in content:\n1155:         warnings.append(\n1156:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md. \"\n1157:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace (XXX-YYYY-NNN).\"\n1158:         )\n1159: \n1160:     return warnings\n1161: \n1162: \n1163: def validate_state_files() -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n1164:     \"\"\"Valida el formato y consistencia cruzada de los archivos de estado.\"\"\"\n1165:     return {\n1166:         \"work_plan.md\": _validate_work_plan(),\n1167:         \"execution_log.md\": _validate_execution_log(),\n1168:         \"notifications.md\": _validate_notifications(),\n1169:         \"TURN.md\": _validate_turn_file(),\n1170:         \"consistency\": _validate_cross_file_consistency(),\n1171:         \"host_project_prefix\": _validate_host_project_prefix(),\n1172:     }\n1173: \n1174: \n1175: def fix_corrupted_notifications() -> bool:\n1176:     \"\"\"Intenta reparar notifications.md si esta corrupto.\"\"\"\n1177:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1178:     if not content:\n1179:         return False\n1180:     original = content\n1181:     content = re.sub(r\"</thinking>\\s*\", \"\", content)\n1182:     content = re.sub(r\"\\n{4,}\", \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\", content)\n1183:     if content != original:\n1184:         write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, content)\n1185:         return True\n1186:     return False\n1187: \n1188: \n1189: def archive_old_notifications() -> str | None:\n1190:     \"\"\"Archiva notificaciones antiguas si el archivo es muy grande.\"\"\"\n1191:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1192:     if not content:\n1193:         return None\n1194: \n1195:     file_size_kb = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\")) / 1024\n1196:     entry_count = len([e for e in content.split(\"---\") if e.strip()])\n1197: \n1198:     if (\n1199:         file_size_kb < MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB\n1200:         and entry_count <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES\n1201:     ):\n1202:         return None\n1203: \n1204:     parts = content.split(\"---\")\n1205:     entries = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]\n1206: \n1207:     if len(entries) <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:\n1208:         return None\n1209: \n1210:     entries_to_archive = entries[:-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES]\n1211:     entries_to_keep = entries[-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:]\n1212: \n1213:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n1214:     archive_file = ARCHIVE_DIR / f\"notifications_{timestamp}.md\"\n1215: \n1216:     archive_content = \"# Notificaciones Archivadas\\n\\n\"\n1217:     archive_content += f\"**Fecha:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\\n\"\n1218:     archive_content += \"\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_archive)\n1219: \n1220:     write_file(archive_file, archive_content)\n1221: \n1222:     new_content = \"# Registro de Notificaciones\\n\\n---\\n\\n\"\n1223:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_keep)\n1224:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\"\n1225:     write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, new_content)\n1226: \n1227:     return str(archive_file)\n1228: \n1229: \n1230: def run_finalization_checks() -> dict:\n1231:     \"\"\"Checks adicionales para planes de tipo FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n1232:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"summary\": []}\n1233:     checks = {\n1234:         \"README.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"README.md\",\n1235:         \"CHANGELOG.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n1236:         \"closeout_report.md\": COLLAB_DIR / \"closeout_report.md\",\n1237:     }\n1238:     for name, path in checks.items():\n1239:         if path.exists():\n1240:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[OK] {name}: Presente\")\n1241:         else:\n1242:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[WARN] {name}: No encontrado\")\n1243:     return results\n1244: \n1245: \n1246: def run_quality_gates(plan_type: str = \"IMPLEMENTATION\") -> dict:\n1247:     \"\"\"Ejecuta validaciones automaticas.\"\"\"\n1248:     print(\"\\n[QUALITY GATES] Ejecutando Quality Gates...\")\n1249:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"errors\": [], \"summary\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1250: \n1251:     state_errors = validate_state_files()\n1252:     total_state_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in state_errors.values())\n1253:     if total_state_errors > 0:\n1254:         results[\"warnings\"].append(\n1255:             f\"Archivos de estado: {total_state_errors} problemas\"\n1256:         )\n1257:     else:\n1258:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Estado: Archivos validos\")\n1259: \n1260:     src_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"src\"\n1261:     tests_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"tests\"\n1262:     dirs_to_check = [str(d) for d in [src_dir, tests_dir] if d.exists()]\n1263:     if not dirs_to_check:\n1264:         dirs_to_check = [\".\"]\n1265: \n1266:     try:\n1267:         ruff = subprocess.run(\n1268:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"ruff\", \"check\", *dirs_to_check],\n1269:             capture_output=True,\n1270:             text=True,\n1271:             timeout=60,\n1272:         )\n1273:         if ruff.returncode != 0:\n1274:             results[\"passed\"] = False\n1275:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Ruff: Errores de linting\")\n1276:         else:\n1277:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Ruff: Limpio\")\n1278:     except FileNotFoundError:\n1279:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Ruff: No instalado\")\n1280: \n1281:     if tests_dir.exists():\n1282:         try:\n1283:             pytest_result = subprocess.run(\n1284:                 [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pytest\", \"-q\"],\n1285:                 capture_output=True,\n1286:                 timeout=120,\n1287:                 cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n1288:             )\n1289:             if pytest_result.returncode != 0:\n1290:                 results[\"passed\"] = False\n1291:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Pytest: Tests fallando\")\n1292:             else:\n1293:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Pytest: Tests OK\")\n1294:         except FileNotFoundError:\n1295:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Pytest: No instalado\")\n1296: \n1297:     if plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1298:         fin_results = run_finalization_checks()\n1299:         results[\"summary\"].extend(fin_results[\"summary\"])\n1300: \n1301:     status = \"[PASSED]\" if results[\"passed\"] else \"[FAILED]\"\n1302:     print(f\"   {status}\")\n1303:     return results\n1304: \n1305: \n1306: def update_log_status(new_status: str, note: str) -> bool:\n1307:     \"\"\"Actualiza el estado en execution_log.md.\"\"\"\n1308:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1309:     if not content:\n1310:         return False\n1311:     lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n1312:     for i, line in             write_file(EXEC_LOG, new_content)\n1317:             return True\n1318:     return False\n1319: \n1320: \n1321: def create_findings_file(plan_id: str = \"N/A\") -> Path:\n1322:  template_path = AGENT_DIR / \"templates\" / \"findings_template.md\"\n1329: \n1330:     try:\n1331:         if template_path.exists():\n1332:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1333:         else:\n1334:             template = \"\"\"# Hallazgos de Investigacion\n1335: \n1336: **Plan ID:** {{PLAN_ID}}\n1337: **Creado:** {{DATE}}\n1338: \n1339: ---\n1340: \n1341: ## Hallazgos\n1342: \n1343: <!-- Documenta aqui tus hallazgos durante la investigacion -->\n1344: \"\"\"\n1345: \n1346:         content = template.replace(\"{{PLAN_ID}}\", plan_id)\n1347:         content = content.replace(\n1348:             \"{{DATE}}\", datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1349:         )\n1350: \n1351:         write_file(findings_path, content)\n1352:         print(\"  [OK] Creado findings.md\")\n1353: \n1354:     except Exception as e:\n1355:         print(f\"  [WARN] Error creando findings.md: {e}\")\n1356: \n1357:     return findings_path\n1358: \n1359: \n1360: def generate_project_map() -> str:\n1361:     \"\"\"Genera un mapa actualizado del proyecto.\"\"\"\n1362:     output = [\n1363:         \"# Mapa del Proyecto\",\n1364:         f\"**Actualizado:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\",\n1365:         \"\",\n1366:         \"## Estructura de Archivos\",\n1367:         \"```\",\n1368:     ]\n1369: \n1370:     ignore = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\", \".ruff_cache\"}\n1371:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".txt\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n1372: \n1373:     files_found = []\n1374:     for path in sorted(PROJECT_ROOT.rglob(\"*\")):\n1375:         if any(part in ignore for part in path.parts):\n1376:             continue\n1377:         if path.is_file() and path.suffix in extensions:\n1378:             files_found.append(str(path.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)))\n1379: \n1380:     output.extend(files_found[:50])\n1381:     if len(files_found) > 50:\n1382:         output.append(f\"... (+{len(files_found) - 50} archivos mas)\")\n1383: \n1384:     output.append(\"```\")\n1385:     content = \"\\n\".join(output)\n1386:     write_file(PROJECT_MAP, content)\n1387:     return content\n1388: \n1389: \n1390: def _run_pre_action_hooks(\n1391:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, action_type: str\n1392: ) -> None:\n1393:     \"\"\"Run pre-action hooks if available.\"\"\"\n1394:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1395:         hook_registry.execute(\n1396:             \"pre_action\",\n1397:             {\n1398:                 \"action_type\": action_type,\n1399:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1400:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1401:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1402:             },\n1403:         )\n1404: \n1405: \n1406: def _check_quality_gates(\n1407:     plan_id: str, plan_type: str, plan_status: str, skip_gates: bool\n1408: ) -> dict | None:\n1409:     \"\"\"Check quality gates and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1410:     gate_result = run_quality_gates(plan_type=plan_type)\n1411:     if not gate_result[\"passed\"]:\n1412:         update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"AUTO-REJECTED: Quality Gates fallaron\")\n1413:         return {\n1414:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1415:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1416:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1417:             \"instruction\": \"RECHAZADO. Quality Gates fallaron. Corrige errores.\",\n1418:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1419:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1420:             \"log_status\": \"AUTO-REJECTED\",\n1421:             \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1422:         }\n1423:     return None\n1424: \n1425: \n1426: def _check_completion_verification(\n1427:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_type: str, strict_mode: bool\n1428: ) -> dict | None:\n1429:     \"\"\"Check completion verification in strict mode and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1430:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1431:         stop_result = stop_hook(\n1432:             {\n1433:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1434:                 \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1435:                 \"mode\": \"strict\" if strict_mode else \"normal\",\n1436:             }\n1437:         )\n1438:         if strict_mode and not stop_result.get(\"can_complete\", True):\n1439:             return {\n1440:                 \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1441:                 \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1442:                 \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1443:                 \"instruction\": (\n1444:                     f\"Plan {plan_id} no supera Completion Verification. \"\n1445:                     \"Corrige advertencias antes de review.\"\n1446:                 ),\n1447:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1448:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1449:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1450:                 \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1451:             }\n1452:     return None\n1453: \n1454: \n1455: # Transition table for base routing\n1456: TRANSITION_TABLE = {\n1457:     (\"no_plan\", \"any\"): (\n1458:         \"MANAGER\",\n1459:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1460:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1461:     ),\n1462:     (\"completed\", \"any\"): (\n1463:         \"MANAGER\",\n1464:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1465:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1466:     ),\n1467:     (\"in_planning\", \"any\"): (\n1468:         \"MANAGER\",\n1469:         \"FINALIZE_PLAN\",\n1470:         \"Plan {plan_id} en borrador. Finaliza y cambia a APPROVED\",\n1471:     ),\n1472:     (\"approved\", \"ready_for_review\"): (\n1473:         \"MANAGER\",\n1474:         \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n1475:         \"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n1476:     ),\n1477:     (\"approved\", \"any\"): (\n1478:         \"BUILDER\",\n1479:         \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1480:         \"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Implementa segun work_plan.md\",\n1481:     ),\n1482:     (\"in_review\", \"any\"): (\n1483:         \"MANAGER\",\n1484:         \"REVIEW_CHANGES\",\n1485:         \"Plan {plan_id} en revision. Verifica cambios.\",\n1486:     ),\n1487:     (\"any\", \"blocked\"): (\n1488:         \"MANAGER\",\n1489:         \"RESOLVE_BLOCK\",\n1490:         \"Builder BLOQUEADO en {plan_id}. Resuelve en review_queue.md\",\n1491:     ),\n1492: }\n1493: \n1494: \n1495: def _lookup_transition(\n1496:     plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_id: str\n1497: ) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:\n1498:     \"\"\"Lookup transition from table.\"\"\"\n1499:     # Normalize status for lookup\n1500:     plan_key = (\n1501:         \"no_plan\"\n1502:         if not plan_status or plan_status in (\"COMPLETED\", \"N/A\")\n1503:         else plan_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\")\n1504:     )\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1504. Use offset=1505 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n\"\"\"\nAgent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n==========================================\nOrquestador con:\n- Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n- Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n- Completion Verification antes de review\n- Quality Gates extendidos\n- Session Recovery\n- Project Map y estado del workflow\n\nUso:\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agent_controller.py","time":{"start":1779824949892,"end":1779824949936}},"id":"prt_e65d56c510011VeKugzMlm5Nj7","sessionID":"ses_19a2af79effeISQ1K84oR5HC46","messageID":"msg_e65d55122001bNEzjGaZM6y1JQ"}}

\"\"\"\n2: Approval System - Configurable timeout and resolution for human approvals.\n3: \n4: WP-2026-127: Implements approval requests with timeout policy, expiration\n5: tracking, and canonical resolution with reason codes.\n6: \"\"\"\n7: \n8: from __future__ import annotations\n9: \n10: import json\n11: from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field\n12: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n13: from enum import Enum\n14: from pathlib import Path\n15: from typing import Any\n16: \n17: from .exceptions import ApprovalExpiredError, ApprovalTimeoutPolicyError\n18: \n19: \n20: class ApprovalStatus(str, Enum):\n21:     \"\"\"Status of an approval request.\"\"\"\n22: \n23:     PENDING = \"pending\"\n24:     APPROVED = \"approved\"\n25:     REJECTED = \"rejected\"\n26:     EXPIRED = \"expired\"\n27:     CANCELLED = \"cancelled\"\n28: \n29: \n30: class ApprovalReason(str, Enum):\n31:     \"\"\"Reason codes for approval resolution.\"\"\"\n32: \n33:     HUMAN_APPROVED = \"human_approved\"\n34:     HUMAN_REJECTED = \"human_rejected\"\n35:     TIMEOUT_EXPIRED = \"timeout_expired\"\n36:     AUTO_APPROVED = \"auto_approved\"\n37:     AUTO_REJECTED = \"auto_rejected\"\n38:     CANCELLED_BY_USER = \"cancelled_by_user\"\n39:     CANCELLED_BY_SYSTEM = \"cancelled_by_system\"\n40: \n41: \n42: @dataclass(slots=True)\n43: class ApprovalRequest:\n44:     \"\"\"Represents a human approval request with timeout policy.\n45: \n46:     Before: Approvals had no timeout or expiration tracking.\n47:     During: Each request has created_at, timeout_seconds, and status tracking.\n48:     After: Expired approvals are resolved with EXPIRED status and TIMEOUT_EXPIRED reason.\n49: \n50:     Attributes:\n51:         approval_id: Unique identifier for this approval request.\n52:         ticket_id: Ticket ID associated with this approval.\n53:         status: Current status of the approval request.\n54:         reason: Reason code for the resolution (if resolved).\n55:         created_at: ISO timestamp when the request was created.\n56:         timeout_seconds: Timeout in seconds for this approval.\n57:         resolved_at: ISO timestamp when the request was resolved (if resolved).\n58:         metadata: Additional metadata for the approval request.\n59:     \"\"\"\n60: \n61:     approval_id: str\n62:     ticket_id: str\n63:     status: ApprovalStatus = ApprovalStatus.PENDING\n64:     reason: ApprovalReason | None = None\n65:     created_at: str = field(\n66:         default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n67:     )\n68:     timeout_seconds: int = 300\n69:     resolved_at: str | None = None\n70:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n71: \n72:     def is_pending(self) -> bool:\n73:         \"\"\"Check if the approval is still pending.\"\"\"\n74:         return self.status == ApprovalStatus.PENDING\n75: \n76:     def is_resolved(self) -> bool:\n77:         \"\"\"Check if the approval has been resolved.\"\"\"\n78:         return self.status in {\n79:             ApprovalStatus.APPROVED,\n80:             ApprovalStatus.REJECTED,\n81:             ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n82:             ApprovalStatus.CANCELLED,\n83:         }\n84: \n85:     def is_expired(self, current_time: datetime | None = None) -> bool:\n86:         \"\"\"Check if the approval has expired based on timeout policy.\n87: \n88:         Args:\n89:             current_time: Optional current time for testing. Defaults to now.\n90: \n91:         Returns:\n92:             True if the approval has expired, False otherwise.\n93:         \"\"\"\n94:         if self.is_resolved():\n95:             return False\n96:         if current_time is None:\n97:             current_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n98:         created = datetime.fromisoformat(self.created_at)\n99:         elapsed = (current_time - created).total_seconds()\n100:         return elapsed >= self.timeout_seconds\n101: \n102:     def resolve(\n103:         self,\n104:         status: ApprovalStatus,\n105:         reason: ApprovalReason,\n106:         resolved_at: str | None = None,\n107:     ) -> None:\n108:         \"\"\"Resolve the approval request with a status and reason.\n109: \n110:         Args:\n111:             status: Final status of the approval.\n112:             reason: Reason code for the resolution.\n113:             resolved_at: Optional ISO timestamp. Defaults to now.\n114: \n115:         Raises:\n116:             ValueError: If attempting to resolve an already resolved request.\n117:         \"\"\"\n118:         if self.is_resolved():\n119:             raise ValueError(\n120:                 f\"Approval {self.approval_id} is already resolved with \"\n121:                 f\"status={self.status.value}, reason={self.reason}\"\n122:             )\n123:         self.status = status\n124:         self.reason = reason\n125:         self.resolved_at = resolved_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n126: \n127:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n128:         \"\"\"Convert to dictionary for JSON serialization.\"\"\"\n129:         result = asdict(self)\n130:         # Convert enums to their string values for JSON serialization\n131:         result[\"status\"] = self.status.value\n132:         if self.reason:\n133:             result[\"reason\"] = self.reason.value\n134:         return result\n135: \n136:     @classmethod\n137:     def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> ApprovalRequest:\n138:         \"\"\"Create from dictionary.\"\"\"\n139:         return cls(\n140:             approval_id=str(data.get(\"approval_id\", \"\")),\n141:             ticket_id=str(data.get(\"ticket_id\", \"\")),\n142:             status=ApprovalStatus(str(data.get(\"status\", \"pending\"))),\n143:             reason=ApprovalReason(data[\"reason\"]) if data.get(\"reason\") else None,\n144:             created_at=str(\n145:                 data.get(\"created_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())\n146:             ),\n147:             timeout_seconds=int(data.get(\"timeout_seconds\", 300)),\n148:             resolved_at=data.get(\"resolved_at\"),\n149:             metadata=dict(data.get(\"metadata\", {})),\n150:         )\n151: \n152: \n153: @dataclass(slots=True)\n154: class ApprovalPolicy:\n155:     \"\"\"Configurable timeout policy for approvals.\n156: \n157:     Before: Single hardcoded timeout for all approvals.\n158:     During: Policies can be defined per escalation type with different timeouts.\n159:     After: Each approval request uses the appropriate policy based on type.\n160: \n161:     Attributes:\n162:         policy_name: Name of the policy (e.g., \"default\", \"human_gate\", \"urgent\").\n163:         timeout_seconds: Timeout in seconds for this policy.\n164:         auto_resolve: Whether to auto-resolve expired approvals.\n165:         auto_resolve_status: Status to apply on auto-resolve (default: EXPIRED).\n166:     \"\"\"\n167: \n168:     policy_name: str = \"default\"\n169:     timeout_seconds: int = 300\n170:     auto_resolve: bool = True\n171:     auto_resolve_status: ApprovalStatus = ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED\n172: \n173:     def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n174:         \"\"\"Validate the policy configuration.\"\"\"\n175:         if self.timeout_seconds <= 0:\n176:             raise ApprovalTimeoutPolicyError(\n177:                 self.policy_name,\n178:                 f\"timeout_seconds must be positive, got {self.timeout_seconds}\",\n179:             )\n180:         if self.auto_resolve and self.auto_resolve_status not in {\n181:             ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n182:         }:\n183:             raise ApprovalTimeoutPolicyError(\n184:                 self.policy_name,\n185:                 f\"Invalid auto_resolve_status: {self.auto_resolve_status.value}\",\n186:             )\n187: \n188:     def create_request(\n189:         self,\n190:         approval_id: str,\n191:         ticket_id: str,\n192:         metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n193:     ) -> ApprovalRequest:\n194:         \"\"\"Create a new approval request with this policy's timeout.\n195: \n196:         Args:\n197:             approval_id: Unique ID for the new request.\n198:             ticket_id: Ticket ID associated with the request.\n199:             metadata: Optional metadata for the request.\n200: \n201:         Returns:\n202:             New ApprovalRequest with this policy's configuration.\n203:         \"\"\"\n204:         return ApprovalRequest(\n205:             approval_id=approval_id,\n206:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n207:             timeout_seconds=self.timeout_seconds,\n208:             metadata=metadata or {},\n209:         )\n210: \n211:     def check_and_expire(\n212:         self, request: ApprovalRequest, current_time: datetime | None = None\n213:     ) -> ApprovalRequest:\n214:         \"\"\"Check if a request has expired and auto-resolve if configured.\n215: \n216:         Args:\n217:             request: ApprovalRequest to check.\n218:             current_time: Optional current time for testing.\n219: \n220:         Returns:\n221:             The same request, potentially modified if auto-resolved.\n222: \n223:         Raises:\n224:             ApprovalExpiredError: If the request expired and auto_resolve is False.\n225:         \"\"\"\n226:         if not request.is_pending():\n227:             return request\n228: \n229:         if not request.is_expired(current_time):\n230:             return request\n231: \n232:         # Request has expired\n233:         if self.auto_resolve:\n234:             request.resolve(\n235:                 status=self.auto_resolve_status,\n236:                 reason=ApprovalReason.TIMEOUT_EXPIRED,\n237:             )\n238:         else:\n239:             raise ApprovalExpiredError(\n240:                 approval_id=request.approval_id,\n241:                 ticket_id=request.ticket_id,\n242:                 timeout_seconds=self.timeout_seconds,\n243:                 elapsed_seconds=(\n244:                     datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n245:                     - datetime.fromisoformat(request.created_at)\n246:                 ).total_seconds(),\n247:             )\n248: \n249:         return request\n250: \n251: \n252: class ApprovalStore:\n253:     \"\"\"Persistent store for approval requests.\n254: \n255:     Before: Approval state was ephemeral or scattered across files.\n256:     During: All approvals are persisted to a JSON store with atomic writes.\n257:     After: Approvals survive restarts and can be queried by ticket or status.\n258: \n259:     Attributes:\n260:         store_path: Path to the JSON store file.\n261:         policy: Default approval policy for new requests.\n262:     \"\"\"\n263: \n264:     def __init__(\n265:         self,\n266:         store_path: Path,\n267:         policy: ApprovalPolicy | None = None,\n268:     ):\n269:         self.store_path = Path(store_path)\n270:         self.policy = policy or ApprovalPolicy()\n271:         self.store_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n272: \n273:     def _read_store(self) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:\n274:         \"\"\"Read the approval store from disk.\"\"\"\n275:         if not self.store_path.exists():\n276:             return {}\n277:         try:\n278:             content = self.store_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n279:             return json.loads(content)\n280:         except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n281:             return {}\n282: \n283:     def _write_store(self, data: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> None:\n284:         \"\"\"Write the approval store atomically.\"\"\"\n285:         import os\n286:         import tempfile\n287: \n288:         fd, temp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(\n289:             dir=str(self.store_path.parent),\n290:             prefix=\"approvals_\",\n291:             suffix=\".json.tmp\",\n292:         )\n293:         try:\n294:             with os.fdopen(fd, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n295:                 json.dump(data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)\n296:             os.replace(temp_path, str(self.store_path))\n297:         except Exception:\n298:             import contextlib\n299: \n300:             with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n301:                 os.unlink(temp_path)\n302:             raise\n303: \n304:     def save(self, request: ApprovalRequest) -> None:\n305:         \"\"\"Save an approval request to the store.\"\"\"\n306:         store = self._read_store()\n307:         store[request.approval_id] = request.to_dict()\n308:         self._write_store(store)\n309: \n310:     def load(self, approval_id: str) -> ApprovalRequest | None:\n311:         \"\"\"Load an approval request by ID.\"\"\"\n312:         store = self._read_store()\n313:         data = store.get(approval_id)\n314:         return ApprovalRequest.from_dict(data) if data else None\n315: \n316:     def delete(self, approval_id: str) -> bool:\n317:         \"\"\"Delete an approval request by ID.\"\"\"\n318:         store = self._read_store()\n319:         if approval_id not in store:\n320:             return False\n321:         del store[approval_id]\n322:         self._write_store(store)\n323:         return True\n324: \n325:     def list_by_ticket(self, ticket_id: str) -> list[ApprovalRequest]:\n326:         \"\"\"List all approval requests for a ticket.\"\"\"\n327:         store = self._read_store()\n328:         return [\n329:             ApprovalRequest.from_dict(data)\n330:             for data in store.values()\n331:             if data.get(\"ticket_id\") == ticket_id\n332:         ]\n333: \n334:     def list_pending(self) -> list[ApprovalRequest]:\n335:         \"\"\"List all pending approval requests.\"\"\"\n336:         store = self._read_store()\n337:         requests = []\n338:         for data in store.values():\n339:             request = ApprovalRequest.from_dict(data)\n340:             if request.is_pending():\n341:                 requests.append(request)\n342:         return requests\n343: \n344:     def check_and_expire_all(self) -> list[ApprovalRequest]:\n345:         \"\"\"Check all pending requests and expire those past timeout.\n346: \n347:         Returns:\n348:             List of requests that were expired in this call.\n349:         \"\"\"\n350:         expired_requests = []\n351:         store = self._read_store()\n352:         modified = False\n353: \n354:         for approval_id, data in store.items():\n355:             request = ApprovalRequest.from_dict(data)\n356:             if request.is_pending() and request.is_expired():\n357:                 self.policy.check_and_expire(request)\n358:                 store[approval_id] = request.to_dict()\n359:                 expired_requests.append(request)\n360:                 modified = True\n361: \n362:         if modified:\n363:             self._write_store(store)\n364: \n365:         return expired_requests\n366: \n367:     def create_request(\n368:         self,\n369:         approval_id: str,\n370:         ticket_id: str,\n371:         metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n372:         policy: ApprovalPolicy | None = None,\n373:     ) -> ApprovalRequest:\n374:         \"\"\"Create and persist a new approval request.\n375: \n376:         Args:\n377:             approval_id: Unique ID for the new request.\n378:             ticket_id: Ticket ID associated with the request.\n379:             metadata: Optional metadata for the request.\n380:             policy: Optional policy override for this request.\n381: \n382:         Returns:\n383:             The newly created ApprovalRequest, already persisted.\n384:         \"\"\"\n385:         effective_policy = policy or self.policy\n386:         request = effective_policy.create_request(\n387:             approval_id=approval_id,\n388:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n389:             metadata=metadata,\n390:         )\n391:         self.save(request)\n392:         return request\n393: \n394:     def resolve_request(\n395:         self,\n396:         approval_id: str,\n397:         status: ApprovalStatus,\n398:         reason: ApprovalReason,\n399:     ) -> ApprovalRequest | None:\n400:         \"\"\"Resolve an existing approval request.\n401: \n402:         Args:\n403:             approval_id: ID of the request to resolve.\n404:             status: Final status to apply.\n405:             reason: Reason code for the resolution.\n406: \n407:         Returns:\n408:             The resolved request, or None if not found.\n409:         \"\"\"\n410:         request = self.load(approval_id)\n411:         if request is None:\n412:             return None\n413: \n414:         request.resolve(status=status, reason=reason)\n415:         self.save(request)\n416:         return request\n\n(End of file - total 416 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"\nApproval
