\"\"\"Tests para HUMAN_GATE timeout y expiracion canonica.\n2: \n3: WP-2026-146: Human gate approval timeout wiring.\n4: \"\"\"\n5: \n6: import json\n7: import sys\n8: from datetime import datetime, sys.path.insert(0, str(_agent_dir.parent))\n18: \n19: import pytest\n20: \n21: \n22: @pytest.fixture\n23: def temp_runtime_dir(tmp_path):\n24:     \"\"\"Crear directorio temporal para \"timeout_seconds\": 180,\n37:             \"max_attempts\": 5,\n38:         },\n39:     }\n40:     config_path = tmp_path / \"agents.json\"\n41:     config_path.write_text(json.dumps(config), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n42:     return config_path\n43: \n44: \n45: class TestHumanGateTimeoutConfig:\n46:     \"\"\"Tests para configuracion de timeout de HUMAN_GATE.\"\"\"\n47: \n48:     def get_human_gate_timeout\n51: \n52:         with patch(\"agent_controller.AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH\", temp_agents_config):\n53:             timeout = get_human_gate_timeout()\n54:             assert timeout json.loads(store_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n225:             approval_id = next(iter(store_data.keys()))\n226: \n227:             # Set created_at to 10 minutes ago (past the 180s timeout)\n228:             past_time = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=10)).isoformat()\n229:             store_data[approval_id][\"created_at\"] = past_time\n230:             store_path.write_text(json.dumps(store_data), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n231: \n232:             # Run check_and_expire_all (simulating supervisor loop)\n233:             expired = store.check_and_expire_all()\n234: \n235:             # Verify the request was expired\n236:             assert len(expired) == 1\n237:             expired_request = expired[0]\n238:             assert expired_request.status == ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED\n239:             assert expired_request.reason == ApprovalReason.TIMEOUT_EXPIRED\n240: \n241:     def \n258:             # Run check_and_expire_all immediately (should not expire)\n259:             expired = store.check_and_expire_all()\n260: \n261:             # Verify no requests were expired\n262: \"pending\"\n269: \n270:     def test_approval_survives_restart(self, temp_runtime_dir, temp_agents_config):\n271:         \"\"\"Un ApprovalRequest debe sobrevivir a un restart {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779824981683,"sessionID":"ses_19a2af79effeISQ1K84oR5HC46","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_cd678bf0efb74137ba634eb8","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\bus\\supervisor.py","limit":200},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\bus\\supervisor.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: from __future__ import annotations\n2: \n3: import json\n4: import os\n5: import re\n6: import sys\n7: import tempfile\n8: from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field\n9: from datetime import datetime\n10: from pathlib import Path\n11: \n12: from .approval import ApprovalPolicy, ApprovalReason, ApprovalStatus, ApprovalStore\n13: from .event_bus import EventBus\n14: from .exceptions import ConcurrentStateError\n15: from .state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n16: \n17: \n18: # Non-terminal states: supervisor must preserve active_ticket while bus is in these states.\n19: # Execution log may lag behind the bus, so we never clear active_ticket based solely on\n20: # execution_log status when the bus confirms a non-terminal state.\n21: NON_TERMINAL_STATES = frozenset(\n22:     {\n23:         TicketState.READY_FOR_REVIEW,\n24:         TicketState.READY_TO_CLOSE,\n25:         TicketState.IN_PROGRESS,\n26:         TicketState.BLOCKED,\n27:         TicketState.HUMAN_GATE,\n28:     }\n29: )\n30: \n31: # Do not relaunch Builder once the ticket has crossed into a gate/closeout state.\n32: RELAUNCH_BLOCKED_STATES = frozenset(\n33:     {\n34:         TicketState.HUMAN_GATE,\n35:         TicketState.READY_TO_CLOSE,\n36:         TicketState.COMPLETED,\n37:     }\n38: )\n39: \n40: \n41: @dataclass(slots=True)\n42: class SupervisorState:\n43:     active_ticket: str | None = None\n44:     completed_tickets: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n45:     last_action: str = \"\"\n46:     last_processed_sequence: int = 0\n47:     loop_current_round: int = 0\n48:     loop_max_rounds: int = 0\n49:     _revision: int | None = field(default=None, repr=False, compare=False)\n50: \n51: \n52: class SequentialTicketSupervisor:\n53:     def __init__(\n54:         self,\n55:         project_root: Path,\n56:         collaboration_dir: Path | None = None,\n57:         runtime_dir: Path | None = None,\n58:         auto_sync: bool = True,\n59:     ):\n60:         self.project_root = Path(project_root)\n61:         self.collaboration_dir = Path(\n62:             collaboration_dir or self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n63:         )\n64:         self.runtime_dir = Path(runtime_dir or self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\")\n65:         self.runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n66:         self.turn_path = self.collaboration_dir / \"TURN.md\"\n67:         self.work_plan_path = self.collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\"\n68:         self.execution_log_path = self.collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\"\n69:         self.state_path_file = self.collaboration_dir / \"STATE.md\"\n70:         self.review_queue_path = self.collaboration_dir / \"review_queue.md\"\n71:         self.notifications_path = self.collaboration_dir / \"notifications.md\"\n72:         self.event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=self.runtime_dir / \"events\")\n73:         self.auto_sync = auto_sync\n74:         self.state_path = self.runtime_dir / \"supervisor_state.json\"\n75:         self.supervisor_lock_path = self.runtime_dir / \"supervisor_lock.txt\"\n76:         self._lock_fd: int | None = None\n77:         self._supervisor_lock_held: bool = False\n78: \n79:     def save_state(self, state: SupervisorState) -> None:\n80:         self.state_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n81:         data = asdict(state)\n82:         data.pop(\"_revision\", None)\n83:         content = json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)\n84:         new_rev = self.write_artifact_atomic(\n85:             self.state_path,\n86:             content,\n87:             expected_revision=getattr(state, \"_revision\", None),\n88:         )\n89:         state._revision = new_rev\n90: \n91:     def _compute_revision(self, content: str) -> int:\n92:         \"\"\"Compute a revision number from content hash.\n93: \n94:         Before: State writes had no revision tracking.\n95:         During: Revision is computed as a hash-based integer from content.\n96:         After: Returns monotonically increasing revision number for OCC.\n97:         \"\"\"\n98:         import hashlib\n99: \n100:         return int(hashlib.sha256(content.encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()[:8], 16)\n101: \n102:     def _read_artifact_with_revision(\n103:         self, artifact_path: Path\n104:     ) -> tuple[str, int | None]:\n105:         \"\"\"Read artifact content and compute its current revision.\n106: \n107:         Args:\n108:             artifact_path: Path to the artifact file.\n109: \n110:         Returns:\n111:             Tuple of (content, revision) where revision is None if file doesn't exist.\n112:         \"\"\"\n113:         if not artifact_path.exists():\n114:             return \"\", None\n115:         content = artifact_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n116:         revision = self._compute_revision(content)\n117:         return content, revision\n118: \n119:     def write_artifact_atomic(\n120:         self,\n121:         artifact_path: Path,\n122:         new_content: str,\n123:         expected_revision: int | None = None,\n124:         ticket_id: str | None = None,\n125:         max_retries: int = 3,\n126:         retry_delay_ms: int = 50,\n127:     ) -> int:\n128:         \"\"\"Write an artifact atomically with optimistic concurrency control.\n129: \n130:         Before: State writes could overwrite concurrent modifications silently.\n131:         During: Writer provides expectedRevision; bus compares with current revision\n132:                 and retries on conflict up to max_retries.\n133:         After: Write succeeds with new revision, or raises ConcurrentStateError.\n134: \n135:         Args:\n136:             artifact_path: Path to the artifact file.\n137:             new_content: New content to write.\n138:             expected_revision: Expected current revision (None for blind in milliseconds.\n142: \n143:         Returns:\n144:             The new revision number after successful write.\n145: \n146:         Raises:\n147:             ConcurrentStateError: If revision mismatch persists after all retries.\n148:         \"\"\"\n149:         import time\n150: \n151:         artifact_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n152:         lock_path = os.open(str(lock_path), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)\n158:             except FileExistsError:\n159:                 # Stale lock recovery: if lock is older than 30 seconds, break it\n160:                 try:\n161:                     if time.time() - os.path.getmtime(lock_path) > 30.0:\n162:                         os.unlink(lock_path)\n163:                         continue  # retry I have verified the implementation against all acceptance criteria:\n\n**Verification Summary:**\n\n1. **`HUMAN_GATE` carries explicit timeout metadata at handoff** ✓\n   - `get_human_gate_timeout()` (lines 195-210) reads from `agents.json` with fallback\n   - `_create_human_gate_approval_request()` (lines 802-881) creates ApprovalRequest with timeout metadata\n\n2. **The escalation persists an `ApprovalRequest` so the gate survives restarts** ✓\n   - Line 2503-2504: `_create_human_gate_approval_request(ticket_id)` called when escalating to HUMAN_GATE\n   - ApprovalStore persists to `runtime/approvals/store.json`\n\n3. **An expired `HUMAN_GATE` resolves automatically through canonical approval-resolution path** ✓\n   - `bus/supervisor.py` line 1050: `store.check_and_expire_all()` in supervisor loop\n   - `bus/approval.py` lines 353-372: Expiration logic with `APPROVAL_RESOLVED` events\n\n4. **A fresh `HUMAN_GATE` does not expire early** ✓\n   - Tests verify non-expired requests remain pending (test_non_expired_approval_remains_pending)\n\n5. **The timeout value is sourced from configuration with a documented fallback** ✓\n   - `agents.json` has `timeout_seconds: 180` configured\n   - Fallback `HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK = 300` documented at line 177\n\n6. **The existing `resume-human-gate` path remains valid** ✓\n   - Lines 2666-2728: `--resume-human-gate` command unchanged, works from HUMAN_GATE state\n\n7. **Canonical validation passes** ✓\n   - Execution log shows: `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`: No errors\n   - 13 tests passed in 0.20s\n   - Ruff checks passed\n\n**Anti-pattern check:**\n- No mock drift detected in tests\n- No floor assertions (thresholds are meaningful)\n- No zero-logic wrappers (functions have substantive logic)\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779825029245,"end":1779825037912}}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779831360546,"sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_9162a98464464d1aa26cee0a","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Work Plan - WP-2026-147\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-147\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** code\n7: - **Titulo:** Graph context adapter\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: Build a lightweight graph-context adapter that reads the already-generated `graphify-out/graph.json` and `GRAPH_REPORT.md` for a destination repository, then emits a short `## Project Context` block for the active ticket. The adapter should stay in Python stdlib, avoid any new graph build pipeline, and extract only the minimum context needed to cut token usage.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `scripts/graph_context.py` will read `graphify-out/graph.json` plus `GRAPH_REPORT.md` and turn them into a compact ticket-scoped context block.\n15: - The adapter will stay in Python stdlib only and reuse the existing graphify output instead of creating a new graph pipeline.\n16: - The output should focus on the active ticket's files, immediate neighbors, and a short summary of the relevant corpus.\n17: - `agent_controller.py` will optionally surface a compact `## Project Context` summary from the graph context adapter when generating work plans for destination projects.\n18: - The design is inspired by graph-based project understanding tools, but intentionally keeps the implementation lightweight and read-only.\n19: - `TURN.md` stays under controller ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n20: - No LLM-per-file analysis is introduced in this ticket.\n21: - The change is about better destination understanding, not a broader runtime refactor.\n22: \n23: ## Files Likely Touched\n24: - `scripts/graph_context.py`\n25: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n26: - `tests/unit/test_graph_context.py`\n27: \n28: ## Fases\n29: 1. Build a lightweight adapter that reads `graphify-out/graph.json` and `GRAPH_REPORT.md`.\n30: 2. Extract a ticket-scoped `## Project Context` block with the relevant files and immediate graph neighbors.\n31: 3. Add a minimal `agent_controller.py` hook to inject the context block when the graph output exists.\n32: 4. Add focused unit tests for parsing, filtering, and compact output generation.\n33: 5. Confirm the hook is optional and the ticket flow still works if no graph output exists yet.\n34: \n35: ## Calidad\n36: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_graph_context.py -q`\n37: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n38: \n39: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n40: - The adapter produces a compact `## Project Context` summary for the destination project.\n41: - The summary is derived from existing `graphify-out` artifacts, not a new graph build.\n42: - The adapter is deterministic and uses Python stdlib only.\n43: - The emitted `## Project Context` block does not exceed 30 lines.\n44: - The optional `## Project Context` injection keeps the ticket context concise.\n45: - The existing ticket flow still works when no project map is available.\n46: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n\n(End of file - total 46 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-147\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-147\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Graph context adapter\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nBuild a lightweight graph-context adapter that reads the already-generated `graphify-out/graph.json` and `GRAPH_REPORT.md` for a destination repository, then emits a short `## Project Context` block for the active ticket. The adapter should stay in Python stdlib, avoid any new graph build pipeline, and extract only the minimum context needed to cut token usage.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `scripts/graph_context.py` will read `graphify-out/graph.json` plus `GRAPH_REPORT.md` and turn them into a compact ticket-scoped context block.\n- The adapter will stay in Python stdlib only and reuse the existing graphify output instead of creating a new graph pipeline.\n- The output should focus on the active ticket's files, immediate neighbors, and a short summary of the relevant corpus.\n- `agent_controller.py` will optionally surface a compact `## Project Context` summary from the graph context adapter when generating work plans for destination projects.\n- The design is inspired by graph-based project understanding tools, but intentionally keeps the implementation lightweight and read-only.\n- `TURN.md` stays under controller ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n- No LLM-per-file analysis is introduced in this ticket.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779831360432,"end":1779831360543}},"id":"prt_e66373484001yjcrKhyeqtzW0i","sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","messageID":"msg_e6636c7ef001G91xMc2oXkxQWG"}}

Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The Builder runs the existing suite, sees it pass, and declares quality gates satisfied — but the new functions introduced in the diff have no direct tests. Th... (unknown)\n30: - [2026-05-25] deliverable_type classification for scaffolding tickets: when Files Likely Touched contains only structural non-Python files (.gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic, the co... (unknown)\n31: - [2026-05-25] Validator evidence gate: when a work_plan explicitly declares a validator as a quality gate (skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), the Manager must find explicit outpu... (unknown)\n32: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard. When a method acquires an exclusive resource (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire, lock-file creation) AND can be reached f... (unknown)\n33: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts. When a method changes from returning implicit None to returning explicit bool, all callers must be updated from generic t... (unknown)\n34: \n35: --- work_plan.md ---\n36: # Work Plan - WP-2026-147\n37: \n38: ## Metadata\n39: - **ID:** WP-2026-147\n40: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n41: - **deliverable_type:** code\n42: - **Titulo:** Graph context adapter\n43: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n44: \n45: ## Objetivo\n46: Build a lightweight graph-context adapter that reads the already-generated `graphify-out/graph.json` and `GRAPH_REPORT.md` for a destination repository, then emits a short `## Project Context` block for the active ticket. The adapter should stay in Python stdlib, avoid any new graph build pipeline, and extract only the minimum context needed to cut token usage.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - `scripts/graph_context.py` will read `graphify-out/graph.json` plus `GRAPH_REPORT.md` and turn them into a compact ticket-scoped context block.\n50: - The adapter will stay in Python stdlib only and reuse the existing graphify output instead of creating a new graph pipeline.\n51: - The output should focus on the active ticket's files, immediate neighbors, and a short summary of the relevant corpus.\n52: - `agent_controller.py` will optionally surface a compact `## Project Context` summary from the graph context adapter when generating work plans for destination projects.\n53: - The design is inspired by graph-based project understanding tools, but intentionally keeps the implementation lightweight and read-only.\n54: - `TURN.md` stays under controller ownership and is out of scope for this ticket.\n55: - No LLM-per-file analysis is introduced in this ticket.\n56: - The change is about better destination understanding, not a broader runtime refactor.\n57: \n58: ## Files Likely Touched\n59: - `scripts/graph_context.py`\n60: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n61: - `tests/unit/test_graph_context.py`\n62: \n63: ## Fases\n64: 1. Build a lightweight adapter that reads `graphify-out/graph.json` and `GRAPH_REPORT.md`.\n65: 2. Extract a ticket-scoped `## Project Context` block with the relevant files and immediate graph neighbors.\n66: 3. Add a minimal `agent_controller.py` hook to inject the context block when the graph output exists.\n67: 4. Add focused unit tests for parsing, filtering, and compact output generation.\n68: 5. Confirm the hook is optional and the ticket flow still works if no graph output exists yet.\n69: \n70: ## Calidad\n71: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_graph_context.py -q`\n72: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n73: \n74: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n75: - The adapter produces a compact `## Project Context` summary for the destination project.\n76: - The summary is derived from existing `graphify-out` artifacts, not a new graph build.\n77: - The adapter is deterministic and uses Python stdlib only.\n78: - The emitted `## Project Context` block does not exceed 30 lines.\n79: - The optional `## Project Context` injection keeps the ticket context concise.\n80: - The existing ticket flow still works when no project map is available.\n81: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n82: \n83: \n84: --- STATE.md ---\n85: # State - WP-2026-147\n86: \n87: Plan Activo: WP-2026-147\n88: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n89: Rol activo: BUILDER\n90: \n91: Resumen:\n92: WP-2026-147 en progreso: el Builder construira un adaptador ligero que derive `## Project Context` desde `graphify-out` para el proyecto destino.\n93: \n94: Notas:\n95: - El alcance es code.\n96: - `TURN.md` sigue bajo control del controller.\n97: - El adaptador debe ser stdlib-only y no introducir dependencias pesadas.\n98: \n99: \n100: --- TURN.md ---\n101: # TURNO ACTUAL\n102: \n103: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-26 23:35:24\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## Agente Activo\n108: \n109: | Campo | Valor |\n110: |-------|-------|\n111: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n112: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-147 |\n113: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n114: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n115: \n116: ---\n117: \n118: ## Instruccion\n119: \n120: > Builder completo WP-2026-147. Revisa el trabajo.\n121: \n122: ---\n123: \n124: ## Archivos a Leer\n125: \n126: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n127: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n128: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n129: \n130: ---\n131: \n132: ## Estado del Sistema\n133: \n134: | Archivo | Estado |\n135: |---------|--------|\n136: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n137: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n138: \n139: ---\n140: \n141: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n142: \n143: \n144: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n145: [execution_log section for WP-2026-147 not found]\n146: \n147: --- git provenance ---\n148: [no new commits since origin/main]\n149: \n150: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n151: Available skills for this role:\n152:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n153:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n154:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n155: \n156: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n157: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n158: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n159: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n160: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n161: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n162: - .session_state.json\n163: \n164: \n165: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n166: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n167: \n168: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n169: \n170: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n171: DECISION: APPROVE\n172: \n173: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n174: \n175: ## SUMMARY\n176: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n177: \n178: ## BLOCKERS\n179: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n180: \n181: ## SUGGESTIONS\n182: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n183: \n184: DECISION: CHANGES\n185: \n186: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n187: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 187 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-147. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-147_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779831360491,"end":1779831360598}},"id":"prt_e6637477d001Kng2oPhM8LRen7","sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","messageID":"msg_e6636c7ef001G91xMc2oXkxQWG"}}

\"\"\"\n2: Graph Context Adapter - Lightweight ticket-scoped context extractor.\n3: \n4: This adapter reads existing graphify-out artifacts (graph.json and GRAPH_REPORT.md)\n5: and emits a compact ## Project Context block for the active ticket.\n6: \n7: Before:\n8:     - Requires graphify-out/graph.json and graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md to exist.\n9:     - Reads the active work_plan.md to determine ticket scope.\n10: \n11: During:\n12:     - Parses the graph structure to identify file nodes and edges.\n13:     - Extracts collaboration files relevant to the active ticket.\n14:     - Computes immediate graph neighbors for context files.\n15:     - Generates a deterministic, compact summary (max 30 lines).\n16: \n17: After:\n18:     - Returns a string containing the ## Project Context markdown block.\n19:     - Raises FileNotFoundError if graphify artifacts are missing.\n20:     - Raises ValueError if graph.json is malformed.\n21: \"\"\"\n22: \n23: import json\n24: import re\n25: import sys\n26: from pathlib import Path\n27: from typing import Any\n28: \n29: \n30: def get_project_root() -> Path:\n31:     \"\"\"Get the project root directory.\"\"\"\n32:     return Path(__file__).parent.parent\n33: \n34: \n35: def get_graphify_dir() -> Path:\n36:     \"\"\"Get the graphify output directory.\"\"\"\n37:     return get_project_root() / \"graphify-out\"\n38: \n39: \n40: def get_collaboration_dir() -> Path:\n41:     \"\"\"Get the collaboration directory.\"\"\"\n42:     return get_project_root() / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n43: \n44: \n45: def load_graph() -> dict[str, Any]:\n46:     \"\"\"\n47:     Load the graph.json file.\n48: \n49:     Before: graph.json must exist in graphify-out/.\n50:     During: Reads and parses JSON.\n51:     After: Returns parsed graph data or raises FileNotFoundError/ValueError.\n52:     \"\"\"\n53:     graph_path = get_graphify_dir() / \"graph.json\"\n54:     if not graph_path.exists():\n55:         raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Graph file not found: {graph_path}\")\n56: \n57:     with open(graph_path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n58:         try:\n59:             return json.load(f)\n60:         except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n61:             raise ValueError(f\"Invalid JSON in graph.json: {e}\") from e\n62: \n63: \n64: def load_graph_report() -> str:\n65:     \"\"\"\n66:     Load the GRAPH_REPORT.md file.\n67: \n68:     Before: GRAPH_REPORT.md must exist in graphify-out/.\n69:     During: Reads the markdown file.\n70:     After: Returns file content or raises FileNotFoundError.\n71:     \"\"\"\n72:     report_path = get_graphify_dir() / \"GRAPH_REPORT.md\"\n73:     if not report_path.exists():\n74:         raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Graph report not found: {report_path}\")\n75: \n76:     return report_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n77: \n78: \n79: def load_work_plan() -> str:\n80:     \"\"\"\n81:     Load the active work_plan.md file.\n82: \n83:     Before: work_plan.md must exist in .agent/collaboration/.\n84:     During: Reads the markdown file.\n85:     After: Returns file content or raises FileNotFoundError.\n86:     \"\"\"\n87:     work_plan_path = get_collaboration_dir() / \"work_plan.md\"\n88:     if not work_plan_path.exists():\n89:         raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Work plan not found: {work_plan_path}\")\n90: \n91:     return work_plan_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n92: \n93: \n94: def extract_active_ticket_id(work_plan_content: str) -> str | None:\n95:     \"\"\"\n96:     Extract the active ticket ID from work_plan.md.\n97: \n98:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n99:     During: Searches for WP-YYYY-NNN pattern in metadata section.\n100:     After: Returns ticket ID (e.g., 'WP-2026-147') or None if not found.\n101:     \"\"\"\n102:     match = re.search(r\"\\*\\*ID:\\*\\*\\s*(WP-\\d{4}-\\d{3})\", work_plan_content)\n103:     if match:\n104:         return match.group(1)\n105: \n106:     match = re.search(r\"#\\s*Work Plan\\s*-\\s*(WP-\\d{4}-\\d{3})\", work_plan_content)\n107:     if match:\n108:         return match.group(1)\n109: \n110:     return None\n111: \n112: \n113: def extract_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n114:     \"\"\"\n115:     Parse Files Likely Touched section from work_plan.md.\n116: \n117:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n118:     During: Finds the section and extracts file paths.\n119:     After: Returns set of file paths or empty set if section not found.\n120:     \"\"\"\n121:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n122:     in_section = False\n123:     files = set()\n124: \n125:     for line in lines:\n126:         stripped = line.strip()\n127:         if stripped.startswith(\"## Files Likely Touched\"):\n128:             in_section = True\n129:             continue\n130:         if in_section:\n131:             if stripped.startswith(\"## \"):\n132:                 break\n133:             if stripped.startswith(\"- \"):\n134:                 file_path = stripped[2:].strip()\n135:                 if file_path and not file_path.startswith(\"#\"):\n136:                     files.add(file_path)\n137: \n138:     return files\n139: \n140: \n141: def get_immediate_neighbors(\n142:     graph: dict[str, Any], target_files: set[str]\n143: ) -> set[str]:\n144:     \"\"\"\n145:     Get immediate neighbors (connected files) from the graph.\n146: \n147:     Before: Requires graph dict with 'edges' key and target file set.\n148:     During: Iterates through edges to find connections to/from target files.\n149:     After: Returns set of neighbor file paths (excluding targets themselves).\n150:     \"\"\"\n151:     neighbors = set()\n152:     edges = graph.get(\"edges\", [])\n153: \n154:     for edge in edges:\n155:         source = edge.get(\"source\", \"\")\n156:         target = edge.get(\"target\", \"\")\n157: \n158:         if source in target_files and target not in target_files:\n159:             neighbors.add(target)\n160:         elif target in target_files and source not in target_files:\n161:             neighbors.add(source)\n162: \n163:     return neighbors\n164: \n165: \n166: def categorize_files(\n167:     nodes: dict[str, Any], files: set[str]\n168: ) -> dict[str, list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]]:\n169:     \"\"\"\n170:     Categorize files by type (Python, Markdown, Other).\n171: \n172:     Before: Requires nodes dict and set of file paths.\n173:     During: Groups files by their type field from graph nodes.\n174:     After: Returns dict with 'python', 'markdown', 'other' keys.\n175:     \"\"\"\n176:     categorized: dict[str, list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]] = {\n177:         \"python\": [],\n178:         \"markdown\": [],\n179:         \"other\": [],\n180:     }\n181: \n182:     for file_path in files:\n183:         node_info = nodes.get(file_path, {})\n184:         file_type = node_info.get(\"type\", \"unknown\")\n185: \n186:         if file_type == \"python\":\n187:             categorized[\"python\"].append((file_path, node_info))\n188:         elif file_type == \"markdown\":\n189:             categorized[\"markdown\"].append((file_path, node_info))\n190:         else:\n191:             categorized[\"other\"].append((file_path, node_info))\n192: \n193:     return categorized\n194: \n195: \n196: def format_file_size(size_bytes: int) -> str:\n197:     \"\"\"Format file size in human-readable form.\"\"\"\n198:     if size_bytes >= 1024 * 1024:\n199:         return f\"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB\"\n200:     elif size_bytes >= 1024:\n201:         return f\"{size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB\"\n202:     else:\n203:         return f\"{size_bytes} B\"\n204: \n205: \n206: def _format_context_section(\n207:     lines: list[str],\n208:     categorized: dict[str, list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]],\n209:     section_name: str,\n210:     file_type: str,\n211:     remaining_lines: int,\n212: ) -> int:\n213:     \"\"\"\n214:     Format a section of files (Python or Markdown) for the context.\n215: \n216:     Returns the updated remaining_lines count.\n217:     \"\"\"\n218:     files = categorized.get(file_type, [])\n219:     if not files or remaining_lines <= 0:\n220:         return remaining_lines\n221: \n222:     lines.append(f\"### {section_name}\")\n223:     for file_path, info in sorted(files)[:5]:\n224:         size = info.get(\"size_bytes\", 0)\n225:         lines.append(f\"- `{file_path}` ({format_file_size(size)})\")\n226:         remaining_lines -= 1\n227: \n228:     if len(files) > 5:\n229:         lines.append(f\"- ... and {len(files) - 5} more {section_name.replace(' Files', '').lower()} files\")\n230:         remaining_lines -= 1\n231: \n232:     lines.append(\"\")\n233:     return remaining_lines\n234: \n235: \n236: def _build_context_lines(\n237:     ticket_id: str | None,\n238:     all_context_files: set[str],\n239:     target_files: set[str],\n240:     categorized: dict[str, list[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]],\n241:     max_lines: int,\n242: ) -> list[str]:\n243:     \"\"\"Build the context lines list.\"\"\"\n244:     lines: list[str] = []\n245:     lines.append(\"## Project Context\")\n246:     lines.append(\"\")\n247: \n248:     if ticket_id:\n249:         lines.append(f\"- **Ticket:** {ticket_id}\")\n250: \n251:     total_files = len(all_context_files)\n252:     lines.append(f\"- **Scope:** {total_files} file(s) in context\")\n253:     lines.append(\"\")\n254: \n255:     remaining_lines = max_lines - len(lines) - 5\n256: \n257:     remaining_lines = _format_context_section(\n258:         lines, categorized, \"Python Files\", \"python\", remaining_lines\n259:     )\n260: \n261:     remaining_lines = _format_context_section(\n262:         lines, categorized, \"Markdown Files\", \"markdown\", remaining_lines\n263:     )\n264: \n265:     if remaining_lines > 2 and target_files:\n266:         lines.append(\"### Ticket Scope (Files Likely Touched)\")\n267:         for file_path in sorted(target_files):\n268:             if remaining_lines <= 1:\n269:                 break\n270:             lines.append(f\"- `{file_path}`\")\n271:             remaining_lines -= 1\n272: \n273:     while len(lines) > max_lines:\n274:         if lines and (lines[-1].startswith(\"-\") or lines[-1] == \"\" or lines[-1].startswith(\"###\")):\n275:             lines.pop()\n276:         else:\n277:             break\n278: \n279:     return lines\n280: \n281: \n282: def generate_project_context(\n283:     max_lines: int = 30,\n284: ) -> str:\n285:     \"\"\"\n286:     Generate a compact ## Project Context block for the active ticket.\n287: \n288:     Before:\n289:         - graphify-out/graph.json must exist.\n290:         - graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md must exist.\n291:         - .agent/collaboration/work_plan.md must exist.\n292: \n293:     During:\n294:         - Loads and parses all input files.\n295:         - Extracts active ticket ID and Files Likely Touched.\n296:         - Computes immediate graph neighbors.\n297:         - Categorizes files by type.\n298:         - Formats a compact summary within line limit.\n299: \n300:     After:\n301:         - Returns markdown string with ## Project Context block.\n302:         - Block does not exceed max_lines (default 30).\n303:         - Raises FileNotFoundError or ValueError on input errors.\n304:     \"\"\"\n305:     graph = load_graph()\n306:     nodes = graph.get(\"nodes\", {})\n307: \n308:     work_plan_content = load_work_plan()\n309:     ticket_id = extract_active_ticket_id(work_plan_content)\n310:     target_files = extract_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n311: \n312:     neighbors = get_immediate_neighbors(graph, target_files)\n313:     all_context_files = target_files | neighbors\n314: \n315:     categorized = categorize_files(nodes, all_context_files)\n316:     lines = _build_context_lines(ticket_id, all_context_files, target_files, categorized, max_lines)\n317: \n318:     return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n319: \n320: \n321: def generate_context_for_destination(\n322:     dest_project_root: Path,\n323:     max_lines: int = 30,\n324: ) -> str | None:\n325:     \"\"\"\n326:     Generate project context for an external destination project.\n327: \n328:     Before:\n329:         - dest_project_root must be a valid path with graphify-out/.\n330:         - graphify artifacts must exist in destination.\n331: \n332:     During:\n333:         - Loads graph and work_plan from destination.\n334:         - Generates context block using same logic as generate_project_context.\n335: \n336:     After:\n337:         - Returns context block string or None if artifacts missing.\n338:     \"\"\"\n339:     graph_path = dest_project_root / \"graphify-out\" / \"graph.json\"\n340:     work_plan_path = dest_project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n341: \n342:     if not graph_path.exists() or not work_plan_path.exists():\n343:         return None\n344: \n345:     try:\n346:         with open(graph_path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n347:             graph = json.load(f)\n348:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n349:         return None\n350: \n351:     try:\n352:         work_plan_content = work_plan_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n353:     except OSError:\n354:         return None\n355: \n356:     nodes = graph.get(\"nodes\", {})\n357:     ticket_id = extract_active_ticket_id(work_plan_content)\n358:     target_files = extract_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n359:     neighbors = get_immediate_neighbors(graph, target_files)\n360:     all_context_files = target_files | neighbors\n361:     categorized = categorize_files(nodes, all_context_files)\n362: \n363:     lines = _build_context_lines(ticket_id, all_context_files, target_files, categorized, max_lines)\n364:     return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n365: \n366: \n367: def main() -> None:\n368:     \"\"\"Main entry point for CLI usage.\"\"\"\n369:     import argparse\n370: \n371:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n372:         description=\"Generate compact project context from graphify artifacts\"\n373:     )\n374:     parser.add_argument(\n375:         \"--max-lines\",\n376:         type=int,\n377:         default=30,\n378:         help=\"Maximum lines in output (default: 30)\",\n379:     )\n380:     parser.add_argument(\n381:         \"--destination\",\n382:         type=str,\n383:         help=\"Path to destination project (optional)\",\n384:     )\n385: \n386:     args = parser.parse_args()\n387: \n388:     try:\n389:         if args.destination:\n390:             dest_root = Path(args.destination)\n391:             context = generate_context_for_destination(dest_root, args.max_lines)\n392:             if context is None:\n393:                 print(\n394:                     \"No graphify artifacts found in destination.\", file=sys.stderr\n395:                 )\n396:                 sys.exit(1)\n397:         else:\n398:             context = generate_project_context(args.max_lines)\n399: \n400:         print(context)\n401:     except FileNotFoundError as e:\n402:         print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n403:         sys.exit(1)\n404:     except ValueError as e:\n405:         print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n406:         sys.exit(1)\n407: \n408: \n409: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n410:     main()\n\n(End of file - total 410 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"\nGraph Context Adapter - Lightweight ticket-scoped context extractor.\n\nThis adapter reads existing graphify-out artifacts
