ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_AGENT_DIR), str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # WP-2026-146: Import approval system for HUMAN_GATE timeout\n100: try:\n101:     from bus.approval import (\n102:         ApprovalPolicy,\n103:         ApprovalReason,\n104:         ApprovalStatus,\n105:         ApprovalStore,\n106:     )\n107: \n108:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = True\n109: except ImportError:\n110:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = False\n111:     ApprovalPolicy = None\n112:     ApprovalReason = None\n113:     ApprovalStatus = None\n114:     ApprovalStore = None\n115: \n116: # ============================================================================\n117: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n118: # ============================================================================\n119: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n120: \n121: \n122: def _project_root() -> Path:\n123:     return resolve_project_root()\n124: \n125: \n126: class _LazyPath:\n127:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n128:         self._resolver = resolver\n129: \n130:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n131:         return self._resolver()\n132: \n133:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n134:         return self.resolve() / other\n135: \n136:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n137:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n138: \n139:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n140:         return str(self.resolve())\n141: \n142:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n143:         return str(self.resolve())\n144: \n145:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n146:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n147: \n148: \n149: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n150: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n151: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_agent_dir)\n152: COLLAB_DIR = _LazyPath(get_collab_dir)\n153: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_context_dir)\n154: \n155: # State files\n156: WORK_PLAN = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\")\n157: EXEC_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"execution_log.md\")\n158: REVIEW_QUEUE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"review_queue.md\")\n159: NOTIFICATIONS = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"notifications.md\")\n160: TURN_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"TURN.md\")\n161: STATE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"STATE.md\")\n162: PROJECT_MAP = _LazyPath(lambda: get_context_dir() / \"project_map.md\")\n163: ARCHIVE_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"archive\")\n164: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\")\n165: \n166: # Archive configuration\n167: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n168: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n169: \n170: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n171: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n172: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n173: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n174: \n175: # WP-2026-146: HUMAN_GATE timeout configuration. Single source of truth is\n176: # manager_review.human_gate_timeout_seconds in agents.json. Fallback used if missing.\n177: # Distinct from manager_review.timeout_seconds (AI subprocess wait, ~180s).\n178: HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK = 86400  # 24 hours\n179: \n180: # WP-2026-147: Graph context adapter import (optional)\n181: try:\n182:     from scripts.graph_context import generate_context_for_destination\n183: \n184:     GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE = True\n185: except ImportError:\n186:     GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE = False\n187:     generate_context_for_destination = None\n188: \n189: \n190: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n191:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n192: \n193:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n194:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n195:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n196:     \"\"\"\n197:     try:\n198:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n199:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n200:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n201:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n202:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n203: \n204: \n205: def get_human_gate_timeout() -> int:\n206:     \"\"\"Return the timeout in seconds for HUMAN_GATE approval requests.\n207: \n208:     Reads manager_review.human_gate_timeout_seconds from agents.json.\n209:     This is intentionally separate from manager_review.timeout_seconds,\n210:     which controls the AI subprocess wait (~180s). Human gates need hours.\n211:     Falls back to HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK (24h) if absent or malformed.\n212: \n213:     Returns:\n214:         Timeout in seconds (positive integer).\n215:     \"\"\"\n216:     try:\n217:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n218:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"human_gate_timeout_seconds\"))\n219:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n220:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n221:         return HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n222: \n223: \n224: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n225: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n226: \n227: # Estados validos para validacion\n228: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n229:     \"DRAFT\",\n230:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n231:     \"APPROVED\",\n232:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n233:     \"COMPLETED\",\n234:     \"N/A\",\n235: }\n236: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n237:     \"PENDING\",\n238:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n239:     \"BLOCKED\",\n240:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n241:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n242:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n243:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n244:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n245:     \"COMPLETED\",\n246:     \"N/A\",\n247: }\n248: \n249: \n250: def _ensure_runtime_dirs() -> None:\n251:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n252:     archive_dir = get_collab_dir() / \"archive\"\n253:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n254:     archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n255: \n256: \n257: def _get_event_bus() -> EventBus | None:\n258:     global event_bus\n259:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE:\n260:         return None\n261:     if event_bus is None:\n262:         event_bus = EventBus(get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n263:     return event_bus\n264: \n265: \n266: def _get_approval_store() -> ApprovalStore | None:\n267:     \"\"\"Get or create the approval store for HUMAN_GATE timeout.\n268: \n269:     Before: No approval store existed in agent_controller.\n270:     During: Creates ApprovalStore under runtime_dir with configurable timeout.\n271:     After: Returns configured ApprovalStore or None if approval system unavailable.\n272:     \"\"\"\n273:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n274:         return None\n275:     store_path = get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"approvals\" / \"store.json\"\n276:     timeout_seconds = get_human_gate_timeout()\n277:     policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n278:         policy_name=\"human_gate\",\n279:         timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n280:         auto_resolve=True,\n281:         auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n282:     )\n283:     return ApprovalStore(store_path=store_path, policy=policy)\n284: \n285: \n286: # Event bus is initialized lazily after the project root is known.\n287: event_bus = None\n288: \n289: # Circuit breaker state\n290: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = _LazyPath(\n291:     lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n292: )\n293: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\")\n294: \n295: # ============================================================================\n296: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n297: # ============================================================================\n298: \n299: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n300:     \"work_plan.md\",\n301:     \"execution_log.md\",\n302:     \"STATE.md\",\n303:     \"TURN.md\",\n304:     \"notifications.md\",\n305:     \".session_state.json\",\n306: }\n307: \n308: \n309: def _exclude_files() -> set[str]:\n310:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n311:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n312:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n313:     exclude_files = {str((collab_dir / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n314:     exclude_files.add(str((context_dir / \"project_map.md\").resolve()))\n315:     # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n316:     exclude_files.add(\n317:         str((agent_dir / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve())\n318:     )\n319:     # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n320:     exclude_files.add(str((agent_dir / \"config\").resolve()))\n321:     return exclude_files\n322: \n323: \n324: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n325:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n326:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n327:     in_section = False\n328:     files = set()\n329: \n330:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n331:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n332:             return False\n333:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n334:             return True\n335:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n336:             return True\n337:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n338:         return \".\" in basename\n339: \n340:     for line in lines:\n341:         line = line.strip()\n342:         if \"## Files Likely Touched\" in line:\n343:             in_section = True\n344:             continue\n345:         if in_section and line.startswith(\"## \"):\n346:             break  # next section\n347:         if in_section and line and not line.startswith(\"---\"):\n348:             # normalize: remove backticks, quotes, bullets, trim\n349:             normalized = (\n350:                 line.lstrip(\"*- \")\n351:                 .replace(\"`\", \"\")\n352:                 .replace('\"', \"\")\n353:                 .replace(\"'\", \"\")\n354:                 .strip()\n355:             )\n356:             if normalized and _looks_like_path_token(normalized):\n357:                 # resolve relative to project root\n358:                 path = (PROJECT_ROOT / normalized).resolve()\n359:                 files.add(str(path))\n360:     return files\n361: \n362: \n363: def get_changed_files() -> set[str] | None:\n364:     \"\"\"Get all changed files: staged, unstaged, untracked. None if not git repo.\"\"\"\n365:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n366:         return None\n367:     try:\n368:         # Use -z for null-byte separated output to handle paths with spaces and renames safely\n369:         result = subprocess.run(\n370:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\", \"-z\"],\n371:             capture_output=True,\n372:             text=True,\n373:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n374:         )\n375:         changed = set()\n376:         # Split by null byte, each entry is: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status\n377:         # Renames have two consecutive entries: \"R old_path\" followed by \"new_path\"\n378:         entries = result.stdout.split(\"\\0\")\n379:         i = 0\n380:         while i < len(entries):\n381:             entry = entries[i]\n382:             if not entry:\n383:                 i += 1\n384:                 continue\n385:             # Format: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status (e.g., \"M \", \"??\", \"R \")\n386:             # When Y=' ' (space), format is \"M path\" (path at index 2)\n387:             # When Y!=' ', format is \"XY path\" with space separator (path at index 3)\n388:             if len(entry) >= 3:\n389:                 status = entry[:2]\n390:                 # Determine path start: if entry[2] is space, path starts at 3; otherwise at 2\n391:                 path = entry[3:] if entry[2] == \" \" else entry[2:]\n392:                 # Handle renames: status starts with 'R', next entry is the new path\n393:                 if status[0] == \"R\" and i + 1 < len(entries):\n394:                     new_path = entries[i + 1]\n395:                     if new_path:\n396:                         changed.add(new_path)\n397:                     i += 2\n398:                     continue\n399:                 else:\n400:                     changed.add(path)\n401:             i += 1\n402:         # resolve to absolute paths\n403:         resolved = set()\n404:         for f in changed:\n405:             path = (PROJECT_ROOT / f).resolve()\n406:             resolved.add(str(path))\n407:         return resolved\n408:     except FileNotFoundError:\n409:         return None\n410: \n411: \n412: def check_scope_gate(\n413:     work_plan_content: str, changed_files: set[str] | None, exclude_files: set[str]\n414: ) -> dict:\n415:     \"\"\"Check if changed files are within scope.\"\"\"\n416:     if changed_files is None:\n417:         return {\n418:             \"valid\": True,\n419:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n420:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n421:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n422:             \"warnings\": [\"Repository is not git-managed\"],\n423:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n424:         }\n425: \n426:     whitelist = parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n427:     if not whitelist:\n428:         return {\n429:             \"valid\": True,\n430:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n431:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n432:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n433:             \"warnings\": [\"No Files Likely Touched section in work_plan.md\"],\n434:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n435:         }\n436: \n437:     relevant_changed = changed_files - exclude_files\n438:     relevant_whitelist = whitelist - exclude_files\n439:     covered_files = relevant_changed & relevant_whitelist\n440:     missing_from_diff = relevant_whitelist - relevant_changed\n441:     out_of_scope = relevant_changed - relevant_whitelist\n442: \n443:     warnings = []\n444:     blocked_reason = None\n445:     valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0\n446: \n447:     if relevant_whitelist and not covered_files:\n448:         valid = False\n449:         blocked_reason = (\n450:             \"None of the declared Files Likely Touched entries appeared in the diff\"\n451:         )\n452:     elif covered_files and missing_from_diff:\n453:         warnings.append(\n454:             \"Partial scope coverage: \"\n455:             f\"{len(covered_files)} of {len(relevant_whitelist)} declared files touched\"\n456:         )\n457: \n458:     return {\n459:         \"valid\": valid,\n460:         \"out_of_scope\": out_of_scope,\n461:         \"missing_from_diff\": missing_from_diff,\n462:         \"covered_files\": covered_files,\n463:         \"warnings\": warnings,\n464:         \"blocked_reason\": blocked_reason,\n465:     }\n466: \n467: \n468: def _load_mark_ready_context() -> tuple[str, str, str]:\n469:     \"\"\"Load the plan and log content for --mark-ready.\"\"\"\n470:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n471:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n472:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n473:     return plan_content, log_content, plan_id\n474: \n475: \n476: def _record_scope_override(scope_override: str, problem_files: set[str]) -> None:\n477:     \"\"\"Record a scope override in the execution log.\"\"\"\n478:     note = (\n479:         f\"Scope override: {scope_override}. \"\n480:         f\"Affected files: {', '.join(sorted(problem_files))}\"\n481:     )\n482:     update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", note)\n483: \n484: \n485: def _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result: dict, scope_override: str | None) -> bool:\n486:     \"\"\"Apply scope gate decision for mark-ready.\"\"\"\n487:     if gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n488:         for warning in gate_result[\"warnings\"]:\n489:             print(f\"[WARN] {warning}\")\n490:         update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Marked ready by Builder\")\n491:         return True\n492: \n493:     if not scope_override:\n494:         print(\"[ERROR] Scope violation detected:\")\n495:         if gate_result.get(\"blocked_reason\"):\n496:             print(f\"  {gate_result['blocked_reason']}\")\n497:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]):\n498:             print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n499:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())):\n500:             print(f\"  - missing: {file_path}\")\n501:         print('Use --scope-override \"reason\" to proceed.')\n502:         return False\n503: \n504:     print(f\"[INFO] Scope override applied: {scope_override}\")\n505:     problem_files = set(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]) | set(\n506:         gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())\n507:     )\n508:     _record_scope_override(scope_override, problem_files)\n509:     return True\n510: \n511: \n512: def _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id: str, plan_content: str) -> None:\n513:     \"\"\"Update TURN.md and STATE.md after mark-ready.\n514: \n515:     WP-2026-124: Uses bus-derived state as authority, not execution_log.md.\n516:     \"\"\"\n517:     # WP-2026-124: Get bus-derived state\n518:     log_status_before = get_status(read_file(EXEC_LOG), \"**Estado:**\")\n519:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n520:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n521: \n522:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n523:         bus_state = (\n524:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n525:             if events\n526:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n527:         )\n528:         # Use bus state if available, fallback to log\n529:         from_state = (\n530:             bus_state.value if bus_state != TicketState.UNKNOWN else log_status_before\n531:         )\n532:     else:\n533:         from_state = log_status_before\n534: \n535:     action = {\n536:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n537:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n538:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n539:         \"instruction\": f\"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n540:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n541:         \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n542:         \"log_status\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n543:         \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n544:         \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n545:     }\n546:     update_turn_file(action)\n547: \n548:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n549:     if not state_content:\n550:         return\n551: \n552:     lines = state_content.split(\"\\n\")\n553:     for index, line in enumerate(lines):\n554:         if \"**Estado actual:**\" in line:\n555:             lines[index] = \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n556:             write_file(STATE_FILE, \"\\n\".join(lines))\n557:             break\n558: \n559:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus idempotently\n560:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n561:         # Check if already emitted for this ticket and state\n562:         latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n563:             ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n564:         )\n565:         if (\n566:             not latest_state_event\n567:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n568:         ):\n569:             event_bus.emit(\n570:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n571:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n572:                 actor=\"BUILDER\",\n573:                 payload={\n574:                     \"from_state\": from_state,\n575:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n576:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n577:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n578:                 },\n579:             )\n580:         if (\n581:             not latest_state_event\n582:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n583:         ):\n584:             bus_from_state = (\n585:                 latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n586:                 if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n587:                 else log_status_before\n588:             )\n589:             event_bus.emit(\n590:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n591:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n592:                 actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n593:                 payload={\n594:                     \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n595:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n596:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n597:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n598:                 },\n599:             )\n600: \n601: \n602: # ============================================================================\n603: # CIRCUIT BREAKER AND CHECKOUT UTILITIES\n604: # ============================================================================\n605: \n606: \n607: def _read_circuit_breaker() -> dict:\n608:     \"\"\"Read circuit breaker state from JSON file.\"\"\"\n609:     if not CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.exists():\n610:         return {\n611:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n612:             \"failures\": 0,\n613:             \"last_failure\": None,\n614:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n615:         }\n616:     try:\n617:         return json.loads(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n618:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n619:         return {\n620:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n621:             \"failures\": 0,\n622:             \"last_failure\": None,\n623:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n624:         }\n625: \n626: \n627: def _write_circuit_breaker(state: dict) -> None:\n628:     \"\"\"Write circuit breaker state to JSON file.\"\"\"\n629:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n630:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.write_text(\n631:         json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n632:     )\n633: \n634: \n635: def _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> dict:\n636:     \"\"\"\n637:     Check circuit breaker state for a ticket.\n638:     Returns dict with 'open', 'reason', 'failures', 'no_progress_count'.\n639:     \"\"\"\n640:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n641:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\":\n642:         return {\n643:             \"open\": True,\n644:             \"reason\": breaker.get(\"reason\", \"Circuit breaker triggered\"),\n645:             \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n646:             \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n647:         }\n648:     return {\n649:         \"open\": False,\n650:         \"reason\": None,\n651:         \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n652:         \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n653:     }\n654: \n655: \n656: def _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n657:     reason: str, plan_id: str, is_no_progress: bool = False\n658: ) -> None:\n659:     \"\"\"Trigger circuit breaker to OPEN state.\"\"\"\n660:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n661:     breaker[\"state\"] = \"OPEN\"\n662:     breaker[\"reason\"] = reason\n663:     breaker[\"last_triggered\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n664:     breaker[\"ticket_id\"] = plan_id\n665:     if is_no_progress:\n666:         breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n667:     else:\n668:         breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n669:     _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n670: \n671: \n672: def _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n673:     \"\"\"Reset circuit breaker to CLOSED state after successful completion.\"\"\"\n674:     _write_circuit_breaker(\n675:         {\n676:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n677:             \"failures\": 0,\n678:             \"last_failure\": None,\n679:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n680:             \"last_reset\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n681:             \"last_ticket\": plan_id,\n682:         }\n683:     )\n684: \n685: \n686: def _record_error_for_breaker(plan_id: str, error_msg: str) -> None:\n687:     \"\"\"Record an error for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n688:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n689:     breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n690:     breaker[\"last_error\"] = error_msg\n691:     breaker[\"last_error_time\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n692: \n693:     # Trigger if 3+ consecutive errors\n694:     if breaker[\"failures\"] >= 3:\n695:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(f\"Repeated errors: {error_msg[:100]}\", plan_id)\n696:     else:\n697:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n698: \n699: \n700: def _record_no_progress_for_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n701:     \"\"\"Record no-progress event for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n702:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n703:     breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n704:     breaker[\"last_no_progress\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n705: \n706:     # Trigger if 3+ no-progress events\n707:     if breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] >= 3:\n708:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n709:             \"No progress detected in multiple iterations\", plan_id, is_no_progress=True\n710:         )\n711:     else:\n712:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n713: \n714: \n715: def _read_builder_lock() -> dict | None:\n716:     \"\"\"Read builder lock file if it exists.\"\"\"\n717:     if not BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.exists():\n718:         return None\n719:     try:\n720:         return json.loads(BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n721:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n722:         return None\n723: \n724: \n725: def _acquire_builder_lock(\n726:     plan_id: str, pid: int, role: str = \"BUILDER\", backend: str = \"unknown\"\n727: ) -> bool:\n728:     \"\"\"\n729:     Attempt to acquire builder lock atomically.\n730:     Returns True if lock was acquired, False if another session holds it.\n731:     \"\"\"\n732:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n733:     if existing is not None:\n734:         # Check if lock is stale (different ticket or dead process)\n735:         if existing.get(\"ticket_id\") != plan_id:\n736:             # Stale lock from different ticket - override\n737:             lock_data = {\n738:                 \"pid\": pid,\n739:                 \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n740:                 \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n741:                 \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n742:                 \"role\": role,\n743:                 \"backend\": backend,\n744:                 \"round\": 1,\n745:             }\n746:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n747:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n748:                 json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n749:             )\n750:             return True\n751:         # Same ticket - check if process is alive (simplified: just allow re-acquisition)\n752:         lock_data = {\n753:             \"pid\": pid,\n754:             \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n755:             \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n756:             \"started_at\": existing.get(\n757:                 \"started_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n758:             ),\n759:             \"role\": role,\n760:             \"backend\": backend,\n761:             \"round\": existing.get(\"round\", 1) + 1,\n762:         }\n763:         BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n764:             json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n765:         )\n766:         return True\n767: \n768:     # No existing lock - acquire fresh\n769:     lock_data = {\n770:         \"pid\": pid,\n771:         \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n772:         \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n773:         \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n774:         \"role\": role,\n775:         \"backend\": backend,\n776:         \"round\": 1,\n777:     }\n778:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n779:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n780:         json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n781:     )\n782:     return True\n783: \n784: \n785: def _release_builder_lock(plan_id: str) -> None:\n786:     \"\"\"Release builder lock after completion.\"\"\"\n787:     import contextlib\n788: \n789:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n790:     if existing and existing.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id:\n791:         with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n792:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.unlink()\n793: \n794: \n795: def _emit_builder_exit(plan_id: str, exit_reason: str, completion_summary: str) -> None:\n796:     \"\"\"Emit BUILDER_EXIT event to the bus - required for ticket closure.\"\"\"\n797:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n798:         event_bus.emit(\n799:             event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\",\n800:             ticket_id=plan_id,\n801:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n802:             payload={\n803:                 \"exit_reason\": exit_reason,\n804:                 \"completion_summary\": completion_summary,\n805:                 \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n806:             },\n807:         )\n808: \n809: \n810: def _create_human_gate_approval_request(\n811:     ticket_id: str, timeout_seconds: int | None = None\n812: ) -> bool:\n813:     \"\"\"Create and persist an ApprovalRequest when escalating to HUMAN_GATE.\n814: \n815:     Before: HUMAN_GATE escalation had no persistent timeout tracking.\n816:     During: Creates an ApprovalRequest with timeout metadata in the ApprovalStore.\n817:     After: The approval request persists across restarts and can be expired by\n818:            the supervisor's check_and_expire_all() loop.\n819: \n820:     Args:\n821:         ticket_id: The ticket ID being escalated to HUMAN_GATE.\n822:         timeout_seconds: Optional timeout override. Defaults to config value.\n823: \n824:     Returns:\n825:         True if the approval request was created successfully, False otherwise.\n826:     \"\"\"\n827:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n828:         print(\n829:             \"[WARN] Approval system unavailable; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n830:             file=sys.stderr,\n831:             flush=True,\n832:         )\n833:         return False\n834: \n835:     store = _get_approval_store()\n836:     if store is None:\n837:         print(\n838:             \"[WARN] Could not create ApprovalStore; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n839:             file=sys.stderr,\n840:             flush=True,\n841:         )\n842:         return False\n843: \n844:     import uuid\n845: \n846:     approval_id = f\"hg-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n847: \n848:     # Create and persist the approval request\n849:     timeout = (\n850:         timeout_seconds if timeout_seconds is not None else get_human_gate_timeout()\n851:     )\n852:     metadata = {\n853:         \"escalation_type\": \"human_gate\",\n854:         \"timeout_seconds\": timeout,\n855:         \"created_by\": \"agent_controller\",\n856:         \"source\": \"escalate-human-gate\",\n857:     }\n858: \n859:     try:\n860:         # Use custom policy if timeout_seconds was provided\n861:         if timeout_seconds is not None:\n862:             custom_policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n863:                 policy_name=\"human_gate_custom\",\n864:                 timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n865:                 auto_resolve=True,\n866:                 auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n867:             )\n868:             store.create_request(\n869:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n870:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n871:                 metadata=metadata,\n872:                 policy=custom_policy,\n873:             )\n874:         else:\n875:             store.create_request(\n876:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n877:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n878:                 metadata=metadata,\n879:             )\n880:         print(\n881:             f\"[OK] Created HUMAN_GATE approval request {approval_id} for {ticket_id} \"\n882:             f\"(timeout={timeout}s).\",\n883:             flush=True,\n884:         )\n885:         return True\n886:     except Exception as exc:\n887:         print(\n888:             f\"[ERROR] Failed to create HUMAN_GATE approval request: {exc}\",\n889:             file=sys.stderr,\n890:             flush=True,\n891:         )\n892:         return False\n893: \n894: \n895: def _auto_archive_closed_artifacts() -> None:\n896:     \"\"\"Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts during mark-ready.\n897: \n898:     Before: Requires COLLAB_DIR to exist with potential closed PLAN/AUDIT files.\n899:     During: Imports and calls archive_collaboration_artifacts.py as a library function.\n900:     After: Closed PLAN/AUDIT files are moved to _archive/plan_audit/ (idempotent, silent on no-op).\n901:     \"\"\"\n902:     try:\n903:         # Import the archive script as a module\n904:         import importlib.util\n905: \n906:         archive_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n907:             \"archive_collaboration_artifacts\",\n908:             PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n909:         )\n910:         if archive_spec and archive_spec.loader:\n911:             archive_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(archive_spec)\n912:             archive_spec.loader.exec_module(archive_mod)\n913: \n914:             # Call the archive function (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n915:             archive_mod.archive_collaboration_artifacts(\n916:                 collaboration_dir=COLLAB_DIR,\n917:                 dry_run=False,\n918:             )\n919:     except Exception as exc:\n920:         # Silent fail with debug logging - archiving is best-effort, not critical path\n921:         print(f\"[WARN] Auto-archive failed: {exc}\")\n922: \n923: \n924: # ============================================================================\n925: # UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n926: # ============================================================================\n927: \n928: \n929: def read_file(path: Path) -> str:\n930:     \"\"\"Lee un archivo si existe, retorna string vacio si no.\"\"\"\n931:     if not path.exists():\n932:         return \"\"\n933:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n934:         return f.read()\n935: \n936: \n937: def write_file(path: Path, content: str) -> None:\n938:     \"\"\"Escribe contenido a un archivo, creando directorios si es necesario.\"\"\"\n939:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n940:     with open(path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n941:         f.write(content)\n942: \n943: \n944: def get_status(content: str, marker: str) -> str:\n945:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado de un archivo buscando un marcador.\"\"\"\n946:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n947:         if marker in line:\n948:             return line.split(marker)[1].strip()\n949:     return \"UNKNOWN\"\n950: \n951: \n952: def get_plan_id(content: str) -> str:\n953:     \"\"\"Extrae el ID del plan de trabajo.\"\"\"\n954:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n955:         if \"**ID:**\" in line or \"**Plan ID:**\" in line:\n956:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n957:     return \"N/A\"\n958: \n959: \n960: def get_plan_type(content: str) -> str:\n961:     \"\"\"Extrae el tipo de plan. Valores: IMPLEMENTATION (default) | FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n962:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n963:         if \"**Tipo:**\" in line:\n964:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip().upper()\n965:     return \"IMPLEMENTATION\"\n966: \n967: \n968: def check_git_status() -> bool | None:\n969:     \"\"\"Verifica si el repositorio esta limpio.\"\"\"\n970:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n971:         return None\n972:     try:\n973:         result = subprocess.run(\n974:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\"],\n975:             capture_output=True,\n976:             text=True,\n977:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n978:         )\n979:         return not result.stdout.strip()\n980:     except FileNotFoundError:\n981:         return None\n982: \n983: \n984: def extract_status_emoji(status_str: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n985:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado limpio y el emoji.\"\"\"\n986:     emojis = {\"🟢\", \"🟡\", \"🔴\", \"🟣\", \"✅\", \"⏳\", \"❌\", \"⚠️\"}\n987:     status_clean = status_str.strip()\n988:     found_emoji = \"\"\n989:     for emoji in emojis:\n990:         if emoji in status_clean:\n991:             found_emoji = emoji\n992:             status_clean = status_clean.replace(emoji, \"\").strip()\n993:             break\n994:     return status_clean, found_emoji\n995: \n996: \n997: _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n998: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n999:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n1000:     re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n1001: )\n1002: \n1003: \n1004: def _check_deliverable_type(content: str) -> list[str]:\n1005:     \"\"\"Check deliverable_type field in work_plan.md content.\n1006: \n1007:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n1008:     During: Searches for deliverable_type field using regex, validates against allowed values.\n1009:     After: Returns list of warning strings (empty if valid). Never returns errors (V1 informational).\n1010:     \"\"\"\n1011:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n1012:     if not match:\n1013:         return [\n1014:             \"work_plan.md missing deliverable_type field. \"\n1015:             \"Add one of: code, documentation, research, analysis, mixed.\"\n1016:         ]\n1017:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n1018:     if \"+\" in value:\n1019:         return [\n1020:             f\"work_plan.md deliverable_type='{value}' uses compound syntax. \"\n1021:             \"Use 'mixed' instead of 'code+documentation' etc.\"\n1022:         ]\n1023:     if value not in _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES:\n1024:         return [\n1025:             f\"work_plan.md has unknown deliverable_type '{value}'. \"\n1026:             f\"Expected one of: {sorted(_VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES)}\"\n1027:         ]\n1028:     return []\n1029: \n1030: \n1031: def _validate_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n1032:     \"\"\"Validate work_plan.md file.\"\"\"\n1033:     errors = []\n1034:     content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1035:     if not content:\n1036:         return errors\n1037: \n1038:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1039:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1040:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_PLAN_STATES:\n1041:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1042:     if \"**ID:**\" not in content:\n1043:         errors.append(\"Falta campo **ID:**\")\n1044:     return errors\n1045: \n1046: \n1047: def _validate_execution_log() -> list[str]:\n1048:     \"\"\"Validate execution_log.md file.\"\"\"\n1049:     errors = []\n1050:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1051:     if not content:\n1052:         return errors\n1053: \n1054:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1055:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1056:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_LOG_STATES:\n1057:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1058:     return errors\n1059: \n1060: \n1061: def _validate_turn_file() -> list[str]:\n1062:     \"\"\"Validate TURN.md file.\"\"\"\n1063:     errors = []\n1064:     content = read_file(TURN_FILE)\n1065:     if content and \"## Agente Activo\" not in content:\n1066:         errors.append(\"Falta secciÃƒÂ³n '## Agente Activo'\")\n1067:     return errors\n1068: \n1069: \n1070: def _validate_notifications() -> list[str]:\n1071:     \"\"\"Validate notifications.md file.\"\"\"\n1072:     errors = []\n1073:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1074:     if content and \"</thinking>\" in content:\n1075:         errors.append(\"Contiene etiquetas </thinking> (corrupto)\")\n1076:     return errors\n1077: \n1078: \n1079: def _validate_cross_file_consistency() -> list[str]:\n1080:     \"\"\"Validate consistency across files.\"\"\"\n1081:     errors = []\n1082:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1083:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1084: \n1085:     if not plan_content or not log_content:\n1086:         return errors\n1087: \n1088:     plan_status_raw = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1089:     log_status_raw = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1090:     plan_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(plan_status_raw)\n1091:     log_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(log_status_raw)\n1092: \n1093:     # Plan APPROVED pero log COMPLETED\n1094:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_clean and \"COMPLETED\" in log_clean:\n1095:         errors.append(\n1096:             \"DRIFT: plan=APPROVED pero log=COMPLETED -- \"\n1097:             \"el log pertenece a un ciclo anterior. Limpia execution_log.md.\"\n1098:         )\n1099: \n1100:     # Plan COMPLETED pero log IN_PROGRESS\n1101:     if \"COMPLETED\" in plan_clean and \"IN_PROGRESS\" in log_clean:\n1102:         errors.append(\n1103:             \"DRIFT: plan=COMPLETED pero log=IN_PROGRESS -- \"\n1104:             \"el Builder no cerro su bitacora correctamente.\"\n1105:         )\n1106: \n1107:     # Plan IN_PLANNING con log READY_FOR_REVIEW\n1108:     if \"IN_PLANNING\" in plan_clean and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_clean:\n1109:         errors.append(\n1110:             \"DRIFT: plan=IN_PLANNING pero log=READY_FOR_REVIEW -- \"\n1111:             \"estado imposible. El plan debe estar APPROVED antes de que el Builder entregue.\"\n1112:         )\n1113: \n1114:     # Plan N/A pero log activo\n1115:     if (\"N/A\" in plan_clean or not plan_clean) and log_clean not in (\n1116:         \"N/A\",\n1117:         \"COMPLETED\",\n1118:         \"PENDING\",\n1119:         \"\",\n1120:     ):\n1121:         errors.append(\n1122:             f\"DRIFT: no hay plan activo pero log={log_clean} -- \"\n1123:             \"limpia execution_log.md o crea un nuevo work_plan.md.\"\n1124:         )\n1125: \n1126:     return errors\n1127: \n1128: \n1129: def _validate_host_project_prefix() -> list[str]:\n1130:     \"\"\"Validate that host-project destinations have 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md.\n1131: \n1132:     Before: Requires PROJECT.md to exist at project root; agents.json to be readable.\n1133:     During: Checks if active_profile is 'host-project' and if PROJECT.md has 'Ticket prefix:'.\n1134:     After: Returns list of warnings (empty if validation passes).\n1135: \n1136:     Returns:\n1137:         List of warning strings (empty if no issues).\n1138:     \"\"\"\n1139:     warnings = []\n1140:     agents_config = AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH\n1141:     if not agents_config.exists():\n1142:         return warnings\n1143: \n1144:     try:\n1145:         config_data = json.loads(agents_config.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n1146:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n1147:         return warnings\n1148: \n1149:     active_profile = config_data.get(\"active_profile\", \"\")\n1150:     if active_profile != \"host-project\":\n1151:         return warnings\n1152: \n1153:     # Only check for host-project profile\n1154:     project_md = PROJECT_ROOT / \"PROJECT.md\"\n1155:     if not project_md.exists():\n1156:         warnings.append(\n1157:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks PROJECT.md. \"\n1158:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace.\"\n1159:         )\n1160:         return warnings\n1161: \n1162:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1163:     content = re.sub(r\"</thinking>\\s*\", \"\", content)\n1191:     content = re.sub(r\"\\n{4,}\", \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\", content)\n1192:     if content != original:\n1193: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB\n1209:         and entry_count <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES\n1210:     ):\n1211:         return None\n1212: \n1213:     parts = content.split(\"---\")\n1214:     entries = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]\n1215: \n1216:     if len(entries) <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:\n1217:         return None\n1218: \n1219:     entries_to_archive = entries[:-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES]\n1220:     entries_to_keep = entries[-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:]\n1221: \n1222:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n1223:     archive_file = ARCHIVE_DIR / f\"notifications_{timestamp}.md\"\n1224: \n1225:     archive_content = \"# Notificaciones Archivadas\\n\\n\"\n1226:     archive_content += f\"**Fecha:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d \n1236:     return str(archive_file)\n1237: \n1238: \n1239: def run_finalization_checks() -> dict:\n1240:     \"\"\"Checks adicionales para planes de tipo FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n1241:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"summary\": []}\n1242:     checks = {\n1243:         \"README.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"README.md\",\n1244:         \"CHANGELOG.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n1245: results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[OK] {name}: Presente\")\n1250:         else:\n1251:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[WARN] {name}: No encontrado\")\n1252:     return results\n1253: \n1254: \n1255: def run_quality_gates(plan_type: str = \"IMPLEMENTATION\") -> dict:\n1256:     \"\"\"Ejecuta validaciones automaticas.\"\"\"\n1257:     print(\"\\n[QUALITY GATES] Ejecutando Quality Gates...\")\n1258:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"errors\": [], \"summary\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1259: \n1260:     state_errors = validate_state_files()\n1261:     total_state_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in state_errors.values())\n1262:     if total_state_errors > 0:\n1263:         results[\"warnings\"].append(\n1264:             f\"Archivos de estado: {total_state_errors} problemas\"\n1265:         )\n1266:     else:\n1267:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Estado: Archivos validos\")\n1268: \n1269:     src_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"src\"\n1270:     tests_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"tests\"\n1271:     dirs_to_check = [str(d) for d in [src_dir, tests_dir] if d.exists()]\n1272:     if not dirs_to_check:\n1273:         dirs_to_check = [\".\"]\n1274: \n1275:     try:\n1276:         ruff = subprocess.run(\n1277:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"ruff\", \"check\", *dirs_to_check],\n1278:             capture_output=True,\n1279:             text=True,\n1280:             timeout=60,\n1281:         )\n1282:         if ruff.returncode != 0:\n1283:             results[\"passed\"] = False\n1284:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Ruff: Errores de linting\")\n1285:         else:\n1286:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Ruff: Limpio\")\n1287:     except FileNotFoundError:\n1288:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Ruff: No instalado\")\n1289: \n1290:     if tests_dir.exists():\n1291:         try:\n1292:             pytest_result = subprocess.run(\n1293:                 [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pytest\", \"-q\"],\n1294:                 capture_output=True,\n1295:                 timeout=120,\n1296:                 cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n1297:             )\n1298:             if pytest_result.returncode != 0:\n1299:                 results[\"passed\"] = False\n1300:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Pytest: Tests fallando\")\n1301:             else:\n1302:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Pytest: Tests OK\")\n1303:         except FileNotFoundError:\n1304:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Pytest: No instalado\")\n1305: \n1306:     if plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1307:         fin_results = run_finalization_checks()\n1308:         results[\"summary\"].extend(fin_results[\"summary\"])\n1309: \n1310:     status = \"[PASSED]\" if results[\"passed\"] else \"[FAILED]\"\n1311:     print(f\"   {status}\")\n1312:     return results\n1313: \n1314: \n1315: def update_log_status(new_status: str, note: str) -> bool:\n1316:     \"\"\"Actualiza el estado en execution_log.md.\"\"\"\n1317:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1318:     if not content:\n1319:         return False\n1320:     lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n1321:     for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n1322:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n1323:             lines[i] = f\"**Estado:** {new_status}\"\n1324:             new_content = \"\\n\".join(lines) + f\"\\n\\n{note}\"\n1325:             write_file(EXEC_LOG, new_content)\n1326:             return True\n1327:     return False\n1328: \n1329: \n1330: def create_findings_file(plan_id: str = \"N/A\") -> Path:\n1331:     \"\"\"Crea findings.md desde template.\"\"\"\n1332:     findings_path = COLLAB_DIR / \"findings.md\"\n1333: \n1334:     if findings_path.exists():\n1335:         return findings_path\n1336: \n1337: template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1342:         else:\n1343:             template = \"\"\"# Hallazgos de Investigacion\n1344: \n1345: **Plan ID:** {{PLAN_ID}}\n1346: **Creado:** template.replace(\"{{PLAN_ID}}\", plan_id)\n1356:         content = content.replace(\n1357:             \"{{DATE}}\", datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1358:         )\n1359: \n1360: {e}\")\n1365: \n1366:     return findings_path\n1367: \n1368: \n1369: def generate_project_map() -> str:\n1370:     \"\"\"Genera un mapa actualizado del proyecto.\"\"\"\n1371:     output = [\n1372:         \"# Mapa del Proyecto\",\n1373:         f\"**Actualizado:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\",\n1374:         \"\",\n1375:         \"## Estructura de Archivos\",\n1376:         \"```\",\n1377:     ]\n1378: \n1379:     ignore = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\", \".ruff_cache\"}\n1380:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".txt\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n1381: \n1382:     files_found = []\n1383:     for path in sorted(PROJECT_ROOT.rglob(\"*\")):\n1384:         if any(part in ignore for part in path.parts):\n1385:             continue\n1386:         if path.is_file() and path.suffix in extensions:\n1387:             files_found.append(str(path.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)))\n1388: \n1389:     output.extend(files_found[:50])\n1390:     if len(files_found) > 50:\n1391:         output.append(f\"... (+{len(files_found) - 50} archivos mas)\")\n1392: \n1393:     output.append(\"```\")\n1394:     content = \"\\n\".join(output)\n1395:     write_file(PROJECT_MAP, content)\n1396:     return content\n1397: \n1398: \n1399: def _run_pre_action_hooks(\n1400:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, action_type: str\n1401: ) -> None:\n1402:     \"\"\"Run pre-action hooks if available.\"\"\"\n1403:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1404:         hook_registry.execute(\n1405:             \"pre_action\",\n1406:             {\n1407:                 \"action_type\": action_type,\n1408:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1409:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1410:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1411:             },\n1412:         )\n1413: \n1414: \n1415: def _check_quality_gates(\n1416:     plan_id: str, plan_type: str, plan_status: str, skip_gates: bool\n1417: ) -> dict | None:\n1418:     \"\"\"Check quality gates and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1419:     gate_result = run_quality_gates(plan_type=plan_type)\n1420:     if not gate_result[\"passed\"]:\n1421:         update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"AUTO-REJECTED: Quality Gates fallaron\")\n1422:         return {\n1423:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1424:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1425:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1426:             \"instruction\": \"RECHAZADO. Quality Gates fallaron. Corrige errores.\",\n1427:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1428:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1429:             \"log_status\": \"AUTO-REJECTED\",\n1430:             \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1431:         }\n1432:     return None\n1433: \n1434: \n1435: def _check_completion_verification(\n1436:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_type: str, strict_mode: bool\n1437: ) -> dict | None:\n1438:     \"\"\"Check completion verification in strict mode and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1439:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1440:         stop_result = stop_hook(\n1441:             {\n1442:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1443:                 \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1444:                 \"mode\": \"strict\" if strict_mode else \"normal\",\n1445:             }\n1446:         )\n1447:         if strict_mode and not stop_result.get(\"can_complete\", True):\n1448:             return {\n1449:                 \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1450:                 \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1451:                 \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1452:                 \"instruction\": (\n1453:                     f\"Plan {plan_id} no supera Completion Verification. \"\n1454:                     \"Corrige advertencias antes de review.\"\n1455:                 ),\n1456:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1457:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1458:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1459:                 \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1460:             }\n1461:     return None\n1462: \n1463: \n1464: # Transition table for base routing\n1465: TRANSITION_TABLE = {\n1466:     (\"no_plan\", \"any\"): (\n1467:         \"MANAGER\",\n1468:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1469:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1470:     ),\n1471:     (\"completed\", \"any\"): (\n1472:         \"MANAGER\",\n1473:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1474:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1475:     ),\n1476:     (\"in_planning\", \"any\"): (\n1477:         \"MANAGER\",\n1478:         \"FINALIZE_PLAN\",\n1479:         \"Plan {plan_id} en borrador. Finaliza y cambia a APPROVED\",\n1480:     ),\n1481:     (\"approved\", \"ready_for_review\"): (\n1482:         \"MANAGER\",\n1483:         \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n1484:         \"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n1485:     ),\n1486:     (\"approved\", \"any\"): (\n1487:         \"BUILDER\",\n1488:         \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1489:         \"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Implementa segun work_plan.md\",\n1490:     ),\n1491:     (\"in_review\", \"any\"): (\n1492:         \"MANAGER\",\n1493:         \"REVIEW_CHANGES\",\n1494:         \"Plan {plan_id} en revision. Verifica cambios.\",\n1495:     ),\n1496:     (\"any\", \"blocked\"): (\n1497:         \"MANAGER\",\n1498:         \"RESOLVE_BLOCK\",\n1499:         \"Builder BLOQUEADO en {plan_id}. Resuelve en review_queue.md\",\n1500:     ),\n1501: }\n1502: \n1503: \n1504: def _lookup_transition(\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1504. Use offset=1505 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n\"\"\"\nAgent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n==========================================\nOrquestador con:\n- Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n- Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n- Completion Verification antes de review\n- Quality Gates extendidos\n- Session Recovery\n- Project Map y estado del workflow\n\nUso:\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agent_controller.py","time":{"start":1779831386745,"end":1779831386803}},"id":"prt_e6637af93001esH7p06pGxnNes","sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","messageID":"msg_e66375556001BfO36X7KT3g7jG"}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779831445666,"sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_c3fa563339dc4505a22be23a","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py","limit":100,"offset":1520},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1520:         ):\n1521:             instruction = template.format(plan_id=plan_id)\n1522:             return role, action, instruction\n1523: \n1524:     return None\n1525: \n1526: \n1527: def _inject_graph_context(instruction: str) -> str:\n1528:     \"\"\"\n1529:     Inject a compact ## Project Context block into the instruction if graphify artifacts exist.\n1530: \n1531:     Before:\n1532:         - instruction is a plain string.\n1533:         - GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE determines if adapter is importable.\n1534: \n1535:     During:\n1536:         - Checks for graphify-out/graph.json existence.\n1537:         - Calls generate_context_for_destination if available.\n1538:         - Prepends context block to instruction.\n1539: \n1540:     After:\n1541:         - Returns instruction with optional ## Project Context prepended.\n1542:         - Returns original instruction unchanged if graph context unavailable.\n1543:     \"\"\"\n1544:     if not GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE:\n1545:         return instruction\n1546: \n1547:     try:\n1548:         context_block = generate_context_for_destination(PROJECT_ROOT.resolve())\n1549:         if context_block:\n1550:             return f\"{context_block}\\n\\n---\\n\\n{instruction}\"\n1551:     except Exception:  # noqa: S110\n1552:         pass  # Gracefully degrade if graph context unavailable\n1553: \n1554:     return instruction\n1555: \n1556: \n1557: def determine_next_action(skip_gates: bool = False, strict_mode: bool = False) -> dict:\n1558:     \"\"\"Analiza el estado y determina la siguiente accion.\"\"\"\n1559:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1560:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1561: \n1562:     plan_status = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1563:     log_status = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1564:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n1565:     plan_type = get_plan_type(plan_content)\n1566: \n1567:     # Determine pre-action type for hooks\n1568:     pre_action_type = \"CHECK_STATUS\"\n1569:     if not plan_content.strip() or \"COMPLETED\" in plan_status or \"N/A\" in plan_status:\n1570:         pre_action_type = \"CREATE_PLAN\"\n1571:     elif \"APPROVED\" in plan_status and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status:\n1572:         pre_action_type = \"REVIEW_WORK\"\n1573:     elif \"APPROVED\" in plan_status:\n1574:         pre_action_type = \"IMPLEMENT\"\n1575: \n1576:     _run_pre_action_hooks(plan_id, plan_status, log_status, pre_action_type)\n1577: \n1578:     # Check quality gates for review state\n1579:     if (\n1580:         \"APPROVED\" in plan_status\n1581:         and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_status\n1582:         and not skip_gates\n1583:     ):\n1584:         gate_action = _check_quality_gates(plan_id, plan_type, plan_status, skip_gates)\n1585:         if gate_action:\n1586:             return {\"plan_type\": plan_type, **gate_action}\n1587: \n1588:     # {"type":"text","timestamp":1779831496415,"sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","part":{"id":"prt_e6639370c0016h7kz7E4zZ64lL","messageID":"msg_e66389f35001y6FXs6dyVZiG98","sessionID":"ses_199c9393cffeIRnBOBfOZM3llh","type":"text","text":"## Revisión WP-2026-147: Graph Context Adapter\n\n### Verificación de Criterios de Aceptación\n\n1. ✅ **Adapter produce `## Project Context` compacto**: `scripts/graph_context.py` genera el bloque mediante `generate_project_context()` y `generate_context_for_destination()`.\n\n2. ✅ **Derivado de artifacts existentes**: Lee `graphify-out/graph.json` y `GRAPH_REPORT.md` sin crear nuevo pipeline de grafo.\n\n3. ✅ **Determinístico y stdlib-only**: Solo usa `json`, `re`, `sys`, `pathlib`, `typing`. Test `test_context_deterministic` verifica consistencia.\n\n4. ✅ **Límite de 30 líneas**: `max_lines=30` por defecto con lógica de truncado. Test `test_context_line_limit` valida el límite.\n\n5. ✅ **Inyección opcional concisa**: `_inject_graph_context()` en agent_controller.py inyecta solo si hay artifacts, con degradación elegante.\n\n6. ✅ **Ticket flow funciona sin graph**: El hook retorna instrucción original si no hay contexto disponible.\n\n7. ✅ **Validación canónica pasa**: execution_log.md confirma \"Controller validation passes without errors\".\n\n### Files Likely Touched (Whitelist)\n- ✅ `scripts/graph_context.py` — adaptador nuevo (410 líneas)\n- ✅ `.agent/agent_controller.py` — hook de inyección agregado\n- ✅ `tests/unit/test_graph_context.py` — 35 tests pasando\n\n### Quality Gates\n- ✅ `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_graph_context.py -q` — 35 tests OK\n- ✅ Ruff checks pass\n- ✅ `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force` — sin errores\n\n### Anti-patterns Check\n- ✅ AP-01 Mock drift: patches apuntan a API real (`scripts.graph_context.*`)\n- ✅ AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: funciones con lógica propia, no delegación 1:1\n- ✅ AP-06 Validator evidence: execution_log.md contiene comandos y resultados\n- ✅ AP-08 Test coverage drift: 35 tests cubren parsing, filtering, context generation\n\n### Implementación Correcta\n- Docstrings estructurados (Before/During/After)\n- Manejo explícito de excepciones (`FileNotFoundError`, `ValueError`)\n- Tests con casos límite (grafo vacío, archivos faltantes, JSON inválido)\n- Hook opcional con fallback silencioso\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Quality gates passed.\n\nDECISION: APPROVE","time":{"start":1779831486220,"end":1779831496410}}}

The ticket should reuse the existing projection probe logic as the foundation, keep the behavior idempotent, and avoid changing the review or builder flows beyond the state sync path.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `STATE.md` will be synchronized from the bus-derived state instead of relying on handwritten markdown updates.\n15: - The existing deterministic probe logic is the source of truth for the comparison and should be refactored or reused, not duplicated.\n16: - The canonical `STATE.md` format will be the simple `Estado actual: VALUE` line, without bullet or bold markers, and the controller write path should be aligned to that format. The sync path must write that same plain line format.\n17: - The sync path must be idempotent and safe to call repeatedly.\n18: - Missing bus data must degrade gracefully without breaking the rest of the workflow.\n19: - `TURN.md` remains controller-owned and out of scope.\n20: - No new dependencies are allowed.\n21: - The change is about eliminating projection drift, not redesigning the bus or the manager review flow.\n22: \n23: ## Files Likely Touched\n24: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n25: - `scripts/state_projection_sync.py`\n26: - `scripts/state_projection_probe.py`\n27: - `tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py`\n28: - `tests/unit/test_state_projection_sync.py`\n29: \n30: ## Fases\n31: 1. Extract or reuse the bus-derived state projection logic so it can be called without mutating the probe contract.\n32: 2. Add an idempotent sync path that updates `STATE.md` from the bus-derived state when drift exists, and writes the canonical plain `Estado actual: VALUE` line.\n33: 3. Call the sync path from the main controller status route (`python .agent/agent_controller.py` with no flags) and from `--validate`, so drift is healed both in the human status path and in diagnostics.\n34: 4. Keep the no-bus and missing-state fallbacks graceful.\n35: 5. Add focused tests for matched, drifted, empty-bus, and missing-state cases.\n36: 6. Fix the markdown parser so it only trusts `Estado actual:` when the `# State - {ticket_id}` header matches the active ticket.\n37: \n38: ## Calidad\n39: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py -q`\n40: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_state_projection_sync.py -q`\n41: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n42: \n43: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n44: - `STATE.md` can be synchronized from the bus-derived state without manual edits.\n45: - The sync path is idempotent and safe to call more than once.\n46: - The controller heals drift from the main status path and from `--validate`.\n47: - Missing bus data or missing `STATE.md` falls back gracefully.\n48: - The deterministic probe remains read-only and still reports drift when appropriate.\n49: - `_parse_markdown_state(ticket_id)` validates the ticket header before trusting the state line.\n50: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n\n(End of file - total 50 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-149\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-149\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Bus-backed STATE projection sync\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nMake `STATE.md` a deterministic projection of the bus state so the human-readable state cannot drift behind `events.jsonl`. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift.
