(unknown)\n34: \n35: --- work_plan.md ---\n36: # Work Plan - WP-2026-149\n37: \n38: ## Metadata\n39: - **ID:** WP-2026-149\n40: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n41: - **deliverable_type:** code\n42: - **Titulo:** Bus-backed STATE projection sync\n43: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n44: \n45: ## Objetivo\n46: Make `STATE.md` a deterministic projection of the bus state so the human-readable state cannot drift behind `events.jsonl`. The ticket should reuse the existing projection probe logic as the foundation, keep the behavior idempotent, and avoid changing the review or builder flows beyond the state sync path.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - `STATE.md` will be synchronized from the bus-derived state instead of relying on handwritten markdown updates.\n50: - The existing deterministic probe logic is the source of truth for the comparison and should be refactored or reused, not duplicated.\n51: - The canonical `STATE.md` format will be the simple `Estado actual: VALUE` line, without bullet or bold markers, and the controller write path should be aligned to that format. The sync path must write that same plain line format.\n52: - The sync path must be idempotent and safe to call repeatedly.\n53: - Missing bus data must degrade gracefully without breaking the rest of the workflow.\n54: - `TURN.md` remains controller-owned and out of scope.\n55: - No new dependencies are allowed.\n56: - The change is about eliminating projection drift, not redesigning the bus or the manager review flow.\n57: \n58: ## Files Likely Touched\n59: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n60: - `scripts/state_projection_sync.py`\n61: - `scripts/state_projection_probe.py`\n62: - `tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py`\n63: - `tests/unit/test_state_projection_sync.py`\n64: \n65: ## Fases\n66: 1. Extract or reuse the bus-derived state projection logic so it can be called without mutating the probe contract.\n67: 2. Add an idempotent sync path that updates `STATE.md` from the bus-derived state when drift exists, and writes the canonical plain `Estado actual: VALUE` line.\n68: 3. Call the sync path from the main controller status route (`python .agent/agent_controller.py` with no flags) and from `--validate`, so drift is healed both in the human status path and in diagnostics.\n69: 4. Keep the no-bus and missing-state fallbacks graceful.\n70: 5. Add focused tests for matched, drifted, empty-bus, and missing-state cases.\n71: 6. Fix the markdown parser so it only trusts `Estado actual:` when the `# State - {ticket_id}` header matches the active ticket.\n72: \n73: ## Calidad\n74: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_state_projection_probe.py -q`\n75: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_state_projection_sync.py -q`\n76: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n77: \n78: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n79: - `STATE.md` can be synchronized from the bus-derived state without manual edits.\n80: - The sync path is idempotent and safe to call more than once.\n81: - The controller heals drift from the main status path and from `--validate`.\n82: - Missing bus data or missing `STATE.md` falls back gracefully.\n83: - The deterministic probe remains read-only and still reports drift when appropriate.\n84: - `_parse_markdown_state(ticket_id)` validates the ticket header before trusting the state line.\n85: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n86: \n87: \n88: --- STATE.md ---\n89: # State - WP-2026-149\n90: \n91: Plan Activo: WP-2026-149\n92: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n93: Rol activo: BUILDER\n94: \n95: Resumen:\n96: WP-2026-149 en progreso: el Builder hara que `STATE.md` se sincronice de forma idempotente con el estado derivado del bus.\n97: \n98: Notas:\n99: - El alcance es code.\n100: - `TURN.md` sigue bajo control del controller.\n101: - El sincronizador debe ser stdlib-only y no debe introducir dependencias pesadas.\n102: \n103: \n104: --- TURN.md ---\n105: # TURNO ACTUAL\n106: \n107: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-27 01:04:55\n108: \n109: ---\n110: \n111: ## Agente Activo\n112: \n113: | Campo | Valor |\n114: |-------|-------|\n115: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n116: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-149 |\n117: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n118: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n119: \n120: ---\n121: \n122: ## Instruccion\n123: \n124: > Builder completo WP-2026-149. Revisa el trabajo.\n125: \n126: ---\n127: \n128: ## Archivos a Leer\n129: \n130: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n131: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n132: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n133: \n134: ---\n135: \n136: ## Estado del Sistema\n137: \n138: | Archivo | Estado |\n139: |---------|--------|\n140: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n141: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n142: \n143: ---\n144: \n145: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n146: \n147: \n148: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n149: [execution_log section for WP-2026-149 not found]\n150: \n151: --- git provenance ---\n152: [no new commits since origin/main]\n153: \n154: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n155: Available skills for this role:\n156:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n157:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n158:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n159: \n160: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n161: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n162: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n163: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n164: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n165: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n166: - .session_state.json\n167: \n168: \n169: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n170: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n171: \n172: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n173: \n174: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n175: DECISION: APPROVE\n176: \n177: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n178: \n179: ## SUMMARY\n180: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n181: \n182: ## BLOCKERS\n183: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n184: \n185: ## SUGGESTIONS\n186: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n187: \n188: DECISION: CHANGES\n189: \n190: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n191: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 191 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-149. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-149_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779836726395,"end":1779836726497}},"id":"prt_e6689208d001xFvc0FaEdAL2AB","sessionID":"ses_199774144ffeY6Pp0r8uyZ6nyB","messageID":"msg_e6688bff60019YnK2JwIiqu4p0"}}

Add project root to path\n17: _PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n18: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n19:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n20: \n21: # Import after path setup (E402 intentional for runtime path resolution)\n22: from scripts.state_projection_probe import ProbeResult, run_probe  # noqa: E402\n23: \n24: \n25: def sync_state_projection(\n26:     runtime_dir: Path | None = None,\n27:     collaboration_dir: Path | None = None,\n28:     ticket_id: str | None = None,\n29: ) -> bool:\n30:     \"\"\"\n31:     Synchronize STATE.md from the bus-derived state if drifted.\n32:     Returns True if state was synced or already matched, False if error.\n33:     \"\"\"\n34:     if collaboration_dir is None:\n35:         collaboration_dir = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n36: \n37:     probe_output = run_probe(\n38:         runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n39:         collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n40:         ticket_id=ticket_id,\n41:     )\n42: \n43:     if probe_output.result == ProbeResult.ERROR:\n44:         return False\n45: \n46:     if probe_output.result == ProbeResult.BUS_EMPTY:\n47:         # Fallback gracefully\n48:         return True\n49: \n50:     if probe_output.result == ProbeResult.MATCHED:\n51:         return True\n52: \n53:     if probe_output.result == ProbeResult.DRIFTED and probe_output.bus_derived_state:\n54:         state_md_path = collaboration_dir / \"STATE.md\"\n55:         actual_ticket_id = probe_output.ticket_id\n56: \n57:         # Write canonical plain line format\n58:         content = f\"# State - {actual_ticket_id}\\n\\nEstado actual: {probe_output.bus_derived_state}\\n\"\n59:         state_md_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n60:         return True\n61: \n62:     return False\n63: \n64: def main() -> int:\n65:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Synchronize STATE.md from events.jsonl\")\n66:     parser.add_argument(\"--ticket-id\", type=str, default=None)\n67:     parser.add_argument(\"--runtime-dir\", type=Path, default=None)\n68:     args = parser.parse_args()\n69: \n70:     success = sync_state_projection(\n71:         runtime_dir=args.runtime_dir,\n72:         ticket_id=args.ticket_id\n73:     )\n74:     if success:\n75:         print(\"Sync completed successfully.\")\n76:     else:\n77:         print(\"Sync encountered an error.\")\n78:     return 0 if success else 1\n79: \n80: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n81:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 81 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nIdempotent STATE projection sync.\n\nUpdates STATE.md from the bus-derived state if drift is detected.\nRelies on state_projection_probe.py for detection.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\n# Add project root to path\n_PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\nif str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n    sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"scripts\\state_projection_sync.py","time":{"start":1779836735547,"end":1779836735607}},"id":"prt_e6689458900112j4sN2uFmiW2h","sessionID":"ses_199774144ffeY6Pp0r8uyZ6nyB","messageID":"msg_e668934d8001OTFHVTGdjcpSHs"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779836735617,"sessionID":"ses_199774144ffeY6Pp0r8uyZ6nyB","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_e678484773474916ad6844da","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\state_projection_probe.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\state_projection_probe.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Deterministic STATE projection probe.\n4: \n5: Read-only probe that reconstructs the active ticket state from events.jsonl,\n6: compares it against STATE.md, and reports any drift.\n7: \n8: This probe validates whether the bus can serve as single source of truth\n9: for state projection, without mutating canonical files.\n10: \n11: Usage:\n12:     python scripts/state_projection_probe.py [--ticket-id WP-YYYY-NNN] [--json]\n13: \n14: Exit codes:\n15:     0 - State matched or drift detected (informational)\n16:     1 - Error (file not found, parse error, etc.)\n17: \"\"\"\n18: \n19: from __future__ import annotations\n20: \n21: import argparse\n22: import json\n23: import sys\n24: from dataclasses import dataclass\n25: from enum import Enum\n26: from pathlib import Path\n27: \n28: \n29: # Add project root to path\n30: _PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n31: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n32:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n33: \n34: _AGENT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n35: if str(_AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n36:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_AGENT_DIR))\n37: \n38: from bus.state_machine import StateMachine  # noqa: E402\n39: \n40: \n41: class ProbeResult(str, Enum):\n42:     \"\"\"Result status of the state projection probe.\"\"\"\n43: \n44:     MATCHED = \"matched\"\n45:     DRIFTED = \"drifted\"\n46:     BUS_EMPTY = \"bus_empty\"\n47:     ERROR = \"error\"\n48: \n49: \n50: @dataclass(slots=True)\n51: class ProbeOutput:\n52:     \"\"\"Structured output from the state projection probe.\"\"\"\n53: \n54:     result: ProbeResult\n55:     ticket_id: str\n56:     bus_derived_state: str | None\n57:     markdown_state: str | None\n58:     drift_detected: bool\n59:     events_count: int\n60:     message: str\n61: \n62:     def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n63:         return {\n64:             \"result\": self.result.value,\n65:             \"ticket_id\": self.ticket_id,\n66:             \"bus_derived_state\": self.bus_derived_state,\n67:             \"markdown_state\": self.markdown_state,\n68:             \"drift_detected\": self.drift_detected,\n69:             \"events_count\": self.events_count,\n70:             \"message\": self.message,\n71:         }\n72: \n73: \n74: def _read_events_jsonl(events_path: Path) -> list[dict]:\n75:     \"\"\"\n76:     Read events from the JSONL bus file.\n77: \n78:     Before: File must exist at events_path.\n79:     During: Parses each line as JSON, skipping empty lines and malformed entries.\n80:     After: Returns list of event dicts; empty list if file missing.\n81:     \"\"\"\n82:     if not events_path.exists():\n83:         return []\n84: \n85:     events: list[dict] = []\n86:     for line in events_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n87:         if not line.strip():\n88:             continue\n89:         try:\n90:             events.append(json.loads(line))\n91:         except json.JSONDecodeError:\n92:             # Skip malformed lines silently (read-only probe)\n93:             continue\n94:     return events\n95: \n96: \n97: def _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path: Path) -> str | None:\n98:     \"\"\"\n99:     Extract active ticket ID from work_plan.md.\n100: \n101:     Before: File must exist at work_plan_path.\n102:     During: Parses markdown metadata section for **ID:** field.\n103:     After: Returns ticket ID string or None if not found.\n104:     \"\"\"\n105:     if not work_plan_path.exists():\n106:         return None\n107: \n108:     content = work_plan_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n109:     for line in content.splitlines():\n110:         # Match both \"**ID:** WP-2026-145\" and \"- **ID:** WP-2026-145\"\n111:         if \"**ID:**\" in line:\n112:             # Extract everything after the colon, strip markdown and whitespace\n113:             parts = line.split(\":\", 1)\n114:             if len(parts) == 2:\n115:                 ticket_id = parts[1].strip()\n116:                 # Remove markdown bold markers\n117:                 ticket_id = ticket_id.replace(\"**\", \"\").strip()\n118:                 return ticket_id\n119:     return None\n120: \n121: \n122: def _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path: Path, ticket_id: str) -> str | None:\n123:     \"\"\"\n124:     Parse current state from STATE.md.\n125: \n126:     Before: File must exist at state_md_path.\n127:     During: Searches for 'Estado actual:' line or plan header matching ticket_id.\n128:     After: Returns state string or None if not found.\n129:     \"\"\"\n130:     if not state_md_path.exists():\n131:         return None\n132: \n133:     content = state_md_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n134: \n135:     expected_header = f\"# State - {ticket_id}\"\n136:     if expected_header not in content:\n137:         return None\n138: \n139:     for line in content.splitlines():\n140:         line_stripped = line.strip()\n141:         # Look for \"Estado actual: STATE\" or \"- **Estado actual:** STATE\"\n142:         if line_stripped.startswith(\"Estado actual:\"):\n143:             return line_stripped.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n144:         if line_stripped.startswith(\"- **Estado actual:**\"):\n145:             return line_stripped.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip().replace(\"*\", \"\")\n146:     return None\n147: \n148: \n149: def _filter_events_for_ticket(all_events: list[dict], ticket_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n150:     \"\"\"\n151:     Filter events belonging to a specific ticket.\n152: \n153:     Before: all_events is a list of event dicts.\n154:     During: Filters by ticket_id field.\n155:     After: Returns filtered list (may be empty).\n156:     \"\"\"\n157:     return [e for e in all_events if e.get(\"ticket_id\") == ticket_id]\n158: \n159: \n160: def run_probe(\n161:     runtime_dir: Path | None = None,\n162:     collaboration_dir: Path | None = None,\n163:     ticket_id: str | None = None,\n164: ) -> ProbeOutput:\n165:     \"\"\"\n166:     Run the deterministic state projection probe.\n167: \n168:     Before:\n169:         - runtime_dir must point to .agent/runtime/events/ or similar.\n170:         - collaboration_dir must point to .agent/collaboration/.\n171:         - If ticket_id not provided, reads from work_plan.md.\n172:         - events.jsonl may or may not exist.\n173:         - STATE.md may or may not exist.\n174: \n175:     During:\n176:         - Reads events from events.jsonl (read-only).\n177:         - Derives state using StateMachine.derive_state_from_events().\n178:         - Parses STATE.md for comparison.\n179:         - Compares bus-derived state vs markdown state.\n180: \n181:     After:\n182:         - Returns ProbeOutput with result status.\n183:         - Does NOT mutate any canonical files.\n184:         - May log warnings for invalid transitions (non-blocking).\n185: \n186:     Raises:\n187:         No exceptions are raised; errors are captured in ProbeOutput.\n188:     \"\"\"\n189:     # Resolve paths\n190:     if runtime_dir is None:\n191:         runtime_dir = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n192: \n193:     if collaboration_dir is None:\n194:         collaboration_dir = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n195: \n196:     events_path = runtime_dir / \"events.jsonl\"\n197:     state_md_path = collaboration_dir / \"STATE.md\"\n198:     work_plan_path = collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\"\n199: \n200:     # Determine ticket ID\n201:     if ticket_id is None:\n202:         ticket_id = _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path)\n203:         if not ticket_id:\n204:             return ProbeOutput(\n205:                 result=ProbeResult.ERROR,\n206:                 ticket_id=\"unknown\",\n207:                 bus_derived_state=None,\n208:                 markdown_state=None,\n209:                 drift_detected=False,\n210:                 events_count=0,\n211:                 message=\"Could not determine ticket ID from work_plan.md\",\n212:             )\n213: \n214:     # Read events from bus\n215:     if not events_path.exists():\n216:         return ProbeOutput(\n217:             result=ProbeResult.BUS_EMPTY,\n218:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n219:             bus_derived_state=None,\n220:             markdown_state=None,\n221:             drift_detected=False,\n222:             events_count=0,\n223:             message=f\"Bus file not found: {events_path}\",\n224:         )\n225: \n226:     all_events = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n227:     ticket_events = _filter_events_for_ticket(all_events, ticket_id)\n228: \n229:     if not ticket_events:\n230:         return ProbeOutput(\n231:             result=ProbeResult.BUS_EMPTY,\n232:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n233:             bus_derived_state=None,\n234:             markdown_state=None,\n235:             drift_detected=False,\n236:             events_count=len(all_events),\n237:             message=f\"No events found for ticket {ticket_id}\",\n238:         )\n239: \n240:     # Derive state from bus events using the canonical StateMachine\n241:     try:\n242:         derived_state = StateMachine.derive_state_from_events(ticket_events)\n243:         bus_derived_state = derived_state.value\n244:     except Exception as e:\n245:         return ProbeOutput(\n246:             result=ProbeResult.ERROR,\n247:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n248:             bus_derived_state=None,\n249:             markdown_state=None,\n250:             drift_detected=False,\n251:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n252:             message=f\"Error deriving state from events: {e}\",\n253:         )\n254: \n255:     # Parse markdown state\n256:     markdown_state = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, ticket_id)\n257: \n258:     # Compare states\n259:     if markdown_state is None:\n260:         return ProbeOutput(\n261:             result=ProbeResult.DRIFTED,\n262:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n263:             bus_derived_state=bus_derived_state,\n264:             markdown_state=None,\n265:             drift_detected=True,\n266:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n267:             message=\"STATE.md not found or state not parseable\",\n268:         )\n269: \n270:     drift_detected = bus_derived_state != markdown_state\n271: \n272:     if drift_detected:\n273:         return ProbeOutput(\n274:             result=ProbeResult.DRIFTED,\n275:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n276:             bus_derived_state=bus_derived_state,\n277:             markdown_state=markdown_state,\n278:             drift_detected=True,\n279:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n280:             message=f\"Drift detected: bus={bus_derived_state}, markdown={markdown_state}\",\n281:         )\n282:     else:\n283:         return ProbeOutput(\n284:             result=ProbeResult.MATCHED,\n285:             ticket_id=ticket_id,\n286:             bus_derived_state=bus_derived_state,\n287:             markdown_state=markdown_state,\n288:             drift_detected=False,\n289:             events_count=len(ticket_events),\n290:             message=f\"State matched: {bus_derived_state}\",\n291:         )\n292: \n293: \n294: def main() -> int:\n295:     \"\"\"\n296:     Main entry point for the state projection probe.\n297: \n298:     Before: Command-line arguments parsed.\n299:     During: Runs probe and formats output.\n300:     After: Prints result and returns exit code.\n301:     \"\"\"\n302:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n303:         description=\"Deterministic STATE projection probe (read-only)\"\n304:     )\n305:     parser.add_argument(\n306:         \"--ticket-id\",\n307:         type=str,\n308:         default=None,\n309:         help=\"Ticket ID to probe (default: read from work_plan.md)\",\n310:     )\n311:     parser.add_argument(\n312:         \"--json\",\n313:         action=\"store_true\",\n314:         help=\"Output in JSON format\",\n315:     )\n316:     parser.add_argument(\n317:         \"--runtime-dir\",\n318:         type=Path,\n319:         default=None,\n320:         help=\"Runtime directory containing events.jsonl\",\n321:     )\n322: \n323:     args = parser.parse_args()\n324: \n325:     output = run_probe(runtime_dir=args.runtime_dir, ticket_id=args.ticket_id)\n326: \n327:     if args.json:\n328:         print(json.dumps(output.to_dict(), indent=2))\n329:     else:\n330:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Ticket: {output.ticket_id}\")\n331:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Result: {output.result.value}\")\n332:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Bus-derived state: {output.bus_derived_state or 'N/A'}\")\n333:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Markdown state: {output.markdown_state or 'N/A'}\")\n334:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Events count: {output.events_count}\")\n335:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Drift detected: {output.drift_detected}\")\n336:         print(f\"[STATE PROBE] Message: {output.message}\")\n337: \n338:     # Exit code: 0 for informational results, 1 for errors\n339:     if output.result == ProbeResult.ERROR:\n340:         return 1\n341:     return 0\n342: \n343: \n344: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n345:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 345 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nDeterministic STATE projection probe.\n\nRead-only probe that reconstructs the active ticket state from events.jsonl,\ncompares it against STATE.md, and reports any drift.\n\nThis probe validates whether the bus can serve as single source of truth\nfor state projection, without mutating canonical files.\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/state_projection_probe.py [--ticket-id WP-YYYY-NNN] [--json]\n\nExit codes:\n    0 - State matched or drift detected (informational)\n    1 - Error (file not found, parse error, etc.)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"scripts\\state_projection_probe.py","time":{"start":1779836735579,"end":1779836735613}},"id":"prt_e66894971001jGCv0vsLX3h7co","sessionID":"ses_199774144ffeY6Pp0r8uyZ6nyB","messageID":"msg_e668934d8001OTFHVTGdjcpSHs"}}

\"\"\"Tests for the deterministic state projection probe.\"\"\"\n2: \n3: import sys\n4: from pathlib import Path\n5: \n6: \n7: # Add project root to path\n8: _PROJECT_ROOT = str(_AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n14:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_AGENT_DIR))\n15: \n16: from scripts.state_projection_probe import (  # noqa: E402\n17:     ProbeOutput,\n18:     ProbeResult,\n19: TestReadEventsJsonl:\n28:     \"\"\"Test reading events from JSONL bus file.\"\"\"\n29: \n30:     def test_read_empty_file(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n31:         \"\"\"Empty file returns empty list.\"\"\"\n32:         events_path = tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"\n33:         events_path.write_text(\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n34:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n35:         assert result == []\n36: \n37:     def test_read_valid_events(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n38:         \"\"\"Valid JSONL returns parsed events.\"\"\"\n39:         events_path = tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"\n40:         events_path.write_text(\n41:             '{\"event_type\": \"STATE_CHANGED\", \"ticket_id\": \"WP-2026-001\"}\\n'\n42:             '{\"event_type\": \"REVIEW_DECISION\", \"ticket_id\": \"WP-2026-001\"}\\n',\n43:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n44:         )\n45:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n46:         assert len(result) == 2\n47:         assert result[0][\"event_type\"] == \"STATE_CHANGED\"\n48:         assert result[1][\"event_type\"] == \"REVIEW_DECISION\"\n49: \n50:     def test_skip_malformed_lines(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n51:         \"\"\"Malformed JSON lines are skipped silently.\"\"\"\n52:         events_path = tmp_path / \"events.jsonl\"\n53:         events_path.write_text(\n54:             '{\"event_type\": \"VALID\"}\\nnot valid json\\n{\"event_type\": \"ALSO_VALID\"}\\n',\n55:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n56:         )\n57:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n58:         assert len(result) == 2\n59:         assert result[0][\"event_type\"] == \"VALID\"\n60:         assert result[1][\"event_type\"] == \"ALSO_VALID\"\n61: \n62:     def test_file_not_exists(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n63:         \"\"\"Non-existent file returns empty list.\"\"\"\n64:         events_path = tmp_path / \"nonexistent.jsonl\"\n65:         result = _read_events_jsonl(events_path)\n66:         assert result == []\n67: \n68: \n69: class TestExtractTicketIdFromWorkPlan:\n70:     \"\"\"Test extracting ticket ID from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n71: \n72:     def test_extract_valid_id(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n73:         \"\"\"Valid work_plan.md returns ticket ID.\"\"\"\n74:         work_plan_path = tmp_path / \"work_plan.md\"\n75:         work_plan_path.write_text(\n76:             \"# Work Plan - WP-2026-145\\n\\n\"\n77:             \"## Metadata\\n\"\n78:             \"- **ID:** WP-2026-145\\n\"\n79:             \"- **Estado:** APPROVED\\n\",\n80:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n81:         )\n82:         result = _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path)\n83:         assert result == \"WP-2026-145\"\n84: \n85:     def test_no_id_field(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n86:         \"\"\"Missing ID field returns None.\"\"\"\n87:         work_plan_path = tmp_path / \"work_plan.md\"\n88:         work_plan_path.write_text(\n89:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\nNo ID here\\n\",\n90:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n91:         )\n92:         result = _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path)\n93:         assert result is None\n94: \n95:     def test_file_not_exists(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n96:         \"\"\"Non-existent file returns None.\"\"\"\n97:         work_plan_path = tmp_path / \"nonexistent.md\"\n98:         result = _extract_ticket_id_from_work_plan(work_plan_path)\n99:         assert result is None\n100: \n101: \n102: class TestParseMarkdownState:\n103:     \"\"\"Test parsing state from STATE.md.\"\"\"\n104: \n105:     def test_parse_valid_state(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n106:         \"\"\"Valid STATE.md returns state.\"\"\"\n107:         state_md_path = tmp_path / \"STATE.md\"\n108:         state_md_path.write_text(\n109:             \"# State - WP-2026-145\\n\\n\"\n110:             \"Plan Activo: WP-2026-145\\n\"\n111:             \"Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\\n\"\n112:             \"Rol activo: BUILDER\\n\",\n113:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n114:         )\n115:         result = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, \"WP-2026-145\")\n116:         assert result == \"IN_PROGRESS\"\n117: \n118:     def test_no_estado_actual_line(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n119:         \"\"\"Missing 'Estado actual:' line returns None.\"\"\"\n120:         state_md_path = tmp_path / \"STATE.md\"\n121:         state_md_path.write_text(\n122:             \"# State - WP-2026-145\\n\\nPlan Activo: WP-2026-145\\nSome other content\\n\",\n123:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n124:         )\n125:         result = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, \"WP-2026-145\")\n126:         assert result is None\n127: \n128:     def test_file_not_exists(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n129:         \"\"\"Non-existent file returns None.\"\"\"\n130:         state_md_path = tmp_path / \"nonexistent.md\"\n131:         result = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, \"WP-2026-145\")\n132:         assert result is None\n133: \n134:     def test_header_mismatch_returns_none(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n135:         \"\"\"Parser returns None when header ticket_id does not match requested ticket_id.\n136: \n137:         This is the Phase 6 fix: _parse_markdown_state must validate the ticket header\n138:         before trusting the state line, preventing cross-ticket state pollution.\n139:         \"\"\"\n140:         state_md_path = tmp_path / \"STATE.md\"\n141:         # STATE.md has header for WP-2026-100 but we request WP-2026-145\n142:         state_md_path.write_text(\n143:             \"# State - WP-2026-100\\n\\n\"\n144:             \"Plan Activo: WP-2026-100\\n\"\n145:             \"Estado actual: COMPLETED\\n\",\n146:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n147:         )\n148:         # Requesting different ticket should return None (header mismatch)\n149:         result = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, \"WP-2026-145\")\n150:         assert result is None\n151: \n152:     def test_header_mismatch_does_not_trust_state_line(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n153:         \"\"\"Parser must not trust 'Estado actual:' when header belongs to different ticket.\n154: \n155:         Regression test: even if 'Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS' exists, if the header\n156:         is for a different ticket, the parser must reject it.\n157:         \"\"\"\n158:         state_md_path = tmp_path / \"STATE.md\"\n159:         # Old ticket header with a state line that could be misleading\n160:         state_md_path.write_text(\n161:             \"# State - WP-2026-099\\n\\n\"\n162:             \"Plan Activo: WP-2026-099\\n\"\n163:             \"Estado actual: READY_FOR_REVIEW\\n\",\n164:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n165:         )\n166:         # Must NOT return 'READY_FOR_REVIEW' for a different ticket\n167:         result = _parse_markdown_state(state_md_path, \"WP-2026-149\")\n168:         assert result is None\n169: \n170: \n171: class TestFilterEventsForTicket:\n172:     \"\"\"Test filtering events by ticket \"WP-2026-001\")\n196:         assert result == []\n197: \n198: \n199: class TestProbeOutput:\n200:     \"\"\"Test ProbeOutput dataclass.\"\"\"\n201: \n202:     def test_to_dict(self) -> None:\n203: is False\n219:         assert result[\"events_count\"] == 5\n220:         assert result[\"message\"] == \"State matched: IN_PROGRESS\"\n221: \n222: \n223: class TestRunProbeMatched:\n224: runtime_dir = tmp_path / \"events\"\n229:         runtime_dir.mkdir()\n230:         collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \"collaboration\"\n231:         collaboration_dir.mkdir()\n232: \n233:         # == 1\n265: \n266: \n267: class TestRunProbeDrift:\n268:     \"\"\"Test run_probe when drift is detected.\"\"\"\n269: \n270:     def test_states_differ(self, tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n271: work_plan.md\n293:         work_plan_path = collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\"\n294:         work_plan_path.write_text(\n295:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-145\\n\",\n296: {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779866050656,"sessionID":"ses_197b78a8dffeevtzSDMZvjmsG0","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_b8ff2848eea1460a89266273","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Work Plan - WP-2026-150\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-150\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** code\n7: - **Titulo:** Project scanner with import map\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: Build a deterministic project scanner that turns a destination repository into a compact, token-efficient `project-map.json` with a real Python `importMap`, file inventory, fingerprinting, and framework hints so the Builder can consume destination context without reading the whole tree.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - The scanner must be stdlib-only and deterministic.\n15: - Python import relationships must come from `ast.parse()` rather than heuristics or LLM output.\n16: - The scanner should bucket files into useful categories, not dump the whole repository.\n17: - The output should be compact enough to seed `## Project Context` in the ticket flow.\n18: - The scanner writes a structured artifact at `.agent/context/project-map.json`; the controller reads that artifact and does not re-scan inline in the hot path.\n19: - The controller hook may consume the scanner output to enrich work-plan context, but the scanner remains the source of truth.\n20: - For local imports that resolve to files inside the repository, preserve the full module path in `importMap`; for stdlib and external imports, collapse to the top-level package name.\n21: - Record `SyntaxError` parse failures explicitly in the output instead of swallowing them silently.\n22: - No new dependencies are allowed.\n23: - Noise-heavy directories must be excluded so the output stays portable and relevant.\n24: \n25: ## Files Likely Touched\n26: - `scripts/project_scanner.py`\n27: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n28: - `tests/unit/test_project_scanner.py`\n29: - `.agent/context/project-map.json` (generated artifact)\n30: \n31: ## Fases\n32: 1. Implement a deterministic scanner over the destination repository that collects the file inventory, fingerprints, and category buckets with exclusion filters for non-product noise.\n33: 2. Add Python AST import extraction so the scanner emits a real `importMap` for source files, preserving local module depth and collapsing only stdlib/external imports.\n34: 3. Detect framework hints from common manifests such as `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, and `package.json`.\n35: 4. Emit a compact `project-map.json` under `.agent/context/` that can be reused by the controller to populate `## Project Context` for work plans.\n36: 5. Wire the controller to consume the scanner artifact when preparing destination context, complementing the existing project-map flow instead of re-scanning inline.\n37: 6. Cover the scanner with focused tests for exclusions, fingerprints, import discovery, framework hints, parse failures, and deterministic output.\n38: \n39: ## Calidad\n40: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_project_scanner.py -q`\n41: - `ruff check scripts tests`\n42: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n43: \n44: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n45: - The scanner emits a compact `project-map.json` for the destination repository.\n46: - Python imports are captured via AST, not by string search.\n47: - Noise directories are excluded so the resulting map stays token-efficient.\n48: - The controller can use the scanner summary to enrich `## Project Context` without duplicating logic.\n49: - The scanner is deterministic and stdlib-only.\n50: - The tests cover the main scan paths and the exclusion behavior.\n51: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n\n(End of file - total 51 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-150\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-150\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Project scanner with import map\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nBuild a deterministic project scanner that turns a destination repository into a compact, token-efficient `project-map.json` with a real Python `importMap`, file inventory, fingerprinting, and framework hints so the Builder can consume destination context without reading the whole tree.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- The scanner must be stdlib-only and deterministic.\n- Python import relationships must come from `ast.parse()` rather than heuristics or LLM output.\n- The scanner should bucket files into useful categories, not dump the whole repository.\n- The output should be compact enough to seed `## Project Context` in the ticket flow.\n- The scanner writes a structured artifact at `.agent/context/project-map.json`; the controller reads that artifact and does not re-scan inline in the hot path.\n- The controller hook may consume the scanner output to enrich work-plan context, but the scanner remains the source of truth.\n- For local imports that resolve to files inside the repository, preserve the full module path in `importMap`; for stdlib and external imports, collapse to the top-level package name.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779866050580,"end":1779866050652}},"id":"prt_e6848911e001Au9LlmVwGU6mZZ","sessionID":"ses_197b78a8dffeevtzSDMZvjmsG0","messageID":"msg_e6848763e001MegW512jFQiOGF"}}

Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The Builder runs the existing suite, sees it pass, and declares quality gates satisfied — but the new functions introduced in the diff have no direct tests. Th... (unknown)\n34: \n35: --- work_plan.md ---\n36: # Work Plan - WP-2026-150\n37: \n38: ## Metadata\n39: - **ID:** WP-2026-150\n40: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n41: - **deliverable_type:** code\n42: - **Titulo:** Project scanner with import map\n43: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n44: \n45: ## Objetivo\n46: Build a deterministic project scanner that turns a destination repository into a compact, token-efficient `project-map.json` with a real Python `importMap`, file inventory, fingerprinting, and framework hints so the Builder can consume destination context without reading the whole tree.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - The scanner must be stdlib-only and deterministic.\n50: - Python import relationships must come from `ast.parse()` rather than heuristics or LLM output.\n51: - The scanner should bucket files into useful categories, not dump the whole repository.\n52: - The output should be compact enough to seed `## Project Context` in the ticket flow.\n53: - The scanner writes a structured artifact at `.agent/context/project-map.json`; the controller reads that artifact and does not re-scan inline in the hot path.\n54: - The controller hook may consume the scanner output to enrich work-plan context, but the scanner remains the source of truth.\n55: - For local imports that resolve to files inside the repository, preserve the full module path in `importMap`; for stdlib and external imports, collapse to the top-level package name.\n56: - Record `SyntaxError` parse failures explicitly in the output instead of swallowing them silently.\n57: - No new dependencies are allowed.\n58: - Noise-heavy directories must be excluded so the output stays portable and relevant.\n59: \n60: ## Files Likely Touched\n61: - `scripts/project_scanner.py`\n62: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n63: - `tests/unit/test_project_scanner.py`\n64: - `.agent/context/project-map.json` (generated artifact)\n65: \n66: ## Fases\n67: 1. Implement a deterministic scanner over the destination repository that collects the file inventory, fingerprints, and category buckets with exclusion filters for non-product noise.\n68: 2. Add Python AST import extraction so the scanner emits a real `importMap` for source files, preserving local module depth and collapsing only stdlib/external imports.\n69: 3. Detect framework hints from common manifests such as `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, and `package.json`.\n70: 4. Emit a compact `project-map.json` under `.agent/context/` that can be reused by the controller to populate `## Project Context` for work plans.\n71: 5. Wire the controller to consume the scanner artifact when preparing destination context, complementing the existing project-map flow instead of re-scanning inline.\n72: 6. Cover the scanner with focused tests for exclusions, fingerprints, import discovery, framework hints, parse failures, and deterministic output.\n73: \n74: ## Calidad\n75: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_project_scanner.py -q`\n76: - `ruff check scripts tests`\n77: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n78: \n79: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n80: - The scanner emits a compact `project-map.json` for the destination repository.\n81: - Python imports are captured via AST, not by string search.\n82: - Noise directories are excluded so the resulting map stays token-efficient.\n83: - The controller can use the scanner summary to enrich `## Project Context` without duplicating logic.\n84: - The scanner is deterministic and stdlib-only.\n85: - The tests cover the main scan paths and the exclusion behavior.\n86: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n87: \n88: \n89: --- STATE.md ---\n90: # State - WP-2026-150\n91: \n92: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n93: \n94: \n95: --- TURN.md ---\n96: # TURNO ACTUAL\n97: \n98: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-27 09:13:57\n99: \n100: ---\n101: \n102: ## Agente Activo\n103: \n104: | Campo | Valor |\n105: |-------|-------|\n106: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n107: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-150 |\n108: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n109: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n110: \n111: ---\n112: \n113: ## Instruccion\n114: \n115: > Builder completo WP-2026-150. Revisa el trabajo.\n116: \n117: ---\n118: \n119: ## Archivos a Leer\n120: \n121: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n122: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n123: 3. `.agent/context/project_map.md` (Estructura)\n124: \n125: ---\n126: \n127: ## Estado del Sistema\n128: \n129: | Archivo | Estado |\n130: |---------|--------|\n131: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n132: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n133: \n134: ---\n135: \n136: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n137: \n138: \n139: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n140: [execution_log section for WP-2026-150 not found]\n141: \n142: --- git provenance ---\n143: [no new commits since origin/main]\n144: \n145: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n146: Available skills for this role:\n147:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n148:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n149:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n150: \n151: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n152: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n153: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n154: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n155: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n156: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n157: - .session_state.json\n158: \n159: \n160: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n161: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n162: \n163: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n164: \n165: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n166: DECISION: APPROVE\n167: \n168: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n169: \n170: ## SUMMARY\n171: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n172: \n173: ## BLOCKERS\n174: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n175: \n176: ## SUGGESTIONS\n177: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n178: \n179: DECISION: CHANGES\n180: \n181: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n182: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 182 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-150. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-150_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779866050571,"end":1779866050688}},"id":"prt_e68488c56001QtQeYjR7b2NIUH","sessionID":"ses_197b78a8dffeevtzSDMZvjmsG0","messageID":"msg_e6848763e001MegW512jFQiOGF"}}

Category buckets (python, markdown, config, etc.)\n7: - Real Python importMap via ast.parse()\n8: - Framework hints from manifests\n9: - Exclusion filters for non-product noise\n10: \n11: Usage:\n12: =============================================================================\n41: # Path Resolution\n42: # =============================================================================\n43: try:\n44:     from runtime.project_root import get_context_dir, resolve_project_root\n45: except ImportError:\n46:     resolve_project_root = None\n47:     get_context_dir = None\n48: \n49: \n50: def _project_root() -> Path:\n51:     if resolve_project_root is not None:\n52:         return resolve_project_root()\n53:     return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]\n54: \n55: \n56: def _context_dir() -> Path:\n57:     if get_context_dir is not None:\n58:         return get_context_dir()\n59:     return _project_root() / \".agent\" / \"context\"\n60: \n61: \n62: class _LazyPath:\n63:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n64:         self._resolver = resolver\n65: \n66:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n67:         return self._resolver()\n68: \n69:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n70:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n71: \n72:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n73:         return self.resolve() / other\n74: \n75:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n76:         return str(self.resolve())\n77: \n78:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n79:         return str(self.resolve())\n80: \n81:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n82:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n83: \n84: \n85: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n86: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(_context_dir)\n87: OUTPUT_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: _context_dir() / \"project-map.json\")\n88: \n89: # =============================================================================\n90: # Exclusion Filters\n91: # =============================================================================\n92: \n93: # Directories to exclude (noise, non-product, runtime artifacts)\n94: EXCLUDE_DIRS = {\n95:     \".git\",\n96:     \".venv\",\n97:     \"venv\",\n98:     \"__pycache__\",\n99:     \".pytest_cache\",\n100:     \".ruff_cache\",\n101:     \".mypy_cache\",\n102:     \".tox\",\n103:     \".eggs\",\n104:     \"node_modules\",\n105:     \"build\",\n106:     \"dist\",\n107:     \"*.egg-info\",\n108:     \".cache\",\n109:     \".uv-cache\",\n110:     \"uv-cache\",\n111:     \"graphify-out\",\n112:     \"reviews\",\n113:     \"review_packets\",\n114: }\n115: \n116: # File patterns to exclude\n117: EXCLUDE_FILE_PATTERNS = {\n118:     \"*.pyc\",\n119:     \"*.pyo\",\n120:     \"*.so\",\n121:     \"*.dll\",\n122:     \"*.exe\",\n123:     \"*.whl\",\n124:     \"*.tar.gz\",\n125:     \"*.zip\",\n126:     \"*.log\",\n127:     \"*.lock\",\n128:     \".DS_Store\",\n129:     \".gitignore\",\n130:     \".gitattributes\",\n131:     \"*.orig\",\n132:     \"*.bak\",\n133:     \"*.swp\",\n134:     \"*.swo\",\n135:     \"*~\",\n136: }\n137: \n138: # Extensions to include (focus on product code and docs)\n139: INCLUDE_EXTENSIONS = {\n140:     \".py\",\n141:     \".md\",\n142:     \".txt\",\n143:     \".json\",\n144:     \".yaml\",\n145:     \".yml\",\n146:     \".toml\",\n147:     \".ini\",\n148:     \".cfg\",\n149:     \".sh\",\n150:     \".ps1\",\n151:     \".bat\",\n152:     \".html\",\n153:     \".css\",\n154:     \".js\",\n155:     \".ts\",\n156:     \".tsx\",\n157:     \".jsx\",\n158: }\n159: \n160: \n161: def _matches_pattern(path: Path, patterns: set[str]) -> bool:\n162:     \"\"\"Check if path matches any glob pattern.\"\"\"\n163:     name = path.name\n164:     for pattern in patterns:\n165:         if pattern.startswith(\"*.\"):\n166:             if name.endswith(pattern[1:]):\n167:                 return True\n168:         elif pattern.endswith(\"*\"):\n169:             if name.startswith(pattern[:-1]):\n170:                 return True\n171:         elif name == pattern:\n172:             return True\n173:     return False\n174: \n175: \n176: def _is_excluded(path: Path, project_root: Path) -> bool:\n177:     \"\"\"Check if path should be excluded from scan.\"\"\"\n178:     # Check directory exclusions\n179:     for part in path.parts:\n180:         if part in EXCLUDE_DIRS:\n181:             return True\n182:         # Handle patterns like *.egg-info\n183:         if part.endswith(\".egg-info\"):\n184:             return True\n185: \n186:     # Check specific path-based exclusions (relative to project root)\n187:     try:\n188:         rel_str = str(path.relative_to(project_root))\n189:         # Exclude .agent runtime subdirectories (but not .agent/config or .agent/collaboration surfaces)\n190:         if rel_str.startswith(\".agent/runtime/tmp\") or rel_str.startswith(\".agent\\\\runtime\\\\tmp\"):\n191:             return True\n192:         if \"collaboration/archive\" in rel_str or \"collaboration\\\\archive\" in rel_str:\n193:             return True\n194:         if \"collaboration/_archive\" in rel_str or \"collaboration\\\\_archive\" in rel_str:\n195:             return True\n196:         # Exclude external agent_system project\n197:         if rel_str.startswith(\"agent_system\") or rel_str.startswith(\"agent_system\\\\\"):\n198:             return True\n199:     except ValueError:\n200:         # Path not relative to project_root - skip relative checks\n201:         pass\n202: \n203:     # Check file pattern exclusions\n204:     if _matches_pattern(path, EXCLUDE_FILE_PATTERNS):\n205:         return True\n206: \n207:     # Check extension whitelist\n208:     return bool(path.suffix and path.suffix not in INCLUDE_EXTENSIONS)\n209: \n210: \n211: def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:\n212:     \"\"\"Compute SHA256 hash of file content.\"\"\"\n213:     h = hashlib.sha256()\n214:     try:\n215:         h.update(path.read_bytes())\n216:         return h.hexdigest()\n217:     except OSError:\n218:         return \"UNREADABLE\"\n219: \n220: \n221: def rel_path(path: Path, project_root: Path) -> str:\n222:     \"\"\"Get relative path as forward-slash string.\"\"\"\n223:     try:\n224:         return path.relative_to(project_root).as_posix()\n225:     except ValueError:\n226:         return path.as_posix()\n227: \n228: \n229: # =============================================================================\n230: # Category Buckets\n231: # =============================================================================\n232: \n233: CATEGORY_RULES = [\n234:     # Source code\n235:     (\".py\", \"python\"),\n236:     (\".js\", \"javascript\"),\n237:     (\".ts\", \"typescript\"),\n238:     (\".tsx\", \"typescript\"),\n239:     (\".jsx\", \"javascript\"),\n240:     (\".html\", \"markup\"),\n241:     (\".css\", \"styles\"),\n242:     # Documentation\n243:     (\".md\", \"documentation\"),\n244:     (\".txt\", \"documentation\"),\n245:     (\".rst\", \"documentation\"),\n246:     # Configuration\n247:     (\".toml\", \"config\"),\n248:     (\".yaml\", \"config\"),\n249:     (\".yml\", \"config\"),\n250:     (\".json\", \"config\"),\n251:     (\".ini\", \"config\"),\n252:     (\".cfg\", \"config\"),\n253:     # Scripts\n254:     (\".sh\", \"scripts\"),\n255:     (\".ps1\", \"scripts\"),\n256:     (\".bat\", \"scripts\"),\n257: ]\n258: \n259: \n260: def categorize_file(path: Path) -> str:\n261:     \"\"\"Categorize file by extension.\"\"\"\n262:     suffix = path.suffix.lower()\n263:     for ext, category in CATEGORY_RULES:\n264:         if suffix == ext:\n265:             return category\n266:     return \"other\"\n267: \n268: \n269: # =============================================================================\n270: # AST-based Import Extraction\n271: # =============================================================================\n272: \n273: \n274: def extract_imports(path: Path, project_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n275:     \"\"\"\n276:     Extract Python imports via AST parsing.\n277: \n278:     Before: Requires valid Python file path and project root.\n279:     During: Parses file with ast.parse(), walks Import/ImportFrom nodes,\n280:             resolves local modules to full paths, collapses stdlib/external.\n281:     After: Returns dict with importMap entries and any parse errors.\n282: \n283:     Returns:\n284:         Dict with keys:\n285:         - 'imports': list of import entries\n286:         - 'error': SyntaxError message if parse failed, else None\n287:     \"\"\"\n288:     result: dict[str, Any] = {\"imports\": [], \"error\": None}\n289: \n290:     try:\n291:         source = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n292:         tree = ast.parse(source, filename=str(path))\n293:     except SyntaxError as e:\n294:         result[\"error\"] = f\"SyntaxError: {e.msg} at line {e.lineno}\"\n295:         return result\n296:     except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:\n297:         result[\"error\"] = f\"ReadError: {type(e).__name__}: {e}\"\n298:         return result\n299: \n300:     # Collect all Python files in project for local module resolution\n301:     local_modules = _collect_local_modules(project_root)\n302: \n303:     for node in ast.walk(tree):\n304:         if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n305:             for alias in node.names:\n306:                 import_entry = _classify_import(alias.name, local_modules, project_root)\n307:                 result[\"imports\"].append(import_entry)\n308:         elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n309:             module = node.module or \"\"\n310:             for alias in node.names:\n311:                 full_name = f\"{module}.{alias.name}\" if module else alias.name\n312:                 import_entry = _classify_import(full_name, local_modules, project_root)\n313:                 result[\"imports\"].append(import_entry)\n314: \n315:     return result\n316: \n317: \n318: def _collect_local_modules(project_root: Path) -> dict[str, str]:\n319:     \"\"\"\n320:     Build a map of importable module names to their file paths.\n321: \n322:     Before: Requires project root directory.\n323:     During: Scans for .py files, computes module names from paths.\n324:     After: Returns dict mapping module name (dotted) to relative file path.\n325:     \"\"\"\n326:     local_modules: dict[str, str] = {}\n327: \n328:     for py_file in project_root.rglob(\"*.py\"):\n329:         if _is_excluded(py_file, project_root):\n330:             continue\n331: \n332:         rel = rel_path(py_file, project_root)\n333: \n334:         # Compute module name from path\n335:         # e.g., \"foo/bar/baz.py\" -> \"foo.bar.baz\"\n336:         # e.g., \"foo/__init__.py\" -> \"foo\"\n337:         parts = list(py_file.relative_to(project_root).parts)\n338:         if parts[-1] == \"__init__.py\":\n339:             parts = parts[:-1]\n340:             if not parts:\n341:                 continue\n342:         else:\n343:             parts[-1] = parts[-1][:-3]  # remove .py\n344: \n345:         module_name = \".\".join(parts)\n346:         local_modules[module_name] = rel\n347: \n348:     return local_modules\n349: \n350: \n351: def _classify_import(\n352:     import_name: str, local_modules: dict[str, str], project_root: Path\n353: ) -> dict[str, str]:\n354:     \"\"\"\n355:     Classify an import as local, stdlib, or external.\n356: \n357:     Before: Requires import name, local_modules map, and project root.\n358:     During: Checks if import resolves to local file, stdlib, or external package.\n359:     After: Returns import entry with type and path/top-level name.\n360: \n361:     Strategy:\n362:     - For local imports: preserve full module path\n363:     - For stdlib/external: collapse to top-level package name\n364:     \"\"\"\n365:     # Try to resolve as local module\n366:     # Check progressively longer prefixes\n367:     parts = import_name.split(\".\")\n368: \n369:     for i in range(len(parts), 0, -1):\n370:         candidate = \".\".join(parts[:i])\n371:         if candidate in local_modules:\n372:             return {\n373:                 \"name\": import_name,\n374:                 \"type\": \"local\",\n375:                 \"path\": local_modules[candidate],\n376:             }\n377: \n378:     # Not local - check if stdlib or external\n379:     top_level = parts[0]\n380: \n381:     # Simple stdlib check (common modules)\n382:     stdlib_modules = {\n383:         \"os\",\n384:         \"sys\",\n385:         \"re\",\n386:         \"json\",\n387:         \"ast\",\n388:         \"pathlib\",\n389:         \"typing\",\n390:         \"collections\",\n391:         \"itertools\",\n392:         \"functools\",\n393:         \"hashlib\",\n394:         \"datetime\",\n395:         \"subprocess\",\n396:         \"io\",\n397:         \"abc\",\n398:         \"contextlib\",\n399:         \"dataclasses\",\n400:         \"enum\",\n401:         \"logging\",\n402:         \"argparse\",\n403:         \"shutil\",\n404:         \"tempfile\",\n405:         \"traceback\",\n406:         \"unittest\",\n407:         \"copy\",\n408:         \"math\",\n409:         \"random\",\n410:         \"time\",\n411:         \"calendar\",\n412:         \"pickle\",\n413:         \"sqlite3\",\n414:         \"csv\",\n415:         \"xml\",\n416:         \"html\",\n417:         \"http\",\n418:         \"urllib\",\n419:         \"socket\",\n420:         \"ssl\",\n421:         \"threading\",\n422:         \"multiprocessing\",\n423:         \"asyncio\",\n424:         \"concurrent\",\n425:         \"queue\",\n426:         \"struct\",\n427:         \"codecs\",\n428:         \"unicodedata\",\n429:         \"string\",\n430:         \"textwrap\",\n431:         \"difflib\",\n432:         \"pprint\",\n433:         \"reprlib\",\n434:         \"numbers\",\n435:         \"cmath\",\n436:         \"decimal\",\n437:         \"fractions\",\n438:         \"statistics\",\n439:         \"array\",\n440:         \"weakref\",\n441:         \"types\",\n442:         \"inspect\",\n443:         \"importlib\",\n444:         \"pkgutil\",\n445:         \"modulefinder\",\n446:         \"runpy\",\n447:         \"warnings\",\n448:         \"contextvars\",\n449:         \"graphlib\",\n450:         \"zoneinfo\",\n451:         \"tomllib\",\n452:     }\n453: \n454:     if top_level in stdlib_modules:\n455:         return {\"name\": import_name, \"type\": \"stdlib\", \"package\": top_level}\n456: \n457:     # External package\n458:     return {\"name\": import_name, \"type\": \"external\", \"package\": top_level}\n459: \n460: \n461: # =============================================================================\n462: # Framework Hints Detection\n463: # =============================================================================\n464: \n465: \n466: def _detect_pyproject_hints(project_root: Path) -> tuple[dict, list, list]:\n467:     \"\"\"Detect hints from pyproject.toml.\"\"\"\n468:     python_hints = {}\n469:     frameworks = []\n470:     tools = []\n471: \n472:     pyproject = project_root / \"pyproject.toml\"\n473:     if not pyproject.exists():\n474:         return python_hints, frameworks, tools\n475: \n476:     python_hints[\"has_pyproject\"] = True\n477:     content = pyproject.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n478: \n479:     if \"poetry\" in content:\n480:         frameworks.append(\"poetry\")\n481:     if \"setuptools\" in content or \"[build-system]\" in content:\n482:         tools.append(\"setuptools\")\n483:     if \"pytest\" in content:\n484:         tools.append(\"pytest\")\n485:     if \"ruff\" in content:\n486:         tools.append(\"ruff\")\n487:     if \"mypy\" in content:\n488:         tools.append(\"mypy\")\n489:     if \"uv\" in content:\n490:         tools.append(\"uv\")\n491: \n492:     return python_hints, frameworks, tools\n493: \n494: \n495: def _detect_requirements_hints(project_root: Path) -> tuple[dict, list, list]:\n496:     \"\"\"Detect hints from requirements.txt.\"\"\"\n497:     python_hints = {}\n498:     frameworks = []\n499:     tools = []\n500: \n501:     requirements = project_root / \"requirements.txt\"\n502:     if not requirements.exists():\n503:         return python_hints, frameworks, tools\n504: \n505:     python_hints[\"has_requirements\"] = True\n506:     content = requirements.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lower()\n507: \n508:     if \"flask\" in content:\n509:         frameworks.append(\"flask\")\n510:     if \"django\" in content:\n511:         frameworks.append(\"django\")\n512:     if \"fastapi\" in content:\n513:         frameworks.append(\"fastapi\")\n514:     if \"pydantic\" in content:\n515:         frameworks.append(\"pydantic\")\n516:     if \"numpy\" in content:\n517:         tools.append(\"numpy\")\n518:     if \"pandas\" in content:\n519:         tools.append(\"pandas\")\n520: \n521:     return python_hints, frameworks, tools\n522: \n523: \n524: def _detect_package_json_hints(project_root: Path) -> tuple[dict, list, list]:\n525:     \"\"\"Detect hints from package.json.\"\"\"\n526:     js_hints = {}\n527:     frameworks = []\n528:     tools = []\n529: \n530:     package_json = project_root / \"package.json\"\n531:     if not package_json.exists():\n532:         return js_hints, frameworks, tools\n533: \n534:     try:\n535:         js_hints[\"has_package_json\"] = True\n536:         pkg_data = json.loads(package_json.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n537:         deps = pkg_data.get(\"dependencies\", {})\n538:         dev_deps = pkg_data.get(\"devDependencies\", {})\n539: \n540:         if \"react\" in deps:\n541:             frameworks.append(\"react\")\n542:         if \"vue\" in deps:\n543:             frameworks.append(\"vue\")\n544:         if \"typescript\" in deps or \"typescript\" in dev_deps:\n545:             tools.append(\"typescript\")\n546:         if \"webpack\" in dev_deps:\n547:             tools.append(\"webpack\")\n548:         if \"vite\" in dev_deps:\n549:             tools.append(\"vite\")\n550:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n551:         pass\n552: \n553:     return js_hints, frameworks, tools\n554: \n555: \n556: def detect_framework_hints(project_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n557:     \"\"\"\n558:     Detect framework hints from common manifests.\n559: \n560:     Before: Requires project root directory.\n561:     During: Reads pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, package.json, etc.\n562:     After: Returns dict with detected frameworks and metadata.\n563:     \"\"\"\n564:     frameworks = []\n565:     tools = []\n566: \n567:     # Check pyproject.toml\n568:     py_python, py_fw, py_tools = _detect_pyproject_hints(project_root)\n569:     frameworks.extend(py_fw)\n570:     tools.extend(py_tools)\n571: \n572:     # Check requirements.txt\n573:     req_python, req_fw, req_tools = _detect_requirements_hints(project_root)\n574:     frameworks.extend(req_fw)\n575:     tools.extend(req_tools)\n576: \n577:     # Check uv.lock\n578:     uv_lock = project_root / \"uv.lock\"\n579:     python_hints = {**py_python, **req_python}\n580:     if uv_lock.exists():\n581:         python_hints[\"has_uv_lock\"] = True\n582:         tools.append(\"uv\")\n583: \n584:     # Check package.json\n585:     pkg_js, pkg_fw, pkg_tools = _detect_package_json_hints(project_root)\n586:     frameworks.extend(pkg_fw)\n587:     tools.extend(pkg_tools)\n588: \n589:     # Check for Makefile\n590:     if (project_root / \"Makefile\").exists():\n591:         tools.append(\"make\")\n592: \n593:     # Check for Dockerfile\n594:     if (project_root / \"Dockerfile\").exists():\n595:         tools.append(\"docker\")\n596: \n597:     # Remove duplicates\n598:     frameworks = list(set(frameworks))\n599:     tools = list(set(tools))\n600: \n601:     return {\n602:         \"python\": python_hints,\n603:         \"javascript\": pkg_js,\n604:         \"frameworks\": frameworks,\n605:         \"tools\": tools,\n606:     }\n607: \n608: \n609: # =============================================================================\n610: # Scanner Main Function\n611: # =============================================================================\n612: \n613: \n614: def scan_project(project_root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n615:     \"\"\"\n616:     Scan project and build project-map.json.\n617: \n618:     Before: Requires project root (defaults to current project).\n619:     During: Collects files, computes fingerprints, extracts imports,\n620:             detects frameworks, builds categorized inventory.\n621:     After: Returns structured project map dict.\n622: \n623:     Output structure:\n624:     {\n625:         \"version\": \"1.0\",\n626:         \"generated\": \"ISO timestamp\",\n627:         \"project_root\": \"absolute path\",\n628:         \"summary\": {\n629:             \"total_files\": int,\n630:             \"total_size_bytes\": int,\n631:             \"categories\": {category: count, ...}\n632:         },\n633:         \"files\": {\n634:             \"category\": [\n635:                 {\"path\": str, \"size\": int, \"sha256\": str},\n636:                 ...\n637:             ],\n638:             ...\n639:         },\n640:         \"importMap\": {\n641:             \"python_files\": {\n642:                 \"relative/path.py\": {\n643:                     \"imports\": [...],\n644:                     \"error\": str | null\n645:                 },\n646:                 ...\n647:             }\n648:         },\n649:         \"frameworks\": {\n650:             \"frameworks\": [...],\n651:             \"tools\": [...],\n652:             \"python\": {...},\n653:             \"javascript\": {...}\n654:         }\n655:     }\n656:     \"\"\"\n657:     if project_root is None:\n658:         project_root = _project_root()\n659: \n660:     project_root = project_root.resolve()\n661: \n662:     # Collect files\n663:     files_by_category: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}\n664:     import_map: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}\n665:     total_size = 0\n666:     parse_errors: list[str] = []\n667: \n668:     for path in sorted(project_root.rglob(\"*\")):\n669:         if not path.is_file():\n670:             continue\n671:         if _is_excluded(path, project_root):\n672:             continue\n673: \n674:         rel = rel_path(path, project_root)\n675:         size = path.stat().st_size\n676:         sha = sha256_file(path)\n677:         category = categorize_file(path)\n678: \n679:         total_size += size\n680: \n681:         # Add to category bucket\n682:         if category not in files_by_category:\n683:             files_by_category[category] = []\n684:         files_by_category[category].append(\n685:             {\"path\": rel, \"size\": size, \"sha256\": sha}\n686:         )\n687: \n688:         # Extract imports for Python files\n689:         if path.suffix == \".py\":\n690:             import_result = extract_imports(path, project_root)\n691:             if import_result[\"imports\"] or import_result[\"error\"]:\n692:                 import_map[rel] = {\n693:                     \"imports\": import_result[\"imports\"],\n694:                     \"error\": import_result[\"error\"],\n695:                 }\n696:                 if import_result[\"error\"]:\n697:                     parse_errors.append(f\"{rel}: {import_result['error']}\")\n698: \n699:     # Detect framework hints\n700:     framework_hints = detect_framework_hints(project_root)\n701: \n702:     # Build summary\n703:     summary = {\n704:         \"total_files\": sum(len(files) for files in files_by_category.values()),\n705:         \"total_size_bytes\": total_size,\n706:         \"categories\": {cat: len(files) for cat, files in files_by_category.items()},\n707:     }\n708: \n709:     # Build project map\n710:     project_map = {\n711:         \"version\": \"1.0\",\n712:         \"generated\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n713:         \"project_root\": str(project_root),\n714:         \"summary\": summary,\n715:         \"files\": files_by_category,\n716:         \"importMap\": {\"python_files\": import_map},\n717:         \"frameworks\": framework_hints,\n718:         \"parse_errors\": parse_errors,\n719:     }\n720: \n721:     return project_map\n722: \n723: \n724: def generate_report(project_map: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n725:     \"\"\"Generate human-readable report from project map.\"\"\"\n726:     summary = project_map[\"summary\"]\n727:     frameworks = project_map[\"frameworks\"]\n728:     parse_errors = project_map.get(\"parse_errors\", [])\n729: \n730:     lines = [\n731:         \"# Project Scanner Report\",\n732:         f\"Generated: {project_map['generated']}\",\n733:         f\"Project Root: {project_map['project_root']}\",\n734:         \"\",\n735:         \"## Summary\",\n736:         f\"- Total files: {summary['total_files']}\",\n737:         f\"- Total size: {summary['total_size_bytes'] / 1024:.1f} KB\",\n738:         \"\",\n739:         \"## Files by Category\",\n740:     ]\n741: \n742:     for category, count in sorted(summary[\"categories\"].items()):\n743:         lines.append(f\"- {category}: {count}\")\n744: \n745:     lines.extend(\n746:         [\n747:             \"\",\n748:             \"## Frameworks Detected\",\n749:             f\"- Frameworks: {', '.join(frameworks['frameworks']) or 'None'}\",\n750:             f\"- Tools: {', '.join(frameworks['tools']) or 'None'}\",\n751:         ]\n752:     )\n753: \n754:     if parse_errors:\n755:         lines.extend(\n756:             [\n757:                 \"\",\n758:                 \"## Parse Errors\",\n759:                 f\"- {len(parse_errors)} files with SyntaxError:\",\n760:             ]\n761:         )\n762:         # Parse errors section\n763:         if parse_errors:\n764:             lines.extend([f\"  - {err}\" for err in parse_errors[:10]])\n765:             if len(parse_errors) > 10:\n766:                 lines.append(f\"  ... and {len(parse_errors) - 10} more\")\n767: \n768:     return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n769: \n770: \n771: # =============================================================================\n772: # CLI\n773: # =============================================================================\n774: \n775: \n776: def main() -> int:\n777:     args = sys.argv[1:]\n778: \n779:     dry_run = \"--dry-run\" in args\n780:     report_only = \"--report\" in args\n781: \n782:     project_root = _project_root()\n783:     context_dir = _context_dir()\n784: \n785:     # Ensure context directory exists\n786:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n787: \n788:     # Scan project\n789:     print(f\"Scanning project: {project_root}\")\n790:     project_map = scan_project(project_root)\n791: \n792:     if report_only:\n793:         print(generate_report(project_map))\n794:         return 0\n795: \n796:     # Serialize output\n797:     output_json = json.dumps(project_map, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n798: \n799:     if dry_run:\n800:         print(output_json)\n801:         return 0\n802: \n803:     # Write output\n804:     output_path = context_dir / \"project-map.json\"\n805:     output_path.write_text(output_json, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n806: \n807:     print(f\"[OK] Project map written to: {output_path}\")\n808:     print(f\"     Total files: {project_map['summary']['total_files']}\")\n809:     print(f\"     Python files with imports: {len(project_map['importMap']['python_files'])}\")\n810:     print(f\"     Frameworks detected: {len(project_map['frameworks']['frameworks'])}\")\n811: \n812:     if project_map.get(\"parse_errors\"):\n813:         print(f\"     Parse errors: {len(project_map['parse_errors'])}\")\n814: \n815:     return 0\n816: \n817: \n818: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n819:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 819 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"\nProject Scanner - Deterministic .agent/context/project-map.json\n\"\"\"\n\nimport
