ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_AGENT_DIR), str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # WP-2026-146: Import approval system for HUMAN_GATE timeout\n100: try:\n101:     from bus.approval import (\n102:         ApprovalPolicy,\n103:         ApprovalReason,\n104:         ApprovalStatus,\n105:         ApprovalStore,\n106:     )\n107: \n108:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = True\n109: except ImportError:\n110:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = False\n111:     ApprovalPolicy = None\n112:     ApprovalReason = None\n113:     ApprovalStatus = None\n114:     ApprovalStore = None\n115: \n116: # ============================================================================\n117: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n118: # ============================================================================\n119: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n120: \n121: \n122: def _project_root() -> Path:\n123:     return resolve_project_root()\n124: \n125: \n126: class _LazyPath:\n127:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n128:         self._resolver = resolver\n129: \n130:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n131:         return self._resolver()\n132: \n133:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n134:         return self.resolve() / other\n135: \n136:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n137:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n138: \n139:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n140:         return str(self.resolve())\n141: \n142:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n143:         return str(self.resolve())\n144: \n145:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n146:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n147: \n148: \n149: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n150: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n151: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_agent_dir)\n152: COLLAB_DIR = _LazyPath(get_collab_dir)\n153: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_context_dir)\n154: \n155: # State files\n156: WORK_PLAN = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\")\n157: EXEC_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"execution_log.md\")\n158: REVIEW_QUEUE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"review_queue.md\")\n159: NOTIFICATIONS = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"notifications.md\")\n160: TURN_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"TURN.md\")\n161: STATE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"STATE.md\")\n162: PROJECT_MAP = _LazyPath(lambda: get_context_dir() / \"project_map.md\")\n163: ARCHIVE_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"archive\")\n164: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\")\n165: \n166: # Archive configuration\n167: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n168: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n169: \n170: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n171: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n172: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n173: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n174: \n175: # WP-2026-146: HUMAN_GATE timeout configuration. Single source of truth is\n176: # manager_review.human_gate_timeout_seconds in agents.json. Fallback used if missing.\n177: # Distinct from manager_review.timeout_seconds (AI subprocess wait, ~180s).\n178: HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK = 86400  # 24 hours\n179: \n180: # WP-2026-147: Graph context adapter import (optional)\n181: try:\n182:     from scripts.graph_context import generate_context_for_destination\n183: \n184:     GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE = True\n185: except ImportError:\n186:     GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE = False\n187:     generate_context_for_destination = None\n188: \n189: # WP-2026-150: Project scanner import (optional)\n190: try:\n191:     from scripts.project_scanner import (\n192:         generate_report as scanner_generate_report,\n193:         scan_project,\n194:     )\n195: \n196:     SCANNER_AVAILABLE = True\n197: except ImportError:\n198:     SCANNER_AVAILABLE = False\n199:     scan_project = None\n200:     scanner_generate_report = None\n201: \n202: \n203: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n204:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n205: \n206:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n207:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n208:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n209:     \"\"\"\n210:     try:\n211:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n212:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n213:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n214:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n215:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n216: \n217: \n218: def get_human_gate_timeout() -> int:\n219:     \"\"\"Return the timeout in seconds for HUMAN_GATE approval requests.\n220: \n221:     Reads manager_review.human_gate_timeout_seconds from agents.json.\n222:     This is intentionally separate from manager_review.timeout_seconds,\n223:     which controls the AI subprocess wait (~180s). Human gates need hours.\n224:     Falls back to HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK (24h) if absent or malformed.\n225: \n226:     Returns:\n227:         Timeout in seconds (positive integer).\n228:     \"\"\"\n229:     try:\n230:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n231:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"human_gate_timeout_seconds\"))\n232:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n233:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n234:         return HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n235: \n236: \n237: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n238: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n239: \n240: # Estados validos para validacion\n241: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n242:     \"DRAFT\",\n243:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n244:     \"APPROVED\",\n245:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n246:     \"COMPLETED\",\n247:     \"N/A\",\n248: }\n249: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n250:     \"PENDING\",\n251:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n252:     \"BLOCKED\",\n253:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n254:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n255:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n256:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n257:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n258:     \"COMPLETED\",\n259:     \"N/A\",\n260: }\n261: \n262: \n263: def _ensure_runtime_dirs() -> None:\n264:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n265:     archive_dir = get_collab_dir() / \"archive\"\n266:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n267:     archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n268: \n269: \n270: def _get_event_bus() -> EventBus | None:\n271:     global event_bus\n272:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE:\n273:         return None\n274:     if event_bus is None:\n275:         event_bus = EventBus(get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n276:     return event_bus\n277: \n278: \n279: def _get_approval_store() -> ApprovalStore | None:\n280:     \"\"\"Get or create the approval store for HUMAN_GATE timeout.\n281: \n282:     Before: No approval store existed in agent_controller.\n283:     During: Creates ApprovalStore under runtime_dir with configurable timeout.\n284:     After: Returns configured ApprovalStore or None if approval system unavailable.\n285:     \"\"\"\n286:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n287:         return None\n288:     store_path = get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"approvals\" / \"store.json\"\n289:     timeout_seconds = get_human_gate_timeout()\n290:     policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n291:         policy_name=\"human_gate\",\n292:         timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n293:         auto_resolve=True,\n294:         auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n295:     )\n296:     return ApprovalStore(store_path=store_path, policy=policy)\n297: \n298: \n299: # Event bus is initialized lazily after the project root is known.\n300: event_bus = None\n301: \n302: # Circuit breaker state\n303: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = _LazyPath(\n304:     lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n305: )\n306: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\")\n307: \n308: # ============================================================================\n309: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n310: # ============================================================================\n311: \n312: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n313:     \"work_plan.md\",\n314:     \"execution_log.md\",\n315:     \"STATE.md\",\n316:     \"TURN.md\",\n317:     \"notifications.md\",\n318:     \".session_state.json\",\n319: }\n320: \n321: \n322: def _exclude_files() -> set[str]:\n323:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n324:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n325:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n326:     exclude_files = {str((collab_dir / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n327:     exclude_files.add(str((context_dir / \"project_map.md\").resolve()))\n328:     # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n329:     exclude_files.add(\n330:         str((agent_dir / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve())\n331:     )\n332:     # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n333:     exclude_files.add(str((agent_dir / \"config\").resolve()))\n334:     return exclude_files\n335: \n336: \n337: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n338:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n339:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n340:     in_section = False\n341:     files = set()\n342: \n343:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n344:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n345:             return False\n346:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n347:             return True\n348:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n349:             return True\n350:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n351:         return \".\" in basename\n352: \n353:     for line in lines:\n354:         line = line.strip()\n355:         if \"## Files Likely Touched\" in line:\n356:             in_section = True\n357:             continue\n358:         if in_section and line.startswith(\"## \"):\n359:             break  # next section\n360:         if in_section and line and not line.startswith(\"---\"):\n361:             # normalize: remove backticks, quotes, bullets, trim\n362:             normalized = (\n363:                 line.lstrip(\"*- \")\n364:                 .replace(\"`\", \"\")\n365:                 .replace('\"', \"\")\n366:                 .replace(\"'\", \"\")\n367:                 .strip()\n368:             )\n369:             if normalized and _looks_like_path_token(normalized):\n370:                 # resolve relative to project root\n371:                 path = (PROJECT_ROOT / normalized).resolve()\n372:                 files.add(str(path))\n373:     return files\n374: \n375: \n376: def get_changed_files() -> set[str] | None:\n377:     \"\"\"Get all changed files: staged, unstaged, untracked. None if not git repo.\"\"\"\n378:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n379:         return None\n380:     try:\n381:         # Use -z for null-byte separated output to handle paths with spaces and renames safely\n382:         result = subprocess.run(\n383:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\", \"-z\"],\n384:             capture_output=True,\n385:             text=True,\n386:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n387:         )\n388:         changed = set()\n389:         # Split by null byte, each entry is: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status\n390:         # Renames have two consecutive entries: \"R old_path\" followed by \"new_path\"\n391:         entries = result.stdout.split(\"\\0\")\n392:         i = 0\n393:         while i < len(entries):\n394:             entry = entries[i]\n395:             if not entry:\n396:                 i += 1\n397:                 continue\n398:             # Format: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status (e.g., \"M \", \"??\", \"R \")\n399:             # When Y=' ' (space), format is \"M path\" (path at index 2)\n400:             # When Y!=' ', format is \"XY path\" with space separator (path at index 3)\n401:             if len(entry) >= 3:\n402:                 status = entry[:2]\n403:                 # Determine path start: if entry[2] is space, path starts at 3; otherwise at 2\n404:                 path = entry[3:] if entry[2] == \" \" else entry[2:]\n405:                 # Handle renames: status starts with 'R', next entry is the new path\n406:                 if status[0] == \"R\" and i + 1 < len(entries):\n407:                     new_path = entries[i + 1]\n408:                     if new_path:\n409:                         changed.add(new_path)\n410:                     i += 2\n411:                     continue\n412:                 else:\n413:                     changed.add(path)\n414:             i += 1\n415:         # resolve to absolute paths\n416:         resolved = set()\n417:         for f in changed:\n418:             path = (PROJECT_ROOT / f).resolve()\n419:             resolved.add(str(path))\n420:         return resolved\n421:     except FileNotFoundError:\n422:         return None\n423: \n424: \n425: def check_scope_gate(\n426:     work_plan_content: str, changed_files: set[str] | None, exclude_files: set[str]\n427: ) -> dict:\n428:     \"\"\"Check if changed files are within scope.\"\"\"\n429:     if changed_files is None:\n430:         return {\n431:             \"valid\": True,\n432:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n433:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n434:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n435:             \"warnings\": [\"Repository is not git-managed\"],\n436:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n437:         }\n438: \n439:     whitelist = parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n440:     if not whitelist:\n441:         return {\n442:             \"valid\": True,\n443:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n444:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n445:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n446:             \"warnings\": [\"No Files Likely Touched section in work_plan.md\"],\n447:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n448:         }\n449: \n450:     relevant_changed = changed_files - exclude_files\n451:     relevant_whitelist = whitelist - exclude_files\n452:     covered_files = relevant_changed & relevant_whitelist\n453:     missing_from_diff = relevant_whitelist - relevant_changed\n454:     out_of_scope = relevant_changed - relevant_whitelist\n455: \n456:     warnings = []\n457:     blocked_reason = None\n458:     valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0\n459: \n460:     if relevant_whitelist and not covered_files:\n461:         valid = False\n462:         blocked_reason = (\n463:             \"None of the declared Files Likely Touched entries appeared in the diff\"\n464:         )\n465:     elif covered_files and missing_from_diff:\n466:         warnings.append(\n467:             \"Partial scope coverage: \"\n468:             f\"{len(covered_files)} of {len(relevant_whitelist)} declared files touched\"\n469:         )\n470: \n471:     return {\n472:         \"valid\": valid,\n473:         \"out_of_scope\": out_of_scope,\n474:         \"missing_from_diff\": missing_from_diff,\n475:         \"covered_files\": covered_files,\n476:         \"warnings\": warnings,\n477:         \"blocked_reason\": blocked_reason,\n478:     }\n479: \n480: \n481: def _load_mark_ready_context() -> tuple[str, str, str]:\n482:     \"\"\"Load the plan and log content for --mark-ready.\"\"\"\n483:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n484:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n485:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n486:     return plan_content, log_content, plan_id\n487: \n488: \n489: def _record_scope_override(scope_override: str, problem_files: set[str]) -> None:\n490:     \"\"\"Record a scope override in the execution log.\"\"\"\n491:     note = (\n492:         f\"Scope override: {scope_override}. \"\n493:         f\"Affected files: {', '.join(sorted(problem_files))}\"\n494:     )\n495:     update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", note)\n496: \n497: \n498: def _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result: dict, scope_override: str | None) -> bool:\n499:     \"\"\"Apply scope gate decision for mark-ready.\"\"\"\n500:     if gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n501:         for warning in gate_result[\"warnings\"]:\n502:             print(f\"[WARN] {warning}\")\n503:         update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Marked ready by Builder\")\n504:         return True\n505: \n506:     if not scope_override:\n507:         print(\"[ERROR] Scope violation detected:\")\n508:         if gate_result.get(\"blocked_reason\"):\n509:             print(f\"  {gate_result['blocked_reason']}\")\n510:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]):\n511:             print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n512:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())):\n513:             print(f\"  - missing: {file_path}\")\n514:         print('Use --scope-override \"reason\" to proceed.')\n515:         return False\n516: \n517:     print(f\"[INFO] Scope override applied: {scope_override}\")\n518:     problem_files = set(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]) | set(\n519:         gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())\n520:     )\n521:     _record_scope_override(scope_override, problem_files)\n522:     return True\n523: \n524: \n525: def _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id: str, plan_content: str) -> None:\n526:     \"\"\"Update TURN.md and STATE.md after mark-ready.\n527: \n528:     WP-2026-124: Uses bus-derived state as authority, not execution_log.md.\n529:     \"\"\"\n530:     # WP-2026-124: Get bus-derived state\n531:     log_status_before = get_status(read_file(EXEC_LOG), \"**Estado:**\")\n532:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n533:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n534: \n535:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n536:         bus_state = (\n537:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n538:             if events\n539:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n540:         )\n541:         # Use bus state if available, fallback to log\n542:         from_state = (\n543:             bus_state.value if bus_state != TicketState.UNKNOWN else log_status_before\n544:         )\n545:     else:\n546:         from_state = log_status_before\n547: \n548:     action = {\n549:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n550:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n551:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n552:         \"instruction\": f\"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n553:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n554:         \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n555:         \"log_status\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n556:         \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n557:         \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n558:     }\n559:     update_turn_file(action)\n560: \n561:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n562:     if not state_content:\n563:         return\n564: \n565:     lines = state_content.split(\"\\n\")\n566:     for index, line in enumerate(lines):\n567:         if \"**Estado actual:**\" in line:\n568:             lines[index] = \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n569:             write_file(STATE_FILE, \"\\n\".join(lines))\n570:             break\n571: \n572:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus idempotently\n573:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n574:         # Check if already emitted for this ticket and state\n575:         latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n576:             ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n577:         )\n578:         if (\n579:             not latest_state_event\n580:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n581:         ):\n582:             event_bus.emit(\n583:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n584:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n585:                 actor=\"BUILDER\",\n586:                 payload={\n587:                     \"from_state\": from_state,\n588:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n589:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n590:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n591:                 },\n592:             )\n593:         if (\n594:             not latest_state_event\n595:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n596:         ):\n597:             bus_from_state = (\n598:                 latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n599:                 if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n600:                 else log_status_before\n601:             )\n602:             event_bus.emit(\n603:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n604:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n605:                 actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n606:                 payload={\n607:                     \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n608:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n609:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n610:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n611:                 },\n612:             )\n613: \n614: \n615: # ============================================================================\n616: # CIRCUIT BREAKER AND CHECKOUT UTILITIES\n617: # ============================================================================\n618: \n619: \n620: def _read_circuit_breaker() -> dict:\n621:     \"\"\"Read circuit breaker state from JSON file.\"\"\"\n622:     if not CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.exists():\n623:         return {\n624:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n625:             \"failures\": 0,\n626:             \"last_failure\": None,\n627:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n628:         }\n629:     try:\n630:         return json.loads(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n631:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n632:         return {\n633:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n634:             \"failures\": 0,\n635:             \"last_failure\": None,\n636:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n637:         }\n638: \n639: \n640: def _write_circuit_breaker(state: dict) -> None:\n641:     \"\"\"Write circuit breaker state to JSON file.\"\"\"\n642:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n643:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.write_text(\n644:         json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n645:     )\n646: \n647: \n648: def _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> dict:\n649:     \"\"\"\n650:     Check circuit breaker state for a ticket.\n651:     Returns dict with 'open', 'reason', 'failures', 'no_progress_count'.\n652:     \"\"\"\n653:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n654:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\":\n655:         return {\n656:             \"open\": True,\n657:             \"reason\": breaker.get(\"reason\", \"Circuit breaker triggered\"),\n658:             \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n659:             \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n660:         }\n661:     return {\n662:         \"open\": False,\n663:         \"reason\": None,\n664:         \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n665:         \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n666:     }\n667: \n668: \n669: def _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n670:     reason: str, plan_id: str, is_no_progress: bool = False\n671: ) -> None:\n672:     \"\"\"Trigger circuit breaker to OPEN state.\"\"\"\n673:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n674:     breaker[\"state\"] = \"OPEN\"\n675:     breaker[\"reason\"] = reason\n676:     breaker[\"last_triggered\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n677:     breaker[\"ticket_id\"] = plan_id\n678:     if is_no_progress:\n679:         breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n680:     else:\n681:         breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n682:     _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n683: \n684: \n685: def _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n686:     \"\"\"Reset circuit breaker to CLOSED state after successful completion.\"\"\"\n687:     _write_circuit_breaker(\n688:         {\n689:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n690:             \"failures\": 0,\n691:             \"last_failure\": None,\n692:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n693:             \"last_reset\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n694:             \"last_ticket\": plan_id,\n695:         }\n696:     )\n697: \n698: \n699: def _record_error_for_breaker(plan_id: str, error_msg: str) -> None:\n700:     \"\"\"Record an error for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n701:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n702:     breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n703:     breaker[\"last_error\"] = error_msg\n704:     breaker[\"last_error_time\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n705: \n706:     # Trigger if 3+ consecutive errors\n707:     if breaker[\"failures\"] >= 3:\n708:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(f\"Repeated errors: {error_msg[:100]}\", plan_id)\n709:     else:\n710:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n711: \n712: \n713: def _record_no_progress_for_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n714:     \"\"\"Record no-progress event for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n715:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n716:     breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n717:     breaker[\"last_no_progress\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n718: \n719:     # Trigger if 3+ no-progress events\n720:     if breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] >= 3:\n721:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n722:             \"No progress detected in multiple iterations\", plan_id, is_no_progress=True\n723:         )\n724:     else:\n725:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n726: \n727: \n728: def _read_builder_lock() -> dict | None:\n729:     \"\"\"Read builder lock file if it exists.\"\"\"\n730:     if not BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.exists():\n731:         return None\n732:     try:\n733:         return json.loads(BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n734:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n735:         return None\n736: \n737: \n738: def _acquire_builder_lock(\n739:     plan_id: str, pid: int, role: str = \"BUILDER\", backend: str = \"unknown\"\n740: ) -> bool:\n741:     \"\"\"\n742:     Attempt to acquire builder lock atomically.\n743:     Returns True if lock was acquired, False if another session holds it.\n744:     \"\"\"\n745:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n746:     if existing is not None:\n747:         # Check if lock is stale (different ticket or dead process)\n748:         if existing.get(\"ticket_id\") != plan_id:\n749:             # Stale lock from different ticket - override\n750:             lock_data = {\n751:                 \"pid\": pid,\n752:                 \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n753:                 \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n754:                 \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n755:                 \"role\": role,\n756:                 \"backend\": backend,\n757:                 \"round\": 1,\n758:             }\n759:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n760:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n761:                 json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n762:             )\n763:             return True\n764:         # Same ticket - check if process is alive (simplified: just allow re-acquisition)\n765:         lock_data = {\n766:             \"pid\": pid,\n767:             \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n768:             \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n769:             \"started_at\": existing.get(\n770:                 \"started_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n771:             ),\n772:             \"role\": role,\n773:             \"backend\": backend,\n774:             \"round\": existing.get(\"round\", 1) + 1,\n775:         }\n776:         BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n777:             json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n778:         )\n779:         return True\n780: \n781:     # No existing lock - acquire fresh\n782:     lock_data = {\n783:         \"pid\": pid,\n784:         \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n785:         \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n786:         \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n787:         \"role\": role,\n788:         \"backend\": backend,\n789:         \"round\": 1,\n790:     }\n791:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n792:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n793:         json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n794:     )\n795:     return True\n796: \n797: \n798: def _release_builder_lock(plan_id: str) -> None:\n799:     \"\"\"Release builder lock after completion.\"\"\"\n800:     import contextlib\n801: \n802:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n803:     if existing and existing.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id:\n804:         with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n805:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.unlink()\n806: \n807: \n808: def _emit_builder_exit(plan_id: str, exit_reason: str, completion_summary: str) -> None:\n809:     \"\"\"Emit BUILDER_EXIT event to the bus - required for ticket closure.\"\"\"\n810:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n811:         event_bus.emit(\n812:             event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\",\n813:             ticket_id=plan_id,\n814:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n815:             payload={\n816:                 \"exit_reason\": exit_reason,\n817:                 \"completion_summary\": completion_summary,\n818:                 \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n819:             },\n820:         )\n821: \n822: \n823: def _create_human_gate_approval_request(\n824:     ticket_id: str, timeout_seconds: int | None = None\n825: ) -> bool:\n826:     \"\"\"Create and persist an ApprovalRequest when escalating to HUMAN_GATE.\n827: \n828:     Before: HUMAN_GATE escalation had no persistent timeout tracking.\n829:     During: Creates an ApprovalRequest with timeout metadata in the ApprovalStore.\n830:     After: The approval request persists across restarts and can be expired by\n831:            the supervisor's check_and_expire_all() loop.\n832: \n833:     Args:\n834:         ticket_id: The ticket ID being escalated to HUMAN_GATE.\n835:         timeout_seconds: Optional timeout override. Defaults to config value.\n836: \n837:     Returns:\n838:         True if the approval request was created successfully, False otherwise.\n839:     \"\"\"\n840:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n841:         print(\n842:             \"[WARN] Approval system unavailable; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n843:             file=sys.stderr,\n844:             flush=True,\n845:         )\n846:         return False\n847: \n848:     store = _get_approval_store()\n849:     if store is None:\n850:         print(\n851:             \"[WARN] Could not create ApprovalStore; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n852:             file=sys.stderr,\n853:             flush=True,\n854:         )\n855:         return False\n856: \n857:     import uuid\n858: \n859:     approval_id = f\"hg-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n860: \n861:     # Create and persist the approval request\n862:     timeout = (\n863:         timeout_seconds if timeout_seconds is not None else get_human_gate_timeout()\n864:     )\n865:     metadata = {\n866:         \"escalation_type\": \"human_gate\",\n867:         \"timeout_seconds\": timeout,\n868:         \"created_by\": \"agent_controller\",\n869:         \"source\": \"escalate-human-gate\",\n870:     }\n871: \n872:     try:\n873:         # Use custom policy if timeout_seconds was provided\n874:         if timeout_seconds is not None:\n875:             custom_policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n876:                 policy_name=\"human_gate_custom\",\n877:                 timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n878:                 auto_resolve=True,\n879:                 auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n880:             )\n881:             store.create_request(\n882:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n883:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n884:                 metadata=metadata,\n885:                 policy=custom_policy,\n886:             )\n887:         else:\n888:             store.create_request(\n889:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n890:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n891:                 metadata=metadata,\n892:             )\n893:         print(\n894:             f\"[OK] Created HUMAN_GATE approval request {approval_id} for {ticket_id} \"\n895:             f\"(timeout={timeout}s).\",\n896:             flush=True,\n897:         )\n898:         return True\n899:     except Exception as exc:\n900:         print(\n901:             f\"[ERROR] Failed to create HUMAN_GATE approval request: {exc}\",\n902:             file=sys.stderr,\n903:             flush=True,\n904:         )\n905:         return False\n906: \n907: \n908: def _auto_archive_closed_artifacts() -> None:\n909:     \"\"\"Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts during mark-ready.\n910: \n911:     Before: Requires COLLAB_DIR to exist with potential closed PLAN/AUDIT files.\n912:     During: Imports and calls archive_collaboration_artifacts.py as a library function.\n913:     After: Closed PLAN/AUDIT files are moved to _archive/plan_audit/ (idempotent, silent on no-op).\n914:     \"\"\"\n915:     try:\n916:         # Import the archive script as a module\n917:         import importlib.util\n918: \n919:         archive_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n920:             \"archive_collaboration_artifacts\",\n921:             PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n922:         )\n923:         if archive_spec and archive_spec.loader:\n924:             archive_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(archive_spec)\n925:             archive_spec.loader.exec_module(archive_mod)\n926: \n927:             # Call the archive function (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n928:             archive_mod.archive_collaboration_artifacts(\n929:                 collaboration_dir=COLLAB_DIR,\n930:                 dry_run=False,\n931:             )\n932:     except Exception as exc:\n933:         # Silent fail with debug logging - archiving is best-effort, not critical path\n934:         print(f\"[WARN] Auto-archive failed: {exc}\")\n935: \n936: \n937: # ============================================================================\n938: # UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n939: # ============================================================================\n940: \n941: \n942: def read_file(path: Path) -> str:\n943:     \"\"\"Lee un archivo si existe, retorna string vacio si no.\"\"\"\n944:     if not path.exists():\n945:         return \"\"\n946:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n947:         return f.read()\n948: \n949: \n950: def write_file(path: Path, content: str) -> None:\n951:     \"\"\"Escribe contenido a un archivo, creando directorios si es necesario.\"\"\"\n952:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n953:     with open(path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n954:         f.write(content)\n955: \n956: \n957: def get_status(content: str, marker: str) -> str:\n958:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado de un archivo buscando un marcador.\"\"\"\n959:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n960:         if marker in line:\n961:             return line.split(marker)[1].strip()\n962:     return \"UNKNOWN\"\n963: \n964: \n965: def get_plan_id(content: str) -> str:\n966:     \"\"\"Extrae el ID del plan de trabajo.\"\"\"\n967:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n968:         if \"**ID:**\" in line or \"**Plan ID:**\" in line:\n969:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n970:     return \"N/A\"\n971: \n972: \n973: def get_plan_type(content: str) -> str:\n974:     \"\"\"Extrae el tipo de plan. Valores: IMPLEMENTATION (default) | FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n975:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n976:         if \"**Tipo:**\" in line:\n977:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip().upper()\n978:     return \"IMPLEMENTATION\"\n979: \n980: \n981: def check_git_status() -> bool | None:\n982:     \"\"\"Verifica si el repositorio esta limpio.\"\"\"\n983:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n984:         return None\n985:     try:\n986:         result = subprocess.run(\n987:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\"],\n988:             capture_output=True,\n989:             text=True,\n990:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n991:         )\n992:         return not result.stdout.strip()\n993:     except FileNotFoundError:\n994:         return None\n995: \n996: \n997: def extract_status_emoji(status_str: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n998:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado limpio y el emoji.\"\"\"\n999:     emojis = {\"🟢\", \"🟡\", \"🔴\", \"🟣\", \"✅\", \"⏳\", \"❌\", \"⚠️\"}\n1000:     status_clean = status_str.strip()\n1001:     found_emoji = \"\"\n1002:     for emoji in emojis:\n1003:         if emoji in status_clean:\n1004:             found_emoji = emoji\n1005:             status_clean = status_clean.replace(emoji, \"\").strip()\n1006:             break\n1007:     return status_clean, found_emoji\n1008: \n1009: \n1010: _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n1011: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n1012:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n1013:     re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n1014: )\n1015: \n1016: \n1017: def _check_deliverable_type(content: str) -> list[str]:\n1018:     \"\"\"Check deliverable_type field in work_plan.md content.\n1019: \n1020:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n1021:     During: Searches for deliverable_type field using regex, validates against allowed values.\n1022:     After: Returns list of warning strings (empty if valid). Never returns errors (V1 informational).\n1023:     \"\"\"\n1024:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n1025:     if not match:\n1026:         return [\n1027:             \"work_plan.md missing deliverable_type field. \"\n1028:             \"Add one of: code, documentation, research, analysis, mixed.\"\n1029:         ]\n1030:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n1031:     if \"+\" in value:\n1032:         return [\n1033:             f\"work_plan.md deliverable_type='{value}' uses compound syntax. \"\n1034:             \"Use 'mixed' instead of 'code+documentation' etc.\"\n1035:         ]\n1036:     if value not in _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES:\n1037:         return [\n1038:             f\"work_plan.md has unknown deliverable_type '{value}'. \"\n1039:             f\"Expected one of: {sorted(_VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES)}\"\n1040:         ]\n1041:     return []\n1042: \n1043: \n1044: def _validate_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n1045:     \"\"\"Validate work_plan.md file.\"\"\"\n1046:     errors = []\n1047:     content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1048:     if not content:\n1049:         return errors\n1050: \n1051:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1052:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1053:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_PLAN_STATES:\n1054:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1055:     if \"**ID:**\" not in content:\n1056:         errors.append(\"Falta campo **ID:**\")\n1057:     return errors\n1058: \n1059: \n1060: def _validate_execution_log() -> list[str]:\n1061:     \"\"\"Validate execution_log.md file.\"\"\"\n1062:     errors = []\n1063:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1064:     if not content:\n1065:         return errors\n1066: \n1067:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1068:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1069:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_LOG_STATES:\n1070:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1071:     return errors\n1072: \n1073: \n1074: def _validate_turn_file() -> list[str]:\n1075:     \"\"\"Validate TURN.md file.\"\"\"\n1076:     errors = []\n1077:     content = read_file(TURN_FILE)\n1078:     if content and \"## Agente Activo\" not in content:\n1079:         errors.append(\"Falta secciÃƒÂ³n '## Agente Activo'\")\n1080:     return errors\n1081: \n1082: \n1083: def _validate_notifications() -> list[str]:\n1084:     \"\"\"Validate notifications.md file.\"\"\"\n1085:     errors = []\n1086:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1087:     if content and \"</thinking>\" in content:\n1088:         errors.append(\"Contiene etiquetas </thinking> (corrupto)\")\n1089:     return errors\n1090: \n1091: \n1092: def _validate_cross_file_consistency() -> list[str]:\n1093:     \"\"\"Validate consistency across files.\"\"\"\n1094:     errors = []\n1095:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1096:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1097: \n1098:     if not plan_content or not log_content:\n1099:         return errors\n1100: \n1101:     plan_status_raw = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1102:     log_status_raw = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1103:     plan_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(plan_status_raw)\n1104:     log_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(log_status_raw)\n1105: \n1106:     # Plan APPROVED pero log COMPLETED\n1107:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_clean and \"COMPLETED\" in log_clean:\n1108:         errors.append(\n1109:             \"DRIFT: plan=APPROVED pero log=COMPLETED -- \"\n1110:             \"el log pertenece a un ciclo anterior. Limpia execution_log.md.\"\n1111:         )\n1112: \n1113:     # Plan COMPLETED pero log IN_PROGRESS\n1114:     if \"COMPLETED\" in plan_clean and \"IN_PROGRESS\" in log_clean:\n1115:         errors.append(\n1116:             \"DRIFT: plan=COMPLETED pero log=IN_PROGRESS -- \"\n1117:             \"el Builder no cerro su bitacora correctamente.\"\n1118:         )\n1119: \n1120:     # Plan IN_PLANNING con log READY_FOR_REVIEW\n1121:     if \"IN_PLANNING\" in plan_clean and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_clean:\n1122:         errors.append(\n1123:             \"DRIFT: plan=IN_PLANNING pero log=READY_FOR_REVIEW -- \"\n1124:             \"estado imposible. El plan debe estar APPROVED antes de que el Builder entregue.\"\n1125:         )\n1126: \n1127:     # Plan N/A pero log activo\n1128:     if (\"N/A\" in plan_clean or not plan_clean) and log_clean not in (\n1129:         \"N/A\",\n1130:         \"COMPLETED\",\n1131:         \"PENDING\",\n1132:         \"\",\n1133:     ):\n1134:         errors.append(\n1135:             f\"DRIFT: no hay plan activo pero log={log_clean} -- \"\n1136:             \"limpia execution_log.md o crea un nuevo work_plan.md.\"\n1137:         )\n1138: \n1139:     return errors\n1140: \n1141: \n1142: def _validate_host_project_prefix() -> list[str]:\n1143:     \"\"\"Validate that host-project destinations have 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md.\n1144: \n1145:     Before: Requires PROJECT.md to exist at project root; agents.json to be readable.\n1146:     During: Checks if active_profile is 'host-project' and if PROJECT.md has 'Ticket prefix:'.\n1147:     After: Returns list of warnings (empty if validation passes).\n1148: \n1149:     Returns:\n1150:         List of warning strings (empty if no issues).\n1151:     \"\"\"\n1152:     warnings = []\n1153:     agents_config = AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH\n1154:     if not agents_config.exists():\n1155:         return warnings\n1156: \n1157:     try:\n1158:         config_data = json.loads(agents_config.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n1159:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n1160:         return warnings\n1161: \n1162:     active_profile = config_data.get(\"active_profile\", \"\")\n1163:     if active_profile != \"host-project\":\n1164:         return warnings\n1165: \n1166:     # Only check for host-project profile\n1167:     project_md = PROJECT_ROOT / \"PROJECT.md\"\n1168:     if not project_md.exists():\n1169:         warnings.append(\n1170:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks PROJECT.md. \"\n1171:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace.\"\n1172:         )\n1173:         return warnings\n1174: \n1175:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1176:     if \"Ticket prefix:\" not in content:\n1177:         warnings.append(\n1178:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md. \"\n1179:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace (XXX-YYYY-NNN).\"\n1180:         )\n1181: \n1182:     return warnings\n1183: \n1184: \n1185: def validate_state_files() -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n1186:     \"\"\"Valida el formato y consistencia cruzada de los archivos de estado.\"\"\"\n1187:     return {\n1188:         \"work_plan.md\": _validate_work_plan(),\n1189:         \"execution_log.md\": _validate_execution_log(),\n1190:         \"notifications.md\": _validate_notifications(),\n1191:         \"TURN.md\": _validate_turn_file(),\n1192:         \"consistency\": _validate_cross_file_consistency(),\n1193:         \"host_project_prefix\": _validate_host_project_prefix(),\n1194:     }\n1195: \n1196: \n1197: def fix_corrupted_notifications() -> bool:\n1198:     \"\"\"Intenta reparar notifications.md si esta corrupto.\"\"\"\n1199:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1200:     if not content:\n1201:         return False\n1202:     original = content\n1203:     content = re.sub(r\"</thinking>\\s*\", \"\", content)\n1204:     content = re.sub(r\"\\n{4,}\", \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\", content)\n1205:     if content != original:\n1206:         write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, content)\n1207:         return True\n1208:     return False\n1209: \n1210: \n1211: def archive_old_notifications() -> str | None:\n1212:     \"\"\"Archiva notificaciones antiguas si el archivo es muy grande.\"\"\"\n1213:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1214:     if not content:\n1215:         return None\n1216: \n1217:     file_size_kb = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\")) / 1024\n1218:     entry_count = len([e for e in content.split(\"---\") if e.strip()])\n1219: \n1220:     if (\n1221:         file_size_kb < MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB\n1222:         and entry_count <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES\n1223:     ):\n1224:         return None\n1225: \n1226:     parts = content.split(\"---\")\n1227:     entries = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]\n1228: \n1229:     if len(entries) <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:\n1230:         return None\n1231: \n1232:     entries_to_archive = entries[:-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES]\n1233:     entries_to_keep = entries[-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:]\n1234: \n1235:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n1236:     archive_file = ARCHIVE_DIR / f\"notifications_{timestamp}.md\"\n1237: \n1238:     archive_content = \"# Notificaciones Archivadas\\n\\n\"\n1239:     archive_content += f\"**Fecha:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\\n\"\n1240:     archive_content += \"\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_archive)\n1241: \n1242:     write_file(archive_file, archive_content)\n1243: \n1244:     new_content = \"# Registro de Notificaciones\\n\\n---\\n\\n\"\n1245:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_keep)\n1246:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\"\n1247:     write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, new_content)\n1248: \n1249:     return str(archive_file)\n1250: \n1251: \n1252: def run_finalization_checks() -> dict:\n1253:     \"\"\"Checks adicionales para planes de tipo FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n1254:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"summary\": []}\n1255:     checks = {\n1256:         \"README.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"README.md\",\n1257:         \"CHANGELOG.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n1258:         \"closeout_report.md\": COLLAB_DIR / \"closeout_report.md\",\n1259:     }\n1260:     for name, path in checks.items():\n1261:         if path.exists():\n1262:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[OK] {name}: Presente\")\n1263:         else:\n1264:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[WARN] {name}: No encontrado\")\n1265:     return results\n1266: \n1267: \n1268: def run_quality_gates(plan_type: str = \"IMPLEMENTATION\") -> dict:\n1269:     \"\"\"Ejecuta validaciones automaticas.\"\"\"\n1270:     print(\"\\n[QUALITY GATES] Ejecutando Quality Gates...\")\n1271:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"errors\": [], \"summary\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1272: \n1273:     state_errors = validate_state_files()\n1274:     total_state_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in state_errors.values())\n1275:     if total_state_errors > 0:\n1276:         results[\"warnings\"].append(\n1277:             f\"Archivos de estado: {total_state_errors} problemas\"\n1278:         )\n1279:     else:\n1280:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Estado: Archivos validos\")\n1281: \n1282:     src_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"src\"\n1283:     tests_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"tests\"\n1284:     dirs_to_check = [str(d) for d in [src_dir, tests_dir] if d.exists()]\n1285:     if not dirs_to_check:\n1286:         dirs_to_check = [\".\"]\n1287: \n1288:     try:\n1289:         ruff = subprocess.run(\n1290:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"ruff\", \"check\", *dirs_to_check],\n1291:             capture_output=True,\n1292:             text=True,\n1293:             timeout=60,\n1294:         )\n1295:         if ruff.returncode != 0:\n1296:             results[\"passed\"] = False\n1297:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Ruff: Errores de linting\")\n1298:         else:\n1299:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Ruff: Limpio\")\n1300:     except FileNotFoundError:\n1301:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Ruff: No instalado\")\n1302: \n1303:     if tests_dir.exists():\n1304:         try:\n1305:             pytest_result = subprocess.run(\n1306:                 [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pytest\", \"-q\"],\n1307:                 capture_output=True,\n1308:                 timeout=120,\n1309:                 cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n1310:             )\n1311:             if pytest_result.returncode != 0:\n1312:                 results[\"passed\"] = False\n1313:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Pytest: Tests fallando\")\n1314:             else:\n1315:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Pytest: Tests OK\")\n1316:         except FileNotFoundError:\n1317:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Pytest: No instalado\")\n1318: \n1319:     if plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1320:         fin_results = run_finalization_checks()\n1321:         results[\"summary\"].extend(fin_results[\"summary\"])\n1322: \n1323:     status = \"[PASSED]\" if results[\"passed\"] else \"[FAILED]\"\n1324:     print(f\"   {status}\")\n1325:     return results\n1326: \n1327: \n1328: def update_log_status(new_status: str, note: str) -> bool:\n1329:     \"\"\"Actualiza el estado en execution_log.md.\"\"\"\n1330:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1331:     if not content:\n1332:         return False\n1333:     lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n1334:     for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n1335:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n1336:             lines[i] = f\"**Estado:** {new_status}\"\n1337:             new_content = \"\\n\".join(lines) + f\"\\n\\n{note}\"\n1338:             write_file(EXEC_LOG, new_content)\n1339:             return True\n1340:     return False\n1341: \n1342: \n1343: def create_findings_file(plan_id: str = \"N/A\") -> Path:\n1344:     \"\"\"Crea findings.md desde template.\"\"\"\n1345:     findings_path = COLLAB_DIR / \"findings.md\"\n1346: \n1347:     if findings_path.exists():\n1348:         return findings_path\n1349: \n1350:     template_path = AGENT_DIR / \"templates\" / \"findings_template.md\"\n1351: \n1352:     try:\n1353:         if template_path.exists():\n1354:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1355:         else:\n1356:             template = \"\"\"# Hallazgos de Investigacion\n1357: \n1358: **Plan ID:** {{PLAN_ID}}\n1359: **Creado:** {{DATE}}\n1360: \n1361: ---\n1362: \n1363: ## Hallazgos\n1364: \n1365: <!-- Documenta aqui tus hallazgos durante la investigacion -->\n1366: \"\"\"\n1367: \n1368:         content = template.replace(\"{{PLAN_ID}}\", plan_id)\n1369:         content = content.replace(\n1370:             \"{{DATE}}\", datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1371:         )\n1372: \n1373:         write_file(findings_path, content)\n1374:         print(\"  [OK] Creado findings.md\")\n1375: \n1376:     except Exception as e:\n1377:         print(f\"  [WARN] Error creando findings.md: {e}\")\n1378: \n1379:     return findings_path\n1380: \n1381: \n1382: def generate_project_map() -> str:\n1383:     \"\"\"Genera un mapa actualizado del proyecto.\"\"\"\n1384:     output = [\n1385:         \"# Mapa del Proyecto\",\n1386:         f\"**Actualizado:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\",\n1387:         \"\",\n1388:         \"## Estructura de Archivos\",\n1389:         \"```\",\n1390:     ]\n1391: \n1392:     ignore = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\", \".ruff_cache\"}\n1393:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".txt\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n1394: \n1395:     files_found = []\n1396:     for path in sorted(PROJECT_ROOT.rglob(\"*\")):\n1397:         if any(part in ignore for part in path.parts):\n1398:             continue\n1399:         if path.is_file() and path.suffix in extensions:\n1400:             files_found.append(str(path.relative_to(PROJECT_ROOT)))\n1401: \n1402:     output.extend(files_found[:50])\n1403:     if len(files_found) > 50:\n1404:         output.append(f\"... (+{len(files_found) - 50} archivos mas)\")\n1405: \n1406:     output.append(\"```\")\n1407:     content = \"\\n\".join(output)\n1408:     write_file(PROJECT_MAP, content)\n1409:     return content\n1410: \n1411: \n1412: def _run_pre_action_hooks(\n1413:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, action_type: str\n1414: ) -> None:\n1415:     \"\"\"Run pre-action hooks if available.\"\"\"\n1416:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1417:         hook_registry.execute(\n1418:             \"pre_action\",\n1419:             {\n1420:                 \"action_type\": action_type,\n1421:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1422:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1423:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1424:             },\n1425:         )\n1426: \n1427: \n1428: def _check_quality_gates(\n1429:     plan_id: str, plan_type: str, plan_status: str, skip_gates: bool\n1430: ) -> dict | None:\n1431:     \"\"\"Check quality gates and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1432:     gate_result = run_quality_gates(plan_type=plan_type)\n1433:     if not gate_result[\"passed\"]:\n1434:         update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"AUTO-REJECTED: Quality Gates fallaron\")\n1435:         return {\n1436:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1437:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1438:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1439:             \"instruction\": \"RECHAZADO. Quality Gates fallaron. Corrige errores.\",\n1440:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1441:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1442:             \"log_status\": \"AUTO-REJECTED\",\n1443:             \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1444:         }\n1445:     return None\n1446: \n1447: \n1448: def _check_completion_verification(\n1449:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_type: str, strict_mode: bool\n1450: ) -> dict | None:\n1451:     \"\"\"Check completion verification in strict mode and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1452:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1453:         stop_result = stop_hook(\n1454:             {\n1455:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1456:                 \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1457:                 \"mode\": \"strict\" if strict_mode else \"normal\",\n1458:             }\n1459:         )\n1460:         if strict_mode and not stop_result.get(\"can_complete\", True):\n1461:             return {\n1462:                 \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1463:                 \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1464:                 \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1465:                 \"instruction\": (\n1466:                     f\"Plan {plan_id} no supera Completion Verification. \"\n1467:                     \"Corrige advertencias antes de review.\"\n1468:                 ),\n1469:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1470:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1471:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1472:                 \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1473:             }\n1474:     return None\n1475: \n1476: \n1477: # Transition table for base routing\n1478: TRANSITION_TABLE = {\n1479:     (\"no_plan\", \"any\"): (\n1480:         \"MANAGER\",\n1481:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1482:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1483:     ),\n1484:     (\"completed\", \"any\"): (\n1485:         \"MANAGER\",\n1486:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1487:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1488:     ),\n1489:     (\"in_planning\", \"any\"): (\n1490:         \"MANAGER\",\n1491:         \"FINALIZE_PLAN\",\n1492:         \"Plan {plan_id} en borrador. Finaliza y cambia a APPROVED\",\n1493:     ),\n1494:     (\"approved\", \"ready_for_review\"): (\n1495:         \"MANAGER\",\n1496:         \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n1497:         \"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n1498:     ),\n1499:     (\"approved\", \"any\"): (\n1500:         \"BUILDER\",\n1501:         \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1502:         \"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Implementa segun work_plan.md\",\n1503:     ),\n1504:     (\"in_review\", \"any\"): (\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1504. Use offset=1505 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n\"\"\"\nAgent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n==========================================\nOrquestador con:\n- Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n- Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n- Completion Verification antes de review\n- Quality Gates extendidos\n- Session Recovery\n- Project Map y estado del workflow\n\nUso:\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agent_controller.py","time":{"start":1779866065063,"end":1779866065112}},"id":"prt_e6848d210001KTgGpEaP1oozaF","sessionID":"ses_197b78a8dffeevtzSDMZvjmsG0","messageID":"msg_e6848a1cc001ULYgWVIrA62iyl"}}

result[\"summary\"][\"categories\"]\n351: \n352:         # Check importMap has our Python file\n353:         python_files = result[\"importMap\"][\"python_files\"]\n354:         assert any(\"main.py\" in path for path in python_files)\n355: \n356:     def test_scan_project_exclusions(self, tmp_path):\n357:         \"\"\"Test that excluded directories are not scanned.\"\"\"\n358:         # Create excluded directories\n359:         (tmp_path / \".git\").mkdir()\n360:         (tmp_path / \".git\" / \"config\").write_text(\"git config\")\n361:         (tmp_path / \"__pycache__\").mkdir()\n362:         (tmp_path / \"__pycache__\" / \"module.pyc\").write_text(\"cache\")\n363:         (tmp_path / \".venv\").mkdir()\n364:         (tmp_path / \".venv\" / \"lib.py\").write_text(\"venv file\")\n365: \n366:         # Create included file\n367:         (tmp_path / \"main.py\").write_text(\"print('hello')\")\n368: \n369:         result = scan_project(tmp_path)\n370: \n371:         # Excluded files should not appear\n372:         all_paths = []\n373:         for category_files in result[\"files\"].values():\n374:             all_paths.extend(f[\"path\"] for f in category_files)\n375: \n376:         assert not any(\".git\" in p for p in all_paths)\n377:         assert not any(\"__pycache__\" in p for p in all_paths)\n378:         assert not any(\".venv\" in p for p in all_paths)\n379:         assert any(\"main.py\" in p for p in all_paths)\n380: \n381:     def test_scan_project_deterministic(self, tmp_path):\n382:         \"\"\"Test that scan_project is deterministic.\"\"\"\n383:         # Create project structure\n384:         (tmp_path / \"a.py\").write_text(\"import os\")\n385:         (tmp_path / \"b.py\").write_text(\"import sys\")\n386:         (tmp_path / \"README.md\").write_text(\"# Test\")\n387: \n388:         result1 = scan_project(tmp_path)\n389:         result2 = scan_project(tmp_path)\n390: \n391:         # Remove timestamp for comparison\n392:         del result1[\"generated\"]\n393:         del result2[\"generated\"]\n394: \n395:         assert result1 == result2\n396: \n397:     def test_scan_project_fingerprints(self, tmp_path):\n398: usa `sorted()` |\n| Tests cubren paths principales y exclusiones | ✅ | 34 tests passing, 1 slow integration test |\n| Validación canonical sin errores | ✅ | `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force` passed |\n\n### Verificación de Anti-Patrones\n\n| Anti-Patrón | Estado | Observación |\n|-------------|--------|-------------|\n| AP-01 Mock drift | ✅ | Tests importan funciones directamente, no hay patches inertes |\n| AP-02 Floor assertion | ✅ | Aserciones validan estructuras y conteos específicos |\n| AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper | ✅ | Funciones tienen lógica real (parsing AST, categorización, detección) |\n| AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition | ✅ | Scanner es solo-lectura, sin locks ni adquisición de recursos |\n| AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression | ✅ | Sin cambios en contratos de retorno |\n| AP-06 Validator evidence missing | ✅ | execution_log muestra comandos exactos + resultados numéricos |\n| AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified | ✅ | `deliverable_type: code` es correcto (3 archivos Python con lógica) |\n| AP-08 Test coverage drift | ✅ | 35 tests nuevos cubren funciones nuevas del scanner |\n\n### Files Likely Touched - Whitelist Compliance\n\n- ✅ `scripts/project_scanner.py` - Implementado (819 líneas)\n- ✅ `.agent/agent_controller.py` - Integración añadida\n- ✅ `tests/unit/test_project_scanner.py` - 34 tests passing\n- ✅ `.agent/context/project-map.json` - Generado exitosamente\n\n### Quality Gates Evidence (execution_log.md)\n\n```\n34 passed, 1 deselected (slow integration test)\nruff check: All checks passed\nCanonical validation: No errors or warnings\n```\n\n---\n\nReview complete. The cleanup should keep the scanner-driven context flow intact and eliminate any fallback that regenerates `project_map.md` inline.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `project-map.json` under `.agent/context/` is the canonical project map artifact.\n15: - The controller hot path must not regenerate `project_map.md` or fall back to legacy map generation.\n16: - `_exclude_files()` must exclude `.agent/context/project-map.json`, not `project_map.md`, so the scanner artifact is treated as runtime context, not Builder scope.\n17: - The legacy `PROJECT_MAP` constant and the `generate_project_map()` helper are both part of the removal surface.\n18: - Any legacy `project_map.md` references in controller prompts or constants should be removed, not renamed.\n19: - The existing scanner path remains the source of truth; this ticket only removes the legacy consumption path.\n20: - No new dependencies are allowed.\n21: - The change should be a cleanup, not a redesign of scanner behavior.\n22: \n23: ## Files Likely Touched\n24: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n25: - `PROJECT.md`\n26: - `tests/unit/test_controller_project_map_cleanup.py`\n27: \n28: ## Fases\n29: 1. Remove the legacy `PROJECT_MAP` constant and the `generate_project_map()` helper, and replace the `_exclude_files()` entry so it excludes `.agent/context/project-map.json` instead of `project_map.md`.\n30: 2. Remove the legacy `project_map.md` line from the controller prompt text rather than replacing it with a JSON path.\n31: 3. Keep the scanner-driven context injection intact, with graceful degradation when the JSON artifact is absent.\n32: 4. Add static tests that read the controller source text and fail if `generate_project_map`, `PROJECT_MAP`, or the legacy `project_map.md` runtime references are still present.\n33: 5. Move the unconditional scanner print inside the `SCANNER_AVAILABLE` branch so the message only appears when the scanner actually runs.\n34: 6. Refresh project metadata so the repo state reflects that the scanner JSON is the only active project-map path.\n35: \n36: ## Calidad\n37: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_controller_project_map_cleanup.py -q`\n38: - `ruff check .agent scripts tests`\n39: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n40: \n41: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n42: - The controller no longer regenerates `project_map.md` in the hot path.\n43: - `project-map.json` is the only runtime-consumed project-map artifact.\n44: - Legacy `project_map.md` references are removed from controller-facing instructions, not renamed.\n45: - `_exclude_files()` excludes `project-map.json`, so the scanner artifact is not treated as Builder scope.\n46: - `PROJECT_MAP` and `generate_project_map()` are gone from the controller source.\n47: - The scanner-driven context flow still works when `project-map.json` is present.\n48: - Tests cover the absence of the legacy fallback with static source inspection and the presence of the scanner path.\n49: - Canonical validation passes without new warnings or errors.\n\n(End of file - total 49 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-151\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-151\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Retire legacy project_map path\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nRemove the legacy `generate_project_map()` path from the controller so `project-map.json` remains the only project-map artifact consumed by the runtime. The cleanup should keep the scanner-driven context flow intact and eliminate any fallback that regenerates `project_map.md` inline.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `project-map.json` under `.agent/context/` is the canonical project map artifact.\n- The controller hot path must not regenerate `project_map.md` or fall back to legacy map generation.\n- `_exclude_files()` must exclude `.agent/context/project-map.json`, not `project_map.md`, so the scanner artifact is treated as runtime context, not Builder scope.\n- The legacy `PROJECT_MAP` constant and the `generate_project_map()` helper are both part of the removal surface.\n- Any legacy `project_map.md` references in controller prompts or constants should be removed, not renamed.\n- The existing scanner path remains the source of truth; this ticket only removes the legacy consumption path.\n- No new dependencies are allowed.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779869068076,"end":1779869068148}},"id":"prt_e68769bf2001IEY5X5fVlEVBrt","sessionID":"ses_19789b5b2ffetKwbNt6cR52bn2","messageID":"msg_e68764b19001TRyN3xz28awG5h"}}

Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The Builder runs the existing suite, sees it pass, and declares quality gates satisfied — but the new functions introduced in the diff have no direct tests. Th... Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-151_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779869068065,"end":1779869068193}},"id":"prt_e68769746001JfrSFjY5oVUYb2","sessionID":"ses_19789b5b2ffetKwbNt6cR52bn2","messageID":"msg_e68764b19001TRyN3xz28awG5h"}}
