{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779872196572,"sessionID":"ses_1975a026fffeKXcZn9T6fEAEF6","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_f03a8eadbe8f41d78c995826","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agent_controller.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n2: \"\"\"\n3: Agent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n4: ==========================================\n5: Orquestador con:\n6: - Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n7: - Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n8: - Completion Verification antes de review\n9: - Quality Gates extendidos\n10: - Session Recovery\n11: - Project Map y estado del workflow\n12: \n13: Uso:\n14:     python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n15:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n16:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n17:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n18:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n19:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n20:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo\n21:     python .agent/agent_controller.py --project-root /path/to/destino  # Operar sobre workspace externo\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: # Fix encoding issues on Windows\n25: import codecs\n26: import json\n27: import os\n28: import re\n29: import subprocess\n30: import sys\n31: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n32: from pathlib import Path\n33: \n34: \n35: if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n36:     sys.stdout = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stdout.buffer)\n37:     sys.stderr = codecs.getwriter(\"utf-8\")(sys.stderr.buffer)\n38: \n39: # Fix imports when run as script (not as package)\n40: # WP-2026-122: project_root is now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root\n41: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n42: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n43: for _path in (str(_AGENT_DIR), str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED)):\n44:     if _path not in sys.path:\n45:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n46: \n47: # WP-2026-122: Import project_root module for dynamic path resolution\n48: # Entry points set AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var after parsing --project-root\n49: # Import AFTER sys.path setup to ensure runtime/ is importable\n50: from runtime.project_root import (  # noqa: E402\n51:     get_agent_dir,\n52:     get_collab_dir,\n53:     get_context_dir,\n54:     resolve_project_root,\n55: )\n56: \n57: \n58: # ============================================================================\n59: # IMPORTS: HOOK SYSTEM, SESSION TRACKER & COMPLETION CHECKER\n60: # ============================================================================\n61: try:\n62:     from hooks import registry as hook_registry\n63:     from hooks.post_tool_hook import post_tool_hook\n64:     from hooks.pre_action_hook import pre_action_hook\n65:     from hooks.stop_hook import stop_hook\n66: \n67:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = True\n68:     hook_registry.register(\"pre_action\", pre_action_hook)\n69:     hook_registry.register(\"post_tool\", post_tool_hook)\n70:     hook_registry.register(\"stop\", stop_hook)\n71: except ImportError:\n72:     HOOKS_AVAILABLE = False\n73:     hook_registry = None\n74: \n75: try:\n76:     from session_tracker import recover_session, save_session, show_recovery_hint\n77: \n78:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = True\n79: except ImportError:\n80:     SESSION_TRACKER_AVAILABLE = False\n81: \n82: try:\n83:     from completion_checker import check_completion, show_completion_report\n84: \n85:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = True\n86: except ImportError:\n87:     COMPLETION_CHECKER_AVAILABLE = False\n88: \n89: try:\n90:     from bus.event_bus import EventBus\n91:     from bus.utils import count_trailing_changes\n92: \n93:     BUS_AVAILABLE = True\n94: except ImportError:\n95:     BUS_AVAILABLE = False\n96:     EventBus = None\n97:     count_trailing_changes = None\n98: \n99: # WP-2026-146: Import approval system for HUMAN_GATE timeout\n100: try:\n101:     from bus.approval import (\n102:         ApprovalPolicy,\n103:         ApprovalReason,\n104:         ApprovalStatus,\n105:         ApprovalStore,\n106:     )\n107: \n108:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = True\n109: except ImportError:\n110:     APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE = False\n111:     ApprovalPolicy = None\n112:     ApprovalReason = None\n113:     ApprovalStatus = None\n114:     ApprovalStore = None\n115: \n116: # ============================================================================\n117: # PATH CONFIGURATION - WP-2026-122: Deferred resolution via project_root module\n118: # ============================================================================\n119: # Paths now resolved dynamically via runtime.project_root functions\n120: \n121: \n122: def _project_root() -> Path:\n123:     return resolve_project_root()\n124: \n125: \n126: class _LazyPath:\n127:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n128:         self._resolver = resolver\n129: \n130:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n131:         return self._resolver()\n132: \n133:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n134:         return self.resolve() / other\n135: \n136:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n137:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n138: \n139:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n140:         return str(self.resolve())\n141: \n142:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n143:         return str(self.resolve())\n144: \n145:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n146:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n147: \n148: \n149: SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\n150: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n151: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_agent_dir)\n152: COLLAB_DIR = _LazyPath(get_collab_dir)\n153: CONTEXT_DIR = _LazyPath(get_context_dir)\n154: \n155: # State files\n156: WORK_PLAN = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\")\n157: EXEC_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"execution_log.md\")\n158: REVIEW_QUEUE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"review_queue.md\")\n159: NOTIFICATIONS = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"notifications.md\")\n160: TURN_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"TURN.md\")\n161: STATE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"STATE.md\")\n162: ARCHIVE_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: get_collab_dir() / \"archive\")\n163: AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\")\n164: \n165: # Archive configuration\n166: MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB = 50\n167: MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES = 20\n168: \n169: # WP-2026-106 hotfix: HUMAN_GATE escalation threshold. Single source of truth\n170: # is manager_review.max_attempts in agents.json (shared with bus/review_bridge.py).\n171: # Fallback used only if config is missing or unreadable.\n172: HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK = 5\n173: \n174: # WP-2026-146: HUMAN_GATE timeout configuration. Single source of truth is\n175: # manager_review.human_gate_timeout_seconds in agents.json. Fallback used if missing.\n176: # Distinct from manager_review.timeout_seconds (AI subprocess wait, ~180s).\n177: HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK = 86400  # 24 hours\n178: \n179: # WP-2026-147: Graph context adapter import (optional)\n180: try:\n181:     from scripts.graph_context import generate_context_for_destination\n182: \n183:     GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE = True\n184: except ImportError:\n185:     GRAPH_CONTEXT_AVAILABLE = False\n186:     generate_context_for_destination = None\n187: \n188: # WP-2026-150: Project scanner import (optional)\n189: try:\n190:     from scripts.project_scanner import (\n191:         generate_report as scanner_generate_report,\n192:         scan_project,\n193:     )\n194: \n195:     SCANNER_AVAILABLE = True\n196: except ImportError:\n197:     SCANNER_AVAILABLE = False\n198:     scan_project = None\n199:     scanner_generate_report = None\n200: \n201: \n202: def get_human_gate_threshold() -> int:\n203:     \"\"\"Return the consecutive-CHANGES count that escalates a ticket to HUMAN_GATE.\n204: \n205:     Reads manager_review.max_attempts from agents.json so both this controller\n206:     and bus/review_bridge.py escalate on the same number. Falls back to\n207:     HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK if the config is absent or malformed.\n208:     \"\"\"\n209:     try:\n210:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n211:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"max_attempts\"))\n212:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n213:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n214:         return HUMAN_GATE_REJECTION_FALLBACK\n215: \n216: \n217: def get_human_gate_timeout() -> int:\n218:     \"\"\"Return the timeout in seconds for HUMAN_GATE approval requests.\n219: \n220:     Reads manager_review.human_gate_timeout_seconds from agents.json.\n221:     This is intentionally separate from manager_review.timeout_seconds,\n222:     which controls the AI subprocess wait (~180s). Human gates need hours.\n223:     Falls back to HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK (24h) if absent or malformed.\n224: \n225:     Returns:\n226:         Timeout in seconds (positive integer).\n227:     \"\"\"\n228:     try:\n229:         cfg = json.loads(AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n230:         value = int(cfg.get(\"manager_review\", {}).get(\"human_gate_timeout_seconds\"))\n231:         return value if value > 0 else HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n232:     except (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n233:         return HUMAN_GATE_TIMEOUT_FALLBACK\n234: \n235: \n236: # Configuracion de comprobaciones\n237: MAX_FILES_CIRCULAR_CHECK = 50\n238: \n239: # Estados validos para validacion\n240: VALID_PLAN_STATES = {\n241:     \"DRAFT\",\n242:     \"IN_PLANNING\",\n243:     \"APPROVED\",\n244:     \"IN_REVIEW\",\n245:     \"COMPLETED\",\n246:     \"N/A\",\n247: }\n248: VALID_LOG_STATES = {\n249:     \"PENDING\",\n250:     \"IN_PROGRESS\",\n251:     \"BLOCKED\",\n252:     \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n253:     # WP-2026-106: HUMAN_GATE and READY_TO_CLOSE are real terminal-adjacent\n254:     # states emitted by bus/state_machine.py; the validator must accept them.\n255:     \"HUMAN_GATE\",\n256:     \"READY_TO_CLOSE\",\n257:     \"COMPLETED\",\n258:     \"N/A\",\n259: }\n260: \n261: \n262: def _ensure_runtime_dirs() -> None:\n263:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n264:     archive_dir = get_collab_dir() / \"archive\"\n265:     context_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n266:     archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n267: \n268: \n269: def _get_event_bus() -> EventBus | None:\n270:     global event_bus\n271:     if not BUS_AVAILABLE:\n272:         return None\n273:     if event_bus is None:\n274:         event_bus = EventBus(get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n275:     return event_bus\n276: \n277: \n278: def _get_approval_store() -> ApprovalStore | None:\n279:     \"\"\"Get or create the approval store for HUMAN_GATE timeout.\n280: \n281:     Before: No approval store existed in agent_controller.\n282:     During: Creates ApprovalStore under runtime_dir with configurable timeout.\n283:     After: Returns configured ApprovalStore or None if approval system unavailable.\n284:     \"\"\"\n285:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n286:         return None\n287:     store_path = get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"approvals\" / \"store.json\"\n288:     timeout_seconds = get_human_gate_timeout()\n289:     policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n290:         policy_name=\"human_gate\",\n291:         timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n292:         auto_resolve=True,\n293:         auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n294:     )\n295:     return ApprovalStore(store_path=store_path, policy=policy)\n296: \n297: \n298: # Event bus is initialized lazily after the project root is known.\n299: event_bus = None\n300: \n301: # Circuit breaker state\n302: CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH = _LazyPath(\n303:     lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"circuit_breaker.json\"\n304: )\n305: BUILDER_LOCK_PATH = _LazyPath(lambda: get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\" / \"builder_lock.txt\")\n306: \n307: # ============================================================================\n308: # SCOPE GATE UTILITIES\n309: # ============================================================================\n310: \n311: EXCLUDE_FILES_REL = {\n312:     \"work_plan.md\",\n313:     \"execution_log.md\",\n314:     \"STATE.md\",\n315:     \"TURN.md\",\n316:     \"notifications.md\",\n317:     \".session_state.json\",\n318: }\n319: \n320: \n321: def _exclude_files() -> set[str]:\n322:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()\n323:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()\n324:     context_dir = get_context_dir()\n325:     exclude_files = {str((collab_dir / f).resolve()) for f in EXCLUDE_FILES_REL}\n326:     exclude_files.add(str((context_dir / \"project-map.json\").resolve()))\n327:     # Exclude bus runtime files (events.jsonl is managed by the bus, not the Builder)\n328:     exclude_files.add(\n329:         str((agent_dir / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\").resolve())\n330:     )\n331:     # Exclude agent config directory (managed by agents_config.py, not the Builder)\n332:     exclude_files.add(str((agent_dir / \"config\").resolve()))\n333:     return exclude_files\n334: \n335: \n336: def parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content: str) -> set[str]:\n337:     \"\"\"Parse Files Likely Touched from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n338:     lines = work_plan_content.split(\"\\n\")\n339:     in_section = False\n340:     files = set()\n341: \n342:     def _looks_like_path_token(token: str) -> bool:\n343:         if not token or \" \" in token:\n344:             return False\n345:         if token.startswith(\".\"):\n346:             return True\n347:         if \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token:\n348:             return True\n349:         basename = token.rsplit(\"/\", 1)[-1].rsplit(\"\\\\\", 1)[-1]\n350:         return \".\" in basename\n351: \n352:     for line in lines:\n353:         line = line.strip()\n354:         if \"## Files Likely Touched\" in line:\n355:             in_section = True\n356:             continue\n357:         if in_section and line.startswith(\"## \"):\n358:             break  # next section\n359:         if in_section and line and not line.startswith(\"---\"):\n360:             # normalize: remove backticks, quotes, bullets, trim\n361:             normalized = (\n362:                 line.lstrip(\"*- \")\n363:                 .replace(\"`\", \"\")\n364:                 .replace('\"', \"\")\n365:                 .replace(\"'\", \"\")\n366:                 .strip()\n367:             )\n368:             if normalized and _looks_like_path_token(normalized):\n369:                 # resolve relative to project root\n370:                 path = (PROJECT_ROOT / normalized).resolve()\n371:                 files.add(str(path))\n372:     return files\n373: \n374: \n375: def get_changed_files() -> set[str] | None:\n376:     \"\"\"Get all changed files: staged, unstaged, untracked. None if not git repo.\"\"\"\n377:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n378:         return None\n379:     try:\n380:         # Use -z for null-byte separated output to handle paths with spaces and renames safely\n381:         result = subprocess.run(\n382:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\", \"-z\"],\n383:             capture_output=True,\n384:             text=True,\n385:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n386:         )\n387:         changed = set()\n388:         # Split by null byte, each entry is: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status\n389:         # Renames have two consecutive entries: \"R old_path\" followed by \"new_path\"\n390:         entries = result.stdout.split(\"\\0\")\n391:         i = 0\n392:         while i < len(entries):\n393:             entry = entries[i]\n394:             if not entry:\n395:                 i += 1\n396:                 continue\n397:             # Format: \"XY path\" where XY is 2-char status (e.g., \"M \", \"??\", \"R \")\n398:             # When Y=' ' (space), format is \"M path\" (path at index 2)\n399:             # When Y!=' ', format is \"XY path\" with space separator (path at index 3)\n400:             if len(entry) >= 3:\n401:                 status = entry[:2]\n402:                 # Determine path start: if entry[2] is space, path starts at 3; otherwise at 2\n403:                 path = entry[3:] if entry[2] == \" \" else entry[2:]\n404:                 # Handle renames: status starts with 'R', next entry is the new path\n405:                 if status[0] == \"R\" and i + 1 < len(entries):\n406:                     new_path = entries[i + 1]\n407:                     if new_path:\n408:                         changed.add(new_path)\n409:                     i += 2\n410:                     continue\n411:                 else:\n412:                     changed.add(path)\n413:             i += 1\n414:         # resolve to absolute paths\n415:         resolved = set()\n416:         for f in changed:\n417:             path = (PROJECT_ROOT / f).resolve()\n418:             resolved.add(str(path))\n419:         return resolved\n420:     except FileNotFoundError:\n421:         return None\n422: \n423: \n424: def check_scope_gate(\n425:     work_plan_content: str, changed_files: set[str] | None, exclude_files: set[str]\n426: ) -> dict:\n427:     \"\"\"Check if changed files are within scope.\"\"\"\n428:     if changed_files is None:\n429:         return {\n430:             \"valid\": True,\n431:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n432:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n433:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n434:             \"warnings\": [\"Repository is not git-managed\"],\n435:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n436:         }\n437: \n438:     whitelist = parse_files_likely_touched(work_plan_content)\n439:     if not whitelist:\n440:         return {\n441:             \"valid\": True,\n442:             \"out_of_scope\": set(),\n443:             \"missing_from_diff\": set(),\n444:             \"covered_files\": set(),\n445:             \"warnings\": [\"No Files Likely Touched section in work_plan.md\"],\n446:             \"blocked_reason\": None,\n447:         }\n448: \n449:     relevant_changed = changed_files - exclude_files\n450:     relevant_whitelist = whitelist - exclude_files\n451:     covered_files = relevant_changed & relevant_whitelist\n452:     missing_from_diff = relevant_whitelist - relevant_changed\n453:     out_of_scope = relevant_changed - relevant_whitelist\n454: \n455:     warnings = []\n456:     blocked_reason = None\n457:     valid = len(out_of_scope) == 0\n458: \n459:     if relevant_whitelist and not covered_files:\n460:         valid = False\n461:         blocked_reason = (\n462:             \"None of the declared Files Likely Touched entries appeared in the diff\"\n463:         )\n464:     elif covered_files and missing_from_diff:\n465:         warnings.append(\n466:             \"Partial scope coverage: \"\n467:             f\"{len(covered_files)} of {len(relevant_whitelist)} declared files touched\"\n468:         )\n469: \n470:     return {\n471:         \"valid\": valid,\n472:         \"out_of_scope\": out_of_scope,\n473:         \"missing_from_diff\": missing_from_diff,\n474:         \"covered_files\": covered_files,\n475:         \"warnings\": warnings,\n476:         \"blocked_reason\": blocked_reason,\n477:     }\n478: \n479: \n480: def _load_mark_ready_context() -> tuple[str, str, str]:\n481:     \"\"\"Load the plan and log content for --mark-ready.\"\"\"\n482:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n483:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n484:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n485:     return plan_content, log_content, plan_id\n486: \n487: \n488: def _record_scope_override(scope_override: str, problem_files: set[str]) -> None:\n489:     \"\"\"Record a scope override in the execution log.\"\"\"\n490:     note = (\n491:         f\"Scope override: {scope_override}. \"\n492:         f\"Affected files: {', '.join(sorted(problem_files))}\"\n493:     )\n494:     update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", note)\n495: \n496: \n497: def _scope_gate_allows_close(gate_result: dict, scope_override: str | None) -> bool:\n498:     \"\"\"Apply scope gate decision for mark-ready.\"\"\"\n499:     if gate_result[\"valid\"]:\n500:         for warning in gate_result[\"warnings\"]:\n501:             print(f\"[WARN] {warning}\")\n502:         update_log_status(\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Marked ready by Builder\")\n503:         return True\n504: \n505:     if not scope_override:\n506:         print(\"[ERROR] Scope violation detected:\")\n507:         if gate_result.get(\"blocked_reason\"):\n508:             print(f\"  {gate_result['blocked_reason']}\")\n509:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]):\n510:             print(f\"  - {file_path}\")\n511:         for file_path in sorted(gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())):\n512:             print(f\"  - missing: {file_path}\")\n513:         print('Use --scope-override \"reason\" to proceed.')\n514:         return False\n515: \n516:     print(f\"[INFO] Scope override applied: {scope_override}\")\n517:     problem_files = set(gate_result[\"out_of_scope\"]) | set(\n518:         gate_result.get(\"missing_from_diff\", set())\n519:     )\n520:     _record_scope_override(scope_override, problem_files)\n521:     return True\n522: \n523: \n524: def _sync_mark_ready_targets(plan_id: str, plan_content: str) -> None:\n525:     \"\"\"Update TURN.md and STATE.md after mark-ready.\n526: \n527:     WP-2026-124: Uses bus-derived state as authority, not execution_log.md.\n528:     \"\"\"\n529:     # WP-2026-124: Get bus-derived state\n530:     log_status_before = get_status(read_file(EXEC_LOG), \"**Estado:**\")\n531:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n532:         from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState\n533: \n534:         events = event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=plan_id)\n535:         bus_state = (\n536:             StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([e.to_dict() for e in events])\n537:             if events\n538:             else TicketState.UNKNOWN\n539:         )\n540:         # Use bus state if available, fallback to log\n541:         from_state = (\n542:             bus_state.value if bus_state != TicketState.UNKNOWN else log_status_before\n543:         )\n544:     else:\n545:         from_state = log_status_before\n546: \n547:     action = {\n548:         \"role\": \"MANAGER\",\n549:         \"context_file\": \".manager_rules\",\n550:         \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md\",\n551:         \"instruction\": f\"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n552:         \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n553:         \"plan_status\": get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\"),\n554:         \"log_status\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n555:         \"action_type\": \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n556:         \"plan_type\": get_plan_type(plan_content),\n557:     }\n558:     update_turn_file(action)\n559: \n560:     state_content = read_file(STATE_FILE)\n561:     if not state_content:\n562:         return\n563: \n564:     lines = state_content.split(\"\\n\")\n565:     for index, line in enumerate(lines):\n566:         if \"**Estado actual:**\" in line:\n567:             lines[index] = \"- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n568:             write_file(STATE_FILE, \"\\n\".join(lines))\n569:             break\n570: \n571:     # Emit STATE_CHANGED to bus idempotently\n572:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n573:         # Check if already emitted for this ticket and state\n574:         latest_state_event = event_bus.latest_event(\n575:             ticket_id=plan_id, event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\"\n576:         )\n577:         if (\n578:             not latest_state_event\n579:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n580:         ):\n581:             event_bus.emit(\n582:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n583:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n584:                 actor=\"BUILDER\",\n585:                 payload={\n586:                     \"from_state\": from_state,\n587:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n588:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n589:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n590:                 },\n591:             )\n592:         if (\n593:             not latest_state_event\n594:             or latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\") != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"\n595:         ):\n596:             bus_from_state = (\n597:                 latest_state_event.payload.get(\"to_state\")\n598:                 if latest_state_event and latest_state_event.payload\n599:                 else log_status_before\n600:             )\n601:             event_bus.emit(\n602:                 event_type=\"STATE_CHANGED\",\n603:                 ticket_id=plan_id,\n604:                 actor=\"SUPERVISOR\",\n605:                 payload={\n606:                     \"from_state\": bus_from_state,\n607:                     \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\",\n608:                     \"reason\": \"Builder completed implementation\",\n609:                     \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n610:                 },\n611:             )\n612: \n613: \n614: # ============================================================================\n615: # CIRCUIT BREAKER AND CHECKOUT UTILITIES\n616: # ============================================================================\n617: \n618: \n619: def _read_circuit_breaker() -> dict:\n620:     \"\"\"Read circuit breaker state from JSON file.\"\"\"\n621:     if not CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.exists():\n622:         return {\n623:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n624:             \"failures\": 0,\n625:             \"last_failure\": None,\n626:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n627:         }\n628:     try:\n629:         return json.loads(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n630:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n631:         return {\n632:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n633:             \"failures\": 0,\n634:             \"last_failure\": None,\n635:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n636:         }\n637: \n638: \n639: def _write_circuit_breaker(state: dict) -> None:\n640:     \"\"\"Write circuit breaker state to JSON file.\"\"\"\n641:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n642:     CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PATH.write_text(\n643:         json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n644:     )\n645: \n646: \n647: def _check_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> dict:\n648:     \"\"\"\n649:     Check circuit breaker state for a ticket.\n650:     Returns dict with 'open', 'reason', 'failures', 'no_progress_count'.\n651:     \"\"\"\n652:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n653:     if breaker.get(\"state\") == \"OPEN\":\n654:         return {\n655:             \"open\": True,\n656:             \"reason\": breaker.get(\"reason\", \"Circuit breaker triggered\"),\n657:             \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n658:             \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n659:         }\n660:     return {\n661:         \"open\": False,\n662:         \"reason\": None,\n663:         \"failures\": breaker.get(\"failures\", 0),\n664:         \"no_progress_count\": breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0),\n665:     }\n666: \n667: \n668: def _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n669:     reason: str, plan_id: str, is_no_progress: bool = False\n670: ) -> None:\n671:     \"\"\"Trigger circuit breaker to OPEN state.\"\"\"\n672:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n673:     breaker[\"state\"] = \"OPEN\"\n674:     breaker[\"reason\"] = reason\n675:     breaker[\"last_triggered\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n676:     breaker[\"ticket_id\"] = plan_id\n677:     if is_no_progress:\n678:         breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n679:     else:\n680:         breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n681:     _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n682: \n683: \n684: def _reset_circuit_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n685:     \"\"\"Reset circuit breaker to CLOSED state after successful completion.\"\"\"\n686:     _write_circuit_breaker(\n687:         {\n688:             \"state\": \"CLOSED\",\n689:             \"failures\": 0,\n690:             \"last_failure\": None,\n691:             \"no_progress_count\": 0,\n692:             \"last_reset\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n693:             \"last_ticket\": plan_id,\n694:         }\n695:     )\n696: \n697: \n698: def _record_error_for_breaker(plan_id: str, error_msg: str) -> None:\n699:     \"\"\"Record an error for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n700:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n701:     breaker[\"failures\"] = breaker.get(\"failures\", 0) + 1\n702:     breaker[\"last_error\"] = error_msg\n703:     breaker[\"last_error_time\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n704: \n705:     # Trigger if 3+ consecutive errors\n706:     if breaker[\"failures\"] >= 3:\n707:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(f\"Repeated errors: {error_msg[:100]}\", plan_id)\n708:     else:\n709:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n710: \n711: \n712: def _record_no_progress_for_breaker(plan_id: str) -> None:\n713:     \"\"\"Record no-progress event for circuit breaker evaluation.\"\"\"\n714:     breaker = _read_circuit_breaker()\n715:     breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] = breaker.get(\"no_progress_count\", 0) + 1\n716:     breaker[\"last_no_progress\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n717: \n718:     # Trigger if 3+ no-progress events\n719:     if breaker[\"no_progress_count\"] >= 3:\n720:         _trigger_circuit_breaker(\n721:             \"No progress detected in multiple iterations\", plan_id, is_no_progress=True\n722:         )\n723:     else:\n724:         _write_circuit_breaker(breaker)\n725: \n726: \n727: def _read_builder_lock() -> dict | None:\n728:     \"\"\"Read builder lock file if it exists.\"\"\"\n729:     if not BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.exists():\n730:         return None\n731:     try:\n732:         return json.loads(BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n733:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n734:         return None\n735: \n736: \n737: def _acquire_builder_lock(\n738:     plan_id: str, pid: int, role: str = \"BUILDER\", backend: str = \"unknown\"\n739: ) -> bool:\n740:     \"\"\"\n741:     Attempt to acquire builder lock atomically.\n742:     Returns True if lock was acquired, False if another session holds it.\n743:     \"\"\"\n744:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n745:     if existing is not None:\n746:         # Check if lock is stale (different ticket or dead process)\n747:         if existing.get(\"ticket_id\") != plan_id:\n748:             # Stale lock from different ticket - override\n749:             lock_data = {\n750:                 \"pid\": pid,\n751:                 \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n752:                 \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n753:                 \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n754:                 \"role\": role,\n755:                 \"backend\": backend,\n756:                 \"round\": 1,\n757:             }\n758:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n759:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n760:                 json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n761:             )\n762:             return True\n763:         # Same ticket - check if process is alive (simplified: just allow re-acquisition)\n764:         lock_data = {\n765:             \"pid\": pid,\n766:             \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n767:             \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n768:             \"started_at\": existing.get(\n769:                 \"started_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n770:             ),\n771:             \"role\": role,\n772:             \"backend\": backend,\n773:             \"round\": existing.get(\"round\", 1) + 1,\n774:         }\n775:         BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n776:             json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n777:         )\n778:         return True\n779: \n780:     # No existing lock - acquire fresh\n781:     lock_data = {\n782:         \"pid\": pid,\n783:         \"ticket_id\": plan_id,\n784:         \"project_root\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n785:         \"started_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n786:         \"role\": role,\n787:         \"backend\": backend,\n788:         \"round\": 1,\n789:     }\n790:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n791:     BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.write_text(\n792:         json.dumps(lock_data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n793:     )\n794:     return True\n795: \n796: \n797: def _release_builder_lock(plan_id: str) -> None:\n798:     \"\"\"Release builder lock after completion.\"\"\"\n799:     import contextlib\n800: \n801:     existing = _read_builder_lock()\n802:     if existing and existing.get(\"ticket_id\") == plan_id:\n803:         with contextlib.suppress(OSError):\n804:             BUILDER_LOCK_PATH.unlink()\n805: \n806: \n807: def _emit_builder_exit(plan_id: str, exit_reason: str, completion_summary: str) -> None:\n808:     \"\"\"Emit BUILDER_EXIT event to the bus - required for ticket closure.\"\"\"\n809:     if BUS_AVAILABLE and event_bus:\n810:         event_bus.emit(\n811:             event_type=\"BUILDER_EXIT\",\n812:             ticket_id=plan_id,\n813:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n814:             payload={\n815:                 \"exit_reason\": exit_reason,\n816:                 \"completion_summary\": completion_summary,\n817:                 \"source\": \"mark-ready\",\n818:             },\n819:         )\n820: \n821: \n822: def _create_human_gate_approval_request(\n823:     ticket_id: str, timeout_seconds: int | None = None\n824: ) -> bool:\n825:     \"\"\"Create and persist an ApprovalRequest when escalating to HUMAN_GATE.\n826: \n827:     Before: HUMAN_GATE escalation had no persistent timeout tracking.\n828:     During: Creates an ApprovalRequest with timeout metadata in the ApprovalStore.\n829:     After: The approval request persists across restarts and can be expired by\n830:            the supervisor's check_and_expire_all() loop.\n831: \n832:     Args:\n833:         ticket_id: The ticket ID being escalated to HUMAN_GATE.\n834:         timeout_seconds: Optional timeout override. Defaults to config value.\n835: \n836:     Returns:\n837:         True if the approval request was created successfully, False otherwise.\n838:     \"\"\"\n839:     if not APPROVAL_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE:\n840:         print(\n841:             \"[WARN] Approval system unavailable; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n842:             file=sys.stderr,\n843:             flush=True,\n844:         )\n845:         return False\n846: \n847:     store = _get_approval_store()\n848:     if store is None:\n849:         print(\n850:             \"[WARN] Could not create ApprovalStore; HUMAN_GATE timeout will not persist.\",\n851:             file=sys.stderr,\n852:             flush=True,\n853:         )\n854:         return False\n855: \n856:     import uuid\n857: \n858:     approval_id = f\"hg-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"\n859: \n860:     # Create and persist the approval request\n861:     timeout = (\n862:         timeout_seconds if timeout_seconds is not None else get_human_gate_timeout()\n863:     )\n864:     metadata = {\n865:         \"escalation_type\": \"human_gate\",\n866:         \"timeout_seconds\": timeout,\n867:         \"created_by\": \"agent_controller\",\n868:         \"source\": \"escalate-human-gate\",\n869:     }\n870: \n871:     try:\n872:         # Use custom policy if timeout_seconds was provided\n873:         if timeout_seconds is not None:\n874:             custom_policy = ApprovalPolicy(\n875:                 policy_name=\"human_gate_custom\",\n876:                 timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n877:                 auto_resolve=True,\n878:                 auto_resolve_status=ApprovalStatus.EXPIRED,\n879:             )\n880:             store.create_request(\n881:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n882:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n883:                 metadata=metadata,\n884:                 policy=custom_policy,\n885:             )\n886:         else:\n887:             store.create_request(\n888:                 approval_id=approval_id,\n889:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n890:                 metadata=metadata,\n891:             )\n892:         print(\n893:             f\"[OK] Created HUMAN_GATE approval request {approval_id} for {ticket_id} \"\n894:             f\"(timeout={timeout}s).\",\n895:             flush=True,\n896:         )\n897:         return True\n898:     except Exception as exc:\n899:         print(\n900:             f\"[ERROR] Failed to create HUMAN_GATE approval request: {exc}\",\n901:             file=sys.stderr,\n902:             flush=True,\n903:         )\n904:         return False\n905: \n906: \n907: def _auto_archive_closed_artifacts() -> None:\n908:     \"\"\"Auto-archive closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts during mark-ready.\n909: \n910:     Before: Requires COLLAB_DIR to exist with potential closed PLAN/AUDIT files.\n911:     During: Imports and calls archive_collaboration_artifacts.py as a library function.\n912:     After: Closed PLAN/AUDIT files are moved to _archive/plan_audit/ (idempotent, silent on no-op).\n913:     \"\"\"\n914:     try:\n915:         # Import the archive script as a module\n916:         import importlib.util\n917: \n918:         archive_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(\n919:             \"archive_collaboration_artifacts\",\n920:             PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / \"archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\",\n921:         )\n922:         if archive_spec and archive_spec.loader:\n923:             archive_mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(archive_spec)\n924:             archive_spec.loader.exec_module(archive_mod)\n925: \n926:             # Call the archive function (idempotent, no-op if nothing to archive)\n927:             archive_mod.archive_collaboration_artifacts(\n928:                 collaboration_dir=COLLAB_DIR,\n929:                 dry_run=False,\n930:             )\n931:     except Exception as exc:\n932:         # Silent fail with debug logging - archiving is best-effort, not critical path\n933:         print(f\"[WARN] Auto-archive failed: {exc}\")\n934: \n935: \n936: # ============================================================================\n937: # UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n938: # ============================================================================\n939: \n940: \n941: def read_file(path: Path) -> str:\n942:     \"\"\"Lee un archivo si existe, retorna string vacio si no.\"\"\"\n943:     if not path.exists():\n944:         return \"\"\n945:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n946:         return f.read()\n947: \n948: \n949: def write_file(path: Path, content: str) -> None:\n950:     \"\"\"Escribe contenido a un archivo, creando directorios si es necesario.\"\"\"\n951:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n952:     with open(path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n953:         f.write(content)\n954: \n955: \n956: def get_status(content: str, marker: str) -> str:\n957:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado de un archivo buscando un marcador.\"\"\"\n958:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n959:         if marker in line:\n960:             return line.split(marker)[1].strip()\n961:     return \"UNKNOWN\"\n962: \n963: \n964: def get_plan_id(content: str) -> str:\n965:     \"\"\"Extrae el ID del plan de trabajo.\"\"\"\n966:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n967:         if \"**ID:**\" in line or \"**Plan ID:**\" in line:\n968:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n969:     return \"N/A\"\n970: \n971: \n972: def get_plan_type(content: str) -> str:\n973:     \"\"\"Extrae el tipo de plan. Valores: IMPLEMENTATION (default) | FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n974:     for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n975:         if \"**Tipo:**\" in line:\n976:             return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip().upper()\n977:     return \"IMPLEMENTATION\"\n978: \n979: \n980: def check_git_status() -> bool | None:\n981:     \"\"\"Verifica si el repositorio esta limpio.\"\"\"\n982:     if not (PROJECT_ROOT / \".git\").exists():\n983:         return None\n984:     try:\n985:         result = subprocess.run(\n986:             [\"git\", \"status\", \"--porcelain\"],\n987:             capture_output=True,\n988:             text=True,\n989:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n990:         )\n991:         return not result.stdout.strip()\n992:     except FileNotFoundError:\n993:         return None\n994: \n995: \n996: def extract_status_emoji(status_str: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n997:     \"\"\"Extrae el estado limpio y el emoji.\"\"\"\n998:     emojis = {\"🟢\", \"🟡\", \"🔴\", \"🟣\", \"✅\", \"⏳\", \"❌\", \"⚠️\"}\n999:     status_clean = status_str.strip()\n1000:     found_emoji = \"\"\n1001:     for emoji in emojis:\n1002:         if emoji in status_clean:\n1003:             found_emoji = emoji\n1004:             status_clean = status_clean.replace(emoji, \"\").strip()\n1005:             break\n1006:     return status_clean, found_emoji\n1007: \n1008: \n1009: _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n1010: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n1011:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n1012:     re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n1013: )\n1014: \n1015: \n1016: def _check_deliverable_type(content: str) -> list[str]:\n1017:     \"\"\"Check deliverable_type field in work_plan.md content.\n1018: \n1019:     Before: Requires work_plan.md content as string.\n1020:     During: Searches for deliverable_type field using regex, validates against allowed values.\n1021:     After: Returns list of warning strings (empty if valid). Never returns errors (V1 informational).\n1022:     \"\"\"\n1023:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n1024:     if not match:\n1025:         return [\n1026:             \"work_plan.md missing deliverable_type field. \"\n1027:             \"Add one of: code, documentation, research, analysis, mixed.\"\n1028:         ]\n1029:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n1030:     if \"+\" in value:\n1031:         return [\n1032:             f\"work_plan.md deliverable_type='{value}' uses compound syntax. \"\n1033:             \"Use 'mixed' instead of 'code+documentation' etc.\"\n1034:         ]\n1035:     if value not in _VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES:\n1036:         return [\n1037:             f\"work_plan.md has unknown deliverable_type '{value}'. \"\n1038:             f\"Expected one of: {sorted(_VALID_DELIVERABLE_TYPES)}\"\n1039:         ]\n1040:     return []\n1041: \n1042: \n1043: def _validate_work_plan() -> list[str]:\n1044:     \"\"\"Validate work_plan.md file.\"\"\"\n1045:     errors = []\n1046:     content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1047:     if not content:\n1048:         return errors\n1049: \n1050:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1051:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1052:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_PLAN_STATES:\n1053:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1054:     if \"**ID:**\" not in content:\n1055:         errors.append(\"Falta campo **ID:**\")\n1056:     return errors\n1057: \n1058: \n1059: def _validate_execution_log() -> list[str]:\n1060:     \"\"\"Validate execution_log.md file.\"\"\"\n1061:     errors = []\n1062:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1063:     if not content:\n1064:         return errors\n1065: \n1066:     status_raw = get_status(content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1067:     status_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(status_raw)\n1068:     if status_clean and status_clean not in VALID_LOG_STATES:\n1069:         errors.append(f\"Estado invalido: '{status_clean}'\")\n1070:     return errors\n1071: \n1072: \n1073: def _validate_turn_file() -> list[str]:\n1074:     \"\"\"Validate TURN.md file.\"\"\"\n1075:     errors = []\n1076:     content = read_file(TURN_FILE)\n1077:     if content and \"## Agente Activo\" not in content:\n1078:         errors.append(\"Falta secciÃƒÂ³n '## Agente Activo'\")\n1079:     return errors\n1080: \n1081: \n1082: def _validate_notifications() -> list[str]:\n1083:     \"\"\"Validate notifications.md file.\"\"\"\n1084:     errors = []\n1085:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1086:     if content and \"</thinking>\" in content:\n1087:         errors.append(\"Contiene etiquetas </thinking> (corrupto)\")\n1088:     return errors\n1089: \n1090: \n1091: def _validate_cross_file_consistency() -> list[str]:\n1092:     \"\"\"Validate consistency across files.\"\"\"\n1093:     errors = []\n1094:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n1095:     log_content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1096: \n1097:     if not plan_content or not log_content:\n1098:         return errors\n1099: \n1100:     plan_status_raw = get_status(plan_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1101:     log_status_raw = get_status(log_content, \"**Estado:**\")\n1102:     plan_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(plan_status_raw)\n1103:     log_clean, _ = extract_status_emoji(log_status_raw)\n1104: \n1105:     # Plan APPROVED pero log COMPLETED\n1106:     if \"APPROVED\" in plan_clean and \"COMPLETED\" in log_clean:\n1107:         errors.append(\n1108:             \"DRIFT: plan=APPROVED pero log=COMPLETED -- \"\n1109:             \"el log pertenece a un ciclo anterior. Limpia execution_log.md.\"\n1110:         )\n1111: \n1112:     # Plan COMPLETED pero log IN_PROGRESS\n1113:     if \"COMPLETED\" in plan_clean and \"IN_PROGRESS\" in log_clean:\n1114:         errors.append(\n1115:             \"DRIFT: plan=COMPLETED pero log=IN_PROGRESS -- \"\n1116:             \"el Builder no cerro su bitacora correctamente.\"\n1117:         )\n1118: \n1119:     # Plan IN_PLANNING con log READY_FOR_REVIEW\n1120:     if \"IN_PLANNING\" in plan_clean and \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\" in log_clean:\n1121:         errors.append(\n1122:             \"DRIFT: plan=IN_PLANNING pero log=READY_FOR_REVIEW -- \"\n1123:             \"estado imposible. El plan debe estar APPROVED antes de que el Builder entregue.\"\n1124:         )\n1125: \n1126:     # Plan N/A pero log activo\n1127:     if (\"N/A\" in plan_clean or not plan_clean) and log_clean not in (\n1128:         \"N/A\",\n1129:         \"COMPLETED\",\n1130:         \"PENDING\",\n1131:         \"\",\n1132:     ):\n1133:         errors.append(\n1134:             f\"DRIFT: no hay plan activo pero log={log_clean} -- \"\n1135:             \"limpia execution_log.md o crea un nuevo work_plan.md.\"\n1136:         )\n1137: \n1138:     return errors\n1139: \n1140: \n1141: def _validate_host_project_prefix() -> list[str]:\n1142:     \"\"\"Validate that host-project destinations have 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md.\n1143: \n1144:     Before: Requires PROJECT.md to exist at project root; agents.json to be readable.\n1145:     During: Checks if active_profile is 'host-project' and if PROJECT.md has 'Ticket prefix:'.\n1146:     After: Returns list of warnings (empty if validation passes).\n1147: \n1148:     Returns:\n1149:         List of warning strings (empty if no issues).\n1150:     \"\"\"\n1151:     warnings = []\n1152:     agents_config = AGENTS_CONFIG_PATH\n1153:     if not agents_config.exists():\n1154:         return warnings\n1155: \n1156:     try:\n1157:         config_data = json.loads(agents_config.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n1158:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n1159:         return warnings\n1160: \n1161:     active_profile = config_data.get(\"active_profile\", \"\")\n1162:     if active_profile != \"host-project\":\n1163:         return warnings\n1164: \n1165:     # Only check for host-project profile\n1166:     project_md = PROJECT_ROOT / \"PROJECT.md\"\n1167:     if not project_md.exists():\n1168:         warnings.append(\n1169:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks PROJECT.md. \"\n1170:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace.\"\n1171:         )\n1172:         return warnings\n1173: \n1174:     content = project_md.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1175:     if \"Ticket prefix:\" not in content:\n1176:         warnings.append(\n1177:             \"[WARN] host-project destination lacks 'Ticket prefix:' in PROJECT.md. \"\n1178:             \"Add 'Ticket prefix: XXX' to declare local ticket namespace (XXX-YYYY-NNN).\"\n1179:         )\n1180: \n1181:     return warnings\n1182: \n1183: \n1184: def validate_state_files() -> dict[str, list[str]]:\n1185:     \"\"\"Valida el formato y consistencia cruzada de los archivos de estado.\"\"\"\n1186:     return {\n1187:         \"work_plan.md\": _validate_work_plan(),\n1188:         \"execution_log.md\": _validate_execution_log(),\n1189:         \"notifications.md\": _validate_notifications(),\n1190:         \"TURN.md\": _validate_turn_file(),\n1191:         \"consistency\": _validate_cross_file_consistency(),\n1192:         \"host_project_prefix\": _validate_host_project_prefix(),\n1193:     }\n1194: \n1195: \n1196: def fix_corrupted_notifications() -> bool:\n1197:     \"\"\"Intenta reparar notifications.md si esta corrupto.\"\"\"\n1198:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1199:     if not content:\n1200:         return False\n1201:     original = content\n1202:     content = re.sub(r\"</thinking>\\s*\", \"\", content)\n1203:     content = re.sub(r\"\\n{4,}\", \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\", content)\n1204:     if content != original:\n1205:         write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, content)\n1206:         return True\n1207:     return False\n1208: \n1209: \n1210: def archive_old_notifications() -> str | None:\n1211:     \"\"\"Archiva notificaciones antiguas si el archivo es muy grande.\"\"\"\n1212:     content = read_file(NOTIFICATIONS)\n1213:     if not content:\n1214:         return None\n1215: \n1216:     file_size_kb = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\")) / 1024\n1217:     entry_count = len([e for e in content.split(\"---\") if e.strip()])\n1218: \n1219:     if (\n1220:         file_size_kb < MAX_NOTIFICATIONS_SIZE_KB\n1221:         and entry_count <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES\n1222:     ):\n1223:         return None\n1224: \n1225:     parts = content.split(\"---\")\n1226:     entries = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]\n1227: \n1228:     if len(entries) <= MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:\n1229:         return None\n1230: \n1231:     entries_to_archive = entries[:-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES]\n1232:     entries_to_keep = entries[-MAX_NOTIFICATION_ENTRIES:]\n1233: \n1234:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n1235:     archive_file = ARCHIVE_DIR / f\"notifications_{timestamp}.md\"\n1236: \n1237:     archive_content = \"# Notificaciones Archivadas\\n\\n\"\n1238:     archive_content += f\"**Fecha:** {datetime.now():%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S}\\n\"\n1239:     archive_content += \"\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_archive)\n1240: \n1241:     write_file(archive_file, archive_content)\n1242: \n1243:     new_content = \"# Registro de Notificaciones\\n\\n---\\n\\n\"\n1244:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(entries_to_keep)\n1245:     new_content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\"\n1246:     write_file(NOTIFICATIONS, new_content)\n1247: \n1248:     return str(archive_file)\n1249: \n1250: \n1251: def run_finalization_checks() -> dict:\n1252:     \"\"\"Checks adicionales para planes de tipo FINALIZATION.\"\"\"\n1253:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"summary\": []}\n1254:     checks = {\n1255:         \"README.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"README.md\",\n1256:         \"CHANGELOG.md\": PROJECT_ROOT / \"CHANGELOG.md\",\n1257:         \"closeout_report.md\": COLLAB_DIR / \"closeout_report.md\",\n1258:     }\n1259:     for name, path in checks.items():\n1260:         if path.exists():\n1261:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[OK] {name}: Presente\")\n1262:         else:\n1263:             results[\"summary\"].append(f\"[WARN] {name}: No encontrado\")\n1264:     return results\n1265: \n1266: \n1267: def run_quality_gates(plan_type: str = \"IMPLEMENTATION\") -> dict:\n1268:     \"\"\"Ejecuta validaciones automaticas.\"\"\"\n1269:     print(\"\\n[QUALITY GATES] Ejecutando Quality Gates...\")\n1270:     results = {\"passed\": True, \"errors\": [], \"summary\": [], \"warnings\": []}\n1271: \n1272:     state_errors = validate_state_files()\n1273:     total_state_errors = sum(len(errs) for errs in state_errors.values())\n1274:     if total_state_errors > 0:\n1275:         results[\"warnings\"].append(\n1276:             f\"Archivos de estado: {total_state_errors} problemas\"\n1277:         )\n1278:     else:\n1279:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Estado: Archivos validos\")\n1280: \n1281:     src_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"src\"\n1282:     tests_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"tests\"\n1283:     dirs_to_check = [str(d) for d in [src_dir, tests_dir] if d.exists()]\n1284:     if not dirs_to_check:\n1285:         dirs_to_check = [\".\"]\n1286: \n1287:     try:\n1288:         ruff = subprocess.run(\n1289:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"ruff\", \"check\", *dirs_to_check],\n1290:             capture_output=True,\n1291:             text=True,\n1292:             timeout=60,\n1293:         )\n1294:         if ruff.returncode != 0:\n1295:             results[\"passed\"] = False\n1296:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Ruff: Errores de linting\")\n1297:         else:\n1298:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Ruff: Limpio\")\n1299:     except FileNotFoundError:\n1300:         results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Ruff: No instalado\")\n1301: \n1302:     if tests_dir.exists():\n1303:         try:\n1304:             pytest_result = subprocess.run(\n1305:                 [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pytest\", \"-q\"],\n1306:                 capture_output=True,\n1307:                 timeout=120,\n1308:                 cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n1309:             )\n1310:             if pytest_result.returncode != 0:\n1311:                 results[\"passed\"] = False\n1312:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[FAIL] Pytest: Tests fallando\")\n1313:             else:\n1314:                 results[\"summary\"].append(\"[OK] Pytest: Tests OK\")\n1315:         except FileNotFoundError:\n1316:             results[\"summary\"].append(\"[WARN] Pytest: No instalado\")\n1317: \n1318:     if plan_type == \"FINALIZATION\":\n1319:         fin_results = run_finalization_checks()\n1320:         results[\"summary\"].extend(fin_results[\"summary\"])\n1321: \n1322:     status = \"[PASSED]\" if results[\"passed\"] else \"[FAILED]\"\n1323:     print(f\"   {status}\")\n1324:     return results\n1325: \n1326: \n1327: def update_log_status(new_status: str, note: str) -> bool:\n1328:     \"\"\"Actualiza el estado en execution_log.md.\"\"\"\n1329:     content = read_file(EXEC_LOG)\n1330:     if not content:\n1331:         return False\n1332:     lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n1333:     for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n1334:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n1335:             lines[i] = f\"**Estado:** {new_status}\"\n1336:             new_content = \"\\n\".join(lines) + f\"\\n\\n{note}\"\n1337:             write_file(EXEC_LOG, new_content)\n1338:             return True\n1339:     return False\n1340: \n1341: \n1342: def create_findings_file(plan_id: str = \"N/A\") -> Path:\n1343:     \"\"\"Crea findings.md desde template.\"\"\"\n1344:     findings_path = COLLAB_DIR / \"findings.md\"\n1345: \n1346:     if findings_path.exists():\n1347:         return findings_path\n1348: \n1349:     template_path = AGENT_DIR / \"templates\" / \"findings_template.md\"\n1350: \n1351:     try:\n1352:         if template_path.exists():\n1353:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1354:         else:\n1355:             template = \"\"\"# Hallazgos de Investigacion\n1356: \n1357: **Plan ID:** {{PLAN_ID}}\n1358: **Creado:** {{DATE}}\n1359: \n1360: ---\n1361: \n1362: ## Hallazgos\n1363: \n1364: <!-- Documenta aqui tus hallazgos durante la investigacion -->\n1365: \"\"\"\n1366: \n1367:         content = template.replace(\"{{PLAN_ID}}\", plan_id)\n1368:         content = content.replace(\n1369:             \"{{DATE}}\", datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n1370:         )\n1371: \n1372:         write_file(findings_path, content)\n1373:         print(\"  [OK] Creado findings.md\")\n1374: \n1375:     except Exception as e:\n1376:         print(f\"  [WARN] Error creando findings.md: {e}\")\n1377: \n1378:     return findings_path\n1379: \n1380: \n1381: def _run_pre_action_hooks(\n1382:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, action_type: str\n1383: ) -> None:\n1384:     \"\"\"Run pre-action hooks if available.\"\"\"\n1385:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1386:         hook_registry.execute(\n1387:             \"pre_action\",\n1388:             {\n1389:                 \"action_type\": action_type,\n1390:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1391:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1392:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1393:             },\n1394:         )\n1395: \n1396: \n1397: def _check_quality_gates(\n1398:     plan_id: str, plan_type: str, plan_status: str, skip_gates: bool\n1399: ) -> dict | None:\n1400:     \"\"\"Check quality gates and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1401:     gate_result = run_quality_gates(plan_type=plan_type)\n1402:     if not gate_result[\"passed\"]:\n1403:         update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", \"AUTO-REJECTED: Quality Gates fallaron\")\n1404:         return {\n1405:             \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1406:             \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1407:             \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1408:             \"instruction\": \"RECHAZADO. Quality Gates fallaron. Corrige errores.\",\n1409:             \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1410:             \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1411:             \"log_status\": \"AUTO-REJECTED\",\n1412:             \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1413:         }\n1414:     return None\n1415: \n1416: \n1417: def _check_completion_verification(\n1418:     plan_id: str, plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_type: str, strict_mode: bool\n1419: ) -> dict | None:\n1420:     \"\"\"Check completion verification in strict mode and return action if failed.\"\"\"\n1421:     if HOOKS_AVAILABLE and hook_registry:\n1422:         stop_result = stop_hook(\n1423:             {\n1424:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1425:                 \"plan_type\": plan_type,\n1426:                 \"mode\": \"strict\" if strict_mode else \"normal\",\n1427:             }\n1428:         )\n1429:         if strict_mode and not stop_result.get(\"can_complete\", True):\n1430:             return {\n1431:                 \"role\": \"BUILDER\",\n1432:                 \"context_file\": \".builder_rules\",\n1433:                 \"workflow_file\": \".agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md\",\n1434:                 \"instruction\": (\n1435:                     f\"Plan {plan_id} no supera Completion Verification. \"\n1436:                     \"Corrige advertencias antes de review.\"\n1437:                 ),\n1438:                 \"plan_id\": plan_id,\n1439:                 \"plan_status\": plan_status,\n1440:                 \"log_status\": log_status,\n1441:                 \"action_type\": \"FIX_QUALITY_ISSUES\",\n1442:             }\n1443:     return None\n1444: \n1445: \n1446: # Transition table for base routing\n1447: TRANSITION_TABLE = {\n1448:     (\"no_plan\", \"any\"): (\n1449:         \"MANAGER\",\n1450:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1451:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1452:     ),\n1453:     (\"completed\", \"any\"): (\n1454:         \"MANAGER\",\n1455:         \"CREATE_PLAN\",\n1456:         \"No hay plan activo. Crea un nuevo work_plan.md\",\n1457:     ),\n1458:     (\"in_planning\", \"any\"): (\n1459:         \"MANAGER\",\n1460:         \"FINALIZE_PLAN\",\n1461:         \"Plan {plan_id} en borrador. Finaliza y cambia a APPROVED\",\n1462:     ),\n1463:     (\"approved\", \"ready_for_review\"): (\n1464:         \"MANAGER\",\n1465:         \"REVIEW_WORK\",\n1466:         \"Builder completo {plan_id}. Revisa el trabajo.\",\n1467:     ),\n1468:     (\"approved\", \"any\"): (\n1469:         \"BUILDER\",\n1470:         \"IMPLEMENT\",\n1471:         \"Plan {plan_id} aprobado. Implementa segun work_plan.md\",\n1472:     ),\n1473:     (\"in_review\", \"any\"): (\n1474:         \"MANAGER\",\n1475:         \"REVIEW_CHANGES\",\n1476:         \"Plan {plan_id} en revision. Verifica cambios.\",\n1477:     ),\n1478:     (\"any\", \"blocked\"): (\n1479:         \"MANAGER\",\n1480:         \"RESOLVE_BLOCK\",\n1481:         \"Builder BLOQUEADO en {plan_id}. Resuelve en review_queue.md\",\n1482:     ),\n1483: }\n1484: \n1485: \n1486: def _lookup_transition(\n1487:     plan_status: str, log_status: str, plan_id: str\n1488: ) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:\n1489:     \"\"\"Lookup transition from table.\"\"\"\n1490:     # Normalize status for lookup\n1491:     plan_key = (\n1492:         \"no_plan\"\n1493:         if not plan_status or plan_status in (\"COMPLETED\", \"N/A\")\n1494:         else plan_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\")\n1495:     )\n1496:     log_key = log_status.lower().replace(\" \", \"_\") if log_status else \"any\"\n1497: \n1498:     # Check specific matches first\n1499:     for (p_key, l_key), (role, action, template) in TRANSITION_TABLE.items():\n1500:         if (p_key == plan_key or p_key == \"any\") and (\n1501:             l_key == log_key or l_key == \"any\"\n1502:         ):\n1503:             instruction = template.format(plan_id=plan_id)\n1504:             return role, action, instruction\n1505: \n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1505. Use offset=1506 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603,S607\n\"\"\"\nAgent Controller v5 - Sistema Multi-Agente\n==========================================\nOrquestador con:\n- Hook System (pre-action, post-tool y stop hooks)\n- Native Claude Code hooks (PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, SubagentStop)\n- Completion Verification antes de review\n- Quality Gates extendidos\n- Session Recovery\n- Project Map y estado del workflow\n\nUso:\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py              # Ver estado y turno actual\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --json       # Output en JSON\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --skip-gates # Saltar Quality Gates\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --archive    # Archivar notificaciones antiguas\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate   # Solo validar archivos de estado\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --strict     # Modo estricto (bloquea si falla)\n    python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket # Emitir STATE_CHANGED inicial para el ticket activo","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\agent_controller.py","time":{"start":1779872196522,"end":1779872196568}},"id":"prt_e68a65c64001P90keDYME1C566","sessionID":"ses_1975a026fffeKXcZn9T6fEAEF6","messageID":"msg_e68a64781001R3c2NT1hwV5iqF"}}

\"\"\"Tests for WP-2026-152: request-changes requeue handoff.\n2: \n3: Tests the _handle_request_changes() function to ensure:\n4: - Requeue is accepted only when REVIEW_DECISION=changes is the direct antecedent.\n5: - Generic IN_PROGRESS without that antecedent fails closed.\n6: - UNKNOWN state falls back to execution_log path.\n7: - The handler derives pending_requeue from events[-1], not a second latest_event() read.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: \n10: import json\n11: import sys\n12: from pathlib import Path\n13: from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n14: \n15: import pytest\n16: \n17: # Ensure project root is in path\n18: PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\n19: sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))\n20: \n21: from bus.event_bus import EventRecord  # noqa: E402\n22: \n23: \n24: def _rec(\n25:     seq: int,\n26:     event_type: str = \"REVIEW_DECISION\",\n27:     decision: str | None = None,\n28:     to_state: str | None = None,\n29: ) -> EventRecord:\n30:     \"\"\"Build an EventRecord for testing.\"\"\"\n31:     payload = {}\n32:     if decision is not None:\n33:         payload[\"decision\"] = decision\n34:     if to_state is not None:\n35:         payload[\"to_state\"] = to_state\n36:     return EventRecord(\n37:         event_id=f\"e{seq}\",\n38:         event_type=event_type,\n39:         ticket_id=\"WP-2026-152\",\n40:         actor=\"MANAGER\",\n41:         timestamp=\"2026-05-27T00:00:00+00:00\",\n42:         payload=payload,\n43:         sequence_number=seq,\n44:     )\n45: \n46: \n47: class TestHandleRequestChangesRequeue:\n48:     \"\"\"Test _handle_request_changes() requeue logic.\"\"\"\n49: \n50:     @pytest.fixture\n51:     def mock_files(self, tmp_path: Path):\n52:         \"\"\"Create minimal mock files for testing.\"\"\"\n53:         work_plan = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n54:         work_plan.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n55:         work_plan.write_text(\n56:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n## Metadata\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-152\\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\\n\",\n57:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n58:         )\n59:         exec_log = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"execution_log.md\"\n60:         exec_log.write_text(\n61:             \"# Execution Log\\n\\n**Estado:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\"\n62:         )\n63:         state_file = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"STATE.md\"\n64:         state_file.write_text(\n65:             \"# State\\n\\n- **Estado actual:** READY_FOR_REVIEW\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\"\n66:         )\n67:         turn_file = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"TURN.md\"\n68:         turn_file.write_text(\"# TURN\\n\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n69:         agents_config = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n70:         agents_config.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n71:         agents_config.write_text(\n72:             json.dumps(\n73:                 {\n74:                     \"active_profile\": \"engine-dev\",\n75:   bus.latest_event = MagicMock(return_value=None)\n88:         bus.emit = MagicMock()\n89:         return bus\n90: \n91:     def test_allowed_requeue_with_changes_antecedent(\n92:         self, lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"Tests for WP-2026-152: request-changes requeue handoff.\n\nTests the _handle_request_changes() function to ensure:\n- Requeue is accepted only when {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779872196594,"sessionID":"ses_1975a026fffeKXcZn9T6fEAEF6","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_098c76b374dd4e9dbd4cdf30","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\tests\\unit\\test_review_bridge_request_changes_logging.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\tests\\unit\\test_review_bridge_request_changes_logging.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: \"\"\"Tests for WP-2026-152 Phase 2: bridge stderr logging for failed --request-changes.\n2: \n3: Verifies that the review bridge logs non-zero --request-changes returncodes\n4: to stderr without changing the transition semantics.\n5: \"\"\"\n6: \n7: import subprocess\n8: import sys\n9: from pathlib import Path\n10: from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n11: \n12: import pytest\n13: \n14: \n15: # Ensure project root is in path\n16: PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\n17: sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))\n18: \n19: \n20: class TestReviewBridgeRequestChangesLogging:\n21:     \"\"\"Test that the bridge logs failed --request-changes calls to stderr.\"\"\"\n22: \n23:     @pytest.fixture\n24:     def mock_event_bus(self):\n25:         \"\"\"Create a mock event bus.\"\"\"\n26:         bus = MagicMock()\n27:         bus.read_events = MagicMock(return_value=[])\n28:         bus.latest_event = MagicMock(return_value=None)\n29:         bus.emit = MagicMock()\n30:         return bus\n31: \n32:     @pytest.fixture\n33:     def mock_project_root(self, tmp_path: Path):\n34:         \"\"\"Create a mock project root with minimal structure.\"\"\"\n35:         work_plan = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n36:         work_plan.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n37:         work_plan.write_text(\n38:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n## Metadata\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-152\\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\\n\",\n39:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n40:         )\n41:         agents_config = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n42:         agents_config.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n43:         agents_config.write_text(\n44:             '{\"active_profile\": \"engine-dev\", \"manager_review\": {\"max_attempts\": 5}}',\n45:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n46:         )\n47:         return tmp_path\n48: \n49:     def test_bridge_logs_nonzero_request_changes_returncode(\n50:         self, mock_event_bus: MagicMock, mock_project_root: Path, capfd\n51:     ):\n52:         \"\"\"Bridge logs non-zero --request-changes returncode to stderr.\"\"\"\n53:         from bus import review_bridge\n54: \n55:         bridge = review_bridge.ReviewBridge(\n56:             event_bus=mock_event_bus,\n57:             project_root=mock_project_root,\n58:         )\n59: \n60:         # Mock subprocess.run to simulate failed --request-changes\n61:         mock_result = subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n62:             args=[\"python\", \"agent_controller.py\", \"--request-changes\", \"WP-2026-152\"],\n63:             returncode=1,\n64:             stdout=\"\",\n65:             stderr=\"Error: some error occurred\",\n66:         )\n67: \n68:         with patch.object(review_bridge.subprocess, \"run\", return_value=mock_result):\n69:             # Call the part of run_manager_review_cycle that handles CHANGES\n70:             # We can't call the full cycle easily, so we test the logging directly\n71:             # by simulating what happens after a CHANGES decision\n72:             controller = mock_project_root / \".agent\" / \"agent_controller.py\"\n73:             controller.touch(exist_ok=True)  # Ensure file exists\n74: \n75:             rc_result = subprocess.run(\n76:                 [sys.executable, str(controller), \"--request-changes\", \"WP-2026-152\"],\n77:                 capture_output=True,\n78:                 text=True,\n79:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n80:                 cwd=mock_project_root,\n81:                 env=bridge._review_env(),\n82:                 timeout=180,\n83:             )\n84: \n85:             # Verify the logging behavior\n86:             if rc_result.returncode != 0:\n87:                 print(\n88:                     f\"[manager-review-bridge] --request-changes failed (rc={rc_result.returncode}): \"\n89:                     f\"{rc_result.stderr.strip() or rc_result.stdout.strip()}\",\n90:                     file=sys.stderr,\n91:                 )\n92: \n93:         captured = capfd.readouterr()\n94:         # Verify stderr contains the logging message\n95:         assert \"--request-changes failed\" in captured.err\n96:         assert \"rc=1\" in captured.err\n97: \n98:     def test_bridge_logging_does_not_change_semantics(\n99:         self, mock_event_bus: MagicMock, mock_project_root: Path\n100:     ):\n101:         \"\"\"Bridge stderr logging is observability only, does not change semantics.\"\"\"\n102:         from bus import review_bridge\n103: \n104:         bridge = review_bridge.ReviewBridge(\n105:             event_bus=mock_event_bus,\n106:             project_root=mock_project_root,\n107:         )\n108: \n109:         # Mock subprocess.run to simulate failed --request-changes\n110:         mock_result = subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n111:             args=[\"python\", \"agent_controller.py\", \"--request-changes\", \"WP-2026-152\"],\n112:             returncode=1,\n113:             stdout=\"\",\n114:             stderr=\"Error: some error occurred\",\n115:         )\n116: \n117:         with patch.object(review_bridge.subprocess, \"run\", return_value=mock_result):\n118:             # The bridge should still continue to check consecutive_changes_count\n119:             # The logging is purely observational\n120:             controller = mock_project_root / \".agent\" / \"agent_controller.py\"\n121:             controller.touch(exist_ok=True)\n122: \n123:             rc_result = subprocess.run(\n124:                 [sys.executable, str(controller), \"--request-changes\", \"WP-2026-152\"],\n125:                 capture_output=True,\n126:                 text=True,\n127:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n128:                 cwd=mock_project_root,\n129:                 env=bridge._review_env(),\n130:                 timeout=180,\n131:             )\n132: \n133:             # The returncode is logged but the flow continues\n134:             # (in the real code, it would check _count_prior_changes_from_bus next)\n135:             assert rc_result.returncode == 1\n136:             # Logging happened (we can't easily assert on print to stderr in isolation)\n137:             # but the key point is: the code continues, not exits\n\n(End of file - total 137 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"Tests for WP-2026-152 Phase 2: bridge stderr logging for failed --request-changes.\n\nVerifies that the review bridge logs non-zero --request-changes returncodes\nto stderr without changing the transition semantics.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n\nimport pytest\n\n\n# Ensure project root is in path\nPROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\nsys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))\n\n\nclass TestReviewBridgeRequestChangesLogging:","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"tests\\unit\\test_review_bridge_request_changes_logging.py","time":{"start":1779872196539,"end":1779872196590}},"id":"prt_e68a663bc001djLs8wzmoGkbqH","sessionID":"ses_1975a026fffeKXcZn9T6fEAEF6","messageID":"msg_e68a64781001R3c2NT1hwV5iqF"}}

# ruff: noqa: S603\n2: from __future__ import annotations\n3: \n4: import json\n5: import os\n6: import re\n7: import shutil\n8: import subprocess\n9: import sys\n10: import time\n11: import
