{"type":"step_start","timestamp":1779874611541,"sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","part":{"id":"prt_e68cb4150001rqKiG2Ukt3Z8E7","messageID":"msg_e68cb203a0019gZOsJKSt1v3JN","sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","snapshot":"2b7407fde7d141eeaee9f21d6992d8a516e57d2e","type":"step-start"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779874617846,"sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_49e1a0b4d2b94551b794b5a8","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\README.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\README.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # orquestador_de_agentes\n2: \n3: Central motor for multi-agent orchestration. Operational code lives once here; destination projects keep only their `.agent/` workspace (state, memory, events, config) and reference the motor externally.\n4: \n5: ## What this is\n6: \n7: A **domain-agnostic central motor** that automates work by making one agent generate plans, another implement them, and a third review the result. The motor lives once in this repo; destination projects prepare a workspace (`.agent/`) and reference the motor externally. No copying of operational code.\n8: \n9: ### Three-agent loop\n10: \n11: | Role | Responsibility | Default backend |\n12: |---|---|---|\n13: | **Supervisor** | Generates plans, derives tickets, coordinates the cycle | Claude Code |\n14: | **Builder** | Implements tickets, runs gates, emits `BUILDER_EXIT` | OpenCode → Qwen 3.5 (`opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus`) |\n15: | **Manager** | Reviews Builder output with a deliverable-type-aware rubric; emits `APPROVE` / `CHANGES` | OpenCode → OpenAI (`openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, routed natively since WP-072) |\n16: \n17: Backends are pluggable per role in `.agent/config/agents.json` (`role_assignments` + `role_models`).\n18: \n19: ### Current reference setup\n20: \n21: The author's working configuration on Windows:\n22: \n23: - **Supervisor**: Claude Code (chat-driven planning + terminal control)\n24: - **Manager**: OpenCode routed to OpenAI Codex / GPT (`openai/gpt-5.4-mini`)\n25: - **Builder**: OpenCode routed to Qwen 3.5 Plus via the [opencode.ai/go](https://opencode.ai/go) plan\n26: - **Approximate cost**: ~50 EUR/month (Anthropic + opencode-go bundle)\n27: \n28: This is a **reference** setup, not a constraint. Swap any role for another backend by editing `agents.json`. The bus, state machine and review bridge are backend-agnostic.\n29: \n30: ## Current state\n31: \n32: - **Version**: `v9.14.0`\n33: - **Status**: Stable. Pre-plan grilling skill added (WP-2026-153).\n34: - **Last work**: `WP-2026-153` — Add grill-with-docs skill for pre-plan interrogation.\n35: - **Tests**: 255 passing. Validation 0 errors. Ruff clean. pip-audit clean.\n36: \n37: ### What changed since v9.9.0\n38: \n39: | WP | Topic |\n40: |----|-------|\n41: | WP-2026-153 | Pre-plan grilling skill (`/grill-plan`, `/grill`, `grill-wp`) for resolving ambiguous terminology before work plan creation |\n42: | WP-2026-144 | Destination prefix onboarding + timeout hotfix: `--install --prefix XXX` writes namespace; timeout no longer emits `inspect` to bus |\n43: | WP-2026-087 | Smoke test bus agnóstico + 5-gap analysis (proved bus is domain-neutral) |\n44: | WP-2026-088 | `deliverable_type` field in work_plan schema (V1 informational) |\n45: | WP-2026-089 | Pluggable quality-gates dispatch by deliverable_type (`scripts/run_gates_dispatch.py`) |\n46: | WP-2026-090 | **Host-first skill precedence + config profiles** (`active_profile: engine-dev/host-project`) |\n47: | WP-2026-091 | Pluggable manager review rubric by deliverable_type (code / mixed / docs / research / analysis) |\n48: | WP-2026-092 | Conditional pip-audit by dependency surface (only runs when manifest files in scope) |\n49: | WP-2026-093 | Pre-commit ruff scope guard (Python-only enforcement) |\n50: | WP-2026-094 | **Host setup hook** for post-install bootstrap (`.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}`) |\n51: | WP-2026-095 | **Manager review V2**: single-shot prompt with budget, retry, forensic events |\n52: | WP-2026-111 | **Central motor + destination workspace**: motor lives once, destination references externally |\n53: | WP-2026-113 | **Central motor release consolidation**: manifiestos, documentacion e instalador alineados |\n54: | WP-2026-123 | **Workspace minimo del destino**: enlace motor-destino con schema explicito |\n55: | WP-2026-135 | **Pre-compact hook**: recuperacion selectiva de contexto antes de compactacion |\n56: | WP-2026-136 | **session_close_observations `--candidates`**: canal de inyeccion semantica para el bucle de autoaprendizaje del Manager |\n57: | WP-2026-139 | **Anti-patterns index file**: inventario canonico AP-01..AP-08 con cache y separacion indice/instrucciones |\n58: | WP-2026-140 | **Bus import boundary firewall**: test AST+grep que protege el seam `bus/→scripts/` (`scripts.discover_skills` unica excepcion); 255 tests, ruff limpio |\n59: | WP-2026-141 | **Review standards (eng-practices)**: criterio de aprobacion Google, convencion `Nit`, trazabilidad en AGENTS.md y CREDITS.md |\n60: | WP-2026-142 | **Symmetric scope gate**: `--mark-ready` blocks when no whitelist file appears in diff |\n61: | WP-2026-143 | **Idempotent `--mark-ready`**: bus-state guard prevents double review cycle emission |\n62: | WP-2026-144 | **Destination prefix onboarding + timeout hotfix**: `--install --prefix XXX` writes namespace; timeout no longer emits `inspect` to bus |\n63: \n64: ## Central motor architecture (WP-2026-111)\n65: \n66: The motor lives **once** in this repo. A destination project:\n67: \n68: 1. **Prepares a workspace**: `.agent/` with state, memory, events, config.\n69: 2. **References the motor externally**: no copying of operational code.\n70: 3. **Can override skills**: put its own skills in `<destination>/.agent/skills/`. The motor catalog acts as fallback. Host-first precedence in `scripts/discover_skills.py`.\n71: 4. **Can bootstrap itself**: drop `.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}` in the destination. `scripts/install_agent_system.py` detects and invokes it with interactive confirmation post-copy.\n72: 5. **Chooses deliverable type per ticket**: `deliverable_type: code | documentation | research | analysis | mixed` in `work_plan.md` switches the gate dispatch and Manager review rubric.\n73: 6. **Flips profile automatically**: `agents.json.active_profile` goes from `engine-dev` (motor repo) to `host-project` (destination) during install/sync.\n74: 7. **Receives motor-destination link file**: `.agent/config/motor_destination_link.json` with schema (motor_root, destination_root, motor_version, destination_id, created_at, manifest_version) for portable traceability (WP-2026-123).\n75: 8. **Uses a local ticket namespace in the destination**: the destination `PROJECT.md` declares `Ticket prefix: XXX`, and tickets in that repo use `XXX-YYYY-NNN`. The motor repo keeps `WP-YYYY-NNN`. The installer can write this prefix with `--install --prefix XXX` or `--sync --prefix XXX`.\n76: \n77: ## Version contract\n78: \n79: - `pyproject.toml` → portable package version.\n80: - `.agent/.version_manifest.json` → installed core/system version.\n81: - Canonical upgrade scripts: `scripts/detect_version.py`, `scripts/upgrade.py`, `scripts/rollback.py`.\n82: \n83: ## Main layers\n84: \n85: - `.agent/collaboration/` — canonical operational state (`TURN.md`, `STATE.md`, `work_plan.md`, `execution_log.md`)\n86: - `.agent/runtime/events/events.jsonl` — append-only event bus (authoritative)\n87: - `.agent/runtime/memory/` — persistent project memory (`observations.jsonl` + `MEMORY.md`)\n88: - `.agent/runtime/audit/` — local audit snapshots\n89: - `.agent/runtime/compare/` — repo-compare reports (gitignored)\n90: - `.agent/config/agents.json` — backends + roles + `manager_review` tuning\n91: - `.agent/hooks/` — safety and validation hooks\n92: - `bus/` — event bus, supervisor, state machine, review bridge, redaction\n93: - `skills/` — bundled default skills (catalog of 20); destination can override via `.agent/skills/`\n94: - `scripts/` — install, upgrade, gates dispatch, skill discovery, audits, maintenance\n95: - `MANIFEST.distribute` — frontera del motor central (codigo operativo)\n96: - `MANIFEST.workspace` — contrato del workspace destino (estado, memoria, eventos, config)\n97: \n98: ## Typical flow — engine development\n99: \n100: 1. Edit `work_plan.md` with the next WP.\n101: 2. `python .agent/agent_controller.py --bootstrap-ticket --force` emits initial bus event.\n102: 3. Builder implements, then `python .agent/agent_controller.py --mark-ready --json --force` emits `BUILDER_EXIT` + `STATE_CHANGED → READY_FOR_REVIEW`.\n103: 4. Manager review bridge dispatches; on `DECISION: APPROVE` the canonical close cascade fires.\n104: 5. If the bridge times out / inspects: `python .agent/agent_controller.py --manager-approve --ticket WP-XXXX --force` closes manually.\n105: \n106: ## Memory bootstrap\n107: \n108: **If memory already exists**, read in this order:\n109: \n110: 1. `.agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md` — short index (start here).\n111: 2. `.agent/runtime/memory/observations.jsonl` — full history for depth.\n112: 3. `.agent/runtime/memory/session_close_report.md` — last closeout summary.\n113: 4. `graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md` — compact graph snapshot (optional).\n114: \n115: **If memory is empty or missing**, seed it after the first closed cycle:\n116: \n117: ```powershell\n118: python scripts/session_close_observations.py --ticket WP-YYYY-NNN\n119: python scripts/memory_consolidate.py --apply --verbose\n120: ```\n121: \n122: ## Typical flow — installing in a new project\n123: \n124: ```bash\n125: # From destination project root:\n126: python /path/to/orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --install\n127: ```\n128: \n129: The installer:\n130: 1. Prepares the destination workspace (`.agent/` structure with state, memory, config).\n131: 2. Flips `active_profile` to `host-project` in `agents.json`.\n132: 3. Detects `.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}` if present and (with `y/N` confirmation, or `--yes` for CI) executes it.\n133: 4. Reports the source (host vs motor) of each discovered skill.\n134: 5. Does NOT copy motor code (scripts/, skills/, bus/, agent_system/, core .agent/).\n135: 6. Writes `.agent/config/motor_destination_link.json` with schema explicit for portable traceability (WP-2026-123).\n136: \n137: Before the first ticket in the destination, set the namespace in that repo's `PROJECT.md`:\n138: \n139: ```text\n140: Ticket prefix: XXX\n141: ```\n142: \n143: Or let the installer write it for you:\n144: \n145: ```bash\n146: python /path/to/orquestador_de_agentes/scripts/install_agent_system.py --install --prefix XXX\n147: ```\n148: \n149: Use that prefix for all local ticket IDs and work-plan documents in the destination. Do not reuse the motor's `WP-YYYY-NNN` namespace there.\n150: \n151: ## Common commands\n152: \n153: ```powershell\n154: # Validation & gates\n155: python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force\n156: python scripts/run_gates_dispatch.py        # dispatches by deliverable_type\n157: python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py\n158: ruff check .\n159: uv run pip-audit .\n160: \n161: # Skill discovery\n162: python scripts/discover_skills.py --json\n163: python scripts/check_skill_collisions.py\n164: \n165: # Memory & audit\n166: python scripts/memory_consolidate.py --apply\n167: python scripts/local_audit.py\n168: \n169: # Config migration (idempotent)\n170: python .agent/agents_config.py --migrate [--dry-run]\n171: \n172: # Compare with another GitHub repo\n173: # via repo-compare skill (uses MCP GitHub tools)\n174: ```\n175: \n176: ## Documentation map\n177: \n178: - `AGENTS.md` — transversal rules + dual-mode architecture notes\n179: - `CLAUDE.md` — Claude Code entry guide\n180: - `PROJECT.md` — current project state and contract\n181: - `QUICKSTART.md` — minimal setup checklist and daily commands\n182: - `INTERACTION_MODES.md` — chat-driven vs terminal-driven flow\n183: - `REPOSITORY_STRUCTURE.md` — internal layout and public boundary\n184: - `CHANGELOG.md` — change history (WP-by-WP)\n185: - `CREDITS.md` — external attributions (every WP with `Origen externo` has a row)\n186: - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` — canonical onboarding prompt for fresh agent sessions\n187: \n188: ## License\n189: \n190: MIT (core). See `LICENSE`.\n\n(End of file - total 190 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# orquestador_de_agentes\n\nCentral motor for multi-agent orchestration. Operational code lives once here; destination projects keep only their `.agent/` workspace (state, memory, events, config) and reference the motor externally.\n\n## What this is\n\nA **domain-agnostic central motor** that automates work by making one agent generate plans, another implement them, and a third review the result. The motor lives once in this repo; destination projects prepare a workspace (`.agent/`) and reference the motor externally. No copying of operational code.\n\n### Three-agent loop\n\n| Role | Responsibility | Default backend |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Supervisor** | Generates plans, derives tickets, coordinates the cycle | Claude Code |\n| **Builder** | Implements tickets, runs gates, emits `BUILDER_EXIT` | OpenCode → Qwen 3.5 (`opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus`) |\n| **Manager** | Reviews Builder output with a deliverable-type-aware rubric; emits `APPROVE` / `CHANGES` | OpenCode → OpenAI (`openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, routed natively since WP-072) |\n\nBackends are pluggable per role in `.agent/config/agents.json` (`role_assignments` + `role_models`).\n\n### Current reference setup\n","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"README.md","time":{"start":1779874617780,"end":1779874617843}},"id":"prt_e68cb5000001RZu8bwbZzK9Inj","sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","messageID":"msg_e68cb203a0019gZOsJKSt1v3JN"}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779874617869,"sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_6f5b9eaac80b491e8560a90e","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\CHANGELOG.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\CHANGELOG.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # 2026-05-27 - WP-2026-153 Add grill-with-docs skill\n2: \n3: ### Added\n4: - `skills/grill-work-plan/SKILL.md`: Pre-plan interrogation skill with triggers `/grill-plan`, `/grill`, `grill-wp`.\n5:   - One-question-at-a-time flow with recommended answer before user response\n6:   - Default context: `PROJECT.md` and `MEMORY.md`; `CONTEXT.md` optional at repo root\n7:   - Codebase-first answers (grep/search before speculation)\n8:   - ADR criteria from mattpocock (hard to revert, surprising without context, real trade-off)\n9:   - Exact completion handshake: `> ✅ Grill completo. Términos resueltos: N. Puedes crear el WP con /plan.`\n10: - `skills/README.md`: Registered new skill in catalog table and index.\n11: \n12: ### Changed\n13: - `README.md`: Updated skills count (19 → 20), current state, and changelog table.\n14: - `PROJECT.md`: Updated state to WP-2026-153 COMPLETED.\n15: \n16: ### Summary\n17: - Pre-plan grilling reduces fuzzy scopes and avoidable review loops.\n18: - Skill is documentation-only (no Python runtime, no dependencies).\n19: - Optional integration with `man-create-work-plan` (opt-in, not blocking).\n20: \n21: ---\n22: # 2026-05-26 - WP-2026-144 Destination ticket prefix onboarding + timeout hotfix\n23: \n24: ### Added\n25: - `scripts/install_agent_system.py`: `--install --prefix XXX` and `--sync --prefix XXX` write\n26:   `Ticket prefix: XXX` into destination `PROJECT.md` and `ticket_prefix` into `motor_destination_link.json`.\n27: - `agent_controller.py --validate`: warns when `active_profile == host-project` and `PROJECT.md`\n28:   lacks `Ticket prefix:` (new `host_project_prefix` warning category).\n29: - `tests/unit/test_install_agent_system.py`: 4 new cases for `--prefix` plumbing.\n30: - `tests/unit/test_validate_host_prefix.py`: 5 cases covering prefix-present, prefix-missing,\n31:   engine-dev skip, and forced validation.\n32: \n33: ### Fixed (hotfix)\n34: - `bus/review_bridge.py`: timeout-exhausted retries no longer emit `REVIEW_DECISION: inspect`\n35:   to the bus (which mapped to HUMAN_GATE). Instead the cycle is reclassified as\n36:   `REVIEW_TRANSPORT_FAILED` with `failure_reason: timeout`. Semantic `inspect` is now reserved\n37:   exclusively for explicit Manager requests. Test `test_retry_exhausted_falls_to_inspect`\n38:   renamed and updated to assert `TRANSPORT_FAILED`.\n39: \n40: ### Updated\n41: - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `QUICKSTART.md`, `RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`,\n42:   `CLOSURE_MODEL.md`: destination namespace convention `XXX-YYYY-NNN` with `Ticket prefix: XXX`.\n43: \n44: ### Summary\n45: - Destination projects are now self-describing for ticket IDs via `--prefix`.\n46: - Validation enforces the prefix declaration on host-project workspaces.\n47: - Timeout failures no longer pollute the bus with spurious `inspect` events.\n48: \n49: ---\n50: # 2026-05-26 - WP-2026-143 Idempotent --mark-ready via bus-state guard\n51: \n52: ### Fixed\n53: - `agent_controller.py _handle_mark_ready`: added bus-state guard before the markdown guard.\n54:   Reads `StateMachine.derive_state_from_events()`; returns 0 (no-op) when bus state is already\n55:   READY_FOR_REVIEW, READY_TO_CLOSE or COMPLETED. HUMAN_GATE check also reads bus first.\n56:   Prevents double-emission of STATE_CHANGED→READY_FOR_REVIEW when Builder calls `--mark-ready`\n57:   a second time after the bus has advanced.\n58: - `tests/unit/test_mark_ready_idempotency.py`: 8 tests covering all guard paths.\n59: \n60: ---\n61: # 2026-05-26 - WP-2026-142 Symmetric scope gate in --mark-ready\n62: \n63: ### Added\n64: - `agent_controller.py check_scope_gate`: symmetric check — blocks when the intersection of\n65:   changed files and whitelist is empty (`covered_files = ∅`); warns when partial.\n66:   `_scope_gate_allows_close` updated to handle `blocked_reason` and `missing_from_diff`.\n67: - `tests/unit/test_scope_gate.py`: 16 tests covering parse, get_changed_files, gate cases, and\n68:   end-to-end mark-ready blocking.\n69: \n70: ---\n71: # 2026-05-26 - WP-2026-141 Google eng-practices review standards alignment\n72: \n73: ### Added\n74: - `skills/man-review-implementation/references/review-checklist.md`: sección `## Aprobacion y Nit`\n75:   con criterio positivo de aprobacion (aprobar cuando mejora la salud del codigo, aunque no sea perfecto),\n76:   convencion `Nit` para comentarios no bloqueantes, y principio de CLs pequeños. URLs directas a\n77:   `standard.html`, `comments.html` y `small-cls.html` de `google.github.io/eng-practices`.\n78: - `AGENTS.md`: punto 4 en \"Criterio de cierre\" referenciando el principio de aprobacion Google.\n79: - `CREDITS.md`: fila de atribucion para `google/eng-practices` (CC-BY 3.0, adapted).\n80: \n81: ### Summary\n82: - Adaptacion quirurgica: sin nueva skill ni nueva superficie. Las 3 inserciones son aditivas\n83:   sobre superficies existentes. Trazabilidad completa desde contrato (AGENTS.md) hasta\n84:   ejecucion (review-checklist.md) y atribucion (CREDITS.md).\n85: \n86: ---\n87: # 2026-05-26 - WP-2026-140 Bus import boundary test + ruff test suite cleanup\n88: \n89: ### Added\n90: - `tests/test_bus_boundary.py`: firewall AST-based que verifica que `bus/` no importa\n91:   `scripts.*` salvo el seam permitido (`scripts.discover_skills`). Segunda prueba\n92:   grep-based detecta imports dinamicos (`importlib.import_module`, `__import__`).\n93: \n94: ### Changed\n95: - `pyproject.toml`: `extend-exclude` corregido de `\"tests/\"` a `\"tests/sandbox/\"` para\n96:   que ruff cubra los tests reales. `per-file-ignores` ampliado con politica explicita\n97:   para patrones legitimos en tests: `S603`, `S607`, `S108`, `SIM115`, `PERF203`.\n98: \n99: ### Fixed\n100: - 118 violaciones ruff en 70 archivos de tests: SIM117 (nested-with -> parenthesized),\n101:   PERF401 (for-append -> list comprehension), RUF001/002 (mojibake en docstrings),\n102:   RUF059 (tuplas desempaquetadas sin uso -> `_`), N805/N806 (naming), F841\n103:   (asignaciones sin uso), SIM105 (try/except/pass -> contextlib.suppress), B007,\n104:   E402, ERA001.\n105: \n106: ### Summary\n107: - 255 tests verdes, ruff limpio. Seam `bus/ -> scripts/` protegido por firewall\n108:   estatico + dinamico. `per-file-ignores` como politica (no noqa por linea) para\n109:   patrones sistematicamente legitimos en el suite de tests.\n110: \n111: ---\n112: # 2026-05-25 - WP-2026-136 Semantic --candidates input for session_close_observations\n113: \n114: ### Added\n115: - `scripts/session_close_observations.py`: flag `--candidates <json_file>` para\n116:   inyectar candidatos semanticos externos sin tocar el pipeline de validacion.\n117:   `load_candidates_from_file()` con manejo estricto: FileNotFoundError,\n118:   ValueError (UTF-8 invalido), ValueError (JSON roto), ValueError (non-list).\n119:   Elementos no-dict se saltan con warning.\n120: - `tests/unit/test_session_close_observations.py`: 25 tests (era 16). Nuevos:\n121:   exclusion mutua, JSON valido, archivo ausente, JSON corrupto, top-level invalido,\n122:   elementos no-dict, dispatch correcto, UTF-8 invalido, lista vacia exit 0.\n123: \n124: ### Changed\n125: - `--ticket` y `--candidates` en `add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)`.\n126: - `load_existing_observations()` endurecida con `errors=\"replace\"`.\n127: - `skills/session-close-observations/SKILL.md`: modo --candidates, exclusion mutua,\n128:   workflow con fuente externa documentados.\n129: \n130: ### Fixed\n131: - `load_candidates_from_file` usaba `errors=\"replace\"` silenciando UTF-8 invalido;\n132:   corregido a `read_bytes().decode(\"utf-8\")` para lanzar ValueError como contratado.\n133: - Lista vacia de candidatos tratada como error (exit 1); corregido a exit 0.\n134: \n135: ### Summary\n136: - Canal de inyeccion semantica listo para bucle de autoaprendizaje del Manager.\n137:   Fundamento de: audit_findings -> session_close --candidates -> observations.jsonl\n138:   -> review_bridge inyecta contexto dinamico en el prompt del Manager (WP-B).\n139: - 25 tests verdes, ruff limpio, stdlib only.\n140: \n141: ---\n142: # 2026-05-25 - WP-2026-135 Selective context recovery lite for pre-compact hook\n143: \n144: ### Added\n145: - `.agent/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py`: hook funcional que carga `observations.jsonl`,\n146:   extrae keywords de `work_plan.md`, rankea por recencia + keyword matching (cap 5) y\n147:   proyecta `additionalContext` con sección `Memoria relevante` antes de compactar.\n148: - `tests/unit/test_pre_compact_hook.py`: 25 tests (TestLoadObservationsSafe,\n149:   TestExtractKeywordsFromWorkPlan, TestScoreObservation, TestRankObservations,\n150:   TestFormatMemorySection, TestMainHook, TestRobustness).\n151: \n152: ### Changed\n153: - `bus/review_bridge.py`: rubric de `code` y `mixed` ampliad con checklist de\n154:   anti-patrones como BLOCKERs: mock drift, floor assertion, zero-logic wrapper.\n155: - `AGENTS.md`: documentados los tres anti-patrones bajo secciones de testing (§2)\n156:   e implementación (§3) para guiar a Builder y Manager en cada revisión.\n157: \n158: ### Fixed\n159: - `pre_compact_hook.py` loader: `open(..., errors=\"replace\")` para sobrevivir bytes\n160:   UTF-8 inválidos sin lanzar `UnicodeDecodeError`.\n161: - `score_observation` y `format_memory_section`: normalización `str(x or \"\")`\n162:   para evitar `AttributeError`/`TypeError` con campos `null`, numéricos o booleanos.\n163: \n164: ### Summary\n165: - Hook ligero: sin embeddings, sin LLM, sin dependencias externas (stdlib only).\n166: - Rutas derivadas de `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent` (no `cwd()`).\n167: - Contrato del hook preservado: `continue=true`, JSON válido siempre.\n168: - 242 + 25 = 267 tests verdes (los 25 del hook no están en el runner pytest-safe\n169:   porque el archivo vive en `.agent/`; se ejecutan con pytest directo).\n170: \n171: ---\n172: # 2026-05-20 - WP-2026-113 Central motor release consolidation\n173: \n174: ### Changed\n175: - `PROJECT.md`: Ciclo activo actualizado a WP-2026-113 IN_PROGRESS (consolidacion de release).\n176: - `.agent/collaboration/TURN.md`: Turno actualizado a WP-2026-113, rol BUILDER.\n177: - `.agent/collaboration/STATE.md`: Estado canonico alineado con WP-2026-113.\n178: - `.agent/collaboration/execution_log.md`: Registro de ejecucion inicializado para WP-2026-113.\n179: - `.agent/collaboration/review_queue.md`: Limpieza de reviews historicos de WP-2026-107, WP-2026-111, WP-2026-112.\n180: - `.agent/collaboration/notifications.md`: Handoff vivo para WP-2026-113, residuos historicos mantenidos como referencia.\n181: - `.agent/runtime/events/events.jsonl`: Bus canónico con eventos de WP-2026-113, tickets cerrados permanecen como historial.\n182: \n183: ### Summary\n184: - Consolidacion post-WP-2026-111 y WP-2026-112: motor central descrito una unica vez.\n185: - Manifiestos (`MANIFEST.distribute`, `MANIFEST.workspace`) expresan la misma arquitectura sin ambiguedad.\n186: - Instalador (`scripts/install_agent_system.py`) actua como bootstrapper sin copiar motor.\n187: - Documentacion canonica (`README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `PROJECT.md`) cuenta la misma historia.\n188: - Superficies vivas normalizadas: sin mezcla de tickets cerrados con ciclo activo.\n189: - Validacion canonica y tests verdes: 0 errores, 227 tests passing.\n190: \n191: ---\n192: # 2026-05-20 - WP-2026-111 Central motor and destination workspace contract\n193: \n194: ### Changed\n195: - `MANIFEST.distribute`: Reescrito como contrato del motor central. El motor NO se copia; este manifiesto delimita la frontera del codigo operativo del repo fuente.\n196: - `MANIFEST.workspace`: Nota operativa actualizada para reflejar arquitectura de motor central. El destino conserva solo estado/memoria/eventos/config; el motor se referencia, no se copia.\n197: - `scripts/install_agent_system.py`: Documentacion actualizada. El instalador prepara (bootstrap) el destino para consumir el motor externo; NO copia el codigo operativo.\n198: - `AGENTS.md`: Seccion \"MANIFEST.distribute y MANIFEST.workspace (WP-2026-111)\" reescrita. Contrato de version actualizado a \"motor central + workspace destino\".\n199: - `PROJECT.md`: Estado actualizado a WP-2026-111 COMPLETED. Mision cambiada de \"plantilla portable\" a \"motor central\". Seccion de arquitectura reescrita.\n200: - `README.md`: Titulo y descripcion actualizados a \"Central motor\". Seccion \"Central motor architecture (WP-2026-111)\" reemplaza \"Engine portability\". Instrucciones de instalacion actualizadas.\n201: - `CLAUDE.md`: Version de referencia actualizada a v9.14.0.\n202: \n203: ### Summary\n204: - Cambio arquitectonico completo: de \"plantilla portable copiada\" a \"motor central + workspace destino\".\n205: - El motor (codigo operativo) vive una unica vez en `orquestacion_agentes/`.\n206: - Cada proyecto destino conserva solo su `.agent/` de workspace (estado, memoria, eventos, config) y referencia el motor externo.\n207: - El instalador actua como bootstrapper del destino: prepara la estructura de workspace sin copiar el motor.\n208: - Documentacion canonica actualizada para reflejar la nueva arquitectura de forma coherente.\n209: - Cero cambios de comportamiento en el instalador: ya usaba MANIFEST.distribute como allowlist; ahora la narrativa es correcta.\n210: \n211: ---\n212: # 2026-05-20 - WP-2026-106 Structured manager reviews and human gate escalation\n213: \n214: ### Added\n215: - `bus/review_bridge.py._validate_changes_structure()`: Validates CHANGES responses have SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS, DECISION: CHANGES sections.\n216: - `bus/review_bridge.py._parse_changes_structure()`: Extracts structured sections from CHANGES stdout.\n217: - `bus/review_bridge.py._persist_review_attempt()`: Persists each review attempt to `.agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/attempt-N.md` idempotently.\n218: - `bus/review_bridge.py._generate_human_review_report()`: Generates `human_review_report.md` from template at 5th consecutive CHANGES.\n219: - `bus/review_bridge.py._emit_review_attempt()`: Emits MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT with `review_log_path` and `stdout_tail` (lightweight bus).\n220: - `tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py`: TestStructuredChangesValidation (5 tests), TestReviewAttemptPersistence (4 tests), TestHumanGateEscalation (5 tests).\n221: - `tests/unit/test_review_budget_retry.py`: Tests for persistence, validation, and HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n222: \n223: ### Changed\n224: - `bus/review_bridge.py._load_review_config()`: max_attempts elevated from 2 to 5 (WP-2026-106 threshold).\n225: - `bus/review_bridge.py.run_manager_review_cycle()`: Rewritten to support structured CHANGES validation, per-attempt persistence, and HUMAN_GATE escalation at 5 consecutive CHANGES.\n226: - `.agent/config/agents.json`: `manager_review.max_attempts` updated to 5.\n227: \n228: ### Summary\n229: - CHANGES decisions now require structured sections (SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS).\n230: - Each review attempt persisted idempotently to `attempt-N.md` for full auditability.\n231: - Bus remains lightweight with only `review_log_path` and `stdout_tail` in events.\n232: - 5 consecutive CHANGES decisions trigger HUMAN_GATE escalation with auto-generated `human_review_report.md`.\n233: - All quality gates pass: ruff clean, 176 tests passing, pip-audit clean.\n234: \n235: ---\n236: # 2026-05-20 - WP-2026-105 Bus precedence bootstrap hardening\n237: \n238: ### Added\n239: - `bus/supervisor.py._bus_active_non_terminal_ticket()`: New method that scans all tickets in the event bus and returns the first one in a non-terminal state (READY_FOR_REVIEW, READY_TO_CLOSE, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, HUMAN_GATE).\n240: - `tests/test_supervisor.py`: 6 new tests covering:\n241:   - `test_bus_active_non_terminal_ticket_finds_active_ticket`: Finds tickets in non-terminal states.\n242:   - `test_bus_active_non_terminal_ticket_ignores_completed`: Skips completed tickets.\n243:   - `test_bus_active_non_terminal_ticket_prefers_first_active`: Returns first active ticket.\n244:   - `test_bootstrap_bus_precedence_over_turn_divergence`: Core regression test (bus wins over TURN.md).\n245:   - `test_bootstrap_bus_requeue_repeated_changes`: Verifies repeated requeue works.\n246:   - `test_bootstrap_fallback_to_turn_when_bus_has_no_active`: Fallback to TURN.md when bus has no active.\n247: \n248: ### Changed\n249: - `bus/supervisor.py.bootstrap()`: Modified to prioritize bus active non-terminal ticket over TURN.md and work_plan.md. Precedence chain: bus (non-terminal) -> TURN.md -> work_plan.md -> state.\n250: - `bus/supervisor.py.bootstrap()` docstring: Updated to reflect bus-first precedence.\n251: \n252: ### Summary\n253: - Resolves divergence scenario where TURN.md points to old ticket but bus has active non-terminal ticket.\n254: - Bus is now the authoritative source for active ticket during bootstrap.\n255: - TURN.md and work_plan.md remain as fallbacks when bus has no active non-terminal ticket.\n256: - Repeated requeue cycle (REVIEW_DECISION -> changes -> IN_PROGRESS) preserved.\n257: - Zero regressions: 157 tests passing, ruff clean.\n258: \n259: ---\n260: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-101 P0 cleanup: duplicate scripts and orphaned artifacts\n261: \n262: ### Removed\n263: - `scripts/rollback_agent_system.py`: Exact duplicate of canonical `scripts/rollback.py`\n264: - Bundle surface reduced: root directory now clean of orphaned Python scripts\n289: \n290: ---\n291: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-100 Portable bundle active-only collaboration archive\n292: \n293: ### Added\n294: - `scripts/archive_collaboration_artifacts.py`: Stdlib-only helper that moves closed `PLAN_WP-*.md` and `AUDIT_WP-*.md` files from `.agent/collaboration/` to `.agent/collaboration/_archive/plan_audit/`.\n295:   - Reads active WP ID from `work_plan.md` (regex supports both `- **ID:**` and `**ID:**` formats)\n296:   - Idempotent: re-running archives zero files after first pass\n297:   - Dry-run mode (`--dry-run`) reports without modifying disk\n298:   - List active mode (`--list-active`) shows remaining collaboration files\n299:   - Archive directory created on-demand (`parents=True, exist_ok=True`)\n300: - `tests/unit/test_archive_collaboration_artifacts.py`: 9 unit tests covering:\n301:   - `test_parse_wp_number`: WP number extraction from filenames\n302:   - `test_get_active_wp`: Reading active WP from work_plan.md\n303:   - `test_find_closed_plan_audit_files`: Finding closed PLAN/AUDIT files\n304:   - `test_archive_closed_files`: Archiving closed files to archive dir\n305:   - `test_idempotent_second_run_archives_zero`: Second run archives nothing\n306:   - `test_dry_run_does_not_modify_disk`: Dry-run reports without moving\n307:   - `test_no_closed_files_no_op`: No files touched when all active\n308:   - `test_list_active_collaboration_files`: Listing active collaboration files\n309:   - `test_archive_dir_creation`: Archive directory created when needed\n310: - `.agent/collaboration/_archive/plan_audit/`: Internal archive directory for closed PLAN/AUDIT artifacts (77 files archived on first run)\n311: \n312: ### Changed\n313: - `scripts/archive_collaboration_artifacts.py.get_active_wp()`: Fixed regex to support both `- **ID:** WP-YYYY-NNN` and `**ID:** WP-YYYY-NNN` formats (discovered during implementation)\n314: \n315: ### Summary\n316: - Keeps portable bundle copyable without dragging old ticket forensics\n317: - Active collaboration surface reduced to ~10 files (work_plan.md, TURN.md, STATE.md, execution_log.md, current PLAN/AUDIT pair)\n318: - 77 closed PLAN/AUDIT files archived to `.agent/collaboration/_archive/plan_audit/`\n319: - Idempotent operation verified: second run archives zero files\n320: - Zero dependencies added (stdlib only: pathlib, shutil, re, argparse)\n321: \n322: ---\n323: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-098 Bridge prompt transport via --file for Windows argv limit\n324: \n325: ### Added\n326: - `bus/review_bridge.py.ARGV_PROMPT_THRESHOLD = 8000`: Module-level constant for Windows CreateProcess argv limit (~8191 chars) with safety margin.\n327: - Dual transport dispatch in `bus/review_bridge.py._run_opencode_review()`:\n328:   - Short prompts (<8000 chars): passed directly in argv (unchanged behavior)\n329:   - Long prompts (>=8000 chars): written to `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)` + `--file <path>` flag\n330: - Tempfile cleanup in `finally` block with `os.unlink()` best-effort (catches `OSError` for Windows file-lock transient failures)\n331: - `tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py.TestPromptTransportDispatch`: 4 new tests:\n332:   - `test_short_prompt_uses_argv_path`: verifies argv transport for short prompts\n333:   - `test_long_prompt_uses_file_path`: verifies --file transport for long prompts\n334:   - `test_tempfile_cleaned_up_after_call`: verifies cleanup after successful call\n335:   - `test_tempfile_cleaned_up_on_subprocess_failure`: verifies cleanup on TimeoutExpired\n336: \n337: ### Changed\n338: - `bus/review_bridge.py._run_opencode_review()`: Refactored with try/finally for tempfile lifecycle management. Removed cmd_string length check (no longer needed with --file path).\n339: \n340: ### Summary\n341: - Resolves the Windows command-line length bottleneck captured by WP-095 forensic events in WP-097 closeout (stderr: \"La línea de comandos es demasiado larga (19268 chars)\").\n342: - Self-dogfood: this WP has small diff -> prompt <8000 -> exercises argv path. Tests artificially exercise the --file path.\n343: - Zero dependencies added (stdlib `tempfile` only).\n344: - Backward compatible: short prompts use identical argv path as before.\n345: \n346: ---\n347: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-097 Defense against history truncation + archive integration + bridge V2 smoke\n348: \n349: ### Added\n350: - `scripts/check_no_history_truncation.py`: Stdlib-only guard script that detects dangerous truncation of `execution_log.md`.\n351:   - Threshold: >50 lines removed without archive compensation\n352:   - Checks for compensating archive files in `.agent/runtime/events/archive/`\n353:   - Provides actionable error message with archive command instructions\n354:   - Exit code 1 on dangerous truncation, 0 otherwise\n355: - `tests/unit/test_no_history_truncation.py`: 15 unit tests covering:\n356:   - Truncation without archive compensation (fail case)\n357:   - Truncation with archive compensation (pass case)\n358:   - Changes below 50-line threshold (pass case)\n359:   - Add-only changes (pass case)\n360:   - Boundary cases (exactly 50 vs 51 lines)\n361:   - Archive file detection (added/renamed)\n362:   - Main function integration tests\n363: - `.pre-commit-config.yaml`: Local hook `check-history-truncation` registered for commit stage.\n364: - `skills/project-finalize/SKILL.md`: Step 9e added suggesting `archive_execution_log.py` when log exceeds ~10 WP entries.\n365: \n366: ### Changed\n367: - None (stdlib-only implementation, no existing code modified).\n368: \n369: ### Summary\n370: - Protects `execution_log.md` from accidental/silent truncation without archival.\n371: - Conservative threshold (50 lines) avoids false positives on normal edits.\n372: - Archive integration is manual/suggested only (no auto-invocation).\n373: - Bridge V2 smoke: this ticket's own review validates the manager review cycle.\n374: - Zero dependencies added, fully backward compatible.\n375: \n376: ---\n377: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-096 Caveman-style canonical doc compression helper\n378: \n379: ### Added\n380: - `scripts/compress_canonical.py`: Stdlib-only helper inspired by JuliusBrussee/caveman (MIT) for compressing canonical markdown files.\n381:   - `--dry-run`: Preview changes without modifying files\n382:   - `--backup`: Create `.original.md` backup before overwriting\n383:   - `--restore`: Restore files from `.original.md` backups\n384:   - Preservation of code fences, inline code, URLs, paths, headers, tables, frontmatter\n385:   - Idempotent: `compress(compress(x)) == compress(x)`\n386: - `tests/unit/test_compress_canonical.py`: 25+ tests covering preservation, compression, idempotency, backup/restore, dry-run, and edge cases.\n387: \n388: ### Changed\n389: - `CREDITS.md`: Added MIT attribution row for JuliusBrussee/caveman (Inspiration, no code copied).\n390: \n391: ### Summary\n392: - Implements V1 of caveman-style compression for canonical docs using Python stdlib only.\n393: - Zero dependencies added.\n394: - Conservative compression: only removes clearly redundant filler phrases and excessive whitespace.\n395: - Full technical content preservation via placeholder-based marking/restoration.\n396: \n397: ---\n398: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-095 Manager review V2 (deterministic single-shot, context budget & retries)\n399: \n400: ### Added\n401: - Pluggable Manager Rubrics: `_rubric_for_type()` outputs custom evaluation prompts based on `deliverable_type` (`code`, `mixed`, or non-code/documentation/research).\n402: - Context Prompt Builder: `_build_review_prompt()` formats the single-shot prompt including core canonical files (`work_plan.md`, `STATE.md`, `TURN.md`), the relevant `execution_log.md` section, optional plan/audit files, and git diff.\n403: - Semantic Truncation & Budget: Enforces a strict 80KB cap. Drops/minimizes less critical context first, falls back to `git diff --stat` if canonical files exceed 60KB.\n404: - Configurable Retry Loop: `\"manager_review\"` settings in `.agent/config/agents.json` define timeout limits (default 180s), maximum retries (default 2), and backoff multipliers (default 2.0).\n405: - Exponential Technical Backoff: Retries reviews only on technical timeouts (`TimeoutExpired`), applying exponential backoff. Immediately stops and yields `INSPECT` on structural or semantic errors.\n406: - Event Emitter & Forensics: Emits `MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT` to the event bus containing attempt number, exit code, duration, and output tails for deep inspection.\n407: - Dual Parser: `_parse_opencode_json_decision()` reads JSON event streams when `--format json` is supported, falling back to regex.\n408: - `tests/unit/test_review_budget_retry.py`: 9 new tests covering the retry loop, backoff multipliers, json parsing, budget truncation, and forensic event emission.\n409: \n410: ### Changed\n411: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`: Registered `tests/test_manager_review_bridge.py` and `tests/unit/test_review_budget_retry.py` inside `DEFAULT_PYTEST_ARGS`.\n412: - `PROJECT.md`: Upgraded system version to `v9.14.0`.\n413: - `.agent/.version_manifest.json`: Upgraded agent core version to `v9.14.0`.\n414: - `pyproject.toml`: Upgraded package version to `9.14.0`.\n415: \n416: ---\n417: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-094 Host setup hook for post-install bootstrap\n418: \n419: ### Added\n420: - `scripts/install_agent_system._detect_host_setup()`: Detects `.agent/host-setup.{sh,ps1}` in destination project.\n421: - `scripts/install_agent_system._maybe_invoke_host_setup()`: Shows first 20 lines, prompts for confirmation (unless `--yes`), executes hook, propagates exit code.\n422: - `.agent/host-setup.sh.example`: Bash template with documented contract.\n423: - `.agent/host-setup.ps1.example`: PowerShell template with documented contract.\n424: - `--yes` flag to `install_agent_system.py`: Skip interactive confirmation (CI mode).\n425: - `tests/unit/test_install_agent_system.py`: 8 new tests covering detection, user decline, failure propagation, dry-run, and auto_yes.\n426: \n427: ### Changed\n428: - `scripts/install_agent_system.install_agent_system()`: Invokes host setup hook post-copy (after profile flip).\n429: - `scripts/install_agent_system.sync_agent_system()`: Invokes host setup hook post-copy (after profile flip).\n430: - `AGENTS.md`: Added \"Host setup hook (WP-2026-094)\" section.\n431: - `CREDITS.md`: Added MIT attribution row for OpenHands pattern.\n432: \n433: ### Summary\n434: - Adapts OpenHands `.openhands/setup.sh` pattern (MIT) to our layout.\n435: - Interactive confirmation by default (security boundary: no script execution without human OK).\n436: - Exit code propagation (no masking): failed hook aborts install.\n437: - Backward-compatible: absence of hook is valid (silent no-op).\n438: - Templates use `.example` extension: user renames to activate.\n439: \n440: ---\n441: # 2026-05-19 - Post-audit fixups (session closeout)\n442: \n443: ### Fixed\n444: - `skills/bui-run-quality-gates/SKILL.md`: Corrected dispatch table description — pip-audit is invoked directly by the dispatcher, not via pre-commit hooks.\n445: - `tests/unit/test_pip_audit_policy.py`: Renamed `test_should_run_pip_audit_with_uv_lock` → `test_should_run_pip_audit_with_requirements_file` (test was covering `requirements-dev.txt`, not `uv.lock`). Added a new, dedicated `test_should_run_pip_audit_with_uv_lock` that correctly tests `uv.lock`.\n446: - `scripts/check_ruff_hook_scope.py`: Added `_normalize_types_val()` helper so the scope guard correctly detects `types:` in multi-line YAML list form (e.g. `types:\\n  - python`), not only in inline form.\n447: - `tests/unit/test_check_ruff_hook_scope.py`: Added 2 new tests — `test_valid_config_multiline_types_passes` and `test_multiline_types_with_markdown_fails` — to lock in the multi-line detection.\n448: \n449: ### Result\n450: - **68/68 tests pass** (`+3` vs WP-093 closeout baseline of 65).\n451: - `ruff check .` → All checks passed.\n452: - `--validate` → 0 errors, 0 warnings.\n453: \n454: ---\n455: # 2026-05-19 - WP-2026-093 Pre-commit Ruff scope guard for Markdown-safe hooks\n456: \n457: ### Added\n458: - `scripts/check_ruff_hook_scope.py`: Zero-dependency, stdlib-only scope verification script enforcing that `ruff-check` and `ruff-format` remain strictly Python-only.\n459: - `tests/unit/test_check_ruff_hook_scope.py`: 4 unit tests covering valid configs, missing files, degraded configurations, and markdown/ambiguous configurations.\n460: \n461: ### Changed\n462: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`: Registered `test_check_ruff_hook_scope.py` in default suite.\n463: - `.pre-commit-config.yaml`: Added protective comments alerting future authors of the scope check.\n464: - `AGENTS.md` and `PROJECT.md`: Documented the new scope guard mechanism to prevent documentation-only tickets from inheriting cosmetic linter checks.\n465: \n466: ### Summary\n467: - Completely shields non-Python (e.g. Markdown) deliverables from inheriting unnecessary cosmetic linter checks.\n468: - Guarantees automated failure at check-time if the `.pre-commit-config.yaml` is modified to widen ruff scope.\n469: \n470: ---\n471: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-092 Conditional pip-audit by dependency surface\n472: \n473: ### Added\n474: - `scripts/pip_audit_policy.py`: New policy module deciding whether `pip-audit` should run based on the active work plan's `Files Likely Touched`.\n475: - `tests/unit/test_pip_audit_policy.py`: 5 unit tests validating the dependency surface evaluation and conservative fallback mechanisms.\n476: \n477: ### Changed\n478: - `scripts/run_gates_dispatch.py`: Integrated `pip-audit` execution into `run_code_gates` natively, along with `ruff check .`, executing it only when `should_run_pip_audit()` confirms dependency manifest modifications.\n479: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`: Registered `test_pip_audit_policy.py` in default suite.\n480: - `AGENTS.md` and `PROJECT.md`: Documented the new dependency-surface policy implementation to resolve WP-087 Gap #4.\n481: \n482: ### Summary\n483: - Prevents expensive `pip-audit` executions on code tickets when `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, or locks remain untouched.\n484: - Retains conservative fallback: if missing information, `pip-audit` executes to prioritize security over latency.\n485: \n486: ---\n487: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-091 Pluggable manager review rubric by deliverable_type\n488: \n489: ### Added\n490: - `tests/unit/test_review_strategy_selection.py`: 3 unit tests verifying deliverable_type-based prompt selection and fallback.\n491: \n492: ### Changed\n493: - `bus/review_bridge.py`: Updated `ReviewBridge` to read the active plan's `deliverable_type` and construct tailored prompts for OpenCode manager review (code, mixed, and non-code strategies).\n494: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`: Registered the new `test_review_strategy_selection.py` unit tests in `DEFAULT_PYTEST_ARGS`.\n495: - `AGENTS.md`: Documented the new pluggable review rubric contract.\n496: - `PROJECT.md`: Recorded WP-2026-091 completion.\n497: \n498: ### Summary\n499: - Resolves WP-087 Gap #3 BLOCKER.\n500: - Avoids unified, blind review prompts by dynamically building instructions based on `deliverable_type`:\n501:   - `code` strategy: checks correctness, tests, and styles.\n502:   - `mixed` strategy: combines code checks with thorough structure checks for declared deliverables.\n503:   - `documentation` / `research` / `analysis` strategy: centers strictly on clarity, depth, quality, and structure of requested documents.\n504: - Fallback preserves full backward-compatibility with historical or un-typed tickets.\n505: - Output contract (`APPROVE` / `CHANGES`) remains unchanged for system interoperability.\n506: \n507: ---\n508: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-090 Host-first skill precedence and config profiles\n509: \n510: ### Added\n511: - `tests/unit/test_skill_discovery.py`: 2 new unit tests verifying host-first precedence override and bundle fallback.\n512: - `tests/unit/test_install_agent_system.py`: 1 new unit test verifying profile flip logic at destination.\n513: - `tests/test_check_skill_collisions.py`: added coverage for collisions across bundle and host skill roots.\n514: - `.agent/config/agents.json`: Added `\"active_profile\": \"engine-dev\"` to define the default active profile.\n515: \n516: ### Changed\n517: - `scripts/discover_skills.py`: Updated `discover_skills()` to discover and merge skills from both host directory (`<destino>/.agent/skills/`) and bundle directory (`skills/`), enforcing host precedence.\n518: - `scripts/check_skill_collisions.py`: Expanded collision scanning to include both bundle and host skill roots.\n519: - `scripts/install_agent_system.py`: Added `flip_profile_in_destination()` to automatically flip `\"active_profile\"` from `\"engine-dev\"` to `\"host-project\"` during `--install` or `--sync`.\n520: - `AGENTS.md`: Documented host-first skill precedence and config profiles.\n521: \n522: ### Summary\n523: - Allows the engine bundle to be highly reusable and agnostic across multiple host destination projects.\n524: - Host skills override homonymous bundle skills deterministically.\n525: - Installs with automatically flipped configuration profiles (`engine-dev` -> `host-project`).\n526: - Zero dependencies added, fully backward compatible.\n527: \n528: ---\n529: # 2026-05-18 - `scripts/check_deliverables_exist.py`: Validates that all declared files in `work_plan.md` actually exist on disk.\n534: - `tests/unit/test_run_gates_dispatch.py`: 4 unit tests for deliverable_type reading and fallback.\n535: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`: Added new unit test files to `DEFAULT_PYTEST_ARGS`.\n541: \n542: ### Summary\n543: - Resolves WP-087 Gap #2 WORKAROUND.\n544: - Dispatches conditional gates: `code` / fallback runs code gates (ruff, pytest, pip-audit); `documentation` / `research` / `analysis` runs deliverables check; `mixed` runs both.\n545: - Stdlib only, fully backward-compatible.\n546: \n547: ---\n548: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-088 Add deliverable_type field to work_plan schema\n549: \n550: ### Added\n551: - `.agent/agent_controller.py._check_deliverable_type()`: Validator function that checks for `deliverable_type` field in work_plan.md.\n552: - `skills/man-create-work-plan/SKILL.md`: Added `deliverable_type` to workflow example.\n555: - `tests/unit/test_work_plan_schema.py`: 7 tests covering valid, missing, unknown, compound, case-insensitive, and extra-spaces scenarios.\n556: - `AGENTS.md`: Documentation section \"deliverable_type (work_plan schema, V1)\".\n557: \n558: ### Changed\n559: - `.agent/agent_controller.py._handle_validate()`: Now calls `_check_deliverable_type()` and adds warnings to output.\n560: \n561: ### Summary\n562: - V1 informational: validator emits warning if field missing, unknown value, or compound syntax (e.g., 'code+documentation').\n563: - No dispatch yet (WP-089 will implement conditional gates based on deliverable_type).\n564: - Resolves Gap #1 BLOCKER from WP-2026-087 gap analysis.\n565: - Values: `code | documentation | research | analysis | mixed`.\n566: - Backward compatible: historical work_plans without the field get warning, not error.\n567: \n568: ---\n569: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-085 Config Migration Framework for agents.json\n570: \n571: ### Added\n572: \n573: - `.agent/agents_config.py`: Migration framework idempotente para `agents.json`:\n574:   - `Migration` dataclass: describe transición de schema (id, from_version, to_version, apply).\n575:   - `MigrationReport` dataclass: reporte de ejecución (applied, skipped, backups).\n576:   - `_migrate_1_0_to_1_1()`: handler puro que backfills `role_models` con defaults WP-072.\n577:   - `MIGRATIONS` registry: lista ordenada cronológicamente de migraciones.\n578:   - `migrate_agents_config()`: pipeline 4-paso con backup timestamped + idempotencia.\n579:   - Legacy backfill: config sin `_migrations` con schema actual → poblar retroactivamente sin re-ejecutar.\n580: - CLI `--migrate` / `--dry-run` en `agents_config.py`.\n581: - `tests/unit/test_agents_config.py::TestMigrationFramework`: 7 tests (idempotencia, backup, _migrations update, legacy backfill, dry-run, handler purity, migración 1.0→1.1).\n582: \n583: ### Changed\n584: \n585: - `.agent/config/agents.json`: Añadido `_migrations: [\"1.0_to_1.1\"]` para reflejar estado real.\n586: - `AGENTS.md`: Añadido comando `Migrar config: python .agent/agents_config.py --migrate [--dry-run]`.\n587: \n588: ### Summary\n589: \n590: - Framework V1 scope acotado: solo `agents.json` + `agents_config.py` + tests.\n591: - Idempotencia obligatoria: segunda `--migrate` consecutiva = no-op (sin backup).\n592: - Backup convention: `agents.json.bak.<ISO-timestamp>` antes de cada migración.\n593: - Stdlib only: sin dependencias nuevas (`semver`, `migrate`, etc. prohibidos).\n594: - **Origen externo**: Oportunidad #1 de `code-yeongyu/oh-my-openagent` - ver `.agent/runtime/compare/code-yeongyu-oh-my-openagent-*.md`.\n595: \n596: ---\n597: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-084 Robust Builder Relaunch (supervisor liveness + launcher resume mode)\n598: \n599: ### Added\n600: - `bus/supervisor.py._builder_alive()`: Método helper que verifica liveness del Builder via PID + `tasklist` (Windows) con fallback a mtime <15 min.\n601: - `bus/supervisor.py._relaunch_builder()`: Ahora captura stdout/stderr del launcher (Capa 1), verifica liveness antes de relanzar (Capa 2), y usa flag `-ResumeBuilder` (Capa 4).\n602: - `scripts/launch_agent_terminals.ps1 -ResumeBuilder`: Flag aditivo que skip cleanup agresivo (`Stop-ProjectAgentProcesses`, `Remove-StaleRuntimeArtifacts`, `Assert-StartupAlignment`) cuando viene de requeue del supervisor.\n603: - `tests/test_supervisor.py`: 6 tests nuevos (`test_builder_alive_pid_exists`, `test_builder_alive_pid_dead`, `test_builder_alive_no_lock`, `test_builder_alive_fallback_mtime`, `test_relaunch_uses_resume_flag`, `test_supervisor_skips_relaunch_when_builder_alive`).\n604: \n605: ### Changed\n606: - `bus/supervisor.py._relaunch_builder()`: Ahora devuelve `bool` (True=success/skipped, False=failure). Integrado en `run_once` para relanzamiento condicional.\n607: - `scripts/launch_agent_terminals.ps1`: Cleanup envuelto en `if (-not $ResumeBuilder)` para preservar comportamiento default en primera apertura.\n608: \n609: ### Summary\n610: - Solución estructural al bug de WP-2026-083: supervisor mataba Builder vivo al relanzar tras CHANGES.\n611: - 4 capas: diagnóstico stdout/stderr + liveness PID-based + flag ResumeBuilder + integración supervisor.\n612: - ADITIVIDAD: launcher sin `-ResumeBuilder` comporta igual que antes (cero regresión).\n613: - NO dependencias nuevas (`psutil` prohibido). Solo `tasklist` nativo Windows + stdlib.\n614: - NO tocar agent_controller / event_bus / state_machine / review_bridge.\n615: \n616: ---\n617: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-083 Memory Consolidate V1 (Dream Cycle, deterministic, no LLM)\n618: \n619: ### Added\n620: - `skills/memory-consolidate/SKILL.md`: Skill con triggers `[/consolidate, /memory, /dream-cycle]`.\n623: \n624: ### Changed\n625: - `skills/project-finalize/SKILL.md`: Añadido Paso 9d (invocación opcional al cierre de sesión).\n626: - `AGENTS.md`: Añadido comando `Memoria consolidada: python scripts/memory_consolidate.py [--apply]`.\n627: \n628: ### Summary\n629: - V1 determinista (sin LLM, sin cron) de la \"dream cycle\" de gbrain.\n630: - ver `.agent/runtime/compare/garrytan-gbrain-*.md`.\n633: \n634: ---\n635: # 2026-05-18 - WP-2026-082 Skill repo-compare (GitHub MCP + AUDIT.md as Phase 0)\n636: \n637: ### Added\n638: - `skills/repo-compare/SKILL.md`: Skill pura (triggers: `/repo-compare`, `/compare`, `/gh-compare`) para comparar proyecto local con repositorios GitHub.\n639: - `prompts/session_bootstrap.md`:\n713:   - Eliminada referencia a `~/.claude/projects/*/memory/MEMORY.md` (ruta absoluta externa).\n714:   - Reemplazada por `.agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md` y `observations.jsonl` (rutas relativas portables).\n715:   - **Portabilidad**: Cero referencias absolutas a `c:\\Users\\fdl` o `z_scripts/` en el repo portable (excepto `observations.jsonl` histórico).\n721: - **Drift de versiones**: Todas las menciones de versión ahora coinciden con `pyproject.toml` (v9.9.0) y `.version_manifest.json` (v9.9.0).\n722: - **Duplicidades consolidadas**: Comandos, política de seguridad y flujo Manager→Builder ahora tienen fuente única con pointers explícitos.\n723: \n724: ### Metrics\n725: \n726: | Archivo | Líneas antes | Líneas después | Reducción |\n727: |---------|--------------|----------------|-----------|\n728: | `CLAUDE.md` | 32 | 32 | 0 |\n729: | `README.md` | 63 | 58 | -5 |\n730: | `.claude/rules/00-startup.md` | 22 | 25 | +3 (pointers agregados) |\n731: | `prompts/session_bootstrap.md` | 80 | 80 | 0 |\n732: | **Scope total** | **1828** | **1826** | **-2** |\n733: \n734: > Nota: La reducción real es semántica (pointers en lugar de contenido duplicado), no bruta. Los archivos de entrada ahora son más scannables y la onboarding de cada backend (Claude, Codex/OpenCode, Goose) sigue intacta en ≤2 saltos.\n735: \n736: ### Verified\n737: \n738: - `ruff check .` → All checks passed\n739: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py` → 19 passed\n740: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force` → 0 errors, 0 warnings\n741: - Walk-through mental por backend: Claude (CLAUDE.md), Codex/OpenCode (AGENTS.md), Manager (.opencode/agents/manager.md) — todos siguen siendo onboardables sin saltar fuera del scope inicial.\n742: \n743: ---\n744: \n745: # 2026-05-17 - HOTFIX WP-2026-075 drift correction (post-review manager)\n746: \n747: Manager review smoke-test (Test B/D del `scripts/test_manager_smoke.ps1`) detectó drift entre `work_plan.md` y el código entregado en WP-2026-075. Tres correcciones aplicadas en frío, sin abrir nuevo ticket.\n748: \n749: ### Fixed\n750: \n751: - `bus/event_bus.py`: el parámetro `max_duplicates` del constructor estaba declarado y asignado a `self.max_duplicates` pero `emit()`, `_record_blocked_emit()` y el log forense seguían usando `self.max_consecutive_duplicates`. Resuelto: ambos nombres son ahora aliases del mismo umbral; `max_duplicates` gana si se pasan los dos. Tests con `max_consecutive_duplicates=...` siguen funcionando.\n752: - `tests/unit/test_bus_integrity.py`: eliminados imports muertos `os`, `tempfile` y `EventRecord` (señalados por el Manager; no detectados por `ruff check .` porque `tests/` está en `extend-exclude` de `pyproject.toml`).\n753: \n754: ### Clarified\n755: \n756: - Path del log forense: el criterio de aceptación de `work_plan.md` línea 107 decía `.agent/runtime/logs/event_bus_blocks.jsonl`. La implementación real es `.agent/runtime/events/logs/event_bus_blocks.jsonl` (subsystem-scoped). La línea 61 del plan permitía explícitamente esta interpretación. Se mantiene la ruta del código (los tests la verifican y la separación por subsistema es coherente); se actualiza la entrada Added del WP-075 más abajo.\n757: \n758: ### Verified\n759: \n760: - Manager review (`scripts/test_manager_smoke.ps1` Tests B y D) ahora alinea código y plan en los tres puntos detectados.\n761: \n762: ---\n763: \n764: # 2026-05-17 - WP-2026-075 Event Bus Observability (De-duplication Tuning & Suppressed Logging)\n765: \n766: ### Added\n767: \n768: - `bus/event_bus.py`: Observability constants for duplicate blocking:\n769:   - `DUPLICATE_WINDOW_SIZE = 20`: Window size for counting recent duplicates.\n770:   - `MAX_DUPLICATES_IN_WINDOW = 3`: Maximum duplicates allowed in window before blocking.\n771:   - `STDERR_BLOCK_LIMIT = 5`: Maximum stderr warnings per session before suppression.\n772: - `bus/event_bus.py`: Constructor parameters for tuning:\n773:   - `window_size`: Configurable de-duplication window size (default: 20).\n774:   - `max_duplicates`: Configurable maximum duplicates in window (default: 3).\n775: - `bus/event_bus.py`: Session block counter `_session_block_count` for rate-limiting stderr.\n776: - `bus/event_bus.py`: `_block_log_path` property pointing to `<runtime_dir>/logs/event_bus_blocks.jsonl` (i.e. `.agent/runtime/events/logs/event_bus_blocks.jsonl` in production). Path is subsystem-scoped: logs live alongside the events.jsonl they originate from. The work_plan.md line 61 left this open as \"o `logs/event_bus_blocks.jsonl` según la estructura\" and this resolution was chosen.\n777: - `bus/event_bus.py`: `_record_blocked_emit()` method:\n778:   - Increments session block counter.\n779:   - Writes structured JSONL entry with `timestamp`, `event_type`, `ticket_id`, `actor`, `duplicate_count`, `window_size`, `threshold`, `session_block_number`.\n780:   - Prints to stderr if under `STDERR_BLOCK_LIMIT`.\n781:   - Prints suppression warning when limit exceeded.\n782: - `bus/event_bus.py`: `emit()` now calls `_record_blocked_emit()` when blocking duplicates.\n783: - `tests/unit/test_bus_integrity.py`: `TestBlockedEmitObservability` class with 4 tests:\n784:   - `test_emit_logs_blocked_duplicates_to_file`: Verifies log file creation and entry structure.\n785:   - `test_emit_blocks_multiple_duplicates_all_logged`: Verifies all blocks are logged.\n786:   - `test_stderr_rate_limiting`: Verifies stderr output is rate-limited to 5 warnings + 1 suppression message.\n787:   - `test_block_log_uses_append_mode`: Verifies append mode prevents overwriting.\n788: - `import sys`: Added to `bus/event_bus.py` for stderr output.\n789: \n790: ### Changed\n791: \n792: - `bus/event_bus.py`: `_count_recent_duplicates()` now uses `self.window_size` instead of hardcoded parameter.\n793: - `bus/event_bus.py`: `emit()` returns `None` and logs blocked duplicate instead of silently dropping.\n794: - `PROJECT.md`: Updated version to `v9.9.0` and current cycle to `WP-2026-075`.\n795: \n796: ### Verified\n797: \n798: - `ruff check .`: PASSED\n799: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`: 19 passed (base suite)\n800: - `pytest tests/unit/test_bus_integrity.py -v`: 18 passed (14 existing + 4 new observability tests)\n801: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`: PASSED (0 errors, 0 warnings)\n802: \n803: ### Decision\n804: \n805: - Observability resolves the 3 caveats from WP-2026-074 design: arbitrary window tuning, confusing nomenclature, and silent blocking.\n806: - Forensic logging enables empirical tuning of `DUPLICATE_WINDOW_SIZE` and `MAX_DUPLICATES_IN_WINDOW` based on real blocking patterns.\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-806. Use offset=807 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# 2026-05-27 - WP-2026-153 Add grill-with-docs skill\n\n### Added\n- `skills/grill-work-plan/SKILL.md`: Pre-plan interrogation skill with triggers `/grill-plan`, `/grill`, `grill-wp`.\n  - One-question-at-a-time flow with recommended answer before user response\n  - Default context: `PROJECT.md` and `MEMORY.md`; `CONTEXT.md` optional at repo root\n  - Codebase-first answers (grep/search before speculation)\n  - ADR criteria from mattpocock (hard to revert, surprising without context, real trade-off)\n  - Exact completion handshake: `> ✅ Grill completo. Términos resueltos: N. Puedes crear el WP con /plan.`\n- `skills/README.md`: Registered new skill in catalog table and index.\n\n### Changed\n- `README.md`: Updated skills count (19 → 20), current state, and changelog table.\n- `PROJECT.md`: Updated state to WP-2026-153 COMPLETED.\n\n### Summary\n- Pre-plan grilling reduces fuzzy scopes and avoidable review loops.\n- Skill is documentation-only (no Python runtime, no dependencies).\n- Optional integration with `man-create-work-plan` (opt-in, not blocking).\n","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":"CHANGELOG.md","time":{"start":1779874617804,"end":1779874617864}},"id":"prt_e68cb56c3001GrMRXt909r0Yb0","sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","messageID":"msg_e68cb203a0019gZOsJKSt1v3JN"}}

Mapa del ciclo\n6: \n7: ```text\n8: setup -> plan -> implement -> review -> quality -> close -> memory\n9:    ^                                                        |\n10:    +---------------------- meta / support -------------------+\n11: ```\n12: \n13: Flujo minimo de mejora continua:\n14: - el Builder implementa desde el plan aprobado\n15: - el Manager revisa y deja observaciones o bloqueos\n16: - `session-close-observations` convierte aprendizajes en memoria\n17: - `review_bridge` inyecta memoria curada en revisiones futuras\n18: - `bui-implement-from-plan/references/code-rules.md` y `man-review-implementation` reflejan las reglas activas\n19: - `_shared/anti-patterns.md` mantiene el inventario canonicamente numerado AP-01..AP-07\n20: \n21: ## 2. `false` | Generar observaciones curadas al final de cada sesion para memoria auto-mejorable |\n41: | `memory-consolidate` | `shared` | `memory` | `true` | `false` | Dedupe, filter y archive de `observations.jsonl` |\n42: | `create-agent-skill` | `shared` | `meta` | `false` | `false` | Meta-skill para crear nuevas micro-skills siguiendo el estandar Agent Skills |\n43: | `graphify` | `shared` | `support` | `false` | `false` | Construir grafo de conocimiento persistente del codebase para exploracion eficiente |\n44: | `local-audit` | `shared` | `support` | `false` | `false` | Generar un snapshot rapido y estructurado del estado del repositorio |\n45: | `repo-compare` | `shared` | `support` | `false` | `false` | Comparar proyecto local con repositorio GitHub para detectar funcionalidades de valor |\n46: | `secure-existing-project` | `shared` | `support` | `false` | `false` | Aplicar arquitectura de seguridad privada/publica a proyecto Python existente |\n47: | `scaffold-python-project` | `shared` | `setup` | `false` | `false` | Crear estructura completa de proyecto Python nuevo con seguridad integrada |\n48: \n49: ## 3. Bucle de mejora continua\n50: \n51: ```text\n52: bug / finding humano\n53:   -> observations.jsonl\n54:   -> session-close-observations\n55:   -> review_bridge\n56:   -> Manager review prompt\n57:   -> nueva deteccion / nuevo aprendizaje\n58: ```\n59: \n60: Fuentes y destinos:\n61: - `observations.jsonl` guarda aprendizajes persistentes\n62: - `session-close-observations` consolida aprendizajes al cerrar sesion\n63: - `review_bridge` inyecta memoria curada en el prompt del Manager\n64: - `code-rules.md` del Builder recoge reglas preventivas\n65: - `man-review-implementation` usa el inventario AP-01..AP-07 como checklist bloqueante\n66: - `skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md` es la referencia compartida para Builder y Manager\n67: \n68: ## 4. Indice compacto\n69: \n70: ### Manager\n71: - `man-create-work-plan` - plan\n72: - `man-review-implementation` - review\n73: - `man-resolve-escalation` - review\n74: \n75: ### Builder\n76: - `bui-implement-from-plan` - implement\n77: - `bui-write-deliverable` - implement\n78: - `bui-run-quality-gates` - quality\n79: - `bui-self-audit` - review\n80: \n81: ### Compartidas\n82: - `test-driven-development` - implement\n83: - `systematic-debugging` - implement\n84: - `code-audit` - review\n85: - `refactor-manager` - review\n86: - `project-finalize` - close\n87: - `version-changelog` - close\n88: - `session-close-observations` - close\n89: - `memory-consolidate` - memory\n90: - `create-agent-skill` - meta\n91: - `graphify` - support\n92: - `local-audit` - support\n93: - `repo-compare` - support\n94: - `secure-existing-project` - support\n95: - `scaffold-python-project` - setup\n96: \n97: ### Usuario\n98: - `grill-work-plan` - plan\n99: - `setup-agent-system` - setup\n100: \n101: ## Validacion\n102: \n103: ```bash\n104: python skills/validate_all.py\n105: ```\n106: \n107: Verifica:\n108: - frontmatter YAML valido\n109: - campos requeridos presentes\n110: - enums validos para `role` y `stage`\n111: - tipos booleanos para `writes_memory` y `quality_gate`\n112: - directorios que empiezan por `_` se tratan como infraestructura compartida y no se validan como skills\n113: - `references/` es recomendado; si falta o solo tiene `.gitkeep`, el validador avisa pero no falla\n114: \n115: ## Convenciones\n116: \n117: - `man-[accion]` - Skills del Manager\n118: - `bui-[accion]` - Skills del Builder\n119: - `[accion]` - Skills compartidas\n120: - `_shared/` - Inventario y referencias compartidas, fuera del discovery de skills\n121: - `SKILL.md` - frontmatter con taxonomia operativa\n122: - `references/` - documentacion de apoyo\n123: \n124: ## Referencias\n125: \n126: - [Sistema Multi-Agente](../EMPEZAR-AQUI.md)\n127: - [Flujo del Manager](../.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md)\n128: - [Flujo del Builder](../.agent/workflows/builder_workflow.md)\n\n(End of file - total 128 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Catalogo de Micro-Skills\n\n> Sistema Multi-Agente v6 - Skills portables para Manager, Builder y soporte compartido\n\n## 1. {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779874617902,"sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_be9da5928a8c4fa989872a83","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\grill-work-plan\\SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\skills\\grill-work-plan\\SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: grill-work-plan\n3: version: 1.0.0\n4: description: Pre-plan interrogation skill that resolves ambiguous terminology before a work plan is created\n5: triggers: [/grill-plan, /grill, grill-wp]\n6: author: agent\n7: role: user\n8: stage: plan\n9: writes_memory: false\n10: quality_gate: false\n11: tags: [core, system]\n12: ---\n13: \n14: # grill-work-plan\n15: \n16: Skill de interrogacion pre-plan para resolver terminologia ambigua antes de crear un work plan.\n17: \n18: ## Overview\n19: \n20: Esta skill actua como un filtro de claridad antes de la planificacion. Su objetivo es:\n21: 1. Identificar terminos ambiguos o fuzzy en el requerimiento del usuario\n22: 2. Resolver cada termino con una pregunta a la vez\n23: 3. Proporcionar una respuesta recomendada antes de esperar la respuesta del usuario\n24: 4. Preferir respuestas derivadas del codebase cuando sea posible\n25: 5. Mantener el contexto de dominio compacto usando `PROJECT.md` y `MEMORY.md` por defecto\n26: 6. `CONTEXT.md` es opcional y solo se crea cuando aporta valor como glosario separado\n27: \n28: ## Workflow\n29: \n30: ### Paso 0: Verificar contexto disponible\n31: \n32: Antes de comenzar el interrogatorio, leer el contexto base:\n33: \n34: ```bash\n35: # Leer contexto base (siempre)\n36: cat PROJECT.md\n37: cat .agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md\n38: cat .agent/runtime/memory/observations.jsonl | tail -50\n39: \n40: # Leer contexto opcional (si existe)\n41: if test -f CONTEXT.md; then cat CONTEXT.md; fi\n42: ```\n43: \n44: **Regla:** `PROJECT.md` y `MEMORY.md` son los inputs de contexto por defecto. `CONTEXT.md` vive en la raiz del repositorio y es opcional.\n45: \n46: ### Paso 1: Identificar terminos ambiguos\n47: \n48: Analizar el requerimiento del usuario buscando:\n49: - Terminos tecnicos sin definir\n50: - Acronimos no explicados\n51: - Conceptos de dominio ambiguos\n52: - Alcances fuzzy (\"mejorar\", \"optimizar\", \"arreglar\")\n53: - Dependencias implícitas no declaradas\n54: \n55: **Prioridad de preguntas:**\n56: 1. Primero: preguntas que el codebase puede responder directamente (buscando en codigo existente)\n57: 2. Despues: preguntas de dominio que requieren clarificacion humana\n58: 3. Ultimo: preguntas de preferencia de implementacion\n59: \n60: ### Paso 2: Interrogatorio uno-a-uno\n61: \n62: **Regla de oro:** Una pregunta a la vez. No bloquear al usuario con multiples preguntas.\n63: \n64: Para cada termino ambiguo:\n65: \n66: ```markdown\n67: ## Pregunta N: [Termino/Concepto]\n68: \n69: **Ambiguedad detectada:** [Por que es ambiguo este termino]\n70: \n71: **Respuesta recomendada:** [Tu recomendacion basada en PROJECT.md, MEMORY.md o el codebase]\n72: \n73: **Justificacion:** [Por que esta recomendacion tiene sentido en este contexto]\n74: \n75: **¿Es correcto? (y/n o corrige):**\n76: ```\n77: \n78: Esperar confirmacion o correccion del usuario antes de continuar.\n79: \n80: ### Paso 3: Buscar respuestas en el codebase\n81: \n82: Cuando un termino pueda resolverse con codigo existente:\n83: \n84: ```bash\n85: # Buscar definiciones, clases, funciones relacionadas\n86: grep -r \"termino\" --include=\"*.py\" src/\n87: find . -name \"*.py\" -exec grep -l \"concepto\" {} \\;\n88: \n89: # Buscar en skills existentes\n90: ls skills/\n91: cat skills/*/SKILL.md | grep -i \"termino\"\n92: ```\n93: \n94: Si el codebase ya define el termino, usar esa definicion como respuesta recomendada.\n95: \n96: ### Paso 4: Gestionar CONTEXT.md (opcional)\n97: \n98: **Regla:** Solo proponer entrada en `CONTEXT.md` cuando:\n99: - El termino NO esta ya definido en `PROJECT.md` o `MEMORY.md`\n100: - El termino es especifico del dominio del proyecto (no conocimiento general)\n101: - Vale la pena conservar la definicion para futuros WPs\n102: \n103: **Formato de propuesta:**\n104: \n105: ```markdown\n106: **¿Quieres que añada este termino a CONTEXT.md?**\n107: \n108: Propuesta de entrada:\n109: ```markdown\n110: ### [Termino]\n111: \n112: [Definicion clara y concisa]\n113: \n114: **Ejemplo:** [Ejemplo concreto si aplica]\n115: **Relacionado con:** [Otros terminos del glosario]\n116: ```\n117: \n118: (y/n): \n119: ```\n120: \n121: Si el usuario acepta y `CONTEXT.md` no existe, crearlo en la raiz del repositorio.\n122: \n123: ### Paso 5: Criterios para ADR (Architecture Decision Record)\n124: \n125: Un ADR esta justificado cuando se cumple AL MENOS UNA de estas condiciones (mattpocock):\n126: \n127: 1. **Hard to revert:** La decision es dificil o costosa de revertir una vez implementada\n128: 2. **Surprising without context:** La decision seria sorprendente o confusa sin documentacion del por-que\n129: 3. **Real trade-off:** Hay compensaciones reales entre opciones (no hay una opcion claramente superior)\n130: \n131: **Ejemplos que justifican ADR:**\n132: - Elegir una base de datos (SQLite vs PostgreSQL)\n133: - Cambiar un patron arquitectonico (MVC vs CQRS)\n134: - Adoptar una libreria critica con lock-in\n135: \n136: **Ejemplos que NO justifican ADR:**\n137: - Nombramiento de variables\n138: - Elecciones de estilo de codigo (ya cubiertas por ruff)\n139: - Decisiones reversibles en minutos\n140: \n141: ### Paso 6: Handshake de completado\n142: \n143: Cuando todos los terminos esten resueltos, emitir EXACTAMENTE:\n144: \n145: ```\n146: > ✅ Grill completo. Términos resueltos: N. Puedes crear el WP con /plan.\n147: ```\n148: \n149: Donde `N` es el numero total de terminos resueltos.\n150: \n151: **Regla:** Esta linea es el contrato de completado. Sin ella, el Manager no debe proceder a crear el work plan.\n152: \n153: ## Ejemplo de flujo completo\n154: \n155: ```\n156: Usuario: \"Quiero añadir un sistema de cache para las consultas a la API\"\n157: \n158: Agente (leyendo contexto):\n159: - PROJECT.md: menciona \"API externa de datos meteorologicos\"\n160: - MEMORY.md: registra \"usar Redis para cache distribuida\" (observacion #3)\n161: \n162: Agente (Pregunta 1):\n163: ## Pregunta 1: Alcance de \"cache\"\n164: \n165: **Ambiguedad detectada:** \"cache\" puede referirse a:\n166: - Cache en memoria (dict, lru_cache)\n167: - Cache en disco (SQLite, archivos)\n168: - Cache distribuida (Redis, Memcached)\n169: \n170: **Respuesta recomendada:** Cache en memoria con lru_cache para MVP\n171: \n172: **Justificacion:** MEMORY.md #3 menciona Redis, pero PROJECT.md indica que es un proyecto local. \n173: lru_cache es suficiente para empezar y es reversible. Redis puede añadirse despues si escala.\n174: \n175: **¿Es correcto? (y/n o corrige):**\n176: \n177: Usuario: \"y\"\n178: \n179: Agente (Pregunta 2):\n180: ## Pregunta 2: \"consultas a la API\"\n181: \n182: **Ambiguedad detectada:** ¿Que API? PROJECT.md menciona multiples fuentes.\n183: \n184: **Respuesta recomendada:** API meteorologica (openweathermap.org)\n185: \n186: **Justificacion:** Es la unica API externa mencionada en PROJECT.md como \"activa\".\n187: \n188: **¿Es correcto? (y/n o corrige):**\n189: \n190: Usuario: \"y, pero quiero que sea generico para otras APIs despues\"\n191: \n192: Agente:\n193: **¿Quieres que añada este termino a CONTEXT.md?**\n194: \n195: Propuesta de entrada:\n196: ```markdown\n197: ### Sistema de Cache\n198: \n199: Capa de cache abstracta que puede aplicarse a multiples fuentes de datos (APIs, BD, archivos).\n200: Implementacion inicial: lru_cache para MVP. Extension futura: patron Strategy para Redis/otros.\n201: \n202: **Relacionado con:** API externa, Sistema de datos\n203: ```\n204: \n205: (y/n): \n206: \n207: Usuario: \"y\"\n208: \n209: Agente:\n210: > ✅ Grill completo. Términos resueltos: 2. Puedes crear el WP con /plan.\n211: ```\n212: \n213: ## Output Format\n214: \n215: Al finalizar el interrogatorio:\n216: \n217: 1. **Handshake de completado (obligatorio):**\n218:    ```\n219:    > ✅ Grill completo. Términos resueltos: N. Puedes crear el WP con /plan.\n220:    ```\n221: \n222: 2. **CONTEXT.md (opcional, si el usuario acepto entradas):**\n223:    ```markdown\n224:    # Contexto de Dominio\n225:    \n226:    ## [Termino 1]\n227:    [Definicion]\n228:    \n229:    ## [Termino 2]\n230:    [Definicion]\n231:    ```\n232: \n233: 3. **ADR (opcional, si se justifico):**\n234:    - Crear en `.agent/decisions/ADR-YYYY-NNN.md`\n235:    - Seguir plantilla de ADRs existentes\n236: \n237: ## Constraints\n238: \n239: - **SIEMPRE** una pregunta a la vez\n240: - **SIEMPRE** proporcionar respuesta recomendada antes de esperar respuesta\n241: - **SIEMPRE** leer `PROJECT.md` y `MEMORY.md` antes de preguntar\n242: - **NO** asumir conocimiento del usuario sin confirmar\n243: - **NO** crear `CONTEXT.md` sin aprobacion explicita\n244: - **NO** proceder a `/plan` sin el handshake de completado\n245: - **PREFERIR** respuestas derivadas del codebase sobre especulacion\n246: \n247: ## References\n248: \n249: - `PROJECT.md` - Contexto base del proyecto (obligatorio)\n250: - `.agent/runtime/memory/MEMORY.md` - Memoria curada (obligatorio)\n251: - `.agent/runtime/memory/observations.jsonl` - Historial completo (opcional, para busqueda profunda)\n252: - `CONTEXT.md` - Glosario de dominio (opcional, solo si aporta valor)\n253: - `.agent/decisions/` - ADRs existentes (para referencia de formato)\n254: \n255: ## Integration opcional con man-create-work-plan\n256: \n257: Esta skill es independiente y NO se integra obligatoriamente en `man-create-work-plan`.\n258: \n259: Si el Manager quiere usarla opcionalmente:\n260: 1. Verificar si hay ambiguedad en el requerimiento\n261: 2. Sugerir: \"¿Quieres que ejecute /grill para clarificar terminos antes de planificar?\"\n262: 3. Si el usuario acepta, ejecutar el flujo de grill\n263: 4. Despues del handshake, proceder con `/plan`\n264: \n265: **Regla:** La integracion debe ser opt-in, nunca bloqueante.\n\n(End of file - total 265 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: grill-work-plan\nversion: 1.0.0\ndescription: Pre-plan interrogation skill that resolves ambiguous terminology before a work plan is created\ntriggers: [/grill-plan, /grill, grill-wp]\nauthor: agent\nrole: user\nstage: plan\nwrites_memory: false\nquality_gate: false\ntags: [core, system]\n---\n\n# grill-work-plan\n\nSkill de interrogacion pre-plan para resolver terminologia ambigua antes de crear un work plan.\n\n## Overview\n\nEsta skill actua como un filtro de claridad antes de la planificacion. Su objetivo es:","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"skills\\grill-work-plan\\SKILL.md","time":{"start":1779874617757,"end":1779874617899}},"id":"prt_e68cb48c1001guHXiaCtS1OgQ7","sessionID":"ses_1973508f3ffeCjihXzSCtvJXIO","messageID":"msg_e68cb203a0019gZOsJKSt1v3JN"}}
