{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779885896354,"sessionID":"ses_196890597ffeHW7Mz3onmKqJM1","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_64870cf1ddfb4f2ea2c8a3e0","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Work Plan - WP-2026-154\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-154\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** code\n7: - **Titulo:** Strictness profiles and live guard_paths activation\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: Convertir `guard_paths.py` de stub en un hook funcional y anadir perfiles de strictness en `agents.json` con migracion compatible, sin romper la configuracion existente.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `guard_paths.py` debe ejecutar la logica real en `__main__` en lugar de devolver siempre `{\"continue\": True}`.\n15: - El contrato del hook es `exit(0)` para allow y `exit(2)` para block; la razon de bloqueo va a `stderr`, no a `stdout`.\n16: - `guard_paths.py` lee `agents.json` directamente con `json.loads` desde `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"`; no debe importar `agents_config.py` para resolver el perfil.\n17: - `agents.json` incorpora `strictness_profile` como selector canonico y un mapa `profiles` con `minimal`, `standard` y `strict`.\n18: - `standard` conserva la proteccion actual; `minimal` reduce el conjunto activo a la superficie sensible minima; `strict` endurece el guard sin bloquear superficies canonicas vivas como `execution_log.md`. El scope gate de `strict` lee `Files Likely Touched` del `work_plan.md` activo; si no hay plan activo (estado COMPLETED o archivo ausente), fail-open aplicando solo los patrones base de `standard`.\n19: - La migracion 1.1 -> 1.2 backfillea `strictness_profile = standard` para configs antiguas.\n20: - `agents_config.py` valida el nuevo schema y expone helpers estables para lectura del perfil.\n21: - `agents.json` sigue siendo la autoridad de configuracion.\n22: - Origen externo: affaan-m/ECC repo-compare 2026-05-27.\n23: - Inspired by: ECC hook profiles and confidence-scored curation.\n24: - `agents_config.py` debe seguir funcionando en pytest. La correccion prescrita para este ticket es un `tests/conftest.py` que asegura que el project root se resuelva antes que `.agent/` en el path de import, sin tocar la logica de produccion.\n25: \n26: ## Trade-offs Considered\n27: | Option | Pros | Cons | Decision |\n28: |--------|------|------|----------|\n29: | Binary hook stub | No schema work | Dead code stays dead | Rejected |\n30: | Hardcode strictness in hook | Fast | Drifts from config and is harder to tune | Rejected |\n31: | Config-driven strictness profiles | Portable, testable, backwards compatible | Small migration needed | Chosen |\n32: \n33: ## Files Likely Touched\n34: - `.agent/hooks/guard_paths.py`\n35: - `.agent/agents_config.py`\n36: - `.agent/config/agents.json`\n37: - `tests/conftest.py` (patch si existe, crear si no existe)\n38: - `tests/test_guard_paths.py`\n39: - `tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n40: \n41: ## Fases\n42: 0. Verify `pytest --collect-only` on the target tests does not raise `ImportError`; fix the path precedence in `tests/conftest.py` if it does.\n43: 1. Add `strictness_profile` plus `profiles` to `agents.json` and migrate schema 1.1 -> 1.2 with a standard default.\n44: 2. Validate the new config shape in `agents_config.py` and expose profile access helpers for runtime use.\n45: 3. Wire `guard_paths.py` to load the selected profile, emit stderr reasons, and return the real hook exit codes instead of unconditional continue.\n46: 4. Add focused tests for the three profiles, migration backfill, hook output behavior, and the strict-mode contract.\n47: \n48: ## Calidad\n49: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py --collect-only tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n50: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py -q`\n51: - `ruff check .agent/hooks/guard_paths.py .agent/agents_config.py tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n52: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n53: \n54: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n55: - `guard_paths.py` no devuelve `{\"continue\": True}` incondicionalmente en `__main__`.\n56: - `guard_paths.py` usa `exit(0)` / `exit(2)` y escribe la razon de bloqueo en `stderr`.\n57: - `guard_paths.py` lee `agents.json` directamente y no depende de `agents_config.py` para seleccionar el perfil.\n58: - `agents.json` version 1.2 soporta `strictness_profile` y migra configs legacy a `standard`.\n59: - `minimal`, `standard` y `strict` tienen comportamiento distinto y testeado.\n60: - `pytest --collect-only` sobre `tests/test_guard_paths.py` y `tests/unit/test_agents_config.py` sale limpio antes de ejecutar los tests.\n61: - `execution_log.md` y otras superficies canonicas vivas no quedan bloqueadas por defecto en modo strict.\n62: - Los tests y la validacion canonica pasan sin regresiones.\n\n(End of file - total 62 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-154\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-154\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Strictness profiles and live guard_paths activation\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nConvertir `guard_paths.py` de stub en un hook funcional y anadir perfiles de strictness en `agents.json` con migracion compatible, sin romper la configuracion existente.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `guard_paths.py` debe ejecutar la logica real en `__main__` en lugar de devolver siempre `{\"continue\": True}`.\n- El contrato del hook es `exit(0)` para allow y `exit(2)` para block; la razon de bloqueo va a `stderr`, no a `stdout`.\n- `guard_paths.py` lee `agents.json` directamente con `json.loads` desde `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"`; no debe importar `agents_config.py` para resolver el perfil.\n- `agents.json` incorpora `strictness_profile` como selector canonico y un mapa `profiles` con `minimal`, `standard` y `strict`.\n- `standard` conserva la proteccion actual; `minimal` reduce el conjunto activo a la superficie sensible minima; `strict` endurece el guard sin bloquear superficies canonicas vivas como `execution_log.md`. El scope gate de `strict` lee `Files Likely Touched` del `work_plan.md` activo; si no hay plan activo (estado COMPLETED o archivo ausente), fail-open aplicando solo los patrones base de `standard`.\n- La migracion 1.1 -> 1.2 backfillea `strictness_profile = standard` para configs antiguas.\n- `agents_config.py` valida el nuevo schema y expone helpers estables para lectura del perfil.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779885896277,"end":1779885896351}},"id":"prt_e6977639e001890SVMCEw2c3jm","sessionID":"ses_196890597ffeHW7Mz3onmKqJM1","messageID":"msg_e6976fb39001ag88mU4k1ND7ZE"}}

Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n2: \n3: AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: \n15: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n16: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n17: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n18: \n19: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n20: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n21: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n22: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n23: \n24: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n25: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Declared validator + absent or ambiguous evidence: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n28: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n29: - [2026-05-25] AP-08 candidate: Test coverage drift. The Builder runs the existing suite, sees it pass, and declares quality gates satisfied — but the new functions introduced in the diff have no direct tests. Th... (unknown)\n30: - [2026-05-25] deliverable_type classification for scaffolding tickets: when Files Likely Touched contains only structural non-Python files (.gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic, the co... (unknown)\n31: - [2026-05-25] Validator evidence gate: when a work_plan explicitly declares a validator as a quality gate (skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), the Manager must find explicit outpu... (unknown)\n32: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard. When a method acquires an exclusive resource (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire, lock-file creation) AND can be reached f... (unknown)\n33: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts. When a method changes from returning implicit None to returning explicit bool, all callers must be updated from generic t... (unknown)\n34: \n35: --- work_plan.md ---\n36: # Work Plan - WP-2026-154\n37: \n38: ## Metadata\n39: - **ID:** WP-2026-154\n40: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n41: - **deliverable_type:** code\n42: - **Titulo:** Strictness profiles and live guard_paths activation\n43: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n44: \n45: ## Objetivo\n46: Convertir `guard_paths.py` de stub en un hook funcional y anadir perfiles de strictness en `agents.json` con migracion compatible, sin romper la configuracion existente.\n47: \n48: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n49: - `guard_paths.py` debe ejecutar la logica real en `__main__` en lugar de devolver siempre `{\"continue\": True}`.\n50: - El contrato del hook es `exit(0)` para allow y `exit(2)` para block; la razon de bloqueo va a `stderr`, no a `stdout`.\n51: - `guard_paths.py` lee `agents.json` directamente con `json.loads` desde `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"`; no debe importar `agents_config.py` para resolver el perfil.\n52: - `agents.json` incorpora `strictness_profile` como selector canonico y un mapa `profiles` con `minimal`, `standard` y `strict`.\n53: - `standard` conserva la proteccion actual; `minimal` reduce el conjunto activo a la superficie sensible minima; `strict` endurece el guard sin bloquear superficies canonicas vivas como `execution_log.md`. El scope gate de `strict` lee `Files Likely Touched` del `work_plan.md` activo; si no hay plan activo (estado COMPLETED o archivo ausente), fail-open aplicando solo los patrones base de `standard`.\n54: - La migracion 1.1 -> 1.2 backfillea `strictness_profile = standard` para configs antiguas.\n55: - `agents_config.py` valida el nuevo schema y expone helpers estables para lectura del perfil.\n56: - `agents.json` sigue siendo la autoridad de configuracion.\n57: - Origen externo: affaan-m/ECC repo-compare 2026-05-27.\n58: - Inspired by: ECC hook profiles and confidence-scored curation.\n59: - `agents_config.py` debe seguir funcionando en pytest. La correccion prescrita para este ticket es un `tests/conftest.py` que asegura que el project root se resuelva antes que `.agent/` en el path de import, sin tocar la logica de produccion.\n60: \n61: ## Trade-offs Considered\n62: | Option | Pros | Cons | Decision |\n63: |--------|------|------|----------|\n64: | Binary hook stub | No schema work | Dead code stays dead | Rejected |\n65: | Hardcode strictness in hook | Fast | Drifts from config and is harder to tune | Rejected |\n66: | Config-driven strictness profiles | Portable, testable, backwards compatible | Small migration needed | Chosen |\n67: \n68: ## Files Likely Touched\n69: - `.agent/hooks/guard_paths.py`\n70: - `.agent/agents_config.py`\n71: - `.agent/config/agents.json`\n72: - `tests/conftest.py` (patch si existe, crear si no existe)\n73: - `tests/test_guard_paths.py`\n74: - `tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n75: \n76: ## Fases\n77: 0. Verify `pytest --collect-only` on the target tests does not raise `ImportError`; fix the path precedence in `tests/conftest.py` if it does.\n78: 1. Add `strictness_profile` plus `profiles` to `agents.json` and migrate schema 1.1 -> 1.2 with a standard default.\n79: 2. Validate the new config shape in `agents_config.py` and expose profile access helpers for runtime use.\n80: 3. Wire `guard_paths.py` to load the selected profile, emit stderr reasons, and return the real hook exit codes instead of unconditional continue.\n81: 4. Add focused tests for the three profiles, migration backfill, hook output behavior, and the strict-mode contract.\n82: \n83: ## Calidad\n84: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py --collect-only tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n85: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py -q`\n86: - `ruff check .agent/hooks/guard_paths.py .agent/agents_config.py tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n87: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n88: \n89: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n90: - `guard_paths.py` no devuelve `{\"continue\": True}` incondicionalmente en `__main__`.\n91: - `guard_paths.py` usa `exit(0)` / `exit(2)` y escribe la razon de bloqueo en `stderr`.\n92: - `guard_paths.py` lee `agents.json` directamente y no depende de `agents_config.py` para seleccionar el perfil.\n93: - `agents.json` version 1.2 soporta `strictness_profile` y migra configs legacy a `standard`.\n94: - `minimal`, `standard` y `strict` tienen comportamiento distinto y testeado.\n95: - `pytest --collect-only` sobre `tests/test_guard_paths.py` y `tests/unit/test_agents_config.py` sale limpio antes de ejecutar los tests.\n96: - `execution_log.md` y otras superficies canonicas vivas no quedan bloqueadas por defecto en modo strict.\n97: - Los tests y la validacion canonica pasan sin regresiones.\n98: \n99: \n100: --- STATE.md ---\n101: # State - WP-2026-154\n102: \n103: Plan Activo: WP-2026-154\n104: Estado actual: IN_PROGRESS\n105: Rol activo: BUILDER\n106: \n107: Resumen:\n108: WP-2026-154 activa la logica real de `guard_paths.py` y añade strictness profiles en `agents.json` con migracion compatible.\n109: \n110: Notas:\n111: - El hook no puede quedarse como stub.\n112: - `strictness_profile` debe ser backward compatible.\n113: - `execution_log.md` sigue siendo superficie viva y no debe bloquearse por defecto.\n114: \n115: \n116: --- TURN.md ---\n117: # TURNO ACTUAL\n118: \n119: **Ultima actualizacion:** 2026-05-27 14:40:57\n120: \n121: ---\n122: \n123: ## Agente Activo\n124: \n125: | Campo | Valor |\n126: |-------|-------|\n127: | **ROL** | **MANAGER** |\n128: | **Plan ID** | WP-2026-154 |\n129: | **Tipo** | IMPLEMENTATION |\n130: | **Accion** | REVIEW_WORK |\n131: \n132: ---\n133: \n134: ## Instruccion\n135: \n136: > Builder completo WP-2026-154. Revisa el trabajo.\n137: \n138: ---\n139: \n140: ## Archivos a Leer\n141: \n142: 1. `.manager_rules` (Contexto del rol)\n143: 2. `.agent/workflows/manager_workflow.md` (Flujo de trabajo)\n144: \n145: ---\n146: \n147: ## Estado del Sistema\n148: \n149: | Archivo | Estado |\n150: |---------|--------|\n151: | work_plan.md | APPROVED |\n152: | execution_log.md | READY_FOR_REVIEW |\n153: \n154: ---\n155: \n156: *Generado por agent_controller.py v5*\n157: \n158: \n159: --- execution_log.md (ticket section) ---\n160: [execution_log section for WP-2026-154 not found]\n161: \n162: --- PLAN_WP-2026-154.md ---\n163: # PLAN_WP-2026-154 - Strictness profiles and live guard_paths activation\n164: \n165: ## Contexto\n166: `guard_paths.py` contiene la logica de bloqueo, pero su `__main__` actual no la usa y devuelve `{\"continue\": True}` siempre. Al mismo tiempo, `agents.json` ya tiene `active_profile`, lo que deja espacio para una nueva capa de strictness sin romper la configuracion existente.\n167: \n168: ## Objetivo tecnico\n169: Conectar el hook a la logica real y parametrizarlo por perfil. El cambio debe ser compatible hacia atras, introducir una migracion simple y mantener como superficie viva `execution_log.md` y los otros archivos canonicos del flujo.\n170: \n171: ## Estrategia\n172: 0. Verificar `pytest --collect-only` sobre los dos tests objetivo; si hay `ImportError`, fijar la precedencia de imports en `tests/conftest.py` como correccion aislada de tests.\n173: 1. Añadir `strictness_profile` y `profiles` en `agents.json` con `minimal`, `standard` y `strict`.\n174: 2. Migrar `schema_version` de `1.1` a `1.2` con backfill a `standard`.\n175: 3. Mantener `guard_paths.py` self-contained: leer `agents.json` directamente, emitir razones a `stderr`, y responder con `exit(0)` / `exit(2)` segun allow/block.\n176: 4. Cubrir el comportamiento con tests de migracion, perfiles, salida del hook y el contrato strict.\n177: \n178: ## Diseño de perfiles\n179: - `minimal`: solo la superficie sensible minima.\n180: - `standard`: replica la proteccion actual como default.\n181: - `strict`: endurece el guard y el scope gate con una blacklist de comandos destructivos mas amplia y bloqueo de escrituras fuera de `Files Likely Touched`, pero no convierte `execution_log.md` en una ruta globalmente bloqueada.\n182: \n183: ## Detalles de implementacion\n184: - Unknown profiles should fail closed with a clear reason.\n185: - The hook should reuse the existing helper functions instead of duplicating matching logic.\n186: - Any profile-specific extra patterns should come from config, not hardcoded branch sprawl.\n187: - Security logging should remain best-effort and non-fatal.\n188: - `agents_config.py` should keep its import path robust in tests, even if `.agent/runtime` shadows the root `runtime` package; the prescribed fix is a `tests/conftest.py` that prepends the project root to `sys.path` — do not modify production code for this.\n189: \n190: ## Riesgos\n191: - If the profile schema is too strict, the hook could lock out normal work.\n192: - If the hook keeps fallbacking to `continue`, the change is cosmetic and unsafe.\n193: - If the migration does not backfill existing configs, older workspaces will fail validation.\n194: - If the runtime import precedence is wrong in pytest, the config loader will fail before the new profile logic is exercised.\n195: \n196: ## Validacion\n197: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py --collect-only tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n198: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py -q`\n199: - `ruff check .agent/hooks/guard_paths.py .agent/agents_config.py tests/test_guard_paths.py tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n200: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n201: \n202: \n203: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-154.md ---\n204: # AUDIT_WP-2026-154 - Strictness profiles and live guard_paths activation\n205: \n206: ## Review Focus\n207: This review checks whether the hook is actually live, whether the new profile schema is backward compatible, and whether the strict mode improves safety without breaking canonical collaboration files.\n208: \n209: ## Must-pass criteria\n210: - `guard_paths.py` main path executes real guard logic instead of unconditional `continue`.\n211: - `guard_paths.py` reports block reasons on `stderr` and uses hook exit codes instead of JSON output as the contract.\n212: - `guard_paths.py` loads `agents.json` directly and does not depend on `agents_config.py` for profile selection.\n213: - `agents.json` accepts `strictness_profile` and a profile map, with `standard` as the safe default.\n214: - Migration 1.1 -> 1.2 backfills `strictness_profile = standard` without corrupting existing config.\n215: - `minimal`, `standard`, and `strict` produce different enforcement levels.\n216: - `pytest --collect-only` on the two target tests passes before execution.\n217: - `execution_log.md` and other live canonical surfaces remain usable and are not globally blocked by strict mode.\n218: \n219: ## Files to inspect\n220: - `.agent/hooks/guard_paths.py`\n221: - `.agent/agents_config.py`\n222: - `.agent/config/agents.json`\n223: - `tests/conftest.py`\n224: - `tests/test_guard_paths.py`\n225: - `tests/unit/test_agents_config.py`\n226: \n227: ## Review Questions\n228: 1. Does `__main__` in `guard_paths.py` now invoke the real checks?\n229: 2. Does the new schema fail closed on invalid profile values?\n230: 3. Does the migration preserve older configs?\n231: 4. Do the tests prove the three profile behaviors and the hook output?\n232: 5. Is the live-state surface preserved for canonical collaboration files?\n233: 6. Does test collection stay clean before execution?\n234: \n235: ## Approval bar\n236: - Approve only if the change is functional, backwards compatible, and covered by tests.\n237: - If the hook still behaves like a stub, request changes.\n238: - If strict mode blocks canonical live state files, request changes.\n239: \n240: \n241: --- git provenance ---\n242: [no new commits since origin/main]\n243: \n244: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n245: Available skills for this role:\n246:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n247:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n248:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n249: \n250: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n251: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n252: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n253: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n254: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n255: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n256: - .session_state.json\n257: \n258: \n259: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n260: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n261: \n262: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n263: \n264: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n265: DECISION: APPROVE\n266: \n267: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n268: \n269: ## SUMMARY\n270: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n271: \n272: ## BLOCKERS\n273: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n274: \n275: ## SUGGESTIONS\n276: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n277: \n278: DECISION: CHANGES\n279: \n280: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n281: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 281 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-154. Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n\nImplementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-154_attempt-2.md","time":{"start":1779885896265,"end":1779885896400}},"id":"prt_e69775f07001SGizq7aPddRRxa","sessionID":"ses_196890597ffeHW7Mz3onmKqJM1","messageID":"msg_e6976fb39001ag88mU4k1ND7ZE"}}

self.base_dir / f\"{safe_name}{self._counter:04d}\"\n43:         else:\n44:             path = self.base_dir / safe_name\n45:         path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n46:         return {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779885927532,"sessionID":"ses_196890597ffeHW7Mz3onmKqJM1","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_67c3c5bd39244ff188ae1638","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agents_config.py"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\agents_config.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: \"\"\"\n2: Agent Configuration Loader - Centralized backend and role configuration.\n3: \n4: This module provides a single source of truth for agent backend assignments\n5: and discovery methods, removing hardcoding from the PowerShell launcher.\n6: \n7: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: \n10: import argparse\n11: import json\n12: import shutil\n13: import sys\n14: from collections.abc import Callable\n15: from dataclasses import dataclass\n16: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n17: from pathlib import Path\n18: from typing import Any\n19: \n20: \n21: # WP-2026-122: Single source of truth for project root resolution\n22: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n23: _PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n24: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED) not in sys.path:\n25:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_DERIVED))\n26: \n27: from runtime.project_root import get_agent_dir  # noqa: E402\n28: \n29: \n30: class _LazyPath:\n31:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n32:         self._resolver = resolver\n33: \n34:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n35:         return self._resolver()\n36: \n37:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n38:         return self.resolve() / other\n39: \n40:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n41:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n42: \n43:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n44:         return str(self.resolve())\n45: \n46:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n47:         return str(self.resolve())\n48: \n49: \n50: def _config_path() -> Path:\n51:     return get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n52: \n53: \n54: CONFIG_PATH = _LazyPath(_config_path)\n55: \n56: KNOWN_ROLES = {\"BUILDER\", \"MANAGER\", \"SUPERVISOR\"}\n57: REQUIRED_BACKEND_KEYS = {\"executable\", \"args\", \"discovery\"}\n58: REQUIRED_DISCOVERY_KEYS = {\"method\"}\n59: \n60: \n61: def _validate_skill_allowlists(config: dict, config_path: Path) -> None:\n62:     \"\"\"Validate the skill_allowlists section (optional, retrocompatible).\n63: \n64:     Before: skill_allowlists no existía; fallback en SkillResolver.\n65:     During: Valida que cada rol en skill_allowlists sea conocido y que\n66:             las skills referenciadas existan en el catalogo descubierto.\n67:     After: Permite configuracion vacia o omitida; falla si hay roles\n68:            desconocidos o skills inexistentes explicitamente declaradas.\n69:     \"\"\"\n70:     if \"skill_allowlists\" not in config:\n71:         return  # Retrocompatible: si no hay allowlists, usar fallback\n72: \n73:     allowlists = config[\"skill_allowlists\"]\n74:     if not isinstance(allowlists, dict):\n75:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n76:             f\"Invalid 'skill_allowlists' in {config_path}: must be an object\"\n77:         )\n78: \n79:     # Validar que cada rol en allowlists sea conocido\n80:     for role in allowlists:\n81:         if role not in KNOWN_ROLES:\n82:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n83:                 f\"Unknown role '{role}' in skill_allowlists. Known roles: {KNOWN_ROLES}\"\n84:             )\n85: \n86:     # Validar que cada allowlist sea una lista\n87:     for role, skills in allowlists.items():\n88:         if not isinstance(skills, list):\n89:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n90:                 f\"skill_allowlists['{role}'] must be a list, got {type(skills).__name__}\"\n91:             )\n92: \n93: \n94: class AgentsConfigError(Exception):\n95:     \"\"\"Raised when agent configuration is invalid.\"\"\"\n96: \n97:     pass\n98: \n99: \n100: @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)\n101: class Migration:\n102:     \"\"\"\n103:     Describe una transición de schema para agents.json.\n104: \n105:     Before: Se requiere un id único, from_version, to_version, y una función apply.\n106:     During: El registry MIGRATIONS usa esta dataclass para iterar en orden cronológico.\n107:     After: Cada migración aplicada deja un backup timestamped y actualiza _migrations.\n108:     \"\"\"\n109: \n110:     id: str  # ej. \"1.0_to_1.1\"\n111:     from_version: str  # ej. \"1.0\"\n112:     to_version: str  # ej. \"1.1\"\n113:     apply: Callable[[dict], dict]  # pure: receives config dict, returns new dict\n114: \n115: \n116: @dataclass(slots=True)\n117: class MigrationReport:\n118:     \"\"\"\n119:     Reporte de ejecución de migrate_agents_config().\n120: \n121:     Before: Se crea vacío al iniciar el pipeline.\n122:     During: Se llena con los ids aplicados, skipped y backups creados.\n123:     After: Se retorna al caller para consumo CLI o programático.\n124:     \"\"\"\n125: \n126:     applied: list[str]  # ids aplicados en esta invocación\n127:     skipped: list[str]  # ids ya presentes en _migrations\n128:     backups: list[Path]  # paths a archivos .bak.<ts> creados\n129: \n130: \n131: def load_agents_config(project_root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:\n132:     \"\"\"\n133:     Load and validate the agent configuration.\n134: \n135:     Args:\n136:         project_root: Optional project root path. If None, uses the parent\n137:                       directory of this module's location (or runtime.project_root\n138:                       if available for WP-2026-122 dynamic resolution).\n139: \n140:     Returns:\n141:         Validated configuration dictionary.\n142: \n143:     Raises:\n144:         AgentsConfigError: If config file is missing or invalid.\n145:     \"\"\"\n146:     if project_root is None:\n147:         # WP-2026-122: Use dynamic project_root resolution if available\n148:         if get_agent_dir is not None:\n149:             config_path = get_agent_dir() / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n150:         else:\n151:             config_path = Path(__file__).parent / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n152:     else:\n153:         config_path = project_root / \".agent\" / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n154: \n155:     if not config_path.exists():\n156:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Configuration file not found: {config_path}\")\n157: \n158:     try:\n159:         with open(config_path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n160:             config = json.load(f)\n161:     except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n162:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Invalid JSON in configuration file: {e}\") from e\n163: \n164:     _validate_config(config, config_path)\n165:     return config\n166: \n167: \n168: def _validate_role_models(config: dict, config_path: Path) -> None:\n169:     \"\"\"Validate the role_models section (optional, retrocompatible).\"\"\"\n170:     if \"role_models\" not in config:\n171:         return\n172: \n173:     role_models = config[\"role_models\"]\n174:     if not isinstance(role_models, dict):\n175:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n176:             f\"Invalid 'role_models' in {config_path}: must be an object\"\n177:         )\n178: \n179:     for role in role_models:\n180:         if role not in KNOWN_ROLES:\n181:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n182:                 f\"Unknown role '{role}' in role_models. Known roles: {KNOWN_ROLES}\"\n183:             )\n184: \n185: \n186: def _validate_config(config: dict, config_path: Path) -> None:\n187:     \"\"\"Validate the configuration schema.\"\"\"\n188:     # Check schema_version\n189:     if \"schema_version\" not in config:\n190:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Missing 'schema_version' in {config_path}\")\n191: \n192:     # Check backends\n193:     if \"backends\" not in config or not isinstance(config[\"backends\"], dict):\n194:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Missing or invalid 'backends' in {config_path}\")\n195: \n196:     if not config[\"backends\"]:\n197:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"'backends' cannot be empty in {config_path}\")\n198: \n199:     for backend_name, backend_config in config[\"backends\"].items():\n200:         _validate_backend(backend_name, backend_config, config_path)\n201: \n202:     # Check role_assignments\n203:     if \"role_assignments\" not in config or not isinstance(\n204:         config[\"role_assignments\"], dict\n205:     ):\n206:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n207:             f\"Missing or invalid 'role_assignments' in {config_path}\"\n208:         )\n209: \n210:     for role, backend_name in config[\"role_assignments\"].items():\n211:         if role not in KNOWN_ROLES:\n212:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n213:                 f\"Unknown role '{role}' in role_assignments. Known roles: {KNOWN_ROLES}\"\n214:             )\n215:         if backend_name not in config[\"backends\"]:\n216:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n217:                 f\"Role '{role}' references unknown backend '{backend_name}'\"\n218:             )\n219: \n220:     # Check role_models (optional, retrocompatible)\n221:     _validate_role_models(config, config_path)\n222: \n223:     # Check skill_allowlists (optional, retrocompatible)\n224:     _validate_skill_allowlists(config, config_path)\n225: \n226:     # Check strictness_profile and profiles (schema 1.2+, retrocompatible)\n227:     _validate_strictness_profiles(config, config_path)\n228: \n229: \n230: def _validate_strictness_profiles(config: dict, config_path: Path) -> None:\n231:     \"\"\"Validate the strictness_profile and profiles section (schema 1.2+, retrocompatible).\n232: \n233:     Before: strictness_profile no existía; fallback a standard.\n234:     During: Valida que strictness_profile sea un valor conocido (minimal, standard, strict)\n235:             y que profiles sea un objeto con las tres claves requeridas.\n236:     After: Permite configuracion vacia o omitida (retrocompatible); falla si hay\n237:            valores invalidos explícitamente declarados.\n238:     \"\"\"\n239:     known_profiles = {\"minimal\", \"standard\", \"strict\"}\n240: \n241:     # strictness_profile es opcional para retrocompatibilidad; default = standard\n242:     if \"strictness_profile\" in config:\n243:         profile = config[\"strictness_profile\"]\n244:         if profile not in known_profiles:\n245:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n246:                 f\"Invalid 'strictness_profile' in {config_path}: must be one of {known_profiles}, got '{profile}'\"\n247:             )\n248: \n249:     # profiles es opcional para retrocompatibilidad\n250:     if \"profiles\" in config:\n251:         profiles = config[\"profiles\"]\n252:         if not isinstance(profiles, dict):\n253:             raise AgentsConfigError(\n254:                 f\"Invalid 'profiles' in {config_path}: must be an object\"\n255:             )\n256:         # Validar que las claves conocidas estén presentes si profiles existe\n257:         for profile_name in known_profiles:\n258:             if profile_name not in profiles:\n259:                 raise AgentsConfigError(\n260:                     f\"Missing required profile '{profile_name}' in profiles\"\n261:                 )\n262:         # Validar que cada perfil tenga estructura básica\n263:         for profile_name, profile_config in profiles.items():\n264:             if not isinstance(profile_config, dict):\n265:                 raise AgentsConfigError(\n266:                     f\"Invalid profile '{profile_name}' in profiles: must be an object\"\n267:                 )\n268: \n269: \n270: def _validate_backend(name: str, backend: dict, config_path: Path) -> None:\n271:     \"\"\"Validate a single backend configuration.\"\"\"\n272:     for key in REQUIRED_BACKEND_KEYS:\n273:         if key not in backend:\n274:             raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Backend '{name}' missing required key '{key}'\")\n275: \n276:     if not isinstance(backend[\"args\"], list):\n277:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Backend '{name}': 'args' must be a list\")\n278: \n279:     discovery = backend[\"discovery\"]\n280:     if not isinstance(discovery, dict):\n281:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Backend '{name}': 'discovery' must be an object\")\n282: \n283:     for key in REQUIRED_DISCOVERY_KEYS:\n284:         if key not in discovery:\n285:             raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Backend '{name}' missing discovery key '{key}'\")\n286: \n287:     if discovery[\"method\"] not in (\"vscode_extension\", \"path_only\"):\n288:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n289:             f\"Backend '{name}': unknown discovery method '{discovery['method']}'\"\n290:         )\n291: \n292: \n293: def get_backend_for_role(role: str, config: dict | None = None) -> str:\n294:     \"\"\"\n295:     Get the backend name assigned to a role.\n296: \n297:     Args:\n298:         role: The role name (e.g., \"BUILDER\", \"MANAGER\").\n299:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration. If None, loads from file.\n300: \n301:     Returns:\n302:         The backend name assigned to the role.\n303: \n304:     Raises:\n305:         AgentsConfigError: If role is not assigned or unknown.\n306:     \"\"\"\n307:     if config is None:\n308:         config = load_agents_config()\n309: \n310:     role_assignments = config.get(\"role_assignments\", {})\n311: \n312:     if role not in role_assignments:\n313:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n314:             f\"No backend assigned to role '{role}'. \"\n315:             f\"Available assignments: {list(role_assignments.keys())}\"\n316:         )\n317: \n318:     return role_assignments[role]\n319: \n320: \n321: def get_backend_config(backend_name: str, config: dict | None = None) -> dict:\n322:     \"\"\"\n323:     Get the configuration for a specific backend.\n324: \n325:     Args:\n326:         backend_name: The backend name (e.g., \"kilo\", \"opencode\").\n327:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration. If None, loads from file.\n328: \n329:     Returns:\n330:         The backend configuration dictionary.\n331: \n332:     Raises:\n333:         AgentsConfigError: If backend is unknown.\n334:     \"\"\"\n335:     if config is None:\n336:         config = load_agents_config()\n337: \n338:     backends = config.get(\"backends\", {})\n339: \n340:     if backend_name not in backends:\n341:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n342:             f\"Unknown backend '{backend_name}'. \"\n343:             f\"Available backends: {list(backends.keys())}\"\n344:         )\n345: \n346:     return backends[backend_name]\n347: \n348: \n349: def resolve_executable(backend_name: str, config: dict | None = None) -> str:\n350:     \"\"\"\n351:     Resolve the executable path for a backend.\n352: \n353:     This function returns the executable name. The actual path resolution\n354:     (via PATH lookup or VS Code extension discovery) is performed by the\n355:     launcher at runtime.\n356: \n357:     Args:\n358:         backend_name: The backend name.\n359:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration.\n360: \n361:     Returns:\n362:         The executable name to resolve.\n363: \n364:     Raises:\n365:         AgentsConfigError: If backend is unknown.\n366:     \"\"\"\n367:     backend = get_backend_config(backend_name, config)\n368:     return backend[\"executable\"]\n369: \n370: \n371: def get_backend_args(backend_name: str, config: dict | None = None) -> list[str]:\n372:     \"\"\"\n373:     Get the command-line arguments for a backend.\n374: \n375:     Args:\n376:         backend_name: The backend name.\n377:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration.\n378: \n379:     Returns:\n380:         List of command-line arguments.\n381: \n382:     Raises:\n383:         AgentsConfigError: If backend is unknown.\n384:     \"\"\"\n385:     backend = get_backend_config(backend_name, config)\n386:     return backend[\"args\"]\n387: \n388: \n389: def get_discovery_method(backend_name: str, config: dict | None = None) -> str:\n390:     \"\"\"\n391:     Get the discovery method for a backend.\n392: \n393:     Args:\n394:         backend_name: The backend name.\n395:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration.\n396: \n397:     Returns:\n398:         The discovery method (\"vscode_extension\" or \"path_only\").\n399: \n400:     Raises:\n401:         AgentsConfigError: If backend is unknown.\n402:     \"\"\"\n403:     backend = get_backend_config(backend_name, config)\n404:     return backend[\"discovery\"][\"method\"]\n405: \n406: \n407: def get_model_for_role(role: str, config: dict | None = None) -> str | None:\n408:     \"\"\"\n409:     Get the model override for a role from role_models.\n410: \n411:     This allows changing the model for a role by editing only agents.json\n412:     without touching code. Returns None if no model override is defined\n413:     (the backend should use its default from opencode.json or equivalent).\n414: \n415:     Args:\n416:         role: The role name (e.g., \"BUILDER\", \"MANAGER\").\n417:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration. If None, loads from file.\n418: \n419:     Returns:\n420:         The model identifier string, or None if no override is defined.\n421: \n422:     Raises:\n423:         AgentsConfigError: If role is unknown.\n424:     \"\"\"\n425:     if config is None:\n426:         config = load_agents_config()\n427: \n428:     if role not in KNOWN_ROLES:\n429:         raise AgentsConfigError(f\"Unknown role '{role}'. Known roles: {KNOWN_ROLES}\")\n430: \n431:     role_models = config.get(\"role_models\", {})\n432:     return role_models.get(role)\n433: \n434: \n435: def get_strictness_profile(config: dict | None = None) -> str:\n436:     \"\"\"\n437:     Get the active strictness profile name.\n438: \n439:     Returns the configured strictness_profile or 'standard' as default\n440:     for backward compatibility.\n441: \n442:     Args:\n443:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration. If None, loads from file.\n444: \n445:     Returns:\n446:         The strictness profile name ('minimal', 'standard', or 'strict').\n447: \n448:     Raises:\n449:         AgentsConfigError: If strictness_profile is invalid.\n450:     \"\"\"\n451:     if config is None:\n452:         config = load_agents_config()\n453: \n454:     return config.get(\"strictness_profile\", \"standard\")\n455: \n456: \n457: def get_profile_config(\n458:     profile_name: str | None = None, config: dict | None = None\n459: ) -> dict:\n460:     \"\"\"\n461:     Get the configuration for a specific strictness profile.\n462: \n463:     Args:\n464:         profile_name: Optional profile name. If None, uses the configured\n465:                       strictness_profile or 'standard' as default.\n466:         config: Optional pre-loaded configuration. If None, loads from file.\n467: \n468:     Returns:\n469:         The profile configuration dictionary with keys like\n470:         'write_roots', 'blocked_command_patterns', etc.\n471: \n472:     Raises:\n473:         AgentsConfigError: If profile is unknown.\n474:     \"\"\"\n475:     if config is None:\n476:         config = load_agents_config()\n477: \n478:     if profile_name is None:\n479:         profile_name = config.get(\"strictness_profile\", \"standard\")\n480: \n481:     profiles = config.get(\"profiles\", {})\n482:     if profile_name not in profiles:\n483:         raise AgentsConfigError(\n484:             f\"Unknown strictness profile '{profile_name}'. \"\n485:             f\"Available profiles: {list(profiles.keys())}\"\n486:         )\n487: \n488:     return profiles[profile_name]\n489: \n490: \n491: def _migrate_1_0_to_1_1(config: dict) -> dict:\n492:     \"\"\"\n493:     Pure migration handler 1.0 → 1.1.\n494: \n495:     Before: Config con schema_version \"1.0\" sin role_models.\n496:     During: Backfills role_models con los defaults de WP-072 si falta.\n497:     After: Retorna nuevo dict con schema_version \"1.1\" y role_models populated.\n498: \n499:     Esta migración es retroactiva: formaliza el cambio manual hecho en WP-072.\n500:     \"\"\"\n501:     new = dict(config)\n502:     new[\"schema_version\"] = \"1.1\"\n503:     if \"role_models\" not in new:\n504:         new[\"role_models\"] = {\n505:             \"BUILDER\": \"opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus\",\n506:             \"MANAGER\": \"openai/gpt-5.4-mini\",\n507:         }\n508:     return new\n509: \n510: \n511: def _migrate_1_1_to_1_2(config: dict) -> dict:\n512:     \"\"\"\n513:     Pure migration handler 1.1 → 1.2.\n514: \n515:     Before: Config con schema_version \"1.1\" sin strictness_profile ni profiles.\n516:     During: Backfills strictness_profile = \"standard\" y profiles map con\n517:             minimal, standard, strict si faltan.\n518:     After: Retorna nuevo dict con schema_version \"1.2\", strictness_profile\n519:            y profiles populated.\n520:     \"\"\"\n521:     new = dict(config)\n522:     new[\"schema_version\"] = \"1.2\"\n523:     if \"strictness_profile\" not in new:\n524:         new[\"strictness_profile\"] = \"standard\"\n525:     if \"profiles\" not in new:\n526:         new[\"profiles\"] = {\n527:             \"minimal\": {\n528:                 \"description\": \"Solo la superficie sensible minima\",\n529:                 \"write_roots\": [],\n530:                 \"blocked_command_patterns\": [],\n531:             },\n532:             \"standard\": {\n533:                 \"description\": \"Replica la proteccion actual como default\",\n534:                 \"write_roots\": [],\n535:                 \"blocked_command_patterns\": [],\n536:             },\n537:             \"strict\": {\n538:                 \"description\": \"Endurece el guard sin bloquear superficies canonicas vivas\",\n539:                 \"write_roots\": [],\n540:                 \"blocked_command_patterns\": [],\n541:             },\n542:         }\n543:     return new\n544: \n545: \n546: MIGRATIONS: list[Migration] = [\n547:     Migration(\n548:         id=\"1.0_to_1.1\",\n549:         from_version=\"1.0\",\n550:         to_version=\"1.1\",\n551:         apply=_migrate_1_0_to_1_1,\n552:     ),\n553:     Migration(\n554:         id=\"1.1_to_1.2\",\n555:         from_version=\"1.1\",\n556:         to_version=\"1.2\",\n557:         apply=_migrate_1_1_to_1_2,\n558:     ),\n559:     # Future migrations appended chronologically here.\n560: ]\n561: \n562: \n563: def migrate_agents_config(\n564:     path: Path | None = None,\n565:     *,\n566:     dry_run: bool = False,\n567: ) -> MigrationReport:\n568:     \"\"\"\n569:     Apply pending migrations to agents.json idempotently.\n570: \n571:     Before: agents.json existe con schema_version y opcionalmente _migrations.\n572:     During:\n573:       1. Load JSON (raise si falta o malformed).\n574:       2. Read current schema_version + _migrations (default []).\n575:       3. For each Migration en MIGRATIONS en orden:\n576:          - Si migration.id ya en _migrations: skip (idempotent).\n577:          - Else: backup, apply handler, update _migrations, persist.\n578:       4. Legacy backfill: si _migrations falta pero schema_version es current,\n579:          poblar _migrations retroactivamente sin re-ejecutar handlers.\n580:     After: Retorna MigrationReport con applied, skipped, backups.\n581: \n582:     Args:\n583:         path: Path to agents.json. Default: CONFIG_PATH.\n584:         dry_run: If True, report what would happen without writing.\n585: \n586:     Returns:\n587:         MigrationReport with applied, skipped, and backups lists.\n588: \n589:     Raises:\n590:         FileNotFoundError: If agents.json does not exist.\n591:         json.JSONDecodeError: If agents.json is malformed.\n592:     \"\"\"\n593:     if path is None:\n594:         path = CONFIG_PATH\n595: \n596:     config = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n597:     current_migrations = list(config.get(\"_migrations\", []))\n598:     applied: list[str] = []\n599:     skipped: list[str] = []\n600:     backups: list[Path] = []\n601: \n602:     # Legacy backfill: schema_version already current but _migrations missing\n603:     # Only backfill if schema_version equals the latest known migration to_version\n604:     latest_version = MIGRATIONS[-1].to_version if MIGRATIONS else \"1.0\"\n605:     if \"_migrations\" not in config and config.get(\"schema_version\") == latest_version:\n606:         # Retroactively claim all migrations up to current version\n607:         retroactive = [m.id for m in MIGRATIONS]\n608:         current_migrations.extend(retroactive)\n609:         config[\"_migrations\"] = current_migrations\n610:         if not dry_run:\n611:             path.write_text(\n612:                 json.dumps(config, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n613:             )\n614:         return MigrationReport(applied=[], skipped=retroactive, backups=[])\n615: \n616:     for migration in MIGRATIONS:\n617:         if migration.id in current_migrations:\n618:             skipped.append(migration.id)\n619:             continue\n620:         if not dry_run:\n621:             ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime(\"%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ\")\n622:             backup_path = path.with_suffix(f\".json.bak.{ts}\")\n623:             shutil.copy2(path, backup_path)\n624:             backups.append(backup_path)\n625:             config = migration.apply(config)\n626:             current_migrations.append(migration.id)\n627:             config[\"_migrations\"] = current_migrations\n628:             path.write_text(\n629:                 json.dumps(config, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n630:             )\n631:         applied.append(migration.id)\n632: \n633:     return MigrationReport(applied=applied, skipped=skipped, backups=backups)\n634: \n635: \n636: # CLI interface for PowerShell consumption\n637: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n638:     import sys\n639: \n640:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n641:     parser.add_argument(\n642:         \"--migrate\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Apply pending migrations\"\n643:     )\n644:     parser.add_argument(\"--dry-run\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Report without writing\")\n645:     parser.add_argument(\"command\", nargs=\"?\", help=\"Command to run\")\n646:     parser.add_argument(\"args\", nargs=\"*\", help=\"Command arguments\")\n647:     args = parser.parse_args()\n648: \n649:     # Handle --migrate flag\n650:     if args.migrate:\n651:         report = migrate_agents_config(dry_run=args.dry_run)\n652:         print(\n653:             json.dumps(\n654:                 {\n655:                     \"applied\": report.applied,\n656:                     \"skipped\": report.skipped,\n657:                     \"backups\": [str(p) for p in report.backups],\n658:                     \"dry_run\": args.dry_run,\n659:                 },\n660:                 indent=2,\n661:             )\n662:         )\n663:         sys.exit(0)\n664: \n665:     # Legacy command interface\n666:     if not args.command:\n667:         print(\"Usage: python agents_config.py <command> <args...>\")\n668:         print(\"Commands:\")\n669:         print(\"  get_backend_for_role <role>\")\n670:         print(\"  get_model_for_role <role>\")\n671:         print(\"  get_executable <backend>\")\n672:         print(\"  get_args <backend>\")\n673:         print(\"  get_discovery <backend>\")\n674:         print(\"  validate\")\n675:         print(\"  --migrate [--dry-run]  Apply pending migrations\")\n676:         sys.exit(1)\n677: \n678:     command = args.command\n679: \n680:     try:\n681:         config = load_agents_config()\n682: \n683:         if command == \"get_backend_for_role\":\n684:             if len(args.args) < 1:\n685:                 print(\"Error: missing role argument\")\n686:                 sys.exit(1)\n687:             role = args.args[0]\n688:             backend = get_backend_for_role(role, config)\n689:             print(backend)\n690: \n691:         elif command == \"get_model_for_role\":\n692:             if len(args.args) < 1:\n693:                 print(\"Error: missing role argument\")\n694:                 sys.exit(1)\n695:             role = args.args[0]\n696:             model = get_model_for_role(role, config)\n697:             if model:\n698:                 print(model)\n699:             else:\n700:                 print(\"(no override)\")\n701: \n702:         elif command == \"get_executable\":\n703:             if len(args.args) < 1:\n704:                 print(\"Error: missing backend argument\")\n705:                 sys.exit(1)\n706:             backend_name = args.args[0]\n707:             exe = resolve_executable(backend_name, config)\n708:             print(exe)\n709: \n710:         elif command == \"get_args\":\n711:             if len(args.args) < 1:\n712:                 print(\"Error: missing backend argument\")\n713:                 sys.exit(1)\n714:             backend_name = args.args[0]\n715:             args_list = get_backend_args(backend_name, config)\n716:             print(\" \".join(args_list))\n717: \n718:         elif command == \"get_discovery\":\n719:             if len(args.args) < 1:\n720:                 print(\"Error: missing backend argument\")\n721:                 sys.exit(1)\n722:             backend_name = args.args[0]\n723:             method = get_discovery_method(backend_name, config)\n724:             print(method)\n725: \n726:         elif command == \"validate\":\n727:             print(\"Configuration is valid\")\n728: \n729:         else:\n730:             print(f\"Unknown command: {command}\")\n731:             sys.exit(1)\n732: \n733:     except AgentsConfigError as e:\n734:         print(f\"Error: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n735: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Guard paths hook - minimal stub for TICKET-014 consolidation\"\"\"\n3: \n4: from __future__ import annotations\n5: \n6: import json\n7: import os\n8: import re\n9: import sys\n10: from contextlib import suppress\n11: from pathlib import Path\n12: \n13: \n14: # Constants\n15: DEFAULT_ALLOWLIST: dict[str, list[str]] = {\n16:     \"write_roots\": [],\n17:     \"blocked_command_patterns\": [],\n18: }\n19: \n20: SECURITY_LOG_PATH = Path.home() / \".kilo\" / \"security.log\"\n21: \n22: PROTECTED_PATH_PATTERNS = (\n23:     r\"privada\",\n24:     r\"secrets?\",\n25:     r\"credentials?\",\n26:     r\"\\.git\",\n27:     r\"\\.env\",\n28:     r\"token\",\n29:     r\"api[_-]key\",\n30:     r\"password\",\n31:     r\"bearer\",\n32:     r\"auth\",\n33: )\n34: \n35: PROTECTED_FILENAMES = {\n36:     \".env\",\n37:     \".env.local\",\n38:     \".env.production\",\n39:     \"secrets.json\",\n40:     \"credentials.json\",\n41: }\n42: \n43: PROTECTED_COMMAND_REFS = (\n44:     r\"\\.env\",\n45:     r\"secrets?\",\n46:     r\"credentials?\",\n47:     r\"token\",\n48:     r\"api[_-]key\",\n49:     r\"password\",\n50:     r\"bearer\",\n51:     r\"auth\",\n52:     r\"sk-ant\",\n53:     r\"sk-[a-z]\",\n54: )\n55: \n56: DANGEROUS_COMMAND_PATTERNS = (\n57:     r\"rm\\s+-rf\\s+/\",\n58:     r\"git\\s+push\\s+--force\",\n59:     r\"git\\s+reset\\s+--hard\",\n60:     r\"dd\\s+if=\",\n61:     r\"mkfs\",\n62:     r\"fdisk\",\n63:     r\"format\",\n64:     r\"del\\s+/f\\s+/s\\s+/q\",\n65: )\n66: \n67: \n68: def _log_security_event(event_type: str, path: str, reason: str) -> None:\n69:     \"\"\"Log a security event to the security log file.\"\"\"\n70:     with suppress(OSError):\n71:         SECURITY_LOG_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n72:         with SECURITY_LOG_PATH.open(\"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n73:             f.write(f\"{event_type}: {path} - {reason}\\n\")\n74: \n75: \n76: def _normalize(path: str) -> str:\n77:     \"\"\"Normalize path to lowercase with forward slashes.\"\"\"\n78:     return path.replace(\"\\\\\", \"/\").lower()\n79: \n80: \n81: def _read_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:\n82:     \"\"\"Read and parse JSON file, returning empty dict on error.\"\"\"\n83:     with suppress(OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n84:         return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n85:     return {}\n86: \n87: \n88: def _tool_paths(tool_call: dict[str, object]) -> list[str]:\n89:     \"\"\"Extract file paths from a tool call.\"\"\"\n90:     paths = []\n91:     for key, value in tool_call.items():\n92:         if (\n93:             key in (\"file_path\", \"path\", \"target_path\", \"new_path\")\n94:             and isinstance(value, str)\n95:             and value\n96:         ):\n97:             paths.append(value)\n98:     return paths\n99: \n100: \n101: def _is_within_repo(path_obj: Path, repo_root: Path) -> bool:\n102:     try:\n103:         path_obj.relative_to(repo_root)\n104:         return True\n105:     except ValueError:\n106:         return False\n107: \n108: \n109: def _matches_any_pattern(text: str, patterns: tuple[str, ...]) -> str | None:\n110:     for pattern in patterns:\n111:         if re.search(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE):\n112:             return pattern\n113:     return None\n114: \n115: \n116: def _is_allowed_write_root(\n117:     path_obj: Path, repo_root: Path, write_roots: list[str]\n118: ) -> bool:\n119:     return any(path_obj.is_relative_to(repo_root / root) for root in write_roots)\n120: \n121: \n122: def _is_protected_path(\n123:     path: str, allowlist: dict[str, list[str]], config: dict[str, object]\n124: ) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n125:     \"\"\"Check if a path is protected and should be blocked.\n126: \n127:     Returns (is_blocked, reason) where reason is empty if not blocked.\n128:     \"\"\"\n129:     try:\n130:         path_obj = Path(path).resolve()\n131:     except (OSError, ValueError) as e:\n132:         return True, f\"path invalido: {e}\"\n133: \n134:     # Get repo root from environment or current working directory\n135:     try:\n136:         repo_root = Path(os.getcwd()).resolve()\n137:     except (OSError, ValueError):\n138:         return True, \"directorio actual no accesible\"\n139: \n140:     # Check if path is outside repo - fail closed\n141:     if not _is_within_repo(path_obj, repo_root):\n142:         return True, \"fuera del repo\"\n143: \n144:     # Check protected patterns - fail closed\n145:     # Special check for protected filenames - fail closed\n146:     filename = path_obj.name.lower()\n147:     if filename in PROTECTED_FILENAMES:\n148:         return True, f\"archivo protegido: {filename}\"\n149: \n150:     path_str = str(path_obj)\n151:     pattern = _matches_any_pattern(path_str, PROTECTED_PATH_PATTERNS)\n152:     if pattern:\n153:         return True, f\"ruta protegida por patron: {pattern}\"\n154: \n155:     # Check write roots if specified - fail closed if no roots configured\n156:     write_roots = allowlist.get(\"write_roots\", [])\n157:     if write_roots and not _is_allowed_write_root(path_obj, repo_root, write_roots):\n158:         return True, f\"fuera de write_roots permitidos: {write_roots}\"\n159: \n160:     return False, \"\"\n161: \n162: \n163: def _is_blocked_command(command: str, config: dict[str, object]) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n164:     \"\"\"Check if a command is blocked.\n165: \n166:     Returns (is_blocked, reason) where reason is empty if not blocked.\n167:     \"\"\"\n168:     if not command or not isinstance(command, str):\n169:         return True, \"comando vacio o invalido\"\n170: \n171:     # Path traversal patterns - fail closed\n172:     if re.search(r\"\\.\\./|\\.\\.\\\\\", command):\n173:         return True, \"path traversal detectado\"\n174: \n175:     # Protected file references - fail closed\n176:     ref = _matches_any_pattern(command, PROTECTED_COMMAND_REFS)\n177:     if ref:\n178:         return True, f\"referencia a datos sensibles: {ref}\"\n179: \n180:     # Dangerous commands - fail closed\n181:     pattern = _matches_any_pattern(command, DANGEROUS_COMMAND_PATTERNS)\n182:     if pattern:\n183:         return True, f\"comando destructivo bloqueado: {pattern}\"\n184: \n185:     # Custom blocked patterns from config\n186:     blocked_patterns = config.get(\"blocked_command_patterns\", [])\n187:     if isinstance(blocked_patterns, list):\n188:         for pattern in blocked_patterns:\n189:             if isinstance(pattern, str) and re.search(pattern, command):\n190:                 return True, f\"comando bloqueado por configuracion: {pattern}\"\n191: \n192:     return False, \"\"\n193: \n194: \n195: # Hook logic - read from stdin when called as script\n196: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n197:     # Read from stdin\n198:     try:\n199:         data = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())\n200:     except json.JSONDecodeError:\n201:         data = {}\n202: \n203:     # Load agents.json directly (do not import agents_config.py)\n204:     config_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n205:     config = _read_json(config_path)\n206: \n207:     # Get active strictness profile (default to standard for backward compat)\n208:     profile_name = config.get(\"strictness_profile\", \"standard\")\n209:     profiles = config.get(\"profiles\", {})\n210:     profile_config = profiles.get(profile_name, profiles.get(\"standard\", {}))\n211: \n212:     # Build allowlist from profile\n213:     allowlist = {\n214:         \"write_roots\": profile_config.get(\"write_roots\", []),\n215:         \"blocked_command_patterns\": profile_config.get(\"blocked_command_patterns\", []),\n216:     }\n217: \n218:     # Check for tool calls (file operations)\n219:     tool_calls = data.get(\"tool_calls\", [])\n220:     if isinstance(tool_calls, list):\n221:         for tool_call in tool_calls:\n222:             if not isinstance(tool_call, dict):\n223:                 continue\n224:             tool_name = tool_call.get(\"name\", \"\")\n225:             arguments = tool_call.get(\"arguments\", {})\n226:             if not isinstance(arguments, dict):\n227:                 continue\n228: \n229:             # Extract paths from tool call\n230:             paths = _tool_paths(arguments)\n231:             for path in paths:\n232:                 blocked, reason = _is_protected_path(path, allowlist, config)\n233:                 if blocked:\n234:                     print(f\"guard_paths: {reason}\", file=sys.stderr)\n235:                     sys.exit(2)\n236: \n237:     # Check for shell commands\n238:     shell_command = data.get(\"shell_command\", \"\")\n239:     if shell_command:\n240:         blocked, reason = _is_blocked_command(shell_command, allowlist)\n241:         if blocked:\n242:             print(f\"guard_paths: {reason}\", file=sys.stderr)\n243:             sys.exit(2)\n244: \n245:     # All checks passed\n246:     sys.exit(0)\n\n(End of file - total 246 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Guard paths hook - minimal stub for TICKET-014 consolidation\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__    Before: Config en schema 1.0 sin _migrations.\n380:         During: Primera migración aplica (1.0→1.1→1.2), segunda encuentra _migrations poblado.\n381:         After: Segunda invocación retorna applied=[], skipped=[...], backups=[].\n382:         \"\"\"\n383:         cfg = tmp_path / \"agents.json\"\n384:         cfg.write_text(\n385:             json.dumps(\n386:                 {\n387: Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n4: \n5: Canonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n6: - AP-01 Mock drift\n7: - AP-02 Floor assertion\n8: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n9: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n10: - AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n11: - AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n12: - AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n13: - AP-08 Test coverage drift\n14: - AP-09 Protocol key assumption (implementación contra contrato asumido)\n15: - AP-10 Test surrogate (test que prueba un sustituto, no el código real)\n16: - AP-11 Security gate fail-open on config error\n17: \n18: Test anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n19: - AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n20: - AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).\n21: \n22: Implementation anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n23: - AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper: a function whose entire body is a single 1:1 delegate call with no own logic must be inlined or eliminated.\n24: - AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard: if the diff introduces exclusive resource acquisition (O_CREAT|O_EXCL, flock, Lock.acquire(), lock-file creation) inside a method that can be reached from more than one call site or called twice on the same instance (e.g. standalone + inside a wrapper), verify that an explicit instance-level reentrancy guard exists. Without it: BLOCKER.\n25: - AP-05 Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n26: \n27: --- Cross-cutting check (all ticket types) ---\n28: AP-06 Validator evidence missing: if the work_plan declares an explicit validator as a quality gate (e.g. skills/validate_all.py, agent_controller --validate, ruff, pytest), execution_log.md must contain: (1) the exact command executed, (2) the result, and (3) a numeric outcome where applicable (e.g. '0 invalid skills', '253 passed', 'All checks passed'). Precedence: CLI --project-root > env AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT > derived from __file__\n156: - All Files Likely Touched use centralized resolution via runtime.project_root module\n157: \n158: \n159: Marked ready by Builder\n160: \n161: --- PLAN_WP-2026-155.md ---\n162: # Work Plan - WP-2026-155\n163: \n164: ## Metadata\n165: - **ID:** WP-2026-155\n166: - **Estado:** IN_PLANNING\n167: - **deliverable_type:** code\n168: - **Titulo:** Runtime project_root contract hardening\n169: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n170: \n171: ## Objetivo\n172: Eliminar las derivaciones directas de `project_root` basadas en `__file__` en la capa runtime [A] y consolidar la precedencia canonica `CLI --project-root > env AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT > derivado`.\n173: \n174: ## Contexto\n175: La auditoria WP-2026-121 ya inventario 22 puntos [A] acoplados a `__file__` y definio el contrato deseado. El ticket prepara el runtime para operar sobre un workspace destino sin asumir que el motor vive dentro de ese mismo arbol.\n176: \n177: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n178: - La funcion central de resolucion debe ser `runtime.project_root.resolve_project_root()`.\n179: - El patron de migracion en runtime es lazy: las referencias a `PROJECT_ROOT` deben resolverse dentro de funciones o `__init__`, no a nivel modulo.\n180: - Los entry points de runtime que aceptan `--project-root` deben exportar `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT` temprano y preservar fallback compatible.\n181: - Los hooks sin CLI propia deben leer `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT` y caer al derivado solo si no existe.\n182: - `agents_config.py` conserva una derivacion local minima solo para bootstrap del `sys.path` y para importar `runtime.project_root`; no debe eliminarse ese paso inicial.\n183: - `agent_system/scripts/project_paths.py` se mantiene como helper de instalacion y diagnostico; no se reutiliza para enrutar el runtime [A].\n184: - `guard_paths.py` usa derivacion `__file__` para localizar `agents.json` dentro del motor; es una ruta [E] intencionalmente fuera de alcance.\n185: - La implementacion debe ir en commits atomicos por modulo o por grupo funcional, para mantener revert quirurgico.\n186: \n187: ## Fases\n188: ### Fase 0: Verificacion base de resolucion\n189: - **Tipo:** TAREA AGENTE\n190: - **Archivo:** `tests/unit/test_project_root_resolution.py`\n191: - **Accion:** Verificar\n192: - **Descripcion:** Confirmar que la cobertura existente sigue validando la precedencia `env > derivado` y que `pytest --collect-only` no rompe la coleccion.\n193: - **Riesgo:** 🟢 Bajo\n194: - **Criterio de Aceptacion:** La coleccion de tests del resolver sale limpia y el contrato de precedencia queda explicitado antes de tocar runtime.\n195: - **Si falla:** Corregir el path precedence de imports en `tests/conftest.py` o ajustar el test de resolucion.\n196: \n197: ### Fase 1: Consolidar el contrato de resolucion\n198: - **Tipo:** TAREA AGENTE\n199: - **Archivo:** `runtime/project_root.py`\n200: - **Accion:** Modificar\n201: - **Descripcion:** Verificar que `env > derivado` ya funciona en el modulo y que los entry points exportan `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT` antes de importar dependencias que lo consumen.\n202: - **Riesgo:** 🟡 Medio\n203: - **Criterio de Aceptacion:** La resolucion compartida queda confirmada sin tocar el contrato existente del modulo y los entry points preservan la precedencia documentada.\n204: - **Si falla:** Aislar el entry point que importa demasiado pronto y corregir el orden de inicializacion.\n205: \n206: ### Fase 2: Desacoplar call-sites runtime [A] en `scripts/`\n207: - **Tipo:** TAREA AGENTE\n208: - **Archivo:** `scripts/manager_review_bridge.py`\n209: - **Accion:** Modificar\n210: - **Descripcion:** Pasar los call-sites del grupo `scripts/` a resolucion lazy compartida en vez de derivar `PROJECT_ROOT` localmente.\n211: - **Riesgo:** 🟠 Alto\n212: - **Criterio de Aceptacion:** El bridge opera sobre el workspace destino inyectado y mantiene compatibilidad con el fallback actual.\n213: - **Si falla:** Revertir el commit atomico del modulo o grupo funcional y reintentar.\n214: \n215: ### Fase 3: Refactor de entry points y hooks restantes en `.agent/`\n216: - **Tipo:** TAREA AGENTE\n217: - **Archivo:** `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n218: - **Accion:** Modificar\n219: - **Descripcion:** Ajustar los entry points y hooks del grupo `.agent/` para encadenar `--project-root` y `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT` sin romper el flujo existente.\n220: - **Riesgo:** 🟠 Alto\n221: - **Criterio de Aceptacion:** Los scripts y hooks del alcance [A] ya no dependen de `Path(__file__)` para decidir el root operativo.\n222: - **Si falla:** Restituir el commit atomico afectado y aislar el punto conflictivo.\n223: \n224: ### Fase 4: Validacion final de alcance\n225: - **Tipo:** TAREA AGENTE\n226: - **Archivo:** `tests/unit/test_project_root_resolution.py`\n227: - **Accion:** Modificar\n228: - **Descripcion:** Anadir comprobaciones de workspace destino y asegurar que el alcance [B]/[E] no cambia.\n229: - **Riesgo:** 🟡 Medio\n230: - **Criterio de Aceptacion:** Los tests cubren precedence, fallback, no-regresion de las rutas fuera de alcance y llaman `clear_cache()` en setup/teardown cuando mutan `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT`.\n231: - **Si falla:** Ajustar fixtures, limpiar la cache de `runtime.project_root` y acotar el alcance de runtime.\n232: \n233: ## Files Likely Touched\n234: - `.agent/agent_controller.py`\n235: - `.agent/agents_config.py`\n236: - `scripts/validate_authority.py`\n253: - `scripts/run_pytest_safe.py`\n254: - `tests/unit/test_project_root_resolution.py`\n255: - `tests/conftest.py`\n256: \n257: ## Trade-offs Considerados\n258: | Opcion | Pros | Contras | Decision |\n259: |--------|------|---------|----------|\n260: | Mantener derivacion local en cada script | Menos cambios inmediatos | Mantiene el acoplamiento y duplica logica | Rechazada |\n261: | Centralizar en `runtime.project_root` | Contrato unico y testeable | Requiere refactor transversal | Elegida |\n262: | Mover todo a `agent_system/scripts/project_paths.py` | Reutiliza helper existente | Mezcla instalacion con runtime del motor | Rechazada |\n263: \n264: ## Criterios de Aceptacion Global\n265: - [ ] Los call-sites [A] usan la resolucion compartida para `project_root`.\n266: - [ ] `--project-root` y `AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT` funcionan con precedencia documentada.\n267: - [ ] Los helpers de instalacion/distribucion [B] y los artefactos engine-rooted [E] permanecen sin cambios funcionales.\n268: - [ ] La suite focalizada y `agent_controller --validate` pasan sin regresiones.\n269: \n270: \n271: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-155.md ---\n272: # Auditoria - WP-2026-155\n273: \n274: ## Focus\n275: Validar que el runtime del motor deje de asumir `project_root = Path(__file__).parent.parent` en los call-sites [A] y pase a una resolucion unica, inyectable y retrocompatible.\n276: \n277: ## Checklist de Revision\n278: \n279: - Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\n\nAP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code precheck: if the majority of Files Likely Touched are structural non-Python artifacts (references/, .gitkeep, empty dirs, placeholders, config stubs) with no logic — even if one small support file is included — the correct deliverable_type is 'documentation', not 'code'. Flag 'code' classification for majority-scaffolding tickets as a planning error (SUGGESTIONS, not BLOCKER).\n\nCanonical anti-pattern inventory (from skills/_shared/anti-patterns.md):\n- AP-01 Mock drift\n- AP-02 Floor assertion\n- AP-03 Zero-logic wrapper\n- AP-04 Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard\n- AP-05 Return contract drift (None -> bool)\n- AP-06 Validator evidence missing\n- AP-07 Scaffolding misclassified as code\n- AP-08 Test coverage drift\n- AP-09 Protocol key assumption (implementación contra contrato asumido)\n- AP-10 Test surrogate (test que prueba un sustituto, no el código real)\n- AP-11 Security gate fail-open on config error\n\nTest anti-patterns — flag as BLOCKERS if found:\n- AP-01 Mock drift: each patch/mock must target the actual API the code calls (e.g. patching pathlib.Path.open is inert if the code uses the built-in open()).\n- AP-02 Floor assertion: each numeric threshold must exceed the base value that exists without the tested feature (e.g. assert score >= 150 is trivially true if the base recency score alone is ~20_000_000).","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-155_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779894634138,"end":1779894634249}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_0ed3118810327a7d016a17093a520081919cd9730f89038f64"}},"id":"prt_e69fcc690001bzd59hg6wUL7qO","sessionID":"ses_196034e76ffeeDFNf1q2oARzaq","messageID":"msg_e69fcb243001LnNw0mcpEjyENZ"}}
