{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779894648661,"sessionID":"ses_196034e76ffeeDFNf1q2oARzaq","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_gY5tk4jZ18JCFPKhae6aNDwi","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\runtime\\project_root.py","offset":1,"limit":260},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\runtime\\project_root.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: \"\"\"\n2: Project Root Resolution Module - Central contract for WP-2026-122.\n3: \n4: This module provides a single source of truth for resolving the project root\n5: path, supporting both the default motor repository and external destination\n6: workspaces via environment variable injection.\n7: \n8: Precedence (effective):\n9:     1. AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable (set by entry points after parsing --project-root)\n10:     2. Derived from Path(__file__) (defaults to motor repository)\n11: \n12: Usage:\n13:     from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root, get_agent_dir, get_collab_dir\n14: \n15:     root = resolve_project_root()  # Path to project root\n16:     agent_dir = get_agent_dir()    # Path to .agent/ directory\n17:     collab_dir = get_collab_dir()  # Path to .agent/collaboration/ directory\n18: \n19: Design:\n20:     - Import-safe: no side effects at import time\n21:     - Cacheable: results are memoized for performance\n22:     - Single mechanism: resolve_project_root() is the only resolution path\n23:     - Backward compatible: defaults to motor repo when no external root is injected\n24: \"\"\"\n25: \n26: from __future__ import annotations\n27: \n28: import os\n29: from functools import lru_cache\n30: from pathlib import Path\n31: \n32: \n33: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n34: def resolve_project_root() -> Path:\n35:     \"\"\"\n36:     Resolve the project root path with proper precedence.\n37: \n38:     Before: Requires no state; reads os.environ[\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\"] if set.\n39:     During: Checks environment variable first, then falls back to derivation from __file__.\n40:     After: Returns absolute Path to project root, cached for subsequent calls.\n41: \n42:     Precedence:\n43:         1. AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable (if set and non-empty)\n44:         2. Derived from this module's location (runtime/project_root.py -> parent)\n45: \n46:     Returns:\n47:         Absolute Path to the project root directory.\n48: \n49:     Note:\n50:         Entry points that accept --project-root should export the value to\n51:         AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable immediately after parsing,\n52:         before importing any modules that depend on the project root.\n53:         This ensures a single channel of propagation (env var) rather than\n54:         parallel mechanisms.\n55:     \"\"\"\n56:     # Check environment variable first (set by entry points after CLI parsing)\n57:     env_root = os.environ.get(\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\", \"\").strip()\n58:     if env_root:\n59:         return Path(env_root).resolve()\n60: \n61:     # Fallback: derive from this module's location\n62:     # runtime/project_root.py -> runtime/ -> project root\n63:     return Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n64: \n65: \n66: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n67: def get_agent_dir() -> Path:\n68:     \"\"\"\n69:     Get the .agent/ directory path.\n70: \n71:     Before: Requires resolve_project_root() to be available.\n72:     During: Calls resolve_project_root() and appends \".agent\".\n73:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/ directory, cached.\n74: \n75:     Returns:\n76:         Absolute Path to the .agent/ directory.\n77:     \"\"\"\n78:     return resolve_project_root() / \".agent\"\n79: \n80: \n81: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n82: def get_collab_dir() -> Path:\n83:     \"\"\"\n84:     Get the .agent/collaboration/ directory path.\n85: \n86:     Before: Requires get_agent_dir() to be available.\n87:     During: Calls get_agent_dir() and appends \"collaboration\".\n88:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/collaboration/ directory, cached.\n89: \n90:     Returns:\n91:         Absolute Path to the .agent/collaboration/ directory.\n92:     \"\"\"\n93:     return get_agent_dir() / \"collaboration\"\n94: \n95: \n96: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n97: def get_runtime_dir() -> Path:\n98:     \"\"\"\n99:     Get the .agent/runtime/ directory path.\n100: \n101:     Before: Requires get_agent_dir() to be available.\n102:     During: Calls get_agent_dir() and appends \"runtime\".\n103:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/runtime/ directory, cached.\n104: \n105:     Returns:\n106:         Absolute Path to the .agent/runtime/ directory.\n107:     \"\"\"\n108:     return get_agent_dir() / \"runtime\"\n109: \n110: \n111: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n112: def get_context_dir() -> Path:\n113:     \"\"\"\n114:     Get the .agent/context/ directory path.\n115: \n116:     Before: Requires get_agent_dir() to be available.\n117:     During: Calls get_agent_dir() and appends \"context\".\n118:     After: Returns absolute Path to .agent/context/ directory, cached.\n119: \n120:     Returns:\n121:         Absolute Path to .agent/context/ directory.\n122:     \"\"\"\n123:     return get_agent_dir() / \"context\"\n124: \n125: \n126: @lru_cache(maxsize=1)\n127: def get_scripts_dir() -> Path:\n128:     \"\"\"\n129:     Get the scripts/ directory path.\n130: \n131:     Before: Requires resolve_project_root() to be available.\n132:     During: Calls resolve_project_root() and appends \"scripts\".\n133:     After: Returns absolute Path to scripts/ directory, cached.\n134: \n135:     Returns:\n136:         Absolute Path to scripts/ directory.\n137:     \"\"\"\n138:     return resolve_project_root() / \"scripts\"\n139: \n140: \n141: def clear_cache() -> None:\n142:     \"\"\"\n143:     Clear all cached path resolutions.\n144: \n145:     Before: Requires no state.\n146:     During: Clears lru_cache for all cached functions.\n147:     After: Next call to any resolver will recompute the value.\n148: \n149:     Use this only in testing scenarios where you need to simulate\n150:     environment changes between calls.\n151:     \"\"\"\n152:     resolve_project_root.cache_clear()\n153:     get_agent_dir.cache_clear()\n154:     get_collab_dir.cache_clear()\n155:     get_runtime_dir.cache_clear()\n156:     get_context_dir.cache_clear()\n157:     get_scripts_dir.cache_clear()\n\n(End of file - total 157 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"\nProject Root Resolution Module - Central contract for WP-2026-122.\n\nThis module provides a single source of truth for resolving the project root\npath, supporting both the default motor repository and external destination\nworkspaces via environment variable injection.\n\nPrecedence (effective):\n    1. AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable (set by entry points after parsing --project-root)\n    2. Derived from Path(__file__) (defaults to motor repository)\n\nUsage:\n    from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root, get_agent_dir, get_collab_dir\n\n    root = resolve_project_root()  # Path to project root\n    agent_dir = get_agent_dir()    # Path to .agent/ directory\n    collab_dir = get_collab_dir()  # Path to .agent/collaboration/ directory\n\nDesign:\n    - Import-safe: no side effects at import time","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"runtime\\project_root.py","time":{"start":1779894648572,"end":1779894648659}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_0234d7502b7556e4016a170948d1b88191ac5dcde7d698bfaf"}},"id":"prt_e69fcfef4001geuDR8kl73NAjC","sessionID":"ses_196034e76ffeeDFNf1q2oARzaq","messageID":"msg_e69fcd8a6001CRMAvlWYAV0QzJ"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779894648711,"sessionID":"ses_196034e76ffeeDFNf1q2oARzaq","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_DBcrpAJ6BNSHG4wvRf6F3gLe","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\check_deliverables_exist.py","offset":1,"limit":260},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\scripts\\check_deliverables_exist.py</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Check that all declared deliverables in work_plan.md exist on disk.\n3: \n4: Exits 0 if all exist, 1 if any are missing.\n5: \n6: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n7: \"\"\"\n8: \n9: from __future__ import annotations\n10: \n11: import os\n12: import re\n13: import sys\n14: from pathlib import Path\n15: \n16: \n17: # Bootstrap: project root must be on sys.path before importing runtime.project_root.\n18: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n19: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n20:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP))\n21: \n22: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n23: from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n24: \n25: \n26: PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n27: # Allow overriding PROJECT_ROOT for testing\n28: if \"TEST_PROJECT_ROOT\" in os.environ:\n29:     PROJECT_ROOT = Path(os.environ[\"TEST_PROJECT_ROOT\"])\n30: \n31: WORK_PLAN = PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n32: \n33: \n34: def looks_like_path(token: str) -> bool:\n35:     \"\"\"Determine if a token extracted from backticks looks like a file path candidate.\"\"\"\n36:     if not token or \" \" in token:\n37:         return False\n38:     # Exclude obvious non-paths like uppercase variables/constants\n39:     if token.isupper() and \"_\" in token:\n40:         return False\n41:     # Must have a dot (file extension) or path slashes\n42:     return \".\" in token or \"/\" in token or \"\\\\\" in token\n43: \n44: \n45: def resolve_with_fallbacks(token: str) -> Path | None:\n46:     \"\"\"Resolve a token to a Path using relative, absolute, and common folders fallbacks.\"\"\"\n47:     p = Path(token)\n48:     if not p.is_absolute():\n49:         p = PROJECT_ROOT / p\n50: \n51:     if p.exists():\n52:         return p.resolve()\n53: \n54:     # Fallback to search common directories\n55:     name = p.name\n56: \n57:     # 1. Check in .agent/collaboration\n58:     collab_path = PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n59:     if collab_path.exists():\n60:         return collab_path.resolve()\n61: \n62:     # 2. Check in scripts/\n63:     scripts_path = PROJECT_ROOT / \"scripts\" / name\n64:     if scripts_path.exists():\n65:         return scripts_path.resolve()\n66: \n67:     # 3. Check for hidden file (dot-prepended) in PROJECT_ROOT\n68:     hidden_path = PROJECT_ROOT / f\".{name}\"\n69:     if hidden_path.exists():\n70:         return hidden_path.resolve()\n71: \n72:     # 4. Check in skills/\n73:     skills_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / \"skills\"\n74:     if skills_dir.exists():\n75:         # Check immediate subdirectories first\n76:         for item in skills_dir.iterdir():\n77:             if item.is_dir() and item.name == name:\n78:                 return item.resolve()\n79:             # Check files within skill directories (e.g. SKILL.md)\n80:             skill_file = item / name\n81:             if skill_file.exists():\n82:                 return skill_file.resolve()\n83: \n84:     return None\n85: \n86: \n87: def _process_backtick_tokens(line: str, paths: set[Path]) -> None:\n88:     \"\"\"Extract and validate all backticked tokens from a line, adding them to paths.\"\"\"\n89:     tokens = re.findall(r\"`([^`]+)`\", line)\n90:     for token in tokens:\n91:         token = token.strip()\n92:         if not token:\n93:             continue\n94:         if token.endswith(\"/\") or token.endswith(\"\\\\\"):\n95:             # Explicitly ignore directory trails\n96:             continue\n97:         if any(x in token for x in [\"<\", \">\", \"{\", \"}\", \"YYYY\", \"NNN\"]):\n98:             # Ignore placeholder paths\n99:             continue\n100:         token = token.rstrip(\",\").strip()\n101: \n102:         if not looks_like_path(token):\n103:             continue\n104: \n105:         resolved = resolve_with_fallbacks(token)\n106:         if resolved:\n107:             paths.add(resolved)\n108:         else:\n109:             # If not found but looks like a path, add the default resolved path to missing list\n110:             p = Path(token)\n111:             if not p.is_absolute():\n112:                 p = PROJECT_ROOT / p\n113:             paths.add(p.resolve())\n114: \n115: \n116: def extract_paths_from_work_plan(content: str) -> set[Path]:\n117:     paths = set()\n118:     in_section = False\n119: \n120:     # Sections to scan\n121:     scan_keywords = {\n122:         \"deliverables\",\n123:         \"files likely touched\",\n124:         \"must create\",\n125:         \"must modify\",\n126:     }\n127:     # Sections to explicitly stop scanning\n128:     stop_headers = {\n129:         \"## tareas\",\n130:         \"## acceptance criteria\",\n131:         \"## riesgos\",\n132:         \"## plan de rollback\",\n133:         \"## orden operativo\",\n134:     }\n135: \n136:     for line in content.splitlines():\n137:         line_stripped = line.strip()\n138:         line_lower = line_stripped.lower()\n139: \n140:         # Check for section boundaries (any header starts with '#')\n141:         if line_stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n142:             # Check if this header stops the scanning\n143:             if any(stop in line_lower for stop in stop_headers):\n144:                 in_section = False\n145:                 continue\n146: \n147:             # Check if this header starts the scanning\n148:             if any(kw in line_lower for kw in scan_keywords):\n149:                 in_section = True\n150:                 continue\n151: \n152:             # Any other header (H1, H2, H3 etc.) resets scanning to False\n153:             in_section = False\n154: \n155:         # Also detect bold tags as section starters\n156:         if any(f\"**{kw}\" in line_lower for kw in [\"must create\", \"must modify\"]):\n157:             in_section = True\n158: \n159:         # Only process list items when in the correct section\n160:         if in_section and (\n161:             line_stripped.startswith(\"-\") or line_stripped.startswith(\"*\")\n162:         ):\n163:             _process_backtick_tokens(line_stripped, paths)\n164: \n165:     return paths\n166: \n167: \n168: def main() -> int:\n169:     if not WORK_PLAN.exists():\n170:         print(\n171:             f\"[check-deliverables] Active work_plan.md not found at {WORK_PLAN}\",\n172:             file=sys.stderr,\n173:         )\n174:         return 0  # Fallback if no active plan\n175: \n176:     content = WORK_PLAN.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n177:     declared_paths = extract_paths_from_work_plan(content)\n178: \n179:     if not declared_paths:\n180:         print(\n181:             \"[check-deliverables] No deliverables declared in work_plan.md\",\n182:             file=sys.stderr,\n183:         )\n184:         return 0\n185: \n186:     missing = []\n187:     print(f\"[check-deliverables] Checking {len(declared_paths)} declared deliverables:\")\n188:     for if path.exists():\n195:             print(f\"  [OK] {rel_path}\")\n196:         else:\n197:             print(f\"  [MISSING] {rel_path}\")\n198:             missing.append(path)\n199: \n200:     if missing:\n201:         print(\n202:             f\"\\n[check-deliverables] ERROR: {len(missing)} deliverable(s) are missing!\",\n203:             file=sys.stderr,\n204:         )\n205:         return 1\n206: \n207:     print(\n208:         \"\\n[check-deliverables] SUCCESS: All declared deliverables exist.\",\n209:         file=sys.stderr,\n210:     )\n211:     return 0\n212: \n213: \n214: if Fallback to 'code' with warning if missing.\n6: \n7: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: \n10: from __future__ import annotations\n11: \n12: import re\n13: import subprocess\n14: import sys\n15: from pathlib import Path\n16: \n17: \n18: # Bootstrap: project root must be on sys.path before importing runtime.project_root.\n19: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n20: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n21:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP))\n22: \n23: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n24: from runtime.project_root import get_collab_dir, resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n25: \n26: \n27: PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n28: WORK_PLAN = get_collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\"\n29: _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE = re.compile(\n30:     r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\", re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE\n31: )\n32: \n33: _VALID = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n34: \n35: \n36: def read_deliverable_type() -> str:\n37:     if not WORK_PLAN.exists():\n38:         print(\n39:             \"[dispatch] work_plan.md not found, defaulting to 'code'\", file=sys.stderr\n40:         )\n41:         return \"code\"\n42:     content = WORK_PLAN.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n43:     match = _DELIVERABLE_TYPE_RE.search(content)\n44:     if not match:\n45:         print(\n46:             \"[dispatch] no deliverable_type declared, defaulting to 'code'\",\n47:             file=sys.stderr,\n48:         )\n49:         return \"code\"\n50:     value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n51:     if \"+\" in value:\n52:         print(f\"[dispatch] compound '{value}' treated as 'mixed'\", file=sys.stderr)\n53:         return \"mixed\"\n54:     if value not in _VALID:\n55:         print(\n56:             f\"[dispatch] unknown type '{value}', defaulting to 'code'\", file=sys.stderr\n57:         )\n58:         return \"code\"\n59:     return value\n60: \n61: \n62: def run_code_gates() -> int:\n63:     # 1. ruff check (linter)\n64:     print(\"[dispatch] Running ruff check .\")\n65:     rc_ruff = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n66:         [sys.executable, \"-m\", \"ruff\", \"check\", \".\"], cwd=PROJECT_ROOT\n67:     ).returncode\n68:     if rc_ruff != 0:\n69:         return rc_ruff\n70: \n71:     # 1b. ruff format --check (formatter): la CI quality-gates lo exige.\n72:     # ruff check (linter) y ruff format son cosas distintas; sin este paso\n73:     # un archivo sin formatear pasa el gate local pero rompe la CI.\n74:     print(\"[dispatch] Running ruff format --check .\")\n75:     rc_fmt = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n76:         [sys.executable, \"-m\", \"ruff\", \"format\", \"--check\", \".\"], cwd=PROJECT_ROOT\n77:     ).returncode\n78:     if rc_fmt != 0:\n79:         print(\"[dispatch] ruff format --check failed: ejecuta 'ruff format .'\")\n80:         return rc_fmt\n81: \n82:     # 2. pytest-safe\n83:     print(\"[dispatch] Running pytest-safe\")\n84:     rc_pytest = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n85:         [sys.executable, \"scripts/run_pytest_safe.py\"], cwd=PROJECT_ROOT\n86:     ).returncode\n87:     if rc_pytest != 0:\n88:         return rc_pytest\n89: \n90:     # 3. conditional pip-audit\n91:     try:\n92:         from scripts.pip_audit_policy import should_run_pip_audit\n93: \n94:         run_audit, reason = should_run_pip_audit(PROJECT_ROOT)\n95:     except ImportError:\n96:         run_audit, reason = True, \"Fallback: could not import pip_audit_policy\"\n97: \n98:     if run_audit:\n99:         print(f\"[dispatch] Running pip-audit ({reason})\")\n100:         rc_audit = subprocess.run(\n101:             [\"uv\", \"run\", \"pip-audit\", \".\"],  # noqa: S607\n102:             cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n103:         ).returncode\n104:         if rc_audit != 0:\n105:             return rc_audit\n106:     else:\n107:         print(f\"[dispatch] Skipping pip-audit ({reason})\")\n108: \n109:     return 0\n110: \n111: \n112: def run_deliverable_gates() -> int:\n113:     rc = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n114:         [sys.executable, \"scripts/check_deliverables_exist.py\"], cwd=PROJECT_ROOT\n115:     ).returncode\n116:     return rc\n117: \n118: \n119: def main() -> int:\n120:     dtype = read_deliverable_type()\n121:     print(f\"[dispatch] deliverable_type='{dtype}'\")\n122:     if dtype in (\"code\", \"mixed\"):\n123:         rc = run_code_gates()\n124:         if rc != 0:\n125:             return rc\n126:     if dtype in (\"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"):\n127:         rc = run_deliverable_gates()\n128:         if rc != 0:\n129:             return rc\n130:     return 0\n131: \n132: \n133: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n134:     raise SystemExit(main())\n\n(End of file - total 134 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Dispatch quality gates by deliverable_type from work_plan.md.\n\nReads .agent/collaboration/work_plan.md, extracts deliverable_type, invokes\nthe appropriate gate sequence. #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Run the sequential ticket supervisor for WP-2026-024..026.\n3: \n4: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n5: \"\"\"\n6: \n7: runtime.project_root.\n15: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n16: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n17:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP))\n18: \n19: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n20: from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n21: \n22: \n23: _PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n24: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n25:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n26: _AGENT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n27: if str(_AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n28:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_AGENT_DIR))\n29: \n30: \n31: def _project_root() -> Path:\n32:     \"\"\"Return the resolved project root (cached for performance).\"\"\"\n33:     return _PROJECT_ROOT\n34: \n35: \n36: from bus.supervisor import (  # noqa: E402\n37:     SequentialTicketSupervisor,\n38: )\n39: \n40: \n41: def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:\n42:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Sequential ticket supervisor\")\n43:     parser.add_argument(\n44:         \"--once\",\n45:         action=\"store_true\",\n46:         help=\"Run a single supervision tick (default mode)\",\n47:     )\n48:     parser.add_argument(\n49:         \"--reactive\",\n50:         action=\"store_true\",\n51:         help=\"Poll events for a limited time (acotado, safer than --loop)\",\n52:     )\n53:     parser.add_argument(\n54:         \"--timeout\",\n55:         type=float,\n56:         default=300.0,\n57:         help=\"Idle timeout in seconds for --reactive mode (default 300s / resets on activity)\",\n58:     )\n59:     parser.add_argument(\n60:         \"--loop\",\n61:         action=\"store_true\",\n62:         help=\"Run continuously as a daemon (use with caution)\",\n63:     )\n64:     parser.add_argument(\n65:         \"--poll-interval\",\n66:         type=float,\n67:         default=1.0,\n68:         help=\"Polling interval used by --loop\",\n69:     )\n70:     parser.add_argument(\n71:         \"--no-auto-sync\",\n72:         action=\"store_true\",\n73:         help=\"Do not invoke the controller automatically after ticket transitions\",\n74:     )\n75:     return parser\n76: \n77: \n78: def main() -> int:\n79:     parser = build_parser()\n80:     args = parser.parse_args()\n81:     supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n82:         project_root=_PROJECT_ROOT,\n83:         auto_sync=not args.no_auto_sync,\n84:     )\n85: \n86:     if args.reactive:\n87:         if not supervisor.bootstrap():\n88:             print(\n89:                 \"[ticket-supervisor] bootstrap rejected: another supervisor instance is active\",\n90:                 file=sys.stderr,\n91:                 flush=True,\n92:             )\n93:             return 1\n94:         state = supervisor.load_state()\n95:         print(\n96:             f\"[ticket-supervisor] reactive mode start | active={state.active_ticket or 'NONE'} \"\n97:             f\"| completed={len(state.completed_tickets)} | timeout={args.timeout}s\",\n98:             flush=True,\n99:         )\n100:         supervisor.run_reactive(timeout_seconds=args.timeout)\n101:         return 0\n102: \n103:     if args.loop:\n104:         if not supervisor.bootstrap():\n105:             print(\n106:                 \"[ticket-supervisor] bootstrap rejected: another supervisor instance is active\",\n107:                 file=sys.stderr,\n108:                 flush=True,\n109:             )\n110:             return 1\n111:         state = supervisor.load_state()\n112:         print(\n113:             f\"[ticket-supervisor] loop mode start | active={state.active_ticket or 'NONE'} \"\n114:             f\"| completed={len(state.completed_tickets)} | poll={args.poll_interval}s\",\n115:             flush=True,\n116:         )\n117:         supervisor.run_loop(poll_interval=args.poll_interval)\n118:         return 0\n119: \n120:     # --once mode: acquire lock for single tick, then release\n121:     if not supervisor.bootstrap():\n122:         print(\n123:             \"[ticket-supervisor] once mode skipped: another supervisor instance is active\",\n124:             file=sys.stderr,\n125:             flush=True,\n126:         )\n127:         return 1\n128:     state = supervisor.load_state()\n129:     print(\n130:         f\"[ticket-supervisor] once mode start | active={state.active_ticket or 'NONE'} \"\n131:         f\"| completed={len(state.completed_tickets)}\",\n132:         flush=True,\n133:     )\n134:     try:\n135:         changed = supervisor.run_once()\n136:     finally:\n137:         supervisor._release_supervisor_lock()\n138:     if changed:\n139:         print(\"[ticket-supervisor] once mode processed new events\", flush=True)\n140:     return 0\n141: \n142: \n143: if __name__ \"\"\"\n2: Unit tests for runtime/project_root.py - WP-2026-122.\n3: \n4: Tests cover:\n5: - Precedence: AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT env var > derived from __file__\n6: - Fallback behavior when env var is not set\n7: - Import-safe: no side effects at import time\n8: - Caching: repeated calls return same cached value\n9: - Cache clearing for testing scenarios\n10: \"\"\"\n11: \n12: from __future__ import annotations\n13: \n14: import os\n15: from pathlib import Path\n16: from unittest.mock import patch\n17: \n18: import pytest\n19: from runtime.project_root import (\n20:     clear_cache,\n21: )\n22: \n23: \n24: class TestResolveProjectRoot:\n25:     \"\"\"Tests for resolve_project_root() function.\"\"\"\n26: \n27:     def test_fallback_to_derived_when_no_env(self) -> None:\n28:         \"\"\"When AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT is not set, derive from __file__.\"\"\"\n29:         clear_cache()\n30:         with patch.dict(os.environ, {}, clear=False):\n31:             # Remove AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT if it exists\n32:             env_copy = os.environ.copy()\n33:             env_copy.pop(\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\", None)\n34:             with patch.dict(os.environ, env_copy, clear=True):\n35:                 # Need to reload the module to pick up new env\n36:                 import importlib\n37: \n38:                 import runtime.project_root as pr\n39: \n40:                 importlib.reload(pr)\n41:                 root = pr.resolve_project_root()\n42: \n43:                 # Should be parent of runtime/ directory (which is project root)\n44:                 # runtime/project_root.py -> runtime/ -> project root\n45:                 expected = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent\n46:                 assert root == expected\n47: \n48:     def test_env_var_takes_precedence(self) -> None:\n49:         \"\"\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable takes precedence.\"\"\"\n50:         clear_cache()\n51:         fake_root = Path(\"/fake/project/root\").resolve()\n52: \n53:         with patch.dict(os.environ, {\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\": str(fake_root)}):\n54:             # Need to reload the module to pick up new env\n55:             import importlib\n56: \n57:             import runtime.project_root as pr\n58: \n59:             importlib.reload(pr)\n60:             root = pr.resolve_project_root()\n61: \n62:             assert root == fake_root\n63: \n64:     def test_empty_env_var_falls_back(self) -> None:\n65:         \"\"\"Empty AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT falls back to derivation.\"\"\"\n66:         clear_cache()\n67:         with patch.dict(os.environ, {\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\": \"\"}):\n68:             import importlib\n69: \n70:             import runtime.project_root as pr\n71: \n72:             importlib.reload(pr)\n73:             root = pr.resolve_project_root()\n74: \n75:             # runtime/project_root.py -> runtime/ -> project root\n76:             expected = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent\n77:             assert root == expected\n78: \n79:     def test_whitespace_env_var_falls_back(self) -> None:\n80:         \"\"\"Whitespace-only AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT falls back to derivation.\"\"\"\n81:         clear_cache()\n82:         with patch.dict(os.environ, {\"AGENT_PROJECT_ROOT\": \"   \"}):\n83:             import importlib\n84: \n85:             import runtime.project_root as pr\n86: \n87:             importlib.reload(pr)\n88:             root = pr.resolve_project_root()\n89: \n90:             # runtime/project_root.py -> runtime/ -> project root\n91:             expected = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent\n92:             assert root == expected\n93: \n94:     def test_caching(self) -> None:\n95:         \"\"\"Results are cached for performance.\"\"\"\n96:         clear_cache()\n97:         with patch.dict(os.environ, {}, clear=True):\n98:             import importlib\n99: \n100:             import runtime.project_root as pr\n101: \n102:             importlib.reload(pr)\n103: \n104:             root1 = pr.resolve_project_root()\n105:             root2 = pr.resolve_project_root()\n106: \n107:             # Same object returned (cached)\n108: str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:\n34:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))\n35: _AGENT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n36: if str(_AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n37:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_AGENT_DIR))\n38: \n39: \n40: def _project_root() -> Path:\n41:     \"\"\"Return the resolved project root (cached for performance).\"\"\"\n42:     return _PROJECT_ROOT\n43: \n44: \n45: from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge  # noqa: E402\n46: from bus.state_machine import StateMachine, TicketState  # noqa: E402\n47: from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor  # noqa: E402\n48: from bus.time_utils import now_local  # noqa: E402\n49: \n50: \n51: @dataclass(slots=True)\n52: class BridgeState:\n53:     \"\"\"Persisted bridge state to avoid duplicate reviews.\"\"\"\n54: \n55:     last_processed_sequence: int = 0\n56:     last_ticket_id: str | None = None\n57:     last_ticket_state: str = \"\"\n58:     updated_at: str = \"\"\n59: \n60:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str | None]:\n61:         return {\n62:             \"last_processed_sequence\": self.last_processed_sequence,\n63:             \"last_ticket_id\": self.last_ticket_id,\n64:             \"last_ticket_state\": self.last_ticket_state,\n65:             \"updated_at\": self.updated_at,\n66:         }\n67: \n68:     @classmethod\n69:     def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, object]) -> BridgeState:\n70:         return cls(\n71:             last_processed_sequence=int(data.get(\"last_processed_sequence\", 0)),\n72:             last_ticket_id=data.get(\"last_ticket_id\") or None,\n73:             last_ticket_state=str(data.get(\"last_ticket_state\", \"\")),\n74:             updated_at=str(data.get(\"updated_at\", \"\")),\n75:         )\n76: \n77: \n78: def _state_path() -> Path:\n79:     return _project_root() / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"manager_bridge_state.json\"\n80: \n81: \n82: def _resolve_manager_executable(explicit: Path | None) -> Path:\n83:     if explicit is not None:\n84:         if explicit.exists():\n85:             return explicit\n86:         raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Manager backend executable not found: {explicit}\")\n87: \n88:     # Resuelve el ejecutable del backend asignado a MANAGER en agents.json.\n89:     # WP-2026-072 movio el Manager a OpenCode; no se asume Codex (legacy).\n90:     try:\n91:         from agents_config import get_backend_for_role, load_agents_config\n92: \n93:         backend = get_backend_for_role(\"MANAGER\", load_agents_config(_project_root()))\n94:     except Exception:\n95:         backend = \"opencode\"  # agents.json ilegible -> default OpenCode\n96: \n97:     detected = shutil.which(backend)\n98:     if detected:\n99:         return Path(detected)\n100: \n101:     raise FileNotFoundError(\n102:         f\"No se pudo localizar el ejecutable del backend Manager '{backend}'. \"\n103:         \"Pasa --backend-path con la ruta explicita.\"\n104:     )\n105: \n106: \n107: def _load_state() -> BridgeState:\n108:     path = _state_path()\n109:     if not path.exists():\n110:         return BridgeState()\n111:     return BridgeState.from_dict(json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")))\n112: \n113: \n114: def _save_state(state: BridgeState) -> None:\n115:     path = _state_path()\n116:     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n117:     state.updated_at = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()\n118:     path.write_text(\n119:         json.dumps(state.to_dict(), indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),\n120:         encoding=\"utf-8\",\n121:     )\n122: \n123: \n124: def _append_block(path: Path, block: str) -> None:\n125:     content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else \"\"\n126:     content = content.rstrip()\n127:     if content:\n128:         content += \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\"\n129:     content += block.rstrip() + \"\\n\"\n130:     path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n131: \n132: \n133: def _ticket_state(\n134:     supervisor: SequentialTicketSupervisor,\n135: ) -> tuple[str | None, TicketState, int]:\n136:     state = supervisor.load_state()\n137:     ticket_id = state.active_ticket\n138:     if not ticket_id:\n139:         return None, TicketState.IN_PROGRESS, 0\n140:     events = supervisor.event_bus.read_events(ticket_id=ticket_id)\n141:     latest_sequence = events[-1].sequence_number if events else 0\n142:     current_state = (\n143:         StateMachine.derive_state_from_events([event.to_dict() for event in events])\n144:         if events\n145:         else TicketState.IN_PROGRESS\n146:     )\n147: \n148:     if current_state == TicketState.IN_PROGRESS:\n149:         log_status = _execution_log_state(supervisor)\n150:         fallback_map = {\n151:             \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\": TicketState.READY_FOR_REVIEW,\n152:             \"READY_TO_CLOSE\": TicketState.READY_TO_CLOSE,\n153:             \"COMPLETED\": TicketState.COMPLETED,\n154:         }\n155:         current_state = fallback_map.get(log_status, current_state)\n156: \n157:     return ticket_id, current_state, latest_sequence\n158: \n159: \n160: def _execution_log_state(supervisor: SequentialTicketSupervisor) -> str:\n161:     \"\"\"Read the current execution log status as a fallback signal.\"\"\"\n162:     path = getattr(\n163:         supervisor,\n164:         \"execution_log_path\",\n165:         supervisor.collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\",\n166:     )\n167:     if not path.exists():\n168:         return \"\"\n169:     content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n170:     match = re.search(\n171:         r\"^\\s*-?\\s*\\*\\*Estado:\\*\\*\\s*([^\\n]+)\", content, flags=re.MULTILINE\n172:     )\n173:     if not match:\n174:         return \"\"\n175:     return match.group(1).strip().upper()\n176: \n177: \n178: def _record_review(\n179:     supervisor: SequentialTicketSupervisor,\n180:     ticket_id: str,\n181:     decision: str,\n182:     feedback: str,\n183:     source: str,\n184: ) -> None:\n185:     review_queue = supervisor.collaboration_dir / \"review_queue.md\"\n186:     notifications = supervisor.notifications_path\n187:     timestamp = now_local().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n188: \n189:     _append_block(\n190:         review_queue,\n191:         \"\\n\".join(\n192:             [\n193:                 f\"### MANAGER REVIEW - {timestamp}\",\n194:                 f\"- **Plan ID:** {ticket_id}\",\n195:                 f\"- **Decision:** {decision}\",\n196:                 f\"- **Source:** {source}\",\n197:                 \"\",\n198:                 \"#### Summary\",\n199: #!/usr/bin/env python3\n2: \"\"\"Live monitor for the active ticket's event activity.\"\"\"\n3: \n4: from __future__ import annotations\n5: \n6: import argparse\n7: import json\n8: import re\n9: import sys\n10: import time\n11: from pathlib import Path\n12: \n13: \n14: # Bootstrap: project root must be on sys.path before importing runtime.project_root.\n15: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n16: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n17:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP))\n18: \n19: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n20: from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n21: \n22: \n23: _PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n24: \n25: \n26: def _project_root() -> Path:\n27:     \"\"\"Return the resolved project root (cached for performance).\"\"\"\n28:     return _PROJECT_ROOT\n29: \n30: \n31: def _collab_dir() -> Path:\n32:     return _project_root() / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n33: \n34: \n35: def _events_path() -> Path:\n36:     return _project_root() / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\" / \"events.jsonl\"\n37: \n38: \n39: def _state_path() -> Path:\n40:     return _project_root() / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"supervisor_state.json\"\n41: \n42: \n43: def _read_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:\n44:     if not path.exists():\n45:         return {}\n46:     try:\n47:         return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n48:     except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):\n49:         return {}\n50: \n51: \n52: def _active_ticket_from_work_plan() -> str | None:\n53:     work_plan_path = _collab_dir() / \"work_plan.md\"\n54:     if not work_plan_path.exists():\n55:         return None\n56:     content = work_plan_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n57:     for line in content.splitlines():\n58:         # Match \"- **Plan activo:** WP-YYYY-XXX\" and extract ticket ID robustly\n59:         match = re.search(\n60:             r\"Plan\\s+activo.*?:\\s*(WP-\\d{4}-[A-Za-z0-9]+)\", line, re.IGNORECASE\n61:         )\n62:         if match:\n63:             ticket = match.group(1).strip().lstrip(\"*\").rstrip(\"*\").strip()\n64:             return ticket\n65:     return None\n66: \n67: \n68: def _active_ticket() -> str | None:\n69:     state = _read_json(_state_path())\n70:     ticket = state.get(\"active_ticket\")\n71:     if isinstance(ticket, str) and ticket:\n72:         return ticket\n73:     return _active_ticket_from_work_plan()\n74: \n75: \n76: def _derive_state(events: list[dict[str, object]]) -> str:\n77:     if not events:\n78:         return \"IN_PROGRESS\"\n79:     latest = events[-1]\n80:     event_type = str(latest.get(\"event_type\", \"\"))\n81:     payload = latest.get(\"payload\", {})\n82:     if isinstance(payload, dict):\n83:         to_state = payload.get(\"to_state\")\n84:         if isinstance(to_state, str) and to_state:\n85:             return to_state\n86:     if event_type == \"REVIEW_DECISION\":\n87:         decision = (\n88:             str(payload.get(\"decision\", \"\")).upper()\n89:             if isinstance(payload, dict)\n90:             else \"\"\n91:         )\n92:         if decision == \"APPROVE\":\n93:             return \"READY_TO_CLOSE\"\n94:     return \"IN_PROGRESS\"\n95: \n96: \n97: def _load_events(ticket_id: str) -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n98:     events_path = _events_path()\n99:     if not events_path.exists():\n100:         return []\n101: \n102:     events: list[dict[str, object]] = []\n103:     for line in events_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n104:         if not line.strip():\n105:             continue\n106:         try:\n107:             event = json.loads(line)\n108:         except json.JSONDecodeError:\n109:             continue\n110:         if str(event.get(\"ticket_id\", \"\")) != ticket_id:\n111:             continue\n112:         events.append(event)\n113:     return events\n114: \n115: \n116: def _format_line(event: dict[str, object]) -> str:\n117:     timestamp = str(event.get(\"timestamp\", \"\"))\n118:     actor = str(event.get(\"actor\", \"\"))\n119:     event_type = str(event.get(\"event_type\", \"\"))\n120:     seq = event.get(\"sequence_number\", \"\")\n121:     payload = event.get(\"payload\", {})\n122:     state_hint = \"\"\n123:     if isinstance(payload, dict):\n124:         state_hint = str(payload.get(\"to_state\") or payload.get(\"decision\") or \"\")\n125:     suffix = f\" -> {state_hint}\" if state_hint else \"\"\n126:     return f\"{timestamp} | seq={seq} | {actor:<9} | {event_type:<18}{suffix}\"\n127: \n128: \n129: def _print_snapshot(ticket_id: str, events: list[dict[str, object]]) -> None:\n130:     state = _derive_state(events)\n131:     latest_ts = str(events[-1].get(\"timestamp\", \"n/a\")) if events else \"n/a\"\n132:     print(\"=\" * 72)\n133:     print(f\"Ticket activo : {ticket_id}\")\n134:     print(f\"Estado        : {state}\")\n135:     print(f\"Eventos       : {len(events)}\")\n136:     print(f\"Ultimo evento : {latest_ts}\")\n137:     print(\"-\" * 72)\n138:     for event in events[-12:]:\n139:         print(_format_line(event))\n140:     print(\"=\" * 72)\n141: \n142: \n143: def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:\n144:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Live monitor for the active ticket\")\n145:     parser.add_argument(\"--ticket-id\", type=str, help=\"Explicit ticket to monitor\")\n146:     parser.add_argument(\n147:         \"--interval\",\n148:         type=float,\n149:         default=1.0,\n150:         help=\"Polling interval in seconds\",\n151:     )\n152:     parser.add_argument(\n153:         \"--once\",\n154:         action=\"store_true\",\n155:         help=\"Print one snapshot and exit\",\n156:     )\n157:     return parser\n158: \n159: \n160: def main() -> int:\n161:     args = build_parser().parse_args()\n162:     ticket_id = args.ticket_id or _active_ticket()\n163:     if not ticket_id:\n164:         print(\"[monitor] No active ticket found.\")\n165:         return 1\n166: \n167:     last_signature = \"\"\n168:     while True:\n169:         events = _load_events(ticket_id)\n170:         signature = (\n171:             f\"{len(events)}|{events[-1].get('sequence_number') if events else 0}\"\n172:         )\n173:         if signature != last_signature:\n174:             _print_snapshot(ticket_id, events)\n175:             last_signature = signature\n176:         if args.once:\n177:             return 0\n178:         time.sleep(args.interval)\n179: \n180: \n181: if __name__ == \"tests_passing\": _check_tests_pass(),\n51:         \"no_escalations\": _check_no_pending_escalations(),\n52:         \"log_has_summary\": _check_execution_summary(),\n53:         \"findings_exist\": _check_findings_exist(),\n54:     }\n55: \n56:     passed = sum(1 for value in checks.values() if value)\n57:     total = len(checks)\n58:     percentage = int((passed / total) * 100) if total > 0 else 0\n59:     can_complete = passed >= 4\n60: \n61:     missing = []\n62:     check_names = {\n63:         \"tasks_completed\": \"Tareas pendientes en work_plan.md\",\n64:         \"tests_passing\": \"findings_exist\": \"No existe findings.md (opcional pero recomendado)\",\n68:     }\n69: \n70:     for check_name, passed_check in checks.items():\n71:         if not passed_check:\n72:             missing.append(check_names.get(check_name, check_name))\n73: \n74:     return {\n75:         \"can_complete\": can_complete,\n76:         \"percentage\": percentage,\n77:         \"checks\": checks,\n78:         \"missing\": missing,\n79:         \"passed\": passed,\n80:         \"total\": total,\n81:     }\n82: \n83: \n84: def show_completion_report(result: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n85:     \"\"\"Muestra reporte de completitud de forma legible.\"\"\"\n86:     emoji = \"âœ…\" if result[\"can_complete\"] else \"âš ï¸\"\n87:     status = \"LISTO PARA COMPLETAR\" if result[\"can_complete\"] else \"INCOMPLETO\"\n88: \n89:     print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n90:     print(f\"{emoji} VERIFICACION DE COMPLETITUD: {status}\")\n91:     print(\"=\" * 60)\n92: \n104: def safe_print(text: str, end: str = \"\\n\") -> None:\n105:     try:\n106:         print(text, end=end)\n107:     except UnicodeEncodeError:\n108:         ascii_text = text.encode(\"ascii\", runtime/ is importable\n22: for _path in (str(_PROJECT_ROOT), str(_AGENT_DIR)):\n23:     if _path not in sys.path:\n24:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n25: \n26: from runtime.project_root import get_collab_dir, resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n27: \n28: \n29: COLLAB_DIR = get_collab_dir()\n30: PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n31: WORK_PLAN = COLLAB_DIR / \"work_plan.md\"\n32: EXEC_LOG = COLLAB_DIR / \"execution_log.md\"\n33: REVIEW_QUEUE = COLLAB_DIR / \"review_queue.md\"\n34: \n35: \n36: def _read_text(path: Path) -> str:\n37:     try:\n38:         if not path.exists():\n39:             return \"\"\n40:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n41:     except Exception:\n42:         return \"\"\n43: \n44: \n45: def get_log_status(log_path: Path) -> str:\n46:     content = _read_text(log_path)\n47:     if not content:\n48:         return \"\"\n49:     for line in content.splitlines():\n50:         if \"**Estado:**\" in line:\n51:             return line.split(\"**Estado:**\", 1)[1].strip().upper()\n52:     return \"\"\n53: \n54: \n55: def is_relaxed_completion_status(status: str) -> bool:\n56:     return status.upper() in {\"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"COMPLETED\"}\n57: \n58: \n59: def check_tasks_completed(plan_path: Path, log_status: str) -> bool:\n60:     if is_relaxed_completion_status(log_status):\n61:         return True\n62:     content = _read_text(plan_path)\n63:     if not content:\n64:         return False\n65:     return \"- [ ]\" not in content\n66: \n67: \n68: def check_execution_summary(log_path: Path, log_status: str | None = None) -> bool:\n69:     if log_status and is_relaxed_completion_status(log_status):\n70:         return True\n71:     content = _read_text(log_path)\n72:     if not content:\n73:         return False\n74:     upper = content.upper()\n75:     if \"IN_PROGRESS\" in upper:\n76:         return False\n77:     return \"**ESTADO:**\" in upper or \"RESUMEN\" in upper\n78: \n79: \n80: def check_no_escalations(queue_path: Path) -> bool:\n81:     content = _read_text(queue_path)\n82:     if not content:\n83:         return True\n84:     upper = content.upper()\n85:     return \"PENDING\" not in upper and \"BLOCKED\" not in upper\n86: \n87: \n88: def resolve_test_command(project_root: Path) -> list[str]:\n89:     tests_dir = project_root / \"tests\"\n90:     if not tests_dir.exists():\n91:         return []\n92:     safe_runner = project_root / \"scripts\" / \"run_pytest_safe.py\"\n93:     if safe_runner.exists():\n94:         return [sys.executable, str(safe_runner), \"--level\", \"unit\"]\n95:     return [sys.executable, \"-m\", \"pytest\", \"tests\", \"-q\"]\n96: \n97: \n98: def run_tests(project_root: Path) -> bool:\n99:     cmd = resolve_test_command(project_root)\n100:     if not cmd:\n101:         return True\n102:     try:\n103:         result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=project_root)\n104:         return result.returncode == 0\n105:     except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError):\n106:         return True\n107: \n108: \n109: def check_quality_gates(project_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:\n110:     return {\n111:         \"tests\": run_tests(project_root),\n112:         \"tasks_completed\": check_tasks_completed(WORK_PLAN, get_log_status(EXEC_LOG)),\n113:         \"no_escalations\": check_no_escalations(REVIEW_QUEUE),\n114:         \"execution_summary\": check_execution_summary(\n115:             EXEC_LOG, get_log_status(EXEC_LOG)\n116:         ),\n117:     }\n\n(End of file - total 117 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603\n\"\"\"\nCompletion common utilities.\n\nWP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root No usa hashes MD5 complejos.\n5: \n6: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n7: \n8: Uso:\n9:     from .session_tracker import save_session, detect_stale_session, recover_session\n10: \n11:     # Al finalizar\n12:     save_session()\n13: \n14:     # Al iniciar (detectar sesion antigua)\n15:     if detect_stale_session():\n16:         recover_session()\n17: \"\"\"\n18: \n19: from __future__ import annotations\n20: \n21: import json\n22: \n23: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n24: # Setup sys.path for runtime/ imports\n25: import sys\n26: from contextlib import suppress\n27: from datetime import datetime, timedelta\n28: from pathlib import Path\n29: \n30: \n31: _AGENT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.resolve()\n32: _PROJECT_ROOT = _AGENT_DIR.parent\n33: # Insert project root FIRST so runtime/ is importable\n34: for _path in (str(_PROJECT_ROOT), str(_AGENT_DIR)):\n35:     if _path not in sys.path:\n36:         sys.path.insert(0, _path)\n37: \n38: from runtime.project_root import get_collab_dir  # noqa: E402\n39: \n40: \n41: COLLAB_DIR = get_collab_dir()\n42: SESSION_FILE = COLLAB_DIR / \".session_state.json\"\n43: \n44: STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 2\n45: MAX_FILES_TO_LIST = 10\n46: \n47: \n48: def save_session() -> None:\n49:     \"\"\"Guarda estado simple de la sesion actual.\"\"\"\n50:     with suppress(OSError, TypeError, ValueError):\n51:         COLLAB_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n52: \n53:         session = {\n54:             \"last_activity\": datetime.now().isoformat(),\n55:             \"active_plan\": _get_current_plan_id(),\n56:             \"files_count\": _count_project_files(),\n57:             \"version\": \"1.0-lite\",\n58:         }\n59: \n60:         SESSION_FILE.write_text(\n61:             json.dumps(session, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\"\n62:         )\n63: \n64: \n65: def detect_stale_session() -> bool:\n66:     \"\"\"Detecta si han pasado mas de 2 horas desde la ultima actividad.\"\"\"\n67:     if not SESSION_FILE.exists():\n68:         return False\n69: \n70:     try:\n71:         session = json.loads(SESSION_FILE.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n72:         last_activity = datetime.fromisoformat(session[\"last_activity\"])\n73:         return datetime.now() - last_activity > timedelta(hours=STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS)\n74:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError):\n75:         return False\n76: \n77: \n78: def recover_session() -> dict | None:\n79:     \"\"\"Recupera informacion de la sesion anterior.\"\"\"\n80:     if not SESSION_FILE.exists():\n81:         return None\n82: \n83:     with suppress(Exception):\n84:         session = json.loads(SESSION_FILE.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n85:         last = datetime.fromisoformat(session[\"last_activity\"])\n86:         elapsed = datetime.now() - last\n87:         hours_ago = elapsed.total_seconds() / 3600\n88: \n89:         print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 60)\n90:         print(\"[INFO] SESION ANTERIOR DETECTADA\")\n91:         print(\"=\" * 60)\n92:         print(f\"[INFO] Ultima actividad: hace {hours_ago:.1f} horas\")\n93:         print(f\"[INFO] Plan activo: {session.get('active_plan', 'N/A')}\")\n94:         print(f\"[INFO] Archivos en proyecto: {session.get('files_count', 'N/A')}\")\n95: \n96:         modified = _get_recently_modified_files(hours=int(hours_ago) + 1)\n97:         if modified:\n98:             print(\"\\n[INFO] Archivos modificados:\")\n99:             for filepath in modified[:MAX_FILES_TO_LIST]:\n100:                 print(f\"   - {filepath}\")\n101:             if len(modified) > MAX_FILES_TO_LIST:\n102:                 print(f\"   ... y {len(modified) - MAX_FILES_TO_LIST} mas\")\n103: \n104:         print(\"=\" * 60 + \"\\n\")\n105:         return session\n106:     return None\n107: \n108: \n109: def show_recovery_hint() -> None:\n110:     \"\"\"Muestra hint suave si hay sesion antigua.\"\"\"\n111:     if detect_stale_session():\n112:         print(\"[INFO] Hay una sesion anterior (>2h). Usa --recover para ver detalles.\")\n113: \n114: \n115: def _get_current_plan_id() -> str:\n116:     \"\"\"Obtiene el ID del plan actual desde work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n117:     work_plan = COLLAB_DIR / \"work_plan.md\"\n118:     if not work_plan.exists():\n119:         return \"N/A\"\n120: \n121:     with suppress(Exception):\n122:         content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n123:         for line in content.split(\"\\n\"):\n124:             if \"**ID:**\" in line:\n125:                 return line.split(\":**\")[1].strip()\n126: \n127:     return \"N/A\"\n128: \n129: \n130: def _count_project_files() -> int:\n131:     \"\"\"Cuenta archivos relevantes en el proyecto.\"\"\"\n132:     project_root = COLLAB_DIR.parent.parent\n133:     ignore_dirs = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\"}\n134:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n135: \n136:     count = 0\n137:     with suppress(Exception):\n138:         for filepath in project_root.rglob(\"*\"):\n139:             if any(part in ignore_dirs for part in filepath.parts):\n140:                 continue\n141:             if filepath.is_file() and filepath.suffix in extensions:\n142:                 count += 1\n143:                 if count > 50:\n144:                     break\n145: \n146:     return count\n147: \n148: \n149: def _get_recently_modified_files(hours: int = 2) -> list[str]:\n150:     \"\"\"Obtiene archivos modificados en las ultimas N horas.\"\"\"\n151:     project_root = COLLAB_DIR.parent.parent\n152:     cutoff = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=hours)\n153: \n154:     ignore_dirs = {\".git\", \".venv\", \"__pycache__\", \".pytest_cache\"}\n155:     extensions = {\".py\", \".md\", \".json\", \".yaml\", \".yml\", \".toml\"}\n156: \n157:     modified = []\n158:     with suppress(Exception):\n159:         for filepath in project_root.rglob(\"*\"):\n160:             if any(part in ignore_dirs for part in filepath.parts):\n161:                 continue\n162: \n163:             if filepath.is_file() and filepath.suffix in extensions:\n164:                 try:\n165:                     mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(filepath.stat().st_mtime)\n166:                     if mtime > cutoff:\n167:                         rel_path = filepath.relative_to(project_root)\n168:                         modified.append(str(rel_path))\n169:                 except (OSError, ValueError):\n170:                     continue\n171: \n172:     return sorted(modified)\n173: \n174: \n175: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n176:     import sys\n177: \n178:     if len(sys.argv) > 1:\n179:         command = sys.argv[1]\n180: \n181:         if command == \"save\":\n182:             save_session()\n183:             print(\"[OK] Sesion guardada\")\n184:         elif command == \"check\":\n185:             if detect_stale_session():\n186:                 print(\"[WARN] Sesion antigua detectada (>2h)\")\n187:             else:\n188:                 print(\"[INFO] Sesion reciente o no hay sesion\")\n189:         elif command == \"recover\":\n190:             result = recover_session()\n191:             if not result:\n192:                 print(\"[INFO] No hay sesion previa para recuperar\")\n193:         else:\n194:             print(f\"Comando desconocido: {command}\")\n195:             print(\"Usa: save | check | recover\")\n196:     else:\n197:         recover_session()\n\n(End of file - total 197 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"Session Tracker Lite para proyectos pequenos (<30 archivos).\n\nVersion simplificada de Session Recovery optimizada para uso local\ncon proyectos pequenos. break\n48: \n49:     version_manifest = AGENT_DIR / \".version_manifest.json\"\n50:     if version_manifest.exists():\n51:         with contextlib.suppress(Exception):\n52:             manifest = -> str:\n78:     # Replacement char (U+FFFD) means original byte was lost; surface as '?'\n79:     # instead of leaking the replacement glyph through to JSON consumers.\n80:     text = text.replace(\"\\ufffd\", \"?\")\n81:     try:\n82:         return text.encode(\"cp1252\").decode(\"utf-8\")\n83:     except Exception:\n84:         return text\n85: \n86: \n87: def for dynamic project root resolution.\n12: \"\"\"\n13: \n14: from __future__ import annotations\n15: \n16: import argparse\n17: import json\n18: import os\n19: import shutil\n20: import subprocess\n21: importing runtime.project_root.\n28: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n29: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n30:     sys.path.insert(0, (cached for performance).\"\"\"\n42:     return _PROJECT_ROOT\n43: \n44: \n45: class _LazyPath:\n46:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n47:         self._resolver = resolver\n48: \n49:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n50:         return self._resolver()\n51: \n52:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n53:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n54: \n55:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n56:         return self.resolve() / other\n57: \n58:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n59:         return str(self.resolve())\n60: \n61:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n62:         return str(self.resolve())\n63: \n64: \n65: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n66: AGENT_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: _AGENT_DIR)\n67: RUNTIME_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: AGENT_DIR.resolve() / \"runtime\" / \"pytest-safe\")\n68: LOCK_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: RUNTIME_DIR.resolve() / \"pytest.lock\")\n69: LAST_RUN_LOG = _LazyPath(lambda: RUNTIME_DIR.resolve() / \"last-run.log\")\n70: LAST_RUN_JSON = _LazyPath(lambda: RUNTIME_DIR.resolve() / \"last-run.json\")\n71: \n72: DEFAULT_PYTEST_ARGS = [\n73:     \"tests/test_event_bus.py\",\n74:     \"tests/test_supervisor.py\",\n75: \"tests/unit/test_bus_emission_on_mark_ready.py\",\n93:     \"-q\",\n94:     \"-p\",\n95:     \"no:cacheprovider\",\n96: ]\n97: \n98: LEVEL_CHOICES = {\"unit\", \"integration\", \"all\"}\n99: \n100: \n101: def iso_now() -> str:\n102:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()\n103: \n104: \n105: def ensure_runtime_dir() -> None:\n106:     RUNTIME_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n107: \n108: \n109: def is_pid_running(pid: int) -> bool:\n110:     if pid <= 0:\n111:         return False\n112:     try:\n113:         os.kill(pid, 0)\n114:     except OSError:\n115:         return False\n116:     return True\n117: \n118: \n119: def read_json(path: Path) -> dict:\n120:     try:\n121:         return path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n128: \n129: \n130: def acquire_lock(force_unlock: bool = False) -> dict:\n131:     ensure_runtime_dir()\n132: \n133:     if LOCK_FILE.exists():\n134:         stale = True\n135:         lock_data = read_json(LOCK_FILE)\n136:         lock_pid = int(lock_data.get(\"pid\", 0) or 0)\n137:         if is_pid_running(lock_pid):\n138:             stale = False\n139:         if not stale and not force_unlock:\n140:             raise RuntimeError(\n141:                 f\"Ya hay un pytest activo (pid={lock_pid}). \"\n142:                 f\"Si estas seguro de que es stale, usa --force-unlock.\"\n143:             )\n144:         LOCK_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n145: \n146:     payload = {\n147:         \"pid\": os.getpid(),\n148:         \"started_at\": iso_now(),\n149:         \"cwd\": str(PROJECT_ROOT),\n150:     }\n151:     fd = os.open(str(LOCK_FILE), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY)\n152:     try:\n153:         with os.fdopen(fd, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as handle:\n154:             json.dump(payload, handle, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n155:     except Exception:\n156:         LOCK_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n157:         raise\n158:     return payload\n159: \n160: \n161: def release_lock() -> None:\n162:     LOCK_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n163: \n164: \n165: def iter_project_temp_dirs() -> Iterable[Path]:\n166:     for entry in PROJECT_ROOT.iterdir():\n167:         if not entry.is_dir():\n168:             continue\n169:         if entry.name in {\".pytest_tmp\", \"_pytest_tmp\"} or entry.name.startswith(\n170:             \"_pytest_tmp_\"\n171:         ):\n172:             yield entry\n173: \n174: \n175: def remove_tree(path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n176:     try:\n177:         shutil.rmtree(path)\n178:         return True, \"\"\n179:     except FileNotFoundError:\n180:         return True, \"\"\n\n(Showing lines 1-180 of 448. Use offset=181 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Runner seguro para pytest en agent_system.\n\nObjectives:\n- inspeccionar el estado antes de tocar nada\n- evitar ejecuciones concurrentes de pytest\n- mantener los temporales dentro del proyecto\n- limpiar residuos conocidos antes y despues del run\n- dejar log del ultimo run para diagnostico\n\nWP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport json\nimport os\nimport shutil\nimport subprocess","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":"scripts\\run_pytest_safe.py","time":{"start":1779894672843,"end":1779894672918}},"metadata":{"openai":{"itemId":"fc_00c983a08adc9013016a170961145c8191a4b6be9cf0bd18f4"}},"id":"prt_e69fd5db2001HAZHgailvzS7xP","sessionID":"ses_196034e76ffeeDFNf1q2oARzaq","messageID":"msg_e69fd2435001MXr1Cd4SEujDwz"}}

implements work plan.\n3: \n4: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n5: \"\"\"\n6: \n7: from __future__ import annotations\n8: \n9: import argparse\n10: import sys\n11: import time\n12: from datetime import datetime\n13: from pathlib import Path\n14: \n15: \n16: # Bootstrap: project root must be on sys.path before importing runtime.project_root.\n17: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n18: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n19:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP))\n20: \n21: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n22: from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n23: \n24: \n25: _PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n26: PROJECT_ROOT = _PROJECT_ROOT  # Alias for backward compatibility with subprocess calls\n27: AGENT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / \".agent\"\n28: if str(AGENT_DIR) not in sys.path:\n29:     sys.path.insert(0, str(AGENT_DIR))\n30: \n31: from agent_controller import (  # noqa: E402\n32:     WORK_PLAN,\n33:     get_plan_id,\n34:     publish_state_changed_event,\n35:     read_file,\n36:     update_log_status,\n37: )\n38: \n39: \n40: def main():\n41:     \"\"\"Main builder flow.\"\"\"\n42:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Builder agent for the active ticket\")\n43:     parser.add_argument(\n44:         \"--ticket-id\",\n45:         default=None,\n46:         help=\"Ticket ID to implement (defaults to the active plan_id from work_plan.md)\",\n47:     )\n48:     args = parser.parse_args()\n49: \n50:     print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n51:     print(f\"BUILDER AGENT - {args.ticket_id}\")\n52:     print(\"=\" * 70)\n53: \n54:     # 1. Read work plan\n55:     print(\"\\n[1] Leyendo work_plan.md...\")\n56:     plan_content = read_file(WORK_PLAN)\n57:     plan_id = get_plan_id(plan_content)\n58: \n59:     if not plan_id:\n60:         print(\"[ERROR] No se encontro plan_id en work_plan.md\")\n61:         return 1  # return 1 = error (no plan found)\n62: \n63:     if not args.ticket_id or args.ticket_id != plan_id:\n64:         if args.ticket_id and args.ticket_id != plan_id:\n65:             print(\n66:                 f\"[WARN] Ticket solicitado ({args.ticket_id}) no coincide con el plan activo ({plan_id}). \"\n67:                 \"Se usara el plan activo para evitar drift.\"\n68:             )\n69:         args.ticket_id = plan_id\n70: \n71:     print(f\"[OK] Plan activo: {plan_id}\")\n72:     print(\"     Objetivo: Smoke test del requeue Manager/Builder\")\n73:     print(\"     Estado: APPROVED\")\n74: \n75:     # 2. Log execution start\n76:     print(\"\\n[2] Registrando inicio de implementacion...\")\n77:     timestamp = datetime.now().strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")\n78:     exec_note = (\n79:         f\"\\n### BUILDER START - {timestamp}\\n\"\n80:         f\"- **Agente:** Builder (Python script)\\n\"\n81:         f\"- **Plan ID:** {plan_id}\\n\"\n82:         f\"- **Ticket:** {args.ticket_id}\\n\"\n83:         f\"- **Accion:** Iniciando implementacion del smoke test\\n\"\n84:     )\n85:     update_log_status(\"IN_PROGRESS\", exec_note)\n86:     print(\"[OK] Registro actualizado\")\n87: \n88:     # 3. Prepare implementation\n89:     print(\"\\n[3] Preparando implementacion...\")\n90:     print(\"     Archivos a revisar:\")\n91:     print(\"       - PROJECT.md (documentacion del proyecto)\")\n92:     print(\"       - QUICKSTART.md (instrucciones de arranque)\")\n93:     print(\"       - work_plan.md (este plan)\")\n94:     print(\"       - TURN.md (turno actual)\")\n95:     print(\"       - STATE.md (estado snapshot)\")\n96:     print(\"       - execution_log.md (bitacora)\")\n97:     print(\"       - notifications.md (notificaciones)\")\n98: \n99:     print(\"\\n[4] Ejecutando validaciones...\")\n100:     print(\"     - python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force\")\n101:     import subprocess\n102: \n103:     result = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n104:         [\n105:             sys.executable,\n106:             \".agent/agent_controller.py\",\n107:             \"--validate\",\n108:             \"--json\",\n109:             \"--force\",\n110:         ],\n111:         cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n112:         capture_output=True,\n113:         text=True,\n114:         shell=False,\n115:     )\n116:     if result.returncode != 0:\n117:         print(f\"[WARN] Validacion: {result.stderr[:200]}\")\n118:     else:\n119:         print(\"[OK] Validacion pasada\")\n120: \n121:     print(\"\\n[5] Ejecutando tests...\")\n122:     result = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n123:         [\n124:             sys.executable,\n125:             \"scripts/run_pytest_safe.py\",\n126:             \"tests/test_agent_controller.py\",\n127:             \"-q\",\n128:         ],\n129:         cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n130:         capture_output=True,\n131:         text=True,\n132:         timeout=30,\n133:         shell=False,\n134:     )\n135:     if \"passed\" in result.stdout:\n136:         print(f\"[OK] {result.stdout.splitlines()[-1]}\")\n137:     else:\n138:         print(f\"[WARN] Tests: {result.stdout[:200]}\")\n139: \n140:     # 6. Mark as ready for review\n141:     print(\"\\n[6] Marcando como READY_FOR_REVIEW...\")\n142:     update_log_status(\n143:         \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"\\n### BUILDER COMPLETE\\n- Ready for Manager review\\n\"\n144:     )\n145:     publish_state_changed_event(\n146:         plan_id, \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\", \"Builder complete\"\n147:     )\n148:     print(\"[OK] Estado actualizado a READY_FOR_REVIEW\")\n149: \n150:     # 7. Execute mark-ready\n151:     print(\"\\n[7] Ejecutando mark-ready...\")\n152:     result = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603\n153:         [\n154:             sys.executable,\n155:             \".agent/agent_controller.py\",\n156:             \"--mark-ready\",\n157:             \"--json\",\n158:             \"--force\",\n159:         ],\n160:         cwd=PROJECT_ROOT,\n161:         capture_output=True,\n162:         text=True,\n163:         shell=False,\n164:     )\n165:     if result.returncode == 0:\n166:         print(\"[OK] mark-ready ejecutado\")\n167:     else:\n168:         print(f\"[WARN] mark-ready: {result.stderr[:200]}\")\n169: \n170:     print(\"\\n\" + \"=\" * 70)\n171:     print(\"BUILDER COMPLETE - Esperando revisión del Manager...\")\n172:     print(\"=\" * 70)\n173:     # Normal exit: success if no errors (return 0 = exito real)\n174: \n175:     # Keep the window open, waiting for manager feedback\n176:     print(\"\\nEsperando respuesta del Manager...\")\n177:     print(\"Si se rechaza el trabajo, Builder sera requeued automaticamente.\")\n178:     print(\"Presiona Ctrl+C para salir.\\n\")\n179: \n180:     try:\n181:         while True:\n182:             time.sleep(1)\n183:     except KeyboardInterrupt:\n184:         print(\"\\n[EXIT] Builder terminado por usuario\")\n185:         # Clean exit: user interruption (Ctrl+C) is not a failure, just manual stop (return 0 = salida limpia).\n186:         return 0\n187: \n188: \n189: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n190:     sys.exit(main())\n\n(End of file - Run manually at session close.\n5: Default dry-run; --apply to write changes.\n6: \n7: WP-2026-122: Uses runtime.project_root for dynamic project root resolution.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: \n10: from __future__ import annotations\n11: \n12: import argparse\n13: import json\n14: import shutil\n15: import sys\n16: from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone\n17: from pathlib import Path\n18: from typing import Any\n19: \n20: \n21: # Bootstrap: project root must be on sys.path before importing runtime.project_root.\n22: _PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\n23: if str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP) not in sys.path:\n24:     sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT_BOOTSTRAP))\n25: \n26: # WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n27: from runtime.project_root import get_agent_dir  # noqa: E402\n28: \n29: \n30: AGENT_DIR = get_agent_dir()\n31: MEMORY_DIR = AGENT_DIR / \"runtime\" / \"memory\"\n32: ARCHIVE_DIR = MEMORY_DIR / \"archive\"\n33: OBS = MEMORY_DIR / \"observations.jsonl\"\n34: MEMORY_MD = MEMORY_DIR / \"MEMORY.md\"\n35: REPORT = MEMORY_DIR / \"CONSOLIDATION_REPORT.md\"\n36: \n37: NOISE_PREFIXES = (\"Tool \",)\n38: MIN_SIGNAL_LEN = 30\n39: DEDUPE_WINDOW_HOURS = 24\n40: MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP = 80\n41: \n42: \n43: def is_noise(signal: str) -> bool:\n44:     \"\"\"Check if signal should be dropped as noise.\"\"\"\n45:     signal_stripped = signal.strip()\n46:     if len(signal_stripped) < MIN_SIGNAL_LEN:\n47:         return True\n48:     for prefix in NOISE_PREFIXES:\n49:         if signal_stripped.startswith(prefix) and signal_stripped.endswith(\"called\"):\n50:             return True\n51:     return False\n52: \n53: \n54: def parse_entries(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n55:     \"\"\"Parse JSONL file, skipping malformed lines with warning.\"\"\"\n56:     entries = []\n57:     if not path.exists():\n58:         return entries\n59:     with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n60:         for line_num, line in enumerate(f, 1):\n61:             line = line.strip()\n62:             if not line:\n63:                 continue\n64:             try:\n65:                 entry = json.loads(line)\n66:                 entries.append(entry)\n67:             except json.JSONDecodeError:\n68:                 print(f\"Warning: Skipping malformed JSON at line {line_num}\")\n69:     return entries\n70: \n71: \n72: def parse_timestamp(ts_str: str) -> datetime:\n73:     \"\"\"Parse ISO timestamp string to datetime.\"\"\"\n74:     ts_str = ts_str.replace(\"Z\", \"+00:00\")\n75:     try:\n76:         return datetime.fromisoformat(ts_str)\n77:     except ValueError:\n78:         return datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n79: \n80: \n81: def dedupe(entries: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], int]:\n82:     \"\"\"Dedupe entries within 24h window. Returns (kept_entries, dropped_count).\"\"\"\n83:     if not entries:\n84:         return [], 0\n85: \n86:     keyed: dict[tuple[str, str, str], dict[str, Any]] = {}\n87:     dropped = 0\n88: \n89:     for entry in entries:\n90:         signal = entry.get(\"signal\", \"\")\n91:         source = entry.get(\"source\", \"unknown\")\n92:         topic = entry.get(\"topic\", \"general\")\n93:         ts_str = entry.get(\"timestamp\", \"\")\n94:         ts = parse_timestamp(ts_str)\n95: \n96:         key = (signal, source, topic)\n97: \n98:         if key in keyed:\n99:             existing_ts = parse_timestamp(keyed[key].get(\"timestamp\", \"\"))\n100:             hours_diff = abs((ts - existing_ts).total_seconds()) / 3600\n101:             if hours_diff <= DEDUPE_WINDOW_HOURS:\n102:                 if ts >= existing_ts:\n103:                     keyed[key] = entry\n104:                 dropped += 1\n105:             else:\n106:                 keyed[f\"{key}_{ts_str}\"] = entry\n107:         else:\n108:             keyed[key] = entry\n109: \n110:     return list(keyed.values()), dropped\n111: \n112: \n113: def split_by_age(\n114:     entries: list[dict[str, Any]], days: int = 30\n115: ) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:\n116:     \"\"\"Split entries into recent and archivable by age.\"\"\"\n117:     cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=days)\n118:     recent = []\n119:     archivable = []\n120: \n121:     for entry in entries:\n122:         ts_str = entry.get(\"timestamp\", \"\")\n123:         ts = parse_timestamp(ts_str)\n124:         if ts < cutoff:\n125:             archivable.append(entry)\n126:         else:\n127:             recent.append(entry)\n128: \n129:     return recent, archivable\n130: \n131: \n132: def group_by_topic(entries: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:\n133:     \"\"\"Group entries by topic.\"\"\"\n134:     grouped: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}\n135:     for entry in entries:\n136:         topic = entry.get(\"topic\", \"general\")\n137:         if topic not in grouped:\n138:             grouped[topic] = []\n139:         grouped[topic].append(entry)\n140:     return grouped\n141: \n142: \n143: def regen_memory_md(entries: list[dict[str, Any]], stats: dict[str, int]) -> str:\n144:     \"\"\"Generate MEMORY.md content from entries.\n145: \n146:     Before: Requires entries list and stats dict.\n147:     During: Groups entries by topic, generates markdown with sections.\n148:     After: Returns markdown string capped at MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP (80) lines\n149:            with visible truncation marker if exceeded.\n150:     \"\"\"\n151:     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n152:     grouped = group_by_topic(entries)\n153: \n154:     lines = [\n155:         \"# MEMORY\",\n156:         \"\",\n157:         f\"Regenerated: {now}\",\n158:         \"\",\n159:         f\"Total observations: {len(entries)}\",\n160:         \"\",\n161:     ]\n162: \n163:     for topic in sorted(grouped.keys()):\n164:         topic_entries = grouped[topic]\n165:         topic_name = topic.replace(\"_\", \" \").title()\n166:         lines.append(f\"- {topic_name} ({len(topic_entries)} observations)\")\n167: \n168:     lines.append(\"\")\n169: \n170:     for topic in sorted(grouped.keys()):\n171:         topic_entries = sorted(\n172:             grouped[topic],\n173:             key=lambda e: parse_timestamp(e.get(\"timestamp\", \"\")),\n174:             reverse=True,\n175:         )[:10]\n176:         lines.append(f\"## {topic}\")\n177:         for entry in topic_entries:\n178:             signal = entry.get(\"signal\", \"\")[:200]\n179:             lines.append(f\"- {signal}\")\n180:         lines.append(\"\")\n181: \n182:     lines.append(\"---\")\n183:     lines.append(\"\")\n184:     lines.append(\"## Archive Pointers\")\n185:     lines.append(\"\")\n186:     if ARCHIVE_DIR.exists():\n187:         archive_files = sorted(ARCHIVE_DIR.glob(\"observations.*.jsonl\"))\n188:         if archive_files:\n189:             lines.append(\"Historical archives:\")\n190:             lines.extend(f\"- `{af.name}`\" for af in archive_files)\n191:         else:\n192:             lines.append(\"No archive files yet.\")\n193:     else:\n194:         lines.append(\"Archive directory does not exist yet.\")\n195: \n196:     lines.append(\"\")\n197:     lines.append(\n198:         f\"Stats: kept={stats['kept']}, deduped={stats['deduped']}, dropped={stats['dropped']}, archived={stats['archived']}\"\n199:     )\n200: \n201:     content = \"\\n\".join(lines)\n202: \n203:     # Apply line cap with truncation marker\n204:     all_lines = content.split(\"\\n\")\n205:     if len(all_lines) > MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP:\n206:         cap_marker_lines = [\n207:             \"\",\n208:             \"---\",\n209:             \"\",\n210:             f\"[MEMORY.md truncated at {MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP} lines. \"\n211:             \"Full history available in observations.jsonl]\",\n212:         ]\n213:         truncate_at = MEMORY_MD_LINE_CAP - len(cap_marker_lines)\n214:         truncated_lines = all_lines[:truncate_at] + cap_marker_lines\n215:         content = \"\\n\".join(truncated_lines)\n216: \n217:     return content\n218: \n219: \n220: def write_report(stats: dict[str, Any], dry_run: bool = True) -> None:\n\n(Showing lines 1-220 of 423. WP-2026-122 / WP-2026-155: Centralized path resolution via runtime.project_root\n33: from runtime.project_root import resolve_project_root  # noqa: E402\n34: \n35: \n36: _PROJECT_ROOT = resolve_project_root()\n37: \n38: \n39: def _project_root() -> Path:\n40:     \"\"\"Return the resolved project root (cached for performance).\"\"\"\n41:     return _PROJECT_ROOT\n42: \n43: \n44: def _out_dir(project_root: Path) -> Path:\n45:     return project_root / \"graphify-out\"\n46: \n47: \n48: class _LazyPath:\n49:     def __init__(self, resolver):\n50:         self._resolver = resolver\n51: \n52:     def resolve(self) -> Path:\n53:         return self._resolver()\n54: \n55:     def __getattr__(self, name: str):\n56:         return getattr(self.resolve(), name)\n57: \n58:     def __truediv__(self, other):\n59:         return self.resolve() / other\n60: \n61:     def __fspath__(self) -> str:\n62:         return str(self.resolve())\n63: \n64:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n65:         return str(self.resolve())\n66: \n67:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n68:         return f\"_LazyPath({self.resolve()!r})\"\n69: \n70: \n71: PROJECT_ROOT = _LazyPath(_project_root)\n72: OUT_DIR = _LazyPath(lambda: _out_dir(_project_root()))\n73: GRAPH_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: _out_dir(_project_root()) / \"graph.json\")\n74: CACHE_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: _out_dir(_project_root()) / \"cache\" / \"sha256.json\")\n75: REPORT_FILE = _LazyPath(lambda: _out_dir(_project_root()) / \"GRAPH_REPORT.md\")\n76: \n77: # Extensiones a procesar\n78: EXTENSIONS = {\".py\", \".md\"}\n79: \n80: # Directorios a ignorar\n81: IGNORE_DIRS = {\n82:     \"__pycache__\",\n83:     \".pytest_cache\",\n84:     \".git\",\n85:     \".venv\",\n86:     \"venv\",\n87:     \"graphify-out\",\n88:     \"node_modules\",\n89:     \".mypy_cache\",\n90:     \".ruff_cache\",\n91:     \".tmp\",\n92:     \"tmp_pytest_*\",\n93:     \"agent_system\",  # proyecto externo -- no mezclar\n94:     \"uv-cache\",  # env dependencies -- not product code\n95:     \"_archive\",  # plan/audit history -- internal state\n96:     \"review_packets\",  # review packets -- internal state\n97:     \"reviews\",  # review outputs -- internal state\n98:     \"sandbox\",  # one-shot debug scripts -- not portable product\n99:     \"test_runtime\",  # generated test session state -- not portable product\n100: }\n101: \n102: #
